eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Die Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Die ba yaala ba yigi wo mɩŋ, die chɩaŋ ma die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ ʋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ a yɩ ba aŋ ka dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die zie wo ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Ama die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Nɩɩŋmara dɩ keŋ ŋmɩntɩ jɩŋmɩŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ, Soriŋ jigiri nan dɩ vɩɩna.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Ta sʋkʋleeliŋ dɩaŋ nɩɩŋmara dɩ keŋ ŋmɩntɩ ta bɩ sɩbɩ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ, Nɩɩŋ baa ka nɩɩŋ mɩŋ. Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a daansɩ nɩɩŋmara ta balɩ wudieke dɩ balla ka yi ama nɩ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba wudieke dɩ yinene lele chɩasɩ!.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Nɩnɩŋ, lele vuosi nɩ yiwo vuobɩatɩ ta ka dɩsa Ŋmɩŋ, nɩ yaala nɩ ye wo mamachi wɩɩŋ; ama n kaaŋ wɔŋ yi wɩɩŋ a yɩ nɩ; ama mamachi dieke nyɩɩna n baaŋ nan nagɩ a yɩ nɩ yiwo Jona mamachi ka. Die ʋ balla die a yɩ ba, ta die ga ta va ba.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gasɩna mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ a ga mi wo chaakʋ, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ daaŋ ta ka nagɩ paanʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dabɔtɩ wɩa.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Die ba a bala a yɩa taŋ a baarɩ, Dɩ yiwo tɩ kana ka nagɩ paanʋ wɩa ʋ bala die wo.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna ba balala wudieke wo die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba nɩ yada dɩ kana ka dala, bɩa nɩ bala yɩa taŋ nɩ wone paanʋ wɩa?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Nɩ ye ka sɩba ka chɩaŋ? Nɩ ka tɩɩnsɩ die n nagɩna paanʋ golime anʋ a yɩ dembisi tuse anʋ wa? Die kparɩsɩ aŋmɩa die nɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha a suuli?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Ta die n nagɩ paanʋ golime ayʋpɔyɩ a yɩ dembisi tuse anɩɩsa wa, kparɩsɩ aŋmɩa die nɩ vaarɩ?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Bɩa yine nɩ yile dɩ paanʋ wɩa maŋ bala nɩ? Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dabɔtɩ wɩa! .

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Die ba wa a sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ ka bala ba dɩ ba sɩmma yaa gamma dabɔtɩ dieke ba yalla a yɩa paanʋ sʋŋ ama dɩ ba sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dagɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Yisa die dɩ ga Siisiria Filipi tɩŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Mɩnɩa vuosisi dɩ baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa yiye.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Die ba baarɩ, Bataŋ baarɩ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, bataŋ dɩaŋ baarɩ yiwo Elaja ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ yiwo Jerimaya yaa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Ta ʋ pɩasɩ ba, Ta nɩnɩŋ mɩnɩa nɩ baarɩ n yiye.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Womi Simoni Piita dɩ baarɩ, Fʋ yine miivolitieŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa Masia wa, vuodieke ʋ vʋarɩna.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Fʋnɩŋ Simoni, Jona bʋa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bemme fʋ ma, dama daa vuota yuori wɩarɩ gie a dagɩ fʋ ama dɩ yiwo n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: N bala fʋ, fʋnɩŋ fʋ yiwo Piita ta tanjaalɩka gie sikpeŋ maŋ nan nagɩ n vuosi zieŋ, ta kuŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ ba.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N nan yɩ fʋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sanʋaŋ safɩbie; die wɩa jadieke mana fʋ baarɩ dɩ chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ nan dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma; ta jadieke mana fʋ baarɩ ka ka chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ kaaŋ dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ yine Masia wa.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: A nyɩŋ saŋka mi yaa gamma, Yisa die dɩ piili a bala a yuore ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Sie n ga Jerusalemi a ga dii wahala a nyɩŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba nuusi me. Ba nan kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan hagɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Piita die dɩ nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a piili a galɩma wa a baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, Ŋmɩŋ da vaa die yi. Naa kaaŋ daaŋ yi fʋ.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ Piita, Fʋnɩŋ Sitaani wa gie hagɩ gie ta va mɩŋ. Yiwo taŋgbiŋkiŋ n sieŋ me dama fʋ sʋŋanyilehe gie yiwo vuota sʋŋanyile ama daa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Womi ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ yaala ʋ dɩa n kʋaŋ, sie ʋ zeti ʋ gbaŋ a nagɩ ʋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Dama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka saagɩya dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ miivoli a yɩ mɩŋ nan waarɩ ka, ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ waarɩ ʋ miivoli n wɩa wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nan daansɩ ye ke.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Bɩa vuota dɩ baa ʋ ye, ʋ yallɩ dʋnɩa nyinti mana ta waarɩ ʋ miivoli, bɩa nyʋarɩ ʋ baa ʋ ye? Vuoŋ nan bɩagɩ nagɩ jaaŋ a daa ʋ miivoli?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa baa n yiŋŋi keŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ, aŋaŋ ʋ malakasi, ta saŋŋa mi n nan tuŋ vuoŋ mana yaa gamma ʋ tʋnna die.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuosi bataŋ bie gie ba kaaŋ kpi sie ba keŋ ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa naarɩ diile saŋŋa.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yisa Masia chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ saara wʋnna: die ʋ yiwo Davidi haagɩŋ ta Davidi dɩaŋ dɩ yi Abarahami haagɩŋ.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abarahami die dɩ mɩɩrɩ Aziki, ta Aziki dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Jakobu, ta Jakobu dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Juda aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Juda die mɩɩrɩ wa Perezi aŋaŋ Zera ta ba nuŋ dɩ yi Tamari: Perezi die mɩɩrɩ wa Hezironi, ta Hezironi die mɩɩrɩ wa Rami.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Rami die mɩɩrɩ wa Aminadabi, Aminadabi die mɩɩrɩ wa Naasoni, ta Naasoni die dɩ mɩɩrɩ Salimoni.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Salimoni die mɩɩrɩ wa Buasi vuodieke nuŋ dɩ yine Rahabi; ta Buasi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ wa Obedi, vuodieke nuŋ dɩ yine Rufi; ta Obedi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ wa Jese.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: Jese die mɩɩrɩ wa naaŋ Davidi. Davidi dɩaŋ die mɩɩrɩ wa Solomoni, vuodieke nuŋ die dɩ yine Yuriya kpihɔgʋ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Solomoni die mɩɩrɩ wa Rehobuami, ta Rehobuami dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Abia, ta Abia dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Asa.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Asa die mɩɩrɩ wa Jehosafati, ta Jehosafati dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Jorami, ta Jorami dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Yuzaya.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Yuzaya die mɩɩrɩ wa Jotami, ta Jotami dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Ahazi, ta Ahazi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Hezekaya.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Hezekaya die mɩɩrɩ wa Manase, ta Manase dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Amoni, ta Amoni dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Josaya.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: ta Josaya die mɩɩrɩ wa Jekonaya aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ, saŋŋa gie die ba yigi Izara vuosi a bɩrɩŋ ba yɔŋɩsɩ a yaa ba ga Babiloni tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Die ba yalla ba ga Babiloni tɩŋgbaŋ ma kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Jekonaya die dɩ mɩɩrɩ Salatieli, ta Salatieli dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Zerubabeli.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Zerubabeli die dɩ mɩɩrɩ Abuyidi, ta Abuyidi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Eliakimi, ta Eliakimi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Azori.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Azori die mɩɩrɩ wa Zadoki, ta Zadoki dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Achimi, ta Achimi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Eludi.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Eludi die mɩɩrɩ wa Eliaza, ta Eliaza dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Matani, ta Matani dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Jakobu.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Jakobu die mɩɩrɩ wa Josefu, vuodieke die dɩ yine Meri chʋrʋ, ta Meri dɩ mɩɩrɩna Yisa vuodieke ba wasɩnana Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Die dɩ nyɩnna Abarahami saŋŋa a ga tʋga Davidi saŋŋa die yiwo chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba baŋ aŋaŋ banɩɩsa. Davidi saŋŋa a ga tʋga saŋŋa dieke die ba yigine be a bɩrɩŋ ba yɔŋɩsɩ a yaa ba ga Babiloni die yiwo chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba baŋ aŋaŋ banɩɩsa. Die dɩ nyɩŋ saŋŋa mi a yaa a ga tʋga Masia maarɩŋ saŋŋa die yiwo chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba baŋ aŋaŋ banɩɩsa.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa Masia die wonde. Yisa nuŋ daa die wone Josefu ama die ba ye ka faarɩ tamba, ta Meri die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ sɩba dɩ ʋ yigi wo nyuuti, die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die vana ʋ yigi kanɩŋ nyuuti mi.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Josefu die yiwo daa dieke die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ, ta die ʋ ka yaala ʋ nagɩ Meri a yi viivi me vuoŋ mana nine me. Naa chɩaŋ ma die ʋ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ lɔbɩrɩ a zeti wo.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Die ʋ yilinene naa, Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ daansa ma a baarɩ, Josefu, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Davidi buuriŋ ma, da zeti fʋ balla fʋ nagɩ Meri a bɩrɩŋ fʋ hɔgʋ wa. Dama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yɩna wa nyuutiti.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: Ʋ nan mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ aŋ nɩ wa wa Yisa, dama ʋ nan vʋarɩ ʋ vuosi a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma ma.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Wɩaha gie mana die keŋ yi mɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vana ʋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩna die wo, dɩ.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Hɔgʋ dieke dɩ zɩna dembiŋ nan yigi nyuuti a mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ aŋ ʋ saaŋ yi Emmanueli.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Die wɩa die Josefu die dɩ hagɩna ʋ daansaha ma die ʋ faarɩ Meri sɩba Ŋmɩŋ malakaka die dɩ bala wa dɩ ʋ yi die wo.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Ama die Josefu aŋaŋ Meri die ka dʋagɩ taŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke die ʋ mɩɩrɩna ʋ bʋadembike. Josefu die dɩ yɩ wa saarɩ, dɩ Yisa.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Betilehemi, Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma, saŋka mi Herodi die yine naaŋ. Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die dɩ ka yʋasɩya, dembisi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ a keŋ Jerusalemi.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: a dɩ dɩa a pɩasa, Sɩa ba mɩɩrɩ bʋanyaalɩ dieke dɩ balla ʋ yi Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa? Tɩ ye wo ʋ chɩŋmarɩka dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ, tɩ dɩ a keŋ dɩ tɩ jɩaŋ wa.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Die naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ mana die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma ma sʋgɩtɩ dɩ chʋʋsɩ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Die ʋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba mana a lagɩŋ tamba a die a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Sɩa ba baa ba mɩɩrɩ gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Masia wa.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Die ba baarɩ, Betilehemi tɩŋ, Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma. Naa, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ maagɩ.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Fʋnɩŋ Betilehemi dɩ benne Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ka yi tɩgɩbiŋ dama fʋ sʋŋ naaŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ baa ʋ nyɩŋ a keŋ a dɩ n vuosisi dɩ benne Izara tɩŋgbaŋka ma nɩŋŋa. .

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma die naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wa chaamba die dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaakʋ a lɔbɩrɩ a juu juoŋ me a pɩasɩ ba saŋŋa dieke gbaŋ gbaŋ chɩŋmarɩkʋ die dɩ nyɩnna.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Womi die ʋ taaŋ ba dɩ ba ga Betilehemi aŋaŋ wɩaha gie: Nɩ gamma a kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a daansɩ a yaalɩ bʋa wa jigiŋ amʋ nɩ keŋ a ye ʋ benne jigidieke nɩ vaa n tɩbɩŋ wʋŋ amʋ n gbaŋ n ga a jɩaŋ wa.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Die ba nyɩnna mi a gara wa, sieŋ me die ba bɩ ye chɩŋmarɩka gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ pɔsɩna a nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaakʋ, die ba yene ke wo ba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ paalɩ fɩalɩ, ka dɩ ba nɩŋŋa a ga zie tigidieke me bʋa wa dɩ benne wo sikpeŋ.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Die ba ga a juu tigiri me a ye bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ Meri; die ba sʋʋŋ a gbirigi a jɩaŋ bʋa wa, a yuori ba bʋlɔga a vʋarɩ piini a yɩ wa; die a yiwo salɩma aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ba wasɩnana farankinsinsi aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ba wasɩnana miri.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba ba daansa ma sɩba ba da keŋ yiŋŋi a ga naaŋ Herodi jigiri, die ba nyɩŋ a daagɩ siegaaŋ a kuli ba tɩŋ.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Die sɩsɩbɩrɩba dɩ yiŋŋine a ga ta Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ Josefu jigiŋ ʋ daansa ma a die a balɩ wa, Herodi nan dɩ yaala bʋa wa dɩ ʋ kʋʋ wa. Naa chɩaŋ ma hagɩ a nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a chɩgɩ a ga Ijipiti a bemme mi a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan balɩ fʋ dɩ fʋ nyɩŋ.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Josefu die dɩ hagɩ yuku mi a nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a ga Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: Die ʋ bie wo mi dene a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke naaŋ Herodi die dɩ kpine. Naa die yiye mɩŋ amʋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vana ʋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ dɩ balɩ die wo keŋ yi wusie, a baarɩ dɩ, Die n vʋarɩ wa n bʋa Ijipiti me.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Die Herodi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ chaaŋ diekemba die dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩkɩrɩ die gaaŋ wa wa die ʋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ. Die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ ballɩnyaalɩ dembisi mana dɩ benne Betilehemi aŋaŋ ka tɩgɩbɩsɩ mana mana. Die ba kʋʋ ballɩ dembisi dieke die dɩ yine ballɩnyaalɩsɩ a ga tʋgɩ bɩna ale ballɩ dama sɩsɩbɩrɩba die baarɩ dɩ ba yene chɩŋmarɩka ka bɩna ale wʋnna.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Naa die yiye mɩŋ a yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jerimaya die dɩ balla wudiekemba wo dɩ yi wusie. Die ʋ baarɩ.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Rama tɩŋ ma ba wʋŋ watɩ, kpaŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, ta Rachali dɩ kʋma ʋ ballɩ wɩa, ba halɩŋ wa a jɩɩ, dama ʋ ballɩ die kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ kenne a kpi, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ Josefu jigiŋ ʋ daansa ma ma Ijipiti ma.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: a baarɩ, Hagɩ nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a yiŋŋi a ga Izara tɩŋgbaŋ ma, dama vuodieke die dɩ yaalala ba kʋʋ wa wa kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma Josefu die dɩ hagɩ a nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a yiŋŋi a ga Izara tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Ama die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Akelusi die zene ʋ chɔɔŋ Herodi naŋ ma a dii Judia naarɩ, die ŋmaamɩŋ die yalla wa aŋaŋ ʋ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi a ga mi; ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ a dagɩ wa ʋ daansa sʋŋ dɩ ʋ ga Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Die ʋ ga kalɩ tɩŋgbaŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Nazeriti ma ma. Naa chɩaŋ ma die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ balla wudiekemba wo die dɩ keŋ a yi wusie, dɩ: Ba nan dɩ wasa wa Nazeriti vuoŋ.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Saŋka mi, Jɔɔn, vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die bie wo haagɩŋ ma Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma a mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a balla.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: dɩ Nɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ diile saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die wɔŋ maagɩ Jɔɔn wa wɩaha mɩŋ dɩ, Vuoŋ benne haagɩŋ ma a keese a mʋʋla Nɩ wʋnsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ sieti ta vaa a tʋgɩsɩ a yɩ wa. .

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Jɔɔn die yeegi wo nyɔgɩnɩ kobiti jayeekiŋ ta bɩ bɔbɩ gbanɩŋ ʋ chɩaŋ ma ta ʋ nyindiike die dɩ yi sankpara aŋaŋ siesi.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Saŋka mi vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Judia aŋaŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ gelegele a gara ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a juu haarɩkʋ a yiŋŋi a jʋalɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo a kuli ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ ma.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: a balla ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ta ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii daa wʋnyɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine; ta ʋ dʋa kalɩŋŋa ma a yallɩ wa a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die Yisa die dɩ yene ba paala a yallɩ yada dieke wo, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ yʋagɩtieŋ wo, N bʋa, dii sikimiŋ; n nagɩ fʋ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa fʋ.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Ama die ʋ kenne ye ta Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi, vuosi pam gbaŋ die dɩ kienene ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuukehe gie! Mɩnɩa kpaana nɩ dɩ nɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩ dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ nɩ ma wa?.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ yile ba sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ Daa wa gie bala sɩba ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Nɩ yime wudiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan dagɩ sɩba nɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩtɩ ma.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile die a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yile sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ die?.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ta nɩ da nɩgɩma nɩ nyʋʋŋ a baarɩ nɩ yiwo Abarahami haagɩsɩ, nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi tanaha gie a bɩrɩŋ Abarahami haagɩsɩ.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: A jabɩa yine mɔlɩ Fʋ baaŋ nan balɩ a yɩ wa, n nagɩ fʋ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa fʋ yaa fʋ baaŋ nan balɩ Hagɩ a chʋŋ?.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Lɩaka wɔŋ yi siri mɩŋ a gbarɩ dɩ ka chɩa tɩɩsɩsɩ; tɩɩ dieke dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna ba nan chɩa ka a taaŋ a nagɩ yi boliŋ me.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa ma ma a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba. Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ yʋagɩtieŋ wo, Hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a kulime.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Manɩŋ n sɩa wa aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, ka dagɩ sɩba nɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma wa, ama vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan keŋ n kʋaka tɩaŋ wa manɩŋ; n ka tʋgɩ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan chii ʋ nɩɩra gbaŋ: wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ ʋ baaŋ nan nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ aŋaŋ boliŋ a sɩɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Daa wa die dɩ hagɩ a kuli tigiŋ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ʋ yallɩ wa ʋ kpalɩchaarɩkɩŋ dɩ ʋ chaarɩ zaakpeeti mana mana a nyɩŋ zaaha ma, ʋ nan lagɩsɩ zaaha mana a yi bumbuŋ me ta nagɩ zaakpeetiti a yi boli dieke dɩ kana ka kpise me.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yene die wo, die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be, ta die bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ nagɩna yikoke mi dɩ sɩna naa a yɩ vuota.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Galili a keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma dɩ ʋ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna mi a chʋŋ gara die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ die ba wasɩnana Matiwo, ta ʋ kalɩ ʋ tʋʋma jigiŋ lampotuosiriŋ juoŋ me. Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa Hagɩ a dɩa mɩŋ. Die ʋ hagɩ dɩ wa.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Ama ta Jɔɔn die dɩ ka saagɩya ta balɩ wa, Manɩŋ n tɩnna mʋ n keŋ fʋ jigiŋ aŋ fʋ sɩɩ mɩŋ, ama ta fʋnɩŋ fʋ kieŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Yisa die dɩ gana Matiwo tigiri a ga die nyindiikehe, mi lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka dɩ Juu vuosi mɩraha die dɩ keŋ a gʋtɩ Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ma ma a die aŋaŋ ba.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Ama ta die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Vaa ka yi die lele, dama dɩ mʋ tɩ yi tʋnvɩɩna mana mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala tɩ yi wo. Jɔɔn die dɩ wa saagɩ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ yene die wo die ba pɩasɩ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Bɩa yine nɩ dɩdagɩrʋ wa dɩ die aŋaŋ vuosi gie.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Die ʋ dene a sɩɩ Yisa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ a kpatɩ wa, lele womi Yisa dɩ dene nyɩŋ nyaabʋ ma, ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ die dɩ yuori die ʋ ye Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ dɩ kenne sʋʋna ʋ sikpeŋ sɩba ŋmarɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋŋ ba pɩasɩkʋ, a die a yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla alaafɩa ka yaala gbɩgbaantʋ, sie vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Womi lɔlɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ, N Bʋachotiŋ wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ n nine dɩ suuli.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Nɩ gamma Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma a daansɩ ye wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ dɩ, Zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩŋ maŋ yaala daa dɔŋɩsɩ kaabɩŋ. N ka keŋye dɩ n wa sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ ama n keŋ dɩ n wa wa tʋmbɩatɩtieliŋ.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Womi Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa yine tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ saŋŋa mana ama ta fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ka wɔŋ a bɔba.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ tɩŋ yaala chaaŋ diekemba dɩ gana hɔgʋ faarɩŋ bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa dieke ba ŋaaŋ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a beri wo? Die kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi. Ama saŋŋa nan keŋ ba nan nagɩ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ, saŋka mi ba nan wa bɔbɩ nʋaŋ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Vuoŋ kaaŋ saagɩ a nagɩ garɩhaalɩŋ garɩchɔɔtɩŋ a baalɩ a ligi garɩkʋʋŋ vɔrɩŋ, dama garɩhaalɩkʋ gie nan chɩɩrɩ ta vaa garɩkʋʋrɩ, aŋ ka vɔrɩkʋ wa a faasɩ a dalɩma.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Yaa nɩ yeye ta vuoŋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ a suu gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋkʋra ma? Dama ʋ yi die, daabʋ nan kʋŋ a chɩɩrɩ kolititi aŋ daabʋ aŋaŋ kolititi mana taaŋ yɔrɩ. Ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa daahaalɩŋ a suu gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋhaala aŋ a mana vɩɩŋma.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yisa die dɩ balala naa ta die Juu vuosi jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, Lele n havʋʋbiŋ dɩ kpi ama keŋ a nagɩ fʋ nuuŋ a dɩɩsɩ ʋ sikpeŋ aŋ ʋ hagɩ.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Naa wɩa die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ hagɩ a dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ zɩŋ die dɩ nyɩna ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma dee bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale; die dɩ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ a die a gbi Yisa jayeekiri kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Die ʋ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma, Maŋ gbi ʋ jayeekiri, n nan ye gbaanɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a ye wo ta baarɩ, N lɩa, vaa fʋ sʋŋ dʋa, fʋ yada gbaanna fʋ. Saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ hɔgʋ wa die dɩ ye alaafɩa.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Ta die Yisa die dɩ wana a ga jakʋʋrɩ tigiri, a ye hanye chɩarɩba aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnnana kumbu.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: die ʋ baarɩ Vuoŋ mana tɩaŋ a nyɩŋ. Havʋʋbike ka kpiye, ʋ ta ko a gʋʋra mɩŋ. Die vuosi mana die dɩ dɔŋ laatɩ a laa wa.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Die daadamba dɩ wana a nyɩŋ yenni me wo, Yisa die dɩ ga a juu havʋʋbike dɩ dʋana jigidieke a yigi ʋ nuuŋ me die ʋ hagɩ a zie.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Die ba mʋʋlɩ wɩɩrɩ gie tɩŋgbaŋka ma mana.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna mi a chʋŋ gara dembisi bale die dɩ yine yɩɩsɩ die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a nata a bala dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ gana a ga a juu juoŋ kaanɩ yɩɩsɩ bale wo die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, Nɩ tuo a dii dɩ n nan bɩagɩ yi naa? Ta ba baarɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, wa.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Die wɩa ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a gbi gbi ba nine ta baarɩ, Nɩ tuone a dii die wo ka yi a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Die ba ninehe die dɩ yuori. Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ, Nɩ da keŋ a balɩ a yɩ vuoŋ.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Ama die ba nyɩŋ a mʋʋlɩ Yisa wɩa tɩka mana ma.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Die ba nyɩnnana, vuosi bataŋ die dɩ yaa daa wʋnyɩ die jɩmbɩaŋ die dɩ yalla wa ʋ ka bɩagɩ bala wɩa a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Yisa die dɩ yagɩ jɩmbɩakʋ a nyɩŋ daa wa ma, die ʋ wa bɩagɩ bala wɩa. Die dɩ paalɩ a yi vuoŋ mana mana mamachi ba baarɩ, Tɩ ye ka ye mamachi ka gie chanchaaŋ Izara vuosi tɩŋ ma ma.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ama ta die Farasisi mba die dɩ baarɩ, Jɩmbɩatɩtɩ naaŋ wa yɩnana wa hagɩrɩbʋ ʋ bɩagɩ yaga jɩmbɩatɩ.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Yisa die dɩ dɩa ga tɩgɩkpɩɩma aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ mana mana, a die a ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a daga Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋvɩɩna a yɩa vuosi ta gbaama yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Die ʋ yene kpɩkpaakʋ, zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo, dama die ba yiwo zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ta wo susuŋŋiŋ mana ta nɩasɩ yiise dɩ wone yiyagɩrʋ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, Zaaha bieye mɩŋ ta dala ama zaagobiribe ka dala.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Die wɩa nɩ jʋʋsɩ zaaha tieŋ aŋ ʋ gʋtɩ tʋntʋntɩŋ aŋ ba keŋ gobihe.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Nɩ da keŋ a dii nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩya, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ da keŋ dii nɩ sarɩya.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: dama nɩ dine nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩyaha die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan dii nɩ sarɩya; ta nɩ magɩsɩna die a yɩa nɩ chanchaalɩŋ die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan magɩsɩ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Bɩa yine fʋ daansa yese mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ chanchaaŋ nimbiŋ me, ta a ka yese dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Baa fʋ yiwo lalɩa a balɩ a yɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa, Ko vaa n vʋarɩ mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ nimbiŋ me ta daaŋ dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me wɩa?.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Fʋnɩŋ gɩgaantʋ wa gie, woliŋ a vʋarɩ dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ ninehe me wo amʋ fʋ mɩŋŋɩ a yesime vɩɩnɩŋ a vʋarɩ mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ nɩmbʋa wa nimbiŋ me wo.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Da nagɩ jadieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ tʋaŋ a yɩ gbaasɩ, dama a baa a yiŋŋi a dʋŋ fʋ mɩŋ; da keŋ a nagɩ jadieke dɩ yalla nyʋarɩ a yɩ perukusi, dama a nan tɩbɩtɩbɩha aŋaŋ tantɩ.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta ʋ nan nagɩ a yɩ nɩ; nɩ daansɩma ta nɩ nan ye; nɩ kpaasɩma gbɩabɩkʋ ta ʋ nan yuori a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Womi die Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ die dɩ nagɩ Yisa a yallɩ wa a ga haagɩŋ ma dɩ Sitaani ga magɩsɩ wa.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩŋ tuose mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ yaalɩnana yese mɩŋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kpaasɩnana gbɩabɩkʋ ka yuore a yɩa wa mɩŋ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Die ʋ bie wo mi a ka dii jaaŋ mana ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yugiti baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa ta die kɔŋ die dɩ wa a yallɩ wa.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ chʋalɩba, nɩ mɩnɩa ʋ bʋa dɩ nan jʋʋsɩ wa nyindiike aŋ ʋ nagɩ taŋ a yɩ wa?.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Ta die Sitaani die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, vaa tanaha gie bɩrɩŋ nyindiike.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Yaa nɩ mɩnɩa ʋ bʋa nan jʋʋsɩ wa zaasɩŋ aŋ ʋ nagɩ javʋʋkɩŋ a yɩ wa?.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Ama ta die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ baarɩ vuota kaaŋ bɩagɩ a beri nyindiike nyɩɩna ma wɩa, ama wudieke mana mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balala.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie dɩ sɩba nɩ nagɩ nyɩnvɩɩna a yɩ nɩ ballɩ, die nɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma nan faasɩ yɩ vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩ wa nyɩnvɩɩna.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Womi Sitaani die dɩ yaa Yisa a ga Jerusalemi tɩgɩkasɩ ma ma a yallɩ wa a ga a zieŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sikpeŋ.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Yime fʋ chanchaalɩŋ wudieke fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yaala ba yime a yɩma fʋ; dama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩkʋ dɩ dagɩna die wonde.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: ta die a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ seŋ a yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa fʋ nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ baarɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa ʋ malakasisi fʋ wɩa, aŋ ba nagɩ ba nuusi a tʋgɩ a tuo fʋ amʋ halɩ fʋ naatalɩ gbaŋ kaaŋ gbi tanaha. .

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Nɩ jume sanʋabike dama sanʋa dieke dɩ gana a ga a juu boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩ ma wa tɩɩlɩ mɩŋ, ta ka sieŋ dɩ bɩ vɩɩna aŋaŋ chɔnɩŋ; ta vuosi pam dɩ daagɩ mi juo.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Ama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ bɩ a baarɩ, Vuoŋ da a keŋ magɩsɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ. .

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Ama miivoli sanʋakʋ yiwo jabɩŋ, ta ka sieku dɩaŋ dɩ tʋa aŋaŋ chɔnɩŋ; ta vuosi bɩta juunene mi.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Saŋka mi naaŋ Herodi vuodieke die dɩ yine Galili naaŋ wa die dɩ wʋŋ Yisa wɩa.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Womi Sitaani wa die dɩ bɩ a chii Yisa a yallɩ wa a ga zieŋ taŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ sikpeŋ a dagɩ Yisa dʋnɩa tɩgɩsɩ mana mana aŋaŋ ka nyinti mana ta balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Nɩ sɩmma ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba wɩa, ba kieŋ nɩ jigiŋ sɩba yiise, ama ba yiwo haaŋgbaasɩ wusie.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, Ʋ seŋ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ hagɩna kuŋ me, die wɩa ʋ yallɩ hagɩrɩbʋ mi a yie mamachi wɩa dene.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩna kunkogiri me a keŋ sʋʋŋ, daadaŋ pam die dɩ dɩ wa.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: N nan nagɩ nyinti gie mana a yɩ fʋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ keŋ a sʋʋŋ a gbirigi a jɩaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Ba tʋʋma nan yi nɩ sɩmma ba; ba yese jɩa haŋgɔɔsɩ me a kpaa? Yaa kɩnkama ngbere jigiŋ a kpaa?.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Womi Farasisi bataŋ aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩŋ Jerusalemi a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Die Herodi die yɩ wa nʋaŋ ba yigi Jɔɔn a bɔbɩ wa ta nagɩ wa a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ, die ʋ nɩmbʋa Filipi hɔgʋ Herodiasi wɩa die ʋ yi die.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Womi gamɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbigiri ʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ yaala nan bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Sitaani, gamma ta va mɩŋ. Ka maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ a baarɩ Vuota jɩama ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma a bɩ dɩa ʋ nyɩɩna ma. .

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Tɩɩvɩɩna mana nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna, ama tɩɩ dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ mana ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ wa nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩbɩatɩ.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Dɩ yiwo lalɩa fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka dɩɩsa tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ dagɩna die wo? Ba ka nɩta ba nuusi ta die.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɩ da keŋ suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieliŋ dɩ vuosi ye nɩ, dama nɩ keŋ a yi die nɩ kaaŋ ye nyʋarɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Dama Jɔɔn die ŋaaŋ bala yɩa wa mɩŋ dɩ, Dɩ ka mʋ fʋ faarɩ Herodiasi.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Yisa die dɩ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ daa wa ta balɩ dɩ, N saagɩya, fʋ gamɩŋ kpatɩya mɩŋ! Lele womi ta daa wa dɩ ye alaafɩa.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Die wɩa Sitaani die dɩ ga ta die a va wa, ta malakasi die dɩ keŋ a suŋŋi wo.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Tɩɩvɩɩnɩŋ kaaŋ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩbɩatɩ; ta tɩɩ dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ dɩaŋ kaaŋ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩvɩɩna.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yisa die dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ a yɩ ba yiko dɩ ba vʋarɩma jɩmbɩatɩ ta gbaama yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna yʋagɩbɩatɩ mana mana.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba, Ta dɩ yiwo lalɩa nɩnɩŋ nɩ zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ta dɩ nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba dagɩkʋ?.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Die wɩa, fʋ keŋ yaala fʋ nagɩ piini a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieŋ, da keŋ mʋʋlɩ ka sɩba gɩgaantɩba dɩ ŋaana yie die wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me aŋaŋ tɩgɩsɩ sʋŋ dɩ vuota ye ba tʋnvɩɩna ta bɩrɩ ba wa. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ba wɔŋ ye ba nyʋarɩ mana mɩŋ.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Naaŋ Herodi die yaala ʋ kʋʋ Jɔɔn, ta die chɩga Juu vuosisi, dama die ba sɩba a baarɩ Jɔɔn die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa, Wʋmma, sɩmma fʋ keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ wɩaha gie. Ama gamma a ga nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ wa a vaa ʋ jɩɩŋ fʋ aŋ fʋ kaabɩ kaabɩ dieke Mosisi die dɩ balla dɩ nɩ kaabɩ wa amʋ vuoŋ mana sɩmma a baarɩ ye alaafɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ wʋnna dɩ ba yigi Jɔɔn a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ, die ʋ nyɩŋ a ga Galili me.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Tɩɩ dieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ kana a ka nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩvɩɩna ba nan chɩaka a taaŋ boliŋ me.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: Ʋ tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo saara wʋnna; Simoni, aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋabiŋ Andurusi; aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋabiŋ Jɔɔn, Zebedi ballɩ;.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Die dɩ yine daraa ale kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ chʋŋ tɩanna zaa kʋaŋ ma davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ; ta kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, die wɩa die ba piili a gobe zaaha yɩaga ŋɔba.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die baarɩ, Yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma ta Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ zɩa ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ mʋ ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: ama fʋ a keŋ yɩ zɔɔlɩntieŋ piini da keŋ vaa fʋ zʋa vɩɩnɩŋ gbaŋ sɩmma ka wɩa.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene daadamba die ʋ jʋalɩ a kalɩ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Die ba mɩɩrɩna naaŋ Herodi daa dieke die dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, ʋ yi chaantɩ, ta Herodiasi hɔgʋlɩa die dɩ keŋ sie daadamba mana nɩŋŋa, die naaŋ Herodi sʋŋ die dɩ paalɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ gana a ga juu Kapenɔmi, die Romanisi sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa ta a baarɩ.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ tigiri me a ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaaŋ a sʋʋŋ kalɩ mi dɩ ʋ dagɩ.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: Die ʋ tʋgɩ Nazeriti tɩŋ ama die ʋ ka yʋasɩya mi ta die tɩaŋ ga Kapenɔmi tɩgɩdieke die dɩ gbigine mʋgɩkpɩɩŋ kaanɩ, Zebuluni aŋaŋ Nafitali tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma nɩ nan dɩ sɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba ba tʋʋma ma.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Batolomiwo; aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ Matiwo, vuodieke die dɩ yine lampotuosiru wo; aŋaŋ Jemisi vuodieke dɩ yine Alifusi bʋa aŋaŋ Tadeusi.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ yene die wo ba balɩ a yɩ Yisa, Daansɩ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yinene wudieke tɩ mɩraha dɩ kana ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ tɩ yime davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Ama nɩ daga a baarɩ dɩ vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a yallɩ jadieke ʋ baaŋ nan nagɩ a suŋŋi ʋ nuŋ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ ta baarɩ dɩ Nyinti gie bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ;.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Amʋ ka yi wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ ta fʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yesinene lɔbɩrɩŋ ma nan tuŋ fʋ .

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: die ʋ piili a daga ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a baarɩ:.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: die ʋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ a baarɩ, N hʋʋya, n nan yɩ fʋ jadieke mana fʋ bala dɩ yaala.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: Jakʋʋŋ, n tʋntʋntʋ wʋnyɩ paalɩna a yʋagɩ; ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ kpi, ta ʋ die wahala pam, ta dʋa juoŋ me.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Ta daadamba die dɩ geline wo die faasɩ dala mɩŋ, die wɩa die ʋ ga juu haarɩŋ a kalɩ ka sʋŋ, ta die daadamba die dɩ zie mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ kʋanʋarɩ ma.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Naa die yine amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ balla wudieke wo keŋ a yi wusie.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Da vuodieke mana dɩ wasɩnana mɩŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, balla ʋ juu arɩzanna naarɩ ma, sie vuodiekemba nyɩɩna dɩ yinene n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa choti.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: aŋaŋ Simoni vuodieke dɩ yaala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yalla ba gbaŋ aŋaŋ Judasi Asikaroti, vuodieke die dɩ bala ʋ posi Yisa chɩaŋ.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ Davidi dɩ yine wudieke saŋŋa dieke kɔŋ dɩ yalla wa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋanvuosisi?.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: ka ka bɩ yiwo mugisiŋ dɩ ʋ yɩma ba jɩlɩma ta yɩ ba jaaŋ. Naa nɩ zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ta dɩ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ dagɩŋ.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Fʋ keŋ yaala fʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, da a keŋ a yi sɩba gɩgaantɩba dɩ yinene die wo, dama banɩŋ ba cho dɩ ba zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me aŋaŋ siekpara ma tɩka sʋŋ a jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vuosi ye be. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ ba wɔŋ ye ba tune mana mɩŋ.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bemme vuodiekemba dɩ sɩbɩna dɩ ba yiwo zɔɔlɩntieliŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Die wɩa hɔgʋlɩa wa nuŋ dɩ dagɩ wa jadieke ʋ baaŋ pɩasɩ, die ʋ yiŋŋi ga balɩ naaŋ Herodi dɩ, Gobi Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a yi tasɩŋ ma a keŋ yɩ mɩŋ lele.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, N nan ga a ga gbaaŋ wa.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ wa nandaga a balɩ ba wɩa pam dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die hagɩna daaŋ kaanɩ a ga dɩ ʋ bʋrɩ ʋ nyɩŋbʋra.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Zebuluni tɩŋ aŋaŋ Nafitali tɩgɩsɩ dɩ benne Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ mi wo chaaŋ ta bɩ gbigi mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ: Galili tɩŋ dieke vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ kala mi;.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Sarɩya diile daarɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, daadaaŋ pam nan ballɩ a yɩ mɩŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, die fʋ saaŋ ma tɩ balla Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, die fʋ saaŋ ma die tɩ yaga jɩmbɩatɩtɩ vuosi ma, die fʋ saaŋ ma die tɩ yie mamachi tʋʋma.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo ta kpaaŋ ba, Nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ a ga vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi buuriŋ yaa nɩ ga juu Samaria vuosi tɩŋ ma.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Die ʋ ga a juu wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋanvuosisi dɩ nagɩ paanʋ dieke die ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ a ŋɔbɩ wudieke die Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ kana ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ vuogaaŋ ŋɔbɩ, die paanʋ sie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋra nyɩɩna.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie! Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die yaa wusie die ʋ bala nɩ wɩa die. Die Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Ama fʋ keŋ a baa fʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, juu fʋ juoŋ me ta ligi sanʋarɩ, ta jʋʋsɩ fʋ chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ benne lɔbɩrɩŋ ma, aŋ fʋ Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yene wudieke fʋ yine lɔbɩrɩŋ ma wa tuŋ fʋ .

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnnana wa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ nan halɩŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Naaŋ wa sʋŋ die dɩ chʋʋsɩ, ama die ʋ hʋʋna daadamba nɩŋŋa wɩa die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yi havʋʋbike dɩ yaalala die wo.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ, Aayɩ, n Yɔmʋtieŋ, n ka tʋgɩ vuodieke fʋ mʋna fʋ keŋ n tigiŋ, ama fʋnɩŋ ko yɩ nʋaŋ aŋ ʋ ye alaafɩa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Die ʋ bʋrɩnana nyɩŋbʋrɩkaha kʋaŋ ma wa, ataŋ die dɩ nan sieŋ me, nembisi die dɩ keŋ a dii he.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: vuodiekemba dɩ benne lɩmɩŋ ma nan ye chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ die dɩ benne kuŋ lɩmɩŋ ma, bolibu nan chaaŋ a yi ba sikpigile me.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Saŋka mi n nan yiŋŋi balɩ ba, N ka sɩba nɩ, nɩnɩŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ vuosi gie, nɩ gamma.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ama nɩ gamma Izara vuosi jigiŋ nyɩɩna, ba sɩna sɩba yii diekemba dɩ bana.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Yaa nɩ ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha gbaŋkʋ ma, dɩ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma ŋaaŋ tʋma tʋʋma mi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana a chʋsa mɩraha ama ta wo chʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Vuosi gie yɩa mɩŋ jɩlɩma aŋaŋ ba nʋa, ama ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ bie saasaa aŋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Fʋ a keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ jʋʋsɩma aŋaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ wagɩtɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ jɩannana bugile; banɩŋ ba yile sɩba ba keŋ jʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ wagɩtɩ ba bugilehe nan wʋŋ ba.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ sʋʋna ba gbaŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ dama dʋnɩa mana nan yi ba sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Die wɩa die ba gobi Jɔɔn sikpeŋ dansarika sʋŋ.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Dama manɩŋ n gbaŋ n yaa wa n nyɩŋkʋra, ta yallɩ sojasi ba dɩɩ n kʋaŋ. N ŋaaŋ balɩ a yɩ geŋ wo, Gamma aŋ ʋ ga, ta balɩ a yɩ geŋ wo Kiere aŋ ʋ keŋ; ta bɩ vaa n yɔmʋ yi wudieke n yaalala.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan taŋ sikpeŋ jigidieke tantɩ die dɩ kana ka faasɩ dala. Die nyɩŋbʋrɩkaha die dɩ nyʋʋŋ lagɩ lagɩ dama tantɩ die ka faasɩ dala wa wɩa.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Saŋka mi yaa gamma die Yisa dɩ piili a balla Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a baarɩ, Nɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ diile saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnna manɩŋ n wɩaha gie ta dɩɩsaha nɩasɩ wa dembiyɩantieŋ dieke dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ a zieŋ taŋ sikpeŋ me wo.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Nɩ ganana wa nɩ balɩma Ŋmɩŋ wɩarɩ gie; Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ diile saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Ama n bala nɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ benne giena.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Ba jɩama mɩŋ wa yɔrɩ dama ba daga vuosi vuota mɩra sɩba a yiwo manɩŋ n mɩra. .

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Nɩ da keŋ a sɩɩ sɩba banɩŋ, dama nɩ chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɔŋ sɩba jadieke nɩ yaalala ta nɩ ye ka jʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Vuodieke nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩ ta ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ yaala ba yi Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan yi ba sɩɩtɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ ba ba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ ŋmɩntɩna jadieke wɩa.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Die ba chii sikpeku aŋaŋ tasɩŋ a keŋ yɩ havʋʋbike, ta ʋ dɩaŋ dɩ chiike a ga yɩ ʋ nuŋ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋnna naa die dɩ yi wo mamachi, die ʋ ballɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba die dɩna ʋ kʋaka, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, n ye ka ye vuodieke dɩ yine mɩŋ yadaka gie chanchaaŋ nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi me gbaŋ.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: ama die ŋmɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ a faasɩ tʋa a mana die dɩ ko dama a dagɩrɩsɩ die ka sʋʋŋya die wɩa a mana die dɩ kpi.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ chʋnnana Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa, die ba yiwo Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Andurusi, ta ba taana nɩɩŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma dama die ba yiwo zaasɩyigiriŋ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Nɩɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a nɩɩ, mʋga dɩ kpaa, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ faasɩ a nɩgɩ tigiri, ama tigiri die ka nanya, dama die ka zene taŋ ma wa chɩaŋ wɩa.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Nɩ gbaama vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna, ta nɩ vaa kunti hagɩ kuŋ me, ta gbaaŋ vuodiekemba gamɩŋ yʋagɩŋ dɩ yalla ba, ta yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma. Nɩ tuo wo yɔrɩ die wɩa nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nagɩ a yɩ yɔrɩ.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ yaala zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ nɩ kaaba. Nɩ tɩŋ sɩba wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ, nɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ galɩma vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka chʋʋsɩya;.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ wa kpɩkpaakʋ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a bala yɩ ba, Nɩ wʋmma ta sɩmma a chɩasɩ!.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Naa wana mʋ nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ: Tɩ chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma: Vaa aŋ ba bɩrɩ fʋ saaŋ kasɩka;.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana ba chanchaalɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama banɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ a nagɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka a ga guu, ta die a ga a balɩ Yisa.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: N bala nɩ, daadaŋ pam nan nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩkɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋ juukiŋ a keŋ ta banɩŋ aŋaŋ Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu nan lagɩŋ a kalɩ a die nyindiike arɩzanna ma ma;.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Ta ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma, ta a mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ nyʋʋŋ a bɩrɩŋ, ama haŋgɔɔsɩsɩ die dɩ nyagɩ nyɩŋbʋraha.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ keŋ a dɩa mɩŋ aŋ n vaa nɩ sʋgɩma vuotaŋ a kiere Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Ama vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana manɩŋ n wɩaha gie ta ka dɩɩsaha nɩasɩ wa dembigaamʋ dieke dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ a zieŋ tambʋsɩŋ sikpeku.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Nɩ da a keŋ nagɩ ligire. Nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ a nagɩ kobu gbaŋ a su nɩ ligire bʋlɔga ma;.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa yine davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ tieŋ.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Da jadieke dɩ junene vuota nʋaŋ ma yinene wo dɩgɩntɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ ama jadieke dɩ nyɩnana ʋ nʋaŋ ma yinene wo dɩgɩntɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Vaa aŋ fʋ naarɩ keŋ; vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie me sɩba ka sɩna die arɩzanna ma wa.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ dama ba nan daansɩ a ye Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Die Yisa dɩ wʋnna Jɔɔn kʋʋla wɩaha die ʋ juu haarɩŋ a nyɩŋ mi a ga jigidieke vuoŋ dɩ wone. Die vuosisi dɩ wʋnna naa, die ba va ba tɩgɩsɩ ta chʋŋ nagɩsɩ a dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a ga ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ama nɩnɩŋ die dɩ yine naarɩ vuosisi, ba nan nagɩ nɩ a vigi taaŋ yeŋ me lɩmɩŋ ma; mi nɩ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nɩ nyɩna.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ ma die a mɩŋŋɩ yi bie; ataŋ die yi bie kɔbɩga kɔbɩga ataŋ dɩaŋ baŋɩsɩ-yʋaba yʋaba ataŋ dɩaŋ baŋɩsɩ-taa taa.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Die ba va ba nɩɩtɩ lagɩ lagɩ ta die a dɩ wa.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Nɩɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a nɩɩ, mʋga dɩ kpaa, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ nɩgɩ tigiri, tigiri die dɩ nan a ŋmʋʋrɩ.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Nɩ da keŋ a nagɩ bʋlɔga a gamma nɩ sieku; yaa nɩ pɔgɩlɩ jayeeki gaaŋ a gʋtɩ jadieke nɩ yeegine ma wa, yaa nɩɩrɩ gaasɩ a gʋtɩ nyindiekemba nɩ suune womi, yaa nɩ pɔgɩlɩ daaŋgbanɩŋ. Dama dɩ mʋ aŋ tʋntʋntʋ dii ʋ tʋʋma jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yisa die nyɩŋ mi a ga juu ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ kaanɩ ma.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Womi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ sɩba a baarɩ Farasisi vuosisi dɩ wʋnna fʋ balɩkʋ ba jɩɩ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Yɩ tɩ jinne nyindiike dieke tɩ yaalala.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ gurinene waagɩŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama banɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ nan wa ba ʋ ballɩ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yisa die dɩ kenne nyɩnna haarɩkʋ ma ta ye daadamba zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo pam, die ʋ gbaaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna ba sʋŋ mana.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ Romanisisi sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ, Kulime ta ka nan yi sɩba fʋ yine yada dɩ ka nan yi die wo. Ta ʋ tʋntʋntʋ wa die dɩ seŋ ye gbaamɩŋ lele womi.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a kpatɩ ba dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ yalla tɩba ʋ wʋmma.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Die ʋ nyɩnna mi a ga nɩŋŋa bɩta, ʋ bɩ ye daa wʋnyɩ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa bɩbra, Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn, Zebedi ballɩ. Die banɩŋ aŋaŋ ba chɔɔŋ wa die benne ba haarɩkʋ ma a wʋnsa ba nɩɩtɩ, Yisa die dɩ wa ba, womi.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩna wɩaha gie a kpatɩ, die dɩ yi daadamba mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Nɩ a keŋ a tʋgɩ tɩgɩkpɩɩŋ yaa tɩgɩbiŋ, nɩ juu a yaalɩ vuodieke dɩ yaalala ʋ tuo nɩ aŋ nɩ bemme ʋ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan nyɩŋ mi.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ta daa wʋnyɩ die benne mi a yaa nuukpiikiŋ. Vuosi bataŋ die benne mi a yaala sieti dɩ ba ye Yisa chʋʋsɩŋ ta galɩŋ wa, die wɩa die ba pɩasɩ wa, Tɩ mɩraha yɩ sieŋ mɩŋ dɩ vuoŋ gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ʋ die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Tɩɩ dieke mana n Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩ kana ka suuye sie ba keŋ vʋʋka a taaŋ.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Nagɩ tɩ hamɩŋ a chaa tɩ, sɩba tɩ nagɩna vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnna tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba wa.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna Ŋmɩŋ ta ba mugisi be, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ gbige nannɩŋ mɩŋ ta giena dɩaŋ dɩ yi haagɩŋ; die wɩa vaa vuotamba aŋ ba ga tɩgɩbɩsɩsɩ a yaalɩ nyindiike a dii.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Die Yisa ga wa Piita tigiŋ, mi die ʋ ye Piita hanɩɩŋ hɔgʋ dɩ yʋagɩna a dʋa aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbaŋ tuuliŋ.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa wɩa fʋ naga nandaga bala yɩa ba.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: die ba va ba chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ haarɩkʋ lagɩ lagɩ ta die a dɩ wa.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: dama die ʋ dagɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ yalla yiko, ta ka dagɩ ba sɩba mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ dagɩnana die wo.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Nɩ a keŋ a juu tigidieke mana nɩ waasɩ ba vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ yallɩ yiisiŋ ka nan goliŋ me davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ, ʋ tɩŋ kaaŋ yigi ke a vʋarɩ ka a nyɩŋ goliku me?.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Die wɩa vaa ba aŋ ba ga dama ba sɩɩ sɩba yɩɩsɩ ta data taŋ. Yɩɩ dɩ a keŋ data ʋ chanchaaŋ sie ba mana nan guoliŋ me.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Da a keŋ vaa tɩ juu magɩsɩ dieke dɩ hagɩrɩna ma, ama ta vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ wʋbɩaŋ ma. .

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Vuosi dɩ keŋ a zɩa nɩ ta mugisi nɩ ta chɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩa nɩ ta bala wʋbɩatɩ yiri yiri a yɩa nɩ nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Ba ka mʋ ba ga. Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yɩ ba jaaŋ aŋ ba dii.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ gbɩ ʋ nuuŋ; ta nyɩŋgbaŋ tuulibu die dɩ kpatɩ, die ʋ hagɩ a yi ba chaantɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ nɩ sieŋ dɩ nɩ sɩmma wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ wɩa yaa gamma ʋ naarɩ wɩa ama banɩŋ ba nɩŋ ʋ ka yɩ ba sieŋ.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Yisa die dɩ dɩa Galili mana mana sʋŋ a daga ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone sʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ta die a gbaanna vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtɩ mana mana.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Tigiri mi vuosi dɩ a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo nɩ, nɩ vaa nɩ waasɩkʋ aŋaŋ nɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ wɩarɩ ka ma ama ba a keŋ a ka a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo nɩ, nɩ vaa nɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiŋŋi keŋ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ vuota yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ yiisiŋ mɩŋ, die wɩa tɩ mɩraha yɩ tɩ sieŋ dɩ tɩ suŋŋi vuoŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Nɩ keŋ nagɩ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba, nɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ nan nagɩ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Piita die yiŋŋi a baarɩ: Dagɩ tɩ nandagɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Ba keŋ a yi die wo nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam dama nɩ nyʋarɩ nan dɩ dala pam Ŋmɩŋ tigiri me; die gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba mugisi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Die ba baarɩ, Paanʋ golime anʋ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale nyɩɩna tɩ yallɩ giena.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi jɩŋmɩŋ, vuosi die dɩ chii daadaŋ pam die jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla ba a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ; die ʋ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ ba ma aŋaŋ jabalɩkɩŋ ta gbaaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ yʋagɩna.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yenene a gʋta aŋ ʋ faasɩ yallɩma pam, ama vuodieke dɩ wone, ʋ nan waarɩ bɩta dieke ʋ yalla gbaŋ.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Die ba mʋʋlɩ ʋ saaŋ Siria tɩŋgbaŋ mana mana ma ma. Naa chɩaŋ ma vuosi die dɩ chii vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtɩ mana mana a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ; jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla bataŋ, bataŋ die dɩ yallɩ kpeŋkpeŋsi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine ta die ʋ gbaaŋ ba mana.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Ta vuodieke mana dɩ ka tuo nɩ yaa dɩ ka wʋŋ nɩ wʋbalɩkaha nɩ kpaantɩ nɩ naatala tankɔlɩŋ a taaŋ mi saŋŋa dieke nɩ nyɩnnana kanɩŋ tigiri yaa tɩka ma.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ nuukpiikiri tieŋ dɩ, Tɩɩrɩ fʋ nuuke. Die ʋ tɩɩrɩ ka die ka mana dɩ tɩɩntɩ, a sɩɩ sɩba kaanɩ wa.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Ama nɩ ka nagɩ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba, die nɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ dɩaŋ kaaŋ nagɩ nɩ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ka sɩba n wʋbalɩka gie chɩasɩ?.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Nɩ sɩɩ sɩba yasɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me. Ama yasɩ dɩ a keŋ a chʋʋsɩ a waarɩ ka nansɩŋ, lalɩa ka bɩ baa ka yi a bɩ ye ka nansɩbʋ? Dɩ dagɩ sɩba ka wo nyʋarɩ; sie ba nagɩha a siti taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋ vuosi chʋŋ a ma.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ, Nɩ yaa ha a keŋ giena.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ balla wudieke wo keŋ a yi wusie dɩ, Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ tuo tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ tuuliŋ aŋaŋ tɩ yʋagɩtɩ a chii a ga.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Wudieke wɩa n nagɩnana nandaga a daga ba yiwo, ba daansa mɩŋ ama ba ka yese, ta chɩɩsa a wʋmma, ama ba ka sɩba a chɩasɩ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Daadaŋ pam die dɩ nyɩŋ Galili aŋaŋ Tɩgɩsɩ-baŋ aŋaŋ Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Judia aŋaŋ Jɔɔdani gaarɩ mi wo chaaŋ tɩgɩsɩ die dɩ keŋ a dɩ wa.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya, die tɩka vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ tɩaŋ Sodomi aŋaŋ Gomoraya vuosi tɩbɩdatɩkʋ.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Ama die Farasisi die dɩ nyɩŋ saŋ ba baaŋ kʋʋ Yisa dene.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Ta fʋ a keŋ a bɔbɩ nʋaŋ dɩaŋ, da keŋ chʋʋsɩ fʋ nine chaaŋ sɩba gɩgaantɩba dɩ yine die wo, dama banɩŋ ba chʋʋsɩ wa ba nine chaaŋ dɩ vuoŋ mana ye sɩba ba bɔbɩ wa nʋaŋ. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ ba wɔŋ ye wo ba nyʋarɩ.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ jadieke mana dɩ junene vuota nʋaŋ ma gara ʋ nyuuti ma ta daaga ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a nyɩna?.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie popoli; tɩŋ dieke ba mɩna a zieŋ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ ka bɩagɩ a lɔbɩra.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuotamba dɩ ba sʋʋŋ kala huutiti ma; ta die a nagɩ paanʋ golime anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta die a pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Die ʋ gbieri gbieri paanʋsɩsɩ a nagɩ ha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, die ba nagɩ ha a yɩ vuosisi.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yene dɩ kpɩkpaakʋ geliŋ wo wo die ʋ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nʋaŋ, dɩ ba tɩaŋ ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ balla wudieke wo keŋ yi wusie ba wɩɩrɩ ma. Die ʋ balɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ, Vuosisi gie nan seŋ wʋŋ, ama ba kaaŋ dɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ, ba nan seŋ daansɩ ama ba kaaŋ dɩ sɩba jadieke ba yene wo chɩasɩ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Nɩ wʋmma, n tʋma nɩ sɩba yii diekemba dɩ benne gbaanchʋra sʋnsʋŋ. Die wɩa nɩ yalla yɩaŋ sɩba nyinvuuke ta nɩ bemme sɔmm sɩba ŋmarɩsɩ.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Ama wudiekemba vuota dɩ balala ʋ nʋaŋ ma nyɩna ʋ sʋŋ, die yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ sɩbɩna ba saŋkʋ wɩaha die ʋ nyɩŋ mi, ta vuosi pam die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ. Die ʋ gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ mana.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Fʋ a keŋ a bɔbɩ nʋaŋ fʋ nɩɩtɩ fʋ nine chaaŋ ta chasɩ fʋ sikpeŋ zoosi.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
