local: Ta vuoŋ ka chɔgɩsa popoli ta naga kpalɩ a bubike ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ ka a saagɩ wa jaaŋ ma jigidieke ka baaŋ nan chaaŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne tigiri me.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Vuoŋ mana dɩ dii ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Die Yisa dɩ dagɩna wɩaha gie a yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a kpatɩ wa die ʋ nyɩŋ mi a ga tɩgɩ diekemba die dɩ gbigine mi a bala ta daga Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mi.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, N nan dɩ dɩ fʋ a ga jigidieke mana fʋ ganana.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Dama vuosi gie yɩaŋ ligiye mɩŋ ta ba tɩba dɩaŋ dɩ hagɩrɩ, ta ba bɩ ligi ba nine, dama ba ka yaala ba ye jaaŋ, ta ka bɩ yaala ba wʋŋ wɩɩŋ, ta ka yaala ba yɩaŋ yuori, ta ka bɩ yaala ba vaa ba wʋbɩatɩ aŋ n gbaaŋ ba.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Nɩ sɩmma nɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ vuotaŋ wɩa, dama ba nan yigi nɩ a yaa nɩ ga sarɩya diile vuosi jigiŋ, ta bɩ nɩgɩ nɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone sʋŋ.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Dama vuota sʋŋ ma sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ dɩ nyɩna, sɩba vuota kʋʋla, daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana yaalɩŋ, aŋaŋ gaarɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ aŋaŋ kʋagaarɩŋ.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: ta kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba da keŋ a balɩ a yɩ ba chanchaalɩŋ ʋ wɩa.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: amʋ vuosi kaaŋ mɩŋŋɩ a baarɩ bɔba nʋaŋ, ntaala fʋ Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma, vuodieke dɩ benne lɔbɩrɩŋ ma sɩmma. Amʋ fʋ Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ yesinene jadieke fʋ yinene lɔbɩrɩŋ ma a yɩa wa nan tuŋ fʋ .

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ bie gie; nɩ chaaŋ a yɩ vuoŋ mana, ta ba ye nɩ tʋnvɩɩna ta bɩrɩma nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ dine die yiwo dembisi tuse anʋ, ama die ba ka bɩɩsɩ hɔgʋba aŋaŋ ballɩ a gʋtɩ.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die bie wo dansarɩka sʋŋ, ta die a wʋŋ Yisa Masia tʋʋma wɩa; die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Gbaanchʋra yaa vɔrɩtɩ a juo, nembisi dɩaŋ dɩ yaa tʋʋsɩ a juo ama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wo jigidʋagɩsɩkɩŋ.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ nɩ yaa sikpeŋ nansɩŋ, dama nɩ nine yese mɩŋ ta nɩ tɩba dɩaŋ dɩ wʋmma wɩa.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Ba nan nagɩ nɩ a ga tɩka nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ naalɩba jige n saaŋ wɩa aŋ nɩ balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ banɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Genhe yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ; ama da nuudɩgɩntɩ diile yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ bala die wo keŋ yi wusie.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Da keŋ a yaalɩma nyinti a lɔbɩrɩma tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, jigidieke nyɩnyʋgɩka aŋaŋ tʋgɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan chʋʋsɩha; ta gaarɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ awɩ a juu a gaarɩ.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Nɩ da keŋ yilime sɩba n keŋ dɩ n kpisi Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩkʋ; n ka keŋ dɩ n kpisihe mɩŋ, ama n keŋye mɩŋ dɩ n saagɩha aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ vaa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ juu haarɩkʋ a garɩsɩ a ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ, ta wɩarɩ dɩ ʋ taaŋ vuosisi aŋ ba kuli.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo yaa tɩ chɩsɩma vuogaaŋ bɩbra.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Tɔ, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ keŋ baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, vaa n ye woliŋ kuli a ga a guu n chʋa.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Ama n balɩ nɩ wusie, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ chɩgɩnana Ŋmɩŋ pam tɩŋ yaala ba ye jadieke nɩ yene wo ama ba ka yeke; ta tɩŋ bɩ yaala ba tɩŋ wʋŋ wudieke nɩ wʋnna wa ama ba ka wʋŋ ka.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Ama ba keŋ yaa nɩ ga aŋ ba dii nɩ sarɩya nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ die yaa nɩ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi ba die; dama saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ nan yɩ nɩ wudieke nɩ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni tɩŋgbaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Nɩ ye, n bʋavʋarɩkɩŋ wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ maŋ faasɩ cho, ʋ ma n sʋŋ dɩ fɩalɩ. N nan nagɩ n Halɩkasɩka a yi ʋ ma, aŋ ʋ balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ba nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Ama lɔbɩrɩma fʋ nyinti arɩzanna ma, jigidieke nyɩnyʋgɩka aŋaŋ tʋgɩŋ dɩ kana ka bɩagɩ chʋʋsaha; ta gaarɩŋ dɩ ka bɩagɩ awɩa a juu a gaara;.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ wusie, tɩŋgbaŋka gie aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ nan tɩaŋ ga ama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha bɩta gbaŋ kaaŋ bɩa sie a mana keŋ yi kpatɩ.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Die ʋ taanna vuosisi a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ ga a jʋalɩ kunkogiŋ dɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Yuŋ die dɩ ga tʋgɩ ta ʋ bie mi ʋ nyɩɩna ma;.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩ yiŋŋi a ga balɩ Jɔɔn wudieke nɩ wʋnnana ta yese wudiekemba;.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, Dɩa mɩŋ, ta vaa kunti guu a gbaŋ gbaŋ a kunti.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Nɩ wʋmma ta bʋgɩrɩ bʋbʋrɩtʋ nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Dama wudiekemba nɩ bala a baa nɩ balɩ wa kaaŋ nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ nan dɩ bie nɩ sʋŋ a bala.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Die wɩa Keenani hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ benne mi chaakʋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a wa wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ! Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla n havʋʋbiŋ, dɩ a paalɩ a mugisi wo pam.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Ʋ kaaŋ dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ yaa a nata yaa a zie sieti me faasɩ bala wɩa pam pam.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: jigidieke fʋ nyinti dɩ benne, mi fʋ sʋŋ dɩaŋ dɩ beri.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma vuodieke dɩ a keŋ a yi a chʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha kaanɩ gbaŋ ta bɩ daga ʋ chanchaalɩba die wo ʋ nan wɩarɩ kʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ama vuodieke dɩ dɩ mɩraha ta mɩŋŋɩ dagɩ ʋ chanchaalɩŋ die, wʋnɩŋ ʋ baaŋ nan yi vuokpɩɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: saŋka gie ta haarɩkʋ die bie wo saasaa mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋnsʋŋ aŋaŋ sʋnsʋŋ nyapoŋiku yaa ka dɔma dama bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die nɩgɩnana ka.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: dɩ nɩ ye ta yɩɩsɩ nine dɩ yuorine, gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna gamɩŋ ye wo gbaamɩŋ, tɩkpaara tieŋ dɩ wʋmma, ta kunti dɩ hagɩna kuŋ me, ta zɔɔlɩntieliŋ dɩ wʋmma Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩnaha.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Yisa die dɩ ga a juu haarɩŋ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Vuodieke dɩ keŋ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋvɩɩnaha gie ta ka sɩba a chɩasɩ, Sitaani ŋaaŋ keŋ vʋarɩ wa wudieke dɩ juune ʋ sʋŋ ma; die yine nyɩŋbʋrɩ dieke dɩ nanna sieku me.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Saŋka mi vuosi nan nagɩ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nɩmballɩ a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba, chʋalɩŋ gbaŋ nan yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ a yɩ ba ballɩ. Ballɩ dɩaŋ nan yiŋŋi a wagɩ ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta nagɩ ba a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Ama Yisa die ka balɩ wɩɩŋ mana a yɩ wa. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ yagɩ wa; dama ʋ dɩ tɩ mɩŋ a yie watɩ.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Ʋ kaaŋ wɩɩ yɩbɩkʋʋkɩŋ dieke dɩ goorine a tɩɩntɩ yaa a kpisi popoli dieke dɩ kana a ka mɩŋŋɩ a die. Ntaala ʋ yi wusie sarɩya diile keŋ ye nyaŋŋɩŋ.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Fʋ nine yine fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ popoli. Die wɩa fʋ nine dɩ keŋ vɩɩna, fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana nan suuli aŋaŋ chaanɩŋ;.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Dama manɩŋ n balɩ nɩ, sie nɩ tʋnvɩɩna dala a tɩaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi tʋnvɩɩna, dɩ daa die, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Yaa gamma kparaaŋ kpaŋ saŋŋa die Yisa die dɩ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a keŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Ta sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka chɩɩlɩya mɩŋ ma.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya ta bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ piili a nɩga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma, nyaabʋ dɩ poŋŋi a juo haarɩkʋ ma a yalla bʋ suuli haarɩkʋ; ama saŋka mi die Yisa die gʋʋra mɩŋ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Nyɩŋbʋrɩ diekemba dɩ nanna tanjaalɩka sikpeku sɩɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ ŋaana a wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha ta tuohe lagɩ lagɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ;.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Ta vuosi mana nan dɩ haa nɩ, nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa. Ama vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a bɩagɩ a tuoli wɩaha a ga a tʋgɩ kpatɩkʋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Izara vuodiekemba dɩ sɩna sɩba yii diekemba dɩ bana, banɩŋ ba jigiŋ nyɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋŋ mɩŋ.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Ʋ saaŋ wɩa, vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi nan dɩ yallɩ tama.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: ama fʋ nine dɩ keŋ a ka vɩɩna, fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana nan dɩ bie lɩmɩŋ ma. Die wɩa chaaŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ ma ma dɩ keŋ yi lɩmɩŋ, lɩmɩŋkʋ nan dɩ dala pam.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Nɩ wʋŋya die ba balla kʋrɩŋ ma dɩ, Da a keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ, fʋ a keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ ba nan chii fʋ ga naalɩaŋ a dii fʋ sarɩya.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Die ba yene wo ʋ chʋnnana nyaabʋ sikpeku ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be. Die ba baarɩ, Kogiŋ wonde ta faasɩ keesi.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋine a gara wa, Yisa die dɩ piili a bala yɩa kpɩkpaakʋ Jɔɔn wɩa, Die nɩ nyɩnna a ga Jɔɔn jigiŋ haagɩrɩ ma wa, bɩa die nɩ tɩŋ a yaala nɩ ye? Die nɩ tɩŋ yile a baarɩ nɩ ye hʋʋtɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana?.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga sʋgɩrɩ wa a baarɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ, ta tɩ baa tɩ sʋʋŋ wa nyaabʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: ama wɩaha ka sʋʋna ba sʋgɩtɩ ma ta a yʋasa. Die wɩa wahala yaa mugisiŋ dɩ keŋ wɩaha wɩa, ba ŋaaŋ nagɩ wɩaha a taaŋ bʋnyɩ.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Ta ba keŋ mugisi nɩ tɩŋ kaanɩ ma, nɩ chɩgɩ a ga tɩgɩgaaŋ. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ kpatɩ nɩ tʋʋmaha Izara tɩgɩsɩ mana ma ta manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa keŋ.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Ta hɔgʋ wa die dɩ a keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, suŋŋi mɩŋ.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Womi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii daa wʋnyɩ dɩ yine yɩɩ aŋaŋ gaamʋ dama jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa; ta Yisa die dɩ gbaaŋ wa, die ʋ bɩagɩ a bala wɩa ta yese.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Vuobalɩmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama naalɩŋ bale. Ʋ nan haa wʋnyɩ ta chome wʋnyɩ, yaa ʋ nan yɩ wʋnyɩ jɩlɩma ta wʋnyɩ ʋ nine kaaŋ dɩ suuli. Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ligire.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Ama lele, nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yi vuosi , ba nan dii ʋ sarɩya, sarɩya daaŋ. Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a zɩa vuoŋ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ vuotɔkʋ gie ba nan chii fʋ ga naalɩaŋ a ga dii fʋ sarɩya. Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ a zɩa ʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ, Gaamʋka gie nan daansɩ bie wɩa ma, ta juu boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩ ma.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Ama Yisa dɩ balɩ wɩa a yɩ ba bʋnyɩ a baarɩ, Nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, wo manɩŋ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Bɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye? Daa dɩ yeegine nyiŋyeekivɩɩna? Vuodieke dɩ yeegine nyiŋyeekivɩɩna bie wo naalɩsɩ ma.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Dɩ bɩa ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ faasɩ a yallɩ nɩ die, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana ka dala. Womi die ʋ hagɩ a natɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ aŋaŋ nyaabʋ poŋŋiku dɩ a fɩalɩ, mi mana die dɩ seŋ a fɩalɩ sɔmm.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Nyɩŋbʋrɩ diekemba dɩ nanna haŋgɔɔsɩ jigiri sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, ama ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie wahala aŋaŋ nyinti yaalɩŋ nyagɩ wɩaha ʋ wʋnna wa ʋ sʋŋ ma, ta ka ka nyɩnna nyɩŋnyɩŋka.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Bʋbʋgɩrʋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ dɩdagɩrʋ; yɔmʋ dɩaŋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ tieŋ.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Dɩ ka mʋ dɩ vuoŋ nagɩ ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike a yɩ gbaasɩ.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Die dɩ yi daadamba mana mamachi aŋaŋ Yisa die dɩ yine die wo wɩa, die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Daa wa gie yiwo Naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ yaa.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma wɩa maŋ bala nɩ da vaa ka mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ jadieke nɩ baaŋ nan dii aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan nyuu a bemme nɩ miivoli me; nɩ da bɩ vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ, nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan yeegi jadieke. Mɩsɩ ka tɩaŋ nyindiike? Ta nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ka tɩaŋ nyiŋyeeke?.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke dɩ a keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ tigiŋ a yaala ʋ nagɩ piini a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta tɩɩnsɩ sɩba ʋ nɩmbʋa yaa wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Ta Piita dɩ baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yiwo fʋnɩŋ fʋ vaa aŋ n chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a haarɩ fʋ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ, bɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye? Die nɩ yile sɩba nɩ nan ye Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa? Wusie, n bala nɩ, nɩ ye vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Die dɩ yi be mamachi, ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Daa lalɩa wʋnna? Halɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋiŋ dɩ wʋmma yɩa wa.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ama nyɩŋbʋrɩ diekemba dɩaŋ die dɩ nanna tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩŋkʋ ma sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ wɩaha ta sɩba a chɩasɩ; ta nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka, a taŋ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga bataŋ dɩaŋ baŋɩsɩ-yʋaba yʋaba bataŋ dɩaŋ baŋɩsɩ-taa taa.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Die wɩa dɩ mʋ aŋ bʋbʋgɩrʋ sɩmma sɩba ʋ dɩdagɩrɩ wa; ta yɔmʋ dɩaŋ sɩɩ sɩba ʋ tieŋ wo. Ba a keŋ a wa tigiŋ tieŋ jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli, die nɩŋ lalɩa ba baa ba wa ʋ dembe?.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Die wɩa hɔgʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ ka yiwo wusie, ama gbaaŋ diekemba dɩ dʋana ba tieŋ nɩŋŋa ŋaaŋ nagɩ nyindiike chɔɔta dieke dɩ nannana ʋ nɩŋŋa a die.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Ama Farasisi die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ba baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli die yine daa wa gie yiko dɩ ʋ vʋarɩma jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Nɩ daansɩ a ye nembisi dɩ yʋgɩnana; a ka bʋra; a ka bɩ gobe ta nagɩ yie napooti me, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa daansɩnana ha. Nɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisi?.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: ʋ vaa ʋ piinike dʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku nɩŋŋa, ta yiŋŋi a ga ʋ nɩmbʋa wa jigiŋ ba wʋnsɩ wɩarɩ ta ʋ yiŋŋi a keŋ nagɩ ʋ piinike a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Yisa dɩ baa, Kiere. Naa chɩaŋ ma Piita die dɩ nyɩŋ haarɩkʋ ma a piili chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a gara ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Jɔɔn wɩa die ba maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma a baarɩ, N tʋntʋntʋ wʋnna n baa n tʋŋ wa mɩŋ aŋ ʋ woliŋ dɩa fʋ nɩŋŋa a ga yuori sieku a yɩ fʋ. .

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yisa die dɩ kenne tʋgɩ Gadara tɩŋ ma mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ, die dembisi bale jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla dɩ nyɩŋ jigidieke ba gunene vuosi a keŋ tuoli wo. Die ba wɩa paalɩ yaa wa ŋmaamɩŋ die wɩa vuoŋ die ka bɩagɩ daaga mi.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bɩ a taaŋ ba nandagɩŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bʋrɩna zaabʋrɩvɩɩna ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ da a keŋ chɩgɩma vuotaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ, dama jadieke mana dɩ lɔbɩrɩna lele nan daansɩ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ, ta wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ mana dɩaŋ nan nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, Yiwo hɔgʋ dieke yada dɩ dala! Fʋ yaalala jadieke wo nan yi a yɩ fʋ. Lele womi ʋ hɔgʋlɩa wa dɩ ye alaafɩa.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba yilinene wudieke ta yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Tɩŋgbaŋ dieke mana dɩ puonene ka gbaŋ a waga taŋ kaaŋ faasɩ a yʋasɩ. Tigidieke dɩaŋ yaa buuri dieke dɩ puonene ka gbaŋ a waga taŋ nan nan.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Nɩ jabɩa baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a gʋtɩ ʋ gbaŋ ʋ miivoli bɩta ʋ wuyilimugisike wɩa.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ yaa wɩɩŋ ta gara naalɩaŋ aŋ nɩ ga wʋnsɩ, kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ aŋ nɩ wʋnsɩ wɩarɩ nɩ yene bie sieŋ me; dɩ daa die ʋ nan yaa fʋ a ga sarɩyadiiru wo jigiŋ aŋ ʋ dɩaŋ ʋ nagɩ fʋ a yi dansarɩka vuosi dɩdaansɩrɩba nuusi me aŋ ba nagɩ fʋ yi dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Ama die ʋ yene bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ dɩ faasɩna nɩgɩ wa ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo die ʋ piili a sʋʋna nyaabʋ chɩaŋ. Die ʋ keesi a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ gbatɩ mɩŋ taaŋ.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Ta Yisa dɩ bɩ a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ ba ye ka mɩɩrɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ama vuodieke dɩ yine vuobiŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma tɩaŋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Womi bʋnyɩ die ba faasɩ keese, Fʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, bɩa fʋ yaala tɩ jigiŋ. Keŋ dɩ fʋ mugisi tɩ mɩŋ ta saŋka mi wa tʋgɩ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Ama yuŋ kaanɩ vuoŋ mana die dɩ gʋʋrɩnana ʋ dataaŋ die dɩ ga a ga bʋrɩ huuti a gʋtɩ ʋ zaaha ma ta yiŋŋi ga.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Wudieke n balala nɩ lɩmɩŋ ma wa, nɩ balɩ ka yaalɩŋ ma; ta wudieke n pʋsɩna balɩ yɩ nɩ wa, nɩ balɩ ka yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaaŋ. Die a jʋalɩ a kalɩ kunkogiŋ me.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Dɩɩ yi sɩba Sitaani vʋarɩna Sitaani vuosi sʋŋ, dɩ dagɩ sɩba ʋ yiŋŋi a waga ta puo wo ʋ gbaŋ; lalɩa ʋ naarɩ dɩ baa ka zie?.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Bɩa yine nɩ mugisi nɩ gbaŋ yaa gamma nyiŋyeeke wɩa? Nɩ daansɩ a ye tɩɩwute aŋaŋ ka vɩɩnɩŋ: ama a ka tʋma a yaala ka wute.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Wusie maŋ bala yɩa fʋ, kaaŋ bɩ nyɩŋ mi sie fʋ keŋ tuŋ ligire dieke mana ba bala dɩ fʋ tuŋ wo.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Yisa die dɩ deeŋ nuuŋ a yigi wo bʋnyɩ ta baarɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yada dɩ kana ka dala vuoke gie, bie yine fʋ chɩɩlɩ.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: A nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn die dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, vuodiekemba nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa, pɔgɩlɩha aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba juu aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Die perukusi a taŋ die ziene a gbigi mi a die.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Die zaaha die dɩ bɩrɩna a piili dɩ a yi sikpigile die huutiti dɩaŋ die dɩ hagɩ.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Nɩ da keŋ a chɩgɩma vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka, ama ta kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ haalɩbʋ; ama nɩ chɩgɩma Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka aŋaŋ nɩ haalɩbʋ mana boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ ma.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Ta kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ aŋaŋ gbilisi aŋaŋ yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gbarɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ gaantɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ yʋagɩna yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana a nagɩ ba dʋaŋ ʋ nɩŋŋa; die ʋ gbaaŋ ba.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Dɩ yiwo Beelizebuli yɩnana mɩŋ yiko maŋ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma, ta nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ mɩnɩa yɩnana nɩ vuosisi yiko dɩ ba vʋarɩmaha? Nɩ vuosi gbaŋ gbaŋ tʋʋma daga sɩba nɩ wo wusie.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ama n balɩ nɩ, halɩ naaŋ Solomoni aŋaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna mana die ka yeegi jayeekiŋ ka vɩɩna a tʋgɩ tɩɩwutehe gie.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Nɩ wʋŋya die ba bala Da a keŋ a dʋagɩ vuoŋ hɔgʋ.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ba mana die dɩ juu haarɩkʋ sʋŋ die bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ nan.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba mana aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die balɩ ʋ naarɩ gie wɩa ta Jɔɔn dɩ wa keŋ.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa a baarɩ, Fʋ keŋ yagɩ tɩ, fʋ vaa tɩ ga juu perukusisi me.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Daa wa tʋntʋntɩŋ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, daa zaabʋrɩvɩɩna die fʋ bʋrɩ fʋ kʋakʋ ma? Ama sɩa huutiti dɩ nyɩŋ.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Ba daa nembisi ale wo kobu, ama dɩ ka yi tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ, a kaanɩ mana kaaŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yene ta gaantɩba balɩ wɩa, ta gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta gbilisi dɩ ye alaafɩa, yɩɩsɩ dɩ yese wo die dɩ yi be mamachi. Die ba bɩrɩ Izara vuosi Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama manɩŋ nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yinene maŋ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma; die dagɩna sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yinene huuti dɩ vɩɩna, nyindieke dɩ benne jinne ta soriŋ ba jʋʋ ha a taaŋ wa, ʋ kaaŋ wa a faasɩ yeegi nɩ nyiŋyeeke? Ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yeegi nɩ? Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana a ka dala!.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Ama manɩŋ n bala nɩ lele dɩ vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ daansɩ hɔgʋ ta yaa lɔla aŋaŋ wa ʋ yi wo hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩ benne haarɩkʋ sʋŋ die dɩ keŋ gbirigi a jɩama Yisa ta baarɩ, Wusie, seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Ta nɩ a keŋ a yaala nɩ tuo wɩaha dii, Jɔɔn yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Elaja, vuodieke die dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ha, Nɩ gamma. Die wɩa die a nyɩŋ dembisi bale wo me, a ga a juu perukusisi sʋŋ; Die perukusisi mana dɩ chɩgɩ birrr sʋʋŋ tanɩ chɩaŋ a ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma ta nyaabʋ dɩ dii he.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dataaŋ wʋnyɩ yine die. Die ba pɩasɩ wa Yaala tɩ ga a vʋʋ huutiti mɩŋ.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ zoosi nʋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Yisa die dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Zɔɔlɩŋ yigi mɩŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi gie wɩa, dama ba bie wo n jigiŋ daraa ataa, ta lele ba wo jaaŋ ba baaŋ dii. N ka yaala n vaa ba kuli ta ka yɩ ba nyindiike, dama ba nan keŋ chaa sieŋ me.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a wɩɩ a juu hagɩrɩtieŋ tigiŋ a vaarɩ ʋ nyinti, ntaala ʋ woliŋ a bɔbɩ wa ta wa bɩagɩ a vaarɩ ʋ nyintiti.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Die wɩa nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ ta yilime, Bɩa tɩ nan dii? Yaa Bɩa tɩ nan nyuu? Yaa Bɩa tɩ nan yeegi.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Dɩ yiwo fʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ nimbiŋ vana fʋ yi bɩaŋ, vʋarɩka taaŋ dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ wone nimbiŋ kaanɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan nagɩ fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana a yi boli dieke dɩ wone kpisiŋ me.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Die ba garɩsɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ a keŋ Genezariti tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yallɩ tɩba ʋ wʋŋ.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ daansɩnana perukusisi die dɩ chɩgɩ a ga tɩka sʋŋ a mʋʋlɩ wudieke die dɩ yine wo, aŋaŋ jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ nyɩna dembisi bale wo me die.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Ʋ baarɩ, Aayɩ, dama nɩ a keŋ a vʋa huutiti nɩ nan gʋtɩ a vʋʋ zaaha ataŋ.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Die wɩa nɩ da chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ; dama nɩ yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisi pam.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ pɩasɩ wa, Sɩa tɩ nan ye nyindiike haagɩrɩ gie me a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ gie.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka zie n kʋaŋ waga mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka suŋŋi mɩŋ a lagɩsa vuosi n jigiŋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ jatɩnana ba.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Vuodieke dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ ŋaana chɩga a yaala nyinti gie mana, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nɩ yaalaha mɩŋ.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Dɩ yiwo fʋ nuudiigiŋ yinene fʋ tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ, fʋ gobike a vigi taaŋ, dama dɩ kpɩa pam fʋ baaŋ nan waarɩ fʋ nuubalɩmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ baaŋ nan juu boliŋ me.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: mi vuosi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ Yisa. Die wɩa die ba mʋʋlɩ a yɩ vuosi mana, ta ba chii vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yʋagɩna a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Lele, bɩa maŋ baa n nagɩ jinne vuosi gie a magɩsɩ? Ba nɩasɩ wa ballɩbɩsɩ dɩ kalla nyʋʋgɩtɩ jige, a wasa tamba.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma, vuoŋ mana die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩka ma a tuole Yisa; die ba yene wo wo die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ ba tɩŋgbaŋka ma.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Die wɩa nɩ vaa zaaha aŋaŋ huutiti mana lagɩsɩ bɩrɩŋ a ga tʋgɩ gobiŋ saŋŋa. Saŋka mi n nan balɩ zaagobiribe; nɩ woliŋ vʋʋ huutiti ta bɔbɩha a huubɔba a jʋʋha boliŋ; ta lagɩsɩ zaaha a nagɩha a yi n napooti me. .

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me dɩ ʋ yiwo n vuoŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan vʋarɩ wa yaalɩŋ ma n Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba, Paanʋsɩ aŋmɩa nɩ yallɩ? Ba baarɩ, Paanʋsɩ golime ayʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩ bɩta.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma, maŋ bala nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ a chaa vuotaŋ wʋbɩatɩ aŋaŋ wʋbɩa diekemba ʋ balala mana; ama vuodieke mana dɩ balɩ wʋbɩaŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Ama nɩ woliŋ yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ aŋaŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala wɩa ta ʋ nan nagɩ nyindiekemba nɩ yaalɩnana mana a yɩ nɩ.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Die ba bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ baa ʋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ sie ʋ yɩ wa gbanɩŋ a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ zeti wo mɩŋ.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: Die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ ko a vaa vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna wa ko a gbɩ ʋ jayeekiri kʋanʋaŋ; die vuodiekemba mana die dɩ gbɩna ka wa die ye wo gbaanɩŋ.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: Tɩ wɩwa wɩɩŋ a yɩ nɩ ama nɩ ka sieye, ta tɩ chɩa hanye a yɩ nɩ ama nɩ ka kʋŋya.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a taaŋ ba nandagɩŋ bɩbra dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba kaŋmɩnɩŋ biŋ daa dɩ nagɩna a ga bʋrɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ yi kpɩkpaakʋ nʋaŋ dɩ ba sʋʋŋ kala tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Vuodieke dɩaŋ mana mana dɩ keŋ balɩ wʋbɩaŋ a yɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ balɩ wʋbɩaŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ wɔŋ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa dʋnɩa ka gie me yaa dʋnɩa dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ wo.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka yi n vuoŋ vuosi jigiŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan balɩ dɩ n ka sɩba wa n Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Ama lele, manɩŋ n bala nɩ dɩ vuodieke dɩ a keŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ta hɔgʋ wa ka dʋaga dembisi, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine hɔgʋ wa dɩ ga a dʋaga dembisi; ta dembiŋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ faarɩ hɔgʋ dieke ba zetine wo gbaŋ tʋŋ wa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Die wɩa da keŋ vaa soriŋ wɩa mugisi nɩ dama soriŋ nan dɩ yallɩ ka gbaŋ gbaŋ mugisikiŋ. Daaŋ mana yaa ka wumugisi dieke dɩ mʋgɩsɩna ka mɩŋ.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ keŋ ta bɔba nʋaŋ ta ka nyuu daaŋ vuosi dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla wa.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Ka yine nyɩŋbʋrɩka mana jabɩŋ, ama ka keŋ bɩrɩŋ ka yine vapɔgɩtɩ mana jakpɩɩŋ. Ka ŋaaŋ bɩrɩŋ wa tɩɩŋ ta nembisi keŋ yʋga a tose ka chagɩsa ma.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ paanʋ golime ayʋpɔyɩ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩsɩ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die gbieri gbieri he a nagɩ ha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ba nagɩha a yɩ vuosisi.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Nɩ da keŋ a yilime sɩba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ maŋ yaa keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi jigiŋ; Aayɩ, n ka keŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ama n keŋ wo aŋaŋ puole.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Nɩ bɩ wʋŋ die ba bala a yɩ kʋrɩŋ vuosi dɩ, Da a keŋ a hʋʋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ ama fʋ keŋ hʋʋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ mʋ fʋ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi ta tɩɩŋ dɩ vɩɩna, ka nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna; ama dɩɩ yi ta tɩɩŋ dɩ ka vɩɩna, ka ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ wa nyɩŋnyɩŋka bɩatɩ dama tɩɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ma ba sɩba ka.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Ta manɩŋ vuota Bʋa die dɩ keŋ, a die ta nyuo, vuosi dɩ baarɩ, Nɩ ye daa wa gie ʋ yiwo wutulitieŋ aŋaŋ daanyuru, ta yi lampotuosiriŋ aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ zʋa. Ama Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋnna die wo dagɩya sɩba ʋ yaa yɩaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Die ba mana dɩ dii ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: N keŋ dɩ n yiwo ballɩ dembisi tuoli ba chʋalɩŋ, ta havʋʋbisi tuoli ba nɩɩlɩŋ, lɩahaalʋ tuoli ʋ nuŋ.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Ama manɩŋ n balɩ nɩ, dɩ nɩ sɩmma nɩ hʋʋ gbaŋ; da keŋ a baarɩ, N hʋʋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dama dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ jigiberisikiŋ;.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a taaŋ ba nandagɩŋ bɩbra dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba dabɔtɩ hɔgʋ dɩ nagɩna a lagɩsɩ paanʋ zɔŋ dieke dɩ dala ma aŋ bʋ mana kʋŋ a yiri die wo.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuuke buuribu gie, nɩ yine vuobɩatɩ die, lalɩa wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩna dɩ nan bɩagɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ nʋa ma? Dama jadieke vuota dɩ yiline ʋ sʋŋ ma die nyɩnnana ʋ nʋaŋ ma.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne tɩgɩ diekemba ma ma Yisa die dɩ yine ʋ mamachi wɩa die, ta ka chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma, die wɩa die ʋ galɩŋ ba.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Die dembiŋ diekemba die dɩ dine wo die yiwo vuosi tuse anɩɩsa, ama die ba ka bɩɩsɩ hɔgʋba aŋaŋ ballɩ.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Vuota dataasɩ nan yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: yaa fʋ hʋʋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie, dama ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naatala jigizielikiŋ; yaa fʋ hʋʋ Jerusalemi dama ka yiwo Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Yisa die taaŋ wa nandaga a bala wɩaha gie mana mana a yɩ daadamba: Die ʋ ka bala wɩɩŋ a yɩa ba ta ka ka yi nandagɩŋ.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Vuovɩɩnɩŋ yie wʋvɩɩna, dama ʋ sʋŋ vɩɩna mɩŋ; ta vuobɩaŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yie wʋbɩatɩ dama ʋ sʋkʋ ka vɩɩna.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩnɩŋ Korazini vuosi me! Wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩnɩŋ Befisada vuosi ma ma! Dama mamachi wudieke n yine nɩ tɩgɩsɩ ma, maŋ tɩŋ a yi die Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni ma ma, vuodiekemba dɩ benne mi wo tɩŋ nan vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ta nagɩ vaatɩ aŋaŋ tanyeeliŋ a bʋʋlɩ ba gbaŋ aŋ die dagɩ sɩba ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma ma mɩŋ.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Die Yisa dɩ wa a taaŋ kpɩkpaakʋ ta die juu haarɩŋ a ga Magadani tɩŋgbaŋ chaaŋ ma.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ chone ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ chone ʋ naahaalʋ yaa ʋ havʋʋbiŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Da a keŋ a hʋʋ fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ sikpeŋ gbaŋ, dama kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi fʋ sikpeŋ zoosi kaanɩ gbaŋ bɩrɩŋ jayɩalɩŋ yaa jasɔbɩlɩŋ.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bala wudieke wo keŋ yi wusie, dɩ Maŋ a keŋ n bala wɩɩŋ n nan nagɩ nandaga a balɩ ba, n nan balɩ ba wudieke mana die dɩ lɔbɩrɩna a nyɩŋ dʋnɩa dɩ piiline a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: N bala nɩ, sarɩya diile daraaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya sɩba die ba balala wʋtɔgɩtɩ mana wɩa.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ, dɩ daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya nɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala pam a tɩaŋ Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni vuosi sarɩya.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ kana ka vigi ʋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩa mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Nɩ ko vaa aŋ nɩ Wa, yi nɩ Wa, ta nɩ Aayɩ yi nɩ Aayɩ dama nɩ hʋʋ jaaŋ a gʋtɩ, die nyɩŋ wa Sitaani jigiŋ.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Ta nɩnɩŋ Kapenɔmi vuosi aŋaŋ nɩ kɔtɩna nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ mana, Ŋmɩŋ nan vigi nɩ a taaŋ kunti jigiberisikiŋ dama mamachi diekemba die n yine nɩ jigiri die dɩ tɩŋ yi Sodomi ma ma dɩ mʋna naa a tɩŋ ye beri a ga tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: dama vuoŋ mana wʋbalɩka bala ka vʋarɩ wa dɩnda daarɩ mi yaa ʋ wʋbalɩka dɩaŋ bala ka chʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ va daadamba mi ta ga a juu tigidieke mi die ʋ benne. Ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, Dagɩ tɩ huutiti nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ yaalala dɩ ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ miivoli nan waarɩ ka; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ waarɩ ʋ miivoli n wɩa wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nan daansɩ ye ke.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Nɩ wʋŋya die ba bala dɩ, Vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a vʋʋ fʋ nimbiŋ fʋ dɩaŋ fʋ vʋʋ ʋ nimbiŋ a tuŋ hamɩŋ; ta vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a vʋʋ fʋ nyɩŋ fʋ dɩaŋ fʋ vʋʋ ʋ nyɩŋ a tuŋ hamɩŋ.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Ama n bala nɩ, sarɩyaka diile daraaŋ nɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala a tɩaŋ Sodomi vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Womi mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ tɩ yaala fʋ yiwo mamachi wɩɩŋ aŋ tɩ ye.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Ta ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yine vuodieke die dɩ bʋrɩna nyɩŋbʋrɩvɩɩnaha.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a tuo nɩ chaantɩ, mɩŋŋɩ tuo mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ mɩŋŋɩna tuo mɩŋ, mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Ama manɩŋ lele n balɩ nɩ, nɩ da keŋ tuŋ nɩ chanchaaŋ hamɩŋ; vuoŋ dɩ keŋ a nɩgɩ fʋ nuudiigiŋ kaamɩŋ ma fʋ yiŋŋi fʋ nuugalɩ kaamɩŋ dɩaŋ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ nɩgɩ;.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ baarɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie tieŋ, n bɩra fʋ, dama fʋnɩŋ lɔbɩrɩ wɩaha gie yɩantieliŋ aŋaŋ sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ jigiŋ ama ta yuori he a yɩ ballɩbɩsɩ;.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Ama die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ, Lele vuosi dɩ yine vuobɩatɩ ta ka bɩ jɩama Ŋmɩŋ yaalala ba ye mamachi wɩɩŋ; ama n kaaŋ wɔŋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ a yɩ ba; Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jona dagɩŋ chanchaaŋ nyɩɩna maŋ nan dagɩ ba.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Kʋakʋ dɩaŋ yine dʋnɩa, nyɩŋbʋrɩvɩɩnaha dɩaŋ yine vuodiekemba dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ta huutiti dɩaŋ yine Sitaani vuosi.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa chaantɩ ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa tune; vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ chaantɩ ʋ yine sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ tune.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: vuoŋ dɩ keŋ nagɩ fʋ a ga naalɩaŋ me, a yaala ʋ gbatɩ fʋ jayeekibiŋ, fʋ nagɩ fʋ jayeekikpeŋkpɩɩŋ a gʋtɩ wa.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: dama die fʋ tʋma fʋ tʋʋma a yie fʋ nansɩŋ.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Die Jona dɩ benne zaasɩkpɩɩkʋ sʋkʋ ma ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ataa wa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩaŋ dɩ nan dɩ beri tɩŋgbaŋka gie chɩaŋ ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ataa.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Dataa dieke dɩaŋ dɩ bʋrɩna huutiti yine Sitaani; gobiku saŋka dɩaŋ yine dʋnɩa kpatɩ saŋŋa, gogobiribe dɩaŋ yine Ŋmɩŋ malakasi.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a yuori nyasʋnsʋŋ a yɩ n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gie wʋnyɩ dɩ ʋ yine n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wɩa nan daansɩ ye tune.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a mugisi fʋ dɩ fʋ chii ʋ chiiti chʋŋ mali balɩmɩŋ fʋ chii a chʋŋ malisi ale.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa jaaŋ mana a yɩ mɩŋ; vuoŋ ka sɩba manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa sie n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ, vuoŋ dɩaŋ ka sɩba n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ sie manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba n yaala n nagɩ wa a dagɩ.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Nineva vuosi nan hagɩ zie a galɩŋ nɩ, dama Jona dɩ mʋʋlɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩ ba wa die ba va ba tʋmbɩatɩ mɩŋ; n bala nɩ, vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Jona benne gie.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Sɩba die ba lagɩsɩna huutiti a jʋʋha bolibu die wo, die dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a jʋʋsɩ fʋ jaaŋ fʋ yɩ wa ta da a keŋ a mʋŋ wa, vuodieke mana dɩ yaalala ʋ paŋŋɩ jaaŋ fʋ jigiŋ fʋ paŋŋɩ wa da keŋ mʋŋ wa.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Nɩ keŋ n jigiŋ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chiine chiidʋnsa aŋ n yɩ nɩ voosiŋ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Die nɩ wʋŋya ba bala dɩ, Nɩ yaalɩma nɩ zʋalɩŋ, ta hama nɩ dataasɩ.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Nɩ nagɩ n chiiti a chii ta nɩ bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ n ma dama n yiwo vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna n gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ n gbaŋ ta nɩ nan ye voosiŋ.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Siaba hɔgʋ naaŋ wa nan hagɩ a zie a galɩŋ nɩ dama die ʋ paalɩ nyɩŋ wa tɩgɩ saasaa a ga dɩ ʋ wʋŋ naaŋ Solomoni yɩaŋ wʋbalɩkaha, ama n bala nɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna naaŋ Solomoni benne giena.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan daansɩ tʋŋ n malakasi aŋ ba ga vʋarɩ a nyɩŋ n naarɩ ma vuodiekemba die dɩ yinene vuosi dɩ yie bɩaŋ aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ mana.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Ama manɩŋ lele n bala nɩ dɩ, nɩ chome nɩ dataasɩ wɩa ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ mugisinene nɩ.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Dama n kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ ka tʋa ta n chiiti dɩaŋ dɩ ka dʋnsɩ.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ vuota ma, ka ŋaaŋ dɩa wa jigikʋʋkɩŋ a yaala jigiŋ dɩ ka voosi, ka ka yeye;.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: aŋ ba nagɩ ba a taaŋ boliŋ me, mi ba nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba ba nyɩna.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: aŋ die vaa nɩ seŋ yi tɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma ballɩ, dama ʋ yinene ŋmɩŋ dɩ pɔsa a yɩa vuobɩatɩ aŋaŋ vuovɩɩna mana ta bɩ vasa nɩɩŋ dɩ nɩa a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yinene vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene bɩaŋ.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: ʋ ŋaaŋ balɩ a yɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ, N nan yiŋŋi a ga n tigidieke n vana wa. Die wɩa ka ŋaaŋ yiŋŋi ga mi a ye dɩ vuoŋ wo mi, ta ka sʋŋ mana saarɩya a wʋnsɩ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Dama fʋ yaala vuodiekemba dɩ yaalala fʋ nyɩɩna, bie nyʋarɩ fʋ baaŋ fʋ ye a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ? Daa die gbaŋ gbaŋ vuobɩatɩ die dɩ yie?.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Ama saŋŋa mi ŋmɩŋchɩgɩrɩba mana nan dɩ bie n Chʋa wa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma a chaaŋ sɩba ŋmɩŋ dɩ chaannana die wo. Vuodieke dɩ yaa tɩba ʋ wʋmma.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: Ka ŋaaŋ wa nyɩŋ mɩŋ a ga a chii jɩmbɩatɩ diekemba dɩ tɩanna ka gbaŋ gbaŋ ayʋpɔyɩ aŋ a ga bemme mi. Naa chɩaŋ ma daa wa beriŋ nan faasɩ chʋʋsɩ pam a tɩaŋ die ʋ sɩna die wo. Naa bala ka yi a yɩ lele gie vuobɩatɩtɩ.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Ta fʋ a keŋ waasa fʋ zʋalɩŋ nyɩɩna ma bie wʋvɩɩna fʋ yie a tɩaŋ fʋ chanchaalɩba? Daa die vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka dɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ yie?.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba jadɩɩŋ dieke die dɩ gune tɩŋgbaŋ ma vuoŋ kʋaŋ ma. Ta daa wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ ye ke, a kpɩarɩ tantɩ a ligi ke, ta ga aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ a ga nagɩ ʋ yalla nyindieke mana a daa a yiŋŋi keŋ daa kʋakʋ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yisa die ye ko bala wɩa a yɩa vuotamba ta ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ. Die ba zie wo yeŋ me ta tʋŋ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ wa wa aŋ ba balɩ wɩa.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Die wɩa nɩ bemme vɩɩnɩŋ sɩba nɩ Chɔɔŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne die wo arɩzanna ma.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ bɩ sɩɩ sɩba nyugidiiru dɩ dɩna dɩdɩa yaala nyielevɩɩna dɩ ʋ daa.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Die wɩa vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Daansɩ ye, fʋ naa aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmballɩ zie yeŋ me ta yaala ba aŋaŋ fʋ balɩ wɩa.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: Die ʋ ga ye kaanɩ dɩ faasɩna vɩɩna die ʋ ga a ga nagɩ ʋ nyinti mana a daa ta keŋ daa ka.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Mɩnɩa yine n naa? Mɩnɩa yine n nɩmballɩ.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ bɩ sɩɩ sɩba, zaasɩŋ nɩɩŋ ba nagɩna a vigi taaŋ nyaaŋ ma ka yigi zaasɩŋ yiri yiri nɩŋ mana.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Womi ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a dɩɩŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta baarɩ, Ye, n naa aŋaŋ n nɩmballɩ wʋnna.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Ka suuline wo zaasɩyigiribe dɩ datɩ ka nyɩŋ gaaŋ ma, a sʋʋŋ kalɩ a lugi zaasɩvɩɩnaha a yi kparɩŋ ma ama ta nagɩ zaasɩbɩatɩtɩ a vigi taaŋ wa.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ yinene wudieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne arɩzanna ma choti wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine n nɩmbʋa aŋaŋ n taa aŋaŋ n naa.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Naa dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ. Ŋmɩŋ malakasi nan keŋ a lugi vuobɩatɩtɩ a nyɩŋ vuovɩɩna ma.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ta nagɩ vuobɩatɩtɩ a vigi taaŋ boliŋ me. Mi ba nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba ba nyɩna.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba, Nɩ sɩba wɩaha gie chɩasɩ mɩŋ? Die ba baarɩ Wa.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Die wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrʋ dieke mana dɩ bʋgɩrɩnana n dagɩkʋ yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa ta mɩŋŋɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tieŋ dieke dɩ vʋarɩna nyɩŋhaala aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋra a nyɩŋ ʋ nyinti ma.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagaha gie a kpatɩ, die ʋ nyɩŋ mi.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: a yiŋŋi a ga ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ, mi die ʋ ga daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me. Die ʋ dagɩkʋ dɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnnana wa mamachi. Die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Sɩa ʋ ye yɩabʋ gie? Sɩa ʋ ye yiko ke gie a yie mamachi wɩaha gie.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Daa kampɩnta wa bʋa wa wondie? Daa ʋ nuŋ yine Meri, ta ʋ nɩmballɩ dɩ yi Jemisi aŋaŋ Josefu aŋaŋ Simoni aŋaŋ Judasi?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Da ʋ tamba benne giena wa? Sɩa ʋ ye genhe mana.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Die wɩa die ba zeti wo. Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Ba yɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa jɩlɩma jigiŋ mana mana mɩŋ, ntaala ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi jigiŋ.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Die ʋ ka yi mamachi wɩa pam mi dama die ba ka yi wo yada wɩa.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Daraa ayʋaba kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa die dɩ nagɩ Piita, aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, a yaa ba ga jʋalɩ taŋbɩrɩŋkɩŋ sikpeŋ me, ba nyɩɩna.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Womi ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ tarɩgɩ ba nɩŋŋa. Ʋ nine dɩ chaaŋ sɩba ŋmɩŋ ta ʋ nyiŋyeeke dɩ yɩantɩ sɩba chaanɩŋ.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Ta ba ye Mosisi aŋaŋ Elaja dɩ nyɩnna bʋnyɩ ba jigiŋ a bala wɩa aŋaŋ Yisa.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ vɩɩna tɩ benne giena. Fʋ tɩŋ nan saagɩ, n tɩŋ nan kpii viisi ataa giena, ta kaanɩ fʋ jaaŋ, ta kaanɩ yi Mosisi jaaŋ ta kaanɩ dɩaŋ dɩ yi Elaja jaaŋ.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Die Piita die dɩ yene ko bala wɩaha, nɩɩŋmaayɩalɩŋ dɩ keŋ ligi be, ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ nyɩŋ nɩɩŋmaakʋ ma a baarɩ, N Bʋachoti wʋnna, ʋ ma n sʋŋ dɩ paalɩ a fɩalɩ, nɩ wʋmma a yɩma wa.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna lɔlɩkʋ die ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ faasɩ a yigi be, die ba gbirigi tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Yisa die dɩ keŋ ba jigiŋ a gbi gbi ba ta baarɩ, Nɩ hagɩ zie. Da nɩ chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Die wɩa die ba kɔtɩ ba sikpigile a daansɩ ta ka ye vuoŋ mi, sie Yisa nyɩɩna ma.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Die ba nyɩnna tanɩ ma a keŋ sʋʋna wa, Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ nɩ yene wudieke wo, a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa dɩ balla ʋ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa wɩa mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ sie Elaja woliŋ keŋ Masia ma.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Ka yiwo wusie dɩ sie Elaja woliŋ keŋ, amʋ ʋ wʋnsɩ wɩɩŋ mana a mɩŋŋɩ zieŋ.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Ama manɩŋ n bala nɩ dɩ, Elaja wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ ama ta vuosi dɩ ka mɩŋŋɩ wa, ta yi wo yɔrɩ yɔrɩ die ba yaalala die wo. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nan yi manɩŋ vuota Bʋa aŋ n dii wahala ba nuusi me.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Womi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a baarɩ Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ wɩa ʋ bala die.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataa wa dɩ yiŋŋine a ga kpɩkpaakʋ jigiri daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ chɩgɩ n bʋa dembike gie zɔɔlɩŋ, dama ʋ yiwo kpenkpennantʋ, die dɩ paalɩ a mugisi wo a yi ʋ nanna boliŋ me yaa nyaaŋ sʋŋ daaŋ mana.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Maŋ yaa wa keŋ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ, ama die ba ka bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ wa.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩnɩŋ lele vuosi gie dɩ wone yada ta bɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ, nɩ yaala n bemme nɩ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaala n yaa wa suguru aŋaŋ nɩ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaa bʋa wa a keŋ giena.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Yisa die dɩ natɩ jɩmbɩakʋ die ka nyɩŋ bʋa wa ma, die bʋa wa dɩ ye a laafɩa lele womi.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, Bɩa wɩa tɩ ka bɩagɩ a yagɩ jɩmbɩakʋ.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Dɩ yiwo nɩ yada dɩ kana ka dala wɩa. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada ka dala sɩba kaŋmɩŋ biŋ, nɩ nan bɩagɩ a balɩ yɩ tanɩ gie Vʋʋ a nyɩŋ giena a ga nan mi aŋ ka vʋʋ; nɩ nan bɩagɩ yi jaaŋ mana.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ lagɩsɩna Galili mi, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Ba nan nagɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa a yi vuosi nuusi me.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: aŋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kumbu me. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ paalɩ a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gana Kapenɔmi, Ŋmɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ lampotuosiribe dɩ keŋ Piita jigiŋ a pɩasɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ wa tune Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ lampoke mɩŋ.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ, wa, ʋ tune mɩŋ. Piita die dɩ gana a juu tigiri me die Yisa die woliŋ balɩ wɩaha, ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Simoni lalɩa fʋ yile? Ba mba tunene lampo a yɩa tɩŋgbaŋka gie naakpɩɩma ha? Tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi yaa chaanɩŋ.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Ta ʋ yiŋŋi a baarɩ Chaanɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Dɩ mʋ mɩŋ, dɩ dagɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi ka mʋ dɩ ba tuŋ.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Ama tɩ kana ka yaala tɩ chʋʋsɩ ba sʋgɩtɩ wɩa, ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma a ga taaŋ kagɔntɩ ta datɩ zaasɩ dieke fʋ wolinne a yigi, fʋ yuori ka nʋaŋ nan ye ligire biŋ dieke dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a tuŋ manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ ma; ta fʋ nagɩ ka a tuŋ be.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Saŋka mi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasa wa dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine vuokpɩɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Die ʋ wa bʋabiŋ a vaa ʋ zie ba nɩŋŋa.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: ta balɩ dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ kana ka tarɩgɩ ʋ beriŋ a sɩɩ sɩba bʋabiŋ beriŋ, ʋ kaaŋ wɔŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ sɩba bʋabike gie, wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ yi vuokpɩɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a tuose bʋabike gie chanchaaŋ n saaŋ ma, mɩŋŋɩ tuose mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Ballɩbɩ diekemba dɩ yine mɩŋ yada wa gie, vuodieke dɩ yi ba yi bɩaŋ, ba tɩŋ bɔbɩ nɩɩŋ a yi ʋ nyie ta nagɩ wa taaŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma ta nyaabʋ dɩ dii wo dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Wʋbɩaŋ nan dɩ beri tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuodieke dɩ gaanana ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ yie bɩaŋ wɩa. Magɩsɩŋ nan keŋ ama wʋbɩaŋ bie vuodieke dɩ yinene a kieŋ vuosi mi wo.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Dɩ yiwo fʋ nuuŋ yaa fʋ nachɩgɩsɩŋ yinene fʋ yie tʋmbɩatɩ die nɩŋ fʋ gobike a vigi taaŋ. Dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nuubalɩmɩŋ yaa nachɩgɩbalɩmɩŋ a juu miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩ ma a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nuusi mana aŋaŋ nachɩgɩsɩ mana ta Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ fʋ a vigi taaŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Dɩ bɩ yiwo fʋ nimbiŋ dɩaŋ yinene fʋ tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ fʋ vʋarɩka a vigi taaŋ dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan juu miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma aŋaŋ nimbibalɩmɩŋ a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nine ale ta Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ fʋ a taaŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɩ sɩmma nɩ da keŋ a daansɩ ballɩbɩsɩ gie wʋnyɩ yɔrɩ. Ama wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, ba malakasisi bie wo Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ saŋŋa mana.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ yallɩ yiise kɔbɩga, ta kaanɩ dɩ bɩa, lalɩa nɩ yile ʋ nan yi? Ʋ nan vaa baŋɩsɩ-wayɩ aŋaŋ awayɩ wa a zieŋ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ ta yaalɩ jadieke dɩ bana wa.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, ʋ keŋ ye ke ʋ nan dɩ yallɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiibalɩŋka gie wɩa a tɩaŋ yiise baŋɩsɩ-wayɩ aŋaŋ wayɩ dɩ kana ka baya wa.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa dɩ ka yaala ballɩbɩsɩ gie wʋnyɩ bɩa.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Fʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ keŋ tʋŋ fʋ bɩaŋ, ga ʋ jigiŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ benne ʋ nyɩɩna ma a balɩ wa wudieke ʋ yine. Dɩɩ yi ʋ saagɩ ʋ tʋnna die, nɩ bɩ yiwo nɩmballɩ bɩbra die.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Ama ʋ a keŋ ka yaala ʋ wʋŋ a yɩ fʋ, fʋ nagɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ yaa vuosi bale a gʋtɩ fʋ ma aŋ nɩ ga ʋ jigiŋ, aŋ vuosi bale wo yaa bataa wa wʋŋ wɩarɩ ta yi fʋ siaratieliŋ.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Ʋ bɩ ka yaala ʋ wʋŋ a yɩ ba, fʋ balɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vuosi mana, dɩɩ yi ʋ ka bɩ wʋŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vuosisi, nɩ va wa aŋ ʋ yi sɩba vuodieke dɩ kana ka jɩama Ŋmɩŋ yaa lampotuosiru.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, jadieke dɩ baa dɩ chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ nan dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma; ta jadieke dɩ kana ka chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ kaaŋ dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: N bɩ bala nɩ, nɩnɩŋ nɩ vuosi bale dɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a jʋʋsɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jaaŋ, ʋ nan yi nɩ yaalala die a yɩ nɩ.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Dama jigidieke mana vuosi bale yaa bataa dɩ lagɩsɩna n saaŋ ma, mi maŋ gʋtɩ a bie ba sʋŋ.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Die Piita die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi n nɩmbʋa dɩ yi mɩŋ bɩaŋ, bʋŋmɩa maŋ baa n nagɩ a chaa wa? Bʋyʋpɔyɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Aayɩ, daa bʋyʋpɔyɩ ama baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ jigiŋ ayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ wana ʋ tigiŋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ʋ bɩɩsɩ ba hamɩŋ ligire dɩ mʋna die.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Die ʋ piiline a bɩɩsa ba ba yaa daa wʋnyɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ dii wo ʋ hamɩŋ ligire tuse tuse pam.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Die ʋ kaaŋ dɩ bɩagɩ a tuŋ; naa chɩaŋ ma naaŋ wa die dɩ a baarɩ ba nagɩ wa aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ aŋaŋ ʋ ballɩ aŋaŋ nyindiekemba ʋ yalla mana a daa a keŋ tuŋ hannɩ.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ama yɔmʋ wa die dɩ gbirigi naaŋ wa nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa a baarɩ, Dii suguru, n nan tuŋ fʋ jaaŋ mana.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Ta zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi naaŋ wa die ʋ nagɩ hannɩ a chaa wa, ta va ʋ ga.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Womi yɔmʋ wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga haarɩ ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaaŋ die dɩ dine ʋ hamɩŋ ligire bɩta, die ʋ yigi ʋ nyie ta balɩ wa, Tuŋ mɩŋ n hannɩ lele.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ʋ chanchaaka die dɩ a gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa a baarɩ, Dii suguru n nan tuŋ fʋ.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Ama ta die ʋ zeti ta baarɩ ba yigi wo a ga a yi dansarɩka ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan tuŋ hannɩ.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Die ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ yene die wo die ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ die ba ga balɩ ba naaŋ wa wudieke mana dɩ yine.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Naaŋ wa die dɩ wa wa ʋ juu; ʋ piili a zɩa wa a baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ vuobɩakʋ gie die n nagɩ hannɩ dieke fʋ dinene a chaa fʋ mɩŋ dama die jʋʋsɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Dɩ tɩŋ mʋ fʋ chɩgɩ wa fʋ chanchaaka zɔɔlɩŋ sɩba die n chɩgɩna fʋ zɔɔlɩŋ die wo gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Die sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ paala yallɩ naaŋ wa ʋ nagɩ daa wa a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ dɩ ʋ dii wahala a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan tuŋ hannɩ mana.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Nandagɩrɩ gie kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa dɩ balɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ ka nagɩ a chaa fʋ chanchaalɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana die gbaŋ gbaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa dɩ nan yi fʋ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tieŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna sʋkʋleeliŋ a yaala vuosi dɩ ʋ nagɩ aŋ ba keŋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba die dɩ a balɩ a zieŋ dɩ ʋ nan tuŋ be dɩnda daarɩ mi tune; die ba ga piili kpɩaŋ.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Die dɩ wa tʋga hawa awayɩ saŋŋa, tigiŋ tieŋ wo dɩ bɩ nyɩŋ a ga a ga ye vuosi bataŋ ba zene nyʋʋŋ jigiŋ ta ka yie wɩɩŋ mana.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Ta ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba, Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ga a ga tʋŋ tʋʋma n kʋakʋ ma, ta n nan tuŋ nɩ tuŋ dieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
