local: Die wɩa die ba ga. Die dɩ tʋgɩna agbaa saŋŋa die ʋ bɩ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ, ta agbaa die dɩ tɩanna hawasɩ ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ bɩ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Die dɩ wa tʋga jɩŋmɩŋ , die ʋ bɩ a ga nyʋʋkʋ chaaŋ a ye dembisi bataŋ dɩ zene mi ta ka tʋma tʋʋmɩŋ mana. Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ zie giena yɔrɩ damʋʋmɩŋ ta ka yie wɩɩŋ.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Vuoŋ ka keŋ nagɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ ga tʋŋ ʋ tʋʋma. Die wɩa ʋ balɩ ba, Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ga a ga tʋŋ tʋʋma n kʋakʋ ma.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Die ŋmɩŋ dɩ nanna, tigiritieŋ wo dɩ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba jakʋʋŋ dɩ, Ga wa tʋntʋntɩba a tuŋ be ba tune, piili a nyɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kenne kʋaka a ga tʋgɩ vuodiekemba dɩ wolinne wo.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Ta ʋ tʋntʋntʋ jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ tuŋ vuodiekemba dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaka a keŋ wo dɩnda daarɩ mi tune.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Die wɩa die dɩ kenne a tʋgɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kenne sʋkʋleeliku, die ba yile dɩ ba tune nan dɩ dala ama die ʋ tuŋ ba gbaŋ dɩnda daarɩ mi tune.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Die ba tuo ba ligirehe a kpatɩ die ba piili a vʋʋna ta galɩma tigiritieŋ wo.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: a baarɩ, Vuosi gie wɩarɩ wa kʋaŋ a keŋ kʋakʋ, agbaa kʋaŋ hawasɩ anʋ saŋŋa ta kʋa hawa balɩmɩŋ, ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ dine wahala a ga a gbalɩgɩ a nyɩŋ sʋkʋʋŋ mana, ta ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ nɩgɩ tɩ pam, ama tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ banɩŋ dɩ lagɩsɩ a tuo tuŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Die tigiritieŋ wo dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba wʋnyɩ, N zʋa, wʋmma, n ka nyɩrɩ nɩ, dama tɩ tɩŋ balɩ zie mɩŋ nɩ saagɩ dɩnda daarɩ mi tune.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Die wɩa nagɩ fʋ tʋaŋ a ga tigiŋ. Manɩŋ n yaala n tuŋ wo nɩ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kenne kʋaka bʋnyɩ.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: N wo sieŋ dɩ n nagɩ n ligirehe a yi wudieke n yaalala? Yaa wa sʋgɩbɩaŋ dama n kana ka mʋma wɩa? .

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ a kpatɩ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Die wɩa vuodiekemba dɩ benne kʋaŋ nan dɩ bie nɩŋŋa ta vuodiekemba dɩ benne nɩŋŋa wɩarɩ kʋaŋ.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩna a gara Jerusalemi, die ʋ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a die a balɩ a yɩ ba.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Nɩ wʋmma, tɩ jʋalɩ a gara Jerusalemi ma, jigidieke ba balla ba nagɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa a yi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba nuusi me, aŋ ba dii n sarɩya a baarɩ dɩ n chʋʋsɩya amʋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: ta nagɩ mɩŋ a yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi nuusi me aŋ ba vʋarɩ mɩŋ fala, ta nɩgɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta kpaasɩ mɩŋ a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma, aŋ n kpi ama a daraa ataa ma n nan hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Womi Zebedi hɔgʋ die dɩ chii ʋ ballɩ bale a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ, ta gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta jʋʋsɩ wa suŋŋiŋ.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ yaala? Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa, Yɩ mɩŋ nʋaŋ dɩ n ballɩ gie wʋnyɩ nan daansɩ kalɩ fʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ fʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ saŋŋa dieke fʋ keŋ dii fʋ naarɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ zɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩnana jadieke. Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a chii chiiti dieke n balla n chii wo? Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Wa, tɩ nan bɩagɩ.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Wusie, nɩ nan chii chiiti dieke n bala n chii wo, ama n wo sieŋ dɩ n vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ mʋna ba kala n nuudiigiŋ aŋaŋ n nuugalɩ chaaŋ. Jigehe gie zie gbarɩ wa vuodiekemba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ ba kala mi.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ tɩalɩkaha die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yi ba bale wo.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ wa ba mana a lagɩsɩ taŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ ba, Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ daansɩnana vuosi tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ŋaaŋ daga wa ba gbaŋ ta nyɩŋkʋraha dɩaŋ ŋaaŋ pɔgɩlɩ wa ba vuosi aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ die wo, nɩ sʋnsʋŋ vuodieke dɩ yaala nyɩŋkʋrɩtɩ, sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ wabʋ a tʋma a yɩma vuoŋ mana.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ bɩ yaala ʋ bemme nɩŋŋa, sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ nɩ mana yɔmʋ.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: sɩba manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ sɩna die wo, dama n ka keŋye dɩ vuosi tʋma a yɩma mɩŋ ama n keŋye dɩ n tʋŋ yɩ vuosi, ta bɩ kpi a yi vuosi pam ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩnana Jeriko me, die kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ta yɩɩsɩ bale die dɩ kalɩ sieku nʋaŋ a wʋŋ dɩ Yisa keŋ tɩanna mɩŋ, die wɩa die ba piili a keese a baarɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ama kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ natɩ ba dɩ ba tarɩ watɩ, ama ta die ba wa a faasɩ a keese, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Yisa die dɩ zie ta wa ba a keŋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yaala n yi a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ yaala fʋ yuori wo tɩ nine.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Ta zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi Yisa, ta ʋ gbi gbi ba ninehe, die ba ninehe die dɩ yuori yuori, ta ba dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Die ba kenne a keŋ gbigi Jerusalemi ta keŋ Betifeji tɩŋ, tɩŋ dieke die dɩ benne kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Olivi Tɩɩsɩ Kunkogiŋ lʋgɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale dɩ ba ga nɩŋŋa.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: ta balɩ ba, Nɩ gamma tɩŋkpaŋka dɩ benne nɩ nɩŋŋa, womi nɩ nan ye bonnɩɩŋ dɩ bɔbɩna zie ta ka bʋa dɩ zie mi; nɩ forisike a yaa ha a keŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ pɩasɩ nɩ wɩɩŋ, nɩ balɩ wa, nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yaalala ka, ta ʋ nan vaa nɩ yaa ha keŋ.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Wɩaha gie mana die keŋ yi mɩŋ sɩba die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vana ʋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩna die wo dɩ.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Nɩ balɩ Zayɔni tɩka vuosi dɩ, Ye, nɩ naaŋ wa kieŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, ta jʋala boŋ dieke dɩ yi bonpɔlɩ. .

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga yi wudieke Yisa die dɩ balla ba dɩ ba yi wo.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Die ba yaa bonɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ ka bʋa a keŋ, a nagɩ ba nyinyeeke a diisi a kʋasɩ ta Yisa dɩ jʋalɩ.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nagɩ ba nyinyeeke a jatɩ sieku me, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ chɩa tɩɩsɩ naasɩ a jatɩ sieku me.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ta kpɩkpaaŋ dieke dɩ benne ʋ nɩŋŋa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne ʋ kʋaŋ dɩ nata a baarɩ, Hozaana! Nɩ bɩrɩma Davidi haagɩrɩ gie. Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kennene ʋ saaŋ ma. Nɩ faasɩ natɩma hozaana ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ gana a ga juu Jerusalemi, tɩka vuosi mana die dɩ hagɩ a zie a pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Mɩnɩa wʋnna.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Yisa, vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa ta nyɩŋ Nazeriti, Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ ga a juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a yagɩ vuodiekemba mana die dɩ daanana aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla nyinti a daa mi; die ʋ kpaŋŋɩ vuodiekemba dɩ tarɩgɩnana ligirehe teebulisi a bubi bubi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla ŋmarɩsɩ a daa wa chɩasɩ a bubi.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: ta die a balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ, N tigiri gie nan dɩ wasa Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ nagɩ ka a bɩrɩŋ gaarɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Die yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gbarɩgɩsɩ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma die ʋ gbaaŋ ba mana.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Ama die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ die ba yene ʋ yinene mamachi wɩaha ta bɩ wʋŋ ballɩbɩsɩsɩ dɩ natɩnana Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a baarɩ, Hozaana, nɩ bɩrɩma Davidi haagɩrɩ gie die wo.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa, Wʋŋ wudieke ba balala wa mɩŋ? Ta ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Wa, ama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Fʋ yine ballɩbɩsɩsɩ aŋaŋ ballɩnyaalɩsɩ dɩ bɩra fʋ vɩɩnɩŋ. Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ wɩaha gie Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Yisa die dɩ va ba, ta nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ a ga Betani, die a dʋa mi tʋŋ dɩ vʋŋ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋʋna sʋkʋleeliŋ die ʋ yiŋŋi a gara tɩka sʋŋ, die kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ wa.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Die ʋ ye kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩŋ dɩ zene gbigi sieku nʋaŋ die ʋ ga ka jigiŋ, ama die ʋ ka ye jaaŋ mana ka sikpeŋ sie vaatɩ nyɩɩna ma. Na chɩaŋ ma die ʋ balɩ yɩ tɩɩka dɩ, Kaaŋ bɩ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka! Lele womi tɩɩka mana die dɩ ko.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yene naa wa die dɩ yi be mamachi. Die ba pɩasa taŋ, Lalɩa kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka dɩ ko lagɩ lagɩ die.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada ta ka chɩɩlɩya nɩ nan bɩagɩ a yi wudieke n yine yɩ kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka gie; daa genke nyɩɩna ma, nɩ nan bɩagɩ a balɩ yɩ kunkogiri gie dɩ, vʋʋ a ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma aŋ ka seŋ yi.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada nɩ nan ye jadieke mana nɩ jʋʋsɩnana a yaala.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, ta die daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa yiko fʋ yaa yie wɩaha gie? Mɩnɩa yɩna fʋ yiko ke gie.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ta Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, N nan pɩasɩ nɩ wʋbalɩmɩŋ, dɩɩ yi nɩ bɩagɩ a balɩ mɩŋ ka chɩaŋ, n nan balɩ nɩ yiko dieke a yalla a yie wɩaha gie:.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, Ŋmɩŋ die yɩna wa yiko yaa vuota? Ba piili a nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ, Bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩ keŋ a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ʋ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ tɩ dɩ, Ta bɩa wɩa nɩ ka yi wo yada.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Ama tɩ keŋ baarɩ ka nyɩŋ vuota jigiŋ, tɩ chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ aŋaŋ wudieke kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ baaŋ yi, dama vuoŋ mana die yi yada dɩ Jɔɔn seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Die wɩa die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ Yisa dɩ, Tɩ ka sɩba. Die ʋ balɩ yɩ ba, Die nɩŋ n kaaŋ balɩ nɩ vuodieke dɩ yɩna mɩŋ yiko maŋ yaa yie tʋʋmaha gie.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Lalɩa nɩ yile yaa gamma wɩarɩ gie? Daa wʋnyɩ die yalla ballɩ bale, ta die balɩ a yɩ jakʋʋrɩ dɩ, N bʋa, ga kʋakʋ ma a tʋŋ tʋʋma jinne.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Bʋa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, N kaaŋ ga ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tarɩgɩ ʋ sʋŋanyile ta ga kʋakʋ.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Die chɔɔŋ wa dɩ ga bʋabike jigiŋ a balɩ die gbaŋ gbaŋ a yɩ wa, ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, N chʋa n nan ga, ama die ʋ ka gaya.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, Ballɩ bale wo gie, ba jabɩa yine ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ yaalala dene? Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Jakʋʋrɩ. Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dʋaga hɔgʋkpana yaa daakpana nan woliŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma nɩ ma.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Dama Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die keŋ nɩ jigiŋ a dagɩ nɩ sievɩɩŋkʋ ama die nɩ ka yi wo yada, ama lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana aŋaŋ daakpana die dɩ yi wo yada. Die nɩ yene wɩaha gie mana kʋaŋ chaaŋ, nɩ ka tarɩgɩ nɩ sʋŋanyile ta yi wo yada.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a balɩ ba, Nɩ bɩ wʋŋ nandagɩŋ kaanɩ bɩbra. Tigiŋ tieŋ wʋnyɩ die benne a sʋʋ tɩɩ diekemba dɩ baaŋ nan nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ʋ kʋaŋ ma, a nagɩ daatɩ a kpie giliŋke, ta die a wʋnsɩ vɔrɩŋ, jigidieke ba baaŋ dɩ ŋmʋa nyɩŋnyɩŋka nyaabʋ a yie, ta kpii vɩɩŋ mi. Ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ tʋntʋntɩba nuusi me dɩ ba daansɩma. Dɩɩ yi a keŋ ŋmɩntɩ dɩ ba kpaa a yɩ wa ta kpaa ba tʋaŋ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ dɩa a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Die nyɩnyɩŋkaha kpaala saŋŋa die dɩ kenne tʋgɩ die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba ga tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ a ga tuo ʋ tʋaŋ.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Ama ta tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yigi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a nɩgɩ wʋnyɩ pam ta kʋʋ wʋnyɩ ta taa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ aŋaŋ tana.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Daa wa dɩ bɩ tʋŋ yɔŋɩsɩ bataŋ a tɩaŋ vuodiekemba ʋ wolinne a tʋŋ wa, ta tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yi be die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tʋŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ. A baarɩ, N sɩba baarɩ ba nan yɩ n bʋa wa jɩlɩma.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Ama tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yene ʋ bʋa wa die ba balɩ yɩ tamba, Ʋ faarɩ diiru wo wʋnna, nɩ vaa tɩ kʋʋ wa ta nyinti bɩrɩŋ tɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ta die ba yigi wo a nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ kʋakʋ lʋgɩŋ a kʋʋ wa.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, Die wɩa kʋakʋ tieŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ bɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi tʋntʋntɩba mi.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Ʋ nan kʋʋ banɩŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tielibe ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ vuogaasɩ, vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan yɩ wa ʋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkaha tʋaŋ a kpaala saŋŋa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ yɩ ba, Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die dɩ maagɩna die wo? Dɩ maagɩya dɩ Taŋ dieke mɩmɩɩrɩba die dɩ zetine ta baarɩ ba ka yaala ka wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ yi gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ yine die ta a faasɩ yi tɩ mamachi! .

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan gbatɩ ʋ naarɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ ta nagɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan vaa ʋ dii ka nyʋarɩ.

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi die dɩ wʋnna nandagaha die ba mɩŋŋɩ sɩba banɩŋ ba wɩa Yisa die taaŋ nandagɩrɩ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Die ba yaala ba yigi wo, ama die ba chɩga kpɩkpaakʋ ŋmaamɩŋ dama vuosi die yi yada a baarɩ dɩ Yisa yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balla wɩaha a kpatɩ naa, ʋ nyɩŋ Galili a ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ benne Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ chaakʋ.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Ta kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ die ʋ gbaaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna mi.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba hɔgʋlɩaŋ baŋ dieke die dɩ nagɩna ba popolisi a ga dɩ ba ga tuoli hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ die wo.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Ta Farasisi bataŋ dɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a yaala ba magɩsɩ wa, ta pɩasɩ wa, Tɩ mɩraha yɩ sieŋ dɩ dembiŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ta ʋ ka yi wɩɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Hɔgʋlɩaŋ baŋ wa sʋŋ, banʋ die yiwo gaantɩŋ, ta banʋ wa dɩaŋ dɩ yi sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba, Nɩ ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bala die wo? Dɩ Piiliku me, Ŋmɩŋ die naanna vuota, dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Gaantɩba die dɩ nagɩ ba popolisi ama ta die ka nagɩ kpaaŋ a gʋtɩ pɔgɩlɩ.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: ta baarɩ, Naa chɩaŋ ma dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ nan va ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta faarɩ taŋ ta ba vuosi bale wo lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: ama ta sɩsɩbɩrɩba die dɩ nagɩ kpaaŋ a siti siti a yi kɔlɩbɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ pɔgɩlɩ aŋaŋ popolisi.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa ba ka bɩ yi vuosi bale, ba wa yiwo vuobalɩmɩŋ. Die wɩa jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a lagɩŋ taŋ, vuota ka mʋ ʋ puoke.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Die hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa die dɩ yʋasɩ keniŋ, die gbieŋ die dɩ yigi be, ba mana die dɩ gʋʋrɩ.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Die Farasisi die dɩ pɩasɩ wa, Bɩa wɩa Mosisi die dɩ yɩ mɩra dɩ dembiŋ dɩ a keŋ a baa ʋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ sie ʋ woliŋ a yɩ wa gbanɩŋ a baarɩ dɩ ʋ ka bɩ yaala wa.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Ta die tanseese die ba wʋŋ kuli; Hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa wʋnna! Nɩ keŋ nyɩŋ a tuoli wo.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ tɩba dɩ hagɩrɩna wɩa die Mosisi die dɩ yɩ nɩ sieŋ dɩ nɩ nan bɩagɩ a zeti nɩ hɔɔŋ. Ama piiliku me da die ka sɩɩ.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Saŋka mi hɔgʋlɩaba baŋka mana die dɩ hagɩ a ŋmɩɩlɩ ba popoli dɩ jʋalɩ.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ keŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ da daakpana tʋʋma wɩa ta faarɩ hɔgʋ gaaŋ ʋ yiwo hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma; .

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Die hɔgʋlɩa gaantɩba die dɩ jʋʋsɩ sɩsɩbɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ kaaŋ yɩ tɩ nɩ kpaabʋ bɩta? Dama tɩ popolisi yaala a kpi mɩŋ.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Dɩ yiwo naa benne dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ sʋnsʋŋ die nɩŋ dembiŋ dɩ ka faarɩ hɔgʋ dɩ kpɩa.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Ta sɩsɩbɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Aayɩ, bʋ kaaŋ tʋgɩ tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ mana. Nɩ gamma vuodiekemba dɩ yalla daa wa jigiŋ a daa bʋtaŋ yɩ nɩ gbaŋ.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Da vuoŋ mana baaŋ bɩagɩ a tuo dagɩkʋ gie sie vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna ba sieŋ dɩ ba tuohe wo.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Saŋŋa dieke hɔgʋlɩa gaantɩba dɩ benne sieŋ me a gara dɩ ba daa kpaabʋ, ta hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ. Sɩsɩbɩrɩba banʋ wa die dɩ yine siri wo die dɩ dɩ hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa a ga juu tigidieke banwʋlaka die dɩ benne, ta die ba ligi sanʋarɩ.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Dama wɩa pam chɩaŋ ma dembisi bataŋ dɩ ka faara hɔɔŋ; bataŋ ba ŋaaŋ mɩɩrɩ ba ta ba ka yi dembisi; bataŋ dɩaŋ vuota yine be die; ta bataŋ dɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa ba zete; ama vuodieke dɩ nan bɩagɩ a tuo dagɩkʋ gie, ʋ tuoke vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋlɩa gaantɩba die dɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ, a zie sanʋarɩ jigiŋ a wasa, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, yuori sanʋarɩ a yɩ tɩ.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii ba ballɩ a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ba ma ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ ba ama die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nata ba.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Ama ta hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, n ka sɩba nɩ. .

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, Nɩ vaa ballɩbɩsɩsɩ keŋ n jigiŋ ta nɩ da kagɩ ba sieŋ dama ba chanchaalɩŋ sɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Die wɩa, nɩ sɩmma, dama nɩ ka sɩba daa dieke yaa saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala n keŋ.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi diisi ba ma ta die chʋŋ ga.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa nan bɩ sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tieŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bala ʋ dɩa, ta wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ a tɩa ba ligire dɩ ba tuo a dime nyʋʋŋ.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Saŋka mi dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, lalɩa maŋ nan yi ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Die ʋ yɩ ba wʋnyɩ mana ʋ sɩbɩna ʋ kpaŋŋɩnana ʋ gbaŋ dene; die ʋ nagɩ salɩma ligiribie tuse tuse anʋ a yɩ wʋnyɩ, ta die a nagɩ tuse tuse ale a yɩ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ, ta die a bɩ a nagɩ tusibalɩmɩŋ a yɩ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ. Ta die a nyɩŋ a ga ʋ dɩasɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa wɩa fʋ pɩasa mɩŋ wʋvɩɩna wɩa? Vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma vɩɩŋ na; ama fʋ yaala fʋ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ fʋ dɩa mɩraha.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Die yɔmʋ dieke die dɩ tuone tuse anʋ wa die dɩ nyɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ a nagɩ ligirehe a tʋŋ tʋʋma a bɩ a ye tuse anʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ a gʋtɩ.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Ta daa wa die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa, Mɩrɩ bɩaŋtɩ? Ta ʋ balɩ wa dɩ, Da keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ; da keŋ a yaalɩ vuoŋ hɔgʋ; da keŋ a gaarɩ; da keŋ a yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ siaratieŋ;.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Vuodieke die dɩ tuone tuse ale wo gbaŋ die dɩ nagɩha a tʋŋ tʋʋma a ye tuse ale a gʋtɩ.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma; ta chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ chone fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die wo.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Ama yɔmʋ dieke die dɩ tuone tusibalɩŋka die dɩ ga a tuu vɔrɩŋ a nagɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ ligirehe a lɔbɩrɩ mi.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Ta dalʋakɩrɩ die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, N wɔŋ a dɩɩ mɩraha gie mana mɩŋ. Wʋbɩa bɩna a tɩalɩ dɩ n yi a gʋtɩ.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Die dɩ wana a yʋasɩ pam, tigiri tieŋ wo die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ, ta wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba keŋ aŋ ʋ biisi, ta ʋ ye ba yene nyʋarɩ a mʋ die.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yaala fʋ bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩnɩŋ wusie ga a ga nagɩ fʋ nyinti mana a daa ta nagɩ ligirehe a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ta fʋ bɩrɩŋ ligiretieŋ arɩzanna ma, ta fʋ keŋ a dɩa n kʋaŋ.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Ta vuodieke die dɩ tuone tuse anʋ wa die dɩ a keŋ a juu saŋka mi, a nagɩ tuse anʋ dieke ʋ bɩna a ye wo, ta baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, die yɩ mɩŋ wa salɩma ligiribie tuse anʋ, ama n bɩ ye wo nyʋarɩ tuse anʋ a gʋtɩ.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Die dalʋakɩrɩ dɩ wʋnna naa wa ʋ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ ʋ chʋŋ ga, dama die ʋ faasɩ yiwo nyintitieŋ.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Die ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ balɩ wa, Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ, fʋnɩŋ yiwo yɔmʋvɩɩnɩŋ, ta bɩ tʋma vɩɩnɩŋ; mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ wa nyinti bɩta wa gie, die wɩa n nan nagɩ pam a yi fʋ nuusi me. Sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke n yalla, yiwo manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma Yisa die dɩ a balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, dɩ nan dɩ tʋa dɩ ligiretieŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Ta die vuodieke dɩaŋ die dɩ tuone tuse ale wo die dɩ keŋ a juu a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ye, die fʋ yɩ mɩŋ wa salɩma ligire bie tuse ale, ama n dii wo nyʋʋŋ a ye nyʋarɩ tuse ale a gʋtɩ.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: N bala nɩ bɩbra, dɩ nan yi mɔlɩ dɩ nyɔgɩnɩ daagɩ garɩyɩɩŋ vɔrɩŋ ma a nyɩŋ a tɩaŋ ligiretieŋ dɩ baaŋ nan juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo dɩ balɩ wa, Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ, fʋ yiwo yɔmʋvɩɩnɩŋ ta bɩ tʋma vɩɩnɩŋ, mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ wa nyinti bɩta wa gie, die wɩa n nan nagɩ pam a yi fʋ nuusi me. Sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke n yalla, yiwo manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna naa die dɩ paalɩ a yi be mamachi die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Mɩnɩa baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Die yɔmʋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ tuone tusibalɩŋka die dɩ keŋ a juu a baarɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n sɩba dɩ fʋnɩŋ yiwo nyɩnyɩrɩtʋ, a gobe jigidieke fʋ kana a ka bʋrɩya, ta bɩ lagɩsa nyindiike jigidieke die fʋ kana a ka jatɩ nyɩŋbʋra.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Die Yisa dɩ kɔtɩ ʋ sikpeŋ a daansɩ ba ta die balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Vuota nɩŋ jigiŋ dɩ tʋa mɩŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi jaaŋ mana.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Die wɩa ŋmaamɩŋ die yalla mɩŋ die maŋ nyɩŋ a nagɩ fʋ ligirebu a lɔbɩrɩ tɩŋgbaŋ. Die wɩa, ka wʋnna, tuo fʋ jaaŋ.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Die Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ va wa tɩ nyinti mana ta keŋ dɩ fʋ. Bɩa yine tɩ nyʋarɩ.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Die wɩa ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yɔmʋtɔgʋ aŋaŋ walɩnkpɩatieŋ; sɩba baarɩ n yiwo vuodieke dɩ gobinene jigidieke n kana ka bʋrɩya ta lagɩsa nyindiike jigidieke n kana ka jatɩ nyɩŋbʋra.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ keŋ kalɩ n naara gbaŋtɩ ma aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ dʋnɩa haalɩkʋ ma, nɩnɩŋ die dɩ yine n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩaŋ nan dɩ kalɩ naara gbaŋtɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale mi a dii Izara buuriŋ baŋ aŋaŋ ale wo sarɩya.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: die nɩŋ bɩa yine ta fʋ ka nagɩ n ligirebu a paŋŋɩ aŋ ba yiŋŋi tuŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ nyʋarɩ? N tɩnna a keŋ naa wa n tɩŋ nan tuo n jaabʋ ta nyʋarɩ diisi ka ma.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Ta vuodieke mana dɩ vana ʋ tigiŋ yaa ʋ nɩmballɩ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ yaa ʋ ballɩ yaa ʋ kʋatɩ n wɩa, ʋ nan daansɩ ye a chanchaaŋ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga, ta bɩ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Die wɩa nɩ tuo ligirebu a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ a nagɩ ka yɩ vuodieke dɩ yalla tuse baŋ wa.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Ama vuosi pam die dɩ wolinne a bie nɩŋŋa nan wɩarɩ kʋaŋ ta vuosi pam die dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaŋ nan tɩaŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ yalla nyinti, ba nan ye nyinti pam a gʋtɩ amʋ ba faasɩ yallɩma pam. Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ wone jaaŋ ba nan tuo bɩta gbaŋ ʋ yalla wa a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ama yɔmʋtɔgʋ wa nɩŋ, nɩ nagɩ wa a vigi taaŋ yeŋ me lɩmɩŋ ma, jigidieke ʋ baaŋ nan kʋŋ ta ŋɔba nyɩna.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ keŋ keŋ aŋaŋ n hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ n malakasisi n nan dɩ kalɩ n naara gbanɩŋ ma aŋaŋ jɩlɩma pam.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Saŋka mi dʋnɩa vuosi mana mana nan lagɩsɩ n nɩŋŋa, mi n nan puo be bule, sɩba yiyagɩrʋ dɩ ŋaana a puo yiise a nyɩŋ bie sʋŋ die wo.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: N nan nagɩ yiisehe a zieŋ n nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, ta nagɩ biehe a zieŋ n nuugalɩ chaaŋ.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Saŋka mi manɩŋ naaŋ wa nan balɩ yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne n nuudiigiri chaakʋ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine alibarika yɩ, nɩ keŋ a juu naarɩ dieke ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ naanna a gbarɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ dʋnɩa piiliŋ me.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ Yisa Masia wɩa die dɩ piiline dene wʋnna.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: dama kɔŋ die yalla mɩŋ die nɩ yɩ mɩŋ nyindiike; ta nyanyuule die dɩ yalla mɩŋ, die nɩ yɩ mɩŋ nyaaŋ maŋ nyu. Die n yiwo chaanʋ ta nɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tuo mɩŋ nɩ tige me a yi mɩŋ chaantɩ.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ maagɩna die wo: Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ ye, n baaŋ n tʋŋ wa n tʋntʋntʋ aŋ ʋ woliŋ a dɩa fʋ nɩŋŋa a wʋnsɩ fʋ sieti a yɩ fʋ.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Die n chʋŋ wa yɔrɩ die nɩ yɩ mɩŋ garɩtɩ, ta die maŋ yʋagɩ ta nɩ suŋŋi mɩŋ, die maŋ juu dansarɩka sʋŋ ta nɩ keŋ kaagɩ mɩŋ.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Vuoŋ benne haagɩŋ ma a keese a mʋʋla Nɩ wʋnsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ sieti ta vaa a tʋgɩsɩ a yɩ wa. .

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Womi ŋmɩŋchɩgɩrɩba gie nan yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta kɔŋ yallɩ fʋ ta tɩ yɩ fʋ nyindiike? Yaa saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta nyanyuule dɩ yallɩ fʋ ta tɩ yɩ fʋ nyaaŋ fʋ nyu?.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuotamba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die bie wo haagɩŋ ma a mʋʋla a yɩa vuotamba dɩ ba vaa ba wʋbɩatɩ ta ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ aŋ Ŋmɩŋ vaa a chaa ba ba wʋbɩatɩ.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta fʋ yi chaanʋ die tɩ tuo fʋ tɩ tige me a yi fʋ chaantɩ? Yaa saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta chʋŋ yɔrɩ ta tɩ yɩ fʋ garɩtɩ?.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Ta vuotamba pam die dɩ nyɩŋ Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ Jerusalemi tɩŋgbaŋ ma, a keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ a balɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ tʋʋma a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta Jɔɔn dɩ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩŋ ma.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta yʋagɩ ta tɩ suŋŋi fʋ? Yaa fʋ benne dansarɩka sʋŋ tɩ keŋ kaagɩ fʋ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Jɔɔn die yeegi wo nyiŋyeeki diekemba die ba nagɩna nyɔgɩnɩ kobiti a wʋnsɩ, ta bɩ bɔbɩ gbanɩŋ ʋ chɩaŋ ma, die ʋ nyindiike die dɩ yi sankpara aŋaŋ siesi.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Ta manɩŋ Naaŋ wa nan yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, wudieke die nɩ yine a yɩ vuodieke dɩ yine zɔɔlɩntieŋ nɩ nɩmballɩ sʋnsʋŋ wʋnyɩ wa die nɩ yi a yɩ wa manɩŋ.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuosisi dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ hagɩrɩna a tɩaŋ mɩŋ balla ʋ keŋ n kʋaŋ chaaŋ, n ka tʋgɩ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta vʋarɩ ʋ nɩɩra guune gbaŋ.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne n nuugalɩ ka chaakʋ, Nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie, nɩ vɩɩna aŋaŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ, nɩ chʋnna ga ta va mɩŋ. Nɩ gamma a ga juu boli dieke dɩ kana a ka kpise ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wʋnsɩna a gbarɩ Sitaani aŋaŋ ʋ vuosi wo.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Manɩŋ n sɩa wa aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, ama wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ a sɩɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Dama kɔŋ die yallɩ mɩŋ ta die nɩ mʋŋ mɩŋ nyindiike, ta nyanyuule die dɩ yallɩ mɩŋ ama die nɩ mʋŋ mɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya die Yisa dɩ nyɩŋ Nazeriti tɩŋ dɩ benne Galili me wo a keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ, ta Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: Die n yine chaanʋ, ta nɩ ka saagɩ a tuo mɩŋ nɩ tige me, ta die maŋ chʋŋ yɔrɩ ta nɩ mʋŋ mɩŋ garɩtɩ, ta die yʋagɩ ta nɩ ka suŋŋi mɩŋ, die maŋ juu dansarɩka ta nɩ ka keŋ kaagɩ mɩŋ.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnnana nyaabʋ ma wa ʋ ye ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ yuorine, Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ dɩ keŋ a sʋʋna ʋ sikpeŋ me sɩba ŋmarɩŋ.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Saŋka mi ba nan yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, saŋŋa bɩa die tɩ ye fʋ ta kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ fʋ, yaa nyanyuule die dɩ yallɩ fʋ, yaa fʋ yi chaanʋ, yaa fʋ chʋŋ yɔrɩ, yaa fʋ yʋagɩ, yaa fʋ bie dansarɩka sʋŋ ta tɩ ka suŋŋi fʋ.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine n Bʋachoti; fʋnɩŋ n nine dɩ suuli.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Saŋka mi n nan yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie n bala nɩ, die nɩ zetine nɩ suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieŋ nɩ nɩmballɩ sʋnsʋŋ wʋnyɩ wa, die manɩŋ nɩ zeti suŋŋiŋ dene.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ die dɩ nagɩ Yisa a ga haagɩŋ ma; Die ʋ bie wo haagɩrɩ ma a ga tʋgɩ daraa baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa, ta Sitaani die dɩ magɩsɩ wa.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Vuosi gie nan ga juu tɩbɩdatɩ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma, ama ŋmɩŋchɩgɩrɩba nan ga juu miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ sʋŋ.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: Yisa aŋaŋ haagɩŋ nɩŋ dɔŋɩsɩ die benne mi; ama ta Ŋmɩŋ malakaka die dɩ sʋʋŋ a suŋŋi wo.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Die ba yigine Jɔɔn a yi dansarɩka ma kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa die ga wa Galili me a bala Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha mi.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: a baarɩ Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ balla ka keŋ wo gbigiye mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha gie yada ta va nɩ wʋbɩatɩ.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Yisa die dɩ chʋnnana Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgɩrɩ die ʋ ye Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Andurusi, ta ba taana nɩɩŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma dama die ba yiwo zaasɩyigiriŋ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ʋ balɩ ba, Nɩ dɩa mɩŋ ta n nan vaa nɩ sʋgɩma vuosi a kiere n jigiŋ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Lagɩ lagɩ ba vaa ba nɩɩtɩ ta hagɩ a dɩ wa.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Ta bɩ ga nɩŋŋa bɩta ta a ye Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, Zebedi ballɩ, ta ba bie wo ba haarɩŋ sʋŋ a wʋnsa ba nɩɩtɩ.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Ʋ wa ba, die ba va ba chɔɔŋ wa Zebedi aŋaŋ vuosisi die dɩ tʋnnana a yɩa ba wa ta hagɩ a dɩ wa.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Kapenɔmi; ta Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ dɩ keŋ ba ga Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku me, ʋ piili a daga vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Ʋ dagɩkʋ gie die dɩ yi be mamachi pam, dama ʋ dagɩ ba sɩba vuodieke dɩ yalla yiko, die ka ka sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ ŋaana a daga ba wa.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Womi ta dembi dieke jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ yalla wa die dɩ keŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku sʋŋ.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: a keesi pam ta baarɩ dɩ, Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti, bɩa fʋ yaala tɩ jigiŋ? Keŋ dɩ fʋ chʋʋsɩ tɩ mɩŋ? N sɩba vuodieke dɩ yine fʋ, yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋŋ kasɩ vuoŋ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ natɩ wa, Tarɩ watɩ ta va wa.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ paalɩ a dɔŋ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ pam ta taaŋ keesiŋ pam ta die nyɩŋ ʋ ma.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Die, die dɩ yi ba mana mamachi ta ba pɩasa ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ, Bɩa wʋnna? A yiwo wʋhaala? Ʋ yaa yiko ta yɩa jɩmbɩatɩ nʋaŋ ta a tuose nʋarɩ.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa, Yisa saaŋ die dɩ dɩa Galili tɩŋgbaŋ mana.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Die ba dene a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku sʋŋ die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ ga a juu Simoni aŋaŋ Andurusi tigiŋ me.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Ta Simoni hanɩɩŋ hɔgʋ die dɩ dʋa a yʋagɩ, die ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ faasɩ tuuli, ba balɩ Yisa ʋ yʋagɩbʋ gie wɩa.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Yisa dɩ ga a yigi ʋ nuuŋ ta suŋŋi wo ʋ hagɩ kalɩ, ʋ yʋagɩbʋ mana dɩ kpatɩ ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ fɩalɩ, ta die ʋ hagɩ a yi chaantɩ a yɩ ba.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Ta dɩnda daarɩ mi ŋmɩŋ die dɩ june, vuotamba die dɩ chii yʋagɩtieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ yalla ba a kieŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Ta tɩka gie vuosi mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ a keŋ suuli tigiri me.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Die ʋ vaa vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ, ta bɩ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩ vuotamba pam ma, ama ʋ ka saaga dɩ jɩmbɩatɩ balɩ wɩɩŋ, dama die a sɩba vuodieke ʋ yine mɩŋ.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Yisa die dɩ woliŋ a nyɩŋ tigiri gie me ta kparaasɩ ye ka kʋma, ta ga haagɩŋ ma a ga jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Ta Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ dɩa a yaala ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: die ba yene wo wo ba balɩ wa, Vuoŋ mana yaala ba ye jigidieke fʋ benne.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Sie tɩ ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ diekemba dɩ gbigine giena wa. Ntaala n balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mi gbaŋ, dama die wɩa maŋ keŋ.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die ʋ dɩdɩa Galili tɩka mana mana, a ga bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juone nɩŋ mana ta die a yaga jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi me.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Dembiŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ gobine gamɩŋ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa, ta jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ suŋŋi wo. Gaŋkʋ nɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ dʋŋŋʋ fʋ gbaaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi Yisa ʋ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ wa ta balɩ wa, N saagɩya. Fʋ yʋagɩbʋ kpatɩya mɩŋ.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Womi lagɩ lagɩ daa wa yʋagɩbʋ die dɩ kpatɩ bʋnyɩ, ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka die dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi a sɩɩ sɩba die ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka die dɩ wolinne a sɩɩ die wo.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yi ʋ ga lagɩ lagɩ ama ta a paalɩ a balɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Wʋmma, sɩmma fʋ keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ wɩaha gie. Ama gamma a ga nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ wa a vaa ʋ jɩɩŋ fʋ aŋ fʋ kaabɩ kaabɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balla Mosisi yaa gamma gbaanɩŋ jigiri, aŋ vuoŋ mana seŋ a sɩmma a baarɩ wusie fʋ yʋagɩtɩtɩ kpatɩya mɩŋ.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Ama daa wa die dɩ ga a bala wɩaha gie jigiŋ mana mana. Wusie, die ʋ seŋ ga a balɩ wa die amʋ Yisa die ka bɩ a bɩagɩ juu tɩka sʋŋ vuosi me. Ta wɩarɩ jigidieke vuosi die dɩ worine, vuosi die dɩ nyɩna jigiŋ mana mana a kieŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yisa die dɩ balla wɩaha gie mana a kpatɩ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ vʋntʋŋ yine gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga, ta ba nan nagɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa a yi vuosi nuusi me dɩ ba kpaasɩ mɩŋ a dɩɩsɩ dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Saŋka mi die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ Kayafasi tigiri me, vuodieke die dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: Die ba saŋ ba baaŋ yi die a lɔbɩrɩ a yigi Yisa a kʋʋ wa.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Die ba baarɩ, Tɩ ka mʋ tɩ yigi wo dʋʋgaka saŋŋa, tɩ yi die vuosisi nan yi watɩ aŋ waagɩŋ nan.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Die Yisa die benne Betani tɩka ma wa, Simoni vuodieke die dɩ yine gamɩŋ tigiŋ me wo.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: die hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɔgɩlɩ tulaari bʋnyɩ ligire dɩ faasɩna a tʋa a sitibu a yi Yisa sikpeŋ me saŋŋa dieke ʋ kala a die nyindiike.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yene naa, die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ. Die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Bɩa ʋ chʋʋsa tulaaribu die?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Tulaaribu gie tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ daa ligirikpɩɩma a nagɩ ligirehe a suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieliŋ.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ wudieke ba balala, ta balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ mugise hɔgʋ wa gie dene? Wʋvɩɩnɩŋ ʋ yi a yɩ mɩŋ dene.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Zɔɔlɩntieliŋ nɩŋ nan dɩ beri nɩ jigiŋ daaŋ mana ama manɩŋ n kaaŋ dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ daaŋ mana.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Tulaari dieke ʋ sitine a yi n ma wa, ʋ yi a gbarɩ wa n guule.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, jigidieke mana ba bala ba mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ba nan balɩ wudieke ʋ yine wo a tɩɩnsɩ ʋ wɩa.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ, vuodieke die dɩ dine Judasi Asikaroti wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha jigiŋ.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: a baarɩ, Bɩa nɩ nan yɩ mɩŋ dɩɩ yi maŋ dagɩ nɩ nɩ baaŋ nan yi die a yigi Yisa wa? Die ba biisi salɩmayɩala ligiribie baŋɩsɩ-taa a yɩ wa.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Die a nyɩŋ daarɩ mi Judasi die wa a paalɩ a yaala sievɩɩnɩŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ Yisa a yi ba nuusi me.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Ta paanʋ dieke die ba kana a ka yi dabɔtɩ dʋʋga piiliku daarɩ, ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, Sɩa fʋ yaala tɩ ga wʋnsɩ a yɩ fʋ a gbara gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiike daraaŋ.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gamma tɩka sʋŋ daa wʋnyɩ tigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ dɩdagɩrʋ tʋnna tɩ dɩ tɩ keŋ balɩ fʋ dɩ ʋ saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ, ta ʋ yaala wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka nyindiikehe fʋ tigiŋ me. .

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ dɩ ʋ bala ba die wo, ta wʋnsɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka diile siri.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo die dɩ sʋʋŋ kalɩ dɩ ba dii.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Die ba dinene wo Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ wʋnyɩ bala ʋ posi n chɩaŋ.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ, die ba piili a pɩasa wa wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ, dɩ N Yɔmʋtieŋ, wusie n ka yi tama dɩ manɩŋ fʋ daga die.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Vuodieke maŋ aŋaŋ wa dɩ lune kpalɩ gie mi wo bala ʋ posi n chɩaŋ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan kpi sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ die dɩ dagɩna die wo, ama wʋbɩaŋ bie vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ posi n chɩaŋ wa ma! Ba tɩŋ ka mɩɩrɩ dɩ tieŋ gbaŋ dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Saŋka mi Judasi vuodieke die dɩ bala ʋ posi ʋ chɩaka gbaŋ dɩ baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, manɩŋ fʋ daga? Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Wa, dɩ yiwo fʋnɩŋ.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Die ba yene ko die, Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋ, a waasɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta yi ke gbieri gbieri a nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, ta baarɩ, Nɩ tuo a dii. N nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wʋnna.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Ta die bɩ nagɩ nyaaŋchibiŋ aŋaŋ daaŋ, a pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die a nagɩ ka a yɩ ba ta baarɩ, Nɩ mana tuo a nyu.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Gembu yine n zɩŋ dieke dɩ bala ka yi Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ zie aŋaŋ wusie, zɩŋ dieke dɩ bala ka nyɩŋ n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma vuosi pam wɩa, aŋ ka vaa Ŋmɩŋ vaa a chaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: N bala nɩ, n kaaŋ bɩ nyu daabʋ gie, sie daa dieke manɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ dɩ bala tɩ lagɩsɩ a nyu daahaalɩbʋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Die ba wa chɩa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ yɩlɩ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba chʋŋ ga Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ bɩbra Yisa dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me. Daa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die benne mi a yallɩ nuukpiikiŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Saŋka mi die Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Yuku gie nɩ mana nan chɩgɩ ta va mɩŋ, dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan kʋʋ yiyagɩrʋ wa ta yiisehe ʋ yagɩnana wa jaasɩ jigiŋ mana.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Farasisi vuosi dɩaŋ die benne mi a yaala wɩɩŋ ba nagɩ a yigi Yisa, die wɩa die ba paalɩ a paala wa dɩ ba ye sɩba ʋ nan gbaaŋ daa wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Ama maŋ a keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan woliŋ nɩ nɩŋŋa a ga Galili tɩŋ ma.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ nuukpiikiri tieŋ, Keŋ tɩaŋ a zie nɩŋŋa.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Ta Piita die dɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ mana dɩ chɩgɩ ta va fʋ, ama manɩŋ n kaaŋ chɩgɩ ta va fʋ.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ daadamba, Bɩa mʋna tɩ yi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ? Tɩ mʋ tɩ yi vɩɩnɩŋ yaa, tɩ yi bɩaŋ? Tɩ mʋ tɩ gbatɩ miivoli yaa, tɩ kʋʋ? Ama die ba ka bɩagɩ ye wɩɩŋ a balɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, jinne yuku gie, kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma, ta chiisi mɩŋ wo bʋtaa ma dɩ zɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Yisa die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta daansɩ ba a keŋ tɩaŋ aŋaŋ die mana yɔrɩ ta die ʋ chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ dama die ba yiwo vuotɔgɩtɩ. Womi ʋ balɩ daa wa, Tɩɩrɩ fʋ nuuke.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ ŋaŋ fʋ dɩ baa tɩ kpi gbaŋ, n kaaŋ chiisi dɩ n zɩ fʋ. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ balɩ die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Ʋ tɩɩrɩka, a ye gbaanɩŋ. Farasisi vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ a ga ba aŋaŋ naaŋ Herodi vuosi dɩ ye tamba a lagɩŋ taŋ a yaala sieti ba baaŋ nan nagɩ a kʋʋ Yisa.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga jigidieke die ba wasɩnana Getisemani, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ kala giena ta n ga nɩ nɩŋŋa a ga jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ hagɩ a sʋʋŋ ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgaha ta vuotamba pam die dɩ dɩɩ ʋ kʋaŋ, vuodiekemba die dɩ nyɩnna Galili aŋaŋ Judia.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Ta die nagɩ Piita aŋaŋ Zebedi ballɩ bale wo a gʋtɩ ʋ ma. Die ʋ sʋŋ die dɩ faasɩ a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: aŋaŋ Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Idumia aŋaŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩŋ gaaŋ aŋaŋ Taya aŋaŋ Sadoni tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta ba dala pam ta lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ; die ba wʋnna dɩ ʋ yi wo tʋntʋnkpɩɩma wɩa, die ba keŋ ʋ jigiŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgɩŋ.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba, Sʋgɩchʋʋsɩ dieke dɩ benne ʋ sʋŋ ma wa faasɩ dala mɩŋ a yaala ka kʋʋ mɩŋ. Nɩ kala gie ta yesime aŋaŋ manɩŋ.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Ta ʋ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba nagɩ haarɩŋ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ juu a kala ta dagɩ vuosi gie Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, amʋ ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kpaŋŋɩ wa.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Die ʋ chʋŋ a ga nɩŋŋa bɩta, a gbirigi tɩŋgbaŋ ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, dɩ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi, fʋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ wahalaka gie me! Ama daa vaa n dʋŋŋʋ yi, ama vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: dama die ʋ yine yʋagɩtieliŋ pam die dɩ ye gbaama wɩa, die dɩ yi vuodiekemba mana mana dɩ yʋagɩna die dɩ kieŋ dɩ ba gbɩ wa.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi ga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataa wa jigiŋ ta ye ta ba gʋʋra; die ʋ pɩasɩ Piita, Die wɩa nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yesime aŋaŋ mɩŋ saŋŋa bɩta gbaŋ?.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Vuodiekemba jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla dɩ keŋ ye wo saŋŋa mana, ba ŋaaŋ nan wa ʋ nɩŋŋa ta nata ta baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Nɩ yesime ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ amʋ nɩ da keŋ juu magɩsɩŋ ma, dama nɩ haalɩtɩtɩ yaala a yi jadieke dɩ vɩɩna, ama nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋɩsɩsɩ ka hagɩrɩ.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Ta ʋ die dɩ balɩ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba da keŋ mʋʋlɩ a yɩ vuosi.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Die ʋ bɩ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ bule ma a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, dɩɩ yi wahala dieke dɩ kienene naa dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a tɩaŋ, sie n diike, fʋ vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩ taŋ sikpeŋ ta wa wa vuodiekemba ʋ yaalɩnana dɩ ba keŋ, die ba keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Die ʋ bɩ yiŋŋi keŋ, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die bɩ gʋʋra dama gbieŋ die dɩ faasɩna yallɩ ba pam.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Yisa die dɩ vʋarɩ vuosi baŋ aŋaŋ bale, a wa ba tʋntʋntɩba, dɩ ba bemme mi ʋ jigiŋ aŋ ʋ tʋmma ba, ba mʋʋlɩma ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Die ʋ bɩ va ba, ta ga a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ bʋtaa ma a balɩ ʋ wolinne a jʋʋsa wudieke wo.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: ta bɩ yallɩma yiko a vʋarɩma jɩmbɩatɩ vuotamba ma.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Saŋka mi die ʋ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ a keŋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ye ko a gʋʋra ta voose mɩŋ? Nɩ ye, saŋka gbigiye mɩŋ. Ba nan nagɩ wa manɩŋ vuota Bʋa a yi vuobɩatɩ nuusi me.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Vuotamba baŋ aŋaŋ bale dieke ʋ vʋarɩna Saara wʋnna; Simoni, vuodieke Yisa die yɩna wa Piita aŋaŋ.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Nɩ hagɩ aŋ tɩ ga. Nɩ ye, daa dieke dɩ posine n chɩaka gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, vuodiekemba die dɩ yine Zebedi ballɩ; ta Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba Bʋanejesi, ka chɩaŋ yiwo nɩɩŋ natɩŋ ballɩ;.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Ʋ nʋaŋ die ye ka nan tɩŋgbaŋ, ta Judasi, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ mi, aŋaŋ kpɩkpaaŋ, bataŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ jɩbɩwagɩtɩ ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ dengbile, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha tʋnna ba.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: aŋaŋ Andurusi aŋaŋ Filipi, aŋaŋ Batolomiwo, aŋaŋ Matiwo, aŋaŋ Tomasi, aŋaŋ Jemisi vuodieke die dɩ yine Alifusi bʋa aŋaŋ Tadeusi, aŋaŋ Simoni vuodieke die dɩ yaalala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yallɩma ba gbaŋ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Judasi vuodieke die dɩ posine ʋ chɩaka aŋaŋ vuosisi die wɔŋ balɩ zieŋ mɩŋ dɩ, Maŋ keŋ a mʋgɩsɩ vuodieke kaamɩŋ, daa dieke nɩ yaalala wonde, nɩ yigi wo.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: aŋaŋ Judasi Asikaroti vuodieke die dɩ nyɩnna Yisa kʋaka.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Judasi die dɩ kenne tʋgɩ, ʋ ga Yisa nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, aŋ fʋ ŋmɩɩ, ta mʋgɩsɩ ʋ kaamɩŋ.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ kuli; ta daadamba pam die dɩ lagɩsɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ ta die ka yaa saŋŋa dɩ ba dii nyindiike.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, N zʋa, yi fʋ balla fʋ yi die wo lagɩ lagɩ! Womi die ba a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ yigi wo.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Ʋ tigiŋ vuosi die dɩ wʋnna die wo, ba keŋ dɩ ba yigi wo, dama vuotamba die bala dɩ ʋ yi wo yɩɩŋyaaŋ.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ vʋarɩ jɩbɩwaŋ a chɩa gobi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ yɔmʋ tɩbɩŋ a taaŋ.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩnna Jerusalemi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli die yalla wa ʋ bɩagɩ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ daadamba ma, dɩ yiwo Beelizebuli yiko wɩa.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, Nagɩ fʋ jɩbɩwaŋkʋ a yiŋŋi a sʋʋ ka fɔgʋ ma, dama vuodieke dɩ wagɩnana aŋaŋ jɩbɩŋ, ŋaaŋ kpi wo jɩbɩka kuŋ.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ wa dɩdagɩrɩba gie dɩ ba keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, ta die yɩ ba nandagɩŋ, a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Sitaani nan saagɩ a vʋara ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me?.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ n tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a wa n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ suŋŋi mɩŋ, aŋ bʋnyɩ ʋ tɩŋ nan tʋŋ malakasi a tɩaŋ tuse baŋ aŋaŋ ale?.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Naaŋ vuosi dɩ yiŋŋi a waga tamba, ba naarɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Ama maŋ yi die, lalɩa wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa dɩ naa nan yiwo, keŋ yi wusie.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi tigiŋ vuosi dɩ yiŋŋi a waga tamba, ba tigiri kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ, Nɩ mʋ dɩ nɩ keŋ wo aŋaŋ jɩbɩwagɩtɩ aŋaŋ dengbile dɩ nɩ keŋ a yigi mɩŋ sɩba n yiwo gbɩgbatɩrʋ? Daaŋ mana die n bie wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daga ta nɩ ka yigi mɩŋ.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Ta Sitaani dɩ yiŋŋi a waga aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie, ʋ kpatɩŋ wonde.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Ama naa mana yiye mɩŋ amʋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa keŋ yi wusie. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana dɩ chɩgɩ ta va wa.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a ga hagɩrɩtieŋ tigiŋ me a ga gaarɩ ʋ nyinti, sie ʋ woliŋ bɔbɩ wa ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ gaarɩ ʋ tigiŋ nyinti mana.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ yigine Yisa wa die dɩ yallɩ wa a ga Kayafasi tigiŋ, vuodieke die dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ, mi die mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa chaa vuosi ba wʋbɩatɩ aŋaŋ ba wʋtɔgɩbɩatɩ mana;.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Die ba yalla Yisa a gara wa, die Piita dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a bie saasaa, a ga tʋgɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ gbaaŋ sʋŋ. Die ʋ juu gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩba dɩ ʋ ye wudieke dɩ balla ka yi.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: ama vuodieke mana dɩ balɩ wʋtɔgɩbɩatɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa naada yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋraha mana die dɩ yaala dɩ ba ye vuodieke dɩ baaŋ chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ yaa gamma wudieke Yisa dɩ yine aŋ ba nagɩ a baarɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ ta kʋʋ wa.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Yisa die balɩ wa wɩaha gie, dama die ba baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Daadaŋ pam die dɩ keŋ nɩŋŋa a chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ wa, ama die mana yɔrɩ, die ba ka ye wudieke ba baaŋ nan nagɩ a yigi wo. Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die dembisi bale die dɩ keŋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ dɩ.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa nuŋ wo aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ die dɩ keŋ ta zie yeŋ me, ta tʋŋ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ wa wa mi.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: Daa wa gie die baarɩ dɩ ʋ nan bɩagɩ a kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ a taaŋ ta yiŋŋi mɩɩ ka daraa ataa daraaŋ.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Daadamba die dɩ taanna a giliŋ wo wo die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa, Fʋ naa aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmballɩ bie yeŋ me a yaala fʋ.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ hagɩ zie a baarɩ, Wo wɩɩŋ a baa fʋ balɩ yaa gamma wudieke ba balla yaa gamma fʋ wɩa naa.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine n naa, ta mɩnɩa yine n nɩmballɩ.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ama die Yisa dɩ tarɩ. Ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ wa, Balɩ tɩ miivolitieŋ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma, fʋ yine Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Ta daansɩ daadamba die dɩ kalla ʋ jigiri ta baarɩ dɩ, N nuŋ aŋaŋ n nɩmballɩ benne giena.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Wa, fʋnɩŋ fʋ balla. Ama n bala nɩ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma nɩ nan daansɩ ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa ta n kalɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, ta bɩ ye ta n bie nɩɩŋmara sʋŋ a keŋ sʋʋna.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ yinene Ŋmɩŋ choti, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine n nɩmbʋa aŋaŋ n taa aŋaŋ n nuŋ.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ datɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nyinyeeke a chɩɩrɩ ta natɩ dɩ, Ʋ naga ʋ gbaŋ a magɩsa Ŋmɩŋ! Tɩ ka bɩ yaala daansɩa. Nɩ mana wɔŋ wʋŋ ʋ nagɩna ʋ gbaŋ a magɩsa Ŋmɩŋ die.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Lalɩa nɩ yile? Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Ʋ mʋ ʋ kpi mɩŋ.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Womi die ba piile a tɩɩra nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yie ʋ nine me, ta nɩgɩ wa; ta vuodiekemba die dɩ falala wa wa.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: dɩ baarɩ, Fʋnɩŋ Masia, bugi a dagɩ vuodieke dɩ nɩgɩna fʋ wa.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Piita die dɩ kala Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ gbaakʋ ma wa, die tigiri mi havʋʋbiŋ tʋntʋntʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a keŋ balɩ dɩ, Fʋ gbaŋ die gʋtɩ wa Yisa vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Galili wo ma.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ bɩbra die Yisa die dɩ kalɩ a daga wɩa Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgɩŋ chaaŋ. Daadamba die dɩ gilinne wo die paalɩ a dala mɩŋ die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ a ga juu haarɩkʋ sʋŋ a bie nyaaŋ sʋŋ, ta vaa vuosisi a zieŋ nyaabʋ kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Ama ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka sɩba wa ba mana nɩŋŋa. Dɩ, N ka sɩba vuodieke nɩ balala wa wɩa.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ nandaga a dagɩ ba wɩa pam.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Die ʋ nyɩŋ ga yeŋ me, mi havʋʋbiŋ tʋntʋntʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bɩ ye wo a balɩ yɩ vuosisi dɩ, Daa wa gie dɩaŋ die gʋtɩ wa Yisa vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti wa ma.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Ʋ dagɩkʋ sʋŋ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ: Nɩ wʋmma, daa wʋnyɩ die nyɩnna a ga dɩ ʋ bʋrɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Die Piita die dɩ bɩ chiisike aŋaŋ huusi dɩ, N ka sɩba daa wa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: Die ʋ bʋrɩnana ʋ kʋakʋ ma wa, nyɩŋbʋraha ataŋ die dɩ nan sieku me, nembisi die dɩ keŋ diihe.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Die dɩ bɩna a yi bɩta, vuodiekemba dɩ zene mi wo dɩ keŋ Piita jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, Wusie, yiwo ba wʋnyɩ dama fʋ bala sɩba Galili vuoŋ.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Nyɩŋbʋra ataŋ die dɩ nan tanjaalɩŋ ma jigidieke tantɩ pam die dɩ worine. Nyɩŋbʋraha die dɩ nyʋʋŋ lagɩ lagɩ, dama tantɩ die ka dala.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Womi Piita dɩ huu dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ datɩ n tɩbɩŋ dɩɩ yi maŋ ka bala wusie! N ka sɩba daa wa mi! Womi kparaaŋ die dɩ kʋŋ.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ zie die a mana die dɩ kpi dama die a dagɩrɩsɩ ka sʋʋŋya.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: die Piita dɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke Yisa dɩ bala a yɩ wa wa dɩ, Kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma, nan chiisi mɩŋ bʋtaa dɩ ka sɩba mɩŋ. Ta Piita die dɩ nyɩŋ a kʋŋ pam.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Ataŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma, haŋgɔɔsɩ die dɩ bɩrɩŋ a nyagɩha a ka bɩagɩ yi nyɩŋbʋra.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Ama ta nyɩŋbʋraha ataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ ma a mɩŋŋɩ a nyʋʋŋ, a bɩrɩŋ a yi nyɩŋbʋra. Ataŋ die nyɩŋ wa zaabie baŋɩsɩ-taa taa, ataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩŋ baŋɩsɩ-yʋaba yʋaba, ta ataŋ dɩaŋ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga.
