eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Womi die Yisa dɩ balɩ ba, Die wɩa vuodieke mana dɩ yallɩ tɩba a nan wʋŋ, ʋ wʋmma.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Yisa die dɩ yine ʋ nyɩɩna a kalɩ, vuodiekemba die dɩ benne mi a wʋŋ ʋ balɩkʋ bataŋ die dɩ lagɩŋ a gʋtɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ma a ga ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ dagɩ ba nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ maŋ baa n dagɩ nɩ dene, ama vuotɩalɩkaha nɩŋ nan wʋŋ wɩaha gie mana mana nandaga sʋŋ.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: Amʋ Ba nan dɩ daansa a daansa ama ba kaaŋ ye jaaŋ; ba nan wʋŋ a bɩ wʋŋ a gʋtɩ ama ba kaaŋ dɩ sɩba ka chɩaŋ. Dama ba keŋ a sɩba a chɩasɩ ba nan yiŋŋi a keŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, aŋ Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋ nagɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba. .

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba, Die nɩŋ nɩ ka sɩba nandagɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ? Nɩ wa baa nɩ yi wo lalɩa a sɩmma nandagaha mana chɩasɩ?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Bʋbʋrɩtʋ wa nɩasɩ wa vuodieke dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Nyindiekemba die dɩ nanna sieku me wo yi wo vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana a wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha ama dɩ ka yʋasa ta Sitaani keŋ vʋarɩha ba ma.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Vuosi bataŋ dɩaŋ nɩasɩ wa nyɩŋbʋra diekemba die dɩ nanna taŋjaalɩka sikpeku; saŋŋa dieke mana ba keŋ a wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha dɩ ka yʋasa ta ba mɩŋŋɩ a tuohe aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: ama a ka dʋaga ba sʋgɩtɩ ma a yʋasa, dama wɩɩrɩ gie ka sʋʋŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ ma ma, die wɩa ba keŋ mugisi be yaa wahala dɩ keŋ juu be wɩaha gie chɩaŋ ma ba ŋaaŋ nagɩha a taaŋ mɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Bataŋ dɩaŋ nɩasɩ wa nyindiekemba die dɩ nanna haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma wa. Vuosi gie ŋaaŋ wʋŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: ama dʋnɩaka gie wumugisike, aŋaŋ ligire yaalɩŋ aŋaŋ nyinti yiri yiri yaalɩŋ ŋaaŋ juu ba sʋŋ mɩŋ a nyagɩ wɩaha amʋ a kaaŋ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Ama vuota diekemba dɩ nɩasɩna nyɩnbʋrɩ diekemba die dɩ nanna tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ ma, ba wʋma wɩaha gie ta tuo dii, ta yie vɩɩnɩŋ. Bataŋ dɩ nyɩna baŋɩsɩ-taa taa, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩna baŋɩsɩ-yʋaba yʋaba, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩna kɔbɩga kɔbɩga.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Vuoŋ nan chogisi popoli a zieŋ juoŋ sʋŋ ta ligike aŋaŋ kpalɩ, yaa ta nagɩka a zieŋ gado chɩaŋ? Aayɩ, ʋ nan nagɩka a zieŋ jaaŋ sikpeŋ.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Jadieke mana dɩ lɔbɩrɩna nan nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma, ta jadieke mana jaaŋ dɩ bubine nan dɩ bie yeŋ me.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ;.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke dɩ yaa tɩba a nan wʋŋ, ʋ wʋmma.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi yine vuodiekemba ba yɩna sieŋ dɩ ba yiŋŋi balɩma Mosisi mɩraha chɩasɩ.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Die ʋ bɩ balɩ a gʋtɩ ba, Nɩ chɩɩsɩma a wʋmma nɩ wʋnnana wudiekemba ba, sie diekemba nɩ dɩɩsɩna a die vuosi sarɩya, Ŋmɩŋ baa ʋ dɩ wa hanɩŋ sieti mi gbaŋ gbaŋ a dii nɩ sarɩya. Ama Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ sietiti nan paalɩ a tʋa a tɩaŋ nɩ sietiti.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Die chɩaŋ ma dɩ vɩɩna aŋ nɩ saagɩ ta dɩa ba dagɩna nɩ die mana, ama nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ yime wudieke ba yinene, dama ba ka dɩ wudieke ba balala wa.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla nan ye a gʋtɩ ama vuodieke dɩ wone jaaŋ, halɩ bɩta dieke gbaŋ ʋ yalla wa ʋ nan waarɩka.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba ŋaaŋ vaa wa vuosi chii chiidʋnsa, chii dieke dɩ dʋnsɩna a yɩa ba, ama ba ka saaga a suŋŋe be aŋ ba chii chiititi.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ nɩasɩ wa sɩba vuoŋ dɩ bʋrɩna ʋ nyɩŋbʋra ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Wudieke mana ba yinene, ba yie mɩŋ dɩ vuota ye be. Ye, ba nagɩna Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ maagɩna bɩta gbanbisi me a bɔbɩ yi ba die aŋaŋ ba nuusi me ta a faasɩ dala dala die; bɩ ye ba nyiŋyeeki wagɩtɩ kʋaŋ nʋa dɩ wagɩrɩna ta faasɩ teeli die.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: ʋ ŋaaŋ gʋʋra yuŋ ta haga sʋkʋʋŋ ta a nyuuŋ ye mɩŋ ta bɩrɩma; ama ʋ zɩ a yinene die a nyuune ta bɩrɩma wa.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba dʋʋga jige ba ŋaaŋ yaala ba kala sikpeŋkpɩɩma tieliŋ jigikasɩka; ta ba keŋ ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone jigiŋ, ba ŋaaŋ yaala ba kala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka;.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Tɩŋgbaŋka gbaŋ gbaŋ yinene nyɩŋbʋraha dɩ nyuune ta bɩrɩma a nyɩnna bie. Nyɩŋbʋraha daakʋ ŋaana a woliŋ a bɩrɩŋ, kʋaŋ chaaŋ ka pɔsɩ vaatɩ ta kʋaŋ kʋaŋ ka sikpeku mana mana nyɩŋ bie.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: ta bɩ yaala ba waasɩma ba aŋaŋ jɩlɩma nyʋgɩtɩ jige ta wasɩma ba Dɩdagɩrʋ.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Nyɩŋbʋraha dɩ keŋ a bii kʋakʋ tieŋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa ʋ jɩbɩŋ a piili a gobe dama a gobiŋ saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ da keŋ yi ba wasɩma nɩ Dɩdagɩrʋ dama nɩ mana yiwo nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ taŋ ta yaa nɩ Dɩdagɩrʋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa jaaŋ tɩ baa tɩ nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ a magɩsɩ? Yaa nandagɩ bɩa tɩ baa tɩ nagɩ a dagɩ ka chɩaŋ aŋ vuoŋ mana sɩmma?.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Ta nɩ daa keŋ wa vuoŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me Chɔɔŋ dama nɩ yaa wa Chɔɔŋ balɩmɩŋ; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Ka sɩɩ sɩba kaŋmɩnɩŋ biŋ vuoŋ dɩ nagɩna a bʋrɩ tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ka yi jabɩŋ a tɩaŋ nyɩŋbʋra mana mana dʋnɩa ma ma.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Nɩ daa bɩ vaa vuosi wa nɩ ba Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dama nɩ yaa wa nɩ Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩ balɩmɩŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Yisa Masia.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: ama dɩ ka yʋasa ta ka nyuuŋ a bɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ dʋnɩa vapɔgɩtɩ mana. Ka ŋaaŋ faasɩ yaa wa dachagɩsɩkpɩɩma pam aŋaŋ nembisi dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a yʋgɩ ba tuosi ka sikpeŋ.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yine vuokpɩɩŋ nɩ sʋŋ ʋ yi ʋ gbaŋ nɩ tʋntʋntʋ.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Yisa die nagɩ wa nandaga nɩasa sɩba naa, a dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa pam. Die ʋ dagɩ ba wa nandaga diekemba ba baaŋ nan dɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ kɔtɩna ʋ gbaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan dansɩ sʋʋŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋ; ama vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan daansɩ kɔtɩ wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Die ʋ ka balɩ wɩɩŋ a yɩa ba ta ka nagɩ nandagɩŋ a balɩ, ama saŋŋa dieke nɩŋ mana die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩɩna ma dɩ keŋ a kalɩ, ʋ ŋaaŋ dagɩ ba mɩŋ nandagaha mana chɩasɩ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Ta Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba yine gɩgaantɩba dɩ nan dɩ bɩaŋ a yɩ nɩ, dama nɩnɩŋ nɩ ligi wo Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sanʋarɩ dɩ vuosi daa juu. Nɩ gbaŋ ka baa nɩ juu, ama ta kaga vuodiekemba dɩ yaalala ba juu wo.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi gbaŋ gbaŋ jɩŋmɩŋ, Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, Nɩ vaa tɩ jʋalɩ a tɩaŋ a ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo chaakʋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba yine gɩgaantɩba, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ gasa mʋgɩkpɩɩma ta chʋŋ gara tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ ta yaala vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan tarɩgɩ ʋ beriŋ a dɩa nɩ kʋaŋ, dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ ye vuoŋ, nɩ ŋaaŋ yiwo ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ dieke dɩ yalla wa a gara Sitaani boliŋ me bɩ gʋtɩ die bule sɩba nɩnɩŋ.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Die wɩa die ba va daadamba mi ta die a juu haarɩkʋ Yisa die wone bie ka sʋŋ, banɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa dɩ ga. Haarɩsɩ ataŋ gbaŋ die benne mi.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ dɩdatɩrɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma! Nɩ daga vuosi dɩ, Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ huu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, dɩ ka yi nɩŋŋmɩna, ama dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ huu ligire dieke dɩ benne juoku me wo sie ʋ yi dene.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Womi bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ piili a nɩga; nyaabʋ die dɩ poŋŋi a juo haarɩkʋ sʋŋ. Die wɩa die haarɩkʋ die dɩ yaala ka suuli aŋaŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gaantɩŋ, nyinti ale wo gie a jabɩa yine nɩŋŋmɩna, ligirehe yaa Ŋmɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ die dɩ yine ligirehe dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ?.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Yisa die bie wo haarɩkʋ kʋaŋ a nagɩ ʋ sikpeŋ a kpagɩlɩ dafere a gʋʋra. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, dɩ ka mugisi fʋ tɩ balla tɩ kpi naa.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ta nɩ bɩ daga dɩ, Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu kaabɩŋ koriŋ, ka ka yi nɩŋŋmɩna, ama ʋ keŋ huu kaabɩŋ dɔŋ dɩ diisine koriku me wo, sie ʋ yi dene gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Yisa die dɩ hagɩ a yɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ nʋaŋ, Vaa nɩgɩŋ ta balɩ a yɩ nyaaŋ poŋŋiku dɩaŋ Zie dene. Bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ nan, mi mana die dɩ paalɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Nɩ seŋ yiwo yɩɩsɩ wusie. Nyinti ale wa a jabɩa yine nɩŋŋmɩna, kaabɩŋ dɔŋkʋ yaa koriku dɩ yine kaabɩkʋ dɔnka die dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ?.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Womi die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Bɩa yine ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yallɩ nɩ, nɩ ye ko wo yada.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Die wɩa dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ huu kaabɩkʋ koriku, dɩ dagɩ sɩba ʋ huu kanɩŋ aŋaŋ jadieke mana dɩ diisine ka ma.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Ama ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a yallɩ ba, die ba pɩasa tamba, Mɩnɩa yine daa wa gie? Halɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋiŋ dɩ wʋma ʋ wʋbalɩka yɩa wa.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, dɩ dagɩya sɩba ʋ huu kanɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ benne ka ma.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Dɩɩ bɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu arɩzanna, ʋ huu wa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ gbaŋtɩtɩ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kalala ka ma.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba die yine gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ naga nanjʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ bʋŋʋ aŋaŋ neeri aŋaŋ nyinti a taŋ baŋ sʋŋ a nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, ama ta va jadieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna ʋ mɩraha ma; sɩba wusie sarɩya diile, aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩla aŋaŋ yada yiile. Genhe die mʋna nɩ yime, ta da va hanɩŋ ba yiile.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ diekemba dɩ datɩnana nɩ chanchaalɩŋ naa, nɩ ŋaaŋ vʋara nanjʋʋŋ nyaaŋ ma a taaŋ ama ta nyɩa nyɔgɩnɩ aŋaŋ nyaabʋ!.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ daaraa nɩ nyanyuuli kpala aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbala kʋasɩ, ama a sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ gbatɩŋ aŋaŋ halɩ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Nɩnɩŋ Farasisi mba yine yɩɩsɩ, nɩ woliŋ a daarɩ nɩ nyanyuuli kpalaha aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbalaha sʋgɩtɩ, ta a kʋasɩ dɩaŋ nan daarɩ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Dama nɩ sɩɩ sɩba vɔrɩtɩ dieke ba mɩɩ a penti zie, ta a kʋasɩ dɩ vɩɩna ama ta a sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ vuota kɔba aŋaŋ nyʋʋgɩŋ.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ta vuosi yese nɩ yeŋ me ta nɩ vɩɩna, ama nɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ mɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba vɔrɩtɩ, ta penti zieŋ, ta yi jadieke dɩ baaŋ nan tɩɩnsɩ vuosi yaa gamma vuodiekemba die dɩ chɩgɩnana Ŋmɩŋ kuŋ wɩa.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: ta baarɩ dɩ, die tɩ tɩŋ beri tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ saŋka, tɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ yi wudieke ba yine wo ta kʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Die wɩa nɩ seŋ a saagɩ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yine vuodiekemba die dɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wo haagɩsɩ.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Die wɩa nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ piiline a yie tʋmbɩatɩ die wo, nɩ yi he a kpatɩ.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuuke aŋaŋ nyinvuuke ballɩ. Nɩ baa nɩ yiwo lalɩa a chɩgɩ nyɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpisiŋ sʋŋ?.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Die wɩa n bala nɩ dɩ, n nan tʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ dɩdagɩrɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ama nɩ nan kʋʋ bataŋ, ta kpaasɩ bataŋ a diisi dagarɩkɩŋ ma aŋ ba kpi, ta nɩgɩ bataŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me ta dɩ ba nagɩsɩ a nyɩŋ tɩŋ kaanɩ a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ba kʋna vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana Ŋmɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan keŋ nɩ ma, a nyɩŋ Abeli a tʋgɩ Berakia bʋa Zakaria ma, vuodieke die nɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ kaabɩŋ koriku sʋnsʋkʋ.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lele gie vuosi nan daansɩ ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ tʋmbɩatɩ gie mana wɩa.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Nchʋanaawee, Jerusalemi vuosi, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba, ta bɩ taa vuodiekemba ʋ tʋnna nɩ jigiŋ wo tana, bʋŋmɩa maŋ mɩa, a yaala dɩ n lagɩsɩ nɩ bʋnyɩ sɩba kpɩanɩɩŋ dɩ ŋaana lagɩsɩ ka ballɩ ka chobiti chɩaŋ ta nɩ ka saaga.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Ye, Ŋmɩŋ va nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ta ka zie yɔrɩ.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: N bala nɩ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, nɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ alibarika bie vuodieke dɩ kennene tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ ma. .

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ nagɩ nandaga a balɩ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi dɩ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ wʋnsɩna hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋla dɩ ʋ yɩ ʋ bʋa.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Ta die tʋŋ ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba ga balɩ vuodiekemba ʋ wone a posi wo dɩ ba keŋ banwʋlaka, ama die ba ka yaala ba keŋ.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Die Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ dɩ tɩanna, tʋŋ die dɩ vʋʋna Alasiri daaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ ta Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Meri wʋnɩŋ wa die dɩ ga dɩ ba daansɩ vɔrɩkʋ.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Die ʋ bɩ tʋŋ yɔŋɩsɩ bataŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gamma a ga balɩ vuodiekemba die n wone a posi wo dɩ chaantɩ nyindiike yi siri mɩŋ; n wɔŋ kʋʋ narapɔlɩka aŋaŋ naara narɩka ta a mana yi wo siri; die wɩa nɩ keŋ a keŋ dii banwʋlaka chaantɩ.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Ta tɩŋgbaŋ die dɔŋ pam bʋnyɩ, dama die Ŋmɩŋ malaka die nyɩnna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ, a biliŋ tanɩ a kalɩ ka ma.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Ama die ʋ posine vuodiekemba ba die ka tɔgɩŋ wa ta die ga ba tʋʋma jige; wʋnyɩ dɩ ga ʋ kʋaŋ, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ ga ʋ nyinti daala jigiŋ nyʋʋŋ ma.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Die ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die nyɩgɩsa sɩba nɩɩnyɩgɩsɩŋ ta ʋ nyiŋyeeke die dɩ yɩantɩ sɩba chɩɩŋ.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: ta bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ yigi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a nɩgɩ nɩgɩ ba ta kʋʋ ba.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi vuodiekemba die dɩ gbarɩna vɔrɩkʋ, die ba cheeli ta nan nan sɩba kunti.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Die naaŋ wa dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ pam; ʋ tʋŋ ʋ sojasi ba ga kʋʋ vuokʋʋlaha ta jʋʋ ba tɩka boliŋ.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Die malakaka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ hɔgʋbaha, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, n sɩba dɩ nɩ yaala Yisa vuodieke die ba kpaasɩna dagarɩkʋ ma wa.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ die naaŋ wa dɩ wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a balɩ ba dɩ, N hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋlaka chaantɩ yi siri mɩŋ, ama vuodiekemba n wone a posi wo ka mʋ dɩ ba keŋ.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Ʋ wo giena ʋ hagɩ kumbu me sɩba die ʋ balla die wo. Nɩ keŋ a ye jigidieke die ʋ dʋana wa.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Die wɩa nɩ wa gamma tɩka sʋŋ siekpɩɩma ma, a wa vuodieke mana nɩ baaŋ nan tuoli dɩ ba keŋ a dii laalɩkʋ chaantɩ.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Nɩ gamma lagɩ lagɩ a balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ʋ hagɩ kuŋ me mɩŋ ta dɩ nɩ nɩŋŋa a gara Galili, mi nɩ nan ye wo. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Die ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ dɩ nyɩŋ ga siekpɩɩma ma, a ga wa vuodiekemba mana ba yene, vuovɩɩna aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ mana; ta die ba keŋ a suuli banwʋlaka tigiri me.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Die wɩa hɔgʋbaha die dɩ nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ lagɩ lagɩ aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ ta bɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam ta chɩgɩ a ga balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Ta Naaŋ wa die dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩ ye vuodiekemba dɩ kenne banwʋlaka, die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ dɩ benne vuosi sʋnsʋŋ ta ka yeegi banwʋla nyiŋyeeke.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Die ba ganana wa Yisa die dɩ tuoli be sieŋ me a waasɩ ba; die ba gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta jɩaŋ wa.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Ta Naaŋ wa dɩ pɩasɩ wa, N zʋa yiwo lalɩa a keŋ juu giena ta ka yeegi banwʋlaka nyiŋyeeke? Ama daa wa die ka balɩ wɩɩŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Ta ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba, Nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, nɩ ga a balɩ n nɩmballɩlɩ dɩ ba gamma Galili me, mi ba nan ye mɩŋ.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Womi naaŋ wa die dɩ balɩ yɩ ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ, Nɩ bɔbɩ ʋ nuusi aŋaŋ ʋ nagɩsɩ a nagɩ wa a taaŋ yeŋ me lɩmɩŋ ma. Mi ʋ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nyɩna. .

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Die hɔgʋba die yene be sieŋ me a gara, ta sojasisi bataŋ die dɩ gbarɩna vɔrɩkʋ dɩ yiŋŋi a ga tɩka ma a balɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha wudieke dɩ yine.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ a kpatɩ, ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ wa wa vuosi pam, ama ʋ nan vʋarɩ vuosi bɩta.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ lagɩsɩ a saŋ; a nagɩ ligire pam a yɩ sojasisi.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Die Farasisi vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga a lagɩsɩ tamba a yaala sieti dɩ ba yigi Yisa aŋaŋ wʋpɩasɩka.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: a balɩ ba, Nɩ balɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba kenne tanseese saŋŋa dieke tɩ dʋana gʋʋra a keŋ gaarɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Die ba tʋŋ ba kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Herodi vuosi bataŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ sɩba a baarɩ bala wusie, ta daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ sieti aŋaŋ wusie, ta ka tɔgɩma vuosi, dama fʋ ka tɔgɩma vuota dɩ sɩna die.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ vʋʋna sʋkʋleeliŋ, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ saŋ ba baaŋ yi die a kʋʋ Yisa.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi tɩka jakʋʋrɩ dɩ wʋŋ naa, tɩ nan ligi nɩ ʋ jigiŋ ta vʋarɩ nɩ wɩa ma.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Die wɩa, balɩ tɩ, lalɩa fʋ yile yaa gamma lampo tuniŋ wɩa? Tɩ mɩraha yɩ sieŋ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ tumme lampo a yɩma Roma tɩŋgbaŋ naaŋ wa Siiza yaa tɩ daa tumme.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Die ba bɔbɩ wa aŋaŋ chɔrɩma, a nagɩ wa a ga tɩŋgbaŋka jakʋʋŋ die ba wasɩnana Paliti jigiŋ.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Die gbɩgbarɩtɩba dɩ tuo ligirehe ta die ga balɩ vuosi wudieke ba balla dɩ ba balɩ wa. Die wɩa ta Juu vuosi die dɩ tuo wudieke ba bala wa a dii a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ wɔŋ sɩba ba sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ, die wɩa die ʋ balɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie, bɩa nɩ yaala nɩ magɩsɩ mɩŋ?.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Die Judasi, vuodieke die dɩ posine Yisa chɩaka dɩ wʋnna ba dine ʋ sarɩya a baarɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya amʋ ba kʋʋ wa wa, die ʋ sʋŋ dɩ faasɩ chʋʋsɩ; die ʋ wa nagɩ salɩnyɩala ligire bie baŋɩsɩ-taa wa a yaa a yiŋŋi ga yɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa die dɩ ga Galili kunkogiri me, jigidieke Yisa die dɩ bala ba dɩ ba ga wa. Mi die ba ye wo.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Nɩ nagɩ ligiribiŋ dieke nɩ balla nɩ nagɩ a tuŋ lampoke a dagɩ mɩŋ. Die ba yaa ligire bini a keŋ yɩ wa.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Ta baarɩ, N tʋŋ wa bɩaŋ dama n posi wo vuodieke dɩ wone taalɩ chɩaŋ. Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Tɩnɩŋ tɩ baga bɩ beri genke ma mɩŋ? Fʋnɩŋ fʋ wɩa wondie.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa nine chaaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ saaŋ diisine ka ma die.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Die ba yene wo die ba gbirigi a jɩaŋ wa ama bataŋ die dɩ chɩɩlɩ wa.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Die wɩa Judasi die dɩ nagɩ ligirehe a vigi taaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ, ta die a nyɩŋ a ga a taaŋ ʋ gbaŋ guuŋ.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi baarɩ, Naaŋ Siiza. Die chɩaŋ ma die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Die nɩŋ nɩ tuŋ a yɩ naaŋ Siiza jadieke dɩ yine ʋ sɩɩtɩ ta tuŋ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jadieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Die ʋ keŋ gbigi be a baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ yɩ mɩŋ wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie yiko mana mana mɩŋ.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ vaarɩ ligirehe ta baarɩ, Gembu wa yiwo zɩŋ ligire, ta tɩ mɩraha ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ tɩ nagɩha a gʋtɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ligirehe sʋŋ.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Die ba wʋnna naa wa die dɩ yi be mamachi, die ba va wa ta ga.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Die wɩa nɩ gamma tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi buuriŋ mana jigiŋ a yi be n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ, ta sɩma ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma, aŋaŋ manɩŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ saaŋ ma, aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka saaŋ ma.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Die wɩa die ba saŋ dɩ ba nagɩ ligirebu a daa kpagɩwʋnsɩrʋ tɩŋgbaŋ, dɩ ba gume chaanɩŋ mi.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die Sadusi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: ta nɩ dagɩma ba aŋ ba saagɩ dɩa wudieke n dagɩna nɩ wa mana. Ama nɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ n nan dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ saŋŋa mana a ga tʋgɩ dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Die wɩa ba ye a wasa tɩŋgbaŋka mi dɩ Zɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋ a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne die.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Die a baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, Mosisi die balɩ dɩ dembiŋ dɩ keŋ faarɩ hɔgʋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ, sie ʋ nɩmbʋa faarɩ kpihɔgʋ wa a mɩɩrɩ ballɩ a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kpine wo.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Die dɩ keŋ yi wusie Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jerimaya die dɩ balla wudieke wo dɩ, Ba nagɩ wa salɩnyɩala ligire bie baŋɩsɩ-taa dieke die Izara vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nagɩna a daa wa.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Die nɩmballɩ bayʋpɔyɩ die benne. Die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ, ta ʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ faarɩ kpihɔgʋ wa.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: a nagɩbʋ a daa kpagɩwʋnsɩrʋ tɩŋgbaŋ, sɩba n Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dagɩna mɩŋ dene.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ yi ʋ nɩmbʋa die dɩ diisine bule wo a yaa ga tʋgɩ vuodieke die dɩ diisine bʋtaa wa, a yaa die a ga kpatɩ dembisi bayʋpɔyɩ wa mana.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Yisa die zie wo tɩŋgbaŋka jakʋʋrɩ Paliti nɩŋŋa; ta Paliti die pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ, fʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa? Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ, Wa, fʋnɩŋ fʋ balala.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋ wa dɩaŋ die dɩ kpi.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Ama die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋ die dɩ galɩnnana wa, die ʋ ka balɩ ba wɩɩŋ.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi ta kunti hagɩŋ daaŋ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, mɩnɩa hɔgʋ baaŋ yi wo? Dama ba mana die faarɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Die Paliti dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Ka wʋmma ba galɩnnana fʋ die mana.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩ baya mɩŋ, dama nɩ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ, ta bɩ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ dene.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Ama Yisa die ka yiŋŋi a balɩ wa wɩɩŋ, die wɩa die dɩ paalɩ yi Paliti mamachi.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Dama kunti dɩ keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me ba nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba malakasi dɩ benne arɩzanna ma, ta hɔgʋ faarɩŋ aŋaŋ dembiŋ yallɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ beri.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Dɩɩ yi gasɩtɩanɩŋ dʋʋga saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ tɩka jakʋʋrɩ ŋaaŋ vʋarɩ wa vuobalɩmɩŋ dansarɩka sʋŋ, vuodieke vuosisi dɩ yaalala a taaŋ.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Ʋ bɩ yiŋŋi ga Kapenɔmi die vuosi dɩ wʋŋ dɩ ʋ bie mi.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Yaa gamma kunti hagɩŋ ma nɩ ye ka karɩŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala a yɩ nɩ wa ʋ gbaŋkʋ ma?.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Saŋka mi daa wʋnyɩ die benne dansarɩka sʋŋ ba wasa wa Barabasi, die vuoŋ mana die dɩ sɩba ʋ wɩa aŋaŋ ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Die vuosi die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die wɩa jigiŋ die bɩ wori aŋaŋ vuogaasɩ dɩ baaŋ nan dɩ beri, die juoku mana suuliye mɩŋ aŋaŋ yeŋ me mana. Die ʋ bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩa ba.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Die wɩa die vuosisi die dɩ lagɩŋna wa, Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa nɩ yaala n vʋarɩ dansarɩka ka sʋŋ a taaŋ a yɩ nɩ? Barabasi yaa Yisa, vuodieke dɩ dine Masia wa.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Dɩ Manɩŋ n yine Ŋmɩŋ dieke Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu dɩ jɩannana. Vuosi gie die kpiye mɩŋ ama ba ye ko bie ba mɩsɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ ka yi kunti Ŋmɩŋ ama vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Womi ta dembisi banɩɩsa wonde a chii vuodieke nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ kpine a kieŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Dama Paliti die sɩba a baarɩ haasɩ wɩa die ba yigi Yisa a keŋ yɩ wa.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ wʋnna naa, die dɩ yi be mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Die Paliti die dɩ sʋʋna kala ʋ sarɩya diile chɩaka ma wa, die ʋ hɔgʋ die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ jigiŋ dɩ, Vʋarɩ fʋ nuusi a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuovɩɩŋka wɩa ma, dama diene wo yuku die n die wo wahala pam n daansa sʋŋ yaa gamma ʋ ma.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Die daadamba dɩ dala wa chɩaŋ ma die ba ka bɩagɩ a yaa wa tʋgɩ ʋ jigiŋ. Ba yuori toli mampili dieke ma ma Yisa Masia dɩ benne wo. Ba wana yuori toli mampili me a kpatɩ wa, ba nagɩ daa wa aŋaŋ giliŋgiliŋke a yaa sʋʋŋ dʋaŋ Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Die Farasisi dɩ wʋnna dɩ Yisa nyɩŋbalɩkaha die nyaŋŋɩ Sadusisi die ba lagɩsɩ tamba a keŋ.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ bala a yɩa vuosisi dɩ ba balɩ Paliti dɩ ʋ vʋarɩ Barabasi a taaŋ ta vaa ba kʋʋ Yisa.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye ta ba tuo wo dii mɩŋ, ʋ balɩ daa wa, N bʋa, n nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa fʋ.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ die dɩ yine mɩraha dɩdagɩrʋ dɩ magɩsɩ wa aŋaŋ wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ dɩ.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Ta Paliti die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Vuosi bale wo gie, mɩnɩa nɩ yaala n vʋarɩ a taaŋ? Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Barabasi.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Die vuodiekemba dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die bie mi a yile.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Dɩdagɩrʋ, mɩrɩ bɩa tɩanna mɩraha mana.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Ta Paliti die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Lalɩa nɩ wa yaala n yi Yisa vuodieke ba wasɩnana Masia ka? Ba mana die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, Kpaasɩ wa diisi dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: dɩ Wʋbɩa daa wa gie dɩ bala die? Ʋ bala sɩba ʋ yi wo Ŋmɩŋ. Vuobɩa baaŋ nan nagɩ vuoŋ taalɩ a chaa wa ntaala Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Chome fʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ haalɩŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋanyile mana.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ama Paliti die dɩ yiŋŋi pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa wʋbɩaŋ ʋ yi? Die ba piili a faasɩ nata dɩ, Kpaasɩ wa a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ wɔŋ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile, a balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ bɩa nɩ yile nɩ sʋŋ ma die?.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Genke yine mɩrɩ dieke dɩ benne nɩŋŋa ta bɩ tɩaŋ mɩraha mana.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Die Paliti die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ dɩ ka bɩ amʋ dɩ ʋ balɩ wɩɩŋ dama ba nan yi watɩ ta waagɩŋ nan, die ʋ nagɩ nyaaŋ a nɩɩtɩ ʋ nuusi vuosisi nɩŋŋa ta baarɩ dɩ, N nuuŋ wo daa wa gie kumbu me! Nɩ wɩɩŋ wonde.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Wʋbɩa yine mɔlɩ? Fʋ baaŋ nan balɩ a yɩ vuodieke nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ kpine, N nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa fʋ? Yaa fʋ baaŋ nan balɩ Hagɩ nagɩ fʋ giliŋgiliŋke a chʋŋ.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ jigiŋ a chʋŋ gara, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ wa, ye Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ dɩ vɩɩna die.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Ta jadieke dɩaŋ dɩ diisine bule wo dɩaŋ bɩ yiwo nɩŋŋmɩna a gʋtɩ, Chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Ta vuosi mana dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, Wa, vaa ʋ zɩŋ bemme tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ ballɩ ma.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa ma ma a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba. Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuodieke nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine wo.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ye nyinti gie mana mɩŋ? Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ba nan kpaŋŋɩ a mana a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta taŋ balɩmɩŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ wɩarɩ a sʋgɩ ka chanchaaŋ ma.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Mosisi mɩraha mana aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩtɩ mana diisi wo mɩraha ale wo gie me.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Die wɩa Paliti die dɩ vʋarɩ Barabasi a taaŋ a yɩ ba, ta die a yi ba nɩgɩ Yisa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta nagɩ wa yɩ dɩ ba ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: N bala fʋ, hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ giliŋgiliŋke, a kuli.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Yisa die dɩ sʋʋna a kalɩ kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ ba nyɩɩna a pɩasɩ wa, Balɩ tɩ saŋŋa dieke genhe mana dɩ bala a yi aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ bala ka yi a dagɩ sɩba fʋ kieŋku aŋaŋ dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Saŋŋa dieke ta Farasisi dɩ lagɩsɩna tamba die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Die Paliti sojasisi die nagɩ Yisa a ga juu gbaakʋ sʋŋ ta sojasisi mana dɩ giliŋ wo.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Ʋ hagɩ bʋnyɩ a nagɩ ʋ giliŋgiliŋke, a chʋŋ ba mana nine me. Die ba yene wo die wo, ka paalɩ a yi be mamachi. Ba baarɩ, Tɩ bɩra Ŋmɩŋ. Tɩ ye ka ye wɩɩŋ ka nɩasɩ naa.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩ sɩmma aŋ vuoŋ da keŋ gaaŋ nɩ.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Bɩa nɩ yile yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa wɩa? Mɩnɩa bʋa yine wo? Ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa Ʋ yiwo Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Die ba yi ʋ nyiŋyeekehe awʋrɩ ta die a nagɩ jayeekiwaŋ ŋmɩnɩŋ a yeegi wo.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gie lʋgɩŋ ta vuosi pam dɩ lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ a giliŋ wo, ta ʋ daga ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: dama vuosi pam nan nagɩ n saaŋ a keŋ a baarɩ banɩŋ ba yine manɩŋ Masia wa ta gaaŋ vuosi pam.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba, Ta bɩa yine saŋŋa dieke Davidi die dɩ suuline aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ die dɩ wasa wa dɩ, n Yɔmʋtieŋ? Ta baarɩ.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: ta die a nagɩ haŋgɔɔsɩ a wʋnsɩ sibubiŋ a nagɩ ka a bubi wo ta a nagɩ daaŋgbanɩŋ a yɩ ʋ nuudiigiŋ me, ta die a wa gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a vʋarɩ wa fala, die a baarɩ dɩ, Juu vuosi naaŋ wa, tɩ waasa fʋ.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Die ʋ dɩna chʋŋ wa die ʋ ga haarɩ lampotuosiru wʋnyɩ Liiva, vuodieke die dɩ yine Alifusi bʋadembiŋ, ta ʋ kalɩ ʋ tʋʋma juoŋ me. Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa, Hagɩ a dɩa n kʋaŋ, Liiva die dɩ hagɩ a dɩɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Nɩ nan wʋŋ tɔntɩ watɩ nɩ chaaŋ aŋaŋ saasaa wɩa, ama nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dama sie wɩaha gie yi, ama a ka dagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa kpatɩkʋ keŋye mɩŋ.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die bala n Yɔmʋtieŋ; Kala n nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Die ba tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yi wo ta tuo daa dieke ba nagɩna a yɩ wa wa a nɩgɩ ʋ sikpeŋ me.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Die kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ die nyindiike Liiva tigiŋ me, lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha pam die dɩdɩ Yisa kʋaŋ. Ba pam die dɩ keŋ a gʋtɩ ba ma aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyindiikehe jigiŋ.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Vuosi buuriŋ nan wagɩ taŋ, naalɩŋ nan wagɩ naalɩŋ. Kɔŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ dɔmɩŋ nan dɩ bie jigiŋ mana.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta Davidi die dɩ wasa Masia wa dɩ N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩ bɩ baa dɩ yiwo lalɩa Masia wa bɩ yi Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Die ba vʋarɩna wa falaha a kpatɩ wa die ba tuo jayeeki dieke ba nagɩna a yɩ wa wa ta nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ jayeekiŋ a yeegi wo. Die ba yaa wa nyɩŋ dɩ ba ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ yine Farasisi die dɩ yene Yisa aŋaŋ lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha die dɩ lagɩsɩna tamba a die nyindiikehe, die ba pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Bɩa yine wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha mi chanchaalɩŋ dɩ lagɩsa a die.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Wɩaha gie mana nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba maarɩŋ yʋagɩŋ piiliŋ.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Die ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wɩɩŋ, die wɩa a nyɩŋ dɩnda daarɩ mi yaa gamma vuoŋ die ka bɩ yaa sikimiŋ a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a pɩasɩ Yisa wʋpɩasɩka.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Die ba ganana wa die ba tuoli daa wʋnyɩ dɩ nyɩnna Saarini ba wasa wa Simoni, die ba mugisi wo dɩ ʋ tuo Yisa dagarɩkʋ a chii.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋŋ ba ta balɩ ba dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla alaafɩa ka yaala gbɩgbaantʋ, sie vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna. Dɩ ka yi vuovɩɩna wɩa maŋ keŋ, wʋbɩatɩtieliŋ wɩa maŋ keŋ.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Saŋka mi ba nan yigi nɩ a nagɩ nɩ yɩ aŋ ba vaa nɩ dii wahala, ta kʋʋ nɩ. Tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ mana nan dɩ haa nɩ dama nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Die ba a keŋ a tʋgɩ jigidieke die ba wasɩnana Goligota wa, ka chɩaŋ yine Sikpeŋ kogilike jigiŋ.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ, Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ ba wasɩnana Farasisi die dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ. Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa yine Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ, ta fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka bɔba.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Saŋka mi vuosi pam nan zeti n kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ, ba nan posi tamba chɩasɩ ta haa tamba.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: mi die ba nagɩ daa dieke ba lagɩna aŋaŋ nyɩntʋatɩ a yɩ wa dɩ ʋ nyuu, ama die ʋ lannabʋ wa die ʋ zeti bʋ nyuule.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Nɩ tɩŋ yaala chaaŋ diekemba dɩ gana hɔgʋ faarɩŋ ma bɔbɩ nʋaŋ? Banɩŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a beri wo, die kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Ta ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba nan keŋ a gaaŋ vuosi pam.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Die ba kpaasɩna wa dagarɩkʋ ma a kpatɩ wa, die ba nagɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke a puo taŋ aŋaŋ chaachaa.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama saŋŋa nan keŋ ba nan nagɩ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ, saŋka mi ba nan wa bɔbɩ nʋaŋ.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Bɩaŋ dɩ bala ka dalɩma tɩŋgbaŋka gie wɩa, ta vuosi pam choti nan dɩ sʋnsɩ.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: die ba sʋʋŋ kalɩ a gbarɩ wa.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Vuoŋ kaaŋ saagɩ a nagɩ garɩhaalɩŋ garɩchɔɔtɩŋ a baalɩ a ligi garɩkʋʋŋ vɔrɩŋ, dama garɩhaalɩkʋ gie nan chɩɩrɩ ta vaa garɩkʋʋrɩ, aŋ ka vɔrɩkʋ wa a faasɩ a dalɩma.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ dine sikimiŋ a ga tʋgɩ wahalaka gie kpatɩŋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Die ba maagɩ wudieke ba bala dɩ ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ wa a marɩ ʋ dagarɩkʋ sikpeŋ; naa die ba maagɩ dɩ, Yisa, Vuodieke Dɩ Yine Juu Vuosi Naaŋ Wa Wɔnna .

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Yaa nɩ yeye ta vuoŋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ a sʋʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋkʋra ma? Dama ʋ yi die daabʋ nan kʋŋ a chɩɩrɩ kolititi aŋ daabʋ aŋaŋ kolititi mana taaŋ yɔrɩ. Ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa daahaalɩŋ a sʋʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋhaala ma.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Ta ba nan mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋvɩɩnaha gie dʋnɩa ka gie mana mana ma, dɩ vuoŋ mana wʋŋ, ta dʋnɩa ka kpatɩŋ ye keŋ.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Die ba bɩ kpaasɩ wa gbɩgbatɩrɩŋ bale dagarɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ ʋ ma, die wʋnyɩ die bie wo ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ bie ʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Nɩ nan ye Jachʋʋsɩ dieke ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Danieli die dɩ balla ka wɩa wa dɩ, genke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chʋarɩŋ, ka nan dɩ zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Tɔ, Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ, Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ chʋŋ tɩanna zaa kʋaŋ ma. Die ba ganana wa die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gobe zaapakɩtɩ a yɩaga ŋɔba ta gara.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ tɩannana wa die vige ba sikpigile ta die taana wa jome.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Saŋka mi vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia ma ma wa, chɩgɩ a ga jʋalɩ tana sikpeŋ.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Die wɩa Farasisi vuosi die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Ye, bɩa wɩa ba yie wudieke tɩ mɩraha dɩ kana ka saaga dɩ ba yi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: a baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ baarɩ nan kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ a taaŋ, ta nagɩ daraa ataa a yiŋŋi mɩɩ ka, gbatɩ fʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ. Fʋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, fʋ keŋ sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ bie ʋ mampili sikpeŋ da keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩŋgbaŋ dɩ ʋ juu juoku a nagɩ ʋ nyinti.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba, Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ Davidi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yine wudieke kɔŋ die dɩ yalla ba wa? Kɔŋ die yigine wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Naa gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ vʋarɩ wa fala.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ bie ʋ kʋaŋ ma, ʋ da keŋ kuli dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiŋ.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: die wɩa die ʋ ga a juu Ŋmɩŋ tigiri me a ŋɔbɩ paanʋ dieke die ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ. Saŋka mi Abiata die yine Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ. Ama tɩ mɩra die ka baarɩ vuogaasɩ ŋɔbɩha sie Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩɩna ma; ama ta Davidi die dɩ ŋɔbɩ paanʋkʋ gie, halɩ ta die a nagɩ ataŋ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gbaŋ ba ŋɔbɩ.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: ta die baarɩ dɩ, Die ʋ gbatɩ wa ʋ chanchaalɩŋ a taana ama ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ a taaŋ! Dɩɩ yi ʋ seŋ yi Izara vuosi naaŋ wa, ʋ keŋ sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ ma aŋ tɩ yiwo yada.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Saŋka mi hɔgʋ nyuutitieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ yalla ballɩnyaalɩsɩ nan dii wahala pam!.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Womi die ʋ balɩ a kpatɩ ba dɩ, Vuotaŋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ ama daa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ wɩa ʋ naaŋ vuotaŋ.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: Ʋ tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ dii ta baarɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa. Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaala wa, nɩ vaa tɩ ye sɩba ʋ nan gbatɩ wa a taaŋ lele.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ʋ da keŋ yi wahalaha keŋ yɩbɩŋ saŋŋa yaa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Die wɩa, manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ yine davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ jakʋʋŋ.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ gbɩgbatɩrɩŋ diekemba die ba kpaasɩna a gʋtɩ ʋ ma wa dɩ zɩa wa.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Dama saŋka mi wo, wahala pam nan dɩ beri; Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ naanna dʋnɩa a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, kanɩŋ wahalaka mi ye ka keŋye; ta kanɩŋ wahalaka mi kaaŋ bɩ keŋ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wana a tʋgɩ sikpeŋ sʋnsʋŋ, tɩŋgbaŋka mana mana die dɩ sibi. Die ka sibi wo dee a ga tʋgɩ ŋmɩŋgbɩlɩkɩŋ hawasɩ ataa saŋŋa.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ ka gobi daraaha a taaŋ vuoŋ tɩŋ kaaŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ; ama ʋ vuovʋarɩkaha wɩa ta ʋ nan gobi daraaha a taaŋ.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Awasɩ ataa wa saŋŋa die Yisa dɩ faasɩ keesi ta natɩ dɩ Eli, Eli lema sabakitani? Ka chɩaŋ wone N Ŋmɩŋ, n Ŋmɩŋ, bɩa yine fʋ va mɩŋ.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Saŋka mi dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a balɩ nɩ dɩ, Nɩ ye, Masia wa wʋnna yaa ʋ benne mi wo, nɩ daa keŋ yi wo yada;.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ zene mi wo ta wʋŋ wa wa die ba baarɩ dɩ Ʋ wasa Elaja.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: dama saŋka mi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ Masiasi aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba nan keŋ nyɩŋ a tʋŋ mamachi wɩa dɩ ba gaaŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna wa gbaŋ, ba tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ die dɩ chɩgɩ lagɩ lagɩ a ga nagɩ gbara alʋ daŋmɩɩsɩŋ ma a nagɩka a sagɩ daaŋ sikpeŋ ta a kɔtɩ yɩ wa dɩ ʋ mʋgɩsɩ.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Nɩ wʋmma! N wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ ta saŋka ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Ama ʋ chanchaalɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vaa wa aŋ tɩ ye sɩba Elaja nan keŋ a gbatɩ wa a taaŋ.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Die wɩa, ba keŋ balɩ nɩ, Ye, ʋ bie wo haagɩŋ ma! Nɩ da keŋ ga mi yaa ba keŋ a balɩ nɩ, Ye, ʋ bie wo juoku me! Nɩ da keŋ yi yada;.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ a faasɩ a keesi ta kpi.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa keniŋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba nɩɩŋ dɩ ŋaana nyɩgɩsɩ a nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ a ga tʋgɩ ŋmɩŋ nanɩŋ chaakʋ die wo.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Saŋka mi die garɩ dieke die dɩ sagɩna Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wa a puoke bule wo die dɩ puo bule a nyɩŋ ka sikpeŋ me a ga a tʋgɩ ka chɩaŋ ma ta tɩŋgbaŋ dɩ dɔŋ ta tankpɩɩma dɩ ŋmabɩ ŋmabɩ.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Jigidieke jakpiikiŋ nyʋʋgɩŋ dɩ benne, mi zuuti dɩ lagɩma.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: kunti vɔrɩtɩ die dɩ yuori yuori ta vuodiekemba die dɩ kpine ta yi Ŋmɩŋ vuosisi bataŋ pam die dɩ hagɩ kunti me.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Wahalaha mi kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta ŋmɩŋ nan sɩbɩ, chɩɩkʋ kaaŋ bɩ chaaŋ; ta chɩŋmarɩsɩsɩ nan nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a nan ta nyindieke dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ nan dɔŋ a lagɩŋ taŋ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: die ba nyɩŋ ba vɔrɩtɩ ma; ta Yisa die hagɩna kumbu me kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba ga Jerusalemi ta vuosi pam die dɩ ye be aŋaŋ ba nine.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Saŋka mi ta vuosi nan ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa yiŋŋi kieŋku siara ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, buuri dieke mana dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋka gie me nan kʋŋ, ta ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ benne nɩɩŋmara ma ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ sʋʋna aŋaŋ yiko chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ sojasi dieke die dɩ gʋtɩna ʋ ma a gbarɩ Yisa wa die dɩ yene tɩŋgbaŋka dɔŋkʋ aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ yine wo, die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be, ba baarɩ, Wusie, die ʋ seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Malaka nan wɩɩ wɩɩŋ, ta n tʋŋ n malakasisi aŋ ba ga dʋnɩa lʋga a nɩɩsa mana mana a lagɩsɩ n vuovʋarɩkaha dieke dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋka gie lʋga a nɩɩsa wa mana.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Hɔgʋba pam dieke die dɩ dɩna Yisa kʋaŋ a nyɩŋ Galili ta suŋŋi wo wo die dɩ zie saasaa a jɩɩma wudieke dɩ yinene wo.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Nɩ vaa chunchule tɩɩka dagɩ nɩ yɩaŋ. Ka nagɩsɩ dɩ keŋ pɔsa ta vaatɩ dɩ nyɩnna nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ yɩbɩŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Bataŋ die yiwo Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Josefu nuŋ Meri, aŋaŋ Zebedi ballɩ nuŋ.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Nɩ bɩ a keŋ ye ta nyinti gie yie naa gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ n keniŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Die dɩ tʋgɩna jɩŋmɩŋ chaaŋ ligiretieŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ nyɩnna Arimatia, ʋ saaŋ dɩ yine Josefu, die ʋ gbaŋ ʋ yiwo Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lele vuosi kaaŋ kpi a kpatɩ ta wɩaha gie mana keŋ yi.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Die ʋ ga Paliti jigiŋ a ga jʋʋsɩ wa Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka, die Paliti dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba nagɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka a yɩ wa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ nan keŋ a tɩaŋ ga, ama n wʋbalɩkaha kaaŋ tɩaŋ naa daa.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Naa wɩa die Josefu die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka, a yallɩ garɩhaalɩ yɩalɩŋ a muuri wo.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ama vuoŋ ka sɩba daarɩ yaa saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ keŋ; malakasi dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ ka sɩba, manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa gbaŋ ka sɩba, sie n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna sɩbɩna.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: a nagɩ wa a dʋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ vɔrɩhaalɩ dieke ʋ tune taŋ lʋgɩŋ. Ta die biliŋ taŋ kpeŋkpɩɩŋ a keŋ ligi vɔrɩkʋ nʋaŋ ta die yiŋŋi.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Die dɩ sɩna die wo Nowa saŋka dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ die manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa keniŋ saŋŋa.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Ta die Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Meri wʋnɩŋ wa die kalɩ wa mi a daansa vɔrɩkʋ.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Nowa saŋŋa, saŋŋa dieke nyaakpɩɩbʋ dɩ yene ka keŋye, vuosi die dɩ die ta nyuo, ta faara hɔɔŋ, ta naga ba hɔgɩlɩasɩ a yɩa chʋllɩŋ, a ga tʋgɩ daa dieke Nowa dɩ june haarɩkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋʋna dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi, die dɩ yine banɩŋ Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi die dɩ ga Paliti jigiŋ.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Ta vuosi gie die ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yinene a tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nyaabʋ dɩ kenne dii ba mana. Die dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa dɩ bala n keŋ wo.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: a die baarɩ, Jakʋʋŋ tɩ tɩɩnsɩya dɩ saŋŋa dieke ŋmɩnchɩbɩtieŋ wo die dɩ benne ʋ miivoli me wo, die ʋ baarɩ, N nan hagɩ kuŋ me daraa ataa daraaŋ.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Saŋka mi dembisi bale nan dɩ tʋma kʋakʋ ma, ta n nan nagɩ wʋnyɩ ta va wʋnyɩ.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Die wɩa yɩ nʋaŋ aŋ ba mɩŋŋɩ gbara ʋ vɔrɩkʋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ataa wa daraaŋ. Dɩ daa die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nan keŋ a gaarɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka, ta balɩ yɩ vuosisi dɩ ʋ hagɩ wa kuŋ me. Ta kʋaŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ gie nan wa a tɩaŋ bʋmbʋŋaŋ jaabʋ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Hɔɔŋ bale nan dɩ nʋma zaa nɩa ma, ta n nagɩ wʋnyɩ ta va wʋnyɩ.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Paliti die dɩ balɩ ba, Nɩ nagɩ gbɩgbarɩtɩŋ aŋ ba ga a mɩŋŋɩ gbara vɔrɩkʋ, nɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a gbaraka die mana.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Die wɩa nɩ gbara dama nɩ zɩ daa dieke manɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala n keŋ.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Die wɩa, die ba ga a nagɩ gbɩgbarɩtɩŋ a ga ba ga gbarɩ vɔrɩkʋ, ta die a bʋʋlɩ tantɩ a mɩŋŋɩ taarɩ.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Ama nɩ sɩmma wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ; dɩɩ yi ta tigiŋ tieŋ dɩ keŋ sɩba saŋŋa dieke gaarʋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ yuŋ ʋ tigiŋ me, ʋ tɩŋ nan dɩ gbarɩ ta kaaŋ vaa ʋ wɩɩ ʋ tigiri a juu a gaarɩ.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Die wɩa dɩ vɩɩna ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yi siri saŋŋa mana dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan keŋ saŋŋa dieke nɩ kana ka yile dɩ n nan keŋ wo.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Yɔmʋ bɩa yine yɩantieŋ ta yie ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna dɩ ʋ yi wudieke? Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ nan vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta yɩma ba ba nyindiike saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ keŋ tigiŋ ta ye wo ta ʋ tʋma die wo gbaŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan dɩ bie ʋ ma.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ʋ nan nagɩ wa a zieŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ nyinti mana mana.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Ama ʋ yi yɔmʋbɩaŋ, ʋ nan dɩ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo kaaŋ keŋ lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: ta piili a nɩga ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan dɩ die ta nyuo aŋaŋ daanyuuriŋ.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: Ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo nan yiŋŋi keŋ daa dieke aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ kana ka yile dɩ ʋ nan keŋ.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ʋ keŋ keŋ, ʋ nan datɩ ʋ tɩbɩŋ ta nagɩ wa a gʋtɩ gɩgaantɩba ma; mi ʋ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nyɩna.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ tɩŋ, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ die ʋ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me a piili a daga kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ benne mi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Daadamba die dɩ wʋnna ʋ dagɩkʋ die dɩ yi ba mana mana mamachi, die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Sɩa ʋ ye wɩaha gie mana? Bɩa yɩaŋ wʋnna Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ a yɩ wa? Halɩ ʋ tʋmma mamachi tʋʋma.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Daa ʋ yine kampɩnta ka? Daa Meri bʋa wa wonde? Daa ʋ yine Jemisi aŋaŋ Josefu aŋaŋ Judasi aŋaŋ Simoni mɩɩ wa? Daa ʋ tamba benne giena wa? Ta ba zeti wo wo.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Ba yɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa jɩlɩma jigiŋ mana mana mɩŋ ama ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ vuosi aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ deŋ ka yɩa wa jɩlɩma.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Die ʋ ka bɩagɩ a tʋŋ mamachi tʋʋma pam mi ama ntaala yʋagɩtielibe bɩta die ʋ bɩagɩ a nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ba ma die ba ye alaafɩa.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Die dɩ paalɩ yi wo mamachi, die ba wone yadaka wɩa. Womi Yisa die dɩ ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ die dɩ benne mi chaakʋ a daga vuosisi.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Die ʋ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a lagɩsɩ taŋ a tʋŋ ba bale bale. Die ʋ yɩ ba yiko dieke die dɩ tɩanna jɩmbɩatɩtɩ.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Ta die a yɩ ba nʋarɩ gie dɩ, Daa nɩ pɔgɩlɩ jaaŋ a gamma nɩ dɩasɩsɩ ntaala, daaŋgbanɩŋ nyɩɩna ma. Nɩ daa keŋ a pɔgɩlɩ nyindiike, nɩ daa keŋ a pɔgɩlɩ bʋlɔgɩŋ nɩ daa keŋ a suu ligire nɩ jafasɩ ma.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Nɩ suu nɩɩra ama nɩ daa keŋ pɔgɩlɩ nyiŋyeeke a gʋtɩ nyiŋyeeki diekemba nɩ balla nɩ yeegi wo me.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Die ʋ bɩ a balɩ a gʋtɩ ba dɩ, Ba keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo nɩ jigidieke mana mana nɩ bemme tigiri me a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ balla nɩ nyɩŋ tɩka ma.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ama nɩ keŋ a tʋgɩ jigidieke ba keŋ a ka tuo nɩ yaa ba ka wʋŋ nɩ wʋbalɩka nɩ nyɩŋ mi ta pɩsɩ nɩ naatala kɔlɩŋ mi. Aŋ naa yi wɩɩŋ a gbara ba.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Die ba nyɩŋ a ga a bala vuosi dɩ ba chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Die ba vʋarɩ jɩmbɩatɩ pam vuosi me ta die a duuri yʋagɩtieliŋ aŋaŋ kpaaŋ ta die gbaaŋ ba.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Tɔ, Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wʋŋ Yisa wɩaha gie dama ʋ saaŋ die dɩa wa jigiŋ mana mɩŋ. Vuosi bataŋ die balɩ dɩ Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ hagɩna kuŋ me a yiŋŋi keŋ. Die wɩa ʋ yaa hagɩrɩbʋ a tʋma mamachi tʋʋmaha gie.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Ama bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ Elaja. Bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ Ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntɩba sɩba daa daa saŋka tʋntʋntɩba wʋnyɩ.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wʋnna die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ. Die n yi ba gobi wo ʋ sikpeŋ ama ʋ yiŋŋi a keŋ wo ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Naaŋ Herodi gbaŋ gbaŋ die yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ba yigi Jɔɔn a bɔba wa a nagɩ wa a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ. Herodi die yi wo die dama die wʋnɩŋ Herodi die faarɩ wa Herodiasi vuodieke ʋ kana ka mʋ ʋ faarɩ dama ʋ nɩmbʋa Filipi hɔgʋ die wonde.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die ŋaaŋ a bala Herodi daaŋ daaŋ mana dɩ, Dɩ wo sieŋ aŋaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan faarɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa hɔgʋ.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Herodiasi die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ Jɔɔn, ta die a yaala ʋ kʋʋ wa, ama Herodi wɩa die ʋ ka bɩagɩ, dama Herodi gbaŋ die ka saaga.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Herodi die chɩga Jɔɔn, dama die ʋ sɩba a baarɩ Jɔɔn die yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ta dɩgɩntɩ die dɩ wo ʋ sʋŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ ka yaala wɩɩŋ yi wo. Aŋaŋ die ʋ ŋaana wʋŋ Jɔɔn wʋbalɩkaha a daama wa mana yɔrɩ, die ʋ ŋaaŋ yaala ʋ wʋmma ʋ wʋbalɩka.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Herodiasi daaŋ die dɩ keŋ. Herodi maarɩŋ daraaŋ die dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, die ʋ digi nyindiike pam ta wa tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha mana aŋaŋ sojasi nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Galili tɩŋ nyɩŋkʋraha a lagɩsɩ taŋ.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Die Herodiasi hɔgʋlɩa die dɩ keŋ a sie dɩ paalɩ a yi naaŋ Herodi aŋaŋ ʋ chaamba mana mana sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ. Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die naaŋ wa die dɩ pɩasɩ havʋʋbike dɩ, Bɩa fʋ yaala n yɩ fʋ? N nan yɩ fʋ jadieke mana mana fʋ yaalala.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: A kaa kaatɩ pam ta a balɩ a yɩ bʋa wa N hʋʋya, jadieke mana mana fʋ yaalala, n nan yɩ fʋ halɩ fʋ yaala n puo n naarɩ a nagɩ bʋnyɩ a yɩ fʋ n nan yi.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Die wɩa die bʋa wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga pɩasɩ ʋ nuŋ dɩ, N baarɩ n yaala bɩa? Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Baarɩ dɩ yaala Jɔɔn dieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Die wɩa die bʋa wa dɩ chɩgɩ a ga naaŋ wa jigiŋ lagɩ lagɩ a baarɩ dɩ, N yaala fʋ gobi Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a diisi kpalɩ ma a yɩ mɩŋ lelele.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Naa die dɩ yi naaŋ wa sʋŋ die dɩ paalɩ a chʋʋsɩ pam, ama die ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zeti dama ʋ kaana kaatɩ diekemba ʋ chaamba nɩŋŋa wa wɩa.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ yi vuodiekemba die dɩ gbarɩna wa wʋnyɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ yaa Jɔɔn sikpeŋ a keŋ lagɩ lagɩ. Tʋntʋntʋ wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga dansarɩka sʋŋ a gobi Jɔɔn sikpeŋ.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: A die a nagɩ ka a diisi kpalɩ ma a yallɩka a keŋ a yɩ havʋʋbike, havʋʋbike die dɩ nagɩ ka a yɩ ʋ nuŋ.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna, die ba ga a nagɩ Jɔɔn nyɩŋgbaŋka a ga guu.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Yisa tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale die ʋ tʋnna wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ a balɩ wa wudiekemba mana ba yine aŋaŋ ba dagɩna.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Vuosi pam die kienene ta yiŋŋe, die wɩa die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die wo saŋŋa aŋaŋ ba baaŋ nan dii nyindiike. Die wɩa die Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ vaa aŋ tɩ ga haagɩyeŋ dieke nɩ baaŋ nan voosi.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Die wɩa die ba gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba juu haarɩŋ a gara jigidieke vuoŋ die dɩ wone.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Ama daadaŋ pam die dɩ ye be ba ganana, a mɩŋŋɩ ba. Die wɩa die ba chɩgɩ aŋaŋ nagɩsɩ a nyɩŋ tɩgɩsɩ mana mana, a woliŋ a ga a tʋgɩ jigidieke Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ganana wa.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna haarɩkʋ sʋŋ ta die a ye kpɩkpaakʋ ba wɩa dɩ yaa wa zɔɔlɩŋ, dama die ba nɩasɩ wa yii diekemba dɩ wone yiyagɩrʋ. Die wɩa die ʋ piili a daga ba wɩa pam.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Die jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩnnɩ wɔŋ a juu mɩŋ ta giena dɩaŋ dɩ yi jigidieke jadiikiŋ dɩ worine ba baaŋ daa a dii.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Vaa vuosi ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ kʋaŋ diekemba dɩ gbigine giena wa a yaalɩ jaaŋ a dii ba gbaŋ.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ ba aŋ ba dii. Die ba pɩasɩ wa, yaala tɩ nagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa mana ligire a yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ ba aŋ ba dii?.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Paanʋsɩ aŋmɩa nɩ yallɩ? Nɩ ga a ga daansɩ a ye. Die ba gana a ga daansɩ ta yiŋŋi a keŋ wo die ba balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Paanʋsɩ anʋ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale.
