eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba yi vuosisi mana puo puo a lagɩsɩ a kala kala hɔɔyebiti me.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Die wɩa die ba lagɩsɩ a kalɩ kalɩ, bataŋ baŋɩsɩ-nʋ nʋ bataŋ dɩaŋ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋsɩ anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Ʋ yi paanʋsɩsɩ a gbieri gbieri ta nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba tuohe a tɩa tɩa a yɩ vuosisi. Die ʋ bɩ nagɩ zaasɩtɩtɩ ale wo a gbieri gbieri a yɩ ba mana mana.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Ba mana mana die dɩ dii a chagɩ, ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ vaarɩ paanʋsɩ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ tɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Dembiŋ diekemba die dɩ ŋɔbɩna paanʋsɩsɩ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩtɩ die yiwo dembisi tuse tuse anʋ.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Yisa die dɩ yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ juu haarɩkʋ lagɩ lagɩ a dɩ ʋ nɩŋŋa a gara mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ a ga Befisada, ta die ʋ wa a taanna vuosisi.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Die ʋ taanna vuosisi a kpatɩ wa die ʋ jʋalɩ ga kunkogiŋ me dɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Die jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, haarɩkʋ die bie wo mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ aŋaŋ sʋŋ ta Yisa dɩ a ye bie tɩŋgbaŋ ma ʋ nyɩɩna.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Die ʋ ye ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die wahala aŋaŋ haarɩkʋ dɔmɩŋ, dama bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die nɩgɩnana ba a gara kʋaŋ kʋaŋ. Die wɩa die dɩ gana a tʋgɩ kparaasɩ kpaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ, Yisa die dɩ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a kieŋ ba jigiŋ. Die ʋ chʋŋ a tɩanna ba ma.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: ama die ba ye wo ta ʋ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ. Die ba yene dɩ ʋ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeku die ba keesi ta yi tama sɩba dɩ yiwo kogiŋ.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Dama die ba mana die dɩ yene wo ŋmaamɩŋ die yigine be. Yisa die dɩ a balɩ a yɩ ba bʋnyɩ dɩ, Nɩ yigi sikimiŋ, wo manɩŋ nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yigi nɩ.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Womi die ʋ juu a gʋtɩ ba ma haarɩkʋ ma, bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ nan sɔmm. Die dɩ paalɩ a yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mamachi pam.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: dama die ba ka sɩba die ʋ nagɩ paanʋsɩsɩ a yɩ vuosisi ba dii wo chɩaŋ. Ba sʋgɩtɩ ye ko bie wo lɩmɩŋ ma mɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Die ba ga a ga a tʋgɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ a keŋ jʋalɩ Genezariti tɩŋgbaŋ ma, a bɔbɩ haarɩkʋ mi.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Die ba nyɩnana haarɩkʋ ma vuosisi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ Yisa bʋnyɩ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die ba chɩgɩ ga juu tɩŋgbaŋka mana a chii yʋagɩtieliŋ a diisi ba kalɩŋasɩ sikpeŋ a ga jigidieke mana die ba wʋnna dɩ Yisa gaya wa.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Jigidieke mana die ʋ gana tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ yaa tɩŋkpɩɩma vuosi ŋaaŋ chii wo ba yʋagɩtieliŋ a ga nyugiti ma ta jʋʋsa wa dɩ ʋ ko a vaa yʋagɩtielibe gbɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke kʋanʋaŋ, vuodiekemba mana mana die dɩ gbɩna ka die ye wo alaafɩa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ jʋalɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaakʋ mi, Gerasa tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yisa die dɩ dene nyɩnna haarɩkʋ sʋkʋ, lele womi daa wʋnyɩ dɩ nyɩnna jigidieke ba lɔbɩrɩnana vuosi dɩ keŋ a haarɩ wa.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: Jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa, daa wa gie die dɩ ga gʋʋra kunti vɔrɩtɩtɩ ma, die ba ka bɩagɩ a bɔbɩ wa a dʋaŋ aŋaŋ chɔrɩma.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Dama ba keŋ bɔbɩ wa ʋ nuusi aŋaŋ ʋ nagɩsɩ ʋ ŋaaŋ kpaa kpaa chɔrɩmaha mɩŋ ʋ nuusi me ta wɩɩ kʋʋtɩtɩ ʋ nagɩsɩ ma. Die ʋ paalɩ a hagɩrɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ dɩ baaŋ nan nyaŋŋɩ wa.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Daaŋ mana sʋkʋʋŋ aŋaŋ yuŋ ʋ ŋaaŋ dɩ chʋŋ wa vɔrɩtɩ aŋaŋ kunkogehe me a nata ta naga tana a gobe ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Die ʋ bie wo saasaa ta die a ye Yisa, die wɩa die ʋ chɩgɩ a ga gbirigi ʋ dune me Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: ta faasɩ a taaŋ keesiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ Yisa, bɩa fʋ yaala n jigiŋ, Ŋmɩŋ wɩa daa keŋ datɩ n tɩbɩŋ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Die ʋ balɩ wa die dama Yisa die balɩ a yɩ jɩmbɩakʋ mɩŋ dɩ, Vaa daa wa gie.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ saaŋ? Daa wa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N saaŋ yine Dala, dama tɩ faasɩ a dala mɩŋ.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ta die ʋ ye ko a jʋʋsa Yisa dɩ ʋ da keŋ a yagɩ ba a nyɩŋ lʋgɩrɩ mi chaaŋ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Perukusi pam die gbigine mi a die nyinti kunkogiri mi chaaŋ.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ, Vaa tɩ gamma perukusisi jigiŋ a ga juu ba sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Die chɩaŋ ma die Yisa dɩ yi die jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ nyɩŋ daa wa ma a ga juu perukusisi ma. Perukusisi die yiwo sɩba tuse tuse ale a mana mana die dɩ chɩgɩ a dɩɩ jigidieke dɩ golinne wo a ga a juu mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ, nyaabʋ die dɩ dii he.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Dembiŋ diekemba die dɩ jɩɩnana perukusisi die chɩgɩ a ga tɩka sʋŋ aŋaŋ ka tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ a balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie a yɩ ba. Vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga a ye wudieke die dɩ yine wo.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Die ba ga Yisa jigiŋ a ye daa dieke die jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ yalla wa ta ʋ kallɩ wa mi a yeegi nyiŋyeeke ta ʋ sikpeŋ dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tʋma, ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yallɩ ba mana mana.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ yene wudieke die dɩ yine wo die dɩ piili a bala a yɩa vuosi wudieke dɩ yine daa dieke die jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ yalla wa aŋaŋ perukusisi.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Die wɩa die vuosi dɩ piili a jʋʋsa Yisa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ ba tɩka sʋŋ.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yisa die dɩ gana a juu haarɩkʋ ma wa daa dieke die jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ yalla wa die dɩ jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ, Vaa manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ ga.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ ka saagɩya, ta yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Yiŋŋi a ga fʋ dembe jigiŋ a balɩ ba Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala fʋ wɩa dene ta piili a yi wudieke a yɩ fʋ.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Die wɩa daa wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ baŋ wa mana mana a balɩ wudieke Yisa die dɩ yine a yɩ wa wa. Vuodiekemba mana mana die dɩ wʋnna ʋ balɩkʋ die dɩ yi be mamachi.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi a jʋalɩ a ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo chaaŋ, daadaŋ pam die dɩ keŋ mili wo.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Jaarusi, ta die yi Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ mi. Die ʋ dene ye Yisa wa, ʋ nan a gbirigi ʋ dune me.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: ta jʋʋsa wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta baarɩ dɩ, N hɔgʋlɩabiŋ faasɩna a yʋagɩ. N jʋʋsa fʋ, keŋ a nagɩ fʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma aŋ ʋ ye alaafɩa a bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Womi Yisa aŋaŋ wa die dɩ gara, daadaŋ pam die faasɩna a dɩ wa a gara die wɩa die ba kpaŋŋɩ wa jigiŋ mana mana.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne mi zɩŋ dɩ nyɩna ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma, die dɩ faasɩ a wʋŋ wa mɩŋ bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Die ʋ paalɩ a ga wa gbɩgbaantɩŋ pam jige dɩ ʋ ye gbaamɩŋ. Die ʋ dii ʋ ligire mana mana a ga kpatɩ, ama ʋ tɩŋ baaŋ nan ye alaafɩaka die dɩ wa a paalɩ a mugise wo daaŋ mana mana.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Die ʋ wʋnna Yisa wɩa wa die ʋ ga ʋ jigiŋ a dɩɩ ʋ kʋaŋ daadamba sʋŋ.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ, Maŋ tɩŋ a ko a bɩagɩ a gbɩ ʋ nyiŋyeekehe gie n tɩŋ nan ye alaafɩa.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Die ʋ gbɩ Yisa nyiŋyeekehe gie die ʋ nyɩŋbaŋka zɩmbʋ dɩ gobi bʋnyɩ, die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ ʋ sʋŋ ma sɩba ʋ wahala mana nyɩŋya mɩŋ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Die dɩ dene a yi, Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ lagɩ lagɩ sɩba hagɩrɩŋ nyɩŋ ʋ ma ma mɩŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ daadamba dɩ, Mɩnɩa gbɩna n nyiŋyeekehe.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Ye daadamba dɩ milinene fʋ die, dɩ bɩa fʋ pɩasa dɩ mɩnɩa gbɩna fʋ.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Ama ta Yisa dɩ ye ko a daansa a tɩanna dɩ ʋ ye vuodieke dɩ yine die wo.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ sɩbɩna wudieke die dɩ yine wo die ʋ keŋ a cheele aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ ta gbirigi ʋ dune me a balɩ wusie a yɩ wa.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, N lɩa, fʋ yadaka gbaaŋ fʋ. Gamma aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ, fʋ yʋagɩbʋ kaaŋ bɩ a yiŋŋi a keŋ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene ko bala naa wa, tʋntʋntɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ Jaarusi tigiŋ a keŋ balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ lɩa wa yʋagɩbʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, dɩ bɩa fʋ bɩ a daama dɩdagɩrʋ wa bɩbra.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Yisa baga die wo ba balɩkʋ sʋŋ ta die a balɩ a yɩ Jaarusi dɩ, Da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ, fʋnɩŋ ko tuo dii.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Womi die ʋ yi Piita aŋaŋ Jemisi, aŋaŋ Jemisi nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn nyɩɩna ma die dɩ dɩɩ wa.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Die ba gana a tʋgɩ Jaarusi tigiri, die Yisa die dɩ ye jigiŋ mana die dɩ chine a zie naanyʋŋʋlʋŋ.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Die ʋ ga a juu a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ bɩa watɩ gie mana? Dɩ bɩa nɩ kʋma? Bʋa wa ka kpiye, ʋ ta ko gʋʋra mɩŋ.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Die ba piili a vʋara wa fala. Die wɩa die ʋ yagɩ ba mana a nyɩŋ ta die a wa bʋa wa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba bataa wa a ga juu jigidieke bʋa wa die dɩ dʋana wa.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Die ʋ yigi ʋ nuuŋ me ta balɩ a yɩ wa, Talɩta kumi , ka chɩaŋ wone Havʋʋbiŋ n baa fʋ hagɩ.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Die ʋ hagɩ bʋnyɩ a dɩdɩ chʋŋ chʋŋ. Naa die dɩ yine wo, die dɩ paalɩ a yi be mamachi pam.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Ama Yisa dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ ta paalɩ balɩ ba dɩ ba daa keŋ a balɩ a yɩ vuoŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ ba yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ bʋa wa aŋ ʋ dii.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Tɔ, Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ nyɩnna Jerusalemi a keŋ die dɩ taaŋ giliŋ Yisa atɩaŋ.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Die ba daansɩ a ye ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die nyindiike aŋaŋ nuudɩgɩnta. Dama die ba ka nɩtɩ ba nuusi sɩba Farasisi vuosi die dɩ bala dɩ vuosi nɩtɩma ba nuusi die wo.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Ba keŋ nyɩŋ nyugiti jige a keŋ, ba ka die sie ba nɩtɩ ba nuusi ta dɩɩsa ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ wɩa, sɩba nyanyuule kpala daarɩŋ aŋaŋ gbiesi daarɩŋ aŋaŋ kalɩŋasɩ sugiriŋ wɩa.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ bɩa yine fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka dɩɩsa sie diekemba tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ nagɩna a yɩ tɩ wa ta die nyinti aŋaŋ nuudɩgɩnta.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die yaa wusie die ʋ bala nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie wɩa sɩba ka maagɩna die wo dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ vuosi gie yɩa mɩŋ jɩlɩma aŋaŋ ba nʋa ama ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ bie saasaa aŋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Dɩ yi wo yɔrɩ ba jɩama mɩŋ dama ba daga vuota ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba mɩra sɩba a yiwo manɩŋ n mɩra. .

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Nɩ zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ mɩra ta dɩɩsa vuotaŋ mɩra.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a balɩ a gʋtɩ ba dɩ Nɩ yaa wa yɩaŋ sɩbɩŋ a zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩra ta bɩagɩ a daga nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yaala wudiekemba.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Mosisi die baarɩ dɩ, Yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ a kaa kaatɩ a yɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ mʋ ba kʋʋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ baarɩ dɩ Vuoŋ dɩ keŋ a balɩ a yɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ dɩ jadieke mana dɩ yine n sɩɩtɩ maŋ tɩŋ nan nagɩ a suŋŋi nɩ bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jaaŋ.

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Die nɩŋ nɩnɩŋ Farasisi yɩa vuosi sieŋ dɩ ba da keŋ suŋŋi ba chʋalɩŋ yaa ba nɩɩlɩŋ nɩ dagɩkʋ ma.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Naa dɩ a keŋ a yi die nɩŋ nɩ chʋsa Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha, nɩ dagɩnana vuosi die wo sʋŋ. Nɩ yie wɩa pam a nɩasa die.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ bɩ a wa daadamba bɩbra a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, ta balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ mana mana chɩɩsɩma a wʋmma mɩŋ ta sɩmma n wʋbalɩkaha chɩasɩ.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Jadieke vuotaŋ dɩ dinene a yie ʋ sʋŋ ma ka bɩagɩ a yie wo dɩgɩntɩ ama wudieke dɩ nyɩnnana ʋ nʋaŋ ma yine ʋ yaa dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Die ʋ vana daadamba ta kuli tigiŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ka sɩba ka chɩaŋ? Nɩ ka sɩba sɩba dɩ jadieke dɩ junene vuotaŋ nʋaŋ ma ka bɩagɩ yie wo ʋ yalla dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Dama a juu wo ʋ nyuuti ma a daaga nyɩŋgbaŋka a nyɩnna ama da ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Die ʋ ye a ko bala a gʋta dɩ Wudieke dɩ nyɩnnana vuotaŋ sʋŋ yine ʋ yaa dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Dama ʋ sʋŋ ma sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ sɩba hɔɔŋ yaalɩŋ aŋaŋ dembisi yaalɩŋ, aŋaŋ gaarɩŋ aŋaŋ vuotaŋ kʋʋla.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: aŋaŋ jome aŋaŋ kalinbaani aŋaŋ sugigoliŋ wuyiike aŋaŋ halɩ aŋaŋ gaanta aŋaŋ baga wori wuyiike.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Wʋbɩatɩ gie mana mana nyɩnna vuotaŋ sʋŋ a yie wo ʋ bɩrɩma vuodɩgɩntɩŋ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ die dɩ bene Taya tɩŋgbaŋ ma, die ʋ ga a juu tigiŋ kaanɩ sʋŋ ta die a ka yaala vuoŋ ye wo, ama die ʋ ka bɩagɩ a lɔbɩrɩ ʋ gbaŋ.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne mi ta jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yallɩ ʋ havʋʋbiŋ, die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Yisa die bie mi die ʋ chɩgɩ a keŋ gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die ka yi Juu vuoŋ, die ba mɩɩrɩ wa wa Fonisia, Siria tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma. Die ʋ jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ ʋ bʋa wa ma ma.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Ama Yisa die yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Vaa tɩ woliŋ a yɩ ballɩbɩsɩ ba nyindiike, dɩ wo sieŋ aŋaŋ tɩ baaŋ nan nagɩ ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike a yɩ gbaasɩ.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ka yiwo wusie, ama gbaasɩsɩ die dɩ dʋana teebulisi chɩaŋ gbaŋ naga ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike chɔɔta die dɩ nannana.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, Fʋ mɩŋŋɩna balɩ balɩkʋ gie wɩa nan bɩagɩ a kuli, jɩmbɩakʋ nyɩŋ fʋ bʋa wa ma mɩŋ.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a kuli ta ʋ bʋa wa dʋa gado sikpeŋ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ seŋ a nyɩŋ ʋ ma.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Yisa die dɩ wa a nyɩŋ Taya tɩŋgbaŋ ma a daagɩ Sadoni a chʋŋ a daagɩ tɩgɩsɩ baŋka sʋgɩtɩ a ga Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ yaa daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ kana a ka mɩŋŋɩ bala wɩa a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Yisa die dɩ yigi daa wa nuuŋ me a yaa wa nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ ta vaa daadamba. Yisa die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuubisi a yi daa wa tɩba ma, ta tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ ta gbɩ daa wa jiliŋ.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta paalɩ a voosi ta a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Effata! Ka chɩaŋ wone, Yuori.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Daa wa tɩbaha sʋŋ die dɩ yuori bʋnyɩ ʋ wʋmma wʋbalɩka ta bɩagɩ a bala wɩa vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba mana mana nʋaŋ dɩ ba sɩmma ba keŋ a balɩ wɩɩrɩ mi a yɩ vuoŋ. Ama saŋŋa dieke mana die Yisa dɩ a keŋ a balɩ die, die dɩ wana a faasɩ a yie ba bala wɩɩrɩ jigiŋ mana.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wʋnna die dɩ yi be mamachi pam pam. Die ba baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ mɩŋŋɩ a yi wɩɩrɩ mana vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ. Halɩ ʋ yi vuodiekemba mana dɩ kana a ka bɩagɩ a wʋmma wɩa dɩ wʋmma ta vuodieke dɩ kana ka bɩagɩ bala wɩa dɩ bala.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya ta daadaŋ pam bɩbra die dɩ keŋ a lagɩsɩ Yisa jigiŋ. Jadiikiŋ die wori aŋaŋ ba baaŋ dii, Yisa die dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Zɔɔlɩŋ yigi mɩŋ mɩŋ pam aŋaŋ vuosi gie dama manɩŋ aŋaŋ ba daraa ataa wʋnna, ba ka bɩ yaa jaaŋ aŋaŋ ba baaŋ dii.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: Maŋ keŋ yi ba kuli ta ka dii jaaŋ bataŋ nan chaa sieŋ me dama ba nyɩŋ wa saasaa.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Haagɩrɩ gie me, sie vuoŋ dɩ baa ʋ ye jaaŋ a yɩ daadamba gie mana aŋ ba dii.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Paanʋsɩ aŋmɩa nɩ yallɩ? Ba baarɩ dɩ, A bayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ daadamba dɩ ba sʋʋŋ kala tɩŋgbaŋ, ta nagɩ paanʋsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ wa a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta yi tɩa tɩa a nagɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba nagɩ a yɩ daadamba, ba yi dene.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Die ba bɩ yaa zaasɩbɩsɩ bɩta ba jigiŋ. Yisa dɩ nagɩha a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta nagɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba bɩ tuo a tɩa tɩa a yɩ daadamba.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Vuoŋ mana die diye mɩŋ ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ, vuosisi die yiwo sɩba vuosi tuse tuse anɩɩsa.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩna nyindiike tɩalɩkaha, die a suuli wo kparɩsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ. Yisa die dɩ wa taaŋ vuosisi ba kuli.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: ta die wa juu haarɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba ga Dalimanuta tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, a piili nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ. Die ba yaala ba yigi wo mɩŋ, die wɩa die ba baarɩ dɩ ʋ yi mamachi a dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna wa.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Yisa dɩ voosi pam ta pɩasɩ, dɩ bɩa lele gie vuosi dɩ pɩasa a yaala mamachi tʋʋma? Ʋ baarɩ dɩ wusie, n kaaŋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ aŋ vuosi gie ye.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Ta die ʋ va ba ta yiŋŋi a juu haarɩkʋ ba nagɩ ga a gara mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ kaanɩ wa ma.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die daaŋya aŋaŋ ba baaŋ pɔgɩlɩ nyindiike. Die ba pɔgɩlɩ wa paanʋ balɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna haarɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ a baarɩ, dɩ ba sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Herodi vuosi wɩa.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Die ba piili a bala a yɩa tamba, ʋ balla naa dama tɩ wo paanʋ wɩa.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba wʋbalɩkaha, ta pɩasɩ ba, dɩ bɩa nɩ bala sɩba nɩ wo paanʋ wɩa? Nɩ ye ka yeye, yaa nɩ ye ka sɩba, dɩ bɩa nɩ wo sʋŋanyile dene?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Nɩ yaa nine, ama nɩ ka yese. Nɩ yaa tɩba, ama bɩa wɩa nɩ ka wʋmma? Nɩ ka tɩɩnsa.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: die n nagɩna paanʋsɩ anʋ a puo puo a yɩ vuosi tuse anʋ wa nyintɩalɩkaha kparɩsɩ aŋmɩa die nɩ vaarɩ a suuli? Ba baarɩ Kparɩsɩ baŋ-ale.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Ta die n nagɩna paanʋsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ a puo puo a yɩ vuosi tuse anɩɩsa wa nyintɩalɩkaha kparɩsɩ aŋmɩa die nɩ vaarɩ? Ba baarɩ kparɩsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: ʋ pɩasɩ ba, ta nɩ ye ka sɩba?.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Die ba tʋgɩna Befisada ma vuosi bataŋ die yaa dembiŋ yɩɩ a keŋ jʋʋsɩ wa, ta baarɩ dɩ ʋ gbɩ wa.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Die ʋ yigi yɩɩ wa nuuŋ me a yaa wa nyɩŋ tɩka ma. Atɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yi yɩɩ wa nine me, ta nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩagɩ ye jaaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Daa wa dɩ daansɩ sikpeŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, Wa, n ye vuosi ama ba nɩasɩ wa tɩɩsɩ dɩ dɩna dɩ chʋŋ chʋŋ.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Yisa dɩ bɩ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi daa wa ninehe me, saŋka mi nɩŋ daa wa nine dɩ yuori, ʋ ye jaaŋ mana kasɩ.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ ʋ kulime ta yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ, Da keŋ yiŋŋi ga tɩka ma.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die gara tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ die dɩ gbigine Siisiria Filipi tɩŋ ma. Sieku me ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ, mɩnɩa vuosi dɩ baarɩ n yi ye.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ba baarɩ dɩ, Bataŋ baarɩ yiwo Jɔɔn die dɩ kenne a keŋ a sɩɩ vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ Elaja, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ wʋnyɩ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ mɩnɩa nɩ baarɩ n yiye? Piita dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Yiwo Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Masiaka.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Mi, Yisa dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ dɩ, Nɩ da keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ n wɩa.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Ta wa piili a daga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Sie manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembike dii wahala pam, ta nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ tigiri me mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba keŋ zeti mɩŋ, a bɩ kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Die ʋ paalɩ balɩ ba mɩŋ, die ʋ ka lɔbɩrɩ ba. Die Piita die dɩ yaa wa ga lʋgɩŋ a ga galɩŋ wa.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Ama Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a daansa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta kpaaŋ Piita a baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ Sitaani wa gie hagɩ giena. Fʋ sʋŋanyile yiwo vuota sʋŋanyile daa Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋanyile.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Yisa die dɩ wa wa daadamba aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a balɩ ba dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ keŋ a yaala ʋ dɩa n kʋaŋ sie ʋ daaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wɩa ta nagɩ ʋ kuŋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yaalala ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ miivoli nan waarɩ ka, ama vuodieke dɩ a keŋ waarɩ ʋ miivoli n wɩa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩna wɩa nan ye ke.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Bɩa vuoŋ dɩ baa ʋ ye, ʋ keŋ a ye dʋnɩa mana mana nyinti ta waarɩ ʋ miivoli? Wusie.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Jaaŋ wori vuotaŋ dɩ nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ a gbatɩ ʋ miivoli.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Lele gie vuosi yi wo bɩaŋ ta ka dɩa Ŋmɩŋ. Vuodieke dɩ a keŋ chɩga viivi a ka balɩ manɩŋ aŋaŋ n dagɩkʋ gie vuosi gie jigiŋ, die nɩŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ nan daaŋ chɩgɩ dɩ tieŋ gbaŋ viivi maŋ keŋ keŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kasɩ malakasisi dɩ n yaa wa a ga gʋtɩ n vuosi me.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, N bala nɩ, bataŋ bie giena ba kaaŋ kpi ntaala ba keŋ a ye Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keniŋ aŋaŋ ka hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Daraa ayʋaba kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ wa Piita aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn, a dɩ ba nɩŋŋa a yaa ba a jʋalɩ taŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ sikpeŋ, jigidieke vuoŋ die dɩ wone mi. Die ba daansɩnana ba keŋ a ye ta Yisa tarɩgɩya mɩŋ.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: ʋ nyɩŋsuuke die dɩ yɩantɩ ta nyɩgɩsa, a paalɩ yɩantɩ tɩaŋ vuota dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a sugiri jaaŋ ka yɩantɩ die dʋnɩa ma.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Mi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Jemisi dɩ ye Elaja aŋaŋ Mosisi vuodiekemba die dɩ kpine kʋrɩŋ ma wa aŋaŋ Yisa dɩ balala wɩa.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Die wɩa, Piita die dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, dɩ vɩɩna tɩ benne giena. Tɩ nan kpii viisi ataa, fʋ kaanɩ, Mosisi kaanɩ, Elaja dɩaŋ kaanɩ.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Ŋmaamɩŋ die faasɩna a yigi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wɩa, Piita die dɩ zɩ ʋ baaŋ balɩ die.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Womi nɩɩŋmara dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ ligi be, ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ nyɩŋ nɩɩŋmaraha ma ma a bala dɩ, N Bʋadembiŋ dieke n chone wʋnna, nɩ wʋmma a yɩma wa.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a daansɩ lʋga lagɩ lagɩ ta ba ka ye vuoŋ sie banɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa nyɩɩna ma.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Die ba kenne a sʋʋna tanɩ ma wa, Yisa dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ dɩ, Da nɩ keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ nɩ yene die, sie manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ dɩ keŋ a hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Die ba saagɩ a yɩ wa ama ta wa piili a bala tamba wɩɩrɩ wɩa, ta baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ balala kumbu hagɩŋ ma wa, ka chɩaŋ.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Lele mi ba pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, Bɩa yine Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ baarɩ sie Elaja woliŋ keŋ ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Masia wa dɩaŋ keŋ.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Yisa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Wusie Elaja baa ʋ woliŋ keŋ mɩŋ a keŋ wʋnsɩ wɩɩŋ mana a zieŋ, ta bɩa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dɩ wa daga sɩba vuota Bʋadembiŋ nan faasɩ gbalɩgɩ ta ba bɩ zeti wo?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ, Elaja wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ vuosi dɩ yi wo ba dʋŋŋʋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka die dɩ dagɩna ʋ wɩa die wo.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Die banɩŋ ba ga haarɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ tɩalɩkaha ta daadaŋ giliŋ be, Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Die vuosisi dɩ yene Yisa dɩ faasɩ a yi be wuboŋkeŋ ba chɩgɩ ga ʋ jigiŋ a waasɩ wa.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ bɩa wɩɩŋ wɩa nɩ ŋaŋ ba dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ?.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Daadamba sʋŋ daa wʋnyɩ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, n yaa wa n Bʋadembiŋ a keŋ fʋ jigiŋ, dama jɩmbɩatɩ die yalla wa ta ʋ ka bɩagɩ a bala wɩa.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Jɩmbɩatɩ gie dɩ keŋ a hagɩ a ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa taaŋ mɩŋ, ta fʋnfʋgɩtɩ dɩ bie ʋ nʋaŋ ma, ʋ nyɩna ŋaaŋ chɩa wa taŋ ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ hagɩrɩ. Die maŋ balɩ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba gbaaŋ wa die ba bɩlɩ waarɩ.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ wo yada, lalɩa nɩ bɩ yaala maŋ ŋaŋ nɩ bemme a yʋasɩ, nɩ yaa bʋa wa a keŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ba yaa bʋa wa a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ yene Yisa, ka yi bʋa wa dɩ chaa, die wɩa a yi ʋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ a bilime mi, ta fʋnfʋgɩtɩ dɩ nyɩnna ʋ nʋaŋ ma.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ bʋa wa chɔɔŋ wa dɩ, Bɩna aŋmɩa wʋnna a yalla wa? Ʋ chɔɔŋ wa dɩ baarɩ Die ʋ yine bʋabiŋ mana.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Daaŋ mana jɩmbɩakʋ ŋaaŋ yaala ka kʋʋ wa mɩŋ, ka ŋaaŋ vigi wo mɩŋ a taanna boliŋ me aŋaŋ nyaaŋ ma. Chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ ta fʋ suŋŋi tɩ, fʋ nan bɩagɩ suŋŋi.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ daa wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ bala fʋ nan bɩagɩ suŋŋi! Wɩɩŋ wori a nyaŋŋa vuodieke dɩ yalla yada.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Bʋa wa chɔɔŋ wa dɩ keesi bʋnyɩ, ta baarɩ dɩ, N yaa yada mɩŋ ama n yadaka ka dala, suŋŋi mɩŋ aŋ bʋ dalɩma.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yisa die ye ta daadamba gbigiye ʋ yɩ lɔlɩŋ dɩ, N baarɩ, jɩmbɩakʋ dieke dɩ yine bʋa wa dɩ ka wʋmma wa ta bala wɩaha, nyɩŋ ʋ ma ta da bɩ yiŋŋi a juu ʋ ma.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ keesi ta nagɩ bʋa wa a nɩgɩ tɩŋgbaŋ pemm ta die faasɩ a datɩ wa ta nyɩŋ ʋ ma, bʋa wa dɩ nɩasɩ kuŋ, vuoŋ mana die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ yigi ʋ nuuŋ me a suŋŋi wo ʋ hagɩ zie.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yiŋŋine a juu juoŋ me ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa die yine tɩ ka bɩagɩ a yagɩ jɩmbɩakʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Die ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ nyɩɩna nan bɩagɩ a yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ nɩasɩna naa, jaaŋ bɩ wori.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Die ba nyɩŋ mi a ga Galili me, Yisa die ka yaala vuoŋ sɩmma ba benne jigidieke.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: dama die ʋ daga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mɩŋ. Die ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Ba nan nagɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋadembiŋ wa a yɩ vuosi nuusi me aŋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ, ama ka daraa ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan hagɩ kumbu me.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Die ba ka sɩba ʋ dagɩkʋ chɩaŋ ama ŋmaamɩŋ die yalla ba aŋaŋ ba baaŋ nan pɩasɩ wa.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Ba keŋ Kapenɔmi a juu juoŋ a kpatɩ, ʋ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Bɩa wɩɩŋ nɩ tɩŋ yaa nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩbʋ sieku me wo.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Die ba ka yaala ba balɩ wa dama die ba nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ ba jabɩa yine jakʋʋŋ.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Yisa die dɩ sʋʋŋ kalɩ ta wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo, a balɩ ba, vuodieke mana dɩ yaala ʋ yi jakʋʋŋ sie ʋ wɩarɩ kʋaŋ ta nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi vuoŋ mana yɔmʋ.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Die ʋ wa bʋabiŋ a nagɩ wa zieŋ ba nɩŋŋa a nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma ta balɩ ba.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ a keŋ saagɩ a tuo bʋabike gie chanchaaŋ n saaŋ ma tuose mɩŋ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩaŋ dɩ tuosine mɩŋ, tuo wo vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, die tɩ ye wo vuoŋ dɩ yagɩnana jɩmbɩatɩ vuoŋ me fʋ saaŋ ma, die tɩ balɩ wa dɩ ʋ vaa dama die ʋ ka yi tɩ wʋnyɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba, Daa nɩ yi ʋ vaa dama, vuoŋ dɩ bɩagɩ a yie mamachi wɩa n saaŋ ma ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsa n saaŋ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka waga tɩ gʋtɩ wa tɩ ma.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ naa, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kenne yɩ nɩ nyaaŋ nɩ nyuu dama nɩ dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋ nan daansɩ ye ka nyʋarɩ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Yisa dɩ baarɩ, Vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ yi ballɩbɩsɩ gie wʋnyɩ dɩ tuone mɩŋ a dii wo dɩ yi wʋbɩaŋ, dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa ba nagɩna nɩɩŋ a bɔbɩ a yeegi ʋ nyie ta nagɩ wa a taaŋ nyaakpɩɩbʋ ma.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ nuuŋ yinene fʋ yie wʋbɩatɩ fʋ gobike taaŋ, dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan juu arɩzanna aŋaŋ nuubalɩmɩŋ, aŋaŋ fʋ baaŋ juu boli dieke me dɩ wone kpatɩŋ aŋaŋ nuusi ale.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ naŋ yinene fʋ yie wʋbɩatɩ fʋ gobike taaŋ dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ juu arɩzanna aŋaŋ naŋ gobiŋ a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ dɩ yallɩ nagɩsɩ ale ta juu boliŋ me.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Dɩ bɩ yiwo fʋ niŋ dɩaŋ yine fʋ yie wʋbɩatɩ vʋarɩka taaŋ, dama, dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan juu arɩzanna aŋaŋ nimbalɩmɩŋ a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan juu boliŋ me aŋaŋ nine ale.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Jigidieke zɩŋzʋa dɩ benne ba sʋŋ a die ta ka kpie, ta bolibu dɩaŋ ka kpise.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nan daansɩ nagɩ boliŋ a wʋnsɩ vuoŋ mana sɩba ba ŋaana naga yasɩ a wʋnsa kaabɩŋ nɔŋ die wo.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yasɩ nansɩ mɩŋ, ama ka nansɩbʋ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ, lalɩa ka bɩ baa ka ye ka nansɩbʋ bɩbra? Nɩ bemme aŋaŋ taŋ sɩba yasɩ nansɩbʋ aŋ nɩ bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ tamba.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Yisa die dɩ taanna ba nandaga dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die benne a suu tɩɩsɩ dieke dɩ nyɩnana bie, ta mɩɩ gaamɩŋ a geliŋke, ta die a tuu vɔrɩŋ jigidieke ba baaŋ nan dɩ ŋmʋa nyɩŋ biehe nyaabʋ a yie: ta die a mɩɩ jagɩŋ kʋakʋ ma, ta die nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ tʋntʋntɩŋ nuusi me dɩ ba daansɩma ka. Dɩ ka diile saŋŋa dɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ ba kpaa a yɩ wa ta dɩaŋ wa kpaa ba tʋaŋ. Ta die wa nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ ba ta dɩa ga jigiŋ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Tɩɩsɩsɩ bie saŋŋa die dɩ tʋgɩna ka nyɩŋ ʋ tʋŋ ʋ yɔmʋ dɩ ʋ ga vuodiekemba dɩ benne kʋakʋ ma a tuo ʋ tʋaŋ a keŋ.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Vuosisi die dɩ yigi yɔmʋ wa, a nɩgɩ wa, ta yi ʋ yiŋŋi a ga aŋaŋ nuuwɩaga.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Tɩɩsɩsɩ tieŋ die dɩ bɩ tʋŋ yɔmʋ wʋnyɩ bɩbra, vuosisi die dɩ nɩgɩ ʋ sikpeŋ me ta nagɩ wa a yi viivi me.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Tɩɩsɩsɩ tieŋ die dɩ bɩ tʋŋ ʋ yɔmʋ wʋnyɩ bɩbra die ba kʋʋ wa. Die ba yi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ pam dene gbaŋ gbaŋ a nɩga bataŋ ta kʋa bataŋ.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Vuodieke nyɩɩna die dɩ tɩala daa wa dɩ baaŋ nan tʋŋ die wone ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ bʋadembiŋ dieke ʋ yaalala ʋ wɩa. Kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ tʋŋ wa ta baarɩ N yi yada dɩ ba nan yɩ n bʋa wa jɩlɩma.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama vuosisi gie die dɩ balɩ a yɩ tamba, Kʋakʋ tieŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ bʋadembiŋ wʋnna, nɩ kiere aŋ tɩ kʋʋ wa, ta ʋ sɩɩtɩ yi tɩ nyinti.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Die ba yigi bʋa wa a kʋʋ wa ta nagɩ wa vigi taaŋ kʋakʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Lalɩa kʋakʋ tieŋ dɩ baa ʋ yi? Ʋ nan keŋ kʋʋ vuosisi gie ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ vuogaasɩ.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ a wʋŋ wɩɩrɩ gie? Dɩ Taŋ dieke mɩmɩɩrɩba die dɩ zetine ta baarɩ ba ka yaala ka wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ yi gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Ŋmɩŋ yine die; ka faasɩ yi tɩ mamachi. .

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yaala sieŋ dɩ ba yigi wo, dama die ba sɩba a baarɩ ba wɩa die ʋ taaŋ nandagɩrɩ gie. Ama die ba chɩga daadamba ta die vaa wa ta ga.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Die ba tʋŋ Farasisi vuosi aŋaŋ Herodi vuosi bataŋ dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa wʋpɩasɩka ta ye dɩ ʋ nan balɩ wʋbɩaŋ.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Die ba ga Yisa jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ sɩba a baarɩ bala wusie, ta ka chɩɩlɩ vuosi sʋŋanyilike. Fʋnɩŋ bala wusie yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaala vuotaŋ sɩmma die ta ka daansa yese vuoŋ dɩ sɩna die. Balɩ tɩ, dɩ mʋ dɩ tɩ tumme lampo a yɩma Roma naaŋ wa mɩŋ? Tɩ tumme yaa tɩ zeti.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Ama Yisa die mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ba paalɩya mɩŋ ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yaala nɩ magɩsɩ mɩŋ? Nɩ yaa ligiribiŋ a keŋ aŋ n ye ke.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ba yaa a keŋ ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa nine chaaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ saaŋ benne ka ma dene? Ba baarɩ dɩ, Naaŋ Siiza.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Yisa die dɩ wa baarɩ dɩ, Tɔ, nɩ tuŋ naaŋ wa dɩ mʋna ʋ tuo dene ta tuŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ mʋna ʋ tuo dene. Yisa wɩa die dɩ yi be mamachi.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Womi Sadusisi, vuosi bataŋ dɩ balla dɩ vuoŋ kaaŋ hagɩ kuŋ me, dɩ ga Yisa jigiŋ a ga pɩasɩ wa dɩ.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Dɩdagɩrʋ, Mosisi die nagɩna mɩrɩka gie a yɩ tɩ: dɩ dembiŋ dɩ a keŋ kpi ta vaa ʋ hɔgʋ ta wo ballɩ, dɩ daa wa nɩmbʋa mʋ ʋ faarɩ wa kpihɔgʋ wa amʋ ba mɩɩrɩ ballɩ aŋ ba yi vuodieke dɩ kpine ballɩ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Tɔ, saŋŋa kaanɩ dembisi bayʋpɔyɩ die benne a yi nɩmballɩ: jakʋʋrɩ chaaŋ die dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke die dɩ diisine jakʋʋrɩ ma die dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ wa gbaŋ ta kpi ta vaa hɔgʋ wa ta wo ballɩ. Dene gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ bɩ a yi vuodieke die dɩ diisine bʋtaa wa ma.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Aŋaŋ tɩalɩka ha mana. Ba bayʋpɔyɩ wa mana die faarɩ hɔgʋ wa mɩŋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ, ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋ wa gbaŋ die dɩ kpi.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Lele, kuŋ hagɩŋ daraaŋ ba mana dɩ a keŋ hagɩ, ba jabɩa balla ʋ sɩmma hɔgʋ wa? Ba bayʋpɔyɩ wa mana die faarɩna wa mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ka sɩba wusie! Dama nɩ zɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbanɩŋ wɩa yaa Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Kunti dɩ a keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ba nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba malakasi dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dene wo, ta hɔɔŋ faarɩŋ yaa dembiŋ yallɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ beri.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Dɩ tɩalɩ kunti hagɩŋ nɩŋ wɩa nɩ ka karɩŋ Mosisi gbaŋkʋ yaa gamma tɩɩka diile wɩa? Dɩ maagɩya mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die balɩ a yɩ Mosisi dɩ, N yiwo Abarahami Ŋmɩŋ, Aziki Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ Jakobu Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Vuosi gie die wɔŋ kpi mɩŋ ama ba ye ko bie wo ba mɩsɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ ka yiwo kunti Ŋmɩŋ ʋ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba mɩsɩ ma ma Ŋmɩŋ. Nɩ daga vuosi dɩ kunti kaaŋ bɩ hagɩ wa ka yi wusie.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha wʋnyɩ die bie mi a wʋŋ ba balɩkʋ. Die ʋ yeye sɩba Yisa die mɩŋŋɩ dagɩ Sadusisi vuosisi pɩasɩkʋ chɩaŋ, die wɩa die ʋ keŋ mi aŋaŋ wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ dɩ, Mɩra bɩa tɩanna mɩraha mana.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Jadieke dɩ tɩanna a mana wʋnna Wʋmma, Izara vuosi! Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna yine tɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Chome Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ yɩaŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋanyile mana aŋaŋ fʋ hagɩrɩŋ mana.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Jadieke dɩ gʋtɩna bule wone, chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ chone fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ dene. Mɩra bɩ wori a tɩaŋ mɩra ale wo gie.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Daa wa dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ dɩdagɩrʋ! Dɩ yiwo wusie fʋ balɩ baarɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma yine Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ŋmɩŋ bɩ wori a gʋtɩ ʋ ma sie ʋ nyɩɩna ma wa.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: dɩ vuota yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ sʋŋ mana, aŋaŋ ʋ sʋŋanyile mana aŋaŋ ʋ hagɩrɩŋ mana, ta yaalɩma ʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba ʋ yaalala ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ dene wo. Fʋ baaŋ tuo mɩra ale wo gie dɩ tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan kʋa dɔŋɩsɩ ta jʋʋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta bɩ kaaba nyinti yɩa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene ta daa wa mɩŋŋɩ a balɩ wa, ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa, Fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ka yʋa. Na kʋaŋ chaaŋ vuoŋ die bɩ wo sikimiŋ a baaŋ pɩasɩ wa wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yisa die dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ʋ pɩasɩ ba, wʋpɩasɩkɩrɩ gie dɩ, Dɩ yiwo lalɩa Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa baa ʋ yi wo Davidi haagɩŋ?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die june Davidi ma a yi ʋ baarɩ: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ n Yɔmʋtieŋ: Kala n nuudiigiri gie chaaŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuuŋ me.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Davidi gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ wasa wa N Yɔmʋtieŋ Dɩ baa dɩ yiwo lalɩa ʋ bɩ yi Davidi haagɩŋ? Daadaŋ pam die kalla a gbɩlɩŋ tɩba a wʋmma ʋ dagɩkʋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Ʋ dagɩkʋ sʋŋ die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma vuodiekemba dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, ba nan dɩ yaala ba yeegi guuwagɩtɩ a chʋmma gilime, ta yaala ba wasɩma ba aŋaŋ jɩlɩma nyʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: ba keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me, ba yaala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka, ba keŋ lagɩ laalɩŋ jigiŋ ba yaala sikpeŋ kpɩɩmatieliŋ jigikasɩka.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Naa ba ŋaaŋ a nyɩra kpihɔgʋba ta gbata ba nyinti mana, ta jʋʋsa jʋʋsɩ wagɩtɩ sɩba ba chɩga Ŋmɩŋ. Vuodiekemba dɩ yinene dene nan faasɩ ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ pam.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yisa die kalɩ a gbigi wo jigidieke ba nagɩna ligire a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daansa ba nagɩnana ba ligire a yɩa, ligiretieliŋ pam die dɩ nagɩ ligire pam a yɩ:.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Womi kpihɔgʋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ dɩ keŋ a nyɩŋ a nagɩ tɔgɩfaasɩ ale a yɩ.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Yisa dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N bala nɩ kpihɔgʋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ wo gie nagɩ ligire pam a yi a tɩaŋ banɩŋ ba mana.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: dama banɩŋ ba mana nagɩ wa ligire a yi ta tɩalɩ ligire pam, ama wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ zɔɔlɩbʋ mana ʋ nagɩ wa ʋ yalla die mana a yɩ, ta ka tɩalɩ ligire dieke ʋ baaŋ nan dii gbaŋ.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Die dɩ wa tɩalɩ wa daraa ale aŋaŋ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga aŋaŋ daraa diekemba ba dinene paanʋ diekemba ba ŋaana ka yie dabɔtɩ bʋ ma wa piili, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die dɩ yaala sieŋ aŋ ba maansɩ a yigi Yisa a kʋʋ wa.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Die ba baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ ka mʋ tɩ yigi wo dʋʋgaka saŋŋa, dɩ daa die vuosisi nan yi watɩ aŋ waagɩŋ nan.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yisa die bie wo Betani, Simoni, vuodieke die dɩ yine gamɩŋ tigiŋ me. Yisa die dɩ kalɩ a die nyindiike ta hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ dɩ a keŋ juu a pɔgɩlɩ tulaari bʋnyɩ ligire die dɩ faasɩna a tʋa ta yaa nyʋʋgɩvɩɩnɩŋ, a yuori kɔlɩbɩka a sitibu a yi Yisa sikpeŋ me.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne mi wo bataŋ dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a bala a yɩa tamba dɩ, Bɩa yine ʋ chʋʋsa tulaaribu?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Bʋ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ a daa ligire pam aŋ tɩ tɩŋ nan nagɩ ligirehe a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ. Ta die ba zɩa hɔgʋ wa.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa wa sɔmm, bɩa yine nɩ daama wa? Wʋvɩɩnɩŋ ʋ yi a yɩ mɩŋ dene.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: nɩ ŋaŋ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ nan dɩ beri daaŋ mana, nɩ keŋ yaala saŋŋa dieke mana nɩ nan suŋŋi be ama n kaaŋ dɩ beri nɩ jigiŋ daaŋ mana.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Ʋ yiwo wudieke ʋ bɩagɩna, ʋ siti wo tulaari a yi n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma a gbarɩ saŋŋa dieke ba baaŋ nan guu mɩŋ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Nɩ tuo a dii sɩba jigidieke mana Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩna dɩ baaŋ nan tʋgɩ dʋnɩa mana ma ma, ba nan dɩ bala ʋ yine wudieke wo gie a tɩɩnsa ʋ wɩa.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Womi Judasi Asikaroti, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ dɩ nyɩŋ a ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra jigiŋ dɩ ʋ posi Yisa chɩaŋ a yɩ ba.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Die ba wʋnna ʋ kenne wudieke wɩa wa, die ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ fɩalɩ ta yɩ wa lɔlɩŋ dɩ ba nan yɩ wa ligire. Judasi die dɩ wa piili a yaala sievɩɩnɩŋ ʋ baaŋ yi die a nagɩ Yisa a yi ba nuusi me.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Dʋʋgaka bʋmbʋŋaŋ daarɩ, daa dieke daaŋ die ba kʋnana gasɩtɩaŋkʋ yiisehe, Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Sɩa fʋ yaala tɩ ga wʋnsɩ a gbara gasɩtɩaŋkʋ nyindiikehe a yɩ fʋ.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale ta balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ga a juu tɩka ma nɩ nan haarɩ daa wʋnyɩ dɩ chine jaaŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, nɩ dɩa wa.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: a ga juu tigidieke me ʋ junene a balɩ tigiritieŋ: Dɩdagɩrʋ wa baarɩ, juoŋ beri mɩŋ manɩŋ aŋaŋ n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ baa tɩ dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ nyindiikehe.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Mi ʋ nan dagɩ juokpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ benne sikpeŋ me a yallɩ jaaŋ mana a gbarɩ, mi nɩ nan wʋnsɩ wɩɩŋ mana a gbara.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga tɩka ma a ye wɩɩŋ mana die ʋ balla dene gbaŋ gbaŋ. Die ba wʋnsɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ nyindiikehe mi.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo die dɩ keŋ mi.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Die ba gana ga kalɩ a die Yisa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N bala nɩ, nɩ wʋnyɩ balla ʋ posi n chɩaŋ, vuodieke aŋaŋ mɩŋ dɩ dinene.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ chʋʋsɩ ba pɩasa wa wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ, a baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, n ka yi tama dɩ manɩŋ fʋ daga dene.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ yiwo nɩ baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo gie sʋnsʋŋ wʋnyɩ, vuodieke aŋaŋ mɩŋ dɩ lagɩŋna a luo kpalɩ gie me.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Vuota Bʋadembiŋ nan kpi sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dɩ dagɩna die wo, ama bɩa wʋbɩaŋ balla ka bemme dembiŋ dieke dɩ balla ʋ posi n chɩaka! Ba tɩŋ ka mɩɩrɩ wa gbaŋ dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa a yɩ wa.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Die ba ye ko die mɩŋ Yisa dɩ nagɩ paanʋkʋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yi ke gbieri gbieri a nagɩ a yɩ ba ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ nagɩ, n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wʋnna.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Ta ʋ bɩ nagɩ nyaaŋchibiŋ aŋaŋ daaŋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yi ke ta nagɩ a yɩ ba, ba mana dɩ nyuu ka ma.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, N Zɩŋ wʋnna, bʋ balla bʋ nyɩŋ a yɩ vuosi pam, bʋ balla bʋ dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa nʋahaalɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩ vaa n balɩ nɩ wusie, n kaaŋ bɩ a nyuu daabʋ gie sie daa dieke n baaŋ nan nyuu daahaalɩbʋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Die ba wa chɩa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ yɩlɩ ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba chʋŋ ga Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ mana nan chɩgɩ ta va mɩŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka dagɩya dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan kʋʋ yiyagɩrʋ wa ta yiisehe puo taŋ.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Ama maŋ a keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan woliŋ nɩ nɩŋŋa a ga Galili tɩŋ ma.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Piita dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Manɩŋ n kaaŋ chɩgɩ ta va fʋ, halɩ vuoŋ mana dɩ chɩgɩ gbaŋ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ Piita dɩ, Vaa n balɩ fʋ wusie, jinne yuku gie kparaaŋ nan keŋ a baarɩ ka kʋmma bule ta baarɩ zɩ mɩŋ wa bʋtaa.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Piita dɩ wa faasɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ dɩ keŋ baa tɩ kpi gbaŋ n kaaŋ baarɩ n zɩ fʋ! Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ tɩalɩkaha die dɩ balɩ dene gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Die ba tʋgɩna jigiŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Gatisimani, Yisa dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ kala gie ta n jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Ta die ʋ yi Piita aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn dɩ dɩ wa. Die ʋ sʋŋ dɩ faasɩ chʋʋsɩ sɩba ʋ baa ʋ kpi mɩŋ.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Ʋ balɩ ba, N sʋgɩchʋʋsɩkʋ wa faasɩ dala mɩŋ, a ga yaala ka kʋʋ mɩŋ, nɩ wɩarɩ gie ta yesime.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Die ʋ chʋŋ a ga nɩŋŋa bɩta a nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, Dɩ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi, gbalɩgɩsɩ gie saŋŋa tɩŋ tɩaŋ ta va mɩŋ.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Ʋ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, N chʋa, n chʋa, wɩɩŋ ka bɩagɩ fʋ, nagɩ gbalɩgɩsɩsɩ gie ta fʋ va mɩŋ, ama da n dʋŋŋʋ, vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataa wa jigiŋ ta ba gʋʋra, ʋ pɩasɩ Simoni Piita dɩ, Simoni, gʋʋra mɩŋ? Tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yesime bɩta gbaŋ.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ mana hagɩ a gbara ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ, amʋ nɩ da keŋ a jʋʋ magɩsɩŋ ma. Nɩ sʋgɩtɩ yaala nɩ yi wudieke dɩ mʋna, ama nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka wo hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Ʋ bɩ a yiŋŋi a ga jʋʋsɩ a bala wɩaha mi gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: ta bɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ a ye be ba gʋʋrɩnana; dama gbieŋ die yalla ba pam die ba zɩ ba baaŋ balɩ wa die.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a keŋ bʋtaa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ye ko gʋʋra mɩŋ ta voose? Dɩ mʋ! Nɩ daansɩma, saŋka tʋgɩya mɩŋ dɩ ba nagɩ vuota Bʋadembiŋ wa a yi vuobɩatɩ nuusi me.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Nɩ hagɩ tɩ ga. Nɩ daansɩ gie, daa dieke dɩ balla ʋ posi n chɩaka wʋnna.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Die ʋ ye ko balla wɩa ta Judasi, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo sʋŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ. Die ʋ ŋaŋ daadaŋ pam die kenne, bataŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ time bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ dengbile. Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋra die tʋnna ba.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Judasi aŋaŋ ba wɔŋ balɩ zieŋ mɩŋ dɩ, Maŋ a keŋ mʋgɩsɩ vuodieke kaamɩŋ, daa dieke nɩ yaalala wa ʋ wondene, nɩ yigi wo a mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ wa a yaa wa a ga.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Judasi die dɩ dene tʋgɩ mi ʋ ga Yisa jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ! Ta mʋgɩsɩ ʋ kaamɩŋ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Die wɩa die ba yigi wo a mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ wa.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ zene mi wo wʋnyɩ dɩ vʋarɩ jɩbɩŋ a gobi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra yɔmʋ wʋnyɩ tɩbɩŋ a taaŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ nagɩ wa manɩŋ a bɩrɩŋ wa gaarʋ a wa nagɩ time aŋaŋ dengbile a keŋ nɩ yigi mɩŋ?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Daaŋ mana die n bie wo nɩ jigiŋ a daga nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, ta nɩ ka yigi mɩŋ. Ama sie Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka keŋ a yi wusie.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ dɩna ʋ kʋaka dɩ chɩgɩ ta va wa.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bɔbɩ garɩŋ nyɩɩna a dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ, die ba yaala ba yigi wo ta yigi ʋ garɩŋ ta garɩkʋ dɩ forisi.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: ʋ va garɩkʋ ba nuusi me ta chɩgɩ yɔrɩ a ga.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Die ba yaa Yisa a ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ tigiŋ, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka mana nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die dɩ lagɩsɩ mi.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Piita die bie wo saasaa a dɩ ba kʋaŋ a ga juu Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ gbaakʋ ma. Die ʋ kalɩ mi aŋaŋ gbɩgbarɩtʋ a weeli boliŋ.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋraha mana die dɩ yaala ba yaalɩ Yisa wʋbɩaŋ amʋ ba kʋʋ wa ta die yaalɩ a waarɩ.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Vuosi pam die dɩ chibi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ wa ama ba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ die dɩ ka dɩ tamba.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Mi bataŋ die dɩ hagɩ a chibi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ gie a yɩ wa:.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: Die tɩ wʋŋya ʋ balla dɩ, N nan kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie vuota dɩ nagɩna ʋ nuusi a mɩɩ a taaŋ, ama ka daraa ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan mɩɩ kaanɩ, vuota nuuŋ dɩ kana ka mɩɩ ka.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Aŋaŋ die mana yɔrɩ ba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ die dɩ ka dɩa tamba.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ hagɩ a zie ba jigiŋ a pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, Wo wʋbalɩkɩŋ balɩŋ yaa gamma wɩaha gie vuosisi dɩ balala wa.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ tarɩ ta ka yuori ʋ nʋaŋ. Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, Fʋ yine Masia, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ wa.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Yisa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N yine wo, nɩ nan ye vuota Bʋadembiŋ dɩ kala Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ a kieŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ nɩɩŋmara.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta chɩɩrɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke ta baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ ka bɩ yaala daansɩatieliŋ bɩbra.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Nɩ wʋŋ ʋ zana Ŋmɩŋ, lalɩa nɩ balɩ? Die ba mana dɩ saagɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, dɩ die wɩa ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Bataŋ die dɩ piili a tɩɩra nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yie Yisa. Die ba mʋʋ ʋ nine ta nɩgɩ wa, ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ ʋ bʋgɩ a ye vuodieke dɩ nɩgɩna wa. Vuodiekemba die dɩ gbarɩna dɩ nagɩ wa ta fala wa.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Piita die ye bie wo gbaakʋ ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ tʋntʋntɩba hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ mi.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Die ʋ yene Piita dɩ weelinene bolibu ʋ mɩŋŋɩ daansɩ wa ta baarɩ dɩ, Fʋ gbaŋ die gʋtɩ wa Yisa, Nazeriti vuoke me.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Ama Piita dɩ baarɩ, Aayɩ, N ka sɩba fʋ balala wudieke wo. N ka sɩba ka chɩaŋ. Ta chʋŋ a ga sanʋarɩ tʋŋ.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Die hɔgʋ wa gie die dɩ yene womi, ʋ piili a bala a yɩa vuodiekemba die dɩ zene mi dɩ, Daa wa gie gbaŋ yiwo Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ama Piita dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ bɩbra. Die dɩ yine sʋaa vuodiekemba die dɩ zene mi dɩ balɩ Piita bɩbra, Kaaŋ bɩagɩ a baarɩ ka yi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ, dama fʋ gbaŋ nyɩŋ wa Galili.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Piita die dɩ hʋʋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ datɩ n tɩbɩŋ maŋ keŋ ka bala wusie! N ka sɩba daa dieke nɩ balala wa.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Womi kparaaka die dɩ kʋŋ ka bule ma ma. Piita die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ Yisa die dɩ balla wudiekemba a yɩ wa wa: Kparaaŋ nan keŋ a baarɩ ka kʋmma bule wo ta baarɩ zɩ manɩŋ bʋtaa. Die ʋ piili kpaŋ.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Die davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩŋ die dɩ tɩanna, Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Meri vuodieke dɩ yine Jemisi nuŋ aŋaŋ Salome die dɩ daa tulaari dɩ ba yaa a ga a duuri Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Alasiri daraaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ, die ba ga ʋ vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Die ba chʋnnana sieku me wo ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Mɩnɩa balla ʋ biliŋ tanɩ vɔrɩkʋ nʋarɩ ma a yɩ tɩ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Die ba daansɩ a ye ta tanɩ wɔŋ a biliŋ mɩŋ.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Saŋka mi die naalɩba jakʋʋŋ Ɔgusitusi die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba bɩɩsɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne Romani tɩŋgbaŋ mana mana ma ma.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Die wɩa die ba juu vɔrɩkʋ ma a ye dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ kalla nuudiigiri chaaŋ a yeegi nyɩŋyɩala, ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Die bɩɩsɩkʋ die dɩ bunne a yi wo, die Kirinusi die yine naaŋ Siria tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Daa wa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ. Nɩ yaala Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti wa, die ba kpaasɩna dagarɩkʋ ma wa, ama ʋ wo giena ʋ hagɩ kuŋ me mɩŋ! Nɩ daansɩ gie, giena die ba nagɩ wa a dʋaŋ wa.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Die wɩa vuoŋ mana die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ tɩŋ dɩ ba bɩɩsɩ wa.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Nɩ gamma a balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ Piita: ʋ baa ʋ dɩa nɩ nɩŋŋa a gamma Galili me; nɩ nan ye wo mi sɩba die ʋ balla nɩ die wo.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma Josefu die nyɩŋ wa Nazeriti Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma a ga Betilehemi, Judia ma ma jigidieke die ba mɩɩrɩna naaŋ Davidi. Josefu die ga wa mi dama die ʋ yiwo naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Die wɩa die dɩ yi ŋmaamɩŋ aŋaŋ mamachi die dɩ juu be, ba chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ ma. Die ba ka balɩ vuoŋ wɩɩŋ, dama ŋmaamɩŋ die yalla ba. .

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Die ʋ ga wa mi aŋaŋ Meri vuodieke die ʋ balla ʋ faarɩ wa, dɩ ba bɩɩsɩ ba, saŋka mi Meri die yaa wa nyuuti.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Die Yisa dɩ hagɩna kumbu me wo dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi sʋkʋʋŋ die ʋ woliŋ nyɩŋ wa Meri Magidalini jigiŋ vuodieke ma die ʋ yagɩna jɩmbɩatɩ ayʋpɔyɩ wa.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: die ba yene bie Betilehemi tɩŋ ma wa, ʋ maarɩŋ saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩ.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Meri Magidalini die dɩ ga a balɩ Yisa zʋalɩŋ die dɩ faasɩna kʋmma aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩkʋ dɩ Yisa hagɩ kuŋ me mɩŋ.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Die ʋ mɩɩrɩ ʋ hɔgʋlɩa bʋa dembiŋ, die ʋ nagɩ garɩŋ a vili wo a nagɩ wa dʋaŋ dɔŋɩsɩ nyindiike kpalɩ ma dama chaamba juonehe mana die suuliye mɩŋ.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Die ba wʋnna Meri Magidalini dɩ balla dɩ Yisa hagɩya mɩŋ wa, ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ ye wo wo, die ba ka yi wo yada.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Die yiyagɩrɩŋ bataŋ die benne tɩka mi chaakʋ a daansa ba yiise hagɩrɩŋ ma yuku mi.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ a chʋŋ sieŋ me. Die ʋ tarɩgɩ ʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Ta die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ malaka wʋnyɩ die dɩ a keŋ a sʋʋŋ ba jigiŋ, ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ chaaŋ a giliŋ be. Die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ faasɩ a yigi be.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a garɩsɩ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ a ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma; daadaŋ pam die dɩ bɩ a keŋ giliŋ wo, ʋ dagɩ ba sɩba ʋ ŋaana wɔŋ daga die wo.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
