local: Die ba yiŋŋi a ga balɩ banɩŋ ba, ama die ba ka yi be yada.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: ama ta malakaka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ! N yallɩ wa wʋvɩɩnɩŋ a keŋ dɩ n yɩ nɩ, wudieke dɩ baaŋ nan yi vuoŋ mana sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Farasisi die dɩ keŋ mi a yaala ba magɩsɩ wa, a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, dagɩ tɩ, dɩ yaa sieŋ mɩŋ tɩ mɩra sʋŋ dembiŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ mɩŋ?.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa jigiŋ ta ba die. Die ʋ galɩŋ ba dɩ ba yine sikpeŋ hagɩrɩtieliŋ ba kana ka tuo dii vuodiekemba die dɩ balla ba dɩ ba ye wo ʋ hagɩna kumbu me wo.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Yuku gie ba mɩɩrɩ wa nɩ Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa, naaŋ Davidi tɩŋ ma.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Yisa gbaŋ dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Lalɩa Mosisi die dɩ balɩ nɩ.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Ʋ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gamma dʋnɩa mana mana a balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ vuoŋ mana.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Naa balla ka dagɩ nɩ dɩ ka seŋ yiwo wusie: nɩ nan ye ba nagɩna garɩŋ a vili bʋanyaalɩŋ a dʋaŋ dɔŋɩsɩ nyindiike kpalɩ ma.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Die ba baarɩ dɩ, Mosisi die baarɩ dembiŋ dɩ keŋ yaala ʋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ʋ ŋaaŋ maagɩ wa gbanɩŋ a yɩ wa a dagɩ sɩba ʋ zeti wo mɩŋ, ta vaa wa aŋ ʋ ga.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a tuo mɩŋ a dii ta nɩ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, ama vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a ka tuo mɩŋ a dii Ŋmɩŋ nan dii dɩ tieŋ sarɩya a baarɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Womi die malakasi pam die dɩ nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma a keŋ a gʋtɩ wʋnɩŋ wa ma ma, ba chɩa yɩla a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ dɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ wɩa die tʋa mɩŋ wɩa Mosisi die dɩ yɩ nɩ nʋarɩ mi.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Wɩaha gie nan dɩ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba tuo mɩŋ a dii wo: ba nan dɩ yaga jɩmbɩatɩ n saaŋ ma: ba nan dɩ bala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Nɩ kɔtɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma saaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, ta sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme ʋ vuovʋarɩka ma tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Ama dʋnɩa piiliŋ, Ŋmɩŋ die naaŋ wa dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Ba nan dɩ naga nyinvuuke aŋaŋ ba nuusi, ta nyuu batɩgɩsɩ ta wɩɩŋ kaaŋ yi be. Ba nan nagɩ ba nuusi a diisi yʋagɩtieliŋ me aŋ ba ye alaafɩa.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Die malakasisi die dɩ vaa yiyagɩrɩba ta yiŋŋi a ga arɩzanna ma, yiyagɩrɩba die dɩ balɩ a yɩ tamba dɩ, Nɩ vaa tɩ ga Betilehemi ma ma a ye wudieke dɩ yine mi, ta Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ tɩ wa.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ nan vaa ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta faarɩ taŋ aŋ ba bale wo gie nan lagɩŋ.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Yisa die dɩ balla wɩaha a kpatɩ wa, die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ wa a jʋalɩ Arɩzanna ma, Yisa dɩ ga kalɩ ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: yi vuobalɩmɩŋ, da vuosi bale bɩbra.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Die wɩa die ba yi lagɩ lagɩ a ga a ye Meri aŋaŋ Josefu; ta bʋanyaalɩka die dɩ dʋa dɔŋɩsɩ nyindiike kpalɩ ma.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga a dagɩ jigiŋ mana, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ suŋŋi be a yɩ ba hagɩrɩŋ aŋ ba yi mamachi tʋʋma a dagɩ sɩba ba wɩaha die yiwo wusie.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ nagɩ jadieke a lagɩŋ, vuota ka mʋ ʋ puoke.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Die ba yene wo wo, die ba balɩ ba wudieke malakaka die dɩ balla yaa gamma bʋa wa wɩa.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Die ba yiŋŋine kuli tigiŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ pɩasɩ wa wɩɩrɩ wɩa.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Vuodieke mana die dɩ wʋnna yiyagɩrɩba die dɩ balla die wo, die dɩ faasɩ yi be mamachi.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Ʋ balɩ ba dɩ Dembiŋ dieke dɩ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ta bɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ gaaŋ juu wo hɔgʋ dieke ʋ zetine wo taalɩ ma.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: ama Meri die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ wɩaha gie mana ta faasɩ a yile ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Hɔgʋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ zeti ʋ chʋrʋ ta bɩ yallɩ dembiŋ gaaŋ ʋ gbaŋ; juu ʋ chʋrʋkʋrɩ taalɩ ma.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die yiyagɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga ba yiise jigiŋ. Die ba ganana die ba chɩa yɩla a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ wudieke ba wʋnna ta ye; die dɩ seŋ yiwo wudieke malakaka die dɩ bala a yɩ ba wa.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ yaa ballɩ a keŋ dɩ Yisa gbɩ ba, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yaga ba.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Ba mɩɩrɩna bʋa wa ʋ daraa anɩɩ daraaŋ, die ba gobi ʋ kɔlɩ ta yɩ wa saaŋ Yisa, saaŋ dieke malakaka die dɩ yɩna ta ba ye yigi ʋ nyuutiti.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Die Yisa dɩ mɩŋŋɩna, ʋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa ballɩbɩsɩsɩ kiere n jigiŋ, daa nɩ yagɩma ba dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ yiwo ba die wo sɩɩtɩ.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Die saŋka dɩ tʋgɩ dɩ Josefu aŋaŋ Meri yi Mosisi mɩraha die dɩ dagɩna die wo, dɩ ba yi kaaba a wʋnsɩ ba gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Die wɩa die ba nagɩ bʋa wa a ga Jerusalemi dɩ ba nagɩ wa a yɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ ka tuo Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩba bʋabiŋ die dɩ tuone nyinti die nɩŋ ʋ kaaŋ juu mi.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: sɩba ba maagɩna die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ma dɩ, Hɔgʋlɩa ŋaŋ Bʋadembiŋ nɩŋ mana yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Die ʋ wa nagɩ ba ʋ nuusi me ta nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi diisi ba ma ta yi alibarika a yɩ ba.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Ta ba kaabɩ sɩba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna die dɩ, Ba nagɩ ŋmarɩbɩsɩ ale yi kaabɩŋ.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: N zʋa Tiwofilusi: Vuosi pam kpaŋŋɩna ba gbaŋ a maagɩ wudiekemba die dɩ yine tɩ sʋnsʋŋ.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ dene hagɩ dɩ ʋ dɩa, daa wʋnyɩ dɩ chɩgɩ a keŋ gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ vɩɩnɩŋ, lalɩa maŋ nan yi a ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Die ba maagɩ wa wudiekemba vuodiekemba die dɩ benne piiliku me wo dɩ yene ta balɩ tɩ wa.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Saŋka mi daa wʋnyɩ die benne Jerusalemi ma ma ʋ saaŋ die dɩ yi Simiani. Die ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ a chɩga Ŋmɩŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ bie ʋ ma, die ʋ chɩɩsa saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balla ʋ gbatɩ Izara vuosi a taaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ wasa mɩŋ vuovɩɩnɩŋ? Vuovɩɩnɩŋ wori ntaala Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma die n gbaŋ gbaŋ die n mɩŋŋɩna a pɩasɩ a sɩba wɩaha gie mana chɩasɩsɩ a piiliku me wo aŋaŋ a ganana die wo, ta yile dɩ dɩ nan dɩ vɩɩna n mɩŋŋɩna maagɩ a dɩɩsɩna die a yɩ fʋ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ bala wa dɩ ʋ kaaŋ kpi sie ʋ keŋ ye Ŋmɩŋ vuovaʋrɩkɩrɩ Masia wa.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Fʋnɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha mɩŋ. Da keŋ kʋʋ vuoŋ, da keŋ yaalɩ vuoŋ hɔgʋ, da keŋ gaarɩ, da keŋ yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ daansɩatieŋ, da keŋ nyɩrɩ vuoŋ, yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma. .

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: N yiwo naa amʋ fʋ sɩmma wudiekemba mana ba dagɩna fʋ wa chɩaŋ.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vaa Simiani die dɩ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ. Die Yisa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ die dɩ yalla wa a keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋkʋ dɩ ba yi a yɩ wa wudieke Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die dɩ dagɩna wa.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Daa wa dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ n yine bʋabiŋ mana a yaa ga mʋʋna n dɩɩ sieti gie mana mɩŋ.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Herodi die dɩ yine Judia naakpeŋkpɩɩka, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ wʋnyɩ die benne ba wasa wa Zakaria, die ʋ nyɩŋ wa Abija, Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ buuriŋ ma; die ʋ hɔgʋ Elizabeti dɩaŋ die yiwo Arɔni haagɩŋ.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Simiani die dɩ tuo bʋa wa ʋ nuusi me ta die a pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yisa dɩ daansɩ wa aŋaŋ choti, ta balɩ wa, wʋbalɩmɩŋ tɩala fʋ, Kulime a daa jadieke mana fʋ yalla a nagɩ ligirehe a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ, ta nan yi nyintitieŋ arɩzanna ma, ta fʋ keŋ a dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Ba bale wo mana beriŋ die vɩɩna mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta ba mɩŋŋɩ a dɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ ʋ sieti mana.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ka daaŋ fʋ nʋarɩ vaa manɩŋ fʋ tʋntʋntʋ kpi ta wa kuli aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ, sɩba die fʋ bala mɩŋ die wo;.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Daa wa die dɩ wʋnna naa ʋ sʋŋ dɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ, ʋ nine chaatɩ dɩ tarɩgɩ dama die ʋ faasɩ yiwo ligiretieŋ, die ʋ kuli tigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Ama die ba wo ballɩ dama Elizabeti die yiwo hɔgʋpɩrɩŋ, ta ʋ ŋaŋ Zakaria mana die dɩ faasɩ kʋrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba baaŋ mɩɩrɩ.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: dama n gbaŋ gbaŋ n nine ye Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ wa, vuodieke fʋ tʋnna wa.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a daansɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ tʋa pam aŋaŋ ligiretieŋ dɩ baaŋ nan juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Zakaria aŋaŋ ʋ chaakʋ tʋʋma saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, ta die ʋ ga tʋmma a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, sɩba ʋ ŋaana yie die wo.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Die dɩ yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mamachi ʋ balɩkʋ ma, ta Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ bɩbra dɩ, N ballɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa tʋa mɩŋ.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: vuodieke fʋ vʋarɩna a zieŋ a yɩ buuriŋ mana wa.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Die ba taa wa daraa a vʋarɩ wa sɩba banɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba dagɩŋ dɩ sɩna die, dɩ ba vʋarɩ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a jʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ nyɩnnyʋʋgaha. Die ba taa daraaha ta die Zakaria die dɩ dii. Die wɩa die ʋ ga juu juoku.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Nyɔgɩnɩ dɩ baaŋ yi die a garɩ nyɩŋ garɩyɩɩŋ vɔrɩŋ ma wa ka tʋa a tʋgɩ ligiretieŋ dɩ baaŋ nan yi die a juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Ʋ nan yi chaanɩŋ ta dagɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi fʋ sieŋ, ta nan bɩ yi vuosi yɩma fʋ vuosisi Izara tieliŋ jɩlɩma.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: ta daadamba die dɩ zie yenni me a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ jʋnana nyɩnnyʋʋgaha.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ wʋnna naa, dɩ bɩ faasɩ yi be mamachi, ba wa pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Die nɩŋ mɩnɩa bala ʋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Simiani balɩkʋ yaa gamma bʋa wa wɩa, die dɩ yi bʋa wa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ mamachi.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ keŋ a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ a zie Ŋmɩŋ koriku nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ jigidieke ʋ jʋna nyɩnnyʋʋgaha.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Yisa dɩ daansɩ ba ta baarɩ dɩ, Vuotaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi die ama Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi dama Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ wɩɩŋ wori a tʋa.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Simiani die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika a yɩ ba ta balɩ yɩ bʋa wa nuŋ Meri dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩna bʋa wa gie dɩ ʋ keŋ aŋ Izara vuosi pam nan yɔrɩ; ta ba pam dɩaŋ keŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ baa ʋ yiwo dagɩtɩ a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ aŋ vuosi pam nan balɩ a chʋʋsɩ ka.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Die Zakaria dɩ yene wo wo die ʋ sɩkpaaŋ dɩ vʋʋ ta ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yigi wo.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Piita dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Ta tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, tɩ nagɩna jaaŋ mana a taaŋ ta dɩ fʋ wa.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: amʋ vuosi pam sʋŋanyile nan nyɩŋ yeŋ me ama wɩɩŋ nan yi ta chʋʋsɩ fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ sʋŋ pam.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Ama ta malakaka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Zakaria, da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ! Ŋmɩŋ wʋŋ fʋ jʋʋsɩkʋ mɩŋ, ta fʋ hɔgʋ Elizabeti nan mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ a yɩ fʋ, ta nɩ nan wa wa Jɔɔn.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Vaa n balɩ nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ vana ʋ tigiŋ, yaa ʋ nɩmballɩ yaa ʋ nuŋ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ ballɩ yaa ʋ kʋatɩ n wɩa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die benne mi ta yi hɔgʋ, ʋ saaŋ die yine Anna vuodieke dɩ yine Fanueli bʋa ta bɩ yi Aseri buuriŋ vuoŋ, die ʋ yiwo hɔgʋjakʋʋŋ ta die a yallɩ chʋrʋ bɩna ayʋpɔyɩ nyɩɩna.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Fʋ sʋŋ nan faasɩ fɩalɩ fʋ bʋa wa mɩɩrɩŋ wɩa ta vuosi pam dɩaŋ sʋgɩtɩ nan fɩalɩ ba mɩɩrɩna wa wɩa.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Die ba gana a ga tʋga Jerusalemi ba keŋ Betifiji aŋaŋ Betani tɩgɩsɩ, tɩgɩsɩ gie die bie wo kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Olivi Tɩɩsɩsɩ lʋgɩŋ, Yisa dɩ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale ta.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: nan daansɩ ye nyinti saŋka gie a tɩaŋ ʋ nagɩna nyindiekemba a taaŋ wa. Ʋ nan ye tige, aŋaŋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ nɩɩlɩŋ aŋaŋ ballɩ aŋaŋ kʋatɩ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ta tuo mugisiŋ a gʋtɩ ta saŋŋa dieke dɩ kienene wo ʋ nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: ta ʋ chʋrʋ wa die dɩ kpi ta va wa kpihɔgʋ ta ʋ beri bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩ aŋaŋ anɩɩsa saŋka mi: ama die saŋŋa mana ʋ ŋaaŋ bie wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ a bɔba nʋaŋ ta jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: dama ʋ baa ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa. Ʋ ka baa ʋ nyu daaŋ yaa daahagɩrɩŋ. Ʋ nan suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ a nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ nuŋ dɩ yalla ʋ nyuuti.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gamma tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ dieke dɩ benne nɩ nɩŋka, nɩ die keŋ a juu tɩka ma nɩ nan ye bonpɔlɩ dɩ bɔbɩna zie vuoŋ dɩ ye ka jʋalɩ ka, nɩ forisike a yaa keŋ giena.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Ama vuodiekemba dɩ benne nɩŋŋa lele nan wɩarɩ kʋaŋ ta vuodiekemba dɩ benne kʋaŋ lele nan tɩaŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die ʋ keŋ haarɩ ba ta bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta balɩ bʋa wa wɩa a yɩ vuodiekemba mana die dɩ chɩɩsɩnana saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ gbatɩ Jerusalemi vuosi a taaŋ wa.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Ʋ nan yi Izara vuosi pam yiŋŋi keŋ ba Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ keŋ pɩasɩ nɩ, dɩ bɩa nɩ yi dene, nɩ balɩ wa dɩ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yaalala ka, ama ʋ nan yiŋŋi a yaa ka a keŋ lele.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Die ba wa bie wo sieŋ me a gara Jerusalemi, Yisa die dɩna nɩŋŋa, die dɩ yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mamachi ta ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yallɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ dɩna ba kʋaka. Yisa die dɩ bɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a ga lʋgɩŋ a balɩ ba wudiekemba die dɩ bala a yi wo.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Die Josefu aŋaŋ Meri die dɩ yine wudiekemba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna wa a kpatɩ wa die ba yiŋŋi kuli ba tɩŋ Nazeriti, Galili me.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Ʋ nan dɩ yallɩ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Elaja die dɩ yalla hagɩrɩbʋ die wo. Ʋ nan woliŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, ʋ nan yi vuosi yaalɩma ba ballɩ bɩbra, ta yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ba yaala ba tuo ʋ nʋaŋ, ʋ nan bɩ yi aŋ vuosi yi siri a gbara ba Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Die ba ga a ye bonpɔlɩka dɩ ka zie sieku me a bɔbɩ juoŋ kaanɩ sanʋaŋ ma. Die ba forisinene ke.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ daansɩma, tɩ gara Jerusalemi, jigidieke ba baaŋ nagɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ a ga yɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩŋ nuusi me aŋ ba dii mɩŋ sarɩya ta baarɩ dɩ n chʋʋsɩya dɩ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ, ta nagɩ mɩŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Ta bʋa wa die dɩ bɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ta yaa yɩaŋ pam, ta Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ die dɩ fɩalɩ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Zakaria die dɩ balɩ a yɩ malakaka dɩ, Lalɩa maŋ baaŋ yi a sɩmma a baarɩ naa bala ka yi? Dama n yiwo daajakʋʋŋ, ta n hɔgʋ dɩaŋ dɩ faasɩ a kʋrɩŋ.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: vuosi bataŋ die zie mi, a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yie, nɩ forise bonpɔlɩka mɩŋ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: dɩ ba vʋarɩ mɩŋ fala, a tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yɩ mɩŋ, a nɩgɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kumbu me.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Bɩŋ nɩŋ mana Yisa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ ŋaaŋ ga wa Jerusalemi a ga dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Malakaka die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ, n yiwo Gabiriali, vuodieke dɩ kalla Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa; ta ʋ tʋŋ mɩŋ dɩ n keŋ balɩ wʋvɩɩnaha gie a yɩ fʋ.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ba balɩ ba Yisa die dɩ bala die wo gbaŋ gbaŋ, die wɩa vuosisi die dɩ vaa ba yaa a ga.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Womi Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn, Zebedi ballɩ dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, wɩɩŋ benne tɩ yaala fʋ yi a yɩ tɩ.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Yisa die dɩ tʋgɩna bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale die ba ga dɩ ba dii dʋʋgaka sɩba ba ŋaana a wɔŋ gara die wo.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Ama ka tuo n wɩaha dii wudieke dɩ bala ka yi saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna. Fʋ kana ka tuo mɩŋ dii wo wɩa, kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩa a ga tʋgɩ daa dieke wɩaha dɩ balla a keŋ yi wusie.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Die ba yaa bonpɔlɩka a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a nagɩ ba garɩtɩ a diisi ka sikpeŋ Yisa die dɩ wa jʋalɩ kalɩ ka sikpeŋ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yaala n yi a yɩ nɩ.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Dʋʋgaka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ die dɩ yiŋŋine a kule die ʋ wɩarɩ Jerusalemi ma ama die ba ka sɩba;.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Ama daadamba die dɩ zie a chɩɩsa Zakaria ta yile dɩ bɩa ʋ yʋasɩ die Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Vuosi pam die dɩ nagɩ ba gaatɩ a jaatɩ sieku me bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nagɩ vaatɩ ba chɩana nyɩŋ haagɩŋ ma a jaata sieku me.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ba baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ yaala saŋŋa dieke fʋ keŋ baa fʋ dii fʋ naarɩ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ tɩ ŋaŋ fʋ gʋtɩ kala, wʋnyɩ kala fʋ nuugalɩ wʋnyɩ fʋ nuudiigiŋ.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Die ba yile dɩ ʋ gʋtɩ wa daadamba ma, die wɩa die ba chʋŋ damʋʋmɩŋ ta wa piili a yaala wa ba vuosi aŋaŋ ba zʋalɩŋ jige;.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Die ʋ kenne a nyɩŋ wa, die ʋ ka bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩa a yɩ ba. Die ba mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ʋ ye wo wɩɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ. Die ʋ kana ka bɩagɩ bala wɩaha, die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a dagɩ ba wɩa.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne ʋ nɩŋŋa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋ, die dɩ wa a piili keesiŋ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, hozaana gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ lele. Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kienene wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba, Nɩ zɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩnana jadieke wo chɩaŋ, nɩ nan bɩagɩ a chii chii diekemba n balla n chii wo? Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a gbalɩgɩ gbalɩgɩ diekemba n baaŋ nan gbalɩgɩ wa.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: ama die ba ka ye wo, die wɩa die ba yiŋŋi ga Jerusalemi a yaala wa mi.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Die ʋ tʋʋma saŋŋa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma dɩ tɩanna wa, die ʋ yiŋŋi kuli.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ŋmɩŋ bɩ yi alibarika a yɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Davidi naarɩ dieke dɩ kienene wo ma. Hozaana! Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, gbatɩ tɩ taaŋ leleke gie.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ba baarɩ Wa, tɩ nan bɩagɩ. Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie nɩ nan chii n chiititi, ta bɩ gbalɩgɩ gbalɩgɩ diekemba n balla n gbalɩgɩ wa.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Ka daraa ataa daraaŋ die ba ye wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ, ʋ kalɩ aŋaŋ Juu vuosi dɩdagɩrɩba a wʋmma wudieke ba balala ta pɩasa ba wʋpɩasɩka.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ Elizabeti die dɩ yigi nyuuti, ta die lɔbɩrɩ ʋ gbaŋ tigiri me a ga tʋgɩ chɩɩtɩ anʋ.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yisa die dɩ ga tʋgɩ Jerusalemi, a ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku sʋŋ, a daansɩ jaaŋ mana ka sʋŋ. Ama saŋŋa dieke ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaala ka nan die ʋ ŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo die dɩ nyɩŋ ga Betani.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: ama n wo sieŋ dɩ n dagɩ sɩba geŋ wo baaŋ nan kalɩ n nuudiigiŋ, ta geŋ wo kala n nuugalɩ. Ŋmɩŋ bala ʋ nagɩ jigehe gie a yɩ vuodiekemba wɩa ʋ wʋnsɩna wa.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Die dɩ yiwo vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wʋnnana wa mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ yɩaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ yiŋŋi balɩkʋ.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: ta die ʋ keŋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna naa a yɩ mɩŋ, ta chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ a vʋarɩ mɩŋ viivi me.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋnna ba nyɩŋ Betani a kieŋ, kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ Yisa.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba banɩŋ, baŋ wa die dɩ wʋnna naa die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Die ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ die dɩ ye wo mi, die dɩ yi be mamachi pam; ʋ nuŋ die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, N bʋa, bɩa fʋ yi tɩ naa? Manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ chʋa sʋgɩtɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ tɩ dɩ dɩa a yaala fʋ.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Elizabeti nyuuti chɩɩtɩ ayʋaba ma Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ malaka Gabiriali ʋ ga tɩŋ kaanɩ dɩ benne Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma ba wasɩnana Nazeriti.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: die ʋ ye kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩŋ dɩ gana ga zie ŋaŋ vaatɩ, die ʋ ga ʋ daansɩ ye sɩba ka yaa nyɩŋnyɩŋka mɩŋ. Die ʋ tʋgɩna mi die ʋ ye wo vaatɩ nyɩɩna ka sikpeŋ, dama ka saŋŋa die ye ka tʋgɩya aŋaŋ ka baaŋ nan nyɩŋ.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Die wɩa die Yisa dɩ wa ba mana a lagɩsɩ taŋ ta balɩ ba nɩ sɩba baarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ daansɩnana vuosi tɩŋgbaŋka gie ŋaaŋ dagɩ wa ba gbaŋ ta nyɩŋkʋra dɩaŋ ŋaaŋ pɔgɩlɩ wa ba vuosi aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Bɩa yine nɩ tɩŋ dɩ dɩa a yaala mɩŋ? Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ mʋ dɩ n bemme n Chʋa tigiŋ me.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: dɩ ʋ yaa tʋntʋmɩŋ a ga yɩ havʋʋbiŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ zɩna dembiŋ ta die baa ʋ yallɩ daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Josefu, vuodieke die dɩ yine naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ. Havʋʋbike saaŋ die yine Meri.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ tɩɩka dɩ, Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩ ye nyɩŋnyɩŋka fʋ ma a dii bɩbra. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ wʋŋ ʋ bala wa.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, nɩ sʋnsʋŋ vuodieke dɩ yaalala nyɩŋkʋrɩtɩ, sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ yɔmʋ banɩŋ ba mana sʋnsʋŋ.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Ama die ba ka sɩba ʋ balɩkʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Die malakaka dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bie fʋ ma, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ bie fʋ jigiŋ ta yi alibarika a yi fʋ pam.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Die ba gana ga tʋgɩ Jerusalemi, Yisa dɩ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, a piili a yaga vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla nyinti a daa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ daanana nyintiti ta kpaŋŋɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla ligire a tarɩga aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla ŋmarɩsɩ a daa teebulisi a bubi bubi.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ bɩ yaala ʋ bemme nɩŋŋa sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ vuoŋ mana yɔmʋ.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga Nazeriti aŋaŋ ba a saaga yɩa ba. Ʋ nuŋ die dɩ tɩɩnsa wɩaha gie mana ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Meri sɩkpaaŋ die dɩ faasɩ vʋʋ yaa gamma malakaka wʋbalɩkaha ma, die ʋ yile wɩaha chɩasɩ dɩ sɩna die.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Ta ka saagɩya dɩ vuoŋ chii jaaŋ a daagɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gbaakʋ sʋŋ.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ gbaŋ ka keŋye dɩ vuosi yi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ, ama wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ yi vuoŋ mana yɔmʋ, a kpi a gbatɩ vuosi pam a taaŋ.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩrɩma a gʋta aŋaŋ yɩaŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ vuota sʋgɩtɩ dɩ fɩala ʋ ma.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Malakaka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Meri, daa vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ dama Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ fɩalɩ fʋ ma.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Naalɩba jakʋʋŋ Taberisi die dɩ dine naarɩ bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ anʋ wa, ta Pontiwosi Paliti gbaŋ die yiwo Judia nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ, ta Herodi gbaŋ die dɩ yi Galili naajakʋʋŋ, ta ʋ nɩmbʋa Filipi die yine Ituria aŋaŋ Tirakonitisi naajakʋʋŋ, ta Lasaniasi die dɩ yi Abilene naajakʋʋŋ.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Die ʋ wa dagɩ vuosisi ta baarɩ, Ŋmɩŋ wɩa baarɩ, Ŋmɩŋ tigiŋ baa ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ a yɩ vuoŋ mana, ta nɩ nagɩ ka a bɩrɩŋ gaarɩŋ tigiŋ.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Die ba kenne a keŋ tʋgɩ tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Jariko, Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ wa nyɩna tɩka ma daadaŋ pam die dɩdɩ ba kʋaŋ. Daa yɩɩ wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Batimiisi, Timiisi bʋa dɩ kalɩ sieku me a jʋʋsa.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Nan yigi nyuuti a mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ aŋ nɩ wa wa Yisa.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: ta Anasi aŋaŋ Kayafasi die yine Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra. Saŋka gie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ nagɩ ʋ wɩa a yɩ Jɔɔn, Zakaria bʋa wa; die ʋ bie wo haagɩŋ ma.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die ba piili a yaala sieti dɩ ba kʋʋ Yisa. Die ba chɩga wa mɩŋ, dama die ʋ dagɩkʋ die yi daadamba mana mamachi mɩŋ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Yisa vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti die benne mi wo, ʋ piili a keesi Yisa, Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Ʋ baa ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ ta ba wasɩma wa Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa. Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan yiwo naakpeŋkpɩɩŋ sɩba ʋ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Davidi die dɩ sɩna die wo.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Die wɩa Jɔɔn die dɩ dɩ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋga mana a mʋʋla a yɩa vuosi dɩ ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma aŋ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ aŋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Die jɩŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋgɩna Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩŋ mi.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Die daadamba pam dɩ natɩ wa ta baarɩ dɩ ʋ tarɩ watɩ, yɩɩ wa dɩ wa faasɩ a keesi Yisa, Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: ʋ baa ʋ yiwo Jakobu haagɩsɩ sʋŋ naakpeŋkpɩɩŋ haahuu, ta ʋ naarɩ wo kpatɩŋ.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Sɩba dɩ maagɩna die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Vuoŋ benne haagɩŋ ma a keese a mʋʋla: Nɩ wʋnsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ sieti ta vaa a tʋgɩsɩ a yɩ wa.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Tuŋ die dɩ vʋnna sʋkʋleeliŋ, ba chʋŋ sieku me die ba ye tɩɩ dieke Yisa die dɩ kana a yɩ wa, die ka mana dɩ kpi.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Yisa dɩ zie ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ wa wa. Die wɩa die ba wa yɩɩ wa a balɩ wa dɩ, Hagɩ zie, ʋ wasa fʋ.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Meri dɩ pɩasɩ malakaka dɩ, N zɩ dembiŋ, ta naa baa ka lɩa a yi.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Gbaga mana nan suuli, ta tana aŋaŋ kunkoge mana jatɩ. Siegɔnte mana nan tʋgɩsɩ ta siebalɔtɩ mana nan mɩŋŋɩ vɩɩna.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Piita die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke die dɩ yine wo ta balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, daansɩ, tɩɩka die fʋ kana a yi wa kpiye.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnnana Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, ye mamachi tige die ba mɩna aŋaŋ mamachi tana.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Ʋ nagɩ ʋ nyinyeeke a vigi taaŋ, ta chɩgɩ ga Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Die malakaka die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka nan keŋ a sʋʋŋ fʋ ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩbʋ nan ligi fʋ. Naa chɩaŋ ma ba nan wa bʋakasɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ bʋa.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana nan ye Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baa nan gbatɩ ba a taaŋ dene. .

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada. Nɩ keŋ a yi Ŋmɩŋ yada.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Ye mamachi nyinti gie, taŋ balɩmɩŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ tɩalɩ a bie ka jigiberisikiŋ ba nan kpaŋŋɩ a mana taaŋ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ yaala n yi a yɩ fʋ? Yɩɩ wa dɩ baarɩ Dɩdagɩrʋ, n yaala n bɩ yiŋŋi yesime mɩŋ.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Ama sɩmma a baarɩ fʋ sʋgɩtaa Elizabeti vuodieke dɩ kʋrɩnna wa, lele ʋ yigi wo nyuuti, chɩɩta ayʋaba wʋnna a baa ʋ mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ, wʋnɩŋ vuodieke die ba wasɩnana wa hɔgʋpɩrɩŋ.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Daadaŋ pam die dɩ keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ dɩ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuukehe gie! Mɩnɩa kpaana nɩ dɩ nɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩ dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ nɩ ma wa?.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: nan bɩagɩ a balɩ a yɩ kunkogiri gie, vʋʋ a ga nan nyaakpɩɩbʋ ma, fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ keŋ a balɩ die ta ka chɩɩlɩya fʋ sʋŋ ma, yi yada sɩba fʋ bala die wo nan yi, aŋ ka yi a yɩ fʋ.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Die Yisa dɩ ga a kalɩ Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiŋ me ta Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ die bie wo ka nɩŋŋa. Die Piita aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Andurusi die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ benne ʋ nyɩɩna ma wa.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Gamma, fʋ yada gbaanna fʋ. Lele womi ta ʋ ninehe dɩ yuori ʋ wa dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ sieku me.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: dama wɩɩŋ wori Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Nɩ yime wudiekemba dɩ baaŋ dagɩ dɩ nɩ seŋ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma. Ta nɩ da yilime nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ Abarahami yiwo tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ. N bala nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a vaa tanaha gie bɩrɩŋ Abarahami haagɩsɩ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Vaa n balɩ nɩ, naa chɩaŋ ma nɩ keŋ a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yaala jaaŋ nɩ tuo a dii sɩba ye ke mɩŋ, aŋ ka yi nɩ jaaŋ.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Balɩ tɩ saŋŋa dieke wɩaha gie dɩ balla a yi. Bɩ balɩ tɩ Wudieke dɩ balla a dagɩ sɩba wɩaha gie mana baa a yi mɩŋ.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Meri die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Tɔ, manɩŋ n yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yɔmʋ, die yi a yɩ mɩŋ sɩba fʋ bala die wo. Die malakaka die dɩ wa a nyɩŋ a ga ta va wa.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Ta nɩ keŋ a zie jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ta vuodieke mana dɩ yine nɩ bɩaŋ, nɩ vaa ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa aŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ nan nagɩ nɩ taalɩ a chaa nɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma dɩ vuoŋ gaaŋ nɩ.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Lɩaka wɔŋ yi siri a gbarɩ dɩ ka chɩa tɩɩsɩsɩ; tɩɩ dieke dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna ba nan chɩa ka a taaŋ a nagɩ yi boliŋ me.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Meri die dɩ yi siri lagɩ lagɩ a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ Judia ma, Judia tɩŋgbaŋ yaa wa kunkoge.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Die ba bɩna a yiŋŋi keŋ tʋgɩ Jerusalemi Yisa die dɩ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩŋ ma a dɩ chʋŋ, Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋra bataŋ dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Vuosi pam nan keŋ n saaŋ ma a baarɩ N wone wo, a gaaŋ vuosi pam.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Die daadamba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa tɩ wa mʋ tɩ yi.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: mi ʋ ga juu Zakaria tigiŋ me a waasɩ Elizabeti.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa yiko fʋ yaa yie nyinti gie? Mɩnɩa yɩna fʋ kanɩŋ yiko ke mi.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Nɩ keŋ a wʋŋ tɔntɩ wɩa aŋaŋ tɔntɩ wʋbalɩka nɩ da keŋ vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ. Wɩaha gie nan yi ama a kaaŋ dagɩ sɩba dakpatɩkɩrɩ daarɩ keŋye mɩŋ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, Vuodieke mana dɩ yalla nyinyeeke ale mʋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩ vuodieke dɩ wone, ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ yalla nyindiike mʋ ʋ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Elizabeti die dɩ wʋnna Meri waasɩkʋ, bʋanyaalɩka die dɩ dɔŋ ʋ nyuuti ma. Womi die Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ die dɩ suuli Elizabeti.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa n pɩasɩ nɩ wʋbalɩmɩŋ amʋ nɩ keŋ bɩagɩ a mɩŋŋɩ balɩ mɩŋ ka chɩaŋ, n nan balɩ nɩ vuodieke yiko n yalla a tʋma nyinti gie wo.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Buuriŋ nan wagɩ buuriŋ, tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ nan wagɩ tamba, tɩŋgbaŋ nan dɩ dɔma, vuosi nan kpi kɔŋ ama geŋhe baa a yiwo wahalaha piiliŋ.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Mi lampotuosiriŋ bataŋ dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ ta die a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, lalɩa tɩ ba tɩ yi.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: die ʋ faasɩ a keesi ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ alibarika bie fʋ ma a tɩaŋ hɔgʋba mana, ta bɩ bie bʋa dieke fʋ balla fʋ mɩɩrɩ wa ma!.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ Jɔɔn die ye wo yiko sɩa a yaa sɩa vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, sɩa yiko ke mi die dɩ nyɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ yaa vuotaŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Nɩ daansɩma nɩ gbaŋ, ba nan yigi nɩ a ga naalɩba jigiŋ. Ba nan nɩgɩ nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone jige, ba nan yi nɩ zie nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ naalɩba nɩŋŋa n wɩa, aŋ nɩ balɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da keŋ tuo a tɩaŋ nɩ mʋna nɩ tuo die.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Bɩa yine ta n Yɔmʋtieŋ nuŋ dɩ keŋ n jigiŋ giena?.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Tɩ baa tɩ balɩ wa lalɩa? Tɩ keŋ baarɩ bʋ nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ʋ nan baarɩ, bɩa die wana yi tɩ ka yi Jɔɔn yada?.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: ama sie Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnɩŋ tʋgɩ tɩgɩsɩ mana ta dakpatɩkɩrɩ ye keŋ.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Ta sojasi bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Ta tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, bɩa tɩ mʋ tɩ yi? Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da mugisi vuosi a tuo ba ligire yaa a chibi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ vuosi, ama nɩ vaa nɩ nine suuli nɩ tune.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: N dene a wʋŋ fʋ waasɩkʋ, bʋanyaalɩ dieke dɩ benne n nyuuti ma wa dɩ dɔŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Tɩ nan bɩagɩ baarɩ, vuotaŋ jigiŋ.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Ba keŋ a yigi nɩ a ga naalɩŋ jigiŋ, nɩ da keŋ vaa ka yi nɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ die, ama saŋka dɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ nɩ balɩ wudieke mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala nɩ balɩ. Dama wudiekemba nɩ balla nɩ balɩ wa kaaŋ yi nɩ wʋbalɩka, a baa a nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ ma.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Ta vuosi die dɩ piili a yile ba sʋgɩtɩ ma ta yaa tama Jɔɔn ma, dɩ ʋ nan keŋ a yi Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Fʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tuone a dii dɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala wudieke wo nan keŋ yi wusie, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Die ba balla wudieke a yɩ Yisa die wone Tɩ zɩ. Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Die nɩŋ n gbaŋ n kaaŋ balɩ nɩ jigidieke n yene yiko ke gie a yie nyintiti gie.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Vuosi nan nagɩ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nɩmballɩ a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba, chʋalɩŋ gbaŋ nan yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ a yɩ ba ballɩ. Ballɩ dɩaŋ nan yiŋŋi a wagɩ ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta nagɩ ba a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Die wɩa Jɔɔn die balɩ a yɩ ba mana dɩ, Manɩŋ n sɩwa nɩ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ ama vuodieke dɩ tɩanna mɩŋ kieŋ, n ka tʋgɩ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ forisi ʋ nɩɩra guune gbaŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka aŋaŋ boliŋ a sɩɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Die wɩa Meri die dɩ baarɩ: N bɩra Ŋmɩŋ n sʋŋ ma.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Vuoŋ mana nan haa nɩ n wɩa, ama vuodieke dɩ a keŋ bɩagɩ tuoli a mana a ga tʋgɩ dakpatɩkɩrɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ʋ yallɩ wa ʋ kpalɩchaarɩkɩŋ ʋ nuuŋ me dɩ ʋ chaarɩ zaaha ta nagɩ zaabiehe a yi ʋ bumbuŋ me ta nagɩ zaakpeetiti a yi boli dieke dɩ kana ka kpise me.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: ta yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine n Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ ma.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Nɩ nan ye Jabɩaŋ dɩ zene jigidieke ka kana ka mʋ ka zie. . Saŋka mi vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma nɩ chɩgɩ ga kunkogiŋ me.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ mʋʋlɩna wʋvɩɩnaha gie a yɩa vuosisi, die ʋ balɩ wɩa yiri yiri a kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba tarɩgɩ ba beriŋ.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: dama ʋ chɩgɩ wa manɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine ʋ hɔgʋlɩa yɔmʋ zɔɔlɩŋ. A nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, vuota mana nan baarɩ n yiwo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ tieŋ.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ bie ʋ mampili sikpeŋ ʋ da keŋ a sʋʋŋ a juu tigiri me dɩ ʋ nagɩ jaaŋ a ga.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Ama die ʋ galɩŋ naaŋ Herodi nɩŋ dama die ʋ gbatɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa hɔgʋ Herodiasi a faarɩ ta bɩ yi wʋbɩatɩ pam.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: dama hagɩrɩtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ tʋŋ wa tʋnkpɩɩma a yɩ mɩŋ, ʋ saaŋ yine kasɩ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Vuodieke dɩ benne kʋaŋ ma da keŋ a kuli tigiŋ a nagɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ bɩ yi wʋbɩaŋ a gʋtɩ a ma, ta ligi Jɔɔn dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Ʋ bɩ yaa zɔɔlɩŋ aŋaŋ vuota diekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana wa saŋŋa mana.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Bɩa wʋbɩaŋ balla ka bemme hɔgʋ nyuutitieliŋ me aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla ballɩnyaalɩsɩ!.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋnna sʋkʋleeliŋ, Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋra mana die dɩ balɩ a zieŋ ba balla ba yi wudieke. Die ba bɔbɩ Yisa a yaa wa a ga Paliti jigiŋ a nagɩ wa a yi Paliti nuusi me.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, die Yisa dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ʋ sɩɩ ʋ gbaŋ. Mi Yisa die dɩ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ, ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ die dɩ yuori.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: Ʋ tɩɩntɩ wa ʋ nuuŋ a tʋŋ hagɩrɩŋ tʋʋma, ta chʋʋsɩ kalinbaantieliŋ wʋsaŋka.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ amʋ wɩaha gie da keŋ a yi yɩbɩŋ.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Paliti dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa? Yisa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Wa, fʋ balɩna.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ ʋ sikpeŋ aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ sɩba ŋmarɩŋ. Ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine n Bʋachoti; fʋnɩŋ n nine dɩ suuli.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Ta wuri naakpɩɩma ba naalɩŋ ma, ta kɔtɩ wabɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Yisa die yiwo bɩna baŋɩsɩ-taa ta die a piili ʋ tʋʋma. Vuosi die yile sɩba ʋ yiwo Josefu bʋa; ta Josefu chɔɔŋ die yine Heli.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ balɩ wʋbɩatɩ pam yaa gamma Yisa ma.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Daraaha mi wahala nan faasɩ a tɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naanna dʋnɩa dɩ yaa ga mʋʋna wahala. Wahala dɩaŋ kaaŋ bɩ beri a tɩaŋ naa.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: ta yi vuodiekemba kɔŋ dɩ yalla nyindiiki vɩɩna ta vaa nyintitieliŋ dɩ ga aŋaŋ nuuwɩaga.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: ta Heli chɔɔŋ die yine Matati, ta Matati chɔɔŋ die yine Liiva, ta Liiva chɔɔŋ die yine Meliki, ta Meliki chɔɔŋ die yine Janayi, ta Janayi chɔɔŋ die yine Josefu.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Paliti dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ Yisa bɩbra dɩ, Ka baa fʋ balɩ wɩɩŋ? Ye ba balla dɩ yi wudiekemba.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tɩŋ ka gobi daraaha, dɩ mʋna vuoŋ tɩŋ kaaŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ ama ʋ vuovʋarɩkaha wɩa ʋ gobi daraaha.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Ta dɩ nʋa dieke die ʋ yɩna tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba Izara vuosisi die dɩ yine ʋ tʋntʋntɩba, ta suŋŋi be.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: ta Josefu chɔɔŋ die yine Matatiasi, ta Matatiasi chɔɔŋ die yine Amosi, ta Amosi chɔɔŋ die yine Nahumi, ta Nahumi chɔɔŋ die yine Esili, ta Esili chɔɔŋ die yine Nagayi.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ tarɩ bɩbra, die dɩ yi Paliti mamachi.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Saŋka mi vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ balɩ a yɩ fʋ dɩ, Ye, Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ, Masia wa bie giena yaa ʋ bie mi! Da keŋ a tuo wo a dii.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: Ta bɩ tɩɩnsɩ, a chɩgɩ Abarahami aŋaŋ ʋ haagɩsɩ zɔɔlɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: ta Nagayi chɔɔŋ die yine Maati, ta Maati chɔɔŋ die yine Matatiasi, ta Matatiasi chɔɔŋ die yine Semeni, ta Semeni chɔɔŋ die yine Joseki, ta Joseki chɔɔŋ die yine Joda.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Die dɩ yiwo ba buuriŋ wɩɩŋ dɩ gasɩtɩanɩŋ dʋʋga saŋŋa mana ba dagɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ dansarɩka sʋŋ aŋ Paliti vʋarɩ wa a taaŋ.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Dama saŋka mi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ Masiasi aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba nan keŋ nyɩŋ a tʋŋ mamachi wɩa dɩ ba gaaŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna wa gbaŋ, ba tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Meri die yiwo chɩɩtɩ ataa Elizabeti jigiŋ ta die viiri kuli.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: ta Joda chɔɔŋ die yine Johanani, ta Johanani chɔɔŋ die yine Resa, ta Resa chɔɔŋ die yine Zerubabeli, ta Zerubabeli chɔɔŋ die yine Sialitili, ta Sialitili chɔɔŋ die yine Neri.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Saŋka mi daa wʋnyɩ die dine Barabasi a bie dansarɩka sʋŋ ʋ ŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ. Die ba wagɩ wa Paliti vuosi a yalla ba gbatɩ Juu vuosi a nyɩŋ Paliti vuosisi nuusi me, waagɩbʋ sʋŋ die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ kpi, die wɩa die ba ligi be.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Nɩ mɩŋŋɩ a yalla nɩ gbaŋ, n balɩ nɩ wɩɩŋ mana ta daarɩ ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Die Elizabeti maarɩŋ daaŋ die dɩ tʋgɩ die ʋ mɩɩrɩ Bʋadembiŋ.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: ta Neri chɔɔŋ die yine Meliki, ta Meliki chɔɔŋ die yine Addi, Addi chɔɔŋ die yine Kosami, ta Kosami chɔɔŋ die yine Elimadami, ta Elimadami chɔɔŋ die yine Eri.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Daadamba die dɩ lagɩŋ a balla Paliti dɩ ʋ yi ʋ ŋaana a wɔŋ yie die wo a yɩ ba.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Wahalaha kʋaŋ chaaŋ ŋmɩnnɩ nan sɩbɩ, chɩɩkʋ kaaŋ bɩ yɩantɩ.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Die ʋ jʋjʋrɩtamba aŋaŋ ʋ maarɩŋ die dɩ wʋnna wʋvɩɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine a yɩ wa wa ba mana sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: ta Eri chɔɔŋ die yine Josua, ta Josua chɔɔŋ die yine Eliaza, ta Eliaza chɔɔŋ die yine Jorimi, ta Jorimi chɔɔŋ die yine Matati, ta Matati chɔɔŋ die yine Liiva.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yaala n vʋarɩ Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa a yɩ nɩ.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Chɩŋmarɩsɩsɩ nan nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a nan, ta nyindieke mana dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɔŋ.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Die bʋanyaalɩka die dɩ tʋgɩna daraa anɩɩ die ba baa ba gobi ʋ kɔlɩ, die ba yaala ba nagɩ wa ʋ chɔɔŋ saaŋ Zakaria a yɩ wa.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: ta Liiva chɔɔŋ die yine Simiani, ta Simiani chɔɔŋ die yine Juda, ta Juda chɔɔŋ die yine Josefu, ta Josefu chɔɔŋ die yine Jonami, ta Jonami chɔɔŋ die yine Elayakimi.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Die ʋ paalɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die yaa wa Yisa a keŋ a yɩ wa mɩŋ dama die ba yiwo sugigolitieliŋ.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Saŋka mi vuosi nan ye vuota Bʋadembiŋ dɩ nyɩnna nɩɩŋmara ma a keŋ sʋʋna aŋaŋ yiko pam aŋaŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: ama die ʋ nuŋ wo die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ! Ʋ saaŋ nan yi Jɔɔn.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: ta Elayakimi chɔɔŋ die yine Melia, ta Melia chɔɔŋ die yine Mena, ta Mena chɔɔŋ die yine Matata, ta Matata chɔɔŋ die yine Nafani, ta Nafani chɔɔŋ die yine Davidi.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ hagɩ a zie vuosi me a balɩ ba dɩ ba baarɩ Paliti vʋarɩ Barabasi a yɩ ba.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Ʋ nan tʋŋ malakasisi dɩ ba dɩ dɩa dʋnɩa lʋga mana a lagɩsɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkaha jigiŋ mana.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ta ba balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Nɩ tigiri me vuoŋ wori ʋ saaŋ dɩ yi Jɔɔn.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: ta Davidi chɔɔŋ die yine Jese, ta Jese chɔɔŋ die yine Obedi, ta Obedi chɔɔŋ die yine Boazi, ta Boazi chɔɔŋ die yine Salimoni, ta Salimoni chɔɔŋ die yine Nasoni.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Paliti dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ daadamba dɩ, Ta bɩa nɩ wa yaala n yi vuodieke nɩ wasɩnana Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Nɩ vaa chunchule tɩɩka dagɩ nɩ yɩaŋ. Ka nagɩsɩ dɩ keŋ pɔsa ta vaatɩ dɩ nyɩnna nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ yɩbɩŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Womi die ba nagɩ ba nuusi a dagɩ dagɩtɩ a pɩasɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ wa saaŋ dieke ʋ yaala ba nagɩ yɩ bʋa wa.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: ta Nasoni chɔɔŋ die yine Aminadabi, ta Aminadabi chɔɔŋ die yine Adimini, ta Adimini chɔɔŋ die yine Arini, ta Arini chɔɔŋ die yine Hezironi, ta Hezironi chɔɔŋ die yine Perezi, ta Perezi chɔɔŋ die yine Juda.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a natɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Kpaasɩ wa a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, nɩ bɩ a keŋ a ye ta nyinti gie yie nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ n keniŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a balɩ ba dɩ ba yɩ wa gbanɩŋ, die ba nagɩ a yɩ wa, ʋ maagɩ dɩ, Bʋa wa saaŋ yine Jɔɔn! Die dɩ yi vuoŋ mana mamachi.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: ta Juda chɔɔŋ die yine Jakobu, ta Jakobu chɔɔŋ die yine Aziki, ta Aziki chɔɔŋ die yine Abarahami, ta Abarahami chɔɔŋ die yine Tera, ta Tera chɔɔŋ die yine Nahori.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ama Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Taalɩ bɩa ʋ yi? Ba bɩ faasɩ natɩ dɩ, Kpaasɩ wa a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Wusie nyinti gie mana nan yi ta vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne dʋnɩa mama lele ye kpi.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Lele womi Zakaria die dɩ bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩa bɩbra ta die a piili a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: ta Nahori chɔɔŋ die yine Serugi, ta Serugi chɔɔŋ die yine Rewo, ta Rewo chɔɔŋ die yine Pelegi, ta Pelegi chɔɔŋ die yine Eberi, ta Eberi chɔɔŋ die yine Sela.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Paliti die yaala daadamba sʋŋ fɩalɩ die wɩa ʋ vʋarɩ Barabasi a yɩ ba. Ta die wa yi sojasisi dɩ nɩgɩ Yisa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta wa nagɩ wa a yɩ dɩ ba ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ nan keŋ a tɩaŋ, ama n wʋbalɩka kaaŋ tɩaŋ naa da.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi ʋ jʋjʋrɩtamba mana, wɩaha gie die dɩ mʋʋlɩ a ga tɩgɩ diekemba dɩ benne Judia kunkogiŋ me mana.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: ta Sela chɔɔŋ die yine Kayinani, ta Kayinani chɔɔŋ die yine Afakisadi, ta Afakisadi chɔɔŋ die yine Siami, ta Siami chɔɔŋ die yine Nowa, ta Nowa chɔɔŋ die yine Lameki.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Die sojasisi dɩ wa nagɩ Yisa a juu Paliti gbaakʋ ma ta wa sojasi tɩalɩkaha.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ama vuoŋ ka sɩba daa dieke yaa saŋŋa dieke wɩaha gie dɩ baaŋ nan yi. Malakasisi gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ benne arɩzanna ma aŋaŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ ka sɩba daa dieke sie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna sɩbɩna.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Vuodieke mana die dɩ wʋnna wɩɩrɩ gie mana die dɩ yile a pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Vuobɩa bʋa wa dɩ baa ʋ bɩrɩŋ? Dama die ba wɔŋ ye dɩ Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ bie ʋ ma.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: ta Lameki chɔɔŋ die yine Metusila, ta Metusila chɔɔŋ die yine Enɔki, ta Enɔki chɔɔŋ die yine Jaredi, ta Jaredi chɔɔŋ die yine Mahalaleeli, ta Mahalaleeli chɔɔŋ die yine Keenani.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Die ba nagɩ guuŋmɩnɩŋ a yeegi wo, ta nagɩ haŋgɔɔsɩ a wʋnsɩ sibubiŋ a bubi ʋ sikpeŋ me.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Nɩ gbara, a daansɩma, dama nɩ ka sɩba saŋka dɩ baaŋ nan keŋ saŋŋa dieke wo.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ suuli bʋa wa chɔɔŋ, Zakaria, die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dɩ.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: ta Keenani chɔɔŋ die yine Enosi, ta Enosi chɔɔŋ die yine Sefi, ta Sefi chɔɔŋ die yine Adami, ta Adami chɔɔŋ die yine Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ta piili a waasa wa dɩ, Juu vuosisi naaŋ, dʋa dʋa.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma sɩba daa wʋnyɩ dɩ dɩna a ga tɩŋ, ta vaa ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ aŋaŋ ba wʋnyɩ mana tʋʋmɩŋ. Ta balɩ dɩ vuodieke dɩ gbarɩnana dɩ ʋ gbara.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ bɩrɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi Ŋmɩnnɩ, dama ʋ keŋye mɩŋ a keŋ suŋŋi ʋ vuosi a gbatɩ ba a taaŋ.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Die ba nɩgɩ ʋ sikpeŋ me aŋaŋ daaŋ, a tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yi wo ta gbirigi ba dune me a yie sɩba ba jɩama ba naaŋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Die wɩa nɩ gbara dama nɩ ka sɩba tigiritieŋ dɩ kienene saŋŋa dieke. Dɩ nan bɩagɩ a yi jɩŋmɩŋ yaa tanseese, yaa kparaaŋ kpaŋ saŋŋa yaa tʋnvʋʋnɩŋ saŋŋa.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Ʋ yɩ tɩ wa gbɩgbatɩ hagɩrɩtieŋ ta ʋ yi Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Die ba vʋarɩna wa falaha a kpatɩ wa ba yiŋŋi a vʋarɩ garɩguuŋmɩmbʋ ta nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeeke a yeegi wo. Ta yaa wa a gara dɩ ba ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Ʋ a keŋ bʋnyɩ ʋ da keŋ haarɩ fʋ ta gʋʋra.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Kʋrɩŋ ma wa Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die vaa wa ʋ naazʋalɩŋ kasɩmba dɩ balɩ naa dɩ.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Die ba ganana wa ba ga tuoli Simoni, Alekizanda aŋaŋ Rufusi chɔɔŋ, die ʋ yiwo Sayireni tɩŋgbaŋ vuoŋ ta ʋ nyɩŋ jigiŋ a keŋ a juo tɩka ma ba mugisi wo ʋ tuo dagarɩkʋ a chii.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: N balla wudieke a yɩ nɩ wa, n balɩ a yɩ wa vuoŋ mana dɩ ʋ gbara.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: ʋ nan gbatɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ tɩ dataasɩ nuusi me aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ hana tɩ.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Die ba yaa Yisa a ga jigidieke ba wasɩnana Gɔlɩgɔta .

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: die ʋ bɩ baarɩ ʋ nan chɩgɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, ta ʋ kaaŋ wɔŋ daaŋ ʋ nʋakasɩka.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Mi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nagɩ daaŋ aŋaŋ tɩɩŋ dieke ba wasɩnana mɩrɩ a lagɩŋ taŋ a yɩ wa, die ʋ zeti bʋ nyuule.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: nʋa dieke die ʋ yɩna tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Abarahami wa.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Die ba kpaasɩ wa a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma ta nagɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke a puo dʋaŋ ta yi chaachaa ba wʋnyɩ mana dɩ baaŋ nan ye die.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: Die ʋ hʋʋ dɩ ʋ nan gbatɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ tɩ dataasɩ nuusi me amʋ tɩ nan bɩagɩ a jɩaŋ wa ta ŋmaamɩŋ wo tɩ.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Die ba kpaasɩ wa wa sʋkʋʋŋ hawasɩ awayɩ saŋŋa.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: ta bɩ vaa tɩ yi vuokasɩ aŋaŋ vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa tɩ mɩsɩ ma mana.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Die ba nagɩ gbanɩŋ a maagɩ wɩɩrɩ gie wɩa ba kpaasɩna wa a diisi dagarɩkʋ sikpeŋ dɩ, Juu Vuosisi Naaŋ Wa .

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Ta Fʋnɩŋ n bʋa, ba nan wa fʋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa. Nan woliŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nɩŋŋa a wʋnsɩ ʋ sieŋ a yɩ wa.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Die ba bɩ kpaasɩ wa gaarɩŋ bale dagarɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ ʋ ma, wʋnyɩ dɩ bie ʋ nuudiigiŋ ta wʋnyɩ wa dɩaŋ dɩ bie ʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: ta balɩ ʋ vuosi dɩ ʋ nan gbatɩ ba a taaŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ balla ʋ nagɩ ba taalɩ a chaa ba wa.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ tɩannana die ŋaaŋ vigi wo ba sikpigile ta taanna jome Yisa ma dɩ, Aha! Die fʋ bala nan kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ Jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ a taaŋ ta daraa ataa kʋaŋ fʋ mɩɩ ka wa!.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Wusie, tɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ chɩga zɔɔlɩŋ ta ʋ baga dɩ bie tɩ ma ta tɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ daraaŋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: Lele keŋ a sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ sikpeŋ a gbatɩ fʋ gbaŋ.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: ka nan chaaŋ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne lɩmɩŋ ma aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ gbigine kuŋ; ka nan bɩ chaaŋ tɩ nagɩsɩ ma aŋ tɩ dɩa sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ sieku.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba gbaŋ die dɩ vʋara Yisa fala ta bala a yɩa taŋ dɩ, Die bɩagɩ a gbata bataŋ ta kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ fʋ gbaŋ.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ bʋa wa die dɩ bɩrɩma aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩbɩŋ, ta die keŋ ga haagɩŋ ma a ga bie mi dee a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke die ʋ vʋarɩna ʋ gbaŋ Izara vuosi me.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ ye Masia wa, Izara vuosisi naaŋ wa nan keŋ a sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ ma aŋ tɩ yi wo yada! Die gaarɩŋ bale diekemba ba kpaasɩna a gʋtɩ ʋ ma wa gbaŋ die zɩa wa mɩŋ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Die ŋmɩŋ dɩ wana a ga tʋgɩ sikpeŋ sʋnsʋŋ, tɩŋgbaŋka mana mana die dɩ sibi. Die ka sibi wo dee a ga tʋgɩ ŋmɩŋgbɩlɩkɩŋ hawasɩ ataa saŋŋa.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Hawasɩ ataa wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ faasɩ a keesi a baarɩ dɩ, Eloyi, Eloyi lema Sabakitani? Ka chɩaŋ wone, N Ŋmɩŋ, n Ŋmɩŋ, bɩa yine fʋ va mɩŋ.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ zene mi wo bataŋ dɩ wʋnna naa, ba baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ wʋmma, ʋ wasa Elaja.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ die dɩ chɩgɩ a ga nagɩ gbara alʋ daŋmɩɩsɩŋ ma ta nagɩ ka sagɩ daaŋ ma a yaa ka a yɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ tuo nyuu, ta baarɩ dɩ, Lele nɩ wa vaa ʋ nyɩɩna aŋ tɩ ye sɩba Elaja nan keŋ a sʋʋŋ wa dagarɩkʋ ma.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Yisa die dɩ faasɩ keesi ta kpi.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Die garɩ dieke die dɩ sagɩna a puo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ bule wo, die dɩ puo bule a nyɩŋ sikpeŋ mi a keŋ sʋʋŋ chɩaŋ ma.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋŋ dieke die dɩ zene mi a ye Yisa dɩ keesine dene wo ta kpi wo dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie die seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Hɔɔŋ bataŋ die gana a ga zie saasaa ta daansa. Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Salome aŋaŋ Meri vuodieke dɩ yine Jemisi bike aŋaŋ Josefu nuŋ die gʋtɩna hɔgʋba sʋŋ a zie mi.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Hɔgʋba gie die dɩ wa Yisa a suŋŋi wo saŋŋa dieke die ʋ benne Galili me. Hɔgʋ diekemba die dɩ dɩna wa a ga Jerusalemi gbaŋ die gʋtɩ bie mi.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, Josefu vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Arimatia die dɩ keŋ a tʋgɩ mi.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: Josefu die yiwo vuodieke die ba yɩnana jɩlɩma Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋraha sʋŋ ta die ʋ chɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ baaŋ nan keŋ saŋŋa dieke. Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi ba gɔmɩsɩ a gbarɩ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ. Die Josefu die dɩ dɩ dembisi ta ga Paliti jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ vaa ʋ nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Die dɩ yiwo Paliti mamachi ʋ wʋnna dɩ Yisa wɔŋ kpi mɩŋ. Paliti die dɩ wa sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ a pɩasɩ sɩba Yisa kpi yʋasɩ mɩŋ?.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Die sojaka die dɩ balla wa dɩ Yisa kpiye mɩŋ, ʋ balɩ a yɩ Josefu dɩ ʋ nan bɩagɩ a ga nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Josefu die dɩ daa garɩŋ a nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka a sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ ma ta a vili Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka a nagɩ ka a dʋaŋ ba tune vɔrɩŋ dieke taŋ lʋgɩŋ ma. Ta die wa nagɩ taŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ a ligi vɔrɩkʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Meri vuodieke dɩ yine Josefu nuŋ die zie a daansa mɩŋ, a die ye ba nagɩna Yisa a dʋaŋ jigidieke wo.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaaŋ, ta suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vaa ʋ ga haagɩŋ ma.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
