local: mi die Sitaani die dɩ magɩsɩ wa a ga tʋgɩ daraa baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa. Saŋka mi mana die ʋ ka dii jaaŋ mana, die wɩa ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ wa.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Sitaani die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ bʋa fʋ vaa tanɩ gie bɩrɩŋ nyindiike.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ Da nyindiike nyɩɩna ma wɩa vuota dɩ beri. .

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Mi Sitaani dɩ bɩ nagɩ wa a jʋalɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a dagɩ wa dʋnɩa ka gie mana lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: ta balɩ wa dɩ, N nan nagɩ yikoke gie aŋaŋ nyinti gie mana a yɩ fʋ, die ba nagɩ yɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ ta n nan bɩagɩ nagɩha a yɩ vuodieke mana n yaalala.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: N nan nagɩ yikoke gie aŋaŋ nyinti gie mana a yɩ fʋ dɩɩ yi fʋ sʋʋŋ gbirigi a jɩaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuota jɩama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma ta bɩ dɩa ʋ nyɩɩna ma! .

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Die Sitaani dɩ bɩ nagɩ wa a ga Jerusalemi a nagɩ wa a jʋalɩ zieŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sikpeŋ me, ta balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa yʋgɩ a chigisi tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa ʋ malakasisi mɩŋŋɩ daansɩma fʋ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Dɩ bɩ maagɩ dɩ, Ba nan tuoli a tuo fʋ ba nuusi me amʋ fʋ naabɩŋ da keŋ a gbɩŋ taŋ aŋ fʋ ye daŋŋa. .

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuoŋ da keŋ magɩsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ. .

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Die Sitaani die dɩ magɩsɩna wa a jɩɩ wa die ʋ va wa ta ga, sie saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan ye sieŋ bɩbra.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi ga Galili, ta die a suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ. Die ba mʋʋlɩ ʋ wɩa tɩŋgbaŋka lʋga mana.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Die ʋ dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me; ta vuoŋ mana dɩ bɩrɩ wa.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ ga Nazeriti, jigidieke die ba wubine wo ʋ ga bɩrɩŋ vuota. Ta die davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ die ʋ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me sɩba ʋ wone yie die wo. Die ʋ hagɩ a zie dɩ ʋ karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: ta die ba nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ maagɩna a keŋ yɩ wa. Die ʋ yuori ke a ye jigidieke ba maagɩna dɩ.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ bie n ma, dama ʋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ n mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ. Ʋ tʋŋ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ ga balɩ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ n nan vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ yɔrɩ, ta bɩ balɩ yɩɩsɩ dɩ ba nan ye, ta bɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne mugisiŋ me a taaŋ yɔrɩ.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: ta bɩ mʋʋlɩ dɩ saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ dɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ gbatɩ ʋ vuosi a taaŋ.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Yisa die dɩ karɩnna a kpatɩ wa die ʋ pili gbaŋkʋ a yiŋŋi a nagɩ ka a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ, ta sʋʋŋ kalɩ. Ta vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me wo die dɩ jɩɩma wa.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ piili a bala ba dɩ, Wudieke n karɩnna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋkʋ, keŋ yi wusie jinne, sɩba nɩ wʋnnana die wo.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Die ba mana sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ aŋaŋ wa ta ba bɩra wa, die dɩ yi be mamachi aŋaŋ wudiekemba ʋ bala wa. Die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Daa ʋ yine Josefu bʋa wa.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N ka chɩɩlɩya dɩ nɩ nan balɩ mɩŋ nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Gbɩgbaantʋ gbaaŋ fʋ gbaŋ. Nɩ nan bɩ balɩ a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yi mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma n gbaŋ gbaŋ n tɩŋ ma sɩba nɩ wʋnnana dɩ n yiye Kapenɔmi ma wa.

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ dɩ, vuosi ka tuose Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ ma.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Wusie, kpihɔgʋba pam die benne Izara tɩŋgbaŋ ma, saŋŋa dieke Elaja die dɩ benne wo, saŋka mi die nɩɩŋ dɩ ka nɩɩya bɩna ataa aŋaŋ chɩɩtɩ ayʋaba, ta kɔŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ keŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋka mana ma.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: ama Ŋmɩŋ die ka tʋŋ Elaja ba wʋnyɩ mana jigiŋ; die ʋ tʋŋ wa wa kpihɔgʋ dieke dɩ benne Zarefati Sadoni tɩŋ ma wa jigiŋ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Elasa die dɩ benne wo gama pam die benne Izara tɩŋ ma, ama ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka ye gbaamɩŋ sie Naamani vuodieke dɩ yine Siria dembiŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ nyɩɩna ma die yene gbaamɩŋ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Die vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me wo ta wʋŋ Yisa balɩkʋ gie, die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ pam aŋaŋ wa.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Die ba hagɩ a yagɩ wa a nyɩŋ tɩka a yaa wa ga kunkogi dieke ba mɩna ba tɩka a zie wo sikpeŋ a yaala ba kpaŋŋɩ wa a taaŋ gɔɔlɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: ama die ʋ nyɩɩgɩ ba sʋnsʋŋ a chʋŋ ga.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta Yisa die dɩ ga Kapenɔmi dieke dɩ benne Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa, davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ die ʋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me a piili daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Ʋ dagɩkʋ die dɩ yi be mamachi pam dama die ʋ bala aŋaŋ yiko.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Ta daa wʋnyɩ jɩmbɩaŋ die dɩ yalla ta ʋ bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me die dɩ faasɩ keesi ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Fʋnɩŋ Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti, bɩa fʋ yaala tɩ jigiŋ? Keŋ dɩ fʋ chʋʋsɩ tɩ mɩŋ? N sɩba vuodieke dɩ yine fʋ, yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋnkasɩ vuoŋ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ natɩ jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ, Tarɩ watɩ ta keŋ nyɩŋ daa wa ma. Die jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ nagɩ daa wa taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ba mana nɩŋŋa ta keŋ nyɩŋ ʋ ma, aŋaŋ ka nagɩna wa a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ mana die ka ka yɩ wa daŋŋa.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Die dɩ yi vuotamba mana mamachi die ba pɩasa tamba dɩ, Bɩa balɩŋ wʋnna? Ta ʋ yaa yiko aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ a yɩa jɩmbɩatɩ nʋa, ta a nyɩna vuosi ma.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Die wɩa die ba mʋʋlɩ Yisa saaŋ tɩka ma mana.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me a ga Simoni tigiŋ. Die Simoni hanɩɩŋ hɔgʋ die dɩ dʋa a yʋagɩ, ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ faasɩ tʋʋlɩ, die ba jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ suŋŋi wo.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ ga a ga zie jigidieke ʋ dʋana wa, ta yɩ yʋagɩbʋ nʋaŋ, die yʋagɩbʋ dɩ va wa; ta hɔgʋ wa dɩ hagɩ bʋnyɩ a yi ba chaantɩ.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Die ŋmɩŋ die dɩ june a kpatɩ, vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtieliŋ ba tige me ba yʋagɩ yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri die dɩ chii be a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi diisi ba mana ma die ba mana dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Ta ʋ bɩ yi ta jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ nyɩŋ vuosi pam ma, die a nyɩnana wa die a taaŋ keesiŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa. Ama die ʋ ka bɩ saagɩ dɩ a balɩ wɩɩŋ, dama a sɩba baarɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Die tuŋ die dɩ vʋnna die Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩka ma a ga haagɩŋ ma; die vuosisi die dɩdɩ dɩa a yaala wa, die ba kenne a ye wo wo, die ba ka bɩ yaala ʋ ga ta va ba.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Ama die ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Sie n ga tɩgɩhanɩŋ ba a ga balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩna yaa gamma ʋ naarɩ keniŋ wɩa mi gbaŋ, dama die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋŋ mɩŋ.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma die ʋ dɩdɩ dɩa Judia tɩgɩsɩ ta juo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a daga vuosi wɩaha.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ kaanɩ die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ chʋŋ tɩanna zaa kʋaŋ ma, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gobe zaapakɩtɩ a yɩaga ŋɔba.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ta Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yie wudieke tɩ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna dɩ tɩ da yi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Yisa die a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ Davidi die dɩ yine die wo? Kɔŋ die yalla wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: die ʋ ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku sʋŋ a ga nagɩ paanʋ dieke ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ a ŋɔbɩ ta bɩ nagɩ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba ŋɔbɩ. Ama tɩ mɩraha die ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ vuoŋ ŋɔbɩha sie Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yine davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ tieŋ.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ kaanɩ bɩbra Yisa die dɩ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku a daga Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ta daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie mi ʋ nuudiigiŋ die dɩ kpi.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Ta die Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ bie mi a yaala sieti dɩ ba galɩŋ Yisa, die wɩa die ba daansa wa dɩ ba ye dɩ ʋ nan gbaaŋ wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Ama Yisa die mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyilehe ta balɩ yɩ daa dieke nuuke dɩ kpine wo dɩ, Hagɩ a zie vuoŋ mana nɩŋŋa. Die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ a zie mi.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Die Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa n pɩasɩ nɩ, bɩa tɩ mɩraha dɩ baa tɩ yime davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ? Dɩ tɩ suŋŋi vuosi yaa tɩ chʋʋsɩ ba? Tɩ gbatɩma vuoŋ mɩsɩ taamma yaa tɩ kʋʋ ba.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Die wɩa ʋ daansɩ ba mana a tɩaŋ, ta die balɩ yɩ nuukpiikiri tieŋ dɩ, Tɩɩntɩ fʋ nuuke. Die ʋ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuke die ka bɩ hagɩrɩ bɩbra.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisisi die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ, a piili a bala yɩa taŋ ba baaŋ yi Yisa dene.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ ga jʋalɩ kunkogiŋ dɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, a die jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ kanɩŋ yuku mi mana.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Die tuŋ dɩ vʋnna die ʋ wa ʋ kʋaŋanvuosi a keŋ lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ a vʋarɩ vuosi baŋ-ale, a wa ba tʋntʋntɩŋ.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Die ʋ vʋarɩ wa Simoni aŋaŋ Simoni nɩmbʋabiŋ Andurusi aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Batolomiwo.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: aŋaŋ Matiwo aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ Alifusi bʋadembiŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Simoni vuodieke die dɩ yaala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yallɩma ba gbaŋ wa.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: aŋaŋ Judasi vuodieke die dɩ yine Jemisi bʋa wa aŋaŋ Judasi Asikaroti vuodieke die dɩ posine Yisa chɩaka.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Yisa die dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ kunkogiri me aŋaŋ ba, die a ga zie tɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ mʋna taŋ; ta die ʋ kʋaŋanvuosi pam die dɩ bie mi; vuosi pam die dɩ nyɩŋ Judia chaaŋ mana aŋaŋ Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Taya aŋaŋ Sadoni mana tɩgɩsɩ die dɩ gbigine mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: die ba keŋye mɩŋ dɩ ba wʋŋ ʋ wʋbalɩka ta bɩ yaala ʋ gbaaŋ ba. Vuodiekemba die jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla ba gbaŋ die keŋye mɩŋ, die ʋ gbaaŋ ba.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Die vuosi mana die dɩ mɩa dɩ ba gbɩ wa, dama hagɩrɩŋ die nyɩna ʋ ma a gbaama ba mana.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Saŋka mi die Yisa die dɩ daansɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ!.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba kɔŋ dɩ yalla lele, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ, dama nɩ nan keŋ chagɩ. Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnnana lele, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ, dama nɩ nan daansɩ a laa.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Vuosi dɩ keŋ haa nɩ ta zeti nɩ ta zɩa nɩ ta chʋʋsa nɩ saara, dama nɩ dɩna manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wɩa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta seme dama nɩ nyʋarɩ nan daansɩ dala Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma; die gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba chʋalɩba die dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nyintitieliŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ wɔŋ ye nɩ choti jaaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chagɩna lele, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama kɔŋ nan daansɩ yigi nɩ. Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ lanana lele, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ nan daansɩ ye sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ aŋaŋ kpaŋ.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba vuosi mana dɩ bɩrɩnana, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma; dama die gbaŋ gbaŋ ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ bɩrɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Ama n bala nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnnana n balɩkʋ gie, nɩ chome nɩ dataasɩ ta yime wʋvɩɩna a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ hana nɩ.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Ta vuodiekemba dɩ kanana kaatɩ a yɩa nɩ, nɩ balɩ ba dɩ Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi be, ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ yinene nɩ bɩaŋ.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ keŋ falɩ fʋ kaamɩŋ fʋ yiŋŋi kaanɩ wa gbaŋ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ falɩ. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ keŋ a nagɩ fʋ jayeekikpeŋkpɩɩŋ fʋ vaa ʋ nagɩ fʋ jayeekibiŋ a gʋtɩ.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ jʋʋsɩ fʋ jaaŋ fʋ yɩ wa, ta vuoŋ dɩ keŋ gbatɩ fʋ jaaŋ da keŋ dɩa a ga tuo.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Wudieke fʋ yaala vuosi yi a yɩ fʋ, fʋ yi die a yɩ fʋ chanchaalɩŋ.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ cho vuodiekemba dɩ chone fʋ nyɩɩna ma, bɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baa ʋ nagɩ gʋtɩ fʋ? Tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ gbaŋ cho wo vuodiekemba dɩ chone be.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi fʋ yie vɩɩnɩŋ a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yinene vɩɩnɩŋ a yɩa fʋ bɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baa ʋ nagɩ a gʋtɩ fʋ? Tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ gbaŋ yie die.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ paŋŋa vuodiekemba fʋ sɩbɩna dɩ ba nan yiŋŋi tuŋ fʋ, bɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baa ʋ nagɩ a gʋtɩ fʋ? Tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ gbaŋ paŋŋa die a yɩa ba chanchaalɩŋ ta yiŋŋi tuose ba nyinti.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Die wɩa nɩ chome nɩ dataasɩ ta yime vɩɩnɩŋ a yɩma ba. Ta dɩɩ yi nɩ paŋŋɩ vuoŋ jaaŋ ka ga yʋasɩ, da vaa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ chʋʋsɩ a wɩa, dama nɩ nyʋarɩ nan daansɩ dala, ta nɩ nan bɩ yi Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ. Dama wʋnɩŋ ʋ yie wʋvɩɩna a yɩa vuodiekemba nine dɩ kana ka suule wudieke ʋ yinene a yɩa ba aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ mana.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Nɩ chɩgɩma zɔɔlɩŋ sɩba nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩ chɩgɩnana zɔɔlɩŋ dene wo.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Da keŋ a dii vuoŋ sarɩya amʋ Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ dii fʋ sarɩya, da keŋ balɩ a chʋʋsɩ vuoŋ amʋ Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ balɩ a chʋʋsɩ fʋ. Nagɩ fʋ chanchaalɩŋ taalɩ a chaa ba amʋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa fʋ.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Yɩma vuosi amʋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ nagɩ yɩ fʋ. Dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan nagɩ ʋ kuruba a mɔgɩsɩ zaa, ta ka keŋ suuli aŋaŋ ka kʋanʋaŋ mana aŋ ʋ jɩɩ jɩɩ ta dɔŋ dɔŋ ta a keŋ suuli a jaasa aŋ ʋ nagɩ a yɩ fʋ, fʋ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a pɔgɩlɩ dene. Fʋ nagɩna fʋ kuruba a mɔgɩsa nyinti a yɩa vuosi die, Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ nan mɔgɩsɩ die a yɩ fʋ.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Yɩɩ nan bɩagɩ a datɩ ʋ chanchaaŋ? Ba mana kaaŋ nan goliŋ me?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Bʋbʋgʋrʋ dɩaŋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ dɩdagɩrʋ; ama bʋbʋgʋrʋ dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ mɩŋŋɩ a bʋgɩrɩ a kpatɩ ʋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba ʋ dɩdagɩrɩ wa.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Bɩa fʋ daansa a yese mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ chanchaaŋ nimbiŋ me ta ka ye dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Lalɩa fʋ baa fʋ yi a balɩ yɩ fʋ chanchaaŋ dɩ, N zʋa, vaa n vʋarɩ ŋmɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ nimbiŋ me, ta ka ye dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me? Fʋnɩŋ gɩgaantʋ wa gie, woliŋ nagɩ dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me amʋ fʋ mɩŋŋɩ ye a vʋarɩ ŋmɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ chanchaaka nimbiŋ me wo.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Tɩɩvɩɩnɩŋ ka wala nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩbɩatɩ, ta tɩɩ dieke dɩ yʋagɩna ka wala nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Tɩɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ma ba sɩba ka. Ba ka yese kɩnkamɩŋ haŋgɔɔsɩ jigiŋ a tʋʋra, yaa taama jɩa jigiŋ a tʋʋra.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Vuovɩɩnɩŋ yie tʋnvɩɩna dama ʋ sʋŋ vɩɩna mɩŋ; ta vuobɩaŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yie tʋmbɩatɩ dama ʋ sʋŋ suuli wo aŋaŋ wʋbɩatɩ. Jadieke dɩ benne vuoŋ sʋŋ ma kanɩŋ ʋ bala.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Bɩa nɩ wasa mɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ta ka yie wudieke n yaalala?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kienene n jigiŋ ta wʋmma n wʋbalɩkaha ta dɩha, n nan dagɩ nɩ ʋ sɩna die.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ sɩɩ sɩba daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ, ta die woliŋ a tuu tɩŋgbaŋ a sʋʋŋ a ga tʋgɩ taŋ ta dʋaŋ nyabɩlɩŋ tanɩ sikpeŋ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ nɩɩŋ dɩ keŋ nɩɩ pam ta mʋgɩŋ dɩ kpaa ta nyaabʋ dɩ keŋ nɩgɩ tigiri, ama die tigiri ka nanya dama die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ mɩɩ ka mɩŋ.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Ama vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana n wɩaha gie ta ka dɩha sɩɩ sɩba daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ a zieŋ tambʋsɩŋ me ta ka dʋaŋ nyabɩlɩŋ ka chɩaŋ wa, ta die nɩɩŋ dɩ nɩɩ mʋgɩŋ dɩ kpaa, ta nyaaŋ dɩ keŋ nɩgɩ tigiri, die ka nan bʋnyɩ. Bɩa chʋʋsɩbɩaŋ wʋnna.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩdɩ dɩa tɩgɩkpɩɩma aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ a bala Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale die dɩ wa mɩŋ a ga.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: aŋaŋ hɔgʋba bataŋ die ʋ vʋarɩna jɩmbɩatɩ ta gbaaŋ bataŋ yʋagɩtɩ; die ba yiwo, Meri Magidalini vuodieke die ʋ vʋarɩna jɩmbɩatɩ bayʋpɔyɩ ʋ ma:.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: bataŋ die yiwo Chuza hɔgʋ wa, Johana aŋaŋ Susana aŋaŋ hɔgʋ ba pam. Die hɔgʋba gie die naga ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nyinti a suŋŋe Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Die daadaaŋ pam die dɩ nyɩna tɩgɩsɩ tɩgɩsɩ pam a kieŋ Yisa jigiŋ, ta kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ keŋ lagɩsɩ a taaŋ a giliŋ Yisa, die ʋ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ:.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Daa wʋnyɩ die nyɩna ga ʋ kʋaŋ dɩ ʋ ga bʋrɩ nyɩŋbʋra. Die ʋ bʋrɩnana wa a taŋ die dɩ nan sieŋ me die ba tɩbɩha tɩanna ta nembisi die dɩ keŋ nagɩha dii.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan taŋ sikpeŋ jigidieke tantɩ die kana ka dala, die a nyune wo die a jigi dama tɩŋgbaŋka die ka sʋnsɩ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma die a mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ nyuŋ, haŋgɔɔsɩ die dɩ nyagɩ ha.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ta ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ ma, ta die a nyuŋ a bɩrɩŋ ta nyɩŋ bie kɔbɩga kɔbɩga. Yisa die dɩ wa balɩ ba a kpatɩ dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ yalla tɩba ta nan wʋŋ ʋ wʋmma.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vaa nɩ sɩba ʋ naarɩ wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩrɩ mɩŋ, ama banɩŋ ba nɩŋ ʋ taana ba nandaga, dɩ ba daansɩ ama ba da keŋ ye, ta wʋŋ ama ta kaaŋ dɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ wʋnna: Nyɩŋbʋraha yine Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka ha.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Nyɩŋbʋrɩ dieke dɩ nanna sieku me wo nɩasɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, ama Sitaani keŋ a nagɩ wɩa ha a nyɩŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ ma, amʋ ba da keŋ tuo dii ta ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Nyindiekemba dɩ nanna tanɩ sikpeŋ nɩasɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ wɩaha aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ama a ka sʋʋna ba sʋgɩtɩ ma, ba ŋaaŋ yiwo yada bɩta, ama saŋŋa dieke magɩsɩŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ ba ŋaaŋ nagɩha taaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Nyɩŋbʋrɩ dieke dɩ nanna haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ jigiri nɩasɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ wɩaha ama tɩŋgbaŋka wumugisike aŋaŋ nyinti yaalɩŋ keŋ juu a nyagɩ ha, amʋ a da keŋ bɩrɩŋ a nyɩŋ bie.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Ama nyindiekemba dɩ nanna tɩŋgbaŋ vɩɩkʋ ma wa nɩasɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana wʋŋ wɩaha ta tuohe aŋaŋ sʋgɩvɩɩnɩŋ ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ ha keŋ keŋ ta bɩ yaa hanɩa, ta nyɩŋ bie vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Vuosi ka chɔgɩsa popoli ta naga kpalɩ a bubi ke yaa a naga ka a ziele gado chɩaŋ. Ama ba ŋaaŋ nagɩ ka mɩŋ a sagɩ popoli daaŋ ma amʋ vuosisi dɩ keŋ juu ba ye ka chaaŋkʋ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Wudieke mana dɩ lɔbɩrɩna nan daansɩ a keŋ nyɩŋ yeŋ ta wudieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ ligine nan keŋ yuori ta nyɩŋ chaanɩŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Die wɩa nɩ sɩmma nɩ wʋnnana Ŋmɩŋ wɩa die, dama vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ ka ba nan yɩ wa pam a gʋtɩ, ama vuodieke dɩ wʋnnana ta ka mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ ha, ba nan tuo bɩta gbaŋ ʋ yilinene dɩ ʋ yalla wa a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Womi Yisa nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ die dɩ keŋ, ama die ba ka bɩagɩ a gbigi wo dama vuosi die dala mɩŋ.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Fʋ naa aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmballɩ zie yeŋ me ta yaala ba ye fʋ.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba mana dɩ, Manɩŋ n naa aŋaŋ n nɩmballɩ yine vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnnana Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha ta dɩ ha.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Yisa aŋaŋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ juu haarɩŋ sʋŋ, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa tɩ jʋalɩ ga gbagɩrɩ mi wo gaarɩ. Die wɩa die ba piili sieŋ.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Die ba benne haarɩkʋ sʋŋ a gara Yisa die dɩ gʋʋra. Ta bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ keŋ bʋnyɩ a nɩga gbagɩrɩ ma, die haarɩkʋ dɩ yaala ka suuli aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, die ba bie wʋbɩaŋ ma.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Ta die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ keŋ a sʋgɩrɩ wa a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, jakʋʋŋ tɩ bie kuŋ me. Yisa die dɩ hagɩ a natɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋikiri a mana die dɩ fɩalɩ sɔmm.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Womi die ʋ balɩ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada? Ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be, ka bɩ yi be mamachi ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Manɩa wʋnna? Ta nata bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ ta a tuose ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Ta die ba keŋ tʋgɩ Gerasa tɩŋ ma a zie ta vaa Galili tɩŋ ba kʋaŋ.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ kenne nyɩŋ haarɩkʋ ma wa, ta Gerasa daa wʋnyɩ die jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ yalla wa dɩ keŋ tuoli wo. Die ʋ beri wo die a yʋasɩ ta ka yeegi garɩŋ yaa dʋa tigiŋ me ama die ʋ bie wo jigidieke ba guunene vuosi haagɩŋ ma.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Saŋŋa dieke ʋ yene Yisa wa die ʋ faasɩ keesi a nan ʋ nɩŋŋa a faasɩ nata aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Yisa, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma Bʋa, bɩa fʋ yaala n jigiŋ? N jʋʋsa fʋ da keŋ a datɩ n tɩbɩŋ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Die ʋ balɩ wa naa dama Yisa die yɩ wa jɩmbɩatɩ nʋaŋ dɩ a nyɩŋ ʋ ma. Die saŋŋa pam die a ŋaaŋ mugise wo mɩŋ aŋaŋ ba ŋaana a bɔbɩ wa aŋaŋ chɔrɩma ʋ nagɩsɩ aŋaŋ nuusi mana yɔrɩ, jɩmbɩatɩtɩ ŋaaŋ vaa ʋ kpaa ha mɩŋ ta nagɩ wa juu haagɩŋ ma.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ daa wa dɩ, Fʋ saaŋ? Die ʋ baarɩ, N saaŋ yine Dala ka chɩaŋ yine jɩmbɩatɩ pam die bie ʋ ma.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Womi jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ jʋʋsa wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ʋ da vaa a ga juu vɔrɩgoli me.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Die perukusi pam die benne kunkogiŋ lʋgɩŋ a die; ta jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ vaa a ga juu perukusisi, die ʋ yɩ he sieŋ.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Die wɩa jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ nyɩŋ daa wa ma a ga juu perukusisi mana, die a mana die dɩ chɩgɩ daagɩ kunkogiri lʋgɩŋ a sʋʋŋ ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma nyaabʋ die dɩ dii he.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ daansɩnana perukusisi die dɩ yene wudieke dɩ yine wo die ba chɩgɩ ga tɩka sʋŋ aŋaŋ kʋatɩ ma a mʋʋlɩ wɩarɩ.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Die vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga dɩ ba ye wudieke dɩ yine wo. Die ba keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a ye daa wa ʋ vʋarɩna jɩmbɩatɩ ʋ ma wa ʋ jigiŋ ʋ yeegi jayeekiŋ ta ʋ sikpeŋ dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tʋma, ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi ba mana.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yene Yisa dɩ gbaanna daa wa die wo die ba balɩ vuosisi.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Die wɩa vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Gerasa tɩŋ ma die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ ba tɩka ma, dama ŋmaamɩŋ die faasɩna a yigi be mɩŋ. Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ juu haarɩkʋ dɩ ʋ ga;.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: mi daa dieke die ʋ gbaanna wa die dɩ jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ vaa ʋ dɩa ʋ kʋaŋ, ama Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Yiŋŋi kuli fʋ tigiŋ a ga mʋʋlɩ tʋnkpɩɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine a yɩ fʋ. Die wɩa daa wa die dɩ ga tɩka sʋŋ mana a balla Yisa die dɩ yine wudieke mana a yɩ wa.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Die Yisa dɩ wana a yiŋŋi a ga gbagɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ, kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ tuoli wo aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ a yi wo aŋsɩa dama die ba mana die chɩɩsa wa mɩŋ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Womi daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Jaarusi dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, die ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku jakʋʋŋ. Die ʋ keŋ gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ keŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ tigiŋ.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: dama die ʋ bʋabalɩmɩŋ die dɩ yine havʋʋbiŋ ta yi bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale die yʋagɩna a gbigi kuŋ. Die Yisa die dɩ ganana wa, kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ giliŋ wo jigiŋ mana.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ nyɩna zɩŋ dene bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale, die ʋ nagɩ ʋ yalla jadieke mana mana a yɩ gbɩgbaantɩŋ, ama die ba wʋnyɩ mana ka bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ wa.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Die ʋ daagɩ kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ a keŋ Yisa kʋaŋ a gbɩ ʋ jayeekiri kʋanʋaŋ, lele womi die ʋ zɩmbʋ dɩ gobi bʋnyɩ.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Mi die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Mɩnɩa gbɩna mɩŋ? Die ba mana die dɩ chiisi, ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, kpɩkpaakʋ giliŋ fʋ mɩŋ ta dɩ dɩa chʋa fʋ.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vuoŋ gbɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dama n sɩba dɩ hagɩrɩŋ nyɩŋ n ma.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Die hɔgʋ wa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a lɔbɩrɩ wa, die wɩa die ʋ keŋ a cheele ta gbigiri Yisa nɩŋŋa. Mi die ʋ balɩ Yisa vuoŋ mana nine me wudieke wɩa ʋ gbɩna wa wa ta ye gbaamɩŋ die bʋnyɩ.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, N lɩa, fʋ yada gbaanna fʋ. Gamma aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yene ko bala wɩaha gie, vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ nyɩŋ jakʋʋrɩ tigiŋ a keŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ lɩa wa kpiye; da mugisi Dɩdagɩrʋ wa bɩbra.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ wʋŋ ta balɩ yɩ Jaarusi dɩ, Da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ; fʋnɩŋ ko yi yada ta havʋʋbike nan ye alaafɩa.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Yisa die dɩ keŋ a juu tigiri me, die ʋ ka yi vuoŋ dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ sie Piita, aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ havʋʋbike chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Vuoŋ mana mi die dɩ kʋma ta die zɔɔlɩŋ bʋa wa wɩa. Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da kʋmma, bʋa wa ka kpiye ʋ ko gʋʋra mɩŋ.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Die vuosisi die dɩ laa wa dama die ba sɩba a baarɩ dɩ bʋa wa kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ yigi ʋ nuuŋ me ta baarɩ dɩ, N, bʋa hagɩ.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ ʋ mɩsɩ ma ta hagɩ zie bʋnyɩ. Die dɩ yi bʋa wa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo mamachi, Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ ba yɩ bʋa wa jaaŋ aŋ ʋ dii. Ama Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ wudieke dɩ yine wo.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ dagɩna vuosisi a kpatɩ wa die ʋ nyɩŋ a ga Kapenɔmi tɩŋ ma.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Mi Romanisi sojasi jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie mi a yallɩ ʋ tʋntʋntʋ ʋ chone pam, die yʋagɩŋ dɩ yigi wo ʋ gbigi kuŋ.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ wʋŋ Yisa wɩa, a tʋŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra bataŋ dɩ ba ga a wa wa dɩ ʋ keŋ a gbaaŋ ʋ tʋntʋntʋ wa.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Die ba keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna a baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie mʋ dɩ fʋ suŋŋi wo.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: dama ʋ cho tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi, ta mɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ a yɩ tɩ.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ dɩ ba kʋaŋ a ga. Die ʋ gana a gbige tigiri, jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ zʋalɩŋ dɩ ba keŋ balɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ daa mugisi fʋ gbaŋ a kiere, dama n ka mʋ dɩ fʋ keŋ juu n tigiŋ me.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Die wɩa maŋ ka keŋ fʋ jigiŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ wa. Ama ko yɩ nʋaŋ aŋ n tʋntʋntʋ wa ye alaafɩa.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Dama manɩŋ n gbaŋ n yaa wa n nyɩŋkʋra, ta yallɩ sojasi ba dɩɩ n kʋaŋ. N ŋaaŋ balɩ a yɩ geŋ wo, gamma aŋ ʋ ga, ta balɩ a yɩ geŋ wo kiere aŋ ʋ keŋ; ta bɩ vaa n yɔmʋ yi wudieke n yaalala.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋnna naa die dɩ paalɩ a yi wo mamachi, die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ dɩna ʋ kʋaka dɩ, N bala nɩ, n ye ka ye vuoŋ dɩ yine mɩŋ yada sɩba naa Izara vuosi sʋŋ gbaŋ.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Die sojasi jakʋʋrɩ zʋalɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga ʋ tigiri me a ye ʋ tʋntʋntʋ wa dɩ yene alaafɩa.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Die tuŋ dɩ vʋʋna Yisa die dɩ ga tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Neeni, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Die ʋ dene a ga tʋgɩ tɩka nʋaŋ, die ʋ ye kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ nyɩna tɩka sʋŋ a chii kuŋ dɩ ba ga guu. Vuodieke die dɩ kpine wo die yiwo kpihɔgʋ wʋnyɩ bʋabalɩmɩŋ, die ʋ yiwo dembiŋ. Kpihɔgʋ wa gbaŋ die bie kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yene kpihɔgʋ wa zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Da kʋmma.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Ta die dɩ a ga a gbɩ tɩaka, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ chine wo wo dɩ zie. Yisa die dɩ balɩ dɩ, Dalʋakɩŋ hagɩ.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Die kumbu miivoli die dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ, ta ʋ hagɩ kalɩ a piili bala wɩa. Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a nagɩ wa yɩ ʋ nuŋ.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne mi, ba mana die dɩ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ, a baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋakpeŋkpɩɩŋ keŋ nyɩŋ tɩ jigiŋ mɩŋ; ta bala taŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ keŋ sʋʋŋ a suŋŋi ʋ vuosi mɩŋ.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Yisa wɩaha gie die dɩ mʋʋlɩ ga Judia tɩŋ mana aŋaŋ tɩgɩ diekemba mana dɩ gbigine mi.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Jɔɔn dieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga a balɩ wa wɩaha gie mana. Die ʋ wa vuosi bale ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋŋ.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: a tʋŋ ba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo yaa tɩ ye chɩɩsɩma vuogaaŋ bɩbra.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Die ba kenne tʋgɩ Yisa jigiri, die ba balɩ wa dɩ, Jɔɔn dieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ tʋnna tɩ dɩ tɩ keŋ pɩasɩ fʋ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine vuodieke ʋ bala dɩ ʋ nan keŋ wo yaa tɩ ye chɩɩsɩma vuogaaŋ. .

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Kanɩŋ saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ Yisa die dɩ gbaaŋ vuosi pam, yʋagɩtieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba yʋagɩtɩ die dɩ faasɩna a hagɩrɩ, ta vʋarɩ jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi pam ma, ta die vaa yɩɩsɩ pam nine dɩ yuori.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ Jɔɔn tʋntʋntɩba dɩ, Nɩ yiŋŋi a ga balɩ Jɔɔn wudieke nɩ wʋnnana ta yese wudiekemba; dɩ nɩ ye ta yɩɩsɩ nine dɩ yuorine, gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna gamɩŋ ye wo gbaamɩŋ, tɩkpaara tieŋ dɩ wʋmma, ta kunti dɩ haga kuŋ me, ta zɔɔlɩntieliŋ dɩ wʋmma Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩnaha.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Ta sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodieke dɩ kana ka chɩɩlɩ mɩŋ ma.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Jɔɔn tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yiŋŋine a ga a kpatɩ wa, Yisa die dɩ piili a pɩasa kpɩkpaakʋ yaa gamma Jɔɔn wɩa dɩ, Die nɩ gana Jɔɔn jigiri hagɩrɩ ma wa, bɩa die nɩ yile dɩ nɩ nan ye? Die nɩ yile dɩ nɩ nan ye hʋʋtɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Yaa bɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye? Dembiŋ dɩ yeegine nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna? Vuodiekemba dɩ yeeginene nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna ta bie ba dʋŋŋʋ bie wo naaleesi me.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ, vuobɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye wo? Nɩ tɩŋ yile dɩ nɩ nan ye Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa yaa? Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, Jɔɔn nɩŋ tɩaŋ naazʋa.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Dama Jɔɔn die yine vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bala ʋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Manɩŋ n nan tʋŋ n tʋntʋntʋ aŋ ʋ woliŋ dɩa fʋ nɩŋŋa a wʋnsɩ fʋ keniŋ sieku a dʋaŋ fʋ. .

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba a gʋtɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n bala nɩ, ba ye ka mɩɩrɩ vuoŋ dɩ tɩanna Jɔɔn, ama vuodieke dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma tɩaŋ wa.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Ta Vuoŋ mana aŋaŋ lampotuosiribe die dɩ saagɩ dɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ vɩɩna, dama die ba wʋŋ Jɔɔn mʋʋlɩŋ, ta vaa ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Ama Farasisi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha die dɩ zeti Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sanna die a yɩ ba, dama die ba ka saagɩ dɩ Jɔɔn sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ leleke gie vuosi, bɩa maŋ nan nagɩ a magɩsɩ nɩ? Vuoŋ bɩa yine nɩ?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Nɩ nɩasɩ wa ballɩbɩsɩ dɩ kalla nyʋʋŋ jigiŋ a nata wasa tamba, Tɩ wɩa wɩɩŋ a yɩa nɩ ama nɩ ka sie, ta chɩa hanye a yɩa nɩ ama nɩ ka kʋma.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Nɩnɩŋ leleke gie vuosi nɩasɩ wa ballɩbɩsɩ gie dama Jɔɔn die dɩ kenne ta ka die nyindiike gbaŋ gbaŋ ta ka nyuo daaŋ ta nɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo yɩɩŋyaaŋ;.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: ta manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ keŋ die ta nyuo, nɩ baarɩ Ye, ʋ yiwo wutulitieŋ aŋaŋ daanyuru; ta bɩ yi lampotuosiriŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ zʋa.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Ama vuodiekemba dɩ tuone Ŋmɩŋ yɩaŋ daga dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Die Farasisi wʋnyɩ die dɩ wa Yisa dɩ ʋ keŋ aŋ ba dii nyindiike ʋ tigiŋ me. Die Yisa die dɩ ga daa wa tigiri me, ba mana dɩ lagɩŋ kalɩ a die.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Die hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne tɩka ma a dʋaga dembisi die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ Yisa bie daa wa tigiri me a die nyindiike, die wɩa ʋ yaa kɔlɩba aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ligire dɩ faasɩna tʋa.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: a die a keŋ a gbigiri Yisa nagɩsɩ chɩaŋ, a kʋma ʋ nintaantɩ dɩ nanna Yisa naatala ma. Die Yisa naatala die dɩ sʋnsɩ ta die hɔgʋ wa die dɩ nagɩ ʋ sikpeŋ zoosi a chɩtɩ ha ta die a mɔgɩsɩ ʋ naatala ta sɩɩtɩ tulaaribu a yi he.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Die Farasisi dieke die dɩ wana Yisa wa die dɩ yene naa ʋ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ, Dɩɩ yi daa wa gie dɩ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wusie, ʋ tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩba hɔgʋ wa gie dɩ yine vuodieke. Ʋ tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ Farasisi wa dɩ, Simoni n yaa wɩɩŋ n baa n balɩ fʋ. Ta Simoni die dɩ yiŋŋi baarɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ balɩ mɩŋ.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Die dembisi bale die yalla vuodieke dɩ paŋŋɩnana ligire hamɩŋ. Wʋnyɩ die dii wo ligire bie kɔbɩsɩ-nʋ hamɩŋ, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ dii ligire bie baŋɩsɩ-nʋ hamɩŋ.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Die ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka bɩagɩ tuŋ wo, die wɩa die ʋ nagɩ chaa ba mana dɩ ba gamma. Lele, ba jabɩa balla ʋ chome wo pam.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Simoni die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, N yi tama vuodieke hannɩ die dɩ faasɩna a dala wa. Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ balɩ wa wusie.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a daansɩ hɔgʋ wa ta balɩ yɩ Simoni dɩ, Ye hɔgʋ wa gie? N kenne juu fʋ tigiri gie me, ka yɩ mɩŋ nyaaŋ dɩ n nɩtɩ n naatala ama hɔgʋ wa gie nɩtɩ wa n naatala aŋaŋ ʋ nintaantɩ ta chɩtɩha aŋaŋ ʋ sikpeŋ zoosi.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Ama fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ ka mɔgɩsɩ mɩŋ, ama wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ ʋ ka vaa n naatala mɔgɩsɩŋ n kenne a juu giena wa.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Ta ka bɩ nagɩ kpaaŋ a duuri n sikpeŋ, ama wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ ʋ siti wo tulaari a yi n naatala ma.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: N bala nɩ, n nagɩ wa ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ pam wa mana a chaa wa; die wɩa ʋ cho mɩŋ pam wa, ama vuodieke n nagɩna a chaa wa bɩta, wʋnɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ chome mɩŋ bɩta.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, N nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa fʋ.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Ta vuodiekemba die dɩ kala a gʋtɩ ba die wo die dɩ yile ba sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ, Mɩnɩa wʋnna a bɩagɩ naga vuosi taalɩ a chaa ba.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, Fʋ yada gbatɩna fʋ a taaŋ, gamma aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Yisa die dɩ ga a zie Genezariti gbagɩkpɩɩkʋ kʋanʋaŋ a bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ta daadaŋ pam dɩ taaŋ giliŋ wo a wʋmma wɩaha.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Die ʋ ye haarɩsɩ ale die dɩ zene gbagɩrɩ kʋanʋaŋ, zaasɩyigiribe die vanaha ta die a ga sugire ba nɩɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Yisa die dɩ juu haarɩsɩ kaanɩ, die dɩ yine Simoni jaaŋ ta balɩ wa dɩ ʋ kpaŋŋɩ ka a ga nyaabʋ sʋŋ bɩta. Yisa die dɩ wa a kalɩ haarɩkʋ sʋŋ a daga kpɩkpaakʋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Die ʋ dagɩna kpatɩ wa, die ʋ balɩ Simoni dɩ, Kpaŋŋɩ haarɩkʋ ka ga jigidieke dɩ goline ta nɩ vigi nɩ nɩɩtɩtɩ nyaabʋ sʋŋ a yigi zaasɩŋ.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Simoni die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, tɩ tʋŋ wa yuku mana ama tɩ ka yigi jaaŋ. Tɔ, fʋ bala naa wa wɩa, n nan vigi nɩɩtɩtɩ.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Die Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ vigi ba nɩɩtɩ a yigi zaasɩŋ pam, ta ba nɩɩtɩ die dɩ yaala a kpaa kpaa.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma, die ba wa ba chanchaalɩba die dɩ benne haarɩ kanɩŋ wa ma wa dɩ ba keŋ a suŋŋi be. Die ba keŋ vʋarɩ zaasɩbʋ a suuli ba haarɩsɩ ale wo mana, ta haarɩsɩsɩ dɩ wa dunsi a yaala a sʋʋŋ nyaabʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Die Simoni Piita die dɩ yene wudieke die dɩ yine wo ʋ gbigiri Yisa nɩŋŋa a baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩtieŋ. N ka vɩɩna dɩ n gbigi fʋ. Va mɩŋ ta ga.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: dama die ba yigine zaasɩŋ pam wa die yine Simoni Piita aŋaŋ ʋ zʋalɩba mana mamachi.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba, Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn vuodiekemba dɩ yine Zebedi ballɩ. Ta Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ Simoni dɩ, Da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma nan ga a sʋgɩma vuosi a kiere n jigiŋ.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Die wɩa die ba kpaŋŋɩ ba haarɩsɩsɩ a jʋalɩ gaaŋ a va jaaŋ mana ta dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ Yisa die bie tɩŋ kaanɩ ma ta daa wʋnyɩ dɩ bie mɩŋ a yallɩ gamɩŋ. Die ʋ yene Yisa wa die ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ʋ nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ dʋŋŋʋ fʋ gbaaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Yisa die dɩ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ daa wa ta balɩ dɩ, N saagɩya, fʋ gamɩŋ kpatɩya mɩŋ. Lele womi daa wa ganɩ dɩ kpatɩ bʋnyɩ.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ wa dɩ, Da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ wɩɩrɩ gie, ama ga a ga nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ wa ta kaabɩ kaabɩ dieke Mosisi die dɩ dagɩna wa yaa gamma gamɩŋ gbaanɩŋ wɩa, aŋ vuoŋ mana sɩmma sɩba dɩ lele fʋ gaŋkʋ kpatɩya mɩŋ.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Ama die ba faasɩ mʋʋlɩ Yisa saaŋ jigiŋ mana, ta kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ba wʋŋ wudieke ʋ balala, ta ye gbaamɩŋ a nyɩŋ ba yʋagɩtɩ ma.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Ama die ʋ ŋaaŋ ga wa haagɩŋ ma a ga jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ die Yisa dɩ daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ta Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ kalɩ mi, die ba nyɩŋ wa Galili tɩgɩsɩ mana aŋaŋ Judia tɩgɩsɩ mana aŋaŋ Jerusalemi a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Die Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ die dɩ bie ʋ ma dɩ ʋ gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Die dembisi bataŋ die dɩ chii daa wʋnyɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine kalɩŋŋa ma a mɩa dɩ ba nagɩ wa juu tigiri me a nagɩ wa dʋaŋ Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Ama die vuosi die dɩ dala wa die ba ka bɩagɩ nagɩ wa juu, die wɩa die ba chii wo a jʋalɩ mampili sikpeŋ a chɩa a yuori vɔrɩŋ a nagɩ wa aŋaŋ kalɩŋka a sʋʋŋ dʋaŋ vuosi sʋnsʋŋ Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene ba yine wo yada dene wo, ʋ balɩ yɩ daa wa dɩ, N zʋa n nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa fʋ.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Ta mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi die dɩ piili a yile ba sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine daa wa gie a bala sɩba ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ? Vuobɩa baaŋ nan nagɩ vuoŋ taalɩ a chaa wa, ntaala Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Die Yisa dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yile wɩaha gie nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Genhe jabɩa yine mɔlɩ, fʋ baaŋ balɩ dɩ, N nagɩ fʋ taalɩ a chaa yaa Hagɩ a chʋŋ?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa mama a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba. Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ nyɩŋgbaŋkpiikiri tieŋ wo N bala fʋ, hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a ga tigiŋ.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Lele womi daa wa die dɩ hagɩ ba mana nɩŋŋa a nagɩ ʋ kalɩŋŋa a kuli ta bɩra Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Die dɩ yi vuoŋ mana mamachi pam ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be, die ba pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ a bala dɩ, Tɩ ye mamachi wɩa jinne.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ye lampotuosiru ba wasɩnana Liiva ta ʋ kalɩ jigidieke ba tuosinene lampoke juoŋ me. Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Die Liiva die dɩ hagɩ a vaa jaaŋ mana ta dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Liiva die dɩ paalɩ a yi chaantɩ ʋ tigiŋ me a yɩ Yisa; ta die lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ die dɩ lagɩsɩ die aŋaŋ ba.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Die Farasisi bataŋ aŋaŋ ba mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩ bataŋ die dɩ yine Farasisi die dɩ keŋ pɩasa Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ die ta nyuo aŋaŋ lampotuosiriŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Manɩŋ n ka keŋ dɩ n balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yine vuovɩɩna dɩ ba vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ama n keŋ dɩ n balɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ dɩ ba vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ŋaaŋ bɔba nʋaŋ ta jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana, ta Farasisi mba kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yie die gbaŋ gbaŋ ama fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ŋaaŋ die mɩŋ ta nyuo saŋŋa mana.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ tɩŋ yaala chaaŋ diekemba dɩ gana hɔgʋ faarɩŋ ma bɔba nʋaŋ, banɩŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a beri wo? Die kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama saŋŋa nan keŋ ba nan nagɩ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ, saŋka mi ba nan wa bɔbɩ nʋaŋ.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Vuoŋ kaaŋ saagɩ a chɩɩrɩ garɩhaalɩŋ ta nagɩ ka garɩchɔɔtɩŋ a baalɩ a ligi garɩkʋʋŋ vɔrɩŋ, dama ʋ yi die ʋ nan chʋʋsɩ garɩhaalɩkʋ ta garɩhaalɩkʋ aŋaŋ garɩkʋʋrɩ kaaŋ dɩ mʋ taŋ.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Yaa nɩ yeye ta vuoŋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ asʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋkʋra ma? Dama ʋ yi die daabʋ nan kʋŋ a chɩɩrɩ kolititi aŋ daabʋ aŋaŋ kolititi mana taaŋ yɔrɩ.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Ama sie ʋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ asʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋhaala ma.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ nyuu daakʋʋŋ, ʋ keŋ ye daahaalɩŋ ʋ ka bɩ yaala, ʋ ŋaaŋ baarɩ dɩ Daakʋʋrɩ nansɩna. .

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa die dɩ vʋarɩ dembisi baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ bale ta tʋŋ ba ba balele dɩ ba woliŋ ga tɩgɩdieke aŋaŋ jigidieke ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yinene siri dɩ ʋ ga.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Zaaha biye mɩŋ ta dala ama zaagobiribe ka dala. Die wɩa nɩ jʋʋsɩ zaaha tieŋ aŋ ʋ gʋtɩ tʋntʋntɩŋ aŋ ba keŋ gobihe.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Nɩ gamma! N tʋma nɩ, nɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba yiibisi dɩ benne gbaanchʋra sʋŋ.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Ama nɩ da keŋ a nagɩ ligire yaa bʋlɔgɩŋ yaa nɩɩra, ta nɩ da bɩ chʋʋsɩma saŋŋa a waasɩma vuosi sieŋ me.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Ama tigidieke nɩŋ mana nɩ keŋ a juu nɩ woliŋ a baarɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme tigiri gie vuosi me.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta tigiŋ tieŋ wo dɩ yaala sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ, ʋ nan mɩŋŋɩ tuo nɩ. Ama ʋ ka yi die nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩkʋ nan yiŋŋi keŋ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ juu tigiri me, nɩ bemme mi ta dime ta nyume jadieke mana ba nagɩna yɩ nɩ. Dama tʋntʋntʋ mana mʋ ʋ tuo ʋ tune. Nɩ da tarɩgɩma nɩ tigiberisikiŋ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ga juu tɩŋ diekemba mana ba mɩŋŋɩ tuo nɩ, nɩ dii jadieke mana ba keŋ nagɩ yɩ nɩ.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: ta gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ mana dɩ benne mi, ta balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keŋ a gbigi nɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ juu tɩŋ dieke ba ka mɩŋŋɩ tuo nɩ, nɩ nyɩŋ a ga ba siekpɩɩma ma a balɩ dɩ.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Tɩ nagɩsɩ dɩ yigine tankɔlɩ dieke nɩ tɩka gie me tɩ pɩsɩha a taaŋ ta taana nɩ. Ama nɩ sɩmma wɩɩrɩ gie, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɔŋ keŋ a gbigi nɩ mɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: N bala nɩ dɩ sarɩya diile daaŋ, tɩka mi vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan tɩaŋ Sodomi tɩŋ vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩnɩŋ Korazini vuosi ma; wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩnɩŋ Befisada vuosi mama; dama mamachi wudieke n yine nɩ tɩgɩsɩ ma wa, maŋ tɩŋ a yi die Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni ma ma, vuodiekemba dɩ benne mi wo tɩŋ nan vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ a yʋasɩ ta nagɩ vaatɩ aŋaŋ tanyeeliŋ a bʋʋlɩ ba gbaŋ aŋ die dagɩ sɩba ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma ma mɩŋ.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya, nɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala pam a tɩaŋ Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni vuosi sarɩya.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Ta nɩnɩŋ Kapenɔmi vuosi, Ŋmɩŋ nan kɔtɩ nɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ? Aayɩ, ʋ nan vigi nɩ a taaŋ kunti jigiberisikiŋ.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Saŋka mi die ʋ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana nɩ balɩkʋ, ʋ wʋŋ wa manɩŋ; ama vuodieke dɩ keŋ zeti nɩ, zeti wo manɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ zetine manɩŋ zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Die dembisi baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ bale wo die dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ Yisa jigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ saaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ma jɩmbɩatɩ saagɩ wa tɩ nʋa.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Ta ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, N ye wo Sitaani dɩ nyɩna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ nan sɩba nɩɩnyɩgɩsɩŋ.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Nɩ wʋmma, n yi nɩ wa yiko dɩ nɩ chʋmma nyinvuuke aŋaŋ chɩakʋrɩtɩ ma ta bɩ nyaŋŋɩ nɩ dataaŋ Sitaani hagɩrɩŋ mana; ta wɩɩŋ kaaŋ yi nɩ.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Nɩ da yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ jɩmbɩatɩ saagɩ nɩ nʋa nyɩɩna ma, ama nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dama ba maagɩ nɩ saara arɩzanna ma.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Kanɩŋ saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ yɩ Yisa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ʋ baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie tieŋ, n bɩra fʋ, dama fʋnɩŋ lɔbɩrɩ wɩaha gie yɩantieliŋ aŋaŋ sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ jigiŋ ama ta yuori he a yɩ ballɩbɩsɩ; dama die fʋ tʋma fʋ tʋʋma a yie fʋ nansɩŋ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa jaaŋ mana a yɩ mɩŋ; vuoŋ ka sɩba manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa sie n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ, vuoŋ dɩaŋ ka sɩba n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ sie manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba n yaala n nagɩ wa a dagɩ.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Womi Yisa die yiŋŋi a tuoli ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta balɩ yɩ ba nyɩɩna ma dɩ, Nɩ yaa sikpeŋ nansɩŋ dama nɩ nine ye wudieke nɩ yesinene wo!.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: N bala nɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ pam aŋaŋ naalɩŋ pam die yaala ba ye wudieke nɩ yesinene wo, ama die ba ka ye ke, ta die bɩ yaala ba wʋŋ wudieke nɩ wʋnnana wa, ama die ba ka wʋŋ ka.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Die daaŋ kaanɩ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ magɩsɩ Yisa ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, lalɩa maŋ baa n yi a ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa dɩ maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma? Lalɩa fʋ sɩba a chɩaŋ.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Daa wa dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Yaalɩma fʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ haalɩŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ hagɩrɩŋ mana aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋanyile mana, ta bɩ chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ gbaŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Mɩŋŋɩ balɩ mɩŋ, yime naa ta nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Ama die mɩraha dɩdagɩrʋ wa dɩ bɩ yaala ʋ dagɩ dɩ ʋ yaa wusie, die wɩa die ʋ bɩ pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine n chanchaaŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die nyɩnna Jerusalemi a sʋʋŋ gara Jeriko; die gbɩgbatɩrɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo a wuuri ʋ nyinti ta nɩgɩ wa ʋ ga gbigi kuŋ ba va wa taaŋ ta chʋŋ.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ sʋʋŋ gara sieku me ta keŋ ye daa wa dɩ dʋana sieku me, die ʋ gʋa daagɩ sieku lʋgɩŋ a chʋŋ ga.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ Liiva vuoŋ die dɩ sʋʋŋ gara mi a ye daa wa dɩ dʋana ʋ gʋa daagɩ sieku lʋgɩŋ a chʋŋ ga.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Ama ta Samaria vuoŋ die dɩ chʋŋ sieku me a keŋ ye wo, zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Die ʋ ga ʋ jigiŋ a siti tɩɩŋ a yi ʋ kʋjagaha ta bɔbɩha aŋaŋ garɩchɔɔtɩ. Ta die a nagɩ wa a kalɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ bʋnɩŋ ma a yaa wa a ga chaanɩŋ juone me a daansɩ wa.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ vʋnna die ʋ nagɩ ligire a yɩ vuodieke die dɩ jɩɩnana jigiri ta balɩ wa dɩ, Daansɩma wa, ta maŋ keŋ yiŋŋi kieŋ n nan tuŋ fʋ ligire dieke fʋ nagɩna a gʋtɩ daansɩ wa. .

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Yisa die dɩ balla naa a kpatɩ wa, ʋ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dembisi bataa wa gie ba jabɩa fʋ yile dɩ ʋ dagɩ sɩba vuodieke gbɩgbatɩrɩba dɩ gbatɩna ʋ nyintiti yiwo ʋ chanchaaŋ.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ chɩgɩna wa zɔɔlɩbʋ. Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Die nɩŋ gamma a ga yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gara ba sieŋ a ga tʋgɩ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ kaanɩ, ta die hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Maata die tuo Yisa aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ʋ tigiŋ me.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Die ʋ yallɩ wa ʋ taa ba wasa wa Meri; die Meri die dɩ sʋʋŋ kalɩ Yisa nɩŋŋa a wʋmma wudieke ʋ balala wa.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Ama Maata die dɩ mɩa aŋaŋ chaantɩ, ta die a keŋ balɩ Yisa dɩ N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n mɩa mɩŋ aŋaŋ chaantɩ n nyɩɩna ma, ta ntaa wa dɩ ka suŋŋe mɩŋ, fʋ baga wo ka ma? Balɩ wa dɩ ʋ keŋ suŋŋi mɩŋ.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Maata n sɩba a baarɩ mɩa mɩŋ aŋaŋ nyinti pam, a mugise fʋ ta chʋʋsa fʋ sʋŋ.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: ama wʋbalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna mʋna fʋ yaalɩ; Meri nɩŋ vʋarɩ wa wudieke dɩ vɩɩna, ta vuoŋ kaaŋ tuo ke a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ ta yɩ ba hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ yiko dɩ ba vʋarɩma jɩmbɩatɩ ta gbaama yʋagɩtɩ vuosi me.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Ta die tʋŋ ba dɩ ba ga mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩaha ta gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Ta die balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da nagɩ jaaŋ mana a ga nɩ sieku; nɩ da nagɩ daaŋgbanɩŋ yaa bʋlɔgɩŋ yaa nyindiike yaa ligire aŋaŋ jayeekiŋ a gʋtɩ nɩ wone yeegi jadieke wo me.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Nɩ keŋ a juu tigidieke mana nɩ bemme mi a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ bala nɩ nyɩŋ tɩka ma.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Jigidieke mana ba kana ka tuo nɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, saŋŋa dieke nɩ keŋ nyɩnna tɩka ma nɩ pɩsɩ nɩ nagɩsɩ tankɔlɩŋ a taaŋ mi aŋ ka dagɩ sɩba ba yi chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Die wɩa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩŋ a dɩ dɩa tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ mana a mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha ta gbaama vuosi jige mana.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Die Herodi vuodieke die dɩ yine Galili tɩŋgbaŋka mana naajakʋʋŋ die dɩ wʋŋ wudiekemba mana die dɩ yinene, die a paalɩ mugisi wo dama vuosi bataŋ die baarɩ dɩ Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ hagɩna kuŋ me.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ Elaja yiŋŋine a keŋ, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa wʋnyɩ yiŋŋine keŋ ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Ta Herodi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Die n yi ba gobi wo Jɔɔn sikpeŋ ama mɩnɩa yine daa wa gie maŋ wʋmma ʋ wɩa dɩ ʋ yie wɩaha gie? Die ʋ mɩa dɩ ʋ ye Yisa.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Die Yisa tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ a balɩ wa wudiekemba mana die ba yine. Die ʋ nagɩ ba, ba ga tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Befisada ba nyɩɩna ma.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Ama kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ wʋŋ ta tʋʋ ʋ poli. Die ʋ yene be ba kienene die ʋ tuo be aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ta dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yaa gamma ʋ naarɩ ma ta gbaaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ yaalala gbaanɩŋ.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Saŋŋa dieke ŋmɩnnɩ die dɩ fɩalɩnana wa, ʋ tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Vaa vuosisi ga tɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ diekemba dɩ gbine amʋ ba nan yallɩ nyindiike aŋaŋ jigidʋagɩsɩkɩŋ, dama giena yiwo haagɩyeŋ.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yɩ ba jaaŋ aŋ ba dii. Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ yaa wa paanʋ golime anʋ aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩ ale nyɩɩna ma. Fʋnɩŋ yaala tɩ ga da wa nyindiike a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ gie mana.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ vaa vuosisi puo puo a kala baŋɩsɩ-nʋ nʋ.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yi die ta vaa ba sʋʋŋ kalɩ.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Ta ʋ nagɩ paanʋsɩ golime anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta yi he gbieri gbieri a nagɩ ha yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba nagɩ tɩa tɩa vuosisi.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Die ba mana dɩ dii a chagɩ ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ nyɩŋ tɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ baŋ ale.
