eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ Yisa die dɩ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ʋ nyɩɩna ma, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ baa n yiye.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Bataŋ baarɩ fʋ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ fʋ yiwo Elaja, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa yiŋŋine a keŋ ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Ta nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ mɩnɩa nɩ baarɩ n yiye? Piita dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ, Masia wa.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Mi Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ wɩarɩ gie.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: ta die bɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Sie manɩŋ vuotaŋ Bʋa wa dii wahala pam, ta tɩka nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ tigiri mi mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ keŋ zeti mɩŋ, a bɩ kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Die ʋ balɩ yɩ ba mana mana dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ yaala ʋ dɩa n kʋaŋ sie ʋ daaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wɩa, ta viigi ʋ dagarɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana a dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yaala ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ mɩsɩ nan waarɩ ka, ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ waarɩna ʋ mɩsɩ n wɩa nan ye ke.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Bɩa vuoŋ dɩ baa ʋ ye ʋ a keŋ a ye dʋnɩa mana nyinti ama ta waarɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ mɩsɩ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Vuodieke dɩ a keŋ chɩga viivi a ka balɩ manɩŋ aŋaŋ n dagɩkʋ gie vuosi jigiŋ, die nɩŋ manɩŋ vuotaŋ Bʋa maŋ keŋ keŋ aŋaŋ n hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ malakasisi hagɩrɩŋ n nan daaŋ chɩgɩ dɩ tieŋ gbaŋ viivi dɩ n yaa wa a gʋtɩ n vuosi ma.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuosi bataŋ bie giena ba kaaŋ kpi ntaala ba keŋ ye Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keniŋ.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Die ʋ balla wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, a kʋaŋ chaaŋ die dɩ yi daraa anɩɩ, die ʋ nagɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Jemisi a gʋtɩ ʋ ma a ga jʋalɩ taŋ sikpeŋ dɩ ba jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Die ʋ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩnnɩ ʋ nine chaaŋ die dɩ tarɩgɩ ta ʋ nyiŋyeeke die dɩ faasɩ yɩantɩ ta nyɩgɩsa.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Womi die dembisi bale die dɩ nyɩŋ mi, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ Mosisi aŋaŋ Elaja.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: ta die bala aŋaŋ Yisa yaa gamma ʋ kuŋ wɩa aŋaŋ ʋ bala ʋ kpi die Jerusalemi ma, ta yi Ŋmɩŋ choti.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Ama Piita aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba die faasɩ gʋʋra mɩŋ, ama die ba hagɩna wa die ba ye chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ giline Yisa, ta ba bɩ ye dembisi bale dɩ zene aŋaŋ wa.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Die dembisisi dɩ nyɩnnana Yisa jigiri, Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, dɩ vɩɩna mɩŋ tɩ benne giena. Tɩ nan wʋnsɩ viisi ataa, aŋ kaanɩ yi fʋ jaaŋ, aŋ kaanɩ dɩaŋ yi Mosisi jaaŋ, kaanɩ Elaja dɩaŋ jaaŋ. Die Piita paalɩ ka sɩba wudieke ʋ balala.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Die ʋ yene ko bala wɩaha, nɩɩŋmaaŋ die dɩ keŋ a ligi be, ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be ba benne ka sʋkʋ.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Die lɔlɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ nɩɩŋmaakʋ sʋŋ a baarɩ dɩ, N Bʋadembiŋ wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ maŋ vʋarɩ, nɩ wʋmma wa.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Die lɔlɩkʋ dɩ balɩna a kpatɩ wa, die ba ye Yisa dɩ zene ʋ nyɩɩna ma. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ tarɩ yaa gamma ba yene wudieke wo, ta ka balɩ vuoŋ saŋka mi wudieke ba yene wo.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ tanɩ ma, kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ tuoli wo.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Mi daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie kpɩkpaakʋ ma ta faasɩ wa Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ! N jʋʋsa fʋ, keŋ daansɩ n dalʋabike gie; dama ʋ yiwo n bʋa balɩmɩŋ.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla wa, ka keŋ hagɩ ka ŋaaŋ yi ʋ faasɩ keese ta nagɩ wa a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ sɩba kpenkpennantʋ ta fʋnfʋgɩtɩ dɩ nyɩna ʋ nʋaŋ ma, ka mugisi wa mɩŋ ta ka vasa wa a yʋasa.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Maŋ jʋʋsɩ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba vʋarɩ ka ama ba ka bɩagɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ lele vuosi gie dɩ wone yada ta bɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩa Ŋmɩnnɩ, nɩ yaala n bemme nɩ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaala n yaa wa suguru aŋaŋ nɩ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Ta die balɩ a yɩ daa wa dɩ, Yaa bʋa wa a keŋ giena.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Die bʋa wa dɩ kienene wo jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ nagɩ wa a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta die ʋ cheele. Yisa die dɩ natɩ jɩmbɩakʋ, ta gbaaŋ bʋa wa ta yiŋŋi nagɩ wa yɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Die dɩ yi vuosisi mana mamachi yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dagɩna hagɩrɩŋ dene. Die ʋ yinene wudieke wo dɩ ko yie vuosisi mamachi, saŋka mi die ʋ balɩ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Nɩ yuori nɩ tɩba a wʋŋ vɩɩnɩŋ wudieke n bala n balɩ nɩ naa; ba nan daansɩ nagɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa a nagɩ yi vuota nuusi me.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Ama ta die ba ka sɩba ka wɩɩrɩ chɩaŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ die ligi ba yɩaŋ dɩ ba da sɩmma ka chɩaŋ; ta die ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ bɩ yalla ba dɩ ba pɩasɩ wa ka chɩaŋ.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ yaa gamma ba jabɩa yine vuokpɩɩŋ.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile, die wɩa die ʋ nagɩ bʋabiŋ a vaa ʋ keŋ zie ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: ta balɩ ba dɩ, Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo bʋa wa gie n saaŋ ma, ʋ mɩŋŋɩ tuo mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta fʋ mɩŋŋɩ tuo mɩŋ, mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa. Ama vuodieke dɩ nagɩna ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ bʋabiŋ nɩ mana sʋŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuokpɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ nɩ mana.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, die tɩ ye wo vuoŋ dɩ yagɩnana jɩmbɩatɩ vuoŋ ma fʋ saaŋ ma, die tɩ balɩ wa dɩ ʋ vaa dama die ʋ ka yi tɩ wʋnyɩ.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da kagɩma wa, dama vuodieke mana dɩ kana a waga nɩ, ʋ gʋtɩ wa nɩ ma.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Die saŋka dɩ gbigine dɩ Yisa yiŋŋi a jʋalɩ arɩzanna wa, die ʋ dii sikimiŋ a nyɩŋ a gara Jerusalemi.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Die ʋ woliŋ a tʋŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ dɩ ba ga Samaria vuosi tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ kaanɩ ma a yi jaaŋ mana siri a zieŋ wo.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: ama tɩka vuosi die ka saagɩ ʋ keŋku dama die ba sɩba dɩ ʋ gara Jerusalemi.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ yene naa wa die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ yaala tɩ yi boliŋ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ dii be.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a daansɩ ba ta galɩŋ ba.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: ta die ba ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ gaaŋ bɩbra.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Die ba ganana wa daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, N nan dɩ dɩ fʋ a ga jigidieke mana fʋ ganana.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Gbaanchʋra yaa vɔrɩtɩ a juo, nembisi dɩaŋ dɩ yaa tʋʋsɩ a juo ama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wo jigidʋagɩsɩkɩŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Die ʋ bɩ balɩ yɩ daa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ, Dɩa mɩŋ. Ama daa wa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, vaa n woliŋ, a ga a ga guu n chʋa.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Vaa kunti guu ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba kunti. Fʋnɩŋ gamma a ga mʋʋlɩ yɩ vuosi Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n nan dɩdɩ fʋ ama vaa n woliŋ ga tigiŋ a taaŋ n deŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a pɔgɩlɩ naakʋʋŋ a kʋa ta yiŋŋi daansa ʋ kʋaŋ. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ daansa die, ʋ ka mʋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ tʋʋma tʋmɩŋ.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Saŋka mi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ bie mi a balɩ Yisa yaa gamma Paliti dɩ kʋna Galili vuosi saŋŋa dieke die ba kaabɩna yɩa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yile dɩ Galili vuosisi die ba kʋna wa tʋmbɩatɩ die faasɩ dala mɩŋ a tɩaŋ ba chanchaalɩba mi?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayɩ; ama n bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ ka chɩgɩ nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma, nɩ nan kpi sɩba ba kpine die wo.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Siloami jarɩnbɩsa die dɩ nanna a kʋʋ vuosi baŋ aŋaŋ anɩɩ wo, nɩ yile dɩ die vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ die tɩaŋ ba chanchaalɩba die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma wa?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayɩ, ama n bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ ka chɩgɩ nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma nɩ mana gbaŋ nan kpi.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die sone tɩɩnyɩŋkɩŋ ʋ kʋaŋ ma, ta die ga a yaala nyɩŋnyɩŋka ka sikpeŋ me dɩ ʋ kpaa, ama die ʋ ka yeye.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuodieke dɩ daansɩnana ʋ kʋakʋ dɩ, Ye, bɩna ataa wʋnna maŋ chʋŋ kieŋ gie a yaala nyɩŋnyɩŋka tɩɩka sikpeŋ ta ka yeye. Chɩaka taaŋ, bɩa wɩa ka nan dɩ zie a chʋʋsa tɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ama dɩdaansɩrʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi bala wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, vaa ka bɩmbalɩmɩŋ a gʋtɩ ta n nan tuu a giliŋke a yi hʋʋsɩŋ a yi ka chɩaŋ ma.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: aŋ tɩ ye ka nan nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka bɩŋ chaaŋ? Ka ka nyɩŋya n nan chɩaka taaŋ. .

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ Yisa die dɩ bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me a daga;.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: ta hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie mi jɩmbɩaŋ die dɩ bie ʋ ma a vaa ʋ bɩrɩŋ gbarɩgɩŋ bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ anɩɩ. Die ʋ guriye mɩŋ a ka bɩagɩ hagɩziele.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene wo die ʋ wa wa a keŋ nyɩŋ a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Hɔgʋ, fʋ yʋagɩbʋ vaa fʋ mɩŋ.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Ta die nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a diisi ʋ ma, ta lele womi, die ʋ tɩɩntɩ ta bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku jakʋʋŋ die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ, dama Yisa die dɩ gbaanna hɔgʋ wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ wɩa; die wɩa die ʋ balɩ vuosisi dɩ, Daraa ayʋaba benne bakɔyɩ ma dɩ tɩ yaa a tʋmma, die wɩa nɩ kiere hanɩŋ daraa mi aŋ ʋ gbaaŋ nɩ, ama da keŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ dɩ ʋ gbaaŋ nɩ.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie, nɩ wʋnyɩ ŋaaŋ a forisi wo nɩ niige yaa nɩ bonsi a yaa ga a yɩ ha nyaaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Ta hɔgʋ wa gie dɩ yine Abarahami haagɩŋ vuodieke Sitaani dɩ bɩrɩŋ wa yɔmʋ bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ anɩɩ, dɩ ka mʋ dɩ n gbatɩ wa taaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Yisa die dɩ bala naa wa viivi die dɩ yigi ʋ dataasɩ mana; ta kpɩkpaakʋ sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ aŋaŋ mamachi wudiekemba ʋ yinene wo.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba bɩa? Bɩa maŋ baaŋ nagɩ a magɩsɩ ka?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Ka nɩasɩ wa kaŋmɩŋ biŋ, daa wʋnyɩ dɩ nagɩna a ga bʋrɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ma, die ka bɩrɩŋ a ga bɩrɩŋ tɩɩŋ nembisi die dɩ yʋga a tuusi ka sikpeŋ me.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa maŋ baaŋ nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ a magɩsɩ?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Ka nɩasɩ wa dabɔtɩ hɔgʋ dɩ nagɩna a lagɩŋ paanʋ zɔŋ dieke dɩ dala ma aŋ bʋ mana dɩ yiri wo.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Yisa die dɩ benne sieŋ me a gara Jerusalemi wa, die ʋ daagɩ tɩgɩkpɩɩma aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ a daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa;.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: die vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba ye gbatɩtaaŋkʋ ba nan yi bɩta yaa ba nan dɩ dala? Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Sie nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a dɩa sanʋabike a juu mi. N bala nɩ, vuosi pam nan daansɩ mɩa dɩ ba juu ama ba nan waarɩ ka juule.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Tigiri tieŋ dɩ keŋ hagɩ a ligi sanʋarɩ, ta nɩ wɩarɩ a zie yene me, ta kpaasa sanʋarɩ a wasa a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ yuori sanʋarɩ a yɩ tɩ, ama saŋka mi ʋ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ nɩ dɩ N ka sɩba jigidieke dɩ nyɩnna.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Saŋka mi nɩ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ die dinene ta nyuo, ta fʋ dagɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa tɩ tɩŋ ma.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Ama ʋ nan daansɩ bɩ a baarɩ, N ka sɩba jigidieke nɩ nyɩnna. Ta balɩ nɩ, Nɩ gamma ta va manɩŋ, nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie mana.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Ta n nan nagɩ nɩ a taaŋ yeŋ me; ta nɩ nan ye Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba mana, ta ba bie Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ta nɩ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nɩ nyɩna.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Vuosi nan daansɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩkɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋ juukiŋ aŋaŋ nuudiigiŋ aŋaŋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ a keŋ kalɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma a dii nyindiike.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: N baaŋaa, vuosi bataŋ dieke dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaŋ lele nan keŋ bie nɩŋŋa, ta vuosi bataŋ dieke dɩ benne nɩŋŋa lele nan keŋ wɩarɩ kʋaŋ.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ Farasisi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Sie fʋ nyɩŋ giena a ga jigigaaŋ, dama naaŋ Herodi yaala ʋ kʋʋ fʋ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩndarɩka gie, nɩ ga balɩ wa dɩ, N nan dɩ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ ta gbaama vuosi jinne aŋaŋ soriŋ, ta daraa ataa wa daraaŋ n nan kpatɩ n tʋʋma.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Ama a nyɩŋ jinne aŋaŋ soriŋ aŋaŋ vʋntʋŋ dɩ mʋ dɩ n bemme sieŋ me, dama dɩ ka mʋ dɩ ba kʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa jigigaaŋ sie Jerusalemi ma.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Nchʋanaawee, Jerusalemi vuosi, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba, ta bɩ taa vuodiekemba ʋ tʋnna nɩ jigiŋ wo tana a kʋʋ wa, bʋŋmɩa maŋ mɩa a yaala dɩ n lagɩsɩ nɩ bʋnyɩ sɩba kpɩanɩɩŋ dɩ ŋaana lagɩsɩ ka ballɩ ka chobiti chɩaŋ ta nɩ ka saaga.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Ye, Ŋmɩŋ vaa nɩ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ta ka zie yɔrɩ. N bala nɩ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, nɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ dɩ, Alibarika bie vuodieke dɩ kennene Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma. .

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ kaanɩ Yisa die dɩ ga Farasisi nyɩŋkʋra wʋnyɩ tigiŋ dɩ ʋ dii nyindiike, die vuosisi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a daansa wa.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Mi yʋagɩtieŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ nagɩsɩ aŋaŋ ʋ nuusi mʋrɩna.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ, Tɩ mɩraha saagɩya mɩŋ dɩ tɩ gbaaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ yaa tɩ da gbaaŋ.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ama die ba tarɩ. Yisa die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ daa wa ta gbaaŋ wa ta vaa ʋ ga.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Ta die a balɩ yɩ ba, Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ yallɩ boniŋ yaa naagɩŋ ka nan buluŋ me davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ kaaŋ yi lagɩ lagɩ a ga a vʋarɩ ka.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ama die ba ka bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wa wɩɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Mi Yisa die yene chaamba dɩ vʋarɩnana vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩha, ʋ kpaaŋ ba dɩ;.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba keŋ a wa fʋ dɩ fʋ keŋ banwʋla ma, da keŋ ga kala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka, dama dɩ nan bɩagɩ yi ta ba wa wa vuodieke dɩ tɩanna fʋ.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: aŋ vuodieke dɩ wana nɩ wa nan keŋ fʋ jigiŋ a balɩ fʋ dɩ, Hagɩ ta yɩ wa fʋ jigikasɩkɩrɩ. Mi nan dii viivi ta ga kalɩ vuosi kʋaŋ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Ama ba keŋ a wa fʋ, ga kala vuosi kʋanʋaŋ, amʋ vuodieke dɩ wana fʋ dɩ keŋ keŋ ʋ nan balɩ a yɩ fʋ dɩ, N zʋa, keŋ jʋalɩ kala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩkɩrɩ ma. Mi nan ye jɩlɩma fʋ chanchaalɩba mana jigiŋ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ kɔtɩna ʋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan sʋʋŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋ, ama vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan kɔtɩ wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Mi Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ vuodieke die dɩ wana wa wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ keŋ yaa dʋʋga, da keŋ wa fʋ zʋalɩŋ yaa fʋ nɩmballɩ yaa fʋ maarɩŋ yaa fʋ chanchaalɩŋ ligire tieliŋ, dama fʋ wa ba ba dɩaŋ ba nan yiŋŋi a wa fʋ a yiŋŋi tuŋ fʋ wudieke fʋ yine wo.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Ama fʋ keŋ yaa dʋʋga wa zɔɔlɩntieliŋ aŋaŋ gbarɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ gbɩlɩsɩ aŋaŋ yɩɩsɩ.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: ta nan ye alibarika, dama ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi tuŋ fʋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan tuŋ fʋ saŋŋa dieke vuovɩɩna dɩ keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Die vuodiekemba dɩ dinene aŋaŋ Yisa wʋnyɩ dɩ wʋnnana Yisa balɩkʋ ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba daansɩ kala Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma a dii nyindiike.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die benne a keŋ yaala ʋ dii dʋʋgakpeŋkpɩɩŋ, ta die a posi vuosi pam.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Die dʋʋgaka diile saŋŋa die dɩ gbigine wo, die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ tʋntʋntʋ dɩ ʋ ga balɩ vuodiekemba ba wone a posi wo dɩ, Nɩ keŋ, jaaŋ mana yi siri mɩŋ.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Ama ba mana die dɩ piili a bala wudieke wɩa ba kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a keŋ. Die bʋmbʋŋaŋ vuoke dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N daa wa tɩŋgbaŋ ta dɩ mʋ dɩ n ga ye ke, die wɩa yɩ mɩŋ sieŋ aŋ n ga.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N daa wa naakʋaka baŋ; die wɩa yɩ mɩŋ sieŋ aŋ n yaa ha a ga magɩsɩ ye.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N faarɩ wa hɔgʋ daraa gie nʋaŋ die wɩa n kaaŋ bɩagɩ keŋ.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Die saŋŋa kaanɩ lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ba wʋŋ ʋ wʋbalɩka ha.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Die tʋntʋntʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi ga a balɩ ʋ tieŋ wo wɩaha gie mana. Die ʋ tieŋ wo dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta balɩ yɩ ʋ tʋntʋntʋ wa dɩ, Nyɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ a ga tɩka sieti me a wa zɔɔlɩntieliŋ aŋaŋ gbarɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gbɩlɩsɩ a yaa ba viiri keŋ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Ama Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ piili a vʋʋna wɩa dɩ, Daa wa gie tuose tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ ta die aŋaŋ ba.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ tʋntʋntʋ wa dɩ keŋ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, fʋ bala dɩ n yi wudieke wo, n yiye mɩŋ, ama yɩalɩŋ yene bie juoku sʋŋ.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma Yisa die dɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Die wɩa ʋ tieŋ wo dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Bɩ nyɩŋ ga tɩka lʋga a daansɩ siekpɩɩma aŋaŋ siebisisi me, a yaalɩ vuosi a keŋ juu amʋ n tigiri nan suuli.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ yaa yiise kɔbɩga ta a kaanɩ dɩ bɩa, bɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi? Sie ʋ vaa yiise baŋɩsɩ-wayɩ aŋaŋ awayɩ wa dɩ dinene jigidieke wo, ta ga a yaalɩ jabalɩŋka dɩ bana wa a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan ye ke.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: Ama vuodiekemba die n wolinne a posi wo wʋnyɩ gbaŋ nʋaŋ kaaŋ laŋ n dʋʋgaka nyindiike he. .

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ ye ke ʋ sʋŋ ŋaaŋ fɩalɩ mɩŋ, ta nagɩ ka viigi.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ, die kpɩkpaaŋ pam die dɩdɩ Yisa, die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: ta chii ke a kuli tigiŋ. Saŋka mi ʋ nan wa ʋ zʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ jʋjʋrɩtamba a lagɩsɩ a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ Nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, dama n ye wo n yii dieke dɩ bana wa. .

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ yaala ʋ bemme n jigiŋ, sie ʋ chome mɩŋ a tɩaŋ ʋ chone ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ aŋaŋ ʋ ballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ miivoli die. Dɩ daa die ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Die wɩa Yisa dɩ bɩ bala ba dɩ Tʋntʋmbɩatɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ dɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ʋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan dɩ beri arɩzanna ma ʋ wɩa a tɩaŋ ka bala ka sɩmma die vuovɩɩna baŋɩsɩ-wayɩ aŋaŋ awayɩ dieke dɩ yilinene dɩ dɩ ka mʋ dɩ ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ kana ka viigi ʋ kuŋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩ n kʋaŋ, kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Yaa dɩ yi hɔgʋ dɩ yaa ligiribie baŋ, ta a kaanɩ dɩ bɩa, lalɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi? Sie ʋ chɔgɩsɩ popoli ta mɩŋŋɩ saarɩ ʋ juoku ta mɩŋŋɩ daansɩ jigiŋ mana a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan ye ke.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaala ʋ mɩɩ tigikpɩɩŋ, sie ʋ woliŋ a kala a sɩmma ka ligire nʋaŋ, a sɩmma baarɩ ʋ ligire nan tʋgɩ yaa ka kaaŋ tʋgɩ.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Ʋ keŋ a ye ke ʋ ŋaaŋ wa wa ʋ zʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ jʋjʋrɩtamba a lagɩsɩ a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, dama n ye wo n ligiribiŋ dieke dɩ bana wa.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Dama ʋ ka yi die, ta piili tigiri ta keŋ ka bɩagɩ a mɩɩ ka kpatɩ, ta vuosi dɩ yese ke ta sɩba baarɩ ʋ ka bɩagɩ mɩɩ ka kpatɩ, ba nan vʋarɩ wa fala.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba, dɩɩ yi tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ʋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma, Ŋmɩŋ malakasisi nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam ʋ wɩa.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: Ba nan baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie die piiliye mɩŋ dɩ ʋ mɩɩ ama ʋ ka bɩagɩ kpatɩ.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die yalla dembibisi bale.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Dɩɩ yi naaŋ dɩ yaa vuosi tuse baŋ a nyɩŋ gara ʋ wagɩ ʋ chanchaaŋ dɩ yalla tuse baŋɩsɩ-le, ʋ nan woliŋ kalɩ a daansɩ ʋ gbaŋ ye dɩ ʋ yaa hagɩrɩŋ a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a tuoli ʋ chanchaaka.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: ta bʋabike chaaŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ wa dɩ, N chʋa, n yaala fʋ nagɩ n balla n daansɩ dii faarɩ dieke a yɩ mɩŋ. Saŋka mi ʋ chɔɔŋ wa die dɩ puo faarɩ ka a yɩ ba bale wo.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ wo hagɩrɩŋ ʋ nan tʋŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ aŋ ba woliŋ dɩa ʋ nɩŋŋa a nagɩ vaaŋ a dagɩ ʋ chanchaaka dɩ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a wagɩ wa.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya bʋabike die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nyinti mana a daa, ta nagɩ ligirehe a dɩa, a ga tɩgɩ saasaa. Mi die ʋ ga dii ligirehe yɔrɩ yɔrɩ a kpatɩ.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ kpatɩ ba dɩ, Die gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ wʋnyɩ mana dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ sie ʋ nagɩ nyinti mana a taaŋ.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Die ʋ dine jaaŋ mana a kpatɩ wa, die kɔŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋka mana ma, die dɩ paalɩ a wʋŋ wa.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Yasɩ nansɩ mɩŋ, ama dɩɩ yi ka nansɩbʋ dɩ nyɩŋ, lalɩa ka bɩ baa ka ye ka nansɩbʋ bɩbra?.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ ga bie daa wʋnyɩ tigiŋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma, daa wa die dɩ yaa wa ga ʋ kʋaŋ ma dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ perukusi.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: A ka bɩ vɩɩna dɩ fʋ nagɩ ka a taaŋ fʋ kʋaŋ ma yaa fʋ nagɩ ka a gʋtɩ hʋʋsɩŋ ma. Sie fʋ nagɩ ka a taaŋ. Vuodieke dɩ yaa tɩba ʋ wʋmma.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Die ʋ tɩŋ yaala ʋ dii perukusisi nyindiikehe a chagɩ ama vuoŋ die ka yɩ wa jaaŋ.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ keŋ mɩŋŋɩ a yili, N chʋa tʋntʋntɩba mana yese nyindiike a die ta tɩala, ta giena maŋ baa n kpi aŋaŋ kɔŋ.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Dɩ sɩna die wo, n nan hagɩ a ga n chʋa jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, N chʋa, n yi bɩaŋ ta chʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ jigiŋ.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: N ka bɩ mʋ fʋ wa mɩŋ fʋ bʋa; daansɩma mɩŋ sɩba fʋ tʋntʋntɩba kaanɩ.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ a ga ʋ chɔɔŋ wa jigiŋ. Die ʋ yene bie sieku me a gara wa, ʋ chɔɔŋ wa die dɩ ye wo, zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo pam, die ʋ chɩgɩ ga a gurigi wo.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Die bʋa wa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N chʋa, n yi bɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ jigiŋ. N ka bɩ mʋ dɩ fʋ wa mɩŋ fʋ bʋa.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Ama ʋ chɔɔŋ wa die dɩ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yi lagɩ lagɩ a nagɩ jayeekivɩɩka a keŋ yeegi wo, ta nagɩ nuupiriŋ a keŋ yi ʋ nuuŋ me, ta nagɩ nɩɩra a suu wo;.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: ta ga nagɩ narapɔlɩ dieke dɩ narɩna a keŋ kʋʋ aŋ tɩ dii dʋʋga.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Dama die n yile dɩ n bʋa wa gie kpiye mɩŋ, ama ta ʋ bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ta bɩ yile dɩ ʋ baya mɩŋ, ama ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ mɩŋ. Die wɩa die ba piili a die dʋʋgaka aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Ama saŋka mi, die ʋ bʋa jakʋʋrɩ die ye bie wo kʋakʋ ma. Die ʋ kienene tigiri a keŋ gbigi tigiri, die ʋ wʋŋ yɩlaha aŋaŋ siele he.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ wa tʋntʋntɩba wʋnyɩ a pɩasɩ wa wudieke dɩ yinene tigiri me.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ nɩmbʋa wa yiŋŋi keŋ mɩŋ, fʋ chʋa wa dɩ kʋʋ narapɔlɩ dieke dɩ narɩna, dama ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ alaafɩa.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Die bʋa jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ, ta zeti dɩ ʋ kaaŋ juu gbaakʋ sʋŋ; die wɩa die ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ keŋ juu.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: ama die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ wa dɩ, Ye, bɩnaha gie mana n tʋma a yɩa fʋ mɩŋ sɩba yɔmʋ, ta saaga fʋ nʋaŋ, ama ka yi mɩŋ biibiŋ gbaŋ dɩ n kʋʋ a wa n zʋalɩŋ aŋ tɩ dii ta yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Fʋ bʋa wa gie nɩŋ die chʋʋsɩna fʋ nyinti mana hɔgʋba ma wa, dɩ keŋ tigiŋ fʋ kʋʋ narapɔlɩ narɩkɩŋ a yɩ wa.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Ʋ chɔɔŋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, N bʋa, saŋŋa mana fʋ bie wo n jigiŋ, ta jadieke mana n yalla die dɩ yi fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Ama dɩ mʋ tɩ dime dʋʋga ta yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dama die tɩ yile dɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa wa gie kpiye mɩŋ, ama ta ʋ bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ta bɩ yile dɩ ʋ baya mɩŋ, ama lele tɩ ye wo mɩŋ. .

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Yisa die dɩ ga jigiŋ kaanɩ a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ, die ʋ kpatɩna wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dagɩ tɩ tɩ bala tɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ dene sɩba Jɔɔn die dɩ dagɩna ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die wo.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Naa mʋna dɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ; Tɩ Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ: Vaa aŋ ba yɩ fʋ saaŋ kasɩka jɩlɩma; Vaa aŋ fʋ naarɩ keŋ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Yɩ tɩ daaŋ mana nyindiike dieke tɩ yaalala.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Nagɩ tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa tɩ, sɩba tɩ nagɩna vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnna tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba wa. Da keŋ vaa tɩ juu magɩsɩ dieke dɩ hagɩrɩna ma.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi ta nɩ wʋnyɩ yallɩ zʋa ta ga ʋ jigiŋ tanseese, a baarɩ dɩ, N zʋa, paŋŋɩ mɩŋ paanʋ golime ataa.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Ligiretieŋ wʋnyɩ die benne a yallɩ tʋntʋntʋ jakʋʋŋ ʋ daansa ʋ nyinti, die ba keŋ balɩ wa dɩ ʋ die ʋ ligirehe yɔrɩ yɔrɩ.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: dama chaanʋ wʋnyɩ kenne n jigiŋ maŋ wo jadiikiŋ n baaŋ nagɩ a yɩ wa.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ wa wa a balɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa yine maŋ wʋmma fʋ wɩa dene? Kaaŋ bɩ yi n tʋʋma jakʋʋŋ, die wɩa biisi n nyinti mana aŋ n ye.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi fʋ zʋa wa dɩ bie juoku sʋŋ ta yiŋŋi balɩ fʋ dɩ, Da mugisime mɩŋ! N wɔŋ kparɩ sanʋarɩ mɩŋ ta manɩŋ aŋaŋ n ballɩ wɔŋ gʋʋra mɩŋ; N kaaŋ bɩagɩ a hagɩ a yɩ fʋ jaaŋ.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Ta tʋʋma jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ yile dɩ, Bɩa maŋ baa n yi? N jakʋʋrɩ baa ʋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ tʋʋma ma. N ka hagɩrɩ a baaŋ nan kʋa, ta chɩga viivi n baaŋ nan jʋʋsɩ.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N bala nɩ dɩɩ yi daa wa dɩ ka hagɩya a yɩ fʋ jaaŋ nɩ yine zʋalɩŋ wɩa, ama dɩɩ yi fʋ ka chɩga viivi ta jʋʋsa wa ʋ nan hagɩ a yɩ fʋ jadieke mana fʋ yaalala fʋ mugisinene wo wɩa.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: N sɩba wudieke n baaŋ nan yi lele amʋ maŋ keŋ waarɩ tʋʋmaha vuosi nan tuo mɩŋ ba tige me.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Die wɩa n bala nɩ, nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta ʋ nan nagɩ a yɩ nɩ; nɩ daansɩma ta nɩ nan ye; nɩ kpaasɩma gbɩabɩkʋ ta ʋ nan yuori a yɩ nɩ.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ wa vuodiekemba mana die dɩ dine ʋ jakʋʋrɩ hamɩŋ wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ. Die ʋ pɩasɩ bʋmbʋŋaŋ vuoke dɩ, N jakʋʋrɩ hamɩŋ aŋmɩa benne fʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩŋ tuose mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ yaalɩnana yese mɩŋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kpaasɩnana gbɩabɩkʋ ka yuore a yɩa wa mɩŋ.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Kpaatɩ gbiesi kɔbɩsɩ-nɩɩ. Daa wa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Hamɩŋ gbanɩŋ wʋnna tuo kala lagɩ lagɩ a yiŋŋi maagɩ kɔbɩsɩ-nɩɩsa.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ chʋalɩŋ nɩ jabɩa bʋa dɩ nan jʋʋsɩ wa zaasɩŋ aŋ ʋ nagɩ javʋʋkɩŋ a yɩ wa?.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Ta balɩ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ, Aŋmɩa fʋ gbaŋ fʋ dii? Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Zaa bɔtʋsɩ kobɩga. Ta daa wa dɩ balɩ, Fʋ hamɩŋ gbanɩŋ wʋnna, yiŋŋi maagɩ baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩ.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Yaa mɩnɩa bʋa dɩ nan jʋʋsɩ wa gɩlɩ aŋ ʋ nagɩ chɩakʋrɔŋ a yɩ wa?.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Die ligiretieŋ wo die dɩ wʋnna ʋ tʋʋmaha jakʋʋrɩ nyɩnyɩrɩtʋ wa dɩ yine die wo, die ʋ bɩrɩ wa, dama ʋ tʋŋ wa aŋaŋ yɩaŋ. Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna tɩŋgbaŋka gie sieku yaa yɩaŋ ba sʋnsʋŋ a tɩaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna chaaŋkʋ sieŋ dɩ yalla die wo.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie dɩ sɩba nɩ nagɩ nyɩnvɩɩna a yɩ nɩ ballɩ, die nɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa nan faasɩ nagɩ ʋ Halɩkasɩka a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ jʋʋsɩnana a yaala ka wa.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Manɩŋ, n bala nɩ, nɩ nagɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie ma ligire a yime wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala, amʋ a keŋ kpatɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ nan tuo nɩ arɩzanna ma.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ die benne ta jɩmbɩaŋ die dɩ yallɩ wa ta ʋ ka bɩagɩ bala wɩa, ta Yisa die dɩ vʋarɩ ka, daa wa dɩ wa piili a bala wɩa. Die dɩ yi kpɩkpaakʋ mamachi pam.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩna tʋma tʋnbɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, nan bɩagɩ mɩŋŋɩ tʋŋ tʋnkpɩɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩrɩnana aŋaŋ ligiribiŋ, nan dɩ nyɩra aŋaŋ ligirikpɩɩma.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: ama bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli yɩna wa hagɩrɩbʋ ʋ bɩagɩ a vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ dene.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ nyɩra aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie ligire, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ arɩzanna ligire a yi fʋ nuusi me?.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ yaala ba magɩsɩ wa, die wɩa die ba balɩ wa dɩ ʋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ a dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ sɩba ʋ wɩa.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi fʋ nyɩra aŋaŋ vuoŋ nyinti, mɩnɩa bala ʋ yɩ fʋ fʋ tʋaŋ?.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile ta balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Naaŋ dieke dɩ puone a yiŋŋi waga ka gbaŋ kaaŋ yʋasɩ, ta tigi dieke vuosi dɩaŋ dɩ puone a yiŋŋi waga ka gbaŋ nan nan.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Yɔmʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama naalɩŋ bale. Ʋ nan haa wʋnyɩ ta chome wʋnyɩ, yaa ʋ nan yɩ wʋnyɩ jɩlɩma ta wʋnyɩ ʋ nine kaaŋ dɩ suuli. Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ligire.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Die dɩ baa ka yi dɩ yi Sitaani dɩ puo ta yiŋŋi waga ka gbaŋ gbaŋ, ka naarɩ ka baa ka zie. Nɩ baarɩ dɩ jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli yɩna mɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ maŋ yie naa;.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Die Farasisisi die dɩ wʋŋ wɩaha gie mana, ta die a vʋara Yisa fala, dama die ba yaala ligire wɩa pam.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: dɩɩ yi naa maŋ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩtɩ, ta nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, mɩnɩa yɩnana nɩ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba hagɩrɩŋ ba vʋara ha? Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nan dagɩ dɩ nɩ wo wusie.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Die Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine vuodiekemba dɩ yinene nɩ gbaŋ sɩba vuovɩɩna vuosi nɩŋŋa, ama Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma. Dama jadieke vuota dɩ yilinene dɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ pam wa, Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ nɩŋ ka yiwo jatɔgʋ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Womi Yisa dɩ balɩ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ da yilime dɩ magɩsɩŋ kaaŋ keŋ; ama wʋbɩaŋ bie vuodiekemba dɩ vanana ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ yie bɩaŋ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama manɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ maŋ nagɩ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma, die dagɩna sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɔŋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Mosisi mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ maagɩna wudiekemba ba die beri mɩŋ a keŋ tʋgɩ Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ saŋka, a nyɩŋ Jɔɔn keniŋ saŋka ba mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩna yaa gamma ʋ naarɩ ma, ta vuoŋ mana dɩ mɩa dɩ ba juu mi.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Ba tɩŋ nagɩ nɩɩŋ a bɔbɩ a yeegi ʋ nyie a nagɩ wa a taaŋ nyaakpɩɩŋ ma dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa a tɩaŋ ʋ baaŋ nan yi ballɩbɩsɩ gie wʋnyɩ yi bɩaŋ.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Dɩɩ yi daahagɩrʋ dɩ yi siri a gbarɩ ʋ tigiŋ aŋaŋ dembisi ŋaŋ nyinti, ʋ nyinti mana nan mɩŋŋɩ beri.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nan keŋ tɩaŋ ga ama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha wʋbiŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ tɩaŋ ga.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Die wɩa nɩ yigi nɩ gbaŋ! Dɩɩ yi fʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ yi fʋ bɩaŋ, kpaaŋ wa; ama dɩɩ yi ʋ baarɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩ fʋ mɩŋ, fʋ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi vuodieke dɩ hagɩrɩna a tɩaŋ wa dɩ keŋ tuoli wo ʋ tigiŋ me, ʋ nan nyaŋŋɩ wa ta gbatɩ ʋ dembisi ŋaŋ nyintiti ta bɩ chii ʋ nyintiti.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Daa dieke mana dɩ keŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ta yiŋŋi faarɩ hɔgʋ gaaŋ yiwo hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, ta dembiŋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ faarɩna hɔgʋ wa yiwo hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ yi fʋ bɩaŋ bʋyʋpɔyɩ dabalɩmɩŋ, ama ta yiŋŋi keŋ fʋ jigiŋ bʋyʋpɔyɩ a baarɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya, fʋ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka zie n kʋaŋ waga mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kana ka suŋŋi mɩŋ a lagɩsa vuosi n jigiŋ jata ba mɩŋ.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nyintitieŋ wʋnyɩ die benne a yeegi nyiŋyeeki dieke dɩ yalla ligire pam ta bie ʋ dʋŋŋʋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ daaŋ mana.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa tʋntʋntɩba dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Vaa tɩ yadaka gʋtɩ.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ vuota ma, ka ŋaaŋ dɩa wa jigikʋʋkɩŋ a yaala jigiŋ dɩ ka voosi, ka ka yeye; ʋ ŋaaŋ balɩ a yɩ wa ka gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ, N nan yiŋŋi a ga n tigidieke n vana wa.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Ta die daa zɔɔlɩntieŋ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die benne ba wasa wa Lazurusi, ta die ʋ yaa kʋjaga pam. Die ba ŋaaŋ yaa wa a keŋ dʋaŋ nyintitieŋ wo sanʋaŋ ma.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ yaa yada sɩba kaŋmɩŋ biŋ, nɩ nan bɩagɩ a balɩ yɩ tɩɩkpɩɩka gie dɩ, Vʋʋ a ga nan nyaakpɩɩbʋ gie me aŋ ka seŋ a tuo fʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Die wɩa ka ŋaaŋ yiŋŋi ga wa mi a ye ta ka sʋŋ mana saarɩya a wʋnsɩ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ yallɩ tʋntʋntʋ ta ʋ kʋa yaa ta daansa yiise, ta ʋ keŋ nyɩŋ kʋaŋ a keŋ tigiŋ, nan balɩ wa dɩ, Keŋ lagɩ a keŋ kala dii nyindiike.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: die ʋ ŋaaŋ yaala ʋ dii wo nyintitieŋ wo nyindiiki chʋata dɩ ŋaana nanna tɩŋgbaŋ; die gbaasɩ gbaŋ dɩ keŋ lansa ʋ kʋjagaha.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Ka ŋaaŋ wa nyɩŋ mɩŋ a ga a chii jɩmbɩatɩ diekemba dɩ tɩanna ka gbaŋ gbaŋ ayʋpɔyɩ aŋ a ga bemme mi. Naa chɩama daa wa beriŋ nan faasɩ chʋʋsɩ pam a tɩaŋ die ʋ sɩna die wo.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Aayɩ, nan balɩ wa dɩ, Vʋarɩ fʋ kʋaŋ nyinti ta yeegi nyɩnvɩɩna a yi n nyindiike a zieŋ mɩŋ aŋ n keŋ dii ta nyuu, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ fʋ gbaŋ fʋ dii ta nyuu.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ wo die dɩ kpi, malakasi die dɩ keŋ chii wo a ga Abarahami jigiŋ arɩzanna ma. Ta nyintitieŋ wo dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ kpi, die ba guu wo.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yisa die dɩ bala wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, ta hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ a faasɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie hɔgʋ dieke dɩ mɩɩrɩna fʋ ta fʋ nyʋʋsɩ ʋ bɩɩsɩŋ ma.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Fʋ yile dɩ jakʋʋrɩ nan waasɩ tʋntʋntʋ wa ʋ yine wudieke ʋ bala wa dɩ ʋ yi wo? Aayɩ.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Die ʋ ga bie kpiiliŋ jigiberisikiŋ, die dɩ wʋmma wa pam. Die ʋ daansɩ a ye Abarahami saasaa, ta Lazurusi kalɩ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnnana Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha ta dɩha wa, banɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ bie ba ma.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Die gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ nɩ jigiŋ, saŋŋa dieke nɩ keŋ tʋŋ tʋŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala dɩ nɩ yiwo a kpatɩ, nɩ balɩ dɩ, Tɩ yiwo tʋntʋntɩŋ nyɩɩna. Tɩnɩŋ tɩ yiwo wudieke dɩ mʋna tɩ yi nyɩɩna. .

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Die wɩa die nyintitieŋ wo die dɩ natɩ a wa dɩ, N chʋa Abarahami, chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ ta fʋ tʋŋ Lazurusi ŋaŋ ʋ ga nagɩ nuubiŋ a luo nyaaŋ ma a keŋ gbi n jiliŋ, dama n paalɩ die wahala bolibu gie me.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ giline Yisa wa die ʋ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Lele wo gie vuosi yiwo vuobɩatɩ ta yaala ba ye mamachi wɩɩŋ; ama n kaaŋ wɔŋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ a yɩ ba; sie Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jona dagɩŋ chanchaaŋ nyɩɩna ma maŋ nan dagɩ ba.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yisa die dɩ ganana Jerusalemi die ʋ daagɩ Samaria aŋaŋ Galili sʋnsʋŋ aŋaŋ sʋnsʋŋ.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Ama Abarahami die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, N bʋa, tɩɩnsɩ dɩ die fʋ benne fʋ mɩsɩ ma wa, die ye wo nyɩnvɩɩna, ta Lazurusi dɩ ye nyɩnbɩatɩ mana, ama lele ʋ bie wo nansɩŋ ma giena ta fʋ bie wahala ma.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Wudieke dɩ yine Jona die dagɩna Nineva vuosi dɩ Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna wa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ bala ka yi manɩŋ vuota Bʋa ta dagɩ nɩnɩŋ leleke gie vuosi dɩ Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Die ʋ gana ga juo tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ kaanɩ; ta gama baŋ die dɩ keŋ tuoli wo mi. Die ba zie saasaa.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Ta wudieke dɩ gʋtɩna yine, gulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ benne tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋnsʋŋ, die wɩa vuodiekemba dɩ benne gie a yaala ba gasɩ a keŋ fʋ jigiri kaaŋ bɩagɩ, ta vuodieke dɩ benne fʋ jigiri dɩ yaala ba gasɩ a keŋ tɩ jigiŋ dɩaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Sɩaba hɔgʋ naaŋ wa nan hagɩ a zie a galɩŋ nɩ dama die ʋ paalɩ nyɩŋ wa tɩgɩ saasaa a ga dɩ ʋ wʋŋ naaŋ Solomoni yɩaŋ wʋbalɩkaha, ama n bala nɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna naaŋ Solomoni benne giena.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: ta faasɩ natɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ Yisa, chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Die nyintitieŋ wo dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ Abarahami, die nɩŋ n jʋʋsa fʋ, tʋŋ Lazurusi aŋ ʋ ga n chʋa tigiŋ me.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Nineva vuosi nan hagɩ zie a galɩŋ nɩ, dama Jona dɩ mʋʋlɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩ ba wa die ba va wa ba tʋmbɩatɩ; n bala nɩ, vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Jona benne gie.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye be ta balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gamma Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ jigiŋ aŋ ba jɩɩŋ nɩ ye. Die ba benne sieku me a gara wa die ba ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: n yaa wa n nɩmballɩ banʋ, vaa ʋ ga a ga kpaaŋ ba amʋ ba gbaŋ ba da keŋ keŋ wahalaka gie jigiŋ.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Vuoŋ ka chɔgɩsa popoli ta nagɩ ka a lɔbɩra yaa a nagɩ kpalɩ a bubike ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ ka a sagɩ wa jadieke dɩ jʋalɩna ma, amʋ vuosi dɩ keŋ juu ba nan ye ka chaaŋkʋ.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Ta die ba wʋnyɩ die dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dama die ʋ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Yisa gbaanna wa, a keŋ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Die Abarahami dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Fʋ nɩmballɩ yaa Mosisi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wɩa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma. Ba wʋmma ha.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Fʋ nine yine fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ popoli. Die wɩa fʋ nine dɩ keŋ vɩɩna, fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana nan suuli aŋaŋ chaanɩŋ; ama fʋ nine dɩ keŋ a ka vɩɩna, fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana nan dɩ bie lɩmɩŋ ma.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: A die nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ Yisa nɩŋŋa ta waasa wa; ama die daa wa die yiwo Samaria vuoŋ.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Ta nyintitieŋ wo dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ Abarahami, aayɩ, die nyɩɩna ma ka mʋ; ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ nyɩŋ kuŋ me a ga ba jigiŋ ba nan chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Nɩ wa sɩmma dɩ chaaŋ dieke dɩ benne nɩ ma wa da keŋ bɩrɩŋ lɩmɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Daa vuosi baŋ die yene gbaamɩŋ? Ama bawayɩ wa bie sɩa?.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ama die Abarahami dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi ba kaaŋ wʋŋ Mosisi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩ maagɩna wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa, ba kaaŋ tarɩgɩ ba sʋŋanyile dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ hagɩ kuŋ me gbaŋ a ga ba jigiŋ. .

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Die wɩa dɩɩ yi fʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ suuli aŋaŋ chaanɩŋ, ta jigiŋ dɩ ka bie lɩmɩŋ ma, ka mana nan mɩŋŋɩ chaaŋ farrɩtata a nɩasɩ popoli dɩ ŋaana a chaaŋ yɩ fʋ die wo.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ ka keŋ dɩ ba bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ sie chaanʋ wa gie.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balla wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, ta Farasisi vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ wa wa dɩ ʋ keŋ aŋ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ wa dii nyindiike, die wɩa die ʋ ga a ga a kalɩ dɩ ʋ dii nyindiike he.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Ta die wa balɩ daa wa dɩ, Hagɩ a ga, fʋ yada gbaanna fʋ.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Die dɩ yi Farasisi vuoke mamachi, Yisa die dɩ kana ka nɩɩtɩ ʋ nuusi ta yaala ʋ dii nyindiike he.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Mi die Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ balla ka keŋ. Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Ka ka yi sɩba nɩ nan daansɩ a ye Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ balla ka keŋ die.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ Farasisi vuosi nɩ ŋaaŋ daaraa nɩ nyachibisi aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbala kʋasɩ, ama ta nɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ halɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩtʋaŋ.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ baarɩ dɩ, Daansɩ, ka wʋnna, yaa Ka benne mi wo; dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ bie nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ gaantɩba gie! Daa Ŋmɩŋ dieke die dɩ naanna a kʋasɩsɩ naanna a sʋgɩtɩtɩ gbaŋ?.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ ga a juu Jeriko a chʋŋ tɩanna.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Mi die ʋ balɩ yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Saŋŋa kieŋ nɩ nan dɩ yaala nɩ ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa keniŋ, ama nɩ kaaŋ ye.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Yɩma zɔɔlɩntieliŋ jadieke dɩ benne nɩ nyachibisi me aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbala ma aŋ jaaŋ mana yɩantɩ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Ta daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie mi ba wasa wa Zakiyosi, die ʋ yiwo lampotuosiriŋ jakʋʋŋ, ta die yaa nyinti pam.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Ba nan daansɩ balɩ nɩ, Ye, ʋ benne mi wo yaa Ye, ʋ bie giena. Ama nɩ da keŋ nyɩŋ a dɩ dɩa yaalɩma mɩŋ;.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Nɩnɩŋ Farasisi mba, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ naga nanjʋʋŋ aŋaŋ bʋŋʋ aŋaŋ neeri aŋaŋ nyinti a taŋ baŋ sʋŋ a nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, ama ta va jadieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna ʋ mɩraha ma; sɩba wusie sarɩya diile ta ka cho Ŋmɩŋ. Genhe die mʋna nɩ yime, a gʋtɩ nɩ yɩnana Ŋmɩŋ nyinti baŋ sʋŋ kaanɩ wa.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Die ʋ mɩa ʋ ye Yisa dɩ yine vuodieke, ama die ʋ yine vuogobiri wɩa die ʋ ka bɩagɩ ye wo dama vuosi die dala mɩŋ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Nɩnɩŋ Farasisi, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ yaala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me, ta bɩ yaala ba wasɩma nɩ aŋaŋ jɩlɩma nyʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa keniŋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba nɩɩnyɩgɩsɩŋ dɩ ŋaana nyɩgɩsɩ a chaaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ mana a nyɩŋ tɩŋ chɩaŋ a ga tʋgɩ tɩŋ sikpeŋ die wo.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ chɩgɩ a dɩ kpɩkpaakʋ nɩŋŋa a ga jʋalɩ chunchuliŋ tɩɩŋ dɩ ʋ ye Yisa, dama die ʋ gara mi chaaŋ.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ sɩɩ sɩba kunti vɔrɩtɩ dɩ lɔbɩrɩna vuosi dɩ ka yesehe ta chʋŋ a ma.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Ama sie n woliŋ dii wahala pam ta leleke gie vuosi bɩ zeti mɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yisa die dɩ kenne a tʋgɩ mi wo, ʋ daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Zakiyosi, keŋ sʋʋŋ lagɩ lagɩ. Sie n yi fʋ chaanʋ jinne.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Die dɩ sɩna die Nowa saŋka, die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma manɩŋ vuota Bʋa keniŋ saŋŋa.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Die wɩa mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, fʋ bala naa nɩŋ zɩa wa tɩ gbaŋ.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ta Zakiyosi die dɩ yi lagɩ lagɩ a keŋ sʋʋŋ ta waasɩ wa aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta yaa wa ga ʋ tigiŋ.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Die Nowa saŋka, vuosi die die mɩŋ ta nyuo ta faara hɔɔŋ, ta naga ba hɔgʋlɩaŋ a yɩa chʋllɩŋ, ta die a ga tʋgɩ daa dieke Nowa dɩ juune haarɩkpɩɩkʋ, ta die nyaakpɩɩbʋ die dɩ keŋ kʋʋ vuoŋ mana die dɩ wone haarɩkpɩɩkʋ ma wa.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ma dama nɩ chie vuosi chiidʋnsa dɩ faasɩna a dʋnsɩ aŋaŋ ba baaŋ chii ama nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ ka suŋŋi be dɩ ba chii.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Ta vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yene wo die dɩ piili a vʋʋna wɩa dɩ, Daa wa gie ga bɩrɩŋ wa chaanʋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ wo tigiŋ me.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Loti saŋŋa gbaŋ die sɩɩ wa die; die vuoŋ mana die dɩ die ta nyuo, ta daa a yalla, ta bʋra ta mɩa tige;.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ ka tuose Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋbalɩkaha ama ta mɩa ta wʋnsa ba kunti vɔrɩtɩ a vɩɩna, banɩŋ die ba yine naazʋalɩŋ diekemba nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ kʋna wa ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ka tuose Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Zakiyosi dɩ hagɩ a zie a balɩ yɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, wʋmma, n nan puo nyindiekemba n yalla bule ta nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ; ta dɩɩ yi maŋ nyɩrɩ wa vuoŋ, n nan yiŋŋi tuŋ wo die bʋnɩɩsa.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: ama daa dieke Loti die dɩ nyɩnna Sodomi tɩka, ta boliŋ aŋaŋ maafajiribi die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ kʋʋ ba mana.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Nɩ yine wudieke wo, die dagɩna vuosi dɩ nɩ seŋ saagɩ wudieke nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba dɩ yine wo, dama die ba kʋʋ wa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba ta nɩ dɩaŋ dɩ mɩa ba vɔrɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Gbatɩtaanɩŋ keŋ tigiri gie vuosi me jinne, dama fʋ gbaŋ yiwo Abarahami haagɩŋ.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Naa dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma daa dieke manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ balla n keŋ wo.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma, die yɩantieŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N nan tʋŋ naazʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ ba jigiŋ, ta ba nan kʋʋ bataŋ ta mugisi bataŋ dɩaŋ.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa keŋye mɩŋ dɩ n yaalɩ vuodiekemba dɩ bana ta ye be a gbatɩ ba taaŋ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi daa dieke dɩ benne mampili sikpeŋ da keŋ a sʋʋŋ ʋ juoku sikpeŋ dɩ ʋ ga a nagɩ ʋ nyinti; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ benne ʋ kʋaŋ ma da keŋ yiŋŋi a kuli tigiŋ dɩ ʋ ga a nagɩ ʋ nyinti.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Die wɩa n nan datɩ leleke gie vuosi tɩba aŋaŋ ba kʋna naazʋalɩba a nyɩŋ dʋnɩa piiliku.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Die vuosi die dɩ yene a wʋmma wɩaha gie wo, Yisa die dɩ bɩ a taaŋ ba nandagɩŋ, dama die ba yile dɩ Yisa dɩ gbigine Jerusalemi wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ die baa ka keŋ wo lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Nɩ tɩɩnsɩ Loti hɔgʋ wa wɩaha.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: a nyɩŋ die ba kʋna Abeli a ga tʋgɩ Zakaria, vuodieke die ba kʋna Ŋmɩŋ kaabɩŋ koriku aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋnsʋkʋ. N bala nɩ wusie, leleke gie vuosi nan ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ yaa gamma vuosi gie mana kumbu wɩa.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Daa jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die gana tɩŋgbaŋ saasaa dɩ ba ga kalɩŋ wa naa, ta ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ tigiŋ.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kpaŋŋɩnana ʋ gbaŋ dɩ ʋ gbatɩ ʋ miivoli nan waarɩ ka, ama vuodieke mana dɩ waarɩna ʋ miivoli n wɩa nan daansɩ ye ke.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ ligi wo Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dɩ vuosi da keŋ sɩmma a chɩasɩ, ama nɩ gbaŋ nɩ zeti wo a sɩbɩŋ, ta kaga vuodiekemba dɩ mɩnana dɩ ba sɩmma ha.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: N bala nɩ, saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan keŋ wo, vuosi bale nan dɩ dʋa jadʋakɩbalɩmɩŋ ma, ta n nagɩ wʋnyɩ ta vaa wʋnyɩ.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Die ʋ balla ʋ ga wa die ʋ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ baŋ, ta yɩ yɩ ba salɩma bie kaanɩ kaanɩ, ta balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ nagɩ ligirehe gie a tʋŋ tʋʋma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan viiri keŋ.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bala ʋ nyɩŋ mi wo ta mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ wa, die ba piili a pɩasa wa wʋpɩasɩka pam yaa gamma ʋ balɩkʋ ma.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Hɔɔŋ bale nan dɩ nʋma zaa nɩa ma, ta n nagɩ wʋnyɩ ta vaa wʋnyɩ. .

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Ama die ʋ tɩka vuosi die ka yaala ʋ wɩa, die wɩa die ba tʋŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ dɩ ba ga balɩ naakpɩɩka dɩ ba ka yaala daa wa gie yi ba naaŋ.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: a mɩa yaala dɩ ʋ balɩ a chʋʋsɩ aŋ ba yigi wo.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, sɩa? Ta ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Jigidieke jakpiikiŋ dɩ benne, mi zuuti dɩ lagɩsa.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Ama die daa jakʋʋrɩ dɩ ga dii naarɩ ta yiŋŋi keŋ tigiŋ. Die ʋ kenne wo, die ʋ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba die ʋ yɩna ba ligirehe a lagɩsɩ, dɩ ʋ yaala ʋ ye ba yene nyʋarɩ die a gʋtɩ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Nɩŋŋaŋ vuoke die dɩ keŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, n ye wo salɩma bie baŋ a gʋtɩ fʋ yɩna mɩŋ jadieke wo ma.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ, yiwo tʋntʋntʋ vɩɩnʋ. Fʋ mɩŋŋɩna a pɔgɩlɩ jabike wɩa, n nan nagɩ tɩgɩsɩ baŋ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Die vuodieke dɩ diisine wo dɩ keŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, n ye wo salɩma ligire bie anʋ a gʋtɩ jadieke fʋ yɩna mɩŋ wa ma.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ geŋ wo dɩaŋ dɩ, N nan nagɩ tɩgɩsɩ anʋ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ fʋ ligire bini wʋnna, die n nagɩ vili wo gachɔɔtɩŋ ma a nagɩ lɔbɩrɩ.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: dama die n chɩga fʋ wa ŋmaamɩŋ, dama yiwo vuodieke wɩa dɩ tʋana. Naga jadieke dɩ kana a ka yi fʋ jaaŋ, ta gobe jadieke fʋ kana ka bʋrɩya.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Ta jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ tʋntʋntɩ bɩaŋ, n nan nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ wʋbalɩka a dii fʋ sarɩya. Sɩba a baarɩ n yiwo vuodieke wɩa dɩ tʋana, ta naga jadieke dɩ kana ka yi n jaaŋ, ta gobe jadieke n kana ka bʋrɩya.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Bɩa yine ta fʋ ka nagɩ n ligirebu a paŋŋɩ aŋ ba yiŋŋi tuŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ nyʋarɩ? Amʋ n tɩnna a keŋ naa n tɩŋ nan tuoke aŋaŋ nyʋarɩ.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Womi die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ balɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ zene mi wo dɩ, Nɩ tuo ligire bini a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ ta nagɩ gʋtɩ vuodieke dɩ yalla ligire bie baŋ wa.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, ʋ wɔŋ yaa wa ligire bie baŋ.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N bala nɩ, vuodieke dɩ yalla nyinti, ba nan yɩ wa pam a gʋtɩ, ama vuodieke dɩ wone wo jaaŋ, bɩta gbaŋ ʋ yalla wa ba nan tuoke a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Tɔ, lele n dataasɩ diekemba die dɩ kana ka yaala dɩ n dii naarɩ, nɩ ga yaa ba keŋ giena a kʋʋ ba n nɩŋŋa. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yisa die dɩ bala wɩaha a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ dɩ ba nɩŋŋa ba gara Jerusalemi.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Die ʋ kenne gbigi Betifiji aŋaŋ Betani tɩgɩsɩ die dɩ benne kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Olivi tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri, die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Nɩ gamma tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ dieke dɩ benne nɩ nɩŋŋa wa, nɩ die keŋ juo nɩ nan ye bonpɔlɩ dɩ bɔbɩna zie ta vuoŋ dɩ ye ka jʋalɩ ka. Nɩ forisi ke a yaa ka a keŋ giena.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ pɩasɩ nɩ dɩ, Bɩa nɩ forisi ke? Nɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yaalala ka. .

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Die ba ga a ye wudieke gbaŋ gbaŋ die Yisa dɩ bala a yɩ ba wa.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Die ba forisinene bonpɔlɩka, ka tieŋ die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ forise bonpɔlɩka.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yaalala ka.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: ta die ba datɩ ka a yaa ka a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Ta die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nagɩ gaatɩ a diisi bonka sikpeŋ ta suŋŋi Yisa dɩ jʋalɩ kalɩ ka ma.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Die ʋ jʋalɩna a gara wa vuosisi dɩ nagɩ ba gaatɩ a jaatɩ sieku me.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Die ʋ kenne a gbigi jigidieke sieku dɩ sʋʋna gara Olivi kunkogiri jigiri, ʋ kʋaŋanvuosi mana die dɩ piili a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ta bɩra wa aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩma yaa gamma mamachi wʋkpɩɩma dieke ba yene wo dɩ.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika yi naaŋ dieke dɩ kienene Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma. Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme arɩzanna ma, ta nɩ kɔtɩ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ sikpeŋ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Mi Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ benne kpɩkpaakʋ ma wa die dɩ balɩ yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, balɩ fʋ kʋaŋanvuosisi dɩ ba tarɩ watɩ.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi ba tarɩ, ama tanaha dɩ dʋana wa nan dɩ nata a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Die ʋ kenne gbigi Jerusalemi ta ye ke wo die ʋ kʋŋ ka wɩa.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: ta baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ yiwo bɩaŋ dɩ jinne nɩ vuosi ka sɩba wudieke dɩ baaŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ a keŋ ba jigiŋ. Lele ka lɔbɩrɩya mɩŋ ba jigiŋ.
