eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Jerusalemi, saŋŋa nan keŋ fʋ dataasɩ nan mɩɩ gaamɩŋ a giliŋ fʋ lʋga mana dɩ ba tuoli fʋ. Sojasi nan taaŋ giliŋ fʋ jigiŋ mana.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Ba nan ŋmabɩ fʋ mana ta kʋʋ fʋ vuosi. Ba nan chʋʋsɩ nɩ tige mana ta taŋ balɩmɩŋ kaaŋ wɩarɩ a beri ka jigiberisikiŋ. Naa mana nan keŋ yi dama nɩ kana ka mɩŋŋɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kenne dɩ ʋ gbatɩ nɩ a taaŋ wa.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ a piili a yaga vuodiekemba die dɩ daanana nyinti mi wo.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: ta bala yɩa ba dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, N tigiŋ baa ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ. Ama nɩ nagɩ ka a bɩrɩŋ wa gaarɩŋ jigiberisikiŋ.

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Die daaŋ mana Yisa die bie wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: ama die ba ka ye sie dieke ba baaŋ daagɩ yi die, dama vuotamba mana die chɩɩsa wʋmma ʋ wʋbalɩkaha ta ka yaala ba waarɩ ʋ wʋbalɩkaha kaanɩ gbaŋ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Yisa die dɩ bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daga vuotamba ta mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩa vuosi, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Balɩ tɩ, mɩnɩa yiko me fʋ yie wɩaha gie? Mɩnɩa yɩna fʋ kanɩŋ yiko ke mi.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N gbaŋ n baa n pɩasɩ nɩ wa wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ. Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, Ŋmɩŋ die yɩna wa kanɩŋ yiko ke mi yaa vuota.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Die ba piili a bala yɩa taŋ dɩ, Wʋbɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Dɩɩ yi tɩ baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩna wa yiko ke mi, ʋ nan baarɩ dɩ ta bɩa wɩa die tɩ ka yiwo yada?.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Ta tɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, dɩ vuota die yɩna wa, kpɩkpaakʋ gie mana nan taa tɩ tana dama ba yi yada dɩ Jɔɔn die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Die wɩa die ba yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Tɩ ka sɩba jigidieke die ka nyɩnna.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Die nɩŋ, n gbaŋ n kaaŋ balɩ nɩ, vuodieke dɩ yɩna mɩŋ yiko ke gie maŋ yaa yie wɩaha gie.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ taaŋ vuotamba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ die sone tɩɩsɩ dɩ nyɩnana bie ʋ kʋaŋ ma, ta die nagɩ ka a yɩ tʋntʋntɩŋ nuusi me ta dɩa a ga jigiŋ a ga yʋasɩ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Die a kpaala saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩ ʋ tʋŋ ʋ yɔmʋ tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ dɩ ʋ ga tuo ʋ tʋaŋ a keŋ. Ama die tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ nɩgɩ ʋ yɔmʋ wa ta vaa ʋ yiŋŋi ga aŋaŋ nuuwɩaga.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ bɩ tʋŋ yɔmʋ gaaŋ, tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ nɩgɩ wa ta nagɩ wa yi viivi me ta vaa ʋ viiri ga aŋaŋ nuuwɩaga.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Womi die ʋ bɩ tʋŋ yɔmʋ dieke dɩ gʋtɩna bʋtaa ma, die ba yɩ wa daŋŋa ta vigi wa taaŋ.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Womi kʋakʋ tieŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ Bɩa maŋ baa yi? Die nɩŋ lele n nan tʋŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n bʋa dieke n chone. Wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ ba nan yɩ wa jɩlɩma.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama die tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yene wo wo die ba balɩ yɩ taŋ dɩ, Kʋakʋ tieŋ faarɩ diiru wʋnna. Nɩ vaa tɩ kʋʋ wa ta ʋ faarɩ yi tɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Die wɩa die ba nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ kʋakʋ ma a ga kʋʋ wa. Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa kʋakʋ tieŋ dɩ nan yi tʋntʋntɩba gie?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Ʋ nan keŋ a kʋʋ banɩŋ vuosi mi ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ vuogaasɩ. Die vuosisi die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, ba baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ die a ga saasaa.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Yisa die dɩ daansɩ ba ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ? Dɩ Taŋ dieke mɩmɩɩrɩba die dɩ zetine ta baarɩ ba ka yaala ka wa die yine gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Vuodieke dɩ nanna tanɩ gie sikpeŋ nan ŋmabɩ ŋmabɩ ama vuodieke tanɩ dɩ keŋ nan ʋ sikpeŋ ʋ nan nʋŋ nʋŋ sɩba zɔŋ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Die mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ba wɩa Yisa dɩ taana nandagɩrɩ gie wo, die ba mɩa dɩ ba yigi wo mi, ama die ba chɩga vuotamba ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Die wɩa die ba maansa daansa wa, die ba bɩ a yɩ mɩmaansɩ daansɩrɩba ligire dɩ ba ga Yisa jigiŋ a ga gaaŋ wa sɩba ba yiwo vuovɩɩna, ta pɩasɩ wa wʋpɩasɩka ta ye dɩ ʋ nan balɩ a chʋʋsɩ aŋ ba yigi wo a ga tɩka jakʋʋrɩ jigiŋ.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Die mɩmaansɩ daansɩrɩba dɩ balɩ yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ sɩba baarɩ wudieke fʋ balala ta daga wa yiwo wusie, ta ka luge ama a daga Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Balɩ tɩ, tɩ mɩraha yɩ sieŋ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ tumme lampo a yɩma Romani tɩŋ naaŋ wa Siiza yaa tɩ da tumme.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sanna die wo, ta balɩ yɩ ba dɩ.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Nagɩ ligiribiŋ a dagɩ mɩŋ. Mɩnɩa nine chaaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ saaŋ diisine ka ma? Die ba baarɩ dɩ, Naaŋ Siiza.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ta Yisa dɩ balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Die nɩŋ nɩ nagɩ yɩ Naaŋ Siiza jadieke dɩ yine ʋ sɩɩtɩ ta nagɩ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ jadieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Die ba ka bɩagɩ a yigi wo aŋaŋ wɩɩŋ vuosisi nɩŋŋa, die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩkʋ die yine be mamachi die ba ka bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩɩŋ.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Die Sadusisi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ; banɩŋ die ba balala dɩ vuosi kaaŋ daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me; die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Dɩdagɩrʋ, Mosisi die maagɩna mɩrɩka gie a yɩ tɩ dɩ, dɩɩ yi Dembiŋ dɩ keŋ faarɩ ʋ hɔgʋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ, dɩ mʋ daa wa mi nɩmbʋa faarɩ kpihɔgʋ wa amʋ ʋ mɩɩrɩ ballɩ a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kpine wo.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Die dembisi bayʋpɔyɩ die benne a yi nɩmballɩ, jakʋʋrɩ chaaŋ die dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ, ta ka mɩɩrɩ ballɩ ta kpi;.

eng: The second.
local: womi vuodieke dɩ diisine bule wo dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ wa, ta ka mɩɩrɩ ballɩ ta kpi;.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ diisine bʋtaa gbaŋ dɩ faarɩ wa ta kpi ta ka mɩɩrɩ ballɩ. Vuotɩalɩkaha mana die dɩ faarɩ hɔgʋ wa ta kpi ta ka mɩɩrɩ ballɩ.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋ wa dɩaŋ dɩ kpi.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Lele, kuŋ hagɩŋ daraaŋ mɩnɩa hɔgʋ bala ʋ yi wo? Dama ba bayʋpɔyɩ wa mana die faarɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi faara hɔɔŋ ta naga ba lɩaŋ yɩa chʋllɩŋ.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: ama vuodiekemba dɩ mʋna aŋaŋ kuŋ hagɩŋ ta nan keŋ hagɩ a bemme dʋnɩa haalɩkʋ ma wa, ka faara hɔɔŋ yaa a naga ba lɩaŋ yɩ chʋllɩŋ.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: dama ba kaaŋ bɩ kpi bɩbra; ba nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba malakasi, ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ dama ba hagɩ wa kuŋ me.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Mosisi gbaŋ die dagɩya dɩ kunti nan daansɩ a hagɩ kuŋ me. Mosisi die dɩ maagɩna yaa gamma tɩɩka diilehe, die ʋ wa wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, Abarahami Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ Aziki Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ Jakobu Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Vuosi gie die wɔŋ kpi mɩŋ ama ba ye ko bie wo ba mɩsɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ ka yi wo kunti Ŋmɩŋ, ʋ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba mɩsɩ ma ma Ŋmɩŋ, dama ʋ nɩŋŋa ba mana dɩ beri ba mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Die wɩa mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, mɩŋŋɩ a balɩ mɩŋ.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Die wɩa die ba chɩga dɩ ba bɩ pɩasɩ wa wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Lalɩa ba baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa baa ʋ yiwo Davidi haagɩŋ?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ, Davidi gbaŋ gbaŋ die maagɩya yɩla gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die balɩ a yɩ n Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ: kala n nuudiigiri gie chaaŋ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Davidi die wasa Masia wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ta lalɩa Masia wa dɩ bɩ baa ʋ yi Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Die vuosi mana dɩ wʋnnana Yisa wa, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Nɩ sɩmma vuodiekemba dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, ba nan dɩ yaala ba yeegi guuwagɩtɩ a chʋmma giliŋ me, ta yaala ba wasɩma ba aŋaŋ jɩlɩma nyugiti ma, ba keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me, ba ŋaaŋ yaala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka, ba keŋ laalɩŋ jigiŋ ba yaala sikpeŋ kpɩɩmatieliŋ jigikasɩka.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Banɩŋ ba nyɩra kpihɔgʋba ta gbata ba nyinti mana, ta jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ jʋʋsɩwagɩtɩ sɩba ba chɩga Ŋmɩŋ. Ba tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala pam.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Womi die vuosi tuse tuse die dɩ lagɩŋ ta die a tɩba tamba nagɩsɩ ma, die Yisa die dɩ woliŋ a piili a bala a yɩa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi vuosi beriku dama ba yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Jadieke mana dɩ lɔbɩrɩna lele nan daansɩ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma, ta wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ mana dɩaŋ nan nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Die wudieke mana nɩ bala lɩmɩŋ ma ba nan daansɩ wʋŋ ka yaalɩŋ ma; ta wudieke dɩaŋ nɩ pɔsɩna a balɩ juoŋ me nan daansɩ a mʋʋlɩ yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: N zʋalɩŋ, n bala nɩ, nɩ da chɩgɩma vuosi ŋmaamɩŋ, dama ba nan bɩagɩ kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi nɩ wɩɩŋ.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: N kpaama nɩ yaa gamma vuodieke nɩ mʋna nɩ chɩgɩ: Nɩ chɩgɩma Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ kʋʋ nɩ ta bɩ yaa yiko a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ nɩ a taaŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpisiku me wo. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ dɩ nɩ chɩgɩma wʋnɩŋ.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Ba ka daa nembisi anʋ wa kɔbɩsɩ-le? Ama ta Ŋmɩŋ ka daamma a kaanɩ mana wɩa.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ sikpeŋ zoosi nʋaŋ mɩŋ. Die wɩa nɩ da chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ; dama nɩ yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisisi pam.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: N bala nɩ; vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me dɩ ʋ yiwo n vuoŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ nan vʋarɩ wa yaalɩŋ ma Ŋmɩŋ malakasisi nɩŋŋa.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka yi n vuoŋ vuosi jigiŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ nan balɩ dɩ n ka sɩba wa Ŋmɩŋ malakasisi nɩŋŋa.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana mana dɩ keŋ balɩ wʋbɩaŋ yaa gamma manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ balɩ wʋbɩaŋ yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ma, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ wɔŋ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Ama ba keŋ yaa nɩ ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me yaa tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋra yaa nyɩŋkʋra jige dɩ ba dii nɩ sarɩya nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ die yaa a gbatɩ nɩ gbaŋ a taaŋ.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka nan dagɩ nɩ wudieke nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Mi daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie kpɩkpaakʋ ma ta balɩ yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, balɩ yɩ n mɩɩ wa, dɩ ʋ vaa maŋ ŋaŋ wa puo faarɩ dieke tɩ chɔɔŋ dɩ vana a zieŋ tɩ wo.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, N zʋa, mɩnɩa yɩna mɩŋ sieŋ dɩ n dii nɩ sarɩya yaa dɩ n puo nɩ faarɩ a yɩ nɩ bale wo gie.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ a yɩ ba mana dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma nyinti yaalɩŋ wɩa, daa nyinti pam bala ka yɩ vuota miivoli.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Womi die ʋ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Nyintitieŋ wʋnyɩ die yalla ʋ kʋaŋ, ta ka yɩa wa nyindiike pam;.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Die ʋ piili a yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ, N baaŋ yiwo lalɩa, dama n wo jigiŋ n baaŋ nan nagɩ n nyindiike mana a yi.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Die ʋ balɩ yɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ, Naa maŋ nan yi; n nan ŋmabɩ n bɔmbɔntɩtɩ ta mɩɩ nyɩŋkpɩɩma, ta nagɩ n zaaha aŋaŋ n nyinti mana a yi mi.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Ta wa balɩ yɩ n gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ, Sikpeŋ nansɩtieŋ, n yaa nyindiike pam n baaŋ nan dii a tʋgɩ bɩna pam. Die wɩa n nan dɩ voose ta die ta nyuo ta yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ gaaŋkʋ gie, jinne yuku gie, baa fʋ waarɩ wa fʋ mɩsɩ, aŋ tɩ ye vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ nagɩ fʋ nyinti gie mana fʋ wʋnsɩna a zie wo.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Tɔ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yaalɩnana nyinti a yɩa ʋ gbaŋ, ama ta wo nyinti Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, nan daansɩ sɩɩ sɩba daa gaaŋkʋ gie.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Die wɩa n bala nɩ dɩ nɩ da vaa nɩ beriŋ mugisi nɩ yaa gamma jadieke nɩ baaŋ nan dii yaa jadieke nɩ baaŋ nan yeegi.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: dama mɩsɩ tɩaŋ nyindiike, ta nyɩŋgbaŋka dɩaŋ dɩ tɩaŋ nyiŋyeeke.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Nɩ daansɩma nembisisi, a ka bʋra yaa a gobe; a dɩaŋ a wo nyindiike bɔmbɔntɩ ama Ŋmɩŋ yɩnana ha nyindiike; nɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ pam a tɩaŋ nembisi?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Nɩ jabɩa baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ bɩna a gʋtɩ ʋ bɩna ma ʋ mɩsɩ wɩa dɩ mugisine wo wɩa?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Nɩ kana ka bɩagɩ a yi wubike gie, bɩa nɩ mugisi nɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ nyintialɩkaha.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Nɩ daansɩ tɩɩwute aŋaŋ a vɩɩnɩŋ; ama a ka tʋma yaa a yaala nyiŋyeeke yɩa a gbaŋ. Ama n bala nɩ, naaŋ Solomoni aŋaŋ ʋ nyinti aŋaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeekivɩɩna mana, die wo jayeekiŋ ka vɩɩna a tʋgɩ tɩɩwutehe kaanɩ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Dɩɩ yi naa, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩa huuti a vɩɩnɩŋ, nyindiekemba dɩ ŋaana beri jinne ama soriŋ boliŋ diihe wo, ʋ kaaŋ yɩ nɩ nyiŋyeeke? Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana a ka dala wa gie!.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Na chɩaŋ ma nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ jadieke nɩ baaŋ nan dii yaa nyuu.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Vuodieke dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ ŋaana chɩga a yaala nyinti gie mana, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nɩ yaala ha mɩŋ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Die wɩa nɩ vaa nɩ baga bemme Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma ta ʋ nan nagɩ nyinti gie mana a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Nɩnɩŋ n vuosi dieke dɩ kana ka dala naa, nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ bala ʋ nagɩ naarɩ dieke a yɩ nɩ wa.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Die wɩa nɩ nagɩ nɩ nyinti mana nɩ yalla a daa a nagɩ ligirehe yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ, ta nɩ nan ye nyinti dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, nyinti nan dɩ bie arɩzanna ma, ta kaaŋ vʋarɩ, dama gaarɩŋ kaaŋ gbigi mi, ta nyɩnyʋgɩka kaaŋ chʋʋsɩha.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Dama jigidieke fʋ nyinti dɩ benne, mi dɩaŋ fʋ sʋŋ dɩ beri.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Nɩ yi siri a gbara wudiekemba dɩ kienene ta nɩ vaa nɩ popolisi chaaŋ.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: sɩba yɔŋɩsɩ diekemba dɩ chɩɩsɩnana ba tieŋ dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ banwʋla ma a keŋ wo, amʋ ʋ keŋ keŋ kpaasɩ sanʋarɩ ba nan yuorike lagɩ lagɩ a yɩ wa.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie yɔŋ diekemba ba tieŋ dɩ kenne a haarɩ ba ta ba chɩɩsa wa. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ba tieŋ wo nan vaa ba kala ta yi ba nyindiike aŋ ba dii.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba tieŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ, tanseese yaa kparaaŋ kpaŋ saŋŋa ta ye ta ba chɩɩsa wa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan dɩ bie ba ma.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Nɩ sɩmma genke, dɩɩ yi tigiŋ tieŋ dɩ sɩba saŋŋa dieke gaarʋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ ʋ tigiŋ me, ʋ kaaŋ vaa gaarʋ wa wɩɩ ʋ tigiri a juu; ʋ nan dɩ gbarɩ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yi siri a gbara, dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan keŋ saŋŋa dieke nɩ kana ka yile dɩ n nan keŋ.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Womi Piita die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩnɩŋ fʋ taana nandagɩrɩ gie a yɩa yaa vuoŋ mana.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Yɔmʋ bɩa yine yɩaŋ tieŋ ta yie ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna dɩ ʋ yi wudieke? Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ nan vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta yɩma ba nyindiike saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ keŋ tigiŋ ta ye wo ta ʋ tʋma die wo gbaŋ gbaŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan dɩ bie ʋ ma.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ʋ nan nagɩ wa a zieŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ nyinti mana mana.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: ama ʋ yi yɔmʋbɩaŋ, ʋ nan dɩ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo kaaŋ keŋ lagɩ lagɩ, ta piili a nɩga ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan dɩ die ta nyu bugi.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: Ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo nan yiŋŋi keŋ daa dieke aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ kana ka yile dɩ ʋ nan keŋ. Ʋ keŋ keŋ, ʋ nan datɩ ʋ tɩbɩŋ pam ta yi wo sɩba ʋ ŋaana yie vuodiekemba dɩ zetinene nʋaŋ die wo.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Yɔmʋ dieke dɩ sɩbɩna jadieke ʋ tieŋ dɩ yaalala ʋ yi, ta ka yi siri dɩ ʋ yi wudieke ʋ tieŋ wo dɩ yaalala, nan ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩbɩtɩ pam.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: ama yɔmʋ dieke dɩ kana ka sɩba wudieke ʋ tieŋ wo dɩ yaalala, ta yi wɩɩŋ chʋʋsɩ, ba nan tɩbɩ wa bɩta ta va wa. Vuodieke ba yɩna pam ba nan pɩasɩ pam a nyɩŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ ba nagɩna pam pam a yi ʋ nuusi me, ba nan pɩasɩ pam a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Manɩŋ n keŋye mɩŋ dɩ keŋ taaŋ boliŋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma, dɩ tɩŋ nan yi mɩŋ nansɩŋ dɩɩ yi dɩ tɩŋgbaŋka wɔŋ die.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ sɩɩla dieke n bala n sɩɩ wa vaa n sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ, sie ka keŋ yi a tɩaŋ.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Nɩ yile dɩ n keŋye mɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma? Aayɩ. N bala nɩ, n keŋye mɩŋ aŋaŋ puole.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: A nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, vuosi banʋ nan dɩ bie tigiŋ me, ta keŋ puo; ba bataa nan chʋʋsɩ bale wo, ta bale wo dɩaŋ chʋʋsɩ bataa wa.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Chʋalɩŋ nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba ballɩ dembisi, ta ballɩ dembisi dɩaŋ keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba chʋalɩŋ; nɩɩlɩŋ dɩaŋ nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba hɔgʋlɩaba ta hɔgʋlɩaba dɩaŋ keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba nɩɩlɩŋ; nɩɩlɩŋ nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba lɩahalɩtɩ, ta lɩahalɩtɩ dɩaŋ nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba nɩɩlɩŋ.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ, Nɩ keŋ yese nɩɩŋmaaŋ ŋmɩŋ nanɩŋ chaaŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ dɩ, nɩɩŋ baa ka nɩɩ mɩŋ aŋ ka seŋ nɩɩ.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Nɩ a keŋ a ye ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ nɩga nyɩnna nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ dɩ jigiri nan dɩ tuuli aŋ ka keŋ yi.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie, nɩ ŋaaŋ bɩagɩ a daansɩ tɩŋgbaŋka aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ wudieke dɩ yinene ta dagɩ a chɩasɩ, ta bɩa yine nɩ ka bɩagɩ sɩba lele saŋka gie chɩasɩ?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Bɩa yine nɩ ka bɩagɩ a sɩba wudieke dɩ mʋna dɩ nɩ yi a yi?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaa fʋ gara naalɩaŋ, kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ aŋ nɩ wʋnsɩ wɩɩrɩ nɩ yene bie sieku me wo, dɩ daa die ʋ nan yaa fʋ ga vuodieke dɩ dinene sarɩyaka jigiŋ aŋ sarɩya diiru wo nagɩ fʋ a yi dansarika dɩdaansɩrɩba jakʋʋŋ nuusi me aŋ ʋ nagɩ fʋ yi dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N bala nɩ, kaaŋ keŋ nyɩŋ mi a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke fʋ baaŋ nan tuŋ ba bala fʋ tuŋ ligire dieke wo mana.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ ʋ dagɩ ba dɩ dɩ mʋ ba jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana, ta da keŋ gbalɩgɩma, die ʋ balɩ ba dɩ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Sarɩya diiru wʋnyɩ die benne tɩŋ kaanɩ ma, die ʋ ka chɩga Ŋmɩŋ ta ka bɩ yɩa vuota jɩlɩma.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Ta kpihɔgʋ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die benne tɩka ma, a chʋŋ gara daa wa jigiŋ a jʋʋsa wa dɩ, Dii n wɩɩrɩ gie a yɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ mugisinene mɩŋ.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ama die sarɩya diiru wo die ka saaga, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ yile dɩ, N ka wɔŋ chɩga Ŋmɩŋ yaa a yɩa vuota jɩlɩma.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: ama kpihɔgʋ wa gie dɩ faasɩna daama mɩŋ wɩa, n ko baa n dii ʋ sarɩya ka a yɩ wa mɩŋ, dɩ daa die ʋ nan dɩ kieŋ daaŋ mana ta vaa n gbalɩgɩ aŋaŋ wa. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ wʋmma wudieke sarɩya diiru wo dɩ kana ka chɩga Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balla.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Ta lele, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ dii sarɩya a yɩ ʋ vuovʋarɩka, vuodiekemba dɩ jʋʋsɩnana wa ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ dɩ ʋ suŋŋi be wo? Ʋ nan dɩ yʋasa dɩ ʋ suŋŋi be?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N bala nɩ, ʋ nan dii sarɩya a yɩ ba, ta yi ke lagɩ lagɩ. Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ keŋ keŋ saŋŋa dieke, n nan ye vuodieke dɩ yine mɩŋ yada tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a taaŋ nandagɩrɩ gie a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ bala dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna, ama ta ka yɩa ba chanchaalɩŋ jɩlɩma.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dembisi bale die nyɩnna a ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ dɩ ba jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ; wʋnyɩ die yiwo Farasisi vuoŋ, wʋnɩŋ wa dɩaŋ dɩ yi lampotuosiru.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Die Farasisi vuoke dɩ ga zie lʋgɩŋ a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ, n waasa fʋ, dama n ka yi tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ sɩba n chanchaalɩba. N ka yi halɩ tieŋ, n ka yi ŋmɩŋchɩbɩtieŋ yaa dembiŋ dieke dɩ yaala hɔɔŋ. N bɩ waasa fʋ dama n ka sɩɩ sɩba lampotuosiru wo gie.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: N ŋaaŋ bɔba nʋaŋ daraa ale daraa yʋpɔyɩ mana sʋŋ, ta yɩa fʋ n nyinti baŋ mana sʋŋ kaanɩ.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Ama die lampotuosiru wo die dɩ ga a zie saasaa, ta ka yaala ʋ kɔtɩ ʋ sikpeŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, ta vigi ʋ sikpeŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ, chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, dama n yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ. .

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, N bala nɩ, die dɩ yiwo lampotuosiru wo Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tuo ʋ jʋʋsɩŋ daa Farasi vuoke; dama vuodieke mana dɩ kɔtɩna ʋ gbaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan sʋʋŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan kɔtɩ wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii ba ballɩnyaalɩsɩ a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a dɩɩsɩ dɩɩsɩ ba ma, ama die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yene naa wa, die dɩ nata ba.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Ta Yisa die dɩ wa ballɩbɩsɩsɩ dɩ ba kiere ʋ jigiŋ, ta balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ vaa ballɩbɩsɩsɩ kiere n jigiŋ ta nɩ da kagɩ ba sieŋ dama ba chanchaalɩŋ sɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka tuo Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩba bʋabiŋ, kaaŋ wɔŋ juu ke.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Saŋka mi jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ vɩɩnɩŋ, lalɩa maŋ nan yi ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa wɩa fʋ wasa mɩŋ vuovɩɩnɩŋ? Vuoŋ wori a yi vuovɩɩnɩŋ, sie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Wɔŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha: Da keŋ yaalɩ vuoŋ hɔgʋ yaa vuoŋ chʋrʋ, da keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ, da keŋ a gaarɩ, da keŋ yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ daansɩatieŋ, yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma. .

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Die daa wa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, N yine bʋabiŋ mana a yaa ga mʋʋna, n dɩɩ mɩraha gie mana mɩŋ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ʋ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Wʋbalɩmɩŋ tɩala fʋ. Nagɩ fʋ nyinti mana a daa ta nagɩ ligirehe a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ, ta nan dɩ yallɩ nyinti arɩzanna ma; ta a kʋaŋ chaaŋ fʋ keŋ dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Ama daa wa die dɩ wʋnna naa, ʋ sʋŋ die dɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ, dama die ʋ faasɩ yallɩ wa nyinti pam.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ʋ sʋŋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, ta baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ tʋa pam dɩ nyintitieliŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Dɩ faasɩ tʋa mɩŋ dɩ nyintitieŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma a tɩaŋ nyɔgɩnɩ dɩ baaŋ nan daagɩ garɩyɩɩŋ vɔrɩŋ ma a nyɩŋ dene.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ wʋnna wa wa die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Die nɩŋ manɩa baaŋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wudieke vuota dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi, Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Ta Piita dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Ye, die tɩ va wa tɩ nyinti mana ta keŋ dɩ fʋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ vana ʋ tigiŋ yaa ʋ hɔgʋ yaa ʋ nɩmballɩ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ nuŋ yaa ʋ ballɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: nan bɩ ye he pam tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a tɩaŋ ʋ vana nyindiekemba ba, saŋŋa dieke dɩ kienene wo, ʋ nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yaa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ wʋmma, tɩ gara Jerusalemi, jigidieke ba bala ba yi manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wudiekemba mana die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩ maagɩna yaa gamma n wɩa wa.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: dama ba nan nagɩ mɩŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi nuusi me, dɩ ba vʋarɩ mɩŋ fala ta bɩ zɩa mɩŋ ta tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yi mɩŋ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: a nɩgɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kumbu me.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Ama die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ka sɩba wɩaha kaanɩ gbaŋ chɩaŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ die ligi wo ba yɩaŋ, die yine die ba ka sɩba wudieke ʋ balala wa chɩasɩ.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yisa die dɩ kenne gbige Jeriko daa yɩɩ wʋnyɩ die dɩ kalɩ sieku nʋaŋ a jʋʋsa.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Die ʋ wʋnna ta kpɩkpaakʋ chʋŋ tɩanna wa, die ʋ pɩasɩ dɩ, Bɩa yinene.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Die ba balɩ wa dɩ, Yisa, vuodieke dɩ yine Nazeriti vuoke tɩannana.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Die ʋ faasɩ wa dɩ, Yisa, Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ama vuodiekemba die dɩ benne nɩŋka die dɩ natɩ wa ta balɩ wa dɩ ʋ tarɩ watɩ, ama die ʋ faasɩ nata dɩ, Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ zie ta yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaa daa wa a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die ʋ kenne a gbigi wo, Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Bɩa fʋ yaala n yi a yɩ fʋ? Die ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n yaala fʋ yuori n ninehe mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Mi Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Yesime, fʋ yine yada ka wɩa fʋ ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Lele womi die ʋ ninehe dɩ yuori, die ʋ dɩ Yisa ta bɩra Ŋmɩŋ. Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yene wo wo, die ba mana dɩ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ daansɩ tɩaŋ ye nyintitieliŋ dɩ nagɩnana piinisi a yie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ligire kpalɩ ma;.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: ta die bɩ ye kpihɔgʋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ gbaŋ dɩ nagɩna kobusi ligiribie ale a yie kpalɩ ma.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Ta Yisa die baarɩ dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, kpihɔgʋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ wo gie nagɩ wa pam a yi a tɩaŋ vuoŋ mana.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: dama ba mana vʋarɩ a nyɩŋ wa ba nyinti pam ma, ama kpihɔgʋ zɔɔlɩntieŋ wo gie nɩŋ nagɩ wa jadieke ʋ bala ʋ dii a keŋ yi.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Die bataŋ die dɩ bala yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ wɩa, die ba bala yaa gamma ka vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ tanvɩɩna aŋaŋ piini dieke ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ. Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Nɩ yene nyinti gie mana wa, ama saŋŋa kieŋ ta taŋ balɩmɩŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ wɩarɩ ka jigiberisikiŋ; a mana nan nan tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Die ʋ bala wɩaha gie die ba pɩasɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, saŋŋa bɩa naa dɩ baa dɩ yi? Ta bɩa balla ka yi a dagɩ dɩ saŋka tʋgɩya mɩŋ dɩ ka yi.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma ta nɩ da keŋ vaa ba gaaŋ nɩ. Vuosi pam nan keŋ aŋaŋ n saaŋ a baarɩ Manɩŋ n yine Masia wa ta saŋka gbigiye mɩŋ. Ama nɩ da keŋ dɩa ba.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Nɩ nan wʋŋ tɔntɩ aŋaŋ waagɩŋ wɩa, ama nɩ da keŋ vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dama sie wɩaha gie woliŋ yi ama a ka dagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ nan keŋ lele ke gie.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Die ʋ bɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Buuriŋ nan wagɩ buuriŋ, ta naalɩŋ dɩaŋ wagɩ taŋ.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Tɩŋgbaŋ dɔmɩŋ aŋaŋ kɔŋ aŋaŋ yʋagɩbɩatɩ nan keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie lʋga mana mana. Vuosi nan ye mamachi wʋkpɩɩma dɩ bala ka vaa ŋmaamɩŋ juu be ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Ama ta geŋhe mana keŋ yiwo, ba nan yigi nɩ ta vaa nɩ dii wahala, ta nagɩ nɩ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone nyɩŋkʋra aŋ ba dii nɩ sarɩya, ta nagɩ nɩ yi dansarɩka sʋŋ; ta bɩ nagɩ nɩ a ga naalɩŋ jige aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋra jigiŋ n wɩa.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Naa bala ka yɩ nɩ sieŋ aŋ nɩ balɩ n wɩa a yɩ ba.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Die wɩa nɩ yi siri ta da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ wudiekemba a gbatɩ nɩ gbaŋ a taaŋ;.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: dama saŋka mi n nan yɩ nɩ yɩaŋ aŋaŋ wudieke nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ, amʋ nɩ dataasɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ nyaŋŋɩ nɩ wʋbalɩka yaa a chiisi nɩ.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Ama nɩ chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ nɩɩlɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ nɩ tigiŋ vuosi aŋaŋ nɩ zʋalɩŋ nan keŋ a nyɩŋ nɩ kʋaŋ a posi nɩ chɩaŋ, aŋ ba kʋʋ nɩ bataŋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Ta vuoŋ mana nan keŋ haa nɩ, dama nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Ama nɩ sikpeŋ zuobalɩmɩŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ bɩa.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ka zeti n kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ nɩ nan keŋ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Ama saŋŋa dieke nɩ keŋ ye ta sojasi taaŋ giliŋ Jerusalemi, nɩ nan dɩ sɩba a baarɩ ka chʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Saŋka mi vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia tɩŋ ma chɩgɩ ga tana sikpeŋ; ta vuodiekemba dɩ benne Jerusalemi chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ benne tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩsɩ dɩaŋ da a keŋ a juu tɩka ma.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: dama saŋka mi yiwo tɩbɩdatɩŋ saŋŋa. Wɩaha gie mana nan keŋ yi sɩba die dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma die wo.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Saŋka mi wʋbɩaŋ nan dɩ bie hɔgʋ nyuutitieliŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ diekemba dɩ yalla ballɩnyaalɩsɩ, dama wahala pam balla ka keŋ tɩka gie me, ta Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ nan keŋ vuosi gie me.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Ta sojasi nan keŋ kʋʋ bataŋ aŋaŋ takoobi, ta nagɩ bataŋ a ga tɩgɩsɩ a va ba bɩrɩŋ yɔŋɩsɩ. Ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi nan tɩbɩ tɩbɩ ta dɩdɩa Jerusalemi ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ba saŋŋa dɩ baaŋ kpatɩ.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Ta dagɩtɩ nan dɩ bie ŋmɩnnɩ ma aŋaŋ chɩɩkʋ ma aŋaŋ chɩŋmarɩsɩ ma. Ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie me sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ nan keŋ tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ mana ma, ta ŋmaamɩŋ nan dɩ beri yaa gamma mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ poŋŋiku wɩa.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Ta nyindiekemba dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ nan dɔndɔŋ a lagɩŋ taŋ, die wɩa ŋmaamɩŋ nan juu vuosi a va ba chaa, ta nan dɩ chɩɩsa wudieke dɩ kienene tɩŋgbaŋka ma wa.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Saŋka mi ba nan ye manɩŋ vuota bʋa dɩ nyɩnnana nɩɩŋmara sʋŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ aŋaŋ chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Wɩaha gie dɩ keŋ piili a yie, nɩ yigi sikimiŋ dama nɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Yisa die dɩ taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie dɩ, Nɩ daansɩ chunchuleke tɩɩka aŋaŋ tɩɩsɩ mana.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Nɩ keŋ ye tɩɩsɩsɩ vaatɩ ta a tʋsa nɩ ŋaaŋ mɩŋŋɩ mɩŋ dɩ yɩbɩŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ keŋ ye ta wɩaha gie yie, nɩ nan dɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, leleke gie vuosi kaaŋ kpi a kpatɩ ta wɩaha gie mana keŋ yi.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nan keŋ tɩaŋ ga, ama n wʋbalɩka kaaŋ tɩaŋ ga naa daa.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Nɩ gbara nɩ gbaŋ ta da keŋ a wɩarɩ dʋʋga diile me aŋaŋ daaŋ nyuule me aŋaŋ wumugisike me, dɩ daa die, n keŋku nan keŋ pirigi nɩ ma.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Dɩnda daarɩ nan nan bubi tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana sɩba taŋ chachabɩ.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Nɩ gbara ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana dɩ ʋ yɩ nɩ hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ baaŋ nan va nɩ nyaŋŋɩ wɩaha gie mana dɩ balla a yi wo. Ta bɩ bɩagɩ a zie manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa nɩŋŋa.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Die daaŋ mana Yisa die ŋaaŋ bie wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ta jɩŋmɩŋ mana ʋ ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ a bie wo kunkogi dieke ba wasɩnana Olivisi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me;.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: ama sʋkʋʋŋ mana vuosi mana ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ mɩŋ a ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a ga wʋmma ʋ wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Die daadamba mana dɩ hagɩ a yaa Yisa a ga naaŋ Paliti jigiŋ.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: mi die ba piili a bala a chʋʋsa wa dɩ, Tɩ ye daa wa gie dɩ balala tɩ vuosi dɩ ba da tuŋ lampo a yɩ naaŋ Siiza ta bɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia ta bɩ yi naaŋ.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Die wɩa Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa? Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Sɩba fʋ balla die wo.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Womi Paliti die dɩ balɩ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ, Manɩŋ n ka ye daa wa gie dɩ chʋʋsɩna die.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Ama die ba faasɩ a kpaŋŋɩ ba gbaŋ dɩ, Ʋ dagɩkʋ vaa wa vuosi dɩ yie watɩ aŋaŋ waagɩŋ. Die ʋ woliŋ piili wo Galili me ta die ga Judia tɩgɩsɩ mana mana ta wa keŋ giena.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Die Paliti die dɩ wʋnna naa ʋ pɩasɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie yiwo Galili vuoŋ.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ Yisa nyɩŋ wa Galili tɩŋgbaŋ dieke Herodi dɩ daansɩnana wa, die ʋ vaa ba yaa wa ga Herodi jigiŋ, dama wʋnɩŋ Herodi die bie wo Jerusalemi kanɩŋ saŋka mi.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Die Herodi ŋaaŋ wʋmma Yisa wɩa, ta die yaala ʋ ye wo. Die wɩa die ʋ yene wo wo ʋ sʋŋ die dɩ fɩalɩŋ, ta die yaala Yisa yi mamachi wɩɩŋ aŋ ʋ ye.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ pɩasɩ Yisa wʋpɩasɩka pam, ama ʋ ka tɔgɩŋ wa.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die zie mi ta faasɩ bala a chʋʋsa Yisa.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Die Herodi aŋaŋ ʋ sojasisi die dɩ vʋarɩ Yisa fala ta yiwo wʋbɩatɩ pam, ta die nagɩ garɩvɩɩnɩŋ a yeegi wo ta nagɩ wa yiŋŋi ga Paliti jigiŋ.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Die dɩnda daarɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ Herodi aŋaŋ Paliti dɩ yi zʋalɩŋ, dama die ba yiwo dataasɩ aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Paliti die dɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ ba tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ vuosisi.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yalla daa wa gie a keŋ n jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ ʋ gaama vuosi ba waga wagɩtɩ, ama lele n dii wo ʋ sarɩya nɩ nɩŋŋa ta ka ye wudieke nɩ bala dɩ ʋ yi chʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Herodi gbaŋ ka ye wudieke ʋ yine chʋʋsɩ, die wɩa ʋ vaa ba yiŋŋi nagɩ wa a keŋ n jigiri. Nɩ yeye? Daa wa gie ka yi wɩɩŋ mana amʋ kuŋ.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Die wɩa n nan yi ba tɩbɩ wa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta vaa ʋ ga.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ mana dɩ natɩ dɩ, Kʋʋ daa wa gie. Vʋarɩ Barabasi a yɩ tɩ yɔrɩ.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Die ba nagɩ wa Barabasi a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ dama ʋ vaa wa watɩ aŋaŋ waagɩŋ dɩ nan tɩka sʋŋ aŋaŋ ʋ vuosi kʋʋla wɩa.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Ama Paliti die yaala ʋ vaa wa Yisa a taaŋ yɔrɩ die wɩa die ʋ bɩ balɩ yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ bɩbra.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Ama ta ba ye ko nata dɩ, Kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩrɩ ma, Kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩrɩ ma.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ ba ʋ bʋtaa ma dɩ, Bɩa wʋbɩaŋ ʋ yi? Manɩŋ n ka ye wudieke ʋ yine amʋ kuŋ. Die wɩa n nan vaa ba tɩbɩ wa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta vaa ʋ ga.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Ama die ba ye ko nata aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩma dɩ ba kpaasɩ Yisa a diisi dagarɩkɩrɩ ma; die ba natɩkʋ die dɩ keŋ nyaŋŋɩ Paliti.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Die wɩa Paliti die dɩ yɩ ba sieŋ dɩ ba yi ba dʋŋŋʋ.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Die ʋ vʋarɩ daa dieke ba yaalala wa a taaŋ, vuodieke die dɩ vana watɩ aŋaŋ waagɩŋ dɩ nan tɩka ma wa ta bɩ kʋʋ vuosisi, ta nagɩ Yisa a yɩ ba dɩ ba yi wo ba dʋŋŋʋ.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Die sojasisi die dɩ nagɩ Yisa a ga, die ba ganana wa ba ga tuoli daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Simoni, vuodieke die dɩ nyɩnna Sayireni, die ʋ nyɩŋ wa tɩŋkpaŋa a keŋ juo Jerusalemi sʋŋ. Die ba yigi wo, a nagɩ Yisa dagarɩkɩrɩ a chii wo ta vaa ʋ chiike a dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Die daadaaŋ pam die dɩdɩ Yisa kʋaŋ, die hɔɔŋ bataŋ die gʋtɩna ba ma a kʋma ʋ wɩa.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Jerusalemi hɔɔŋ, nɩ da kʋmma n wɩa ama nɩ kʋmma nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ wɩa aŋaŋ nɩ ballɩ.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Dama saŋŋa kieŋ vuosi dɩ nan baarɩ dɩ, Hɔgʋ pɩrɩsɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka mɩɩrɩ ballɩ ta yɩ ba bɩɩsɩŋ banɩŋ ba yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Kanɩŋ saŋka mi vuosi dɩ nan balɩ a yɩ tana dɩ, Nan tɩ ma. Ta balɩ yɩ kunkogehe dɩ Nan tɩ ma a ligi tɩ.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Dama dɩɩ yi ba yie naa a yɩa mɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ, die nɩŋ lalɩa ba baa ba yi vuodiekemba dɩ yine a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Die ba nagɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ bale gbaŋ a gʋtɩ Yisa ma dɩ ba ga kʋʋ ba.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Die ba kenne a tʋgɩ jigidieke die ba wasɩnana Sikpeŋkogilike jigiŋ, mi die ba kpaasɩ Yisa a diisi dagarɩkɩrɩ ma, ta die kpaasɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ bale wo dagarɩsɩ ma, ta kaanɩ die dɩ bie ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ ta kaanɩ dɩaŋ ʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, nagɩ chaa vuosi gie, dama ba zɩ ba yinene wudieke wo chɩaŋ. Die ba puo ʋ gaatɩ a tɩa taŋ aŋaŋ chaachaa.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Die vuosi dɩ zie mi a daansa ta Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ vʋara wa fala dɩ, Die ʋ gbatɩ wa vuosi a taaŋ, dɩɩ yi ʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa, ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Sojasisi gbaŋ die dɩ vʋara wa fala; die ba nagɩ daŋmɩɩsɩŋ a yɩ wa.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: ta baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ yi Juu vuosi naaŋ, fʋ gbatɩ fʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Die ba maagɩ maagɩŋ a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma dɩ, Juu Vuosisi Naaŋ Wa Wɔnna. .

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Saŋka mi tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tielibe wʋnyɩ die ba kpaasɩna a gʋtɩ wa die dɩ taana wa jome dɩ, Da fʋ yine Masia wa? Gbatɩ fʋ gbaŋ aŋaŋ tɩ a taaŋ.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Ama wʋnyɩ wa die dɩ galɩŋ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ ka chɩga Ŋmɩŋ? Dama tɩ mana bie wo tɩbɩdatɩ balɩmɩŋ ma.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ tɩbɩdatɩkʋ mʋ mɩŋ, dama wudieke tɩ yine wo tune tɩ tuose dene, ama daa wa gie nɩŋ ka yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Ta die a balɩ yɩ Yisa dɩ, Yisa, tɩɩnsɩ n wɩa saŋŋa dieke fʋ keŋ dii fʋ naarɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, jinne manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ nan dɩ bie Paradasi ma.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Die dɩ yiwo ŋmɩntʋasɩ, ŋmɩnnɩ die dɩ ka chaana ta tɩŋgbaŋka mana die dɩ sibi a ga tʋgɩ ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ gbɩlɩnna bimbilimiŋ ataa saŋŋa;.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: ta die garɩ dieke die dɩ sagɩna Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋkʋ die dɩ puo bule.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Yisa die dɩ keŋ a keesi aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ a baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, fʋ nuusi me maŋ naga n haalɩŋ yie. Die ʋ balɩ wa naa ta kpi.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ yene wudieke dɩ yine wo die ʋ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, daa wa gie seŋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Die vuodiekemba mana dɩ lagɩsɩna mi dɩ ba daansɩ wudieke dɩ yinene wo dɩ yene wudiekemba dɩ yine wo ba mana die dɩ yiŋŋi ga tigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Ama ʋ zʋalɩba mana aŋaŋ hɔgʋ diekemba dɩ dɩna wa a nyɩŋ Galili wo die dɩ zie saasaa a daansa wudiekemba mana dɩ yinene wo.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ die benne ba wasa wa Josefu, die ʋ nyɩŋ wa Juu vuosi tɩŋ ba wasɩnana Arimatia. Die ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ta bɩ dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die a chɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keniŋ.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: Die ʋ yiwo Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra wʋnyɩ, ama die ʋ ka saagɩ wudieke ʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ sanna wa ta yi wo.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Die ʋ ga Paliti nɩŋŋa a ga jʋʋsɩ wa Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka, a yallɩ garɩ haalɩyɩalɩŋ a veli wo a yaa ka a ga dʋaŋ vɔrɩhaalɩ dieke ba votine taŋ ma, ta ye ka guu vuoŋ mi.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Die dɩ yiwo Alɩzuma daraaŋ ta davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ dɩ yaala ka piili.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Hɔgʋ diekemba die dɩ dɩna Yisa a nyɩŋ Galili wo die dɩ dɩ Josefu a ga a ye vɔrɩkʋ aŋaŋ ba dʋana Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka dene ka sʋŋ.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi ga tigiŋ a ga wʋnsɩ kpaanyʋga aŋaŋ tulaarisi. Die ba voosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ sɩba ba mɩraha dɩ dagɩna dene wo.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Die Alasiri daraaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ, ta hɔgʋba ha die dɩ ga vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ aŋaŋ ba tulaari diekemba ba wʋnsɩna wa.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Die ba kenne tʋgɩ vɔrɩkʋ jigiri die ba ye ta taŋ dieke ba nagɩna a ligi vɔrɩkʋ nʋarɩ biliŋye.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: die wɩa die ba juu vɔrɩkʋ sʋŋ ama die ba ka ye tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Die ba zene a yile wɩɩrɩ gie wo, dembisi bale die dɩ keŋ zie ba jigiŋ bʋnyɩ ta yeegi nyiŋyeeke dɩ nyɩgɩsɩnana sɩba nɩɩnyɩgɩsɩŋ;.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi hɔgʋba ha ta ba sʋʋŋ gbirigi tɩŋgbaŋ, ama dembisi bale wo die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ dɩ dɩa yaala vuodieke dɩ benne ʋ mɩsɩ ma kunti jigiŋ?.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Ʋ wo giena, ʋ hagɩya. Nɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke die ʋ balla a yɩ nɩ wa saŋŋa dieke ʋ benne Galili me wo;.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: dɩ Ba nan yigi wʋnɩŋ vuota Bʋa a nagɩ yi vuobɩatɩ nuusi me aŋ ba kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma aŋ ʋ kpi; ama ʋ nan hagɩ kuŋ me ka daraa ataa daraaŋ. .

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Womi die hɔgʋbaha dɩ tɩɩnsɩ ʋ wʋbalɩka ha.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: ta die nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ a yiŋŋi kuli, a balɩ wɩaha gie mana a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne mi mana.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Die hɔgʋba ha saara die wone Meri Magidalini aŋaŋ Juanna aŋaŋ Jemisi nuŋ Meri; banɩŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ diekemba die dɩ gʋtɩna ba ma wa die balɩna wɩaha gie a yɩ Yisa tʋntʋntɩba.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Ama tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yile wudieke ba balala wa yiwo wʋtɔgʋ ta die ka tuo be dii.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ hagɩ a chɩgɩ ga vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ; a sʋʋŋ a zʋʋrɩ a daansɩ ta ka ye jaaŋ mana sie kumbu garɩkʋ nyɩɩna ma. Ta die a yiŋŋi ga tigiŋ, ta wudieke dɩ yine wo dɩ paalɩ yi wo mamachi.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Die dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi gbaŋ gbaŋ ba sʋŋ vuosi die dɩ gara tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Emawusi, kanɩŋ aŋaŋ Jerusalemi die yiwo mali ayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Die ba chʋnnana wa, die ba die azama yaa gamma wudiekemba mana dɩ yine wo.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Die ba dinene azama aŋaŋ taŋ die wo, Yisa gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ keŋ gbigi be a chʋŋ aŋaŋ ba;.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: ama Ŋmɩŋ die chɔrɩna ba, ba ka mɩŋŋɩ Yisa.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ chʋŋ bala? Die ba yiŋŋi a zie zie aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ die ba wasɩnana Kulopasi dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ nyɩɩna ma fʋ yine chaanʋ a bie Jerusalemi ka gie me ta ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yinene daraa gie nʋaŋ.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Die ʋ pɩasɩ dɩ, Wʋbɩaŋtɩ? Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Yaa gamma Nazeriti vuoke Yisa wa; die daa wa gie die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa ta yaa hagɩrɩŋ pam ʋ wʋbalɩka ma aŋaŋ ʋ tʋʋma ma Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ vuota nɩŋŋa.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Ta tɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ nagɩ wa yɩ sarɩya diiriŋ ba dii wo sarɩya ta baarɩ dɩ ʋ mʋ dɩ ʋ kpi; die ba kpaasɩ wa a diisi dagarɩkɩrɩ ma.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Ama tɩnɩŋ die tɩ yaa yada dɩ ʋ balla ʋ yi vuodieke dɩ baaŋ gbatɩ tɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi a nyɩŋ tɩ dataasɩ nuusi me. A bɩ gʋtɩ, wɩaha dɩ yine ka daraa ataa wʋnna.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Die wɩa gbaŋ tɩ sʋŋ hɔgʋba bataŋ balɩ tɩ wa wɩɩŋ ka paalɩ yi tɩ mamachi, dama ba ga wa vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ sʋkʋleeliku gie.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: ama ba ka ye ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka. Ba yiŋŋine a keŋ wo, ba baarɩ dɩ banɩŋ ba ye wo Ŋmɩŋ malakasi aŋaŋ ba nine; ta malakasi dɩ balɩ ba dɩ Yisa bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Die wɩa tɩ bataŋ dɩ ga vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ a ga ye wudieke gbaŋ gbaŋ hɔgʋba dɩ balla wa ama banɩŋ ba ka ye Yisa.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa yine nɩ ka sɩba nyinti gie chɩasɩ? Lalɩa nɩ sʋnsɩ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan tuo wudiekemba mana Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩ balla wa a dii!.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa dii wahalaha gie ta wa ye jɩlɩma.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Womi Yisa die piili a dagɩ ba wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ mana dɩ balla yaa gamma ʋ wɩa, ta piili Mosisi dɩ maagɩna wudiekemba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba maagɩŋ mana.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Die ba kenne a gbigi tɩŋkpaŋŋɩ dieke banɩŋ ba ganana wa, Yisa die dɩ paalɩ yi sɩba ʋ tɩaŋ gara nɩŋŋa.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: ama die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ, Bemme giena aŋaŋ tɩ, dama ŋmɩnnɩ wɔŋ nanna mɩŋ ta jigiri dɩaŋ dɩ sibe. Die wɩa die ʋ juu a wɩarɩ ba jigiŋ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Die ʋ ga kalɩ dɩ ʋ dii nyindiike aŋaŋ ba, a die nagɩ paanʋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yi ke gbieri gbieri a nagɩ yɩa ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Womi die ba nine dɩ yuori die ba mɩŋŋɩ wa. Lele wo mi die ʋ nyɩɩgɩ ba nine me.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Die ba bala a yɩa taŋ dɩ, Die ʋ balala tɩ sieku me a daga tɩ wudiekemba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die wo, die tɩ ka yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Die ba hagɩ bʋnyɩ a yiŋŋi ga Jerusalemi a ga haarɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa aŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna taŋ.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ seŋ a hagɩ kuŋ me aŋaŋ wusie, ta nyɩŋ Simoni jigiŋ.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Womi vuosi bale wo gie dɩaŋ dɩ balɩ ba wudieke mana dɩ yine sieku me aŋaŋ ba mɩŋŋɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ yine paanʋkʋ gbieri gbieri die wo.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Die ba yine ko bala wɩaha gie yɩa ba wa, lele wo mi Yisa gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ keŋ zie ba sʋnsʋŋ ta balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme nɩ ma.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Die ba cheele aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ, dama die ba yile dɩ ba ye wo kogiŋ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Ama ta die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ sɩkpara dɩ nɩga? Bɩa chɩɩlɩŋ dɩ bie nɩ ma?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Nɩ daansɩ ye n nuusi aŋaŋ n nachɩgɩsa a ye dɩ dɩ yiwo manɩŋ wusie. Nɩ gbɩ mɩŋ a ye dɩ dɩ yiwo manɩŋ. Kogiŋ wo nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋaŋ kɔba, sɩba nɩ yene mɩŋ n yalla nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋaŋ kɔba die wo.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Die ʋ balla wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusisi aŋaŋ ʋ nachɩgɩsa a dagɩ ba.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Ama die ba ye ka tuo dii dɩ dɩ yiwo wʋnɩŋ; dama die dɩ paalɩ yi be wo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ mamachi dɩ wɩɩrɩ yiwo wusie. Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yaa nyindiike giena a baaŋ nan dii.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Die ba nagɩ zaasɩ dieke ba digine a yɩ wa.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: die ʋ nagɩ a ŋɔbɩ ba nɩŋŋa.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Ta die a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ tɩɩnsɩma n wʋbalɩka die n balla a yɩ nɩ saŋŋa dieke die n benne nɩ jigiri dɩ wɩaha mana ba maagɩna Mosisi gbaŋkʋ ma wa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba gbaŋtɩ ma wa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ dieke ba wasɩnana yɩla gbaŋkʋ, dɩ dɩ yi talasɩ dɩ a mana keŋ yi wusie.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Die ʋ wa yuori ba yɩaŋ dɩ ba sɩmma wudieke dɩ maagɩna wa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa chɩasɩ.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: ta balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya dɩ Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa nan dii wahala ta kpi ta hagɩ kuŋ me ka daraa ataa daraaŋ.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: dɩ ba mʋʋlɩ a yɩ vuosi ʋ saaŋ ma, dɩɩ yi ba vaa ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa a chaa ba; dɩ ba woliŋ a mʋʋlɩma wɩaha a yɩma Jerusalemi vuosi ta bɩ mʋʋlɩ a yɩ buuriŋ mana.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Die ʋ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine wɩaha gie daansɩa tieliŋ.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: Manɩŋ n baaŋ yɩ nɩ wa N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die ʋ yɩna nʋarɩ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ nɩ wa. Ama nɩ chɩɩsɩma Jerusalemi ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ balla ʋ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ nɩ ma wa.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yaa ba nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ a yaa ba ga Betani, mi die ʋ kɔtɩ ʋ nuusi ba ma a yi alibarika a yɩ ba.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Die ʋ yinene alibarika a yɩa ba wa, die ʋ nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ ta jʋalɩ ga arɩzanna ma.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Die ba jɩaŋ wa ta die yiŋŋi a ga Jerusalemi aŋaŋ sʋgʋfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Ta die a bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma saŋŋa mana a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Die saŋka die dɩ gbigi dɩ ba dii dʋʋga dieke ba ŋaana ŋɔba paanʋ dieke ba kana ka nagɩ dabɔtɩ a yi wo, die ba wasa kanɩŋ dʋʋgaka mi gasɩtɩanɩŋ dʋʋga.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: ta die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ mɩa a yaala sieti dɩ ba kʋʋ Yisa, ama die ba chɩga daadamba ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Womi Sitaani die dɩ juu Judasi vuodieke die ba wasɩnana Asikaroti ta bɩ yi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Die wɩa wʋnɩŋ Judasi die dɩ ga balɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ ba sojasi nyɩŋkʋraha ʋ baaŋ nan yi die a vaa ba yigi Yisa.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Die ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ fɩalɩ ta die ba baarɩ ba nan yɩ wa ligire.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Die Judasi die dɩ saagɩ ta wa piili a yaala sie dieke ʋ baaŋ nan nagɩ Yisa a yi ba nuusi me saŋŋa dieke kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ worine.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Die ba dinene paanʋ dieke ba kana ka nagɩ dabɔtɩ a yi wo saŋŋa dieke ba ŋaana kʋa yiisehe a yie gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiikehe dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Yisa die dɩ tʋŋ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ nʋarɩ gie dɩ, Nɩ ga a yi tɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiikehe siri aŋ tɩ dii.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Sɩa fʋ yaala tɩ ga a yi he siri.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ wʋmma, nɩ ganana tɩka sʋkʋ nɩ nan haarɩ daa wʋnyɩ dɩ chine jaaŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, nɩ dɩa wa a ga juu tigidieke me ʋ junene wo.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: a balɩ tigiri tieŋ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ wa baarɩ, chaamba juodieke manɩŋ aŋaŋ n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ balla tɩ dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ nyindiikehe bie sɩa.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Ʋ nan dagɩ juokpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ benne sikpeŋ me a yallɩ jaaŋ mana a gbarɩ, mi nɩ nan yi nyindiikehe.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Die ba nyɩŋ ga a ye jaaŋ mana sɩba Yisa die dɩ balla ba wa die wo, die wɩa die ba yi gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka nyindiikehe mi.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Die gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiikehe diile saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩna, Yisa die dɩ ga kalɩ aŋaŋ ʋ tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo dɩ ba dii.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N paalɩ yaala mɩŋ dɩ n dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ gie dʋʋga nyindiikehe aŋaŋ nɩ ta wahalaka ye keŋ n ma.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: N bala nɩ, n kaaŋ bɩ dii he bɩbra a yaa ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke wusie diile he dɩ bala dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Womi die ʋ tuo nyaaŋ chibiŋ aŋaŋ daaŋ ta bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ tuo geŋke a tɩa yɩ taŋ a nyuu;.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: N bala nɩ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, n kaaŋ bɩ nyuu daabʋ gie sie Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keŋ keŋ.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Die ʋ bɩ nagɩ paanʋ ta bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta gbieri gbieri ke a nagɩ yɩ ba ta baarɩ dɩ, N nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wʋnna maŋ baa n nagɩ aŋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ nɩ wɩa. Nɩ yime naa a tɩɩnsɩma n wɩa.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba dine kpatɩ wa ʋ nagɩ nyaaŋchibike aŋaŋ daabʋ a baarɩ dɩ, Nyaaŋchibike aŋaŋ daabʋ gie yiwo Ŋmɩŋ nʋahaalɩ dieke n ziene aŋaŋ n zɩmbʋ dɩ nyɩnna wa a yɩ nɩ.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Ama nɩ ye, vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ posi n chɩaka kalɩ wa gie a lagɩŋ a die aŋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan seŋ kpi sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sanna die wo, ama wʋbɩaŋ bie vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ posi n chɩaka.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Womi die ba piili a pɩasa tamba ba jabɩa balla ʋ yi die.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Die nɩnhagɩrɩŋ die dɩ nan ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋnsʋŋ dɩ ba jabɩa balla ʋ yi jakʋʋŋ.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Tɩŋgbaŋka gie naalɩŋ ŋaaŋ pɔgɩlɩ ba vuosi aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta vuodiekemba dɩ yine tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋra yaala ba wasɩma ba vuoŋ mana zʋa.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ dɩ ka baa dɩ sɩma die. Vuodieke dɩ yine vuokpɩɩŋ nɩ sʋnsʋŋ, sie ʋ yi sɩba bʋabiŋ, ta nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa yi sɩba tʋntʋntʋ.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Mɩnɩa yine vuokpɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ kala a die yaa vuodieke dɩ tɩanana nyindiikehe? Dɩ yiwo vuodieke dɩ kala die wo, ama manɩŋ n bie nɩ sʋnsʋŋ sɩba tʋntʋntʋ wa.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine vuodiekemba dɩ benne n jigiŋ n wumugisikehe ma saŋŋa ma ma.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: N chʋa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ kalɩnna mɩŋ naarɩ gie wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan yɩ nɩ kanɩŋ naarɩ mi.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Nɩ nan dii ta nyuu aŋaŋ manɩŋ n naarɩ ma, ta kalɩ naagbaŋtɩ ma a dii Izara buurisi baŋ aŋaŋ ale wo sarɩya.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
