local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ balɩ yɩ Simoni Piita dɩ, Simoni, Simoni, wʋmma, Sitaani jʋʋsa wa sieŋ dɩ ʋ magɩsɩ nɩ ta chaarɩ nɩ sɩba ba ŋaana a chaarɩ zaa aŋ ʋ ye dɩ nɩ yiwo zaa bie yaa zaavɔntɩ.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Ama fʋnɩŋ Simoni n jʋʋsɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ fʋ dɩ fʋ yadaka da keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, ta fʋ keŋ a yiŋŋi a keŋ n jigiŋ fʋ kpaŋŋɩsɩma fʋ nɩmballɩlɩ.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, n yi siri dɩ n juu dansarɩka aŋaŋ fʋ, ta kpi aŋaŋ fʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Piita, n bala fʋ, kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma ta fʋnɩŋ chiisi wo bʋtaa dɩ zɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Die n tʋnna nɩ die nɩ ga ta ka pɔgɩlɩ ligire jasʋʋkɩŋ aŋaŋ bʋlɔgɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩɩraha die nɩ waarɩ jaaŋ mɩŋ? Die ba yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ama lele nɩŋ, vuodieke mana dɩ yalla ligire jasʋʋkɩŋ yaa bʋlɔgɩŋ ʋ nagɩ ka, ta vuodieke dɩ kana ka yallɩ takoobi, ʋ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiŋ a daa a daa kaanɩ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: N bala nɩ, dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ Ba nagɩ wa mɩŋ a gʋtɩ vuobɩatɩ ma. Die wɩa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nan yi mɩŋ, dama wudieke mana ba maagɩna yaa gamma n ma Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa nan keŋ yi wusie.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, ye, takoobisi ale wʋnna. Die ʋ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ mʋ.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Die ʋ nyɩŋ mi a ga Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiŋ me sɩba ʋ ŋaana yie die wo; ta die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a ga.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Die ʋ tʋgɩna mi wo, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ amʋ nɩ da keŋ juu magɩsɩŋ ma.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Die ʋ chʋŋ ga ba nɩŋŋa bɩta a sʋʋŋ gbirigi a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ dʋŋŋʋ fʋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ wʋbɩa dieke dɩ kienene n ma naa, ama vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi da manɩŋ n dʋŋŋʋ.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Womi malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma a keŋ a kpaŋŋɩsɩ wa.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Die ʋ benne sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ma wa die ʋ faasɩ a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, die ʋ walɩŋ die dɩ nanna tɩŋgbaŋ sɩba zɩŋ.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Die ʋ kenne hagɩ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩkʋ ma wa die ʋ yiŋŋi ga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ, ta ye ta ba gʋʋra, dama sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ die vana ba gbalɩgɩ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa wɩa nɩ gʋʋra? Nɩ hagɩ a jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ amʋ nɩ da keŋ a juu magɩsɩŋ ma.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Yisa die ye ko bala wɩa ta ye kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ kienene. Ta Judasi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩna ba nɩŋŋa, a die keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ʋ mʋgɩsɩ ʋ kaamɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Ama ta die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Judasi, mʋgɩsɩ wa n kaamɩŋ dɩ fʋ nyɩŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa kʋaŋ.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yene wudieke dɩ bala ka yiwo, ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ wagɩ ba aŋaŋ takoobisi.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ama ta ba wʋnyɩ dɩ wɔŋ yi lagɩ a gobi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ yɔmʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ tɩbɩŋ a taaŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ mʋ, ta die a nagɩ daa wa tɩbɩrɩ a yiŋŋi a marɩ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ ba sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ kenne dɩ ba yigi wo wo dɩ, N yiwo gbɩgbatɩrʋ nɩ keŋ aŋaŋ takoobisi aŋaŋ dengbile dɩ nɩ yigi mɩŋ?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Manɩŋ die n bie nɩ jigiŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma daaŋ mana ta nɩ ka mɩa dɩ nɩ yigi mɩŋ. Ama saŋka gie yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ aŋaŋ Sitaani dɩ yine lɩmɩŋ hagɩrɩtieŋ wo sɩɩtɩ.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Mi die ba yigi Yisa a yaa wa a ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ tigiŋ, ta Piita die dɩ bie saasaa ta dɩ ba.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Die ba chɔgɩsɩ wa boliŋ gbaakʋ sʋnsʋŋ a kalɩ a giliŋbu a weele, Piita die dɩ ga gʋtɩ ba a kalɩ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Die tʋntʋntɩba havʋʋbiŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ ye Piita dɩ kala bolibu jigiŋ, die ʋ keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ daansɩ wa die ta keŋ baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie gbaŋ die gʋtɩ wa Yisa ma.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ chiisike dɩ, N lɩa, n ka wɔŋ sɩba wa.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Die dɩ yi sʋaa, daa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ ye wo a baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ, fʋ gbaŋ yiwo ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ? Ama die Piita dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, N nɩmbʋa, n ka yi ba wʋnyɩ.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Die dɩ bɩ yi sʋaa daa gaaŋ dɩ bɩ keŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, daa wa gie gbaŋ die beri aŋaŋ wa dama ʋ gbaŋ yiwo Galili vuoŋ.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Piita die baarɩ dɩ, N nɩmbʋa, manɩŋ n ka wɔŋ sɩba wudieke fʋ balala wa. Die ʋ yene ko bala wɩaha, kparaaŋ die dɩ kʋŋ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a daansɩ Piita nine me, ta Piita die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa wa dɩ, Kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma soriŋ sʋkʋʋŋ ta baarɩ zɩ mɩŋ bʋtaa mama.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Die Piita dɩ nyɩŋ yeŋ me a kʋŋ pam.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ gbarɩna Yisa wa die dɩ vʋarɩ wa fala ta nɩgɩ wa.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: ta nagɩ jaaŋ a bɔbɩ ʋ nine chaaŋ ta nɩgɩ wa ta balɩ wa dɩ, Bʋgɩ a dagɩ vuodieke dɩ nɩgɩna fʋ wa.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Ta die ba balɩ wɩa pam a chʋʋsa ʋ saaŋ ta zɩa wa.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ vʋnna, die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ nyɩŋkʋra lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ, die ba yaa Yisa a keŋ ba jigiŋ.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Balɩ tɩ, fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa? Die Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ balɩ nɩ, nɩ kaaŋ yi mɩŋ yada.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: ama n dɩaŋ maŋ pɩasɩ nɩ wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ nɩ kaaŋ saagɩ a yiŋŋi mɩŋ.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Ama a nyɩŋ lele yaa gamma manɩŋ vuota Bʋa nan daansɩ kalɩ Hagɩrɩtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Die ba mana dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Die nɩŋ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa? Ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Sɩba nɩ balla die wo.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Womi die ba baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ ka bɩ yaala daansɩatieliŋ bɩbra. Tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ wʋŋ ʋ nʋaŋ wʋbalɩka mɩŋ.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Kʋrɩŋ ma wa, ta jaaŋ die dɩ ye wone wo, ama Ŋmɩŋ Nʋawɩɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine Yisa Masia, die wɔŋ beri mɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ. Ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Piiliku me wo Ŋmɩŋ Nʋawɩɩrɩ gie wɔŋ beri mɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ naaŋ jaaŋ mana a nyɩŋ ʋ ma, ta jaaŋ die wori Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naaŋ ta ka ka nyɩŋ ʋ ma.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Ʋ ma die miivoli dɩ bɩ beri, ʋ naga a yɩa vuota, ta miivolike mi dɩ yi vuota chaanɩŋ.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: ta chaana tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuota lɩmɩŋ ma, ta lɩmɩŋkʋ dɩ ka bɩagɩ a lige chaaŋkʋ.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋŋ daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Jɔɔn.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: dɩ ʋ keŋ a keŋ balɩ vuosi dɩ Yisa Masia yine vuota chaanɩŋ, dɩ ba yi Yisa wa yada.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Jɔɔn womi die ka yi chaaŋkʋ, ama die ʋ ko keŋ mɩŋ dɩ ʋ keŋ balɩ vuosi chaaŋkʋ wɩa.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Yisa wa gie yine wusie chaaŋ dieke die dɩ kenne tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta chaana vuota mana ma.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Die ʋ keŋ wo dʋnɩa ka gie me, aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ naanna dʋnɩaka gie a nyɩŋ ʋ ma wa yɔrɩ, tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi die ka mɩŋŋɩ wa.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Die ʋ kenne tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo, die ʋ bie wo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ vuosi Izara vuosi jigiŋ, ama die ba ka tuo wo.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Ama vuodiekemba mana die dɩ tuone wo ta yi wo yada wa ta nagɩ ba gbaŋ a yɩ wa wa, banɩŋ die ʋ yɩ ba sieŋ ba bɩrɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die vana ba bɩrɩŋ ʋ ballɩ daa vuota die vana ba bɩrɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ saŋŋa dieke ba mɩɩrɩna ba wa.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ Nʋawɩɩrɩ die dɩ kenne ta yi vuota wa, die ʋ beri mɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta die suuli aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla aŋaŋ wusie dagɩŋ. Die tɩ ye ʋ chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ mɩŋ, ta kanɩŋ chaankpeŋkpɩɩkʋ mi die nyɩŋ wa ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ mɩŋ, dama ʋ yine ʋ Bʋa dembiŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ dɩ kenne ye wo wo, die ʋ balɩ ʋ wɩa ta natɩ dɩ, Vuodieke wɩa die n balala nɩ dɩ, Ʋ nan keŋ n kʋaŋ chaaŋ ama ʋ tɩaŋ mɩŋ mɩŋ dama ʋ wɔŋ beri mɩŋ ta ba ye mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ wa, wʋnna. .

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Ʋ suuline aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa ʋ yɩa tɩ suŋŋiŋ a gʋta suŋŋiŋ ma.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Mosisi die yɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha mɩŋ, ama Yisa Masia nɩŋ die keŋ aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla aŋaŋ wusie dagɩŋ.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Vuoŋ wori a ye ye Ŋmɩŋ; ama ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ Bʋadembiŋ balɩŋka vuodieke dɩ benne ʋ lʋgɩŋ wa ta bɩ mʋ wa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yuorine a dagɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩna dene.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma wa die dɩ tʋŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Liiva vuosi Jɔɔn jigiŋ dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine fʋ.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Die ʋ ka zetiye dɩ ʋ kaaŋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba wɩɩŋ, ama ta die balɩ yuori be dɩ, Daa manɩŋ n yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ, Masia wa.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Die wɩa die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Die nɩŋ, mɩnɩa yine fʋ? Yiwo Elaja? Jɔɔn die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Aayɩ, n ka yi wo. Die ba bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wa? Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Aayɩ, n ka bɩ yi wo.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Die wɩa ba bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Ta mɩnɩa yine fʋ? Balɩ tɩ vuodieke fʋ yine aŋ tɩ ga balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnna tɩ wa. Lalɩa fʋ balɩ yaa gamma fʋ ma.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba aŋaŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ bala dɩ, Manɩŋ n yiwo lɔlɩ dieke dɩ benne haagɩŋ ma a nata dɩ Nɩ wʋnsɩ sietʋgɩsɩka a yɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Die tʋntʋntɩba die nyɩŋ wa Farasisi vuosi jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Ta die bɩ pɩasɩ Jɔɔn dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ ka yi Masia wa, ta ka bɩ yi Elaja, ta ka bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wa, bɩa wɩa fʋ sɩa vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Jɔɔn dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n sɩa vuosi aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, ama vuoŋ bie nɩ sʋŋ ta nɩ ka sɩba wa.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ keŋ n kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ta maŋ ka mʋ dɩ n forisi ʋ nɩɩra guune gbaŋ.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Wɩaha gie mana die yiye mɩŋ Betani tɩŋ ma, Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ. Mi die Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩa vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa, Jɔɔn die dɩ ye Yisa dɩ kienene ʋ jigiŋ, ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Ye, Ŋmɩŋ kaabɩŋ Yiipɔlɩka wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ ʋ bala ʋ vʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi bɩaŋ.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Ʋ yine vuodieke wɩa die n balala wa dɩ, Daa wʋnyɩ nan keŋ n kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta tɩaŋ mɩŋ dama ʋ wɔŋ beri mɩŋ ta ba ye mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ wa.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Die n ka sɩba wa, ama die n keŋye mɩŋ a sɩa vuosi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ amʋ Izara vuosi mana nan sɩba wa.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Ta Jɔɔn dɩ bɩ balɩ a yɩ vuosisi dɩ, Saŋŋa dieke die n kana ka sɩba vuodieke ʋ yine wo, ama Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ dɩ n keŋ sɩɩ vuosisi ʋ nyaabʋ die dɩ balɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Vuodieke fʋ keŋ ye ta n Halɩkasɩka dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ kalɩ ta wɩarɩ beri aŋaŋ wa, fʋ sɩmma a baarɩ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ nagɩ n Halɩkasɩka a sɩma vuosi sʋgɩtɩ. Ta die maŋ seŋ ye Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, ta ʋ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ, ta nɩasɩ ŋmarɩŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ kalɩ Yisa ma ta wɩarɩ beri aŋaŋ wa.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Naa maŋ ye, ta bala nɩ dɩ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa bɩbra Jɔɔn die dɩ bɩ zie mi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bale.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: ta keŋ ye Yisa dɩ chʋnnana a tɩanna, ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Ye, Ŋmɩŋ Yiipɔlɩka wʋnna.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bale wo die dɩ wʋnna ʋ balala wa, die dɩ vaa wa ta dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yisa die dɩ keŋ yiŋŋi a daansɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a ye ba dɩna wa, ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yaala? Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ yaala tɩ sɩmma fʋ benne jigidieke wo.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ keŋ a ye jigidieke n benne wo. Die wɩa die ba dɩ wa a ga a ye jigidieke ʋ benne, ta die bie ʋ jigiŋ daarɩ mana.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Vuosi bale dieke die dɩ wʋnna Jɔɔn balɩkʋ ta die dɩ Yisa kʋaka wʋnyɩ die yine Andurusi, die ʋ yiwo Simoni Piita nɩmbʋa.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Die ʋ nyɩna Yisa jigiri kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ ga lagɩ lagɩ a ga yaalɩ ʋ nɩmbʋa Simoni jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ ye Masia wa mɩŋ.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Womi die ʋ nagɩ Simoni a ga Yisa jigiŋ. Yisa die dɩ daansɩ wa die, ta balɩ dɩ, Yiwo Simoni vuodieke dɩ yine Jɔɔn bʋadembiŋ, ama fʋ saaŋ nan bɩ yi Siifasi.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa Yisa die dɩ yaala dɩ ʋ ga Galili, ta die ga jigidieke Filipi die dɩ benne a ga balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Keŋ dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipi die nyɩŋ wa Befisada, tɩgɩdieke Andurusi aŋaŋ Piita dɩ nyɩnna.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Filipi dɩaŋ die dɩ ga yaalɩ Nataniali jigiŋ a balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ ye vuodieke Mosisi dɩ maagɩna ʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha gbaŋkʋ ma wa, ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩaŋ dɩ maagɩ ʋ wɩaha. Ʋ yiwo Yisa, vuodieke dɩ yine Josefu bʋadembiŋ ta nyɩŋ Nazeriti wa.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Nataniali die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nazeriti? Wʋvɩɩnɩŋ nan bɩagɩ nyɩŋ Nazeriti? Filipi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Keŋ a ye.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yisa die dɩ yene Nataniali dɩ kienene ʋ jigiri, die ʋ balɩ naa yaa gamma ʋ ma dɩ, Izara vuoŋ wusie wʋnna dama ʋ yiwo burichini vuoŋ.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Nataniali die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Yiwo lalɩa a sɩba mɩŋ? Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Filipi die dɩ yene ka wa fʋ wa, ta maŋ wɔŋ ye fʋ, fʋ benne kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka chɩaŋ.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Mi Nataniali die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa ta bɩ yi Izara naaŋ wa.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N bala fʋ dɩ n ye fʋ mɩŋ saŋŋa dieke fʋ benne kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka chɩaka wɩa fʋ yi mɩŋ yada? Nan dɩ yese wʋkpɩɩma pam a tɩaŋ naa.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ nan ye arɩzanna dɩ yuorine, ta Ŋmɩŋ malakasi dɩ keŋ a sʋʋna Vuota Bʋa ma, ta jʋala gara ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Ka daraa ataa daraaŋ die ba yaa hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋla siele Keenani tɩŋ ma, Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die Yisa nuŋ die bie mi.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: ta die ba wa Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gbaŋ dɩ ba keŋ banwʋlaka jigiŋ.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Die banwʋlaka daa dieke ba nyunene wo dɩ kenne kpatɩ wa, Yisa nuŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Banwʋlaka daabʋ kpatɩya mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, N naa, bɩa fʋ yaala n yi? N saŋŋa ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Yisa nuŋ die dɩ balɩ tʋntʋntɩba dɩ benne mi wo dɩ, Nɩ yi wudieke ʋ bala nɩ dɩ nɩ yi.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Die nyaaŋ gbiesi ayʋaba die zene mi, nyindiekemba Juu vuosisi dɩ yinene nyaaŋ a yie ta yɔɔra nɩta, sɩba ba jɩamɩŋ dɩ sɩna die wo, die a kaanɩ mana die bɩagɩ juo nyaaŋ sɩba galonsi baŋɩsɩ-le yaa baŋɩsɩ-taa.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ tʋntʋntɩba dɩ, Nɩ yi nyaaŋ suuli gbiesisi. Die wɩa die ba yi nyaaŋ a suulihe pili pili.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Womi die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yuori bʋtaŋ a yaa ga yɩ banwʋlaka jakʋʋrɩ. Die ba yi die.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ laŋ nyaabʋ ta ye bʋ tarɩgɩna a yi daaŋ, ama die ʋ ka sɩba jigidieke daabʋ die dɩ nyɩnna, ama tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yuorine nyaabʋ die dɩ sɩba. Die wɩa die ʋ wa daa dieke die dɩ faarɩna hɔgʋ wa.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: a balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Vuoŋ mana ŋaaŋ woliŋ a tɩa wa daa dieke dɩ nansɩna, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ vuodiekemba ʋ wana dɩ keŋ nyu a bugi ʋ wa tɩa daa dieke dɩ kana ka nansɩ wa. Ama fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nagɩ wa daa dieke dɩ nansɩna wa a lɔbɩrɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ saŋka gie.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Naa die yiwo Yisa bʋmbʋŋaŋ mamachi wʋkpɩɩŋ. Die ʋ yi ke wo Keenani tɩŋ ma, Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Naa die dagɩ vuosisi ʋ yalla Ŋmɩŋ yiko die; die wɩa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yi wo yada.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga Kapenɔmi a ga bie mi daraa ale.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Die Juu vuosi gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga saŋŋa die gbigiye mɩŋ, die wɩa Yisa die dɩ ga Jerusalemi dɩ ʋ ga dii dʋʋgaka mi.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Die ʋ kenne mi wo, ʋ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ, a ye dembisi dɩ yalla niige aŋaŋ yiise aŋaŋ ŋmarɩsɩ a daa, ta bataŋ dɩ kalɩ ba teebulisi me a tarɩga ligire.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Die ʋ nagɩ guune a ŋmʋ kpaasɩŋ a yagɩ dɔŋɩsɩsɩ mana a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, ta die kpaŋŋɩ vuodiekemba dɩ tarɩgɩna ligirehe teebulisi a bubi bubi ta ligiribiehe die dɩ nan a jaasɩ.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: A balɩ yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla ŋmarɩsɩ a daa wa dɩ, Nɩ nagɩha a nyɩŋ giena! Nɩ da nagɩ n Chʋa tigiŋ a bɩrɩŋ nyʋŋ.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Mi die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ baarɩ dɩ, N nine ŋmɩntɩya yaa gamma fʋ tigiri gie wɩa pam.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ ʋ jigiŋ aŋaŋ wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ dɩ, Bɩa mamachi wɩɩŋ fʋ nan yi aŋ ka dagɩ dɩ yaa yiko dɩ fʋ yi wɩaha gie.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie a taaŋ ta n nan nagɩ daraa ataa a bɩ mɩɩ ka.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Die Juu vuosisi dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa aŋaŋ ayʋaba ba nagɩ mɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie, ta fʋnɩŋ fʋ baarɩ nan nagɩ daraa ataa a mɩɩ ka.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke Yisa dɩ balala wa yiwo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ sʋgɩrɩna wa a nyɩŋ kumbu me kʋaŋ chaaŋ wa, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ ʋ balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie mɩŋ, ta die yi Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ aŋaŋ Yisa die dɩ bala wudieke wo yada.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yene bie Jerusalemi ma wa gasɩtɩaŋkʋ saŋka vuosi pam die dɩ ye ʋ tʋnna mamachi wʋkpɩɩmaha ta yiwo yada.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ ka nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yɩ ba, dama die ʋ sɩba vuota mana sʋŋ mɩŋ.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Die vuoŋ ka mʋ ʋ dagɩ wa vuota dɩ sɩna dene dama ʋ wɔŋ sɩba vuota beriŋ.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ die benne ba wasa wa Nikodimosi. Die ʋ yiwo Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ, ta bɩ yi Farasi vuoŋ.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Yuŋ kaanɩ die ʋ ga Yisa jigiŋ a ga balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ sɩba a baarɩ yiwo dɩdagɩrɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋnna, dama vuoŋ wori a nan bɩagɩ tʋŋ tʋnkpɩɩŋ dieke fʋ tʋnnana naa, ntaala Ŋmɩŋ bie ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ntaala ba yiŋŋi mɩɩrɩ wa.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Nikodimosi die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa ba baa ba yiŋŋi mɩɩrɩ vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi bʋabiŋ? Dama ʋ kaaŋ bɩ bɩagɩ a juu ʋ nuŋ nyuuti ma aŋ ba bɩ mɩɩrɩ wa bɩbra.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, dɩ vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma ntaala ʋ ye mɩɩrɩŋ bule, die yine Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ sɩɩla wɩa.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Vuota nɩŋ mɩɩra vuota ama Halɩkasɩka mɩɩra haalɩŋ.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Die wɩa da vaa ka yi fʋ mamachi n bala fʋ dɩ, Sie ba bɩ mɩɩrɩ nɩ wa.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ nɩga gara jigidieke ka yaalala, ŋaaŋ wʋŋ wa ka dɔntɩ ama ta ka sɩba jigidieke ka nyɩnna yaa ka ganana. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ vuodiekemba mana Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ mɩɩrɩna dɩ sɩɩ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Nikodimosi die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa naa dɩ baa ka yi.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Yiwo Izara vuosi dɩdagɩrɩkpɩɩŋ ta ka sɩba wɩaha chɩasɩ?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, tɩ bala nɩ wudieke tɩ sɩbɩna, ta bɩ bala nɩ wudieke tɩ yene, ama nɩ ka tuose tɩ wɩaha.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada yaa gamma tɩŋgbaŋka gie wudiekemba n bala nɩ wa, die nɩŋ maŋ balɩ nɩ arɩzanna ma wɩa nɩ baa nɩ yiwo lalɩa a yi yada?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Vuoŋ ye ka jʋalɩ ga arɩzanna ma sie manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ wa.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Mosisi die dɩ nagɩna kʋʋtɩ javʋʋkɩŋ a bɔbɩ a marɩ daaŋkʋ ta kɔtɩ ka zie haagɩŋ ma dene wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ ba baa ba kɔtɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: amʋ vuodieke mana dɩ yine mɩŋ yada nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ dɩ faasɩna cho tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi wɩa; die ʋ yɩ tɩ ʋ Bʋabalɩmɩŋ amʋ vuodieke mana dɩ yi Bʋadembike gie yada ʋ kaaŋ daansɩ kpi, ama ʋ nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ die ka tʋŋ ʋ Bʋa wa dɩ ʋ keŋ dii tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sarɩya ama dɩ ʋ keŋ a gbatɩ ba taaŋ.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa yada Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ dii ʋ sarɩya a chʋʋsɩ wa, ama vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka yi wo yada nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ wɔŋ dii ʋ sarɩya ta baa ʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, dama ʋ kana ka yi wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋabalɩŋka yada wa wɩa.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Wudieke wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ dine ba sarɩya ta baarɩ ba chʋʋsɩya yiwo naa; Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike die keŋye mɩŋ dɩ ʋ yuori wo tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi nine ama ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ yaala lɩmɩŋ a tɩaŋ chaanɩŋ, dama ba tʋnnana tʋntʋmbɩatɩ wɩa.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yinene bɩaŋ hawa chaanɩŋ ta ka yaala ʋ gbigi chaanɩŋ, dama ʋ ka yaala ʋ tʋmbɩatɩtɩ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ tʋnnana wudieke dɩ mʋna kieŋ wo chaanɩŋ ma, dɩ vuoŋ mana sɩmma a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ beri ʋ tʋʋma mana ma.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Genke kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, die ʋ yi daraa ale aŋaŋ ba, ta sɩa vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Saŋka mi Jɔɔn, vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die ye ka juu dansarɩka ma, ta bie Enoni, jigidieke nyaaŋ die dɩ faasɩna dala, ta vuosi die dɩ kieŋ ʋ jigiŋ ʋ sɩa ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ. Enoni die dɩ ka yʋa aŋaŋ Salimi.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ die Juu vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ piili a nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nyaaŋ sɩɩla wɩa.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Die wɩa die ba ga Jɔɔn jigiŋ a ga balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, vuodieke die dɩ benne aŋaŋ fʋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ, daa dieke die fʋ bala dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ, tɩ wʋŋ dɩ ʋ gbaŋ ʋ sɩa vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ, ta vuoŋ mana dɩ gara ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Jɔɔn die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ ntaala Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩna wa.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yiwo n daansɩatieliŋ dɩ die n balɩya dɩ, manɩŋ n ka yi Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa, ama manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ woliŋ a tʋŋ dɩ n dɩa ʋ nɩŋŋa a keŋ yi ʋ keniŋ siri.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Hɔgʋhaalʋ wa yiwo hɔgʋfaarɩtʋ wa sɩɩtɩ. Hɔgʋfaarɩtʋ wa zʋa ŋaaŋ zie mɩŋ, dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ wʋŋ ʋ lɔlɩŋ, ʋ sʋŋ ŋaaŋ fɩalɩ mɩŋ. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam Masia wa wɩa.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Dɩ mʋ wʋnɩŋ Masia wa saaŋ jʋalɩma ta manɩŋ n saaŋ sʋʋma tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ tɩaŋ vuoŋ mana mɩŋ, ama vuodiekemba dɩ nyɩna tɩŋgbaŋka gie, banɩŋ ba yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie tieliŋ, ta bala vuota wʋbalɩka. Vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ tɩaŋ vuoŋ mana.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: a bɩ gʋtɩ ʋ bala wudieke ʋ yene ta wʋŋ, ama vuoŋ ka tuose ʋ wɩaha.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Ta vuodieke mana dɩ tuone ʋ wɩaha dagɩya dɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiwo wusie.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ dieke ʋ tʋnna wa balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, dama Ŋmɩŋ yi ʋ Bʋadembike Halɩkasɩ dieke dɩ dala ta wo magɩsɩŋ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Ŋmɩŋ cho wo ʋ Bʋa wa ta nagɩ jaaŋ mana a yi ʋ nuusi me.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa yada, yaa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana a yi Bʋa wa yada kaaŋ ye miivoli mi, ama Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ nan dɩ bie ʋ ma yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Tɔ, die Farasisi vuosisi dɩ wʋŋ dɩ Yisa yese vuosi pam a sɩa ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ta ba yie ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ Jɔɔn.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: Wusie, die Yisa gbaŋ gbaŋ die ka sɩa vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ ama die dɩ yiwo ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die sɩnana ba.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ wʋnna wudieke vuosi dɩ balala yaa gamma ʋ ma wa, die ʋ nyɩŋ Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma ta yiŋŋi ga Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Die dɩ yi talasɩ dɩ ʋ daagɩ Samaria tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Die ʋ daagɩ mi a keŋ tʋgɩ tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Sayika, Samaria tɩŋgbaŋ ma, mi die ka yʋa aŋaŋ kʋaŋ dieke Jakobu die dɩ nagɩna a yɩ ʋ Bʋa Josefu wa.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Die buluŋ dieke die dɩ yine Jakobu sɩɩtɩ wa die bie mi, die Yisa dɩ gbalɩgɩ aŋaŋ chɔmbʋ ta sʋʋŋ kalɩ buluke chɩaŋ. Die dɩ yiwo ŋmɩntʋasɩ saŋŋa.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Die Samaria hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ yuori nyaaŋ, Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Yɩ mɩŋ nyaaŋ aŋ n nyu.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: Saŋka mi die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ga wa tɩka sʋŋ dɩ ba daa nyindiike.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Juu vuosi die ka saaga dɩ ba lagɩŋ dii ta nyuo aŋaŋ Samaria vuosi a nyɩna kpalɩbalɩmɩŋ ma. Die wɩa Samaria hɔgʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yiwo Juu vuoŋ ta manɩŋ maŋ yi Samaria vuoŋ, lalɩa fʋ jʋʋsa mɩŋ dɩ n yɩ fʋ nyaaŋ.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ tɩŋ sɩba piini dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaala ʋ yɩ fʋ, ta bɩ sɩba vuodieke n yine ta jʋʋsa fʋ nyaaŋ naa, tɩŋ nan pɩasɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yɩ fʋ miivoli nyaabʋ mi, ta n tɩŋ nan yɩ fʋ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, wo gʋʋga, ta buluke dɩ gole, sɩa fʋ baa fʋ ye miivoli nyaabʋ?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Jakobu die tune buluke gie a yɩ tɩ, ta wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ ballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ dɔŋɩsɩ mana dɩ nyu a nyɩŋ ka ma. Yile dɩ tɩaŋ Jakobu mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ nyune nyaabʋ gie, nyanyuule nan bɩ yigi wo;.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ nyune nyaaŋ dieke n bala n yɩ wa, nyanyuule kaaŋ bɩ yigi wo. Nyaaŋ dieke n bala n yɩ wa wa nan bɩrɩŋ buluŋ ʋ sʋŋ ma a yɩa wa nyaaŋ dieke dɩ yɩnana miivoli, ta yɩ wa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, yɩ mɩŋ nyaabʋ gie a mʋ nyanyuule kaaŋ bɩ yigi mɩŋ ta n kaaŋ bɩ a keŋ gie a yuori nyaaŋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Ga a ga wa fʋ chʋrʋ aŋ nɩ viiri keŋ giena.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Ʋ balɩ wa dɩ, N wo chʋrʋ. Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ balɩ wa wusie, fʋ bala dɩ wo chʋrʋ wa.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Dama die yallɩ wa chʋllɩŋ banʋ ta daa dieke fʋ benne ʋ jigiŋ lele wo dɩ ka seŋ yi fʋ chʋrʋ. Balɩ mɩŋ wa wusie.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, n sɩba a baarɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Tɩnɩŋ Samaria vuosi chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ die jɩama Ŋmɩŋ kunkogiri gie sikpeŋ ama nɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi baarɩ dɩ Jerusalemi ma yine jigidieke dɩ mʋna tɩ jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Yisa dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, N taa, yi n wɩɩrɩ gie yada, saŋŋa kieŋ ta vuota dɩ kaaŋ jɩaŋ tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ kunkogiri gie sikpeŋ yaa Jerusalemi ma.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Nɩnɩŋ Samaria vuosi ka sɩba vuodieke nɩ jɩanna ama tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi nɩŋ sɩba vuodieke tɩ jɩanna mɩŋ dama gbatɩtaanɩŋ wɩa nyɩŋ wa Juu vuosi jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Ama saŋŋa kieŋ, ta wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ, saŋŋa dieke vuodiekemba dɩ senne jɩama tɩ chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ wa nan seŋ jɩaŋ wa ʋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ ma ta sɩmma vuodieke ʋ yine, dama banɩŋ vuosi mi tɩ Chɔɔŋ dɩ yaala dɩ ba jɩama wa.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yiwo Haalɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩ jɩanna wa mʋ ba jɩama wa aŋaŋ wusie, ʋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, N sɩba a baarɩ Masia wa vuodieke ba wasɩnana Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ nan keŋ, ta ʋ keŋ keŋ ʋ nan dagɩ tɩ wɩaha gie mana chɩasɩ.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ n wonde, vuodieke dɩ balala wɩa aŋaŋ fʋ naa.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Womi die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ, die dɩ yi be mamachi pam ʋ balala wɩa aŋaŋ hɔgʋ wa, ama ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka pɩasɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ yaala? Yaa a pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ Bɩa fʋ bala wɩa aŋaŋ hɔgʋ wa.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Womi hɔgʋ wa die dɩ vaa ʋ kpagɩrɩ mi, ta yiŋŋi ga tɩka sʋŋ a balɩ yɩ vuosisi dɩ.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Nɩ keŋ ye daa dieke dɩ bala mɩŋ wudiekemba mana n yine. Ʋ baaŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩ ga Jerusalemi dɩ ʋ dii Juu vuosi dʋʋga kaanɩ.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Die wɩa, die ba nyɩŋ tɩka ma a ga Yisa jigiŋ.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Jerusalemi ma, gbagɩŋ kaanɩ die dʋna aŋaŋ nyaaŋ a gbigi Yiise Sanʋaŋ, ta Juu vuosi jabalɩkɩŋ ma, ba wasa ka Befizata, ta die yaa lɩɩfaasɩ anʋ.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Saŋka mi die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ jʋʋsa wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ dii nyindiike.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Mi die yʋagɩtieliŋ pam die dɩ ŋaaŋ dʋaga, bataŋ die yiwo yɩɩsɩ, bataŋ dɩ yi gbɩlɩsɩ, bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yi nyɩŋgbaŋ kpiititieliŋ. Die ba dʋa a chɩɩsa mɩŋ dɩ nyaabʋ dɔŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, N yaa wa nyindiike a baaŋ dii ta nɩ ka sɩba bʋ wɩa.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ bie mi a yʋagɩ die bɩna baŋɩsɩ-taa aŋaŋ anɩɩ.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Die wɩa, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Dɩ baaŋ yi vuoŋ yalla nyindiike a keŋ yɩ wa.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye wo ʋ dʋna mi ta mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ʋ yʋagɩ yʋasɩ mɩŋ, die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Yaala fʋ ye alaafɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n nyindiike yi wo dɩ n yi vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa dʋŋŋʋ a kpatɩ, ʋ tʋʋmaha ʋ nagɩna yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n tʋŋ wa.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Daa yʋagɩtieŋ wo die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, n wo vuoŋ mana giena ʋ baaŋ nagɩ mɩŋ a yi nyaabʋ ma dɩɩ yi nyaabʋ dɩ keŋ dɔŋ; maŋ keŋ mɩa dɩ n juu bʋ sʋŋ, vuogaaŋ ŋaaŋ woliŋ mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ bala dɩ, Chɩɩtɩ a nɩɩsa tɩala ta zaaha gobiku tʋgɩ. Ama n bala nɩ dɩ nɩ mɩŋŋɩ a daansɩ kʋatɩtɩ, zaaha bieye mɩŋ aŋaŋ gobiŋ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a chʋmma.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Ta zaagobiribe nan daansɩ tuo ba tune, ta zaa dieke ba gobinene wo, banɩŋ ba yine vuota diekemba dɩ baaŋ daansɩ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩ bʋrɩna wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ gobine mana nan daansɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ zaaha wɩa.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Lele womi daa wa die dɩ ye alaafɩa; ta nagɩ ʋ kalɩŋŋa a dɩɩ chʋŋ. Daa dieke wɩɩrɩ gie die dɩ yine wo die yiwo davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Dɩ yiwo wusie ba balla dɩ, Bataŋ bʋrɩnana ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ gobe.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: die wɩa Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ ballɩ a yɩ daa dieke dɩ yene gbaaŋkʋ dɩ, Davʋʋsɩkɩŋ daaŋ wʋnna. Tɩ mɩraha ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ fʋ chii fʋ kalɩŋŋa.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: N tʋma nɩ dɩ nɩ ga gobi jigidieke nɩ kana ka bʋrɩya; vuosi bataŋ die kʋna ta nɩ keŋ die ka nyʋarɩ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Die daa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Daa dieke dɩ gbaanna mɩŋ wa balɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ, Nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a chʋmma. .

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Die Samaria vuosi pam die dɩ wʋŋ hɔgʋ wa dɩ bala dɩ, Yisa balɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ wudiekemba mana n yine, ta die yi Yisa yada.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine daa dieke dɩ balla fʋ dɩ fʋ nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a chʋmma wa.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Die wɩa die ba kenne ʋ jigiri, ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ wɩarɩ a bemme ba jigiŋ; die wɩa Yisa die dɩ wɩarɩ ba jigiŋ damʋʋma ale.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Ama daa wa die ka sɩba vuodieke dɩ gbaanna wa, dama kpɩkpaaŋ die benne mi ta Yisa die dɩ wɔŋ nyɩŋ mi a ga.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Vuosi pam die dɩ yiwo yada a gʋtɩ aŋaŋ wudieke ʋ balla wa.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa die dɩ ye daa wa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Ye, lele ye alaafɩa mɩŋ, die wɩa vaa fʋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩtɩ, dɩ daa die wʋbɩaŋ dɩ tɩanna naa nan keŋ fʋ ma.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: ta balɩ a yɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, Lele tɩ yi yada mɩŋ, daa wudieke fʋ balla wa wɩa ama tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ wʋŋ wa mɩŋ ta sɩba a baarɩ ʋ seŋ yiwo dʋnɩa ka gie gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Yisa die dɩ balla aŋaŋ daa wa a kpatɩ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, daa wa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga balɩ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ dɩ yiwo Yisa die gbaanna wa wa.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Die ka daraa ale kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Ta nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ piili a mugise Yisa dama ʋ gbaanna daa wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ wɩa.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: dama die ʋ wɔŋ baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wo jɩlɩma ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ tʋma mɩŋ saŋŋa mana ta n gbaŋ dɩ tʋma die.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Die ʋ gana a ga tʋgɩ Galili wo, mi vuosi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo chaantɩ dama die ba ga Jerusalemi mɩŋ a ga dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka ta die ye ʋ yine wudiekemba mana dʋʋga ka saŋŋa.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Yisa balɩkʋ gie die dɩ vaa Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ faasɩ a ŋmɩntɩ niŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ wa, daa ʋ chʋʋsɩna davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ mɩrɩka nyɩɩna ma wɩa ama ʋ balla dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo ʋ chɔɔŋ wa, ka dagɩ dɩ ʋ naga wa ʋ gbaŋ a magɩsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Die ʋ benne Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa, ta die keŋ ga Keenani tɩŋ ma, jigidieke die ʋ vana nyaabʋ dɩ bɩrɩŋ daabʋ. Ta jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ bie Kapenɔmi ma ʋ bʋa dɩ yʋagɩ.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ: Manɩŋ Bʋa wa kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n dʋŋŋʋ ma ama n yie wudieke n yene n Chʋa wa dɩ yinene. Dama n Chʋa wa dɩ yinene wudieke wo, kanɩŋ manɩŋ Bʋa wa dɩaŋ dɩ yie.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Yisa nyɩŋ Judia a keŋ Galili me wo, die ʋ ga Yisa jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ keŋ Kapenɔmi a keŋ gbaaŋ ʋ bʋa wa dɩ yaalɩnana ʋ kpi wo.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Dama n Chʋa dɩ faasɩna cho mɩŋ wɩa ʋ dagɩ mɩŋ wudieke mana wʋnɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ yinene. Ʋ nan dagɩ mɩŋ wʋkpɩɩma diekemba dɩ tɩanna naa, aŋ a yi nɩ mamachi.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Nɩ kaaŋ yi yada ntaala nɩ ye dagɩtɩ mamachi wʋkpɩɩma.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Sɩba n Chʋa dɩ vasɩnana kunti dɩ haga kunti me wo, ta yɩa ba miivoli die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ manɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine Bʋa wa dɩ yɩa vuodiekemba n yaalala miivoli.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ kiere dɩ daa die n bʋa wa nan kpi.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: N Chʋa ka die vuoŋ sarɩya, ama ʋ yɩ wa manɩŋ ʋ Bʋa wa sieŋ dɩ n dii vuosi sarɩya.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Gamma, fʋ bʋa wa nan ye gbaamɩŋ. Daa wa die dɩ yi Yisa yada ta ga.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: amʋ vuoŋ mana yɩma mɩŋ jɩlɩma sɩba ba yɩnana n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jɩlɩma dene wo. Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka yɩ mɩŋ jɩlɩma ka dagɩ sɩba ʋ ka yɩa n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa jɩlɩma mɩŋ.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Die ʋ bene sieku me a gara wa, ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ die dɩ tuoli wo sieku mi a balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ bʋa wa ye alaafɩa mɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ wʋnna n wɩaha gie ta yi vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa yada yaa wa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ dii ʋ sarɩya a chʋʋsɩ wa, ama ʋ wɔŋ nyɩŋ wa kuŋ me a juu miivoli me mɩŋ.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ʋ pɩasɩ ba saŋŋa dieke ʋ bʋa wa dɩ yene alaafɩa wa, ba baarɩ dɩ, Nyɩŋgbaŋ tuulibu va wa mɩŋ dienewo ŋmɩntʋasɩ bimbilimiŋ balɩmɩŋ saŋŋa.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, saŋŋa kieŋ ta ka wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ ta kunti nan wʋŋ manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa lɔlɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba mana dɩ wʋnna n lɔlɩkʋ nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Bʋa wa chɔɔŋ wa die dɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ bʋa wa nan ye gbaamɩŋ. Die wɩa wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi mana die dɩ yi Yisa yada.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩna miivoli a yɩa vuosi dene wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yɩ manɩŋ ʋ Bʋa wa dɩ n sɩmma miivoli a yɩma vuosi.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Mamachi wʋkpɩɩka gie die gʋtɩna bule Yisa dɩ yine ʋ nyɩnna Judia a ga Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Ta bɩ yɩ mɩŋ yiko dɩ n dii vuosi sarɩya, dama n yi wo Vuota Bʋa.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Nɩ da vaa ka yi nɩ mamachi, saŋŋa kieŋ ta vuodiekemba mana dɩ kpine nan daansɩ wʋŋ n lɔlɩŋ.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: ta hagɩ kuŋ me; ta vuodiekemba dɩ yine vɩɩnɩŋ nan hagɩ ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ yine wʋbɩatɩtɩ nan hagɩ aŋ n dii ba sarɩya ta baarɩ ba chʋʋsɩya.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Manɩŋ n kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ yiko, n die vuosi sarɩya sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balala mɩŋ die, ta n sarɩyaka dɩ yi wusie, dama n ka mɩa dɩ n yi wudieke n sʋŋ dɩ yaalɩnana, ama n yaala dɩ n yiwo wudieke vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa dɩ yaalɩnana.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ dii n gbaŋ gbaŋ n siara, vuosi kaaŋ tuo a dii dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Ama vuoŋ benne a bala vuosi yaa gamma vuodieke n yine. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ, ta maŋ sɩba dɩ ʋ balɩkʋ yiwo wusie.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Die nɩ tʋŋ wa tʋntʋntɩŋ Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ jigiŋ, ta ʋ gbaŋ die dɩ balɩ wusie yaa gamma n ma.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Dɩ ka yi talasɩ dɩ vuoŋ balɩ wɩa yaa gamma n ma, ama n balɩ nɩ naa amʋ nɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Jɔɔn die sɩɩ sɩba popoli dɩ ŋaana die ta chaana, ta die nɩ nagɩ saŋŋa bɩta a dii nansɩŋ ʋ chaaŋkʋ ma.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Ama mamachi wʋkpɩɩma diekemba n tʋnnana, hanɩŋ daga n siara a tɩaŋ wudieke Jɔɔn die dɩ balala yaa gamma n ma wa. Tʋnkpɩɩma diekemba n tʋnnana wa yiwo tʋŋ dieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n tʋŋ, ta tʋʋmaha dɩ yi siara a dagɩ dɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: A bɩ gʋtɩ, n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa gbaŋ yiwo n siara. Ta nɩ ye ka wʋŋ ʋ lɔlɩŋ yaa a ye wo aŋaŋ nɩ nine.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: ta nɩ ka nagɩ ʋ wɩaha a yi nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma, dama nɩ ka yi vuodieke ʋ tʋnna wa yada.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Nɩ ŋaaŋ karɩma Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ mɩŋ ta yile dɩ nɩ nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ a ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ mi dɩ bala n wɩa.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: ama ta nɩ zeti dɩ nɩ keŋ n jigiŋ aŋ n yɩ nɩ miivoli mi.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: N ka yaala bɩrɩŋ vuota jigiŋ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: N wɔŋ sɩba mɩŋ dɩ nɩ ka wɔŋ cho Ŋmɩŋ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: N keŋye mɩŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma, ta nɩ ka tuo mɩŋ. Ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ keŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ saaŋ ma, nɩ tuose wo mɩŋ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Nɩ yaala nɩ ye bɩrɩŋ a nyɩmma tamba jigiŋ ama nɩ ka mɩa dɩ nɩ ye bɩrɩŋ a nyɩmma Ŋmɩŋ balɩŋka jigiŋ. Die wɩa nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada wa.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Nɩ da yilime dɩ n nan galɩŋ nɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa. Dama vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ galɩŋ nɩ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Mosisi, vuodieke nɩ yine yada wa.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Dɩɩ yi die nɩ seŋ yi Mosisi maagɩkʋ yada, nɩ tɩŋ nan yi mɩŋ yada, dama die ʋ maagɩya mɩŋ yaa gamma n wɩa.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Ama nɩ kana ka yi wudieke ʋ maagɩna yada wa, lalɩa nɩ baa nɩ yi n wʋbalɩkaha gie yada.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a garɩsɩ Galili gbagɩkpɩɩŋ dieke ba bɩna wasa Tiberisi wo.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Die kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ dɩ wa, dama die ba ye ʋ mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma diekemba ʋ yine wo ta yi yʋagɩtieliŋ dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ wa.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Ta Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ jʋalɩ kunkogiŋ a kalɩ.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Saŋka mi ta Juu vuosi gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka die gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yisa die dɩ kala wa die ʋ keŋ ye kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ kienene ʋ jigiŋ. Die ʋ pɩasɩ Filipi dɩ, Sɩa tɩ nan ye nyindiike a daa a yɩ vuosi gie mana.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Die ʋ pɩasa naa, dɩ ʋ magɩsɩ Filipi a ye, dama die ʋ wɔŋ sɩba wudieke ʋ bala ʋ yi mɩŋ.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Ta Filipi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi tɩ yaala dɩ tɩ daa paanʋ a yɩ vuosi gie, bʋ ligire nan tɩaŋ salɩma ligire bie kɔbɩsɩ-le, ta vuoŋ mana ye ye bɩta.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ ba wasɩna Andurusi ta yi Simoni Piita nɩmbʋa wa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Bʋa wʋnyɩ benne giena a yaa paanʋ golime anʋ aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩ ale. Ama a kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi vuosi gie mana wɩɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Vaa vuosisi sʋʋŋ kala tɩŋgbaŋ. Hʋʋbɩsɩ pam die benne mi, ta vuosisi dɩ sʋʋŋ kalɩ tɩŋgbaŋ, ta dembisi die yiwo tuse anʋ mi.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋ golimehe a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die nagɩ a yɩ ba tɩa tɩa a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kala mi wo. Ta die bɩ nagɩ zaasɩbɩsɩsɩ a yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ, ba mana die dɩ dii a tʋgɩ ba yaalɩnana dene.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Die ba mana die dɩ dine a chagɩ wa, ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha mana ta da vaa a taaŋ yɔrɩ.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Die wɩa die ba vaarɩ vuosisi dɩ dine paanʋ golime anʋ ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ wa a suuli kparɩsɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Die vuodiekemba dɩ benne mi ta ye Yisa dɩ yine mamachi wʋkpɩɩka gie wo die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, daa wa seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa dieke Mosisi dɩ bala dɩ ʋ nan keŋ dʋnɩa ka gie me wo.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ba yie siri dɩ ba keŋ a yigi wo aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ a yi wo naaŋ; die wɩa die ʋ bɩ yiŋŋi jʋalɩ ga kunkogiri me a ga bie ʋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Die jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ sʋʋŋ ga gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ ma.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: a nagɩ haarɩŋ a garɩsɩ gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ a yiŋŋi gara Kapenɔmi. Yuŋ die dɩ sibe ta Yisa die dɩ ye ka keŋ gʋtɩ ba ma.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Saŋka gie bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ nɩga ta dɔma nyaabʋ.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ dɔŋ a ga sɩba malisi ataa yaa anɩɩsa, ta die keŋ ye Yisa dɩ chʋnnana nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a keŋ gbige haarɩkʋ, ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dɩ yiwo manɩŋ.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Die ba nagɩ wa aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ a yi haarɩkʋ ma, ta lele womi ba haarɩkʋ die dɩ ga tʋgɩ gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ nʋaŋ jigidieke chaaŋ die ba ganana wa.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die kpɩkpaaŋ dieke die dɩ wɩarɩna gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ die dɩ ye dɩ haarɩ balɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma tɩalɩna mi. Die ba sɩba dɩ dienewo Yisa ka juu haarɩkʋ a ga aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, dama die ba gaya mɩŋ ta va wa.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Die haarɩsɩ ataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ Tiberisi a keŋ zie gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ kʋanʋaŋ jigidieke die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ nagɩna paanʋkʋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ dii wo.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ dɩa Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma; ta die ka dɩa Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma dama Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die yaala ba kʋʋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yene ta Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wo mi wo, die ba juu haarɩsɩ mi a ga Kapenɔmi a dɩ dɩa yaala Yisa.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Die Juu vuosi dʋʋga dieke ba wasɩna jaga dʋʋgaka die dɩ gbigi.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Die vuosisi die dɩ jʋalɩna a ye Yisa gbagɩkpɩɩrɩ mi chaakʋ, die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, saŋŋa bɩa fʋ keŋ giena.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: die wɩa Yisa nɩmballɩ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Nyɩŋ gie a ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma amʋ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne mi wo nan ye tʋŋ dieke fʋ tʋnnana.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩnɩŋ nɩ dɩ dɩa a yaala mɩŋ da nɩ yene mamachi dagɩtɩ wɩa ama die n yɩna nɩ nyindiike nɩ dii chagɩ wa wɩa.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yaalɩnana vuosi sɩmma ʋ wɩa ka lɔbɩra wudieke ʋ yinene. Fʋ yinene wɩaha gie wo, vaa dʋnɩa mana sɩmma fʋ wɩa.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Nɩ da tʋmma tʋʋma nyindiike diekemba dɩ chʋʋsɩnana wɩa, ama nɩ tʋmma tʋʋma nyindiike diekemba dɩ yɩnana vuosi miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ wɩa. Manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa nan yɩ nɩ kanɩŋ nyindiike mi, dama n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ yɩna mɩŋ kanɩŋ yiko dɩ n yɩma nɩ.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Ʋ nɩmballɩ gbaŋ die ka yiwo yada.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Die wɩa die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa tɩ ba tɩ tʋŋ aŋ ka yi Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N saŋŋa ye ka keŋye. Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ saŋŋa mana mʋ a yi nɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ yaala nɩ yiwo vuodieke ʋ tʋnna wa yada.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, dʋnɩaka gie vuosi ka haa nɩ, ama ba haa mɩŋ mɩŋ dama n balala dɩ wudieke ba yinene wo yiwo bɩaŋ wɩa.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Mamachi wʋbɩa fʋ nan yi aŋ tɩ ye ta yi fʋ yada? Bɩa fʋ baa fʋ yi?.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gamma dʋʋgaka, manɩŋ n ka gara dʋʋgaka gie dama n saŋŋa ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dii wo nyindiike dieke ba wasɩnana maana saŋŋa dieke ba benne hagɩrɩ ma, sɩba ba maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma die wo dɩ, Ʋ yɩ ba wa nyindiike a nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma dɩ ba dii. .

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Die ʋ balɩ ba naa, ta wɩarɩ bie Galili me.

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, daa Mosisi die yɩna nɩ nyindiike a nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma wa, ama dɩ yiwo n Chʋa Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yɩna nɩ wusie nyindiike dieke dɩ nyɩna arɩzanna ma wa.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Die Yisa nɩmballɩ die dɩ gana dʋʋgaka kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa gbaŋ die dɩ ga; ama die ʋ ka ga yaalɩŋ ma ama die ʋ lɔbɩrɩ a ga mɩŋ.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Nyindiike dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩnana nɩ wa, yiwo vuodieke dɩ nyɩna arɩzanna a keŋ sʋʋŋ ta yɩa dʋnɩaka gie vuosi miivoli wo.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ dɩdɩa a yaala wa dʋʋgaka ma, ta die pɩasa vuosi dɩ, Sɩa daa wa dɩ beri.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Die ba baarɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, yɩma tɩ nyindiike gie daaŋ mana.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Die vuosi pam die bie mi a pʋʋsa bala ʋ wɩa; bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ, bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ, ʋ gaama vuosi mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine miivoli nyindiikehe. Vuodieke dɩ kienene n jigiŋ, kɔŋ kaaŋ bɩ yigi wo, ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ yi mɩŋ yada nyanyuule kaaŋ yigi wo.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Ama ba wʋnyɩ die ka yuori balɩ ʋ wɩa dama die ba chɩga Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha mɩŋ.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: N wɔŋ balɩ nɩ dɩ nɩ ye mɩŋ mɩŋ, ama nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Die dʋʋgaka die tʋgɩ wa sʋnsʋŋ ta die Yisa dɩ ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a piili a daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna yɩ mɩŋ, nan keŋ n jigiŋ a yi mɩŋ yada, ta n kaaŋ yagɩ vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ kienene n jigiŋ.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ wʋnna wa, die dɩ yi be mamachi, ba pɩasa dɩ, Lalɩa daa wa gie dɩ yi a sɩba wɩa dene ta ka ga tɩ siikusi.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Dama n nyɩŋ wa arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ dɩ n yi Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ daa manɩŋ n dʋŋŋʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Wudieke n dagɩna wa ka yi n gbaŋ gbaŋ n dagɩŋ, ama a nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa, ʋ choti yiwo dɩ n daa vaa vuodiekemba ʋ nagɩna yɩ mɩŋ wa wʋnyɩ bɩa, ama a keŋ sʋgɩrɩ ba mana a nyɩŋ kuŋ me dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ yaalɩnana ʋ yi wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala nan dɩ sɩba dɩ wudieke n dagɩnana wa nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ yaa n jigiŋ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Wudieke n Chʋa dɩ yaalala wʋnna; dɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ yene ʋ Bʋa wa ta yiwo yada wa, yallɩma miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ; ta n nan daansɩ sʋgɩrɩ ba mana a nyɩŋ kuŋ me dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Vuodieke dɩ balala ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ dagɩŋ mɩa mɩŋ dɩ ʋ ye bɩrɩŋ a yɩ ʋ gbaŋ, ama vuodieke dɩ yaalala dɩ ʋ yi aŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna wa wa ye bɩrɩŋ yiwo wusietieŋ ta wʋbɩaŋ dɩ wo ʋ ma.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Die Juu vuosisi die dɩ piili a vʋʋna wɩa, die ʋ bala dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine nyiindiike dɩ nyɩna arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ wa.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Daa Mosisi die yɩna nɩ mɩraha? Ama nɩ wʋnyɩ ka dɩ ha. Ta bɩa nɩ mɩa dɩ nɩ kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Die ba pɩasa tamba dɩ, Daa daa wa gie yine Josefu bʋa, Yisa wa? Ta tɩ sɩba ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo? Lalɩa ʋ baarɩ dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ wa arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yaa wa jɩmbɩaŋ fʋ ma. Mɩnɩa mɩnana ʋ kʋʋ fʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vaa vʋʋnɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n yiwo mamachi wʋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ, dɩ yi nɩ mamachi.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ keŋ n jigiŋ, ntaala n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa yaa wa keŋ, ta n nan daansɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Ama Mosisi die yɩna nɩ nʋaŋ dɩ nɩ gobime nɩ ballɩdembisi kɔlaha , nɩ ŋaaŋ gobi be mɩŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan dagɩ vuota mana. Ta vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnna n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ ta tuose ʋ wɩaha, wʋnɩŋ ʋ kieŋ wo n jigiŋ.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ gobe Bʋadembiŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ amʋ nɩ kaaŋ chʋʋsɩ Mosisi mɩraha, bɩa wɩa nɩ jɩa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ mɩŋ n gbaanna daa wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ?.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Vuoŋ ye ka ye n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, sie manɩŋ vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna ʋ jigiŋ nyɩɩna ma yene wo.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Nɩ daa daansɩ vuoŋ dɩ sɩna dene ta dii wo ʋ sarɩya, ama dii wo ʋ sarɩya aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke dɩ yine mɩŋ yada, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yaa wa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Jerusalemi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Daa daa wa gie ba mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ wa?.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Manɩŋ n yine miivoli nyindiikehe.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Ye, ʋ bala yeŋ me ta vuoŋ dɩ ka bala wa wɩɩŋ. Dɩ baaŋ yi dɩ nyɩŋkʋraha mɩŋŋɩya mɩŋ dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ, Masia wa?.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dii wo maana hagɩrɩ ma, ama ta die kpi.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Tɩ sɩba daa wa dɩ nyɩnna jigidieke; ama dɩɩ yi ta Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ, Masia wa dɩ keŋ keŋ, vuoŋ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba jigidieke ʋ nyɩnna.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Ama nyindiike dieke dɩ nyɩnna arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ nɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ dine he kaaŋ kpi.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yene daga vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wa, die ʋ keŋ balɩ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yile dɩ nɩ sɩba mɩŋ mɩŋ, ta bɩ yile dɩ nɩ sɩba jigidieke n nyɩnna? Manɩŋ n ka keŋ giena aŋaŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n yiko, ama vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa yiwo wusie tieŋ. Nɩnɩŋ nɩ ka sɩba wa.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Manɩŋ n yine miivoli nyindiikehe die dɩ nyɩnna arɩzanna ma a keŋ sʋʋŋ wa. Vuodieke dɩ dine nyindiikehe gie, nan dɩ yaa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ. Nyindiike gie yiwo n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ, kanɩŋ maŋ baa n nagɩ yɩ amʋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi nan dɩ yaa miivoli.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: ama manɩŋ n sɩba wa mɩŋ, dama n nyɩŋ wa ʋ jigiŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Wɩɩrɩ gie die dɩ vaa Juu vuosi dɩ piili a nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ ta pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Lalɩa daa wa gie dɩ baa ʋ yi a yɩ tɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋ tɩ ŋɔbɩ.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Womi die ba mɩa dɩ ba yigi wo, ama vuoŋ die ka bɩagɩ yigi wo dama ʋ yigiŋ saŋŋa die ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ntaala nɩ ŋɔbɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ta nyu n zɩmbʋ, nɩ kaaŋ dɩ yaa miivoli nɩ ma.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Ama kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ vuosi pam die dɩ yiwo yada, ta baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa dɩ keŋ keŋ, ʋ nan tʋŋ mamachi wʋkpɩɩma a tɩaŋ daa wa gie dɩ tʋnna naa.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ ŋɔbɩna n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ta nyu n zɩmbʋ yaa wa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta n nan daansɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Die Farasisi vuosisi die dɩ wʋŋ kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ pʋʋsɩna a bala wɩaha gie yaa gamma Yisa ma wa, die wɩa die banɩŋ aŋaŋ ba kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ tʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gbɩgbarɩtɩŋ bataŋ dɩ ba ga a yigi Yisa.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: N nyɩŋgbanɩŋ yine wusie nyindiike ta n zɩmbʋ dɩ yi wusie nyiŋnyuuke.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N nan dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ daraa ale ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan yiŋŋi ga vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa jigiŋ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
