local: Vuodieke dɩ ŋɔbɩna n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ta nyu n zɩmbʋ bie wo n ma, ta n dɩaŋ maŋ bie ʋ ma.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Nɩ nan dɩ dɩa a yaala mɩŋ ama nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, dama jigidieke n bala n bemme wo nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a keŋ mi.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sɩna miivoli tʋnna mɩŋ, ta ʋ wɩa maŋ yallɩ miivoli. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, vuodieke dɩ ŋɔbɩna n nyɩŋgbaŋka nan dɩ yaa miivoli n wɩa.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Sɩa ʋ baa ʋ ga ta tɩ kaaŋ ye wo? Ʋ baa ʋ ga wa Giriiki vuosi tɩgɩ diekemba Juu vuosisi dɩ benne wo a dagɩ Giriiki vuosisi?.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Manɩŋ n yine nyindiike dieke dɩ nyɩna arɩzanna ma a keŋ wo. Ta vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ dine nyindiikehe, nan dɩ bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma yaa gamma haahuu; hanɩŋ nyindiikehe mi ka sɩɩ sɩba nyindiike dieke nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba dɩ dine ama ta keŋ kpi wo.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ nan dɩ dɩdɩa a yaala mɩŋ ama nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, ta Nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ ga jigidieke n bala n bemme wo, wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Yisa die balɩ wa naa saŋŋa dieke die ʋ dagɩnana Kapenɔmi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me wo.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Die dʋʋgaka dakpatɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ die yiwo daakpɩɩŋ. Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi Yisa die dɩ hagɩ zie a balɩ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana nyanyuule dɩ yalla, ʋ keŋ n jigiŋ a nyu.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba pam die dɩ wʋnna ʋ dagɩkʋ gie die ba baarɩ dɩ, Dagɩkʋ gie faasɩ hagɩrɩ mɩŋ. Mɩnɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ tuoke.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine mɩŋ yada, miivoli nyaaŋ nan dɩ paala a nyɩŋ ʋ ma. .

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Vuoŋ die ka balɩ Yisa, ama die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba vʋʋna wɩa yaa gamma wudieke ʋ bala wa. Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Wɩɩrɩ gie vaa nɩ yaala nɩ yiŋŋi mɩŋ?.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yisa die balɩ wa naa yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ma, Haalɩŋ dieke vuodiekemba dɩ yine wo yada dɩ bala ba keŋ ye wo. Die saŋka mi ta Ŋmɩŋ ye ka yɩ vuosi Halɩkasɩka mi, dama saŋŋa mi ta ʋ ye ka kɔtɩ Yisa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ye manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa dɩ jʋalɩna a gara arɩzanna jigidieke n nyɩnna keŋ wo, lalɩa nɩ baa nɩ yili?.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Die daadamba pam die dɩ wʋŋ ʋ bala die wo ta baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wusie.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yɩnana vuota miivoli, ama vuota gbaŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ yi naa. Wʋbalɩkɩ diekemba n bala a yɩ nɩ wa yaa wa Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ dieke dɩ yɩnana vuosi miivoli a kieŋ.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Ta bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa. Ama bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa kaaŋ nyɩŋ Galili me.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Ama nɩ bataŋ ka yi yada. Yisa die wɔŋ sɩba mɩŋ a nyɩŋ piiliku me vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka baa ba yiwo yada aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ posi ʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dagɩya dɩ Masia baa ʋ yiwo naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ ta ba nan mɩɩrɩ wa Betilehemi ma tɩgɩ dieke die Davidi dɩ benne.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Die ʋ balɩ gʋtɩ dɩ, Naa wɩa maŋ balɩ nɩ dɩ vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a keŋ n jigiŋ ntaala n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa sieŋ dɩ ʋ keŋ.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Naa wɩa vuosisi die dɩ puo yaa gamma Yisa ma.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Naa wɩa die ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba pam die dɩ yiŋŋi ta ka bɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ bɩbra.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Die bataŋ dɩ yaala ba yigi wo ama vuoŋ die ka gbɩ wa.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yisa die dɩ chʋnnana a gara wa die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ ba mɩrɩna wa ta ʋ yi yɩɩ.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo dɩ, Ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nan va mɩŋ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Die gbɩgbarɩtɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi ga kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi jigiŋ, die ba pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa yine nɩ ka yaa wa a keŋ.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, mɩnɩa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ wɩa ba mɩɩrɩ daa wa ta ʋ yi yɩɩ? Wʋnɩŋ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Womi vuoŋ mana die dɩ ga tigiŋ, ama Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩ ga Olivisi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Ta Simoni Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, mɩnɩa jigiŋ tɩ baa tɩ ga? Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yalla wʋbalɩka dieke dɩ yɩnana miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Gbɩgbarɩtɩba dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Vuoŋ ye ka balɩ wɩa sɩba daa wa dɩ bala die wo.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Daa wʋnɩŋ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ bɩaŋ taasɩna ʋ yɩɩsɩsɩ. Ʋ yɩɩŋya mɩŋ dɩ n vaa ʋ nine yuori amʋ vuosi ye Ŋmɩŋ tʋnkpɩɩma.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Die ka sʋkʋleeliŋ die ʋ yiŋŋi ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma. Die vuosi pam die dɩ taaŋ a giliŋ wo, die ʋ sʋʋŋ kalɩ a piili a daga ba.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Ta lele tɩ yi fʋ yada mɩŋ ta sɩba a baarɩ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ vuokasɩ wa.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Farasisi vuosisi dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Ʋ gaaŋ nɩ gbaŋ mɩŋ yaa?.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Yuŋ dɩ yene ka sibiye naa, tɩ mʋ tɩ tʋmma vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa tʋʋma; dama yuŋ dɩ keŋ sibi vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a tʋŋ.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Die mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi bataŋ die dɩ yaa hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ dʋagɩna aŋaŋ dembiŋ dieke dɩ kana ka yi ʋ chʋrʋ a keŋ juu ta vaa ʋ zie ba mana nɩŋŋa.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ vuosi baŋ aŋaŋ bale wa gie, manɩŋ n vʋarɩna nɩ, ama nɩ wʋnyɩ yiwo Sitaani sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Nɩ ye ye ta nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ yaa Farasisi vuoŋ yi wo yada?.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: N yene bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me nɩŋ, n yine ka vuosi chaanɩŋ.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: Ta die balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ yigi wo hɔgʋ wa gie ta ʋ dʋa aŋaŋ dembiŋ dieke dɩ kana ka yi ʋ chʋrʋ.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Die ʋ bala yaa gamma Judasi vuodieke dɩ yine Simoni Asikaroti bʋa wa wɩa. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ, ta die baa ʋ keŋ posi ʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Kpɩkpaakʋ gie dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ nan chʋʋsɩ ba.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Yisa dɩ bala naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yi tantɩ ma, a bʋʋlɩbʋ a nagɩ tɩrɩgɩ daa wa nine.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Tɩ mɩraha sʋŋ Mosisi die baarɩ dɩ hɔgʋ wa mi chanchaaŋ mʋ ba taa wa aŋaŋ tana a kʋʋ mɩŋ, ta fʋnɩŋ, lalɩa fʋ balɩ.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Nikodimosi die yiwo Farasisi vuosi wʋnyɩ, die ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ wolinne a ga Yisa jigiŋ. Die ʋ balɩ ba dɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: ta balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Gamma a ga nɩtɩ fʋ nine Siloami gbagɩrɩ ma. Die wɩa daa wa die dɩ ga, a ga nɩtɩ ʋ nine ta yese, ta viiri kuli tigiŋ.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Die ba balɩ wa naa dɩ ba ye ʋ nan balɩ wʋbɩaŋ aŋ ba galɩŋ wa. Ama Yisa die dɩ sʋʋŋ a zʋʋrɩ a nagɩ ʋ nuubiŋ a maaga wɩa tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Tɩ mɩraha ka yi sieŋ dɩ tɩ dii vuoŋ sarɩya a baarɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya ta ye ka wʋŋ wa ta ye wudieke ʋ yine.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Die ʋ jʋjʋrʋtaŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ wone a ye wo ta ʋ jʋʋsa wa die dɩ pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Daa daa wa gie ŋaana a kala gie a jʋʋsa wa.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Die ba ye a pɩasa Yisa wɩa yaa gamma hɔgʋ wa ma. Womi Yisa die dɩ hagɩ zie a baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ yene ka yi bɩaŋ, die vuoke woliŋ a nagɩ taŋ a taa wa.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ gbaŋ nyɩŋ wa Galili yaa? Karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ta nan ye dɩ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa ye ka nyɩŋ Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ wondene Ama bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ daa ʋ wondie, ʋ ta nɩasɩ wa mɩŋ. Die daa wa gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine daa wa mi.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Ta die a bɩ sʋʋŋ zʋʋrɩ a maaga tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa fʋ yi ta fʋ ninehe dɩ yuori.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Die ba wʋnna balɩkʋ naa wa, die ba mana die dɩ nyɩŋ mi wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ, nyɩŋkʋraha die wolinne nyɩŋ, ta die va Yisa ta hɔgʋ wa die dɩ ye zie mi.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Daa dieke saaŋ dɩ dine Yisa wa bʋʋlɩna tantɩ a nagɩ a tɩrɩgɩ n ninehe, ta balɩ dɩ, Gamma Siloami gbagɩrɩ ma a nɩtɩ fʋ nine. Die wɩa die maŋ ga, n dene nɩtɩ a kpatɩ wa maŋ bɩagɩ a yese.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Yisa die dɩ hagɩ zie a pɩasɩ hɔgʋ wa dɩ, Ba bie sɩa? Vuoŋ ka tɩalɩya dɩ ʋ balɩ a chʋʋsɩ fʋ.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Sɩa ʋ beri? Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N ka sɩba.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, vuoŋ ka tɩalɩya. Ta Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N gbaŋ n kaaŋ balɩ a chʋʋsɩ fʋ, gamma ama da bɩ yi bɩaŋ bɩbra.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Die wɩa die ba nagɩ daa wa ga Farasisi vuosisi jigiŋ.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yi vuosisi bɩbra dɩ, Manɩŋ n yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi chaanɩŋ, ta vuodieke mana dɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ nan dɩ yaa chaaŋ dieke dɩ chaana ʋ beriŋ, ta ʋ kaaŋ dɩ dɩa mɩa lɩmɩŋ ma.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Daa dieke die Yisa die dɩ bʋʋlɩ tantɩ a yuori daa wa ninehe die yiwo davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Ta Farasisi vuosisi die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ nyɩɩna ma fʋ balala yaa gamma fʋ ma, ta wudieke fʋ balala wa dɩ yi yɔrɩ.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Ta Farasisi vuosisi die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ daa wa ʋ ninehe dɩ yuorine dene. Ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Ʋ bʋʋlɩ wa tantɩ a tɩrɩgɩ n nine, maŋ nɩtɩ ha ta yese.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ bala yaa gamma n ma, ama wudieke n bala wa yiwo wusie, dama n sɩba jigidieke n nyɩnna keŋ mɩŋ ta bɩ sɩba jigidieke n ganana. Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ ka sɩba jigidieke n nyɩnna aŋaŋ jigidieke n ganana.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Farasisi vuosisi bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Yisa wa ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, dama ʋ ka dɩɩ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ mɩraha. Ama ba bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Lalɩa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ tʋŋ mamachi wʋkpɩɩka gie? Die wɩa die ba nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ die vuoŋ sarɩya yaa gamma vuota chʋara dɩ sɩna dene, ama manɩŋ n ka die vuoŋ sarɩya.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Die ba bɩ pɩasɩ daa wa dɩ, Daa dieke dɩ yuorine fʋ ninehe, bɩa fʋ yile yaa gamma ʋ ma? Daa wa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ keŋ baa n dii vuoŋ sarɩya, n nan dii ʋ sarɩya aŋaŋ wusie, dama n ka die sarɩya n nyɩɩna ma ama n Chʋa vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa bie n jigiŋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die ye ka yi yada dɩ die ʋ yiwo yɩɩ ama ta wa yese lele. Die wɩa die ba wa daa wa chɔɔŋ wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ a keŋ.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Dɩ maagɩya nɩ mɩra sʋŋ dɩ dɩɩ yi vuosi bale dɩ dii siara ta balɩ wʋbalɩmɩŋ wudieke ba bala wa yiwo wusie.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ bʋa wa wʋnna? Wʋnɩŋ nɩ baarɩ dɩ nɩ mɩɩrɩ wa ta ʋ yɩɩŋya wa? Ta lalɩa ʋ bɩagɩ yese lele.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Manɩŋ n bala yaa gamma n ma, ta n Chʋa vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ bala yaa gamma n ma.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo tɩ bʋa, ta sɩba dɩ tɩ mɩɩrɩ wa ta ʋ yɩɩŋya mɩŋ.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ Chʋa wa bie sɩa? Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ seŋ ka sɩba mɩŋ yaa n Chʋa wa. Dɩɩ yi nɩ tɩŋ sɩba mɩŋ nɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩba ʋ Chʋa gbaŋ.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Ama tɩ ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yine ʋ bɩagɩ yese lele, ta ka bɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ yuorine ʋ ninehe. Nɩ pɩasɩ wa dama ʋ ka yi bʋabiŋ, ʋ nan bɩagɩ balɩ yɩ ʋ gbaŋ.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yisa die balɩ wa wɩaha gie mana saŋŋa dieke ʋ dagɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma jigidieke Ŋmɩŋ piini adaka dɩ benne wo; ama vuoŋ die ka yigi wo, dama ʋ yigiŋ saŋŋa die ye ka tʋgɩya.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Die ʋ chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo die balɩ wa naa, dama die ba chɩga Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha ŋmaamɩŋ, dama die ba wɔŋ saŋ mɩŋ dɩ vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ balala dɩ Yisa yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa, ba nan vʋarɩ wa a nyɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n nan ga, ta nɩ nan dɩ dɩa a yaala mɩŋ ama nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, ta nan keŋ kpi aŋaŋ nɩ wʋbɩatɩ. Nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ ga jigidieke n ganana.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Die wɩa ba balɩ dɩ, Ʋ ka yi bʋabiŋ, nɩ pɩasɩ wa.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Die wɩa Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a ga jigidieke n ganana wa. Ʋ yaala ʋ kʋʋ wa ʋ gbaŋ yaa.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Die ba wa daa dieke nine dɩ yuorine wo ka bule me a balɩ wa dɩ, Hʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa dɩ baa fʋ balɩ wa wusie. Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ Yisa yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ bala gʋtɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ, ama manɩŋ n yiwo arɩzanna sɩɩtɩ. Nɩnɩŋ nɩ nyɩŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ama manɩŋ n ka nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Daa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n ka sɩba dɩ ʋ yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieŋ yaa ʋ ka yiye. Ama wʋbalɩŋ dieke n sɩbɩna yine die n yiwo yɩɩ, ama lele n yese mɩŋ.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Die wɩa maŋ balɩ nɩ dɩ nɩ nan kpi aŋaŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ. Ta dɩɩ yi nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada dɩ n yiwo vuodieke n yine wo nɩ seŋ kpi aŋaŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ mɩŋ.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Ba bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa ʋ yi ta yuori fʋ ninehe.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Manɩa yine fʋ? Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, N yiwo vuodieke n balala nɩ a nyɩŋ piiliku me dɩ n yiye wo.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, N wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ ta nɩ ka wʋmma. Bɩa nɩ yaala n bɩ balɩ nɩ bɩbra? Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yaala nɩ bɩrɩŋ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ yaa.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Wɩa pam benne maŋ tɩŋ nan balɩ a chʋʋsɩ nɩ; ama vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa yiwo wusietieŋ ta manɩŋ n bala dʋnɩa ka gie vuosi wudieke n wʋnna a nyɩna ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Die ba wa zɩa wa ta baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yiwo vuoke me kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ, ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ yiwo Mosisi kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ama vuosisi die ka sɩba dɩ ʋ bala ba yaa gamma ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Tɩnɩŋ tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die balɩ wa wɩa a yɩ Mosisi, ama vuoke gie nɩŋ ta ka wɔŋ sɩba jigidieke ʋ nyɩnna.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Saŋŋa dieke nɩ keŋ kɔtɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, saŋka mi nɩ nan mɩŋŋɩ dɩ n yiwo vuodieke n yine, ta sɩba dɩ n ka yie wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n yiko, ama n bala wudieke n Chʋa dɩ dagɩna mɩŋ dɩ n balɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Daa wa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Bɩa mamachi wɩɩŋ wʋnna! Nɩ ka sɩba ʋ nyɩnna jigidieke, ama wʋnɩŋ ʋ yuorine n ninehe mɩŋ!.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Wʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa bie n jigiŋ. Ʋ ka va mɩŋ dama n yie wudieke dɩ nannana ʋ sʋŋ saŋŋa mana mɩŋ.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ ka wʋmma tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ jʋʋsɩŋ; ama ʋ wʋmma yɩa vuodieke dɩ jɩanna wa ta yie wudiekemba ʋ yaalɩnana mɩŋ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Vuosi pam die dɩ wʋŋ ʋ balala wɩaha gie wo, ta yi wo yada.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: A nyɩŋ dʋnɩa piiliku me, tɩ ye ka wʋŋ dɩ vuoŋ yuori vuodieke ba mɩɩrɩna wa ta ʋ yi yɩɩ nine.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩ yi Juu vuodiekemba dɩ yine wo yada dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ dɩ n dagɩkʋ, nɩ seŋ yiwo n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ;.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Dɩɩ yi daa wa gie dɩ ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ʋ tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: ta nɩ nan dɩ sɩba wusieke ta wusieke nan gbatɩ nɩ taaŋ.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ ba mɩɩrɩ fʋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ, mɩnɩa yine fʋ, fʋ yaala fʋ dagɩ tɩ? Ta die yagɩ wa a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ tɩ yiwo Abarahami haagɩsɩ ta ye ka yi vuoŋ yɔŋɩsɩ. Lalɩa fʋ bala dɩ, Tɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ? .

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ ba yagɩ wa daa wa a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me, die ʋ yaalɩ ʋ jigiŋ a ye wo, a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Yi Vuota Bʋa yada mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yinene bɩaŋ yiwo bɩaŋ yɔmʋ.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Daa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, mɩnɩa yine Vuota Bʋa wa? Dagɩ mɩŋ amʋ n nan yi wo yada.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Yɔmʋ nɩŋ ka gʋtɩ ʋ tieŋ deŋ me, ama tigiŋ bʋa nɩŋ gʋtɩ wa deŋ me yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ wɔŋ ye wo mɩŋ, ta ʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balala wɩa aŋaŋ fʋ leleke giena.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Dɩɩ yi manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa dɩ gbatɩ nɩ a taaŋ, nɩ nan seŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Daa wa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ n yi fʋ yada mɩŋ. Ta die sʋʋŋ gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: N sɩba dɩ nɩ yiwo Abarahami haagɩsɩ, ama nɩ mɩa dɩ nɩ kʋʋ mɩŋ mɩŋ, dama nɩ ka yaala nɩ tuo n dagɩkʋ.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n keŋ wo tɩŋgbaŋka gie me dɩ n dii vuota sarɩya, amʋ vuodiekemba dɩ yɩɩŋna wa nan dɩ yese ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ yesinene wo nan yɩɩŋ.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Manɩŋ n bala wudieke n Chʋa dɩ dagɩna mɩŋ mɩŋ, ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ yie wudieke nɩ chɔɔŋ dɩ bala nɩ.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Die Farasisi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ benne mi wo ta wʋŋ wudieke ʋ bala wa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ gbaŋ tɩ yɩɩŋya mɩŋ.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ chɔɔŋ yiwo Abarahami. Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ seŋ yi Abarahami ballɩ nɩ nan dɩ yie wudiekemba die ʋ yine wo mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ tɩŋ wo ninyuoriŋ, nɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ yaa bɩaŋ, ama nɩ bala dɩ nɩ yaa ninyuoriku nɩŋ, nɩ yaa bɩaŋ.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Ama nɩ yaala nɩ kʋʋ mɩŋ mɩŋ n balala nɩ wusie dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa mɩŋ. Abarahami nɩŋ die ka yi wɩɩŋ sɩba naa.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ kana ka daagɩ yiise juoku sanʋarɩ a juu ama ta daagɩ paŋ a juu yiwo gaarʋ aŋaŋ gbɩgbatɩrʋ.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Nɩ yie wudieke gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ chɔɔŋ dɩ yinene wo. Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ yine tɩ chɔɔŋ dieke nyɩɩna ma tɩ yalla, ta tɩ yi ʋ ballɩ wusie.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ daagɩnana sanʋarɩ a juo wo yiwo yiisehe yiyagɩrʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ seŋ yi nɩ chɔɔŋ nɩ nan dɩ cho mɩŋ, dama n nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ ta wa bie gie lele wo gie. N ka keŋye aŋaŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ama wʋnɩŋ ʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Sanʋagbarɩtʋ wa ŋaaŋ yuori wo sanʋarɩ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ juu, ta ʋ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yiisehe saara ba ŋaaŋ wʋmma ʋ lɔlɩŋ ta ʋ yaa ba nyɩna.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Bɩa wɩa nɩ ka sɩba wudieke n balala wa chɩaŋ? Dɩ yiwo nɩ kana ka bɩagɩ wʋmma wudieke n balala wa mɩŋ.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Ʋ keŋ yaa ha a keŋ nyɩŋ, ʋ ŋaaŋ dɩ wa a nɩŋŋa ta yiisehe dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ, dama a sɩba ʋ lɔlɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yiwo nɩ chɔɔŋ, Sitaani ballɩ ta yaala nɩ yime nɩ chɔɔŋ wa choti. Piiliku me wo die ʋ yiwo vuokʋʋrʋ, ta bɩ wo wusie, dama wusie wo ʋ ma halɩ bɩta gbaŋ. Ʋ keŋ chɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ ka ŋaaŋ yiwo ʋ tʋntʋŋkʋra, dama ʋ yiwo ŋmɩnchɩbɩtieŋ, ta ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ mana nyɩŋ wa ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Ama a kaaŋ wɔŋ a dɩ chaanʋ kʋaŋ, a nan chɩgɩ ta va wa, dama a ka sɩba ʋ lɔlɩŋ.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: N balala nɩ wusieke wɩa, die wɩa nɩ ka yi n balɩkʋ yada wa.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yisa die taaŋ ba nandagɩrɩ gie ama die ba ka sɩba wudieke ʋ balala ba wa chɩaŋ.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Nɩ jabɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ dagɩ n wʋbɩaŋ? Dɩɩ yi maŋ bala wusie, bɩa wɩa nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada?.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Die wɩa Yisa dɩ bɩ bala ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, manɩŋ n yine yiisehe juoku sanʋaŋ.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ wʋmma Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka mɩŋ; ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ nɩ ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, die wɩa nɩ zeti dɩ nɩ wʋŋ wa.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wolinne a keŋ n ma wa yiwo gaarɩŋ aŋaŋ gbɩgbatɩrɩŋ, ta yiisehe dɩ ka wʋŋ a yɩ ba.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Die Juu vuosi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ Yisa dɩ, Tɩ ka yaa wusie tɩ bala dɩ yiwo Samaria vuoŋ ta jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ yallɩ fʋ wa.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Manɩŋ n yine sanʋarɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ daagɩna n ma a juu nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Ʋ nan keŋ juu ta nyɩna, ta yese nyindiike vɩɩna.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Jɩmbɩaŋ ka yallɩ mɩŋ. Manɩŋ n yɩ wa n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jɩlɩma ta nɩnɩŋ nɩ daansa mɩŋ yɔrɩ.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Gaarʋ nɩŋ keŋye dɩ ʋ keŋ gaarɩ yiisehe, ta kʋʋ ha, ta bɩ chʋʋsɩ ha. Manɩŋ, n keŋye mɩŋ dɩ a ye miivoli dieke dɩ yine miivoli wusie ke.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Manɩŋ n ka yaala bɩrɩŋ a yɩa n gbaŋ. Ama Ŋmɩŋ yaalala ka yɩa mɩŋ, ta ʋ bɩ yi wusie sarɩyadiiru.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Manɩŋ n yiwo yiyagɩrɩvɩɩnʋ, ta yiyagɩrɩvɩɩnʋ kpinene ʋ yiise wɩa.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ dɩna n wɩaha kaaŋ wɔŋ kpi.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ yagɩnana yiise ligire wɩa, ta ka yi yiyagɩrʋ wusie, ta ka bɩ sɩɩ yiisehe nɩŋ, dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ ye gbaanchʋŋ dɩ kieŋ saŋŋa dieke, sie ʋ chɩgɩ ta vaa yiisehe; ta gbaanchʋrɩ keŋ yigi he ta yiise tɩalɩkaha chɩgɩ jaasɩ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ta Juu vuosisi die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Lele tɩ wa sɩba a baarɩ dɩ jɩmbɩaŋ yalla fʋ. Abarahami die kpiye mɩŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩaŋ die dɩ kpi, ama baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ dɩna n wɩaha kaaŋ wɔŋ kpi.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Ʋ ŋaaŋ chɩga dama ʋ yaga yiisehe ligire wɩa, ta ʋ baga wo yiisehe me.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Tɩ chɔɔŋ Abarahami die kpiye mɩŋ, tɩ yaa tama dɩ ka yile dɩ tɩaŋ Abarahami mɩŋ? Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba gbaŋ die kpiye mɩŋ. Mɩnɩa fʋ yile dɩ yiye.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Manɩŋ n yiwo yiyagɩrɩvɩɩnʋ; Sɩba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩbɩna mɩŋ, ta n gbaŋ maŋ sɩba wa die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, n sɩba n yiise, ta yiisehe dɩaŋ dɩ sɩba mɩŋ, ta maŋ yi siri dɩ n kpi a wɩa.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Maŋ tɩŋ bɩra n gbaŋ, n bɩrɩkʋ nan yi yɔrɩ. Ama vuodieke dɩ bɩrɩnana mɩŋ yiwo n Chʋa, vuodieke gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ bala dɩ ʋ yiwo nɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: N yaa yiise ataŋ, ama hanɩŋ a wo yiise juoku gie me. Sie n yaa a dɩaŋ a keŋ, ta a nan tuo n nʋaŋ, ta a mana bɩrɩŋ yiise buuri balɩmɩŋ, ta yaa yiyagɩbalɩmɩŋ.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ ye ka sɩba wa, ama manɩŋ n sɩba wa mɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi maŋ baarɩ dɩ n ka sɩba wa, n nan bɩrɩŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩtieŋ sɩba nɩnɩŋ. Ama n sɩba wa mɩŋ ta dɩ ʋ wɩaha.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ cho mɩŋ mɩŋ dama n yi siri dɩ n kpi, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n bɩ ye miivoli.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Nɩ chɔɔŋ Abarahami die yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam, ʋ bala ʋ ye saŋŋa dieke n bala n keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo; die ʋ seŋ ye ke mɩŋ, ta ʋ sʋŋ die dɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Vuoŋ wori a nan bɩagɩ vʋarɩ n miivoli, ntaala n yɩ wa sieŋ. N yaa yiko a nan bɩagɩ nagɩ yi, ta bɩ yaa yiko a nan bɩagɩ keŋ n mɩsɩ ma. Naa n Chʋa die dɩ balɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yi.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Juu vuosisi die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Ye ka tʋgɩ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nʋ, lalɩa fʋ yi ye Abarahami.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Die ʋ wʋbalɩkaha gie die dɩ yi Juu vuosisi dɩ bɩ puo bɩbra.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ba kenne mɩra Abarahami wa, ta n wɔŋ beri mɩŋ.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Ba pam die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla wa ta ʋ juo yɩɩŋyaatɩ. Bɩa wɩa nɩ wʋmma wa.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Die wɩa, ba nagɩ tana dɩ ba taa wa, ama Yisa die lɔbɩrɩ ʋ gbaŋ ta nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Ama bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ yalla kaaŋ bɩagɩ balɩ wɩa sɩba naa. Lalɩa vuodieke jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ yalla dɩ nan bɩagɩ a yuori yɩɩsɩ nine.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Die saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩ dɩ ba dii ba dʋʋga dieke ba ŋaana bɩra Ŋmɩŋ ba jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ wɩa wa. Dʋʋga ka die dii wo Jerusalemi ma. Saŋka mi die yiwo tafaaŋ.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: ta Yisa die dɩ dɩ dɩa lɩɩfaaŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Solomoni lɩɩfaaka, dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wa.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Vuosisi die dɩ taaŋ a giliŋ wo ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yaala tɩ chɩɩsɩ mɩŋ a ga tʋgɩ wa saŋŋa bɩa? Balɩ tɩ wusie: Fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ, ama nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada. Tʋŋ diekemba n tʋnnana wa aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ yikoke, dagɩnana vuodieke n yine.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Ama nɩ ka yi yada dama nɩ ka yi n yiise.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: N yiisehe wʋmma n lɔlɩŋ; ta maŋ sɩba ha ta a dɩ n kʋaŋ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: Manɩŋ n yɩa ha miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta a kaaŋ wɔŋ kpi; ta vuoŋ dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ ha a nyɩŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nagɩna ha a yi n nuusi me, ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ tɩaŋ vuoŋ mana mɩŋ. Die wɩa vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ ha a nyɩŋ n Chʋa wa nuusi me.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Manɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ yiwo jabalɩmɩŋ.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Womi Juu vuosisi die dɩ bɩ nagɩ tana dɩ ba taa wa.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: ama Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Manɩŋ n yi wo wʋvɩɩna dieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yi pam nɩ jigiŋ, a jabɩa wɩa nɩ yaala nɩ taa mɩŋ tana.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Ta ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ ka baa tɩ taa fʋ tana aŋaŋ fʋ yinene wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa, ama fʋ nagɩna fʋ gbaŋ a magɩsa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Fʋnɩŋ ko yiwo vuota, ama ta mɩa dɩ fʋ nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ maagɩya nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ mɩra dieke dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ wa dɩ, wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die baarɩ dɩ, Sarɩya diiribe yiwo n ballɩ.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: ta tɩ sɩba a baarɩ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bala wa yiwo wusie yaa gamma haahuu; ta wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wasa vuodiekemba ʋ nagɩna ʋ wɩaha a yɩ wa ʋ ballɩ.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: Manɩŋ nɩŋ, n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩna mɩŋ a tʋŋ mɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me. Ta lalɩa nɩ nan bɩagɩ a baarɩ dɩ n zɩa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ n balala dɩ n yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Die nɩŋ nɩ da yi mɩŋ yada dɩɩ yi maŋ ka tʋma n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋmaha.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi maŋ tʋʋmaha ta nɩ ka yi mɩŋ yada, nɩ tɩŋ mʋ nɩ yiwo n tʋʋmaha yada amʋ nɩ nan dɩ sɩba dɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ bie n ma ta n dɩaŋ maŋ bie ʋ ma.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Die ba bɩ mɩa dɩ ba yigi wo, ama die ʋ bɩa ta vaa ba.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: ta die bɩ garɩsɩ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ a ga jigidieke Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ ta die bie mi.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Vuosi pam die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a bala yɩa tamba dɩ, Jɔɔn die ka yi mamachi wʋkpɩɩma ama wudieke mana ʋ bala yaa gamma daa wa gie ma die yi wusie.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Die mi vuosi pam die dɩ yi wo yada.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ saaŋ die dine Lazurusi ta ʋ bie Betani ma, ta die dɩ keŋ yʋagɩ. Betani die yiwo tɩŋ dieke Meri aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Maata dɩ benne.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Meri wa gie die yine vuodieke die dɩ sitine tulaarika a yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nagɩsɩ ma wa ta chɩtɩbʋ aŋaŋ ʋ sikpeŋ zoosisi; die dɩ yiwo ʋ nɩmbʋa Lazurusi die dɩ yʋagɩna wa.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Die ʋ tamba Meri aŋaŋ Maata die dɩ tʋŋ balɩ Yisa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ zʋa dieke fʋ faasɩna a cho wo yʋagɩ mɩŋ.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yisa die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Yʋagɩbʋ gie ka baa ka yi Lazurusi miivoli kpatɩŋ, ama dɩ yiwo wudieke vuosi dɩ nan bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ka wɩa, ta bɩ bɩrɩ manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa saaŋ.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yisa die cho wo Maata aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ Meri aŋaŋ Lazurusi mɩŋ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: ama die ʋ wʋnna tʋntʋŋkʋ dɩ Lazurusi yʋagɩ wa, die ʋ wɩarɩ a bie jigidieke die ʋ benne wo damʋʋma ale.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Ta die keŋ a balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ vaa tɩ yiŋŋi ga Judia.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ, daraa ale ka gie, mi vuosi die yaala ba taa fʋ aŋaŋ tana a kʋʋ mɩŋ, bɩa wɩa fʋ bɩ yaala fʋ ga mi.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Chaanɩŋ ŋaaŋ beri mɩŋ a nyɩŋ sʋkʋʋŋ a tʋgɩ jɩŋmɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ chʋŋ ŋmɩntʋasɩ ʋ kaaŋ gbɩŋ nan, dama ʋ yese tɩŋgbaŋka gie chaanɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ʋ chʋŋ yuŋ ʋ nan gbɩŋ nan dama chaanɩŋ wori.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Yisa die bala wa naa, ta bɩ balɩ gʋtɩ dɩ, Tɩ zʋa Lazurusi gʋʋra mɩŋ, ama n nan ga a sʋgɩrɩ wa.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi ʋ gʋʋra nɩŋ ʋ nan ye alaafɩa.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Ama Yisa die daga dɩ Lazurusi kpiye mɩŋ ama ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die yile dɩ ʋ daga dɩ Lazurusi gʋʋra mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ yuori balɩ ba dɩ, Lazurusi kpiye mɩŋ;.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: ama nɩ wɩa n yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ die n wone ʋ jigiri amʋ nɩ nan yi mɩŋ yada. Nɩ vaa tɩ ga ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Die Tomasi, vuodieke ba wasɩnana dɩ balɩ yi ʋ chanchaalɩba dɩ, Nɩ vaa tɩ mana ga aŋaŋ wa, amʋ dɩɩ yi kuŋ, tɩ kpi aŋaŋ wa.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yisa die dɩ gana gbigi Betani wa, die ʋ wʋŋ dɩ ba gune Lazurusi ka daraa anɩɩsa wonde.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Betani die ka yʋa aŋaŋ Jerusalemi; die ka yʋasɩ die yiwo sɩba mali ale.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: die wɩa Juu vuosi pam die dɩ keŋ dɩ ba waasɩ Maata aŋaŋ Meri ta yɩ ba suguru yaa gamma ba nɩmbʋa kumbu wɩa.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Die Maata dɩ wʋnna dɩ Yisa kieŋ wo, die ʋ nyɩŋ ga dɩ ʋ tuoli wo sieŋ me, ama Meri die dɩ wɩarɩ tigiŋ me.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Maata die dɩ balɩ yɩ Yisa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ tɩŋ bie wo giena n nɩmbʋa wa gie tɩŋ kaaŋ kpi.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Ama n sɩba dɩ lele gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ fʋ jadieke mana fʋ jʋʋsɩna wa.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ nɩmbʋa wa nan hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Maata die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, N sɩba mɩŋ dɩ ʋ nan daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine vuodieke dɩ sʋgɩrɩnana kunti ta yɩa ba miivoli. Vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ yi mɩŋ yada, ʋ nan dɩ yallɩ miivoli, ʋ kpi gbaŋ;.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ benne ʋ mɩsɩ ma ta yi mɩŋ yada kaaŋ wɔŋ kpi. Yi wɩɩrɩ gie yada mɩŋ.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Wa, n Yɔmʋtieŋ, n yi yada dɩ fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie wo.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maata die dɩ bala wɩaha a kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ yiŋŋi ga a lɔbɩrɩ a wa ʋ nɩmbʋa Meri, a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩdagɩrʋ wa bie giena ta pɩasa fʋ wɩa.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Meri die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ʋ hagɩ a yi lagɩ lagɩ a nyɩŋ dɩ ʋ ga tuoli wo sieŋ me.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye ka tʋgɩ tɩŋkpaŋka sʋŋ, die ʋ ye bie wo jigidieke Maata dɩ tuoline wo.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Die Juu vuodiekemba die dɩ benne tigiri me ta yɩa Meri suguru wo dɩ yene ʋ hagɩna a yi lagɩ lagɩ a nyɩnna wa die ba dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ. Die ba yile dɩ ʋ gara vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ dɩ ʋ kʋŋ.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Die Meri dɩ tʋgɩna jigidieke Yisa dɩ benne wo, ta ye wo wo, die ʋ sʋʋŋ gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ tɩŋ bie giena n nɩmbʋa wa gie tɩŋ kaaŋ kpi.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye ʋ kʋnnana ta Juu vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ dɩna ʋ kʋaŋ a keŋ wo gbaŋ dɩ kʋnnana wa; die ʋ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ pam.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Ta die pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Sɩa nɩ gu wo? Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, keŋ a ye mi.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Yisa die dɩ kʋŋ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Die wɩa Juu vuosisi dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ ye ʋ faasɩna a cho Lazurusi dene.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Ama bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Daa ʋ yuorine daa yɩɩ wa ninehe? Ʋ tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kagɩ aŋ Lazurusi da kpi.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Yisa sʋŋ die dɩ faasɩ a chʋʋsɩ, ta ʋ ga vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ. Die ka yiwo taŋ lʋgɩŋ vɔrɩŋ ba nagɩ taŋ a ligi ka nʋaŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ nagɩ tanɩ a nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ ma. Maata, vuodieke dɩ kpine wo taa wa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ʋ nan dɩ nyʋʋgɩ dama tɩ gune wo daraa anɩɩsa wʋnna.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Womi Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, N ka balɩ fʋ dɩ dɩɩ yi fʋ yi yada nan ye Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ dene.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Die wɩa die ba nagɩ tanɩ a nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ nʋaŋ, Yisa die dɩ daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, n waasa fʋ dɩ fʋ wʋnnana n jʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: N sɩba a baarɩ saŋŋa mana tuose n jʋʋsɩŋ mɩŋ, ama n bala naa vuosi gie wɩa, amʋ ba nan yi yada dɩ fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Die ʋ balla naa a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ wa aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Lazurusi, keŋ nyɩŋ.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Womi Lazurusi die dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ, logiti die dɩ veli ʋ nuusi aŋaŋ ʋ nagɩsɩ ta garɩbɩŋ die dɩ bɔbɩ ʋ nine. Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ forisi gatɩtɩ ta vaa ʋ ga.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Die vuodiekemba dɩ kenne dɩ ba waasɩ Meri wa pam die dɩ ye wudieke Yisa dɩ yine wo ta yi wo yada.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Ama ba bataŋ die dɩ yiŋŋi ga Farasisi vuosisi jigiŋ a balɩ ba wudieke Yisa dɩ yine.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Die wɩa Farasisi vuosisi aŋaŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Bɩa tɩ nan yi? Nɩ ye mamachi tʋŋ dieke daa wa gie dɩ tʋnnana.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ va wa ʋ tʋma a gara, vuoŋ mana nan keŋ yi wo yada ta Romani nyɩŋkʋraha nan keŋ a chʋʋsɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie mana.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Die ba wʋnyɩ saaŋ dɩ dine Kayafasi ta ʋ yi ba kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ bini mi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ ka sɩba wɩɩŋ!.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ kpɩa dɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ tuo vuosi gie mana kuŋ a kpi a tɩaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana dɩ baaŋ kpi.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Die ʋ ka balɩ naa aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yɩaŋ, ama die ʋ yine kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ bini mi wo, die ʋ bʋga bala mɩŋ dɩ Yisa baa ʋ tuo wo Juu vuosi kuŋ a kpi.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: daa ba nyɩɩna ma, ama ka baa ka yaa wa Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ diekemba dɩ jaasɩna tɩŋgbaŋka gie mana ma wa a keŋ lagɩsɩ taŋ a yi be jabalɩmɩŋ.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Die wɩa a nyɩŋ daarɩ mi yaa gamma die Juu vuosisi dɩ saŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ Yisa.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Die wɩa Yisa die ka bɩ dɩ dɩa yaalɩŋ ma Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta die nyɩŋ mi a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ dɩ gbigine hagɩyeŋ ba wasa ka Efarimi, ta die bie mi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Die Juu vuosi dʋʋga dieke ba wasɩnana gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dɩ gbigi, die vuosi pam die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩgɩsɩ tɩgɩsɩ a jʋalɩ ga Jerusalemi dɩ ba wʋnsɩ ba gbaŋ ta dʋʋgaka ye tʋgɩ.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Die vuosisi die dɩ lagɩŋna Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wa, die ba dɩ dɩa yaala Yisa ta pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Bɩa nɩ yile? Ʋ ka baa ʋ keŋ dʋʋgaka yaa.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Ama kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi die wɔŋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ vuodieke dɩ keŋ ye jigidieke Yisa dɩ benne ʋ keŋ a yɩ ba doose amʋ ba nan yigi wo.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Die daraa ayʋaba die dɩ tɩala dɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka tʋgɩ wa, Yisa die dɩ ga Betani jigidieke Lazurusi die dɩ benne wo, vuodieke Yisa dɩ sʋgɩrɩna a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Mi die ba digi nyindiike a yɩ wa, die Maata dɩ suŋŋi a tɩa nyindiikehe, Lazurusi die yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ dinene aŋaŋ Yisa wa wʋnyɩ.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Womi Meri die dɩ nagɩ kɔlɩba aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ligire dɩ faasɩna a tʋa a sitibu a yi Yisa nagɩsɩ ma, ta nagɩ ʋ sikpeŋ zoosi a chɩtɩ bʋ, ta tulaaribu nyuugiŋ dɩ suuli tigiri mana.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Judasi Asikaroti, vuodieke die dɩ bala ʋ keŋ posi Yisa chɩaka die dɩ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Bɩa wɩa ba ka nagɩ tulaaribu a daa salɩnyɩala kɔbɩsɩ-taa ta nagɩ ligirebu a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Die ʋ ka balɩ naa ʋ baga dɩ benne zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ma wɩa, ama die ʋ yine gaarʋ wɩa. Die ʋ pɔgɩlɩna ba ligire bʋlɔgɩŋ ta die gaara.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ vaa Meri sɔmm! Ʋ yiwo naa a gbarɩ n guule daraaŋ.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ nan dɩ beri saŋŋa mana, ama manɩŋ nɩŋ n kaaŋ dɩ beri aŋaŋ nɩ saŋŋa mana.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Die vuosi pam die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ Yisa keŋ Betani ma, die wɩa die ba ga mi. Die ba ka ga mi Yisa nyɩɩna ma wɩa, ama die ba yaala ba ye wo Lazurusi, vuodieke Yisa dɩ sʋgɩrɩna a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Naa wɩa kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ saŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ Lazurusi gbaŋ;.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: dama die Lazurusi kumbu hagɩkʋ wɩa die Juu vuosi pam dɩ zeti ba kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha ama ta gara Yisa jigiŋ a yie wo yada.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa die kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ kenne gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ Yisa kieŋ wo Jerusalemi ma.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Die wɩa die ba chɩa vaatɩ a nyɩŋ ga dɩ ba tuoli wo sieŋ a faasɩ nata dɩ, Hozaana, Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kienene wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma! Ŋmɩŋ bɩ yi alibarika a yi Izara naaŋ wa ma.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Yisa die wɔŋ vaa ba yaalɩ wa boniŋ die ʋ jʋalɩ a kalɩ ka ma, sɩba dɩ maagɩna die Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ:.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Zayɔni tɩŋ vuosi nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ ye, nɩ naaŋ wa kieŋ, ta jʋalɩ bonpɔlɩ.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Saŋka mi die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ka sɩba wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ; ama Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ kenne a sʋgɩrɩ Yisa a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo die ba tɩɩnsɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ balɩ wa die yaa gamma ʋ ma, ta bɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ ba yiwo dene a yɩ wa.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ benne Yisa jigiri ta ʋ wa Lazurusi a nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ ma ta sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo die dɩ mʋʋla wudieke dɩ yine wo.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Die wɩa kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ dɩ ba tuoli wo sieŋ me, dama die ba wʋŋya mɩŋ ʋ yine mamachika gie.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Die Farasisi vuosi dɩ bala yɩa taŋ dɩ, Tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ lele. Ye, tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana dɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Die Giriiki vuosi bataŋ die gʋtɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ gana Jerusalemi dɩ ba ga jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka saŋka.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Die ba ga Filipi jigiŋ vuodieke die dɩ nyɩnna Befisada tɩŋ, Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma, a balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, tɩ yaala tɩ ye wo Yisa mɩŋ.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Filipi dɩaŋ dɩ ga balɩ Andurusi, ba bale wo dɩaŋ dɩ ga balɩ Yisa.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ kɔtɩ Vuota Bʋa wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ tʋgɩya mɩŋ.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi ba bʋrɩ zabɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma ka ŋaaŋ nyuuŋ mɩŋ ta zabɩnɩ kpi. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ zaaha bɩrɩŋ a nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka pam. Ama ba ka bʋrɩ zabɩŋ ka kaaŋ kpi, ta kaaŋ bɩ a nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka. Ama ka nan yiwo zabɩmbalɩmɩŋ.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ chone ʋ mɩsɩ nan waarɩ ka; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana a cho ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ mɩsɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me nan daansɩ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yaala ʋ tʋŋ n tʋʋma mʋ ʋ dɩ wa n kʋaŋ, amʋ ʋ nan dɩ bie jigidieke n benne wo. Ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan kɔtɩ vuodieke dɩ tʋnnana n tʋʋmaha ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Yisa die baarɩ dɩ, Lele n sʋŋ faasɩ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ, ta bɩa maŋ baaŋ n balɩ? N balɩ dɩ, N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ daa vaa wahalaka gie keŋ n ma yaa? Ama die wɩa maŋ keŋ, dɩ n keŋ a dii wahalaka gie.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vaa fʋ saarɩ ye bɩrɩŋ! Womi lɔlɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ dɩ, N saaŋ wɔŋ ye bɩrɩŋ mɩŋ, ta nan bɩ ye bɩrɩŋ bɩbra.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Die kpɩkpaaŋ dieke dɩ zene mi ta wʋŋ lɔlɩkʋ, bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩɩŋ natɩna Bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ malaka bala wɩa a yɩ wa.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Ama Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Daa n wɩa lɔlɩkʋ gie dɩ balɩ wɩa naa, ama dɩ yiwo nɩ wɩa.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ dii tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi sarɩya keŋye mɩŋ, ta saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ vigi tɩŋgbaŋka gie nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ Sitaani a taaŋ wa dɩaŋ dɩ tʋgɩ.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Ba keŋ kɔtɩ mɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ saŋŋa dieke, n nan vaa vuota mana keŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Ʋ balɩkʋ gie dagɩ wa ʋ bala ʋ kpi kuŋ dieke.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dagɩ tɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa baa ʋ bemme mɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu. Ta lalɩa fʋ bala dɩ sie ba kɔtɩ Vuota Bʋa wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ? Mɩnɩa yine Vuota Bʋa wa gie.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Chaaŋkʋ nan dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ saŋŋa bɩta. Die wɩa nɩ chʋmma ka ma ka yene bie nɩ jigiri amʋ lɩmɩŋ kaaŋ keŋ ligi nɩ, dama vuodieke dɩ chʋnnana lɩmɩŋ ma ka sɩba jigidieke ʋ ganana.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Die wɩa nɩ yi vuodieke dɩ yɩnana chaaŋkʋ yada ʋ yene a bie nɩ jigiŋ naa, ta nɩ nan yi vuodiekemba dɩ benne chaaŋkʋ ma. Die Yisa die dɩ bala naa a kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nyɩŋ kpɩkpaakʋ jigiŋ a ga lɔbɩrɩ.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Aŋaŋ die ʋ tʋnna mamachi tʋnkpɩɩmaha ba jigiri mana yɔrɩ, die ba ka yi wo yada.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Naa die dɩ vaa wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya dɩ bala wa dɩ keŋ yi wusie, die ʋ baarɩ dɩ: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, mɩnɩa tuone tɩ wɩaha? Ta mɩnɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yuori ʋ hagɩrɩbʋ a dagɩ.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Die vuosisi mi die ka bɩagɩ yi Yisa yada dama Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya gbaŋ die bɩ baarɩ dɩ:.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vaa ba yɩɩŋ mɩŋ, ta ligi ba sʋŋanyile, amʋ ba nine kaaŋ dɩ yese, ta ba kaaŋ dɩ sɩba wɩaha chɩasɩ, ta yiŋŋi keŋ manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ n jigiŋ aŋ n gbaaŋ ba.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Azaya die balɩ wa naa, dama die ʋ wɔŋ ye wo Yisa chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta die balɩ yaa gamma ʋ ma.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Die saŋka mi die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha pam gbaŋ die yi Yisa yada mɩŋ; ama die ba chɩga Farasisi vuosi ŋmaamɩŋ ta die ka bala yaalɩŋ ma amʋ ba kaaŋ yagɩ ba nyɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Naa dagɩya dɩ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die yaala vuota bɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ bɩrɩŋ.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine mɩŋ yada ka yi wo n nyɩɩna ma yada, ama ʋ yi wo vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa gbaŋ yada.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩ yenene mɩŋ, yese vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ gbaŋ.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Manɩŋ n keŋ wo tɩŋgbaŋka gie me dɩ n yi chaanɩŋ a yɩ vuosi, amʋ vuodieke mana dɩ yine mɩŋ yada kaaŋ wɩarɩ a bie lɩmɩŋ ma.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩaŋ dɩ wʋnna n wɩaha ta ka dɩɩ ha, daa manɩŋ n bala n dii ʋ sarɩya, dama n ka keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me dɩ n dii vuosi sarɩya, ama n keŋye mɩŋ dɩ n gbatɩ ba a taaŋ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Ta vuodieke dɩ zetine mɩŋ ta ka tuo n wɩaha, sarɩya diiru benne a baa ʋ keŋ dii wo ʋ sarɩya; wudieke n balla wa bala a keŋ dii ʋ sarɩya dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ daraaŋ.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Wudieke n balala ka nyɩŋ n jigiŋ, ama n bala wudieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa dɩ bala dɩ n balɩ.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: Ta n sɩba a baarɩ ʋ wɩaha yɩa vuosi miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ. Wudieke n bala naa, hanɩŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ dɩ n balɩ.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Saŋŋa dieke die gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka die dɩ yene ka piiliye wo, ama Yisa die dɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ, dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a yiŋŋi ga ʋ Chɔɔŋ jigiŋ. Ta die cho ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta die yaala ʋ dagɩ ba ʋ faasɩna cho be die.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Saŋka mi wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die die nyindiike; Sitaani die dɩ wɔŋ woliŋ a yi Judasi Asikaroti vuodieke dɩ yine Simoni bʋa wa dɩ yile dɩ ʋ posi Yisa chɩaŋ.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Ama Yisa nɩŋ die sɩba a baarɩ ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa jaaŋ mana a yi ʋ nuusi me mɩŋ, ta bɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ wa ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ mɩŋ, ta nan yiŋŋi ga ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ nyindiikehe me, ta wuri ʋ jayeekiŋ a dʋaŋ, ta nagɩ bʋdʋba a bɔbɩ ʋ chɩaŋ ma.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ siti nyaaŋ a yi tasɩŋ ma ta piili a nɩta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba naatala ta die naga bʋdʋbɩka dɩ benne ʋ chɩaka ma wa a bilige ba naatalaha.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Ta die keŋ tʋgɩ Simoni Piita jigiŋ, Piita die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, yaala fʋ nɩɩtɩ wa n naatala mɩŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Lele nɩŋ, kaaŋ dɩ sɩba wudieke n yinene wo chɩaŋ, ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ nan dɩ sɩba.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Womi Piita dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ nɩŋ kaaŋ nɩɩtɩ n naatala naada. Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ ka nɩɩtɩ fʋ naatala kaaŋ bɩ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Die wɩa Simoni Piita die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, die nɩŋ da keŋ a nɩɩtɩ n naatala nyɩɩna ma, ama nɩɩtɩ n nuusi aŋaŋ n sikpeŋ a gʋtɩ.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ wone a sɩɩ nyaaŋ, die vuoke bɩ wo dɩgɩntɩ. Die wɩa ka ka yi talasɩ dɩ ʋ bɩ sɩɩ, sie ʋ nɩɩtɩ ʋ naatala nyɩɩna ma. Nɩ mana bɩ wo dɩgɩntɩ, ntaala vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma yalla dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Yisa die wɔŋ sɩba vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ posi ʋ chɩaŋ mɩŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna yalla dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Yisa die dɩ nɩtɩna ba naatala ha a kpatɩ wa, ʋ bɩ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiŋ a yeegi, ta yiŋŋi keŋ kalɩ nyindiikehe jigiŋ, ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ sɩba wudieke n yinene a yɩ nɩ wa chɩaŋ mɩŋ?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Nɩ wasa mɩŋ Dɩdagɩrʋ aŋaŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩ mʋ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ wasɩnana mɩŋ die, dama n seŋ yi mɩŋ.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Manɩŋ n yine nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ aŋaŋ nɩ Dɩdagɩrʋ ta nɩtɩ nɩ naatalaha gie, nɩ gbaŋ nɩ mʋ nɩ nɩtɩma taŋ naatala.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Manɩŋ n yi a dagɩ nɩ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yime sɩba n yine a yɩ nɩ dene wo.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, yɔmʋ wori a tɩaŋ ʋ tieŋ ta tʋntʋntʋ dɩaŋ dɩ wori a tɩaŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna wa wa.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Lele nɩ sɩba wusieke gie, nɩ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam dɩɩ yi nɩ yie he.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: N ka bala yaa gamma nɩ mana wɩa, n sɩba nɩnɩŋ vuodieke n vʋarɩna mɩŋ. Sie wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma keŋ yi wusie. Dɩ maagɩya mɩŋ dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ dinene nyindiike aŋaŋ mɩŋ wa yiŋŋe a tuole mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: N bala nɩ wɩɩrɩ gie ta ka wa keŋ yi, amʋ ka keŋ yi nɩ nan yi yada dɩ n yiwo vuodieke n yine.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ tuone vuodieke nɩŋ mana n tʋnna wa tuo wo n gbaŋ, ta vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ tuone mɩŋ, tuo wo vuodieke dɩ tʋna mɩŋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bala wɩɩrɩ gie kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ pam, die ʋ balɩ yuori be dɩ, Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ wʋnyɩ bala ʋ posi n chɩaŋ.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi daansa taŋ ta ka sɩba vuodieke ʋ dagɩnana wa.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ die ʋ faasɩna cho die dɩ kalɩ a gbigi wo.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Die Simoni Piita die dɩ kamɩsɩ wa a balɩ wa dɩ, Pɩasɩ wa ye vuodieke ʋ dagɩnana.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ wa mi die dɩ vɩɩsɩ a gbigi Yisa a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, mɩnɩa wonde.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Maŋ keŋ nagɩ paanʋkʋ a sugi jɩɩtɩ ma a nagɩ yɩ vuodieke wo, wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi wonde. Die wɩa ʋ nagɩ paanʋkʋ a sugi jɩɩtɩtɩ a nagɩ yɩ Judasi vuodieke die dɩ yine Simoni Asikaroti bʋa wa.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Die Judasi die dɩ dene tuo paanʋkʋ a kpatɩ, Sitaani die dɩ juu wo. Womi Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Yi lagɩ lagɩ a yi wudieke fʋ bala fʋ yi wo.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Ama ba wʋnyɩ die ka sɩba wudieke Yisa dɩ bala a yɩ wa wa chɩaŋ.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Die Judasi dɩ pɔgɩlɩnana ba ligire bʋlɔgɩrɩ wɩa die ba bataŋ dɩ yile dɩ Yisa balɩ wa mɩŋ dɩ ʋ ga daa jadieke ba bala ba nagɩ dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka yaa ʋ ga nagɩ ligire a yɩ zɔɔlɩntielibe.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Judasi die dɩ dene tuo paanʋkʋ die ʋ nyɩŋ bʋnyɩ. Die dɩ yiwo yuŋ.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Judasi die dɩ gana kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Lele gie, manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa wa nan ye bɩrɩŋ, ta n ma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan ye bɩrɩŋ.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ye bɩrɩŋ n ma, die nɩŋ dɩ kaaŋ yʋasɩ ʋ nan vaa manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa gbaŋ ye bɩrɩŋ ʋ ma.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: N ballɩ, n kaaŋ bɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ a yʋasɩ. Nɩ nan dɩ dɩa a yaala mɩŋ, ama lele n bala nɩ wudieke n bala a yɩ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, Nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a ga jigidieke n ganana wa.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Ta lele, n wa yɩa nɩ wa mɩrɩhaalɩŋ: nɩ chome taŋ. N chone nɩ dene wo nɩ mʋ nɩ chome taŋ dene.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ yaa choti aŋaŋ taŋ, die nɩŋ vuoŋ mana nan dɩ sɩba dɩ nɩ yiwo n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Simoni Piita die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, sɩa fʋ gara? Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Lele jigidieke n ganana wa kaaŋ bɩagɩ dɩ n kʋaŋ ama kʋaŋ chaaŋ nan keŋ.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Piita die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, bɩa wɩa maŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ dɩ fʋ kʋaŋ lele? Manɩŋ n yi siri dɩ n zie fʋ naŋ ma a kpi.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ seŋ yi siri mɩŋ dɩ fʋ zie n naŋ ma a kpi? Wusie maŋ bala fʋ, kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma ta chiisi wo bʋtaa dɩ ka sɩba mɩŋ.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine tɩɩ dieke dɩ nyɩnnana nyɩŋnyɩŋka wusie, ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi ka wʋnwʋnsɩrʋ.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Naŋ dieke mana dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ʋ chɩaka taana mɩŋ; ama ta wʋnsa naŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnnana nyɩŋnyɩŋkaha amʋ a nan dɩ vɩɩna a nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka pam.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Lele nɩnɩŋ nɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna wudiekemba n balla a yɩ nɩ wa wɩa.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Nɩ wɩarɩ a bemme aŋaŋ mɩŋ ta n gbaŋ n nan wɩarɩ a beri aŋaŋ nɩ. Tɩɩŋ naŋ ka bɩagɩ nyɩnna nyɩŋnyɩŋka ka gbaŋ; ntaala ka taasɩ aŋaŋ tɩɩka. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ntaala nɩ wɩarɩ n ma.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Manɩŋ n yine nyɩŋnyɩŋka tɩɩka ta nɩ yi ka nagɩsɩ. Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ wɩarɩna n ma, ta n dɩaŋ maŋ bie ʋ ma, nan nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka pam, dama maŋ ka gʋtɩ nɩ ma nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ yi wɩɩŋ.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka wɩarɩ a bie n ma ba nan chɩa wa a taaŋ. Ʋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba naakʋʋka ba lagɩsɩna a juu boliŋ.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wɩarɩ n ma, ta n wɩaha dɩ wɩarɩ nɩ ma, die nɩŋ nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ jadieke nɩ yaalala nɩ nan ye.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ nyɩnna nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna pam, hanɩŋ a bala a dagɩ dɩ nɩ seŋ yiwo n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ wusie, ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan ye bɩrɩŋ.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: N cho nɩ sɩba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chone mɩŋ dene wo; nɩ wɩarɩ n ma aŋ n choti bemme nɩ ma.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ tuo n nʋarɩ, nɩ nan wɩarɩ n chome me sɩba n tuone n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ ta wɩarɩ ʋ chome me dene wo.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: N bala naa a yɩa nɩ dɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke n yalla wa chanchaaŋ nan dɩ bie nɩ ma, amʋ nɩ nan suuli aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: N mɩra wʋnna; nɩ chome taŋ sɩba n chone nɩ dene wo.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Chotikpeŋkpɩɩŋ bɩ wori a tɩaŋ vuoŋ dɩ tuone ʋ zʋalɩŋ kuŋ a kpi.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ yi wudieke n dagɩna nɩ, nɩ yiwo n zʋalɩŋ.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: N ka bɩ wasa nɩ n tʋntʋntɩŋ bɩbra dama tʋntʋntʋ ka sɩba wudieke ʋ jakʋʋŋ dɩ yilinene. Ama n wasa nɩ n zʋalɩŋ dama n balɩ nɩ wudieke mana n wʋnna a nyɩŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Nɩnɩŋ daa nɩ vʋarɩna mɩŋ, ama n vʋarɩna nɩ a yɩ nɩ tʋʋma dɩ nɩ ga nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka pam, ta nyɩŋnyɩŋka mi yi nyindieke dɩ baaŋ dɩ beri haahuu. Ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ nɩ jadieke mana nɩ jʋʋsɩnana yaala n saaŋ ma.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: N mɩra wʋnna maŋ yɩa nɩ; nɩ chome taŋ.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi dɩ haa nɩ, nɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ die ba woliŋ haa mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ tɩŋ yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi, tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi tɩŋ nan dɩ cho nɩ sɩba ba chone ba gbaŋ dene wo. Ama n vʋarɩ nɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma ta nɩ ka yi ka sɩɩtɩ; die wɩa tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ haa nɩ wa.
