eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Nɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke n bala nɩ wa dɩ, Yɔmʋ wori a tɩaŋ ʋ tieŋ. Dɩɩ yi die ba mugisi mɩŋ, ba nan mugisi nɩ gbaŋ; ta dɩɩ yi die ba tuo n dagɩkʋ ba nan tuo nɩ gbaŋ nɩ dagɩkʋ.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Ba nan seŋ a mugisi nɩ dama nɩ yine n sɩɩtɩ wɩa, dama ba ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Die maŋ tɩŋ ka keŋye a balɩ wɩa a yɩ ba, die ba tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ yaa bɩaŋ mana. Ama lele n kenne a balɩ wɩa a yɩ ba naa, sieŋ bɩ wori ba baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a lɔbɩrɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ hana mɩŋ haa wa n Chʋa wa gbaŋ.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Maŋ tɩŋ ka yi tʋnkpɩɩma dieke vuoŋ dɩ yene ka yiye ba jigiŋ, ba tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ yaa bɩaŋ. Ama lele ba yeye mɩŋ ta ye haa manɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Naa yiye mɩŋ dɩ ka vaa wudieke dɩ maagɩna ba mɩraha ma wa keŋ yi wusie, die ba maagɩya dɩ, Ba haa mɩŋ wa yɔrɩ.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: N nan tʋŋ Susuŋŋiru nɩ jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ta daga wusie dagɩkʋ. Ʋ nan nyɩŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ a keŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ta balɩ wɩa yaa gamma n ma.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nan balɩ wɩa yaa gamma n ma, dama nɩ bie wo n jigiŋ a nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke n piiline n tʋʋmaha mɩŋ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: N bala nɩ naa, amʋ nɩ kaaŋ keŋ zeti n dɩɩsɩŋ.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Ba nan keŋ yagɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me. Ta saŋŋa nan keŋ ta vuodieke dɩ keŋ a kʋʋ nɩ ʋ tieŋ nan dɩ yile dɩ ʋ tʋma die a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Ba nan keŋ yi wɩaha gie mana dama ba ka sɩba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ yaa manɩŋ.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Ama n bala nɩ naa amʋ saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ dɩ ba yi wɩaha gie, nɩ nan tɩɩnsɩ dɩ n wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ. Die n ka balɩ nɩ wɩaha gie piiliku me, dama die n bie wo nɩ jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Ama lele nɩŋ n yiŋŋi a gara n Chʋa, vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa jigiŋ. Ama nɩ wʋnyɩ ka pɩasa mɩŋ jigidieke n ganana.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Ama lele n bala nɩ wɩaha gie wo, nɩ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ pam.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, dɩ vɩɩna a yɩ nɩ dɩ n ga, dama dɩɩ yi maŋ ka gaya, Susuŋŋiru wo kaaŋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Ama maŋ ga n nan tʋŋ wa nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: Ʋ keŋ keŋ saŋŋa dieke wo, ʋ nan dagɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi wusie yaa gamma bɩaŋ ma. Ta bɩ dagɩ ba wusie yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋchɩgɩla aŋaŋ wusie yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ sarɩya diile.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Ʋ nan dagɩ ba dɩ ba yaa bɩaŋ ba kana ka yi mɩŋ yadaka wɩa.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: A bɩ gʋtɩ, ʋ nan dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋchɩgɩla dɩ sɩna die, dama n gara n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta nɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: A bɩ dagɩ ba yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ sarɩya diile, dama Ŋmɩŋ wɔŋ dii tɩŋgbaŋka gie naaŋ wa Sitaani sarɩya ta chʋʋsɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: N yaa wɩa pam dɩ n balɩ nɩ, ama lele a nan dɩ dala pam dɩ nɩ pɔgɩlɩ.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka vuodieke dɩ dagɩnana wusieke dɩ keŋ keŋ, ʋ nan dagɩ nɩ wusie mana. Ʋ kaaŋ dɩ bala aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ yiko, ama ʋ nan dɩ bala wudiekemba ʋ wʋnnana a nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta balɩ nɩ wudiekemba dɩ bala a keŋ a yi.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Ʋ nan vaa n ye bɩrɩŋ, dama ʋ nan nagɩ wa n wɩa a balɩ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Jadieke mana n Chʋa dɩ sɩna yiwo n sɩɩtɩ, die wɩa maŋ baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka nan dɩ naga n wɩa a daga nɩ.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Dɩɩ yi sʋaa nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, ta dɩ bɩ yi sʋaa nɩ nan ye mɩŋ.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ? Ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi sʋaa nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, ta dɩ bɩ yi sʋaa, nɩ nan ye mɩŋ; Ta bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Dama n gara n Chʋa jigiŋ.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Sʋaa ka chɩaŋ? Tɩ ka sɩba wudieke ʋ balala wa chɩaŋ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ba yaala ba pɩasɩ wa mɩŋ, die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi sʋaa nɩ kaaŋ ye mɩŋ, ta dɩɩ bɩ yi sʋaa nɩ nan ye mɩŋ. Wɩɩrɩ gie nɩ pɩasa tamba ta yaala nɩ sɩmma ka chɩaŋ?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ nan keŋ kʋma ta die zɔɔlɩŋ ama tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ; nɩ sʋgɩtɩ nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ, ama nɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩkʋ nan keŋ bɩrɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba hɔgʋ dɩ ŋaana baa ʋ mɩɩrɩ ta ʋ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ dene wo, dama ʋ wahala saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ, ama ʋ mɩɩrɩ kpatɩ ʋ ŋaaŋ daaŋ mɩŋ ʋ wahala ka mɩŋ, dama ʋ ŋaaŋ yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ʋ mɩɩrɩna bʋa a keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo wɩa.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Die dɩ sɩɩ aŋaŋ nɩ, lele nɩ sʋgɩtɩ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, ama n nan bɩ ye nɩ bɩbra ta nɩ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam, sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke vuoŋ dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a tuoke a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, nɩ kaaŋ jʋʋsɩ mɩŋ a yaala jaaŋ. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ nɩ jadieke mana nɩ jʋʋsɩna a yaala n saaŋ ma.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Ama a yaa keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, nɩ ye ka jʋʋsɩ a yaala jaaŋ n saaŋ ma. Nɩ jʋʋsɩ ta nɩ nan ye, amʋ nɩ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: N bala nɩ wɩaha gie aŋaŋ nandaga, ama saŋŋa kieŋ n kaaŋ bɩ nagɩ nandaga a balɩ yɩ nɩ ama n nan balɩ yuori nɩ yaa gamma n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Saŋka dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ nɩ nan jʋʋsɩ n Chʋa n saaŋ ma, n ka baarɩ n nan zie nɩ naŋ ma a jʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Dama n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ cho nɩ mɩŋ, dama nɩ cho mɩŋ mɩŋ, ta yi yada dɩ n nyɩŋ wa n Chʋa wa jigiŋ.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: Wusie, die n nyɩŋ wa n Chʋa wa jigiŋ ta keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta lele n nyɩnna tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a yiŋŋi gara ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Womi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Ye, lele ʋ wa yuori balɩ tɩ mɩŋ ta ka naga nandaga.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Lele tɩ wa sɩba dɩ sɩba jaaŋ mana mɩŋ, ta ka bɩ mʋ dɩ vuoŋ pɩasɩ fʋ wʋpɩasɩka. Lele tɩ yi yada mɩŋ dɩ seŋ nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Nɩ da vaa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ chʋʋsɩ. Nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada ta bɩ yi n gbaŋ yada.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Lele nɩ wa yi yada mɩŋ?.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Juone pam bie n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ tigiŋ me ta maŋ gara dɩ n wʋnsɩ a yɩ nɩ. Dɩ tɩŋ ka yi wusie n tɩŋ kaaŋ balɩ nɩ.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Saŋŋa kieŋ, ta ka wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ, ta nɩ mana nan jaasɩ a ga nɩ tige nɩ tige ta n wɩarɩ n nyɩɩna ma. Ama n kaaŋ seŋ a wɩarɩ n nyɩɩna ma, n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan dɩ bie n jigiŋ.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Maŋ keŋ ga a wʋnsɩ a kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ, n nan viiri keŋ a nagɩ nɩ a ga jigidieke n benne wo, amʋ nɩ nan dɩ bie jigidieke n benne wo.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N balɩ nɩ wɩaha gie amʋ nɩ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ n wɩa. Tɩŋgbaŋka gie me nɩ nan dii wahala ama nɩ dii sikimiŋ dama n wɔŋ nyaŋŋɩ tɩŋgbaŋka mɩŋ.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Nɩ sɩba sie dieke dɩ ganana jigidieke n ganana wa.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Tomasi die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ ka sɩba jigidieke fʋ ganana wa, die wɩa lalɩa tɩ baa tɩ yi a ye sieku a ga mi.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine sieku aŋaŋ wusieke aŋaŋ miivoli. Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ keŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ ntaala ʋ daagɩ n ma.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ tɩŋ sɩba mɩŋ, nɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩba n Chʋa wa gbaŋ. Ama a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma nɩ sɩba wa ta ye wo mɩŋ.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Filipi die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, wudieke tɩ yaala wʋnna, dagɩ tɩ fʋ Chʋa wa.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Filipi, n bie nɩ jigiŋ a yʋasɩ mɩŋ, ama fʋ ka sɩba mɩŋ? Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yene mɩŋ ye n Chʋa wa mɩŋ. Bɩa wɩa fʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dagɩ tɩ fʋ Chʋa wa?.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Filipi, ka yi yada dɩ n bie wo n Chʋa wa ma, ta n Chʋa dɩ bie n ma. Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, Wudiekemba n bala nɩ wa ka nyɩŋ n jigiŋ, ama a nyɩŋ wa n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ benne n ma maŋ tʋma tʋʋmaha gie.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ yi mɩŋ yada dɩ n bie wo n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ ma, ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ bie n ma. Dɩ daa die, ama nɩ yi mɩŋ yada mamachi tʋŋ diekemba n tʋnnana wa wɩa.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ yine mɩŋ yada nan tʋŋ tʋŋ diekemba n tʋnnana wa, ta ʋ tʋʋma gbaŋ nan tɩaŋ n tʋʋmaha, dama n gara n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Ta n nan yi wudieke mana nɩ jʋʋsɩna a yaala n saaŋ ma, amʋ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan ye bɩrɩŋ n wɩa.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ jʋʋsɩ mɩŋ jadieke mana n saaŋ ma, n nan yɩ nɩ.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ cho mɩŋ, nɩ nan dɩ dɩ n mɩraha.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: ta n nan jʋʋsɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ʋ yɩ nɩ Halɩkasɩka aŋ ʋ suŋŋime nɩ ta bemme nɩ jigiŋ saŋŋa mana.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Halɩkasɩka nan dagɩ nɩ wudieke dɩ yine wusie. Tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi kaaŋ bɩagɩ a tuo Halɩkasɩka dama ba ka yese wo yaa a sɩba wa. Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, nɩ sɩba Halɩkasɩka vuodieke dɩ benne nɩ jigiŋ ta nan dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ yaa gamma haahuu wo.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: Maŋ keŋ ga n kaaŋ vaa nɩ sɩba bikpiisi. N nan viiri a keŋ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Dɩ kaaŋ yʋasɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra, ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ nan ye mɩŋ. Dama n nan dɩ beri n mɩsɩ ma; die wɩa nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nan dɩ bie nɩ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Maŋ keŋ yiŋŋi a keŋ n mɩsɩ ma, nɩ nan dɩ sɩba dɩ n bie wo n Chʋa wa ma, ta nɩ dɩaŋ nɩ bie n ma sɩba n benne nɩ ma dene wo.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ tuosinene n mɩraha ta dɩ ha banɩŋ ba yine vuodiekemba dɩ chone mɩŋ; ta n Chʋa nan dɩ cho be, ta n gbaŋ n nan dɩ cho be ta yuori a dagɩ ba vuodieke n yine.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Judasi dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, bɩa yine fʋ bala dɩ nan yuori fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ tɩ, ama kaaŋ yuori fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yisa die dɩ bala wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa, saŋka tʋgɩya mɩŋ. Vaa manɩŋ fʋ Bʋa Dembike ye bɩrɩŋ, amʋ n gbaŋ n nan vaa fʋ ye bɩrɩŋ.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ chone mɩŋ nan dɩ dɩɩ n dagɩkʋ. Ta n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nan dɩ cho wo, ta manɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa nan keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a bemme aŋaŋ wa.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Dama fʋ yɩna mɩŋ vuota mana yiko dɩ n yɩ vuodiekemba fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka cho mɩŋ ka dɩɩ n dagɩkʋ. Wudiekemba nɩ wʋnna wa ka nyɩŋ wa n gbaŋ gbaŋ n jigiŋ, ama a nyɩŋ wa n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa jigiŋ.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ yiwo dɩ vuosi sɩmma fʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine wusie Ŋmɩŋ, ta ŋmɩŋ bɩ wori, ta bɩ sɩba manɩŋ Yisa Masia, vuodieke fʋ tʋnna wa.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: N bala wɩaha gie mana a yɩa nɩ n yene bie nɩ jigiŋ naa.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: N vaa fʋ ye bɩrɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me aŋaŋ n kpatɩna wudiekemba mana fʋ yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yi wo.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Ama Susuŋŋiru, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka vuodieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ tʋŋ n saaŋ ma wa, nan dagɩ nɩ jaaŋ mana ta vaa nɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudiekemba mana n bala nɩ wa.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: N Chʋa, yɩ mɩŋ bɩrɩŋ fʋ nɩŋŋa lele, bɩrɩ dieke die n yalla saŋŋa dieke die n benne fʋ jigiŋ ta fʋ ye ka naaŋ dʋnɩaka gie wo.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ maŋ yɩa nɩ daa sʋgɩdʋagɩ dieke dɩ yine sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ. Die wɩa, nɩ daa vaa jaaŋ mugisi nɩ yaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Vuodiekemba fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa, n vaa ba sɩba fʋ mɩŋ. Die ba yiwo fʋ sɩɩtɩ, ta fʋ nagɩ ba a yɩ mɩŋ, ta ba tuo fʋ wɩaha.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Nɩ wɔŋ a wʋŋ n bala nɩ dɩ N gara mɩŋ ama n nan viiri keŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Dɩɩ yi nɩ seŋ cho mɩŋ, nɩ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ n yiŋŋi gara n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, dama ʋ tɩaŋ mɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Ta lele, ba sɩba dɩ jadieke mana fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa nyɩŋ wa fʋ jigiŋ.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: N bala nɩ wɩaha gie lele ta a wa yi, amʋ a keŋ yi, nɩ nan yi yada.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Dama n nagɩ wa wudiekemba fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa a yɩ ba ta ba tuohe; ba sɩba dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ n nyɩŋ wa fʋ jigiŋ, ta bɩ yi yada dɩ fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: N kaaŋ balɩ aŋaŋ nɩ a yʋasɩ, dama tɩŋgbaŋka gie naaŋ Sitaani kieŋ mɩŋ, ama ʋ wo n hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Lele, n jʋʋsa a yɩa ba. N ka jʋʋsa a yɩa tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana, ama n jʋʋsa yɩa vuodiekemba fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa, dama ba yiwo fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: ama n yaala tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sɩmma dɩ n cho n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie; die wɩa maŋ yie jaaŋ mana sɩba ʋ dagɩna mɩŋ dene wo. Nɩ vaa tɩ gamma.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Vuodiekemba mana n yalla yiwo fʋ sɩɩtɩ, ta vuodiekemba mana fʋ yalla dɩ yi n sɩɩtɩ, ta maŋ yese bɩrɩŋ ba wɩa.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Ta lele n kieŋ wo fʋ jigiŋ, ta kaaŋ bɩ a bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ama banɩŋ nɩŋ ba bie wo tɩŋgbaŋka ma. N Chʋa, vuodieke dɩ yine kasɩ, mɩŋŋɩ gbara ba aŋaŋ fʋ saarɩ hagɩrɩbʋ, saaŋ dieke fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa ta daa vaa wɩɩŋ yi be, amʋ ba nan yi jabalɩmɩŋ sɩba maŋ aŋaŋ fʋ dɩ yine jabalɩmɩŋ dene wo.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Die n yene bie ba jigiŋ, die n mɩŋŋɩ gbarɩ ba mɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ saarɩ hagɩrɩbʋ, saaŋ dieke fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa. Die n gbarɩ ba mɩŋ ta ba wʋnyɩ dɩ ka baya ntaala vuodieke dɩ yine Sitaani sɩɩtɩ wa. Naa die yiye mɩŋ amʋ wudieke dɩ maagɩna fʋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa nan keŋ a yi wusie.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Lele, n kieŋ wo fʋ jigiŋ ama n bala wɩaha gie n yene bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo, amʋ ba sʋgɩtɩ nan suuli aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke n yalla wa chanchaaŋ.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Die n nagɩ fʋ wɩaha a yɩ ba mɩŋ, ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ haa ba ba kana ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ wa wɩa, sɩba n gbaŋ dɩ kana ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ die wo.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: N ka jʋʋsa fʋ dɩ fʋ vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ama n jʋʋsa fʋ dɩ fʋ mɩŋŋɩ gbarɩ ba a nyɩŋ wʋbɩatɩtieŋ, Sitaani nuusi me.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: N kana ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ dene wo, die gbaŋ ba gbaŋ dɩ ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Fʋ wʋbalɩkaha yi wo wusie, die wɩa vaa wusieke gie seŋ yi ba yi fʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Die fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie dene wo, die maŋ tʋma ba tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Ba wɩa n naga n gbaŋ yɩa fʋ, dɩ ba yi fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ sɩɩtɩ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: N ka jʋʋsa a yɩa ba nyɩɩna ma, ama n jʋʋsa yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba keŋ yi mɩŋ yada yaa gamma ba wʋbalɩka ma.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: N Chʋa, n jʋʋsa mɩŋ dɩ ba mana yi jabalɩmɩŋ. Vaa ba bemme tɩ ma sɩba fʋ benne n ma ta n dɩaŋ maŋ bie fʋ ma dene wo. Vaa ba yi jabalɩmɩŋ amʋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nan yi yada dɩ fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: Sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ beri dieke die fʋ yɩna mɩŋ wa, kanɩŋ maŋ nagɩ yɩa ba, amʋ ba yi jabalɩmɩŋ sɩba fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ mɩŋ dɩ yine jabalɩmɩŋ dene wo.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: N bie wo ba ma, ta fʋ dɩaŋ fʋ bie n ma, amʋ ba nan keŋ yi jabalɩmɩŋ aŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sɩmma dɩ fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ, ta fʋ cho be sɩba fʋ chone mɩŋ dene wo.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: N Chʋa, fʋnɩŋ fʋ nagɩna ba a yɩ mɩŋ, n yaala ba bemme n jigiŋ, jigidieke n bala n bemme, amʋ ba nan ye fʋ kɔtɩna n saaŋ dene, dama wɔŋ cho mɩŋ mɩŋ ta ye naaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: N Chʋa, sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ tieŋ, tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi ka sɩba fʋ, ama manɩŋ n sɩba fʋ mɩŋ, ta n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gie dɩ sɩba dɩ fʋ tʋnna mɩŋ.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: N balɩ ba fʋ sɩna dene, ta n nan dɩ bala ba yaa gamma, amʋ choti dieke fʋ yalla aŋaŋ mɩŋ wa nan dɩ bie ba ma, ta n dɩaŋ n nan dɩ bie ba ma.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yisa die dɩ jʋʋsɩna Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩkʋ gie a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ nyɩŋ mi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba ga garɩsɩ Kidironi lʋaka. Die kʋaŋ die benne mi, ta ga juu mi.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Die Judasi vuodieke dɩ posine Yisa chɩaka die dɩ sɩba mi dama Yisa die ŋaaŋ lagɩsa mi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Die wɩa Judasi die dɩ ga kʋakʋ mi aŋaŋ Romani sojasi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gbɩgbarɩtɩ diekemba kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi vuosisi dɩ tʋnna. Die ba yaa wa popolisi aŋaŋ kamɔla aŋaŋ ba jɩbɩwagɩtɩ.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yisa die dɩ wɔŋ sɩba wudieke dɩ bala ka yi wo, die wɩa die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa nɩ yaala.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti wa. Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yine wʋnɩŋ? Die Judasi, vuoke dɩ posine ʋ chɩaka die zie wo mi aŋaŋ ba.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Die Yisa dɩ bala dɩ, N yine wʋnɩŋ wa, die ba chʋŋ a nyɩŋ kʋaŋ a nan tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa nɩ dɩ dɩa yaala? Ba baarɩ dɩ, Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti wa.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yine wʋnɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi nɩ dɩ dɩa a yaala manɩŋ, die nɩŋ nɩ vaa n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gie ga.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Die ʋ balɩ wa naa amʋ wudieke die ʋ bala wa nan keŋ yi wusie, die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, N Chʋa, n ka vaa vuodiekemba fʋ nagɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ bɩa.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Saŋka mi ta Simoni Piita die dɩ yaa jɩbɩwaŋ, die ʋ vʋarɩ ka a viisi a gobi kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ yɔmʋ wʋnyɩ nuudiigiŋ tɩbɩŋ a taaŋ. Die yɔmʋ wa saaŋ die yine Malikusi.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yi Piita dɩ, Nagɩ fʋ jɩbɩka a yi ka fɔgʋkʋ ma. Yile mɩŋ dɩ n ka yaala dɩ n dii wahala dieke n Chʋa dɩ nagɩna a zieŋ mɩŋ wo.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Die Romani sojasisi aŋaŋ ba jakʋʋŋ aŋaŋ Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gbɩgbarɩtɩba die dɩ yigi Yisa a bɔbɩ wa.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: ta woliŋ yaa wa a ga Anasi jigiŋ. Anasi die yiwo Kayafasi hanɩɩŋ, Kayafasi die yine kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ kanɩŋ bɩnɩŋ mi.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ ʋ bala Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ dɩ vɩɩna vuobalɩmɩŋ tuo vuosisi mana kuŋ a kpi wo.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Die Simoni Piita aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaaŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ. Die kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa gie die yi wo vuodieke kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ sɩbɩna. Die wɩa die ʋ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ a ga juu kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ gbaakʋ ma.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ wɩarɩ a zie yeŋ me. Die kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi, vuodieke kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ dɩ sɩbɩna wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a ga a balɩ wɩa aŋaŋ havʋʋbɩ dieke dɩ zene sanʋarɩ tɩŋ wa ta yaa Piita a keŋ juu.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Die havʋʋbike dɩ pɩasɩ Piita dɩ, Ka yiwo daa wa mi kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ? Piita die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ, n ka yi ba wʋnyɩ.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Die watɩ benne die wɩa tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ gbɩgbarɩtɩba die dɩ chɔgɩsɩ boliŋ a zie a weele. Piita die dɩ ga ba jigiŋ a zie weele.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Die kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa yaa gamma ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ wɩa.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Die n daga vuosi yaalɩŋ ma, ta daga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma jigidieke Juu vuosi mana dɩ ŋaana a lagɩŋsa wa. Manɩŋ die n ka lɔbɩrɩ a balɩ wɩɩŋ.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Ta bɩa wɩa fʋ pɩasa mɩŋ? Pɩasɩ vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnnana mɩŋ wa. Dama ba sɩba wudieke n bala wa mɩŋ.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ bala naa wa, die gbɩgbarɩtɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ zene gbigi wo die dɩ falɩ wa ta baarɩ dɩ, Die ba bala wɩa a yɩa kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi wʋbɩaŋ maŋ balɩ, balɩ vuoŋ mana giena dɩ dɩ yiwo bɩaŋ, ama dɩɩ yi wusie aŋaŋ wudieke n bala wa, bɩa wɩa fʋ nɩga mɩŋ.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Womi Anasi die dɩ vaa ba nagɩ Yisa a ga Kayafasi jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ; ta die ba ye bɔbɩ Yisa.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Piita die ye zie wo mi a weele bolibu, womi die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Ka yiwo daa wa mi kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ? Ama Piita die dɩ chiisi ta baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ, n ka yi ba wʋnyɩ.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Die kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ yɔŋɩsɩ wʋnyɩ, vuodieke nɩmbʋa Piita dɩ gobine ʋ tɩbɩrɩ dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Die n ka ye fʋ aŋaŋ wa kʋakʋ ma.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Ta Piita die dɩ bɩ chiisi. Lele womi kparaaŋ dɩ kʋŋ.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Die ba nagɩ Yisa a nyɩŋ Kayafasi tigiŋ me a yaa wa ga tɩŋgbaŋka jakʋʋrɩ vuodieke die dɩ dine Paliti tigiŋ. Die sʋkʋleeliŋ die wonde. Die Juu vuosi die ka juu ʋ tigiri dama die ba yaala ba mɩŋŋɩ a pɔgɩlɩ ba gbaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa, amʋ ba nan bɩagɩ dii gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Die wɩa Paliti die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga ba jigiŋ, a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Bɩa nɩ baa daa wa gie yiye.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Ʋ tɩŋ ka yi wɩɩŋ, tɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ yaa wa keŋ fʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Ta Paliti dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yaa wa a ga dii ʋ sarɩya nɩ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna dene. Die Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ wo sieŋ dɩ tɩ kʋʋ vuoŋ.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Naa die yiye mɩŋ, amʋ Yisa die dɩ balla dɩ ʋ nan keŋ kpi kuŋ dieke wo keŋ yi wusie.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Die Paliti die dɩ yiŋŋi juu tigiri sʋŋ ta wa Yisa a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ wʋpɩasɩkɩŋ wʋnna yaa vuosi bala fʋ yaa gamma n ma.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Paliti die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Yi tama n yiwo Juu vuoŋ yaa? Dɩ yiwo fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ vuosi aŋaŋ ba kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ yalla fʋ a keŋ n jigiŋ. Bɩa fʋ yi.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Yisa die baarɩ dɩ, N naarɩ ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ, ka tɩŋ yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ n dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba tɩŋ nan wagɩ dɩ Juu vuosisi da yigi mɩŋ. Aayɩ, n naarɩ ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Die Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Die nɩŋ seŋ yiwo naaŋ? Die Yisa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Wa, fʋnɩŋ fʋ balla dɩ n yiwo naaŋ. Ba mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ a keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wʋbalɩmɩŋ wɩa, dɩ n balɩ yaa gamma wusieke me. Ta vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine wusieke sɩɩtɩ dɩ wʋma mɩŋ.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Bɩa yine wusie? Balɩkʋ gie kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nyɩŋ ga Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha jigiŋ, a baarɩ dɩ, N ka ye wudieke daa wa dɩ yine a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Ama nɩ chʋaraha sʋŋ n ŋaaŋ vʋarɩ wa dansarɩka vuoŋ a yɩa nɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga saŋŋa mana. Nɩ yaala n vʋarɩ wa Juu naaŋ wa a yɩ nɩ yɔrɩ.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Aayɩ, daa wʋnɩŋ, tɩ yaala Barabasi. Barabasi die yiwo gbɩgbatɩrʋ.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Womi Paliti die dɩ vaa ba tɩbɩ Yisa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Ta sojasisi die dɩ nagɩ haŋgɔɔsɩ a wʋnsɩ sibubiŋ a nagɩ a bubi wo, a nagɩ jayeekiwaŋ ŋmɩnɩŋ a yeegi wo.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: a keŋ bala dɩ, Juu vuosi naaŋ wa tɩ waasa fʋ, ta die fala wa.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Die Paliti dɩ bɩ nyɩŋ vuosisi jigiŋ bɩbra a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ ye, n nan yaa wa a keŋ nyɩŋ a yɩ nɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma dɩ manɩŋ a ka ye wudieke ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Die Yisa dɩ kenne nyɩŋ ta bubi haŋgɔɔsɩ sibubike ta yeegi jayeekiwaŋ ŋmɩnka, Paliti die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Daa wa wʋnna.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Die kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gbɩgbarɩtɩba die dɩ yene Yisa wa ba nata dɩ, Nɩ kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma, nɩ kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma. Ama Paliti die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ nagɩ wa a ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma. Dama manɩŋ n ka ye wudieke ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Ʋ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa! Tɩ mɩraha dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ mʋ ba kʋʋ wa mɩŋ.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Paliti die dɩ wʋnna ba bala naa wa, die ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ bɩ faasɩ yigi wo.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Die ʋ yiŋŋi juu gbaakʋ sʋŋ a pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, Sɩa fʋ nyɩŋ? Ama Yisa die ka yiŋŋi balɩ wa wɩɩŋ.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Ta Paliti dɩ bɩ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Kaaŋ tɔgɩŋ mɩŋ? Sɩmma dɩ n yaa yiko a baaŋ bɩagɩ a vaa fʋ ga yɔrɩ, ta bɩ nan bɩagɩ vaa ba kpaasɩ fʋ dagarɩkɩŋ ma.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ yaa wa yiko n ma dama Ŋmɩŋ nagɩna ka a yɩ fʋ. Die wɩa vuodieke dɩ nagɩna mɩŋ a keŋ yi fʋ nuusi me bɩaŋ tɩaŋ fʋ mɩŋ.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Paliti die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die ʋ mɩa dɩ ʋ tɩŋ vaa Yisa ga yɔrɩ. Ama Juu vuosisi die dɩ nata bala wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ vaa ʋ ga yɔrɩ, die nɩŋ ka yi naaŋ Siiza zʋa, dama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ bala dɩ ʋ yiwo naaŋ yiwo naakpeŋkpɩɩŋ Siiza dataaŋ.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Die Paliti dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die ʋ yaa Yisa a keŋ nyɩŋ yeŋ me a sʋʋŋ kalɩ ʋ sarɩya diile gbaŋtɩ ma jigidieke ba wasɩnana Taŋ Koriŋ.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Die ka yiwo ŋmɩntʋasɩ, ta die ka tʋnvʋʋsa dɩ baa dɩ yi gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka daraaŋ. Paliti die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Juu vuosisi dɩ, Nɩ naaŋ wa wʋnna.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Die ba yiŋŋi natɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Kʋʋ wa! Kʋʋ wa! Kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma. Paliti die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yaala n kpaasɩ wa nɩ naaŋ wa dagarɩkʋ ma? Die kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ wo naakpɩɩŋ a gʋtɩ naaŋ Siiza ma.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Womi Paliti die dɩ nagɩ Yisa a yɩ dɩ ba ga kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma. Die wɩa sojasisi die dɩ nagɩ Yisa.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Die ʋ chii ʋ dagarɩkʋ ba yaa wa a nyɩŋ gara jigidieke ba wasɩnana Sikpeŋkogiliŋ jigiŋ. .

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Mi die ba kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma; ta die bɩ kpaasɩ dembisi bale dagarɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ Yisa ma, ta wʋnyɩ mana die dɩ bie ʋ lʋgɩŋ ta ʋ bie ba sʋnsʋŋ.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Paliti die dɩ maagɩ a diisi jaaŋ ma a nagɩ a kpaasɩ a marɩ Yisa dagarɩkʋ dɩ, Nazeriti Vuoŋ Yisa,Vuodieke Dɩ Yine Juu Vuosi Naaŋ Wa. .

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Juu vuosi pam die dɩ karɩŋ wɩɩrɩ gie dama jigidieke die ba kpaasɩna Yisa dagarɩkʋ ma die ka yʋa aŋaŋ Jerusalemi. Die ba maagɩ wɩɩrɩ wa Juu vuosi aŋaŋ Romani vuosi aŋaŋ Giriiki vuosi nyɩŋbalɩka ma mɩŋ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Die Juu vuosi kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Paliti dɩ, Bɩa wɩa fʋ maagɩ dɩ ʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa? Tɩŋ mʋ fʋ maagɩ wa dɩ, Daa wa baarɩ ʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa. .

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Paliti die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, Wudieke n maagɩna wa wo tarɩgɩŋ.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Die sojasisi die dɩ kpaasɩna Yisa dagarɩkʋ ma kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ba nagɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke a puo bʋnɩɩsa, ba wʋnyɩ mana die dɩ nagɩ kaanɩ. A kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba bɩ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiwaŋ dieke dɩ benne sʋŋ ma ba yʋgɩna jamʋʋmɩŋ, ta wo baalɩŋ wa.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Die sojasisi die dɩ balɩ a yɩ taŋ dɩ, Nɩ da vaa tɩ chɩɩrɩ ka; ama nɩ vaa tɩ taa chaachaa a ye vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ dii ke. Naa die yiye mɩŋ amʋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ balla wa nan keŋ a yi wusie dɩ; Die ba puo n gatɩ a yɩ tamba Ta taa chaachaa n jayeekiwaŋ dieke dɩ benne sʋŋ ma wɩa. Naa sojasisi die dɩ yi.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Die Yisa nuŋ die zie mi a gbigi dagarɩkɩrɩ, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Meri vuodieke die dɩ yine Kilopasi hɔgʋ wa, aŋaŋ Meri vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Magidalini.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yisa die dɩ ye ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ dieke ʋ faasɩna cho dɩ zene mi wo, die wɩa die ʋ balɩ yɩ ʋ nuŋ wo dɩ, Geŋ wo wana a yi fʋ Bʋadembiŋ.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Ta balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa dɩ, Fʋ dɩaŋ fʋ naa wʋnna. A nyɩŋ dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi, dɩ nagɩ Yisa nuŋ ʋ ga bie ʋ tigiŋ me.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ wɩɩŋ mana wa kpatɩ mɩŋ, die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nyanyuule yalla mɩŋ. Die ʋ balɩ wa naa amʋ wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa nan keŋ yi wusie.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Die kpagɩŋ die zene mi aŋaŋ daŋmɩɩsɩŋ die ba nagɩ gbaara a lu bʋ ma, a nagɩ saagɩ daaŋ sikpeŋ a kɔtɩ ka jʋalɩ a gbɩ ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Die Yisa dɩ nyune bʋ a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ kpatɩya mɩŋ! Ta die nagɩ ʋ haalɩŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa baa ka yiwo Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ aŋaŋ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka daraaŋ. Die ka yiwo daakpɩɩŋ a yɩ Juu vuosisi, ta die ka yaala vuodiekemba ba kpaasɩna dagarɩsɩ ma nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wɩarɩ mi dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi. Die wɩa die ba jʋʋsɩ Paliti dɩ ʋ vaa ba wɩɩ ba nagɩsɩ ta nagɩ ba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Die sojasisi die dɩ woliŋ a wɩɩ dembisi bale wo ba kpaasɩna mi wo nagɩsɩ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Die ba kenne tʋgɩ Yisa wa, die ba ye ta ʋ wɔŋ a kpi mɩŋ, die wɩa die ba ka wɩɩ ʋ nagɩsɩ.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Ta sojasi wʋnyɩ die dɩ tɔbɩ Yisa lʋgɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ timiŋ. Lele womi zɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ die dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ wɩɩrɩ gie yiwo wusie dama vuodieke dɩ yene ke wo balla. Lele nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nan bɩagɩ yi yada.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Wɩaha gie mana yiye mɩŋ amʋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bala wa nan keŋ yi wusie, die ka baarɩ dɩ, Ba kaaŋ wɩɩ ʋ kɔbɩŋ kaanɩ gbaŋ.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bɩ balɩ jigiŋ kaanɩ dɩ, Vuosi nan keŋ daansa vuodieke ba tɔbɩna ʋ lʋgɩŋ aŋaŋ timiŋ.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Josefu vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Arimatia die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Paliti dɩ ʋ nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka. Die Josefu die yiwo Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ, ama die ʋ lɔbɩrɩ a dɩ wa mɩŋ, dama die ʋ chɩga Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha ŋmaamɩŋ mɩŋ. Paliti die dɩ yɩ wa sieŋ die wɩa Josefu die dɩ a nagɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Ta Nikodimosi vuodieke die dɩ gana Yisa jigiŋ yuŋ kaanɩ wa, die beline Josefu, ta pɔgɩlɩ nyɩnnyʋʋgɩvɩɩna dieke ba bala ba nagɩ yi Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka, die a yiwo sɩba gbarɩgbɩnɩ baŋ anʋ.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Die ba bale wo die dɩ nagɩ Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka a veli ke aŋaŋ garɩhaalɩ yɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ nyɩnnyʋʋgaha, sɩba die Juu vuosisi kuŋ guule chʋara dɩ dagɩna die.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Die ba kpaasɩna Yisa jigidieke wo, kʋaŋ die bene mi, ta vɔrɩŋ die dɩ wɔŋ bie mi ta die ba ye ka guu vuoŋ kanɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ ma.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ bala ka vʋŋ, dɩ Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ wa, ta vɔrɩkʋ dɩaŋ die dɩ gbigi mi wo, die wɩa die ba guu wo mi.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Ta Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ die dɩ keŋ tɩaŋ, ta Alasiri daraaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ ta die dɩ ye sibi, Meri Magidalini die dɩ ga Yisa vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ, ta die a ye taŋ dieke ba nagɩna a ligi vɔrɩkʋ nʋaŋ dɩ biline.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ chɩgɩ a ga Simoni Piita aŋaŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ dieke Yisa dɩ faasɩna a cho wo jigiŋ, ta balɩ ba dɩ, Ba vʋarɩ wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ a nyɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ ma, ta tɩ ka sɩba jigidieke ba nagɩna wa a ga dʋaŋ.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Die Piita aŋaŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi die dɩ chɩga gara.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: ama Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi die dɩ faasɩ a chɩgɩ a tɩaŋ Piita a woliŋ a ga tʋgɩ vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ mi die dɩ sʋʋŋ zʋʋrɩ a ye garɩhaalɩ yɩalɩkʋ ba nagɩna guu Yisa wa dɩ dʋana ama die ʋ ka juu vɔrɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Womi Simoni Piita, vuodieke dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaka dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, a juu vɔrɩkʋ sʋŋ a ye garɩkʋ dɩ dʋana.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: aŋaŋ garɩ dieke ba nagɩna vili Yisa sikpeku. Die garɩkʋ mi die kpeŋ ba kpenne ka dene wo a vili ʋ sikpeku a dʋa ka nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi die dɩ wolinne a tʋgɩ wa gbaŋ die dɩ juu vɔrɩkʋ sʋŋ a ye ta yi yada.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ama die ba ye ka sɩba wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma chɩaŋ, dɩ Yisa nan hagɩ kuŋ me wo.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Genke kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ bɩ nyɩŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ, gbagɩkpɩɩŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Taberisi ma. Naa die ʋ nagɩna ʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ ba:.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Womi die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yiŋŋi a kuli.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Die Simoni Piita aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ Nataniali, , aŋaŋ Zebedi ballɩlɩ, aŋaŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bale bataŋ, die lagɩsɩna.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Ama Meri nɩŋ die dɩ wɩarɩ a zie vɔrɩkʋ jigiŋ a kʋma. Die ʋ kʋnana wa die ʋ sʋʋŋ zʋʋrɩ a daansɩ vɔrɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Womi Simoni Piita dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N gara zaasɩŋ yigiŋ. Die ba balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ nan ga aŋaŋ fʋ. Die wɩa die ba juu haarɩŋ, ama kanɩŋ yuku mi die ba ka yigi jaaŋ mana.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: a ye malakasi bale dɩ yeegine nyɩŋyɩala wʋnyɩ dɩ kalɩ jigidieke die ba nagɩna Yisa nyɩŋgbaŋka a dʋaŋ wa sikpeŋ chaaŋ ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ kalɩ ʋ nagɩsɩ chaaŋ.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Ka sʋkʋleeliŋ Yisa die dɩ keŋ zie gbagɩrɩ nʋaŋ ama ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ka mɩŋŋɩya dɩ dɩ yiwo wʋnɩŋ.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Hɔgʋ, bɩa wɩa fʋ kʋma? Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Ba nagɩ wa n Yɔmʋtieŋ ta maŋ ka sɩba jigidieke ba nagɩna wa a ga dʋaŋ.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Die ʋ wa ba dɩ, N zʋamba, nɩ ka yigi zaasɩŋ? Ta ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa, wa.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Die ʋ balla naa a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ yiŋŋi a ye Yisa dɩ zene mi, ama die ʋ ka mɩŋŋɩya dɩ dɩ yiwo Yisa.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ nagɩ nɩ nɩɩkʋ a taaŋ haarɩkʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, ta nɩ nan yigi ataŋ. Die wɩa die ba nagɩ nɩɩkʋ a taaŋ, ta die ka bɩagɩ a datɩ ka a keŋ jʋalɩ, dama die ka faasɩ yigi wo zaasɩŋ pam.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N taa, bɩa wɩa fʋ kʋma? Mɩnɩa fʋ dɩ dɩa yaala? Meri nɩŋ die yile dɩ dɩ yiwo kʋakʋ tʋntʋntʋ, ta die balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩna wa, dagɩ mɩŋ jigidieke fʋ nagɩna wa a ga dʋaŋ aŋ n ga nagɩ wa.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ dieke ʋ faasɩna cho wo dɩ balɩ a yɩ Piita dɩ, Dɩ yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo. Die Simoni Piita dɩ wʋnna dɩ dɩ yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, ʋ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiŋ a yeegi ta die yugi a sʋʋŋ nyaabʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Yisa die dɩ baarɩ dɩ Meri, die Meri die dɩ yiŋŋi a tuoli wo a wa wa Juu vuosi jabalɩkɩŋ ma dɩ, Raboni.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Die kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ banɩŋ ba die dɩ dɔŋ haarɩkʋ a keŋ gbagɩrɩ nʋaŋ ta haarɩkʋ dɩ data nɩɩkʋ dɩ suuline aŋaŋ zaasɩbʋ, dama die ba ka yʋa aŋaŋ gaaŋ, ba aŋaŋ gaaŋ die yiwo sɩba naŋ magɩsa kɔbɩga.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Da keŋ a gbɩ mɩŋ, dama n ye ka yiŋŋi ga ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ n Chʋa jigiŋ, ama ga a ga balɩ n nɩmballɩlɩ dɩ n yiŋŋi gara n Chʋa vuodieke dɩ yine nɩ gbaŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ wa jigiŋ; wʋnɩŋ ʋ bɩna a yi n Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ gbaŋ nɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Die ba tʋgɩna gaarɩ die ba ye paanʋ aŋaŋ bolikaala, ta zaasɩŋ dɩ diisi bolikaalaha ma.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Die wɩa Meri Magidalini die dɩ ga a ga Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ a balɩ ba dɩ ʋ ye wo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo. Ta balɩ ba wudieke ʋ bala wa wa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ yaa zaasɩ dieke nɩ yigine wo ataŋ a keŋ.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Die Alasirika mi daraaŋ yuŋ, Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ a bie juoŋ me ta kparɩ, dama die ba chɩga Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha ŋmaamɩŋ mɩŋ. Womi Yisa die dɩ keŋ zie ba sʋnsʋŋ, a baarɩ dɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme nɩ ma.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Die wɩa, Simoni Piita die dɩ juu haarɩkʋ a datɩ nɩɩkʋ a keŋ jʋalɩ gaaŋ, ka suuli aŋaŋ zaasɩkpɩɩma; die ba bɩɩsɩ ha a yi nyinti kɔbɩga aŋaŋ baŋɩsɩ-nʋ aŋaŋ ataa. Aŋaŋ die a dala wa mana, ama nɩɩkʋ die ka chɩɩrɩya.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Die ʋ balla naa a kpatɩ wa, die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusisi vɔrɩtɩtɩ aŋaŋ ʋ lʋgɩŋ vɔrɩkʋ a dagɩ ba. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yene wo wo, ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ paalɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ keŋ a ŋɔbɩ. Ama ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ gbaŋ die wo sikimiŋ dɩ ʋ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine fʋ? Dama die ba sɩba dɩ dɩ yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Womi Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme nɩ ma. N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ dene wo, die gbaŋ maŋ tʋma nɩ.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Die wɩa die Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋkʋ a nagɩ yɩ ba, ta bɩ nagɩ zaasɩbʋ dɩaŋ a yɩ ba.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Ʋ balɩ die ta die voosi a yi ba ma a baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Naa die yiwo bʋtaa ma Yisa dɩ nyɩnna ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ ʋ kumbu hagɩŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ nagɩ vuoŋ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa wa, ʋ seŋ ye tʋmbɩatɩ naachaala mɩŋ, ama dɩɩ yi nɩ ka nagɩ vuoŋ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa wa, ʋ kaaŋ seŋ ye tʋmbɩatɩ naachaala.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Die ba dine a kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ Simoni Piita dɩ, Simoni, fʋnɩŋ Jɔɔn bʋa, fʋ cho mɩŋ mɩŋ a tɩaŋ nyinti gie mana? Die ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Wa, n Yɔmʋtieŋ, sɩba a baarɩ n cho fʋ mɩŋ. Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Die nɩŋ daansɩma n yiipɔlaha.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Ama ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Tomasi, ta ba bɩ wasa wa Yiibiri die wo ba jigiŋ saŋŋa dieke Yisa dɩ kenne wo.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Yisa die dɩ bɩ pɩasɩ wa bule me dɩ, Simoni, fʋnɩŋ Jɔɔn bʋa, cho mɩŋ mɩŋ? Ʋ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Wa, n Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩba dɩ n cho fʋ mɩŋ. Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Daansɩma n yiise.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ banɩŋ ba dɩ bala wa dɩ, Tɩ ye tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ mɩŋ. Tomasi die dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ ka ye yɩɩmaha vɔrɩtɩ ʋ nuusi me ta nagɩ n nuubisi a yi kʋjagɩlagɩsɩ ma ta nagɩ n nuuŋ a yi lʋgɩrɩ kʋjagɩlagɩsɩ ma, n kaaŋ yi yada.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa ka bʋtaa ma dɩ, Simoni, fʋnɩŋ Jɔɔn bʋa, cho mɩŋ mɩŋ? Ta Piita sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ, dama Yisa die pɩasɩ wa mɩŋ bʋtaa ma dɩ, Cho mɩŋ mɩŋ. Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, sɩba wɩɩŋ mana mɩŋ, ta bɩ sɩba dɩ n cho fʋ mɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Daansɩma n yiise.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Ka daraa anɩɩ daraaŋ bɩbra, Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ a bie juoŋ me, Tomasi die bie ba jigiŋ saŋka gie, ta die ba bɩ kparɩ sanʋaha. Die sanʋaha die kparɩya mɩŋ ama Yisa die dɩ keŋ a zie ba sʋnsʋŋ, a baarɩ dɩ, Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ bemme nɩ ma.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Wusie maŋ bala fʋ: Die fʋ yine dalʋakɩrɩ, die aŋ bɔba fʋ gbanɩŋ mɩŋ a gara jigidieke mana fʋ yaalɩnana, ama fʋ keŋ kʋrɩŋ, nan tɩɩrɩ fʋ nuusi aŋ vuogaaŋ bɔbɩ fʋ fʋ gbanɩŋ ta yaa fʋ ga jigidieke fʋ kana ka yaala dɩ fʋ ga.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: ta balɩ Tomasi dɩ, Nagɩ fʋ nuubiŋ a nagɩ yi giena, ta daansɩ n nuusi ta nagɩ fʋ nuuŋ a yi n lʋgɩrɩ ma, ta daa bɩ chɩɩlɩ ama ta yi mɩŋ yada. .

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yisa die balɩ wa naa, a dagɩ Piita dɩ bala ʋ kpi kuŋ dieke ta vaa Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ye bɩrɩŋ dene. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Tomasi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yine n Yɔmʋtieŋ aŋaŋ n Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a ye Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ dieke ʋ faasɩna a cho wo dɩ dɩna ba. Wʋnɩŋ die ʋ kalla a gbigi Yisa wa saŋŋa dieke die ba dinene nyindiikehe, ta die pɩasɩ wa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, mɩnɩa bala ʋ posi fʋ chɩaka.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ yene mɩŋ wɩa fʋ yi yada? Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka ye mɩŋ ta yi mɩŋ yada ma.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Die Piita dɩ yene wo, die ʋ pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ta daa wa gie dɩaŋ.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yisa die yi mamachi wɩa ataŋ pam ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nine me ta ba ka maagɩha a yi gbaŋkʋ gie me.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ yaala ʋ bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n balla n yiŋŋi keŋ wo, ka ye fʋ wo sɩa? Fʋnɩŋ ko dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Ama wudieke ba maagɩna gbaŋkʋ gie me, ba maagɩya dɩ nɩ yi yada dɩ Yisa yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa, ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ. Dɩɩ yi fʋ yi wo yada, nan dɩ yaa miivoli ʋ saaŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Die wɩa die ka mʋʋlɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba sʋŋ dɩ, wʋnɩŋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa mi ka baa ʋ kpi. Ama Yisa die ka balɩ dɩ ʋ ka baa ʋ kpi; ama die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ yaala ʋ bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n bala n yiŋŋi keŋ wo, ka ye fʋ sɩa.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Wʋnɩŋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa gie yine vuodieke dɩ bala wɩaha gie mana, ta bɩ maagɩha, ta tɩ sɩba dɩ wudieke ʋ balala wa yiwo wusie.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yisa die yiwo wɩa pam a gʋtɩ wudiekemba dɩ maagɩna gbaŋkʋ gie me. Dɩɩ yi ba tɩŋ maagɩ a mana a yi gbaŋtɩ ma, n yile dɩ yaalɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a baaŋ nan juu.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: N zʋa Tiwofilusi: N bʋmbʋŋaŋ gbaŋkʋ ma die n maagɩ wa wudiekemba mana Yisa dɩ yinene ta dagɩ vuosi a nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke die ʋ piiline ʋ tʋʋma.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: a yaa ga tʋgɩ daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla wa ga arɩzanna ma. Die ʋ yene ka gaya wa die ʋ dagɩ vuodiekemba ʋ vʋarɩna a yi ʋ tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ, ba baaŋ dɩ tʋma dene.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Die ʋ kuŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nagɩ daraa baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa a nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ daga ba, die dɩ seŋ dagɩ dɩ dɩ ka yi chɩɩla dɩ ʋ bie wo ʋ mɩsɩ ma, die ba ŋaaŋ yese wo mɩŋ, ta die ʋ balɩ ba wɩa yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Die saŋŋa kaanɩ, die ʋ dinene aŋaŋ ba wa, die ʋ yɩ ba nʋarɩ gie dɩ, Nɩ daa keŋ a nyɩŋ Jerusalemi, ama nɩ chɩɩsɩma Halɩkasɩka dieke n Chʋa dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ nɩ wa, wudieke wɩa n wone a balɩ wa.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Jɔɔn nɩŋ die sɩɩ wa vuosi aŋaŋ nyaaŋ, ama daraa gie nʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka nan suuli nɩ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Die Yisa tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ lagɩsɩna aŋaŋ Yisa wa, die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, saŋka gie fʋ baa fʋ gbatɩ tɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi a nyɩŋ Romani vuosi nuusi me ta yiŋŋi a yɩ tɩ tɩ naarɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Die Yisa dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ ka yɩ nɩ sieŋ dɩ nɩ sɩmma saŋŋa aŋaŋ daa dieke wɩaha gie dɩ balla a yi, sie ʋ nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Ama saŋŋa dieke ʋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ baaŋ keŋ a sʋʋŋ nɩ ma wa, nɩ nan ye hagɩrɩŋ, ta nan yi n siaratieliŋ Jerusalemi ma, aŋaŋ Judia tɩŋgbaŋ mana aŋaŋ Samaria tɩŋgbaŋ mana aŋaŋ dʋnɩa ka gie lʋga mana mana ma.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Die ʋ bala wɩaha gie a kpatɩ wa, die ba ye Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vana ʋ jʋalɩ gara ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, ba daansa wa, ta die nɩɩŋmara wɩa ba ka bɩ bɩagɩ a ye wo.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Die ba ye ko a zie a daansa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ʋ gana wa, womi dembisi bale dɩ yeegi nyɩŋyɩala a keŋ a zie ba jigiŋ.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ Galili dembisi, bie nɩ zie giena a daansa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dene? Yisa wa gie dɩ nyɩna nɩ jigiŋ a jʋalɩ a ga arɩzanna naa wa, ʋ nan daansɩ a yiŋŋi a keŋ sɩba nɩ yene wo ʋ jʋalɩna a ga arɩzanna die wo.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Womi die Yisa tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yiŋŋi a nyɩŋ Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me a ga Jerusalemi. Mi aŋaŋ Jerusalemi die yiwo sɩba mali chɩbɩŋ.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Die ba gana a ga juu Jerusalemi wa, die ba ga juu juodieke me ba benne wo: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne mi die wone Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Andurusi aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ, Batolomiwo aŋaŋ Matiwo aŋaŋ Alifusi bʋa Jemisi aŋaŋ Simoni, vuodieke die dɩ yaala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yallɩma ba gbaŋ wa aŋaŋ Judasi, Jemisi bʋa.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Die ba ŋaaŋ lagɩma saŋŋa mana aŋaŋ hɔgʋba bataŋ aŋaŋ Yisa nuŋ Meri aŋaŋ Yisa nɩmballɩ a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Die ka daraa ale kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ sɩba vuosi kɔbɩga aŋaŋ baŋɩsɩ-le die dɩ lagɩsɩ kalɩ, ta Piita die dɩ hagɩ a zie ba sʋŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: a baarɩ dɩ, N nɩmballɩ, sie wudieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vana Davidi dɩ maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa yaa gamma Judasi wɩaha keŋ yi wusie. Judasi wa die yine vuodieke dɩ dagɩna vuosisi ba yigi Yisa wa.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Ama Yisa die vʋarɩna tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ wʋnɩŋ dɩ tɩ tʋma Ŋmɩŋ tʋŋ dieke tɩ tʋnna aŋaŋ wa.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Judasi tʋmbɩatɩ nyʋarɩ ligirehe die ʋ nagɩ a daa tɩŋgbaŋ, me gbaŋ die ʋ nan kpi, ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ natɩ yuori ʋ nyuubie mana die dɩ nyɩŋ a jaasɩ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma die dɩ wʋŋ ka wɩa. Naa chɩaŋ ma die ba wasa mi ba wʋbalɩkɩŋ ma dɩ Akelidama, ka chɩaŋ wone Zɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Dama Dɩ maagɩya Yɩla gbaŋ dieke dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vaa ʋ jigiri yi jigizetikiŋ; da vaa vuoŋ bemme ka ma. Dɩ bɩ a maagɩ dɩaŋ bɩbra dɩ, Vaa vuogaaŋ zie ʋ nagɩsɩ ma a tʋma ʋ tʋʋmaha.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Die wɩa dɩ mʋ tɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ dɩna tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa a dɩa saŋŋa mana wa wʋnyɩ.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: a piili a nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ a yaa ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke die Yisa dɩ jʋalɩna a ga arɩzanna ta va tɩ wa, aŋ ʋ gʋtɩ tɩ ma aŋ tɩ dii Yisa kumbu hagɩŋ tʋʋma daansɩa.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Die wɩa die ba vʋarɩ vuosi bale; Josefu vuodieke die ba wasɩnana Baasabasi , aŋaŋ Matiasi.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Womi die ba jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋnɩŋ fʋ sɩbɩna vuoŋ mana sʋŋ dɩ sɩna die, die wɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, dagɩ tɩ vuosi bale wa gie sʋŋ fʋ vʋarɩna vuodieke.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: dɩ ʋ yi tʋntʋntʋ a zie jigidieke Judasi dɩ vana ta ga jigidieke dɩ mʋna dɩ ʋ bemme wo.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Die ba jʋʋsɩna Ŋmɩŋ a kpatɩ wa, die ba yi chaachaa dɩ ba vʋarɩ saara ale wo kaanɩ, die chaachaaka dɩ ga nan Matiasi ma, die ba nagɩ wa gʋtɩ Yisa tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa ma.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ gara Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa, agbaa kʋaŋ chaaŋ hawasɩ ataa saŋŋa die wonde.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Womi sanʋarɩ tɩŋ, sanʋaŋ dieke die ba wasɩnana Sanʋavɩɩka daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ yine gbarɩgɩŋ ʋ mɩsɩ mana ma ma die dɩ kalɩ mi. Daaŋ mana die ba ŋaaŋ chii wo mɩŋ a keŋ kalɩŋ sanʋarɩ gie tɩŋ ʋ dɩ jʋʋsa vuodiekemba dɩ juunene Ŋmɩŋ juoku me ligire.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Die ʋ yene Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn dɩ balla ba juu wo, ʋ jʋʋsɩ ba ligire.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Die ba mana dɩ yiŋŋi a daansɩ wa die, womi Piita die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Daansɩma tɩ.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ daansa ba ta yaa tama sɩba ʋ nan ye jaaŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ wo ligire, ama tɩ nan yɩ fʋ jadieke tɩ yalla. Manɩŋ n yɩ fʋ nʋaŋ Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ yine Nazeriti vuoke saaŋ ma, hagɩ chʋŋ.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: ta yigi ʋ nuudiigiŋ me a suŋŋi wo ʋ hagɩ zie. Lele womi daa wa nagɩsɩ aŋaŋ ʋ naachɩgɩsa dɩ ye hagɩrɩŋ;.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: ʋ hagɩ a yʋgɩ a zie ʋ nagɩsɩ ma a dɩ dɩa a chʋŋ chʋŋ. Womi die ʋ dɩ ba kʋaŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a chʋŋ ta yʋga a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Die vuosi mana dɩ yene wo ʋ chʋnnana ta bɩra Ŋmɩnnɩ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: die ba mɩŋŋɩ wa dɩ vuodieke dɩ ŋaana kala Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sanʋarɩ tɩka jigidieke ba wasɩnana Sanʋavɩɩka a jʋʋsa wa wonde, die dɩ yi be mamachi aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ yine a yɩ wa wa.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Die daa wa die dɩ goori Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn a yallɩ, die dɩ yi vuosi mana mamachi ba chɩgɩ ga ba jigiŋ, jigidieke die ba wasɩnana Solomoni Lɩɩfaaŋ wa.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Piita die dɩ yene vuosisi ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi, bɩa wɩɩrɩ gie dɩ yi nɩ mamachi? Ta bɩa nɩ daansa tɩ sɩba dɩ yiwo tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ hagɩrɩŋ yaa tɩ wʋvɩɩna wɩa tɩ yi daa wa gie dɩ chʋŋ?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke die Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu die dɩ jɩanna wa, ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dieke tɩ chʋalɩba die dɩ jɩanna wa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ kɔtɩna ʋ Tʋntʋntʋ Yisa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ hagɩrɩŋ. Wʋnɩŋ die nɩ nagɩ a yɩ tɩ nyɩŋkʋraha nuusi me, ta zeti wo Paliti nɩŋŋa saŋŋa dieke Paliti die dɩ yaalala dɩ ʋ vaa ʋ ga yɔrɩ wa.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Die ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ vuoŋ ta bɩ yi vuovɩɩnɩŋ ama die nɩ zeti wo mɩŋ ta balɩ Paliti dɩ ʋ vʋarɩ vuokʋʋrʋ wa a yɩ nɩ.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Die wɩa, nɩnɩŋ die nɩ yine ba kʋʋ Miivoliyɩɩrʋ wa; ama Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vaa ʋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ta tɩnɩŋ tɩ yine wɩɩrɩ gie daansɩatieliŋ.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Dɩ yiwo Yisa wa saarɩ yiko tɩ wa dɩ yi daa wa gbaamɩŋ. Gbarɩgɩrɩ gie yiwo vuodieke nɩ wone sɩba ta bɩ yese wo, ama ʋ keŋ ye gbaamɩŋ, dama ʋ yine Yisa saaŋ yada wa wɩa. Ʋ yine Yisa saaŋ yada wɩa ʋ ye gbaamɩŋ ta zie nɩ mana nɩŋŋa naa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Lele n nɩmballɩ, n sɩba a baarɩ wudieke nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ yine ba kʋʋ Yisa wa, die nɩ ka sɩba wudieke die nɩ yinene chɩaŋ.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die wɔŋ balɩ ʋ wɩɩrɩ a yɩ ʋ naazʋalɩba, ta ba gbaŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ vuosi dɩ sie ʋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa dii wahala. Die ʋ vaa ka seŋ yi wusie sɩba nɩ yine die wo.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Die wɩa nɩ vaa nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ta yiŋŋi a keŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ amʋ ʋ nagɩ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: ta ʋ nan bɩ yɩ nɩ haalɩtɩ voosiŋ, ta bɩ tʋŋ Yisa Masia nɩ jigiŋ, vuodieke ʋ wone a vʋarɩ wa a yɩ nɩ wa.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Ama sie Yisa wa wɩarɩ arɩzanna ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ wʋnsɩ jaaŋ mana sɩba die ʋ vana ʋ naazʋalɩkasɩ mba die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa dɩ balɩ wa.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Die gbaŋ Mosisi dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nan daansɩ yɩ nɩ naazʋa sɩba ʋ tʋnna mɩŋ naa, ta ʋ nan yi nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ vuoŋ. Nɩnɩŋ nɩ mʋ nɩ wʋmma wudieke mana ʋ balala nɩ.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka wʋmma wudieke mana wʋnɩŋ naazʋa wa mi dɩ balala, Ŋmɩŋ nan vʋarɩ die vuoke a nyɩŋ ʋ vuosi me ta chʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ banɩŋ ba aŋaŋ naazʋa Samuli aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ kenne ʋ kʋaŋ chaatɩ ta die bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha die wɔŋ balɩ wudieke dɩ yinene daarɩ gie nʋaŋ.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ nʋa diekemba die ʋ yɩna ʋ naazʋalɩba, yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ, a bɩ gʋtɩ, nʋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba gbaŋ yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ. Dama die ʋ balɩ wa Abarahami dɩ, Fʋ haagɩŋ wʋnyɩ ma, maŋ nan daagɩ a yi alibarika yɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ ʋ Tʋntʋntʋ Masia wa ta woliŋ tʋŋ wa nɩ jigiŋ, dɩ ʋ vaa nɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma ta ye alibarika ka.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Die Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ yene ko bala wɩa a yɩa vuosisi, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sojasisi jakʋʋŋ aŋaŋ Sadusisi vuosi dɩ keŋ ba jigiŋ.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: dama die ba jɩɩ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn, dɩ ba dagɩnana vuosisi dɩ Yisa die hagɩ kuŋ me wɩa, ka dagɩ sɩba kunti nan daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Die wɩa die ba yigi be a nagɩ ba a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ dɩ tʋŋ vʋŋ, dama yuŋ die wɔŋ a sibe mɩŋ.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Ama daadaŋ diekemba die dɩ wʋnna Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn balɩkʋ die dɩ yi Yisa yada ta yi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ. Ta vuodiekemba die dɩ dɩna Yisa dɩ yi sɩba vuosi tuse anʋ.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋnna, Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ ba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ ba lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ Jerusalemi ma.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: die ba lagɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ Anasi aŋaŋ Kayafasi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn aŋaŋ Alekizanda aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ tigiŋ vuosi bataŋ.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Die ba yi Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn dɩ keŋ zie ba sʋnsʋŋ ta ba pɩasa ba dɩ, Bɩa hagɩrɩŋ nɩ yallɩ, ta mɩnɩa saaŋ ma nɩ yie wɩaha gie.
