eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ hagɩ Piita ma ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋra.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: dɩɩ yi nɩ pɩasa tɩ jinne yaa gamma wʋvɩɩŋ dieke tɩ yine a yɩ gbarɩgɩrɩ gie aŋaŋ ʋ yene gbaamɩŋ dene.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: die nɩŋ, nɩ mana aŋaŋ Izara vuosi mana sɩmma dɩ dɩ yiwo Nazeriti vuoke Yisa Masia saaŋ ma ta daa wa gie dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ ta zie nɩ nɩŋŋa naa. Yisa wo mi die nɩ yi ba kpaasɩ wa dagarɩkʋ ma a kʋʋ wa wa ama ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vaa ʋ hagɩ kumbu me wo.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ balɩ ʋ wɩaha dɩ Taŋ dieke nɩnɩŋ mɩmɩɩrɩba dɩ zetine wo yiŋŋine a keŋ bɩrɩŋ gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Ta vuoŋ saaŋ bɩ wori tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ vuosi dɩ tɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, sie Yisa nyɩɩna ma.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Die lagɩŋkʋ vuosi dɩ yene Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn dɩ dine dembisi dene wo, ta die ba bɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba gbanɩŋ die dɩ yi be mamachi, ba mɩŋŋɩ dɩ die banɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa die benne.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Ama die ba yene daa dieke die dɩ yene gbaaŋkʋ dɩ ziene Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn jigiri die ba ka bɩagɩ balɩ wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ ba.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Die wɩa die ba vaa ba ye nyɩŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ, ta ba wa ye taŋ.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: ta pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Bɩa tɩ nan yi dembisi gie? Dama vuodieke mana dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma sɩba mamachi wudieke ba yine, ta tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ chiisike.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Ama nɩ vaa aŋ tɩ kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba daa bɩ wa Yisa saaŋ a yɩ vuoŋ, amʋ vuosi kaaŋ wʋŋ Yisa wɩɩrɩ gie.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Die wɩa die ba bɩ wa ba a keŋ juu a balɩ ba dɩ Nɩ sɩmma nɩ bɩ nagɩ Yisa saaŋ a balɩ yaa dagɩ vuoŋ bɩbra.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Ama Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yilime dɩ bɩa mʋna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ? Tɩ saagɩ a yɩ nɩ ta zeti Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ yaa?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Dama tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ vaa wudieke tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ yene ta wʋŋ wa balɩŋ.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Ta die lagɩŋkʋ nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ bɩ kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta vaa ba ga yɔrɩ, dama die ba ka ye wɩɩŋ ba baaŋ nagɩ a datɩ ba tɩbɩŋ, dama vuosisi die faasɩ bala mɩŋ ta bɩra Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ yine wo.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Daa dieke die dɩ yene mamachi gbaaŋkʋ die tɩaŋ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Die ba dine vaa Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn wa, die ba yiŋŋi ga ba chanchaalɩba jigiŋ a balɩ ba wudieke Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ bala ba wa.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Die ba wʋnna naa wa, die ba mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ, fʋ naana ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie, mʋgɩkpɩɩma aŋaŋ nyindiekemba dɩ benne a ma.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: die Halɩkasɩka ma fʋ dagɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Davidi vuodieke die dɩ yine fʋ tʋntʋntʋ, ta die ʋ bʋgɩ balɩ fʋ Halɩkasɩ yiko me dɩ Bɩa yine vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ pɩrɩ aŋaŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ, ta Izara vuosi dɩ sama saŋ tɔgɩtɩ.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie naara aŋaŋ ba nyɩŋkʋra dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ a tuole dɩ ba wagɩ aŋaŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ, Herodi aŋaŋ Pontiwosi Paliti aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi aŋaŋ Izara vuosi die seŋ lagɩsɩ mɩŋ tɩka ma ta saŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ kasɩ tʋntʋntʋ Yisa, vuodieke die fʋ vʋarɩna a zie wo.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Die ba lagɩsɩya mɩŋ dɩ ba yi wudieke mana fʋ hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ yaalɩŋ ma fʋ wone a saŋ dɩ ka nan yi wo.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Ta lele tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, ye vuosi gie dɩ ŋaana a juo tɩ ŋmaamɩŋ dene, ta vaa tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine fʋ yɔŋɩsɩ balɩma fʋ wɩaha gie aŋaŋ dembisi pam.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: ta tɩɩntɩ fʋ nuuŋ ta yɩ yʋagɩtieliŋ ye gbaamɩŋ, ta vaa tɩ yime mamachi wʋkpɩɩma pam aŋaŋ fʋ kasɩ tʋntʋntʋ Yisa saaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Die ba dine a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a kpatɩ wa, jigidieke ba lagɩŋna wa dɩ dɔŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ suuli ba mana die ba piili a bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha aŋaŋ sʋgɩhagɩrɩŋ.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Saŋka mi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana die dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ aŋaŋ sʋŋanyili balɩmɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ, ta tɩa ba nyinti a yɩa taŋ. Vuoŋ die ka baarɩ dɩ ʋ sɩɩwa jaaŋ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Die saŋŋa mi tʋntʋntɩba die dɩdɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa kumbu hagɩŋ daansɩa, ta daansɩaka die yaa hagɩrɩŋ pam, ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yi alibarika pam a yi ba mana ma.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Die vuoŋ wo ba sʋŋ a waarɩ jaaŋ, dama vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla ba gbaŋ gbaŋ kʋatɩ yaa tige ŋaaŋ nagɩha a daa mɩŋ a yaa ligirehe.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: a keŋ tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ, ta ba nagɩ tɩa a yɩ vuoŋ mana ʋ yaala dene.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ die bie mi ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Josefu, ta die yi Liiva buuriŋ vuoŋ, ta ba mɩɩrɩ wa Saapurusi, vuodieke tʋntʋntɩba dɩ wasɩnana Banabasi .

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: die dɩ keŋ nagɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a daa, a yaa ligirehe a keŋ a yɩ tʋntʋntɩba.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Ama daa wʋnyɩ gbaŋ die benne a dɩ Ananiasi ta ʋ hɔgʋ dɩaŋ dɩ dɩ Safara. Die ba nagɩ ba faarɩ a daa.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: ta die nagɩ ligirehe a taŋ a lɔbɩrɩ, ta ʋ hɔgʋ wa dɩ sɩba ha wɩa, ta die nagɩ ligire tɩalɩka ha a yaa ga yɩ tʋntʋntɩba ha.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Piita die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Ananiasi, bɩa fʋ vaa Sitaani dɩ juu fʋ sʋŋ a nyaŋŋɩ fʋ, ta yi fʋ chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ta nagɩ ligirehe a taŋ fʋ tuone a nyɩŋ kʋakʋ ma fʋ nagɩna a daa wa?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Die fʋ yene ka nagɩ ka a daa wa, die ka ka yiwo fʋ sɩɩtɩ? Ta die fʋ nagɩna ka a daa wa, ligirehe die ka yi fʋ sɩɩtɩ? Ta bɩa yine fʋ yile fʋ sʋŋ ma a yi naa? Fʋnɩŋ fʋ ka chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ wa vuota, ama fʋ chɩbɩ yɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Die Ananiasi die dɩ wʋnna naa, die dɩ kpi a nan. Die ŋmaaŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ juu vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wʋnna ʋ kumbu wɩaha.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Womi dalʋaka die dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ a muuri ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka aŋaŋ garɩŋ a chii wo a nyɩŋ a ga guu.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ hawasɩ ataa, ʋ hɔgʋ wa die dɩ keŋ juu jigidieke Piita die benne wo, ta ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yine.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Ta Piita dɩ pɩasɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ Balɩ mɩŋ, fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ nagɩna nɩ kʋakʋ a daa wa ta tuo ligirehe a mana wʋnna? Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ Wa, bʋ mana wʋnna.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ dɩ nɩ magɩsɩ Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Halɩkasɩka. Daa diekemba dɩ guune fʋ chʋrʋ wa, bie wo sanʋarɩ tɩŋ lele, ta ba nan chii fʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ a ga guu.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Lele womi die ʋ kpi a nan tɩŋgbaŋ, ta dalʋakaha die dɩ a keŋ juu a ye dɩ ʋ kpiye mɩŋ, ba chii wo a nyɩŋ a ga guu ʋ chʋrʋ wa lʋgɩŋ.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Saŋŋa mi die ŋmaaŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ juu Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ wʋnna wɩaha.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Saŋŋa mi tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ tʋma mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma pam vuosisi jigiŋ. Ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana die dɩ ŋaaŋ lagɩsɩ taŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, Solomoni lɩɩfaaka jigiŋ.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Ama vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ wo sikimiŋ a baaŋ gaaŋ ba gbaŋ a gʋtɩ ba ma, ama ta die yɩa ba jɩlɩma pam.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: ama dembisi aŋaŋ hɔgʋba pam die dɩ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yada ta gʋtɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Die tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yinene wudiekemba ba wɩa, vuosi die dɩ chii yʋagɩtieliŋ a nyɩŋ sieti ma, a nagɩ ba dʋaŋ gadosi aŋaŋ kalɩŋasɩ ma a mʋ Piita dɩ keŋ chʋŋ tɩanna ʋ ligisiŋ nan nan ba ma aŋ ba ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Ta die kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ die dɩ gbine Jerusalemi a yaa ba yʋagɩtieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ yalla a keŋ, die ba mana dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana aŋaŋ Sadusisi vuosi bataŋ pam die dɩ yaa sʋgɩbɩaŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩba, ta die yɩ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: ba ga yigi be a kparɩ ba dansarɩka ma.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Ama yuku me Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ keŋ sʋʋŋ a yuori dansarɩka ka sanʋaha dɩ tʋntʋntɩba keŋ nyɩŋ, ta balɩ yi ba dɩ.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Nɩ gamma a ga zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a balɩ vuosisi miivoli haalɩkʋ gie wɩa mana.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Die tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ tuo ʋ nʋarɩ, die sʋkʋleeliŋ die ba ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a piili a daga vuosi wɩaha. Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wa Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra mana a lagɩŋ taŋ, ta die yi nʋaŋ dɩ ba ga dansarɩka ka sʋŋ a ga vʋarɩ tʋntʋntɩba a keŋ ba nɩŋŋa.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Ama die ba gana tʋgɩ dansarɩka ka sʋkʋ die ba ka ye be ta yiŋŋi ga lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ a ga baarɩ dɩ.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: Tɩ gana a tʋgɩ dansarɩka ka jigiŋ, tɩ ye ka mɩŋŋɩ kparɩ mɩŋ keŋ keŋ, ta gbɩgbarɩtɩba dɩ zie sanʋaha tɩŋ a gbarɩ, ama tɩ yuorine sanʋaha ta vuoŋ mana wo ka sʋŋ.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die dɩ yi be mamachi ba yile wudieke dɩ yine wo.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Womi vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ juu mi a baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ ye, daa diekemba nɩ nagɩna a yi dansarɩka ma wa zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Die wɩa sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ nagɩ ʋ dembisi ba ga yaa tʋntʋntɩba keŋ, ama die ba ka vʋarɩ ba aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ, dama die ba chɩgɩ ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ vuosi nan taa ba tana.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Die ba yalla ba keŋ wo, die ba vaa ba keŋ zie lagɩŋkʋ nɩŋŋa, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ balɩ ba dɩ.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: Die tɩ yɩ nɩ wa nʋaŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, dɩ nɩ da keŋ dagɩ vuosi Yisa saaŋ ma, ama nɩ ye wudieke nɩ yine, nɩ nagɩ dagɩkʋ a mʋʋlɩ Jerusalemi mana, ta yaala nɩ yi tɩ Yisa kʋkʋʋrɩŋ.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Ta Piita aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩba banɩŋ ba dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ Sie tɩ saagɩ Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ ta daa vuota nʋaŋ.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die vana Yisa dɩ hagɩ kuŋ me, die nɩ kpaasɩna wa dagarɩkʋ ma wa ta kʋʋ wa wa.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ kɔtɩ wa a jʋalɩ wa a kalɩŋ ʋ nuudiigiŋ a yiwo Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ aŋaŋ Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ, dɩ ʋ yɩ Izara vuosi sieŋ dɩ ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ma aŋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yine Yisa kumbu hagɩŋ daansɩa tieliŋ; Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna Haalɩ dieke wo a yɩa vuodieke mana dɩ tuone ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Die nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ pam dɩ ba kʋʋ tʋntʋntɩba.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Ama Farasisi vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die benne lagɩŋkʋ me ta ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Gamalia, ta yi mɩraha dɩdagɩrʋ, vuoŋ mana die dɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma pam; die dɩ hagɩ lagɩŋkʋ ma a balɩ ba dɩ ba yaa tʋntʋntɩba a nyɩŋ aŋ ka yi sʋaa.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Die ʋ wa balɩ yɩ ba dɩ N chanchaalɩŋ Izara vuosi, nɩ sɩmma aŋaŋ nɩ bala nɩ yi vuosi gie wudieke wo.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa, Tudasi die hagɩya a baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ ta die vuosi sɩba kɔbɩsɩ-nɩɩsa die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ, ama vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ kʋʋ wa, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ chɩgɩ a jaasɩ jigiŋ mana, ta ʋ tʋʋmaha mana die dɩ yi yɔrɩ.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ta Judasi, vuodieke dɩ yine Galili vuoke gbaŋ die dɩ keŋ vuosi bɩɩsɩkʋ saŋŋa, die ʋ tarɩgɩ vuosi bataŋ yɩaŋ ta daadaŋ pam die dɩ dɩ ʋ gbaŋ, ta vuosi die dɩ keŋ kʋʋ ʋ gbaŋ, ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ chɩgɩ a jaasɩ jigiŋ mana.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Die wɩa, leleke gie wɩɩrɩ nɩŋ, n kpaama nɩ dɩ nɩ ko vaa vuosi gie sɔmm ta vaa ba ga, dama dɩɩ yi ta ba tʋʋmaha dɩ yiwo vuota tʋʋma ka nan daansɩ kpatɩ.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta ka yi Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ ba; nɩnɩŋ nɩ nan keŋ ye ta nɩ waga aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ. Die lagɩŋkʋ die dɩ tuo Gamalia kpaaŋkʋ.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: ta die yiŋŋi a wa tʋntʋntɩba a keŋ juu, ta vaa ba tɩbɩ ba aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ, ta yɩ ba nʋaŋ dɩ ba da keŋ wɔŋ a balɩ wɩɩŋ Yisa saaŋ ma, ta die vaa ba ga yɔrɩ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Die wɩa, tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ nyɩŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ die yene be ba mʋna wahala diile Yisa saaŋ ma.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Ta die daaŋ mana ba ŋaaŋ gara Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, ta bɩ gara vuosi tige me a daga ta mʋʋla wʋvɩɩnaha dɩ Yisa yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ, Masia wa.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Die dʋʋga dieke ba wasɩnana Pentikosi wa dɩ tʋgɩna Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ jigibalɩmɩŋ.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Womi die watɩ dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a dɔma sɩba bʋlɔgɩsɩ kpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana ta die keŋ suuli juodieke ba kala wa mana.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Ta die ba ye jaaŋ sɩba bolinyaatɩ dɩ nyɩna a keŋ a kalɩ ba wʋnyɩ mana sikpeŋ me.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: ta die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ suuli ba mana, die ba piili a bala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋma sɩba Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ dagɩna ba dene.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Die Juu vuosi die dɩ dɩna Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bie Jerusalemi saŋka mi, die ba nyɩŋ wa tɩgɩsɩ mana dʋnɩa ma a keŋ mi.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Die ba wʋnna watɩ gie die wo, die kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ lagɩsɩ mi, die dɩ yi ba mana mamachi dama die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die bala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ, ba wʋnyɩ mana dɩ wʋmma ʋ jabalɩkɩŋ ma.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Die dɩ yi be mamachi pam die ba pɩasɩ taŋ dɩ, Vuosisi gie dɩ balala naa, ntaa ba mana yiwo Galili vuosi?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Ta bɩa yine tɩ wʋnyɩ mana dɩ wʋmma ba ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wʋbalɩka ma?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Tɩ bataŋ nyɩŋ wa Patia aŋaŋ Midia aŋaŋ Elami aŋaŋ Mesopotamia aŋaŋ Judia aŋaŋ Kapadosia aŋaŋ Pontusi aŋaŋ Asia.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: aŋaŋ Firigia, aŋaŋ Pamifilia aŋaŋ Ijipiti aŋaŋ Libia chaakʋ tɩgɩsɩ dɩ gbigine Sayireni; ta tɩ bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩŋ Romi.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: ta tɩ bataŋ Juu vuosi, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yi buurigaasɩ diekemba dɩ tarɩgɩna ba jɩamɩŋ ta dɩ Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ, ta tɩ bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nyɩŋ Kireeti aŋaŋ Arabia, ama tɩ mana wʋmma ba balala tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ jabalɩkɩŋ ma, ta bɩra Ŋmɩŋ yaa gamma mamachi wʋkpɩɩma ʋ yine.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Die dɩ yi be mamachi pam die ba ko pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ yiwo bɩa.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Ama ta bataŋ die dɩ laa ba ta vʋarɩ ba fala a baarɩ dɩ, Ba nyuu wo daaŋ a bugi.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Womi Piita die dɩ hagɩ ʋ chanchaalɩba baŋ aŋaŋ kaanɩ wa sʋnsʋŋ a zie kpɩkpaakʋ nɩŋŋa; ta die a balɩ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ a yɩa kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ, N chanchaalɩŋ Juu vuosi aŋaŋ nɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne Jerusalemi, nɩ wʋmma mɩŋ ta vaa n bala nɩ wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Nɩ yile dɩ vuosi gie bugiye mɩŋ, ama ba ka bugiye, dama dɩ ye yiwo sʋkʋʋŋ hawasɩ awayɩ saŋŋa.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Ama wudieke nɩ yene wo yiwo wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Joweli die dɩ balɩ dɩ ka nan keŋ yi, dɩ.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ; Dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ saŋŋa, naa maŋ nan yi: n nan daansɩ vaa n Haalɩbʋ sʋʋŋ vuoŋ mana ma; ta nɩ dembibisi aŋaŋ nɩ hɔgʋlɩasɩ nan bʋgɩ balɩ manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka, ta nɩ nahaalɩŋ aŋaŋ daa nyɩŋkʋra nan sɩba wusie yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ba daansa ma.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Saŋka mi n nan tʋŋ n Haalɩbʋ vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnnana wudieke n yaalala ma, dembisi aŋaŋ hɔgʋba mana, ta ba gbaŋ nan daansɩ bʋgɩ balɩ n wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: N nan keŋ tʋŋ tʋnkpɩɩma ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ mamachi dagɩtɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta zɩŋ aŋaŋ boliŋ aŋaŋ nyusi nan daansɩ beri.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ nan sɩbɩ, ta chɩɩkʋ dɩaŋ nan tarɩgɩ a ŋmɩntɩ sɩba zɩŋ, naa nan yi ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ye yiŋŋi keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a dii vuosi sarɩya; ta ʋ yiŋŋi keniŋ daraaŋ nan yi daakpeŋkpɩɩŋ;.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: ta vuodieke mana dɩ keŋ jʋʋsɩ wa ʋ saaŋ ma nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. .

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Die Piita dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi, nɩ wʋmma wudieke n yaala n balɩ nɩ yaa gamma Yisa vuodieke dɩ yine Nazeriti vuoke wɩa; nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ die vana die ʋ tʋma mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma aŋaŋ dagɩtɩ mi nɩ jigiŋ, ta a dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die tʋnna wa.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die sɩba wudieke dɩ bala ka yi, ta die a saŋ a nagɩ Yisa a yɩ nɩ nuusi me, ta yi nɩ vuobɩatɩ dɩ kpaasɩ wa a dɩɩsɩ dagarɩkʋ ma a kʋʋ wa, ka dagɩ sɩba nɩnɩŋ nɩ senne kʋʋ wa.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Ama die ʋ kpine wo ta ye bie kuŋ nuusi me wo, ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ gbatɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ nuusi me, dama kuŋ die wo hagɩrɩŋ dɩ ka pɔgɩlɩ wa.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Dama Davidi die balɩ wɩa yaa gamma ʋ ma dɩ, N sɩba a baarɩ dɩ n Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ beri aŋaŋ mɩŋ saŋŋa mana, ta bɩ zie n kʋaŋ ta suŋŋe mɩŋ amʋ n kaaŋ ye daŋŋa.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma n yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta bala sʋgɩfɩalɩ wʋbalɩka. Maŋ keŋ kpi gbaŋ, ama n nan dɩ yaa tama Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balla ʋ yi wudieke a yɩ mɩŋ wɩa.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: dama ʋ kaaŋ vaa n haalɩbʋ wɩarɩ kpiiliŋ tigiŋ. N yiwo ʋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ, ta ʋ kaaŋ saagɩ aŋ n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ keŋ hʋʋgɩ vɔrɩŋ ma.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Ta bɩ dagɩ mɩŋ miivoli sieti, ta fʋ benne n jigiri dɩaŋ dɩ yɩa mɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam. .

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Ta Piita dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, N nɩmballɩ, n baa n balɩ yuori nɩ mɩŋ, yaa gamma tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Davidi wɩa. Die ʋ kpiye mɩŋ ba guu wo, ta ʋ vɔrɩŋ dɩ ye bie giena a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Die ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa, ta die sɩba dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ, dɩɩ yi ʋ kpi, ʋ nan daansɩ vʋarɩ ʋ haagɩsɩ wʋnyɩ aŋ ʋ kala ʋ naagbaŋtɩ ma.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Davidi die sɩba wudieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balla ʋ yi, ta die balɩ wɩa yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa kuŋ hagɩŋ wɩa, dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ kaaŋ daansɩ wɩarɩ kpiiliŋ tigiŋ; ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩaŋ kaaŋ hʋʋgɩ vɔrɩŋ ma. .

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Ta Piita dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Yisa wa gie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ va ʋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ta tɩ mana dɩ yi ʋ kumbu hagɩŋ daansɩatieliŋ.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ kɔtɩ wa ʋ jʋalɩ kalɩ ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, ʋ kala wa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ wa ʋ Halɩkasɩka die ʋ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ tɩ wa ʋ vaa bʋ keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩ ma. Lele dɩ yiwo Halɩkasɩka nɩ yese ta wʋmma tɩ jigiŋ dene.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Die Davidi dɩ kpine wo, die ʋ ka jʋalɩ ga arɩzanna ma aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ: ama ta die ʋ baarɩ dɩ; Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ balɩ a yɩ n Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ: Kala n nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Die wɩa dɩ vɩɩna ta nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi mana sɩmma dɩ Yisa wa gie, die nɩ vana ba kpaasɩ dagarɩkʋ ma wa, wʋnɩŋ ta Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dagɩ dɩ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa, ta bɩ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Die vuosi gie dɩ wʋnna naa wa die ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ faasɩ chʋʋsɩ pam. Die wɩa ba pɩasɩ Piita aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba dɩ, Tɩ nɩmballɩ, bɩa tɩ baa tɩ yi.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Sie nɩ mana vaa nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ, aŋ tɩ sɩɩ nɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ, ta ʋ yɩ nɩ ʋ piini dieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan nagɩ ʋ Halɩkasɩ a yɩ nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ ballɩ, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne saasaa, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ wa aŋ ba tuo ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Piita die dɩ balɩ ba wɩa pam ta kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ, Nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩ dieke dɩ kienene lele gie vuobɩatɩ gie ma wa.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Die ba pam die dɩ tuo Piita wʋbalɩkaha a dii, die ba yiwo sɩba vuosi tuse ataa, ta ba sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ta ba gʋtɩ ba ma.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Die saŋŋa mana ba ŋaaŋ bʋgɩra a nyɩna tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ, ta die a lagɩma a pɔgɩla taŋ aŋaŋ nʋabalɩmɩŋ, ta lagɩma die nyindiike, ta bɩ lagɩma a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yallɩ vuoŋ mana; tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yi mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma pam aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ wa.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Die Yisa kʋaŋanvuosi mana die dɩ pɔgɩlɩ taŋ aŋaŋ nʋabalɩmɩŋ ta tɩa ba nyinti a yɩa taŋ.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Ta die a naga ba faarɩ a daa a naga ligirehe a tɩa a yɩa taŋ, dɩ mʋna vuoŋ mana tuo die.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Daaŋ mana ba ko lagɩma taŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ama ba nyɩŋ mi, ba ŋaaŋ gara taŋ tige a lagɩŋ die nyindiike aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Ta saŋŋa mana ba ŋaaŋ bɩra Ŋmɩŋ, ta vuoŋ mana die dɩ yallɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba. Ta daaŋ mana tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ŋaaŋ vasa vuosi dɩ yese gbatɩtaanɩŋ ta gʋta ba ma.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Saŋŋa mi die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ gʋtɩnana wa, ta vʋʋnɩŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ Juu vuodiekemba dɩ balala Giriiki jabalɩkɩŋ aŋaŋ Juu vuodiekemba die dɩ bala ba gbaŋ gbaŋ jabalɩkɩŋ sʋnsʋŋ, dama vuodiekemba die dɩ balala Giriiki jabalɩkɩrɩ die baarɩ dɩ daaŋ mana ba ŋaana tɩa nyindiikehe ba kpihɔgʋba ka tuose sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ bala Juu jabalɩkɩrɩ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Die wɩa tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ ale wo die wa Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ a lagɩsɩ taŋ a baarɩ dɩ Dɩ ka mʋ dɩ tɩ vaa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mʋʋlɩŋ ta wɩarɩ nyindiike tɩala ma.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Die wɩa n nɩmballɩ, nɩ daansɩ nɩ sʋŋ a vʋarɩ dembisi ba bayʋpɔyɩ nɩ sɩbɩna a baarɩ ba beriŋ vɩɩna, ta yaa yɩaŋ ta suuli wo aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, aŋ tɩ nagɩ tʋʋmaha gie yɩ ba nuusi me.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: ta tɩ nagɩ tɩnɩŋ tɩ saŋŋa a yi Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ mi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dagɩŋ ma.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana die yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩba balɩkʋ. Die wɩa ba vʋarɩ Sitiiveni vuodieke die yallɩ yada pam ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ suuli wo aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Porokorusi aŋaŋ Nikanori aŋaŋ Timoni aŋaŋ Pamenasi aŋaŋ Nikolasi, vuodieke die nyɩna Antiyoku, ta die dɩ ka yi Juu vuoŋ, ama die dɩ tarɩgɩ ʋ jɩamɩŋ ta dɩa Juu vuosi jɩaŋkʋ.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Die ba yaa ba ga tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ, ta tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ nagɩ ba nuusi a diisi ba sikpigile me ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ ba.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa die dɩ bɩ mʋʋlɩ a ga jigiŋ mana, ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ gʋta lagɩ lagɩ Jerusalemi ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba pam die dɩ saagɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha ta yiwo yada.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Sitiiveni die dɩ ye Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ pam, ta die dɩ yie mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma vuosisi sʋŋ.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Ama die dembisi bataŋ die dɩ ka tuo wo. Vuodiekemba die dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Sitiiveni die yiwo Juu vuodiekemba dɩ yine yɔŋɩsɩ, ta die ba vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ ba yɔŋɩsɩ ma. Die ba nyɩŋ wa Sayireni aŋaŋ Alekizandira, banɩŋ aŋaŋ Juu vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩna Silisia aŋaŋ Asia ma. Die ba gbaŋ ba yaa wa ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ yɩ Sitiiveni yɩaŋ pam, die ba ka bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ wa aŋaŋ ʋ wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Die ba kana ka bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ wa wɩa, die ba ga kpalɩ dembisi bataŋ dɩ ba balɩ dɩ Tɩnɩŋ tɩ wʋŋ wa ʋ balala a chʋʋsa Mosisi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ba bala naa wɩa, ta vuosisi aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta ga Sitiiveni jigiŋ a yigi wo a yaa wa ga ba nyɩŋkʋra lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Womi die ba yaa dembisi bataŋ dɩ ba chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ wa. Die ba baarɩ Daa wa gie ŋaaŋ bala saŋŋa mana a chʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ Mosisi mɩraha;.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: dama tɩ wʋŋ wa ʋ bala dɩ Yisa vuodieke dɩ yine Nazeriti vuoke nan kpaŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie a taaŋ, ta tarɩgɩ chʋara diekemba Mosisi dɩ yɩna tɩ wa.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ kala lagɩŋkʋ jigiri die dɩ mɩɩrɩgɩ ba nine a daansa Sitiiveni, ta die ye ʋ ninechaaŋ dɩ tarɩgɩ a sɩɩ sɩba malaka ninechaaŋ.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ pɩasɩ Sitiiveni dɩ Ka seŋ yiwo wusie.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Sitiiveni die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ N nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ n chʋalɩŋ, nɩ wʋmma mɩŋ, saŋŋa dieke tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Abarahami dɩ yene bie Mesopotamia ma ta ye ka hagɩ ga Harani ma, ta hagɩrɩtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: ta balɩ wa dɩ Vaa fʋ deŋ aŋaŋ fʋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta ga tɩŋgbaŋ dieke n balla n dagɩ fʋ wa.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ nyɩŋ ʋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma a ga bie Harani ma. Ʋ chɔɔŋ wa kuŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vaa ʋ hagɩ a keŋ kalɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me jigidieke nɩ benne lele naa.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die ka yɩ wa tɩŋgbaŋka halɩ bɩta gbaŋ dɩ ʋ sɩna, ama ta die yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan daansɩ nagɩ ka a yɩ wa, ta ka nan yi wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ haagɩsɩ sɩɩtɩ. Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yine nʋarɩ gie wo ta Abarahami die wo bʋa.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Naa die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ haagɩsɩ nan daansɩ beri tɩŋgbaŋ gaaŋ ma, mi ba nan yi yɔŋɩsɩ, aŋ ba mugisi be pam bɩna kɔbɩsɩ-nɩɩsa.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Ama manɩŋ n nan daansɩ datɩ tɩŋgbaŋ dieke vuosi ba bala ba tʋŋ a yɩa wa tɩbɩŋ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba nan keŋ a nyɩŋ kanɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka mi a keŋ tɩka gie me a jɩama mɩŋ.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Womi Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ Abarahami nʋaŋ, ta die dagɩ wa nʋarɩ dagɩtɩ, dɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ haagɩsɩ gobi ba kɔla. Die Abarahami dɩ keŋ mɩɩrɩ Aziki, ta die gobi ʋ kɔlɩ ka daraa nɩɩ daraaŋ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Aziki dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Jakobu ta gobi ʋ kɔlɩ, ta Jakobu dɩaŋ dɩ gobi ʋ ballɩ baŋ aŋaŋ bale die dɩ yine tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩkpɩɩma wa kɔla.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Die Jakobu ballɩlɩ die dɩ keŋ haa ba nɩmbʋa Josefu ta die nagɩ wa a daa a yɩ vuosi bataŋ ba yaa wa ga Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma a bɩrɩŋ wa yɔmʋ. Ama Ŋmɩŋ die bie ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: ta die vʋarɩ wa a nyɩŋ wumugisike mana ma, ta die dɩ yɩ wa yɩaŋ ta yi Ijipiti naaŋ wa Faro dɩ yaala ʋ wɩa ta yi wo jakʋʋŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta bɩ yi wo jakʋʋŋ ʋ tigiŋ me.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ kɔŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ keŋ nan Ijipiti aŋaŋ Keenani tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ mana ma, die ba dii wahala pam, ta tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die ka ye nyindiike.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Die wɩa die Jakobu dɩ wʋnna dɩ nyindiike bie Ijipiti ma wa die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ ballɩlɩ, tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba mi, die ba bʋmbʋŋaŋ garɩŋ wonde.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Die ba garɩŋ bule wo ma wa Josefu die dɩ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ ʋ nɩmballɩlɩ, mi Faro die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ Josefu deŋ.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Josefu die dɩ tʋŋ a wa ʋ chɔɔŋ Jakobu aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ mana dɩ ba keŋ Ijipiti ma; die ba mana die yiwo vuosi baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ banʋ.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Die wɩa Jakobu aŋaŋ ʋ dembe mana die dɩ hagɩ a ga Ijipiti, mi die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ ballɩ dembisi die dɩ kpi.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Die ba nagɩ ba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a yiŋŋi a ga Saakemi a ga guu vɔrɩŋ dieke Abarahami die dɩ dana a nyɩŋ Hamori buuriŋ jigiŋ ma.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Die dɩ wana a gbigi dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yi suuli ʋ nʋarɩ die ʋ yɩna Abarahami wa, die tɩ vuosisi die dɩ benne Ijipiti ma wa die dɩ wa faasɩ dala pam.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ dii Ijipiti naarɩ, ama wʋnɩŋ die ʋ ka sɩba Josefu wɩa.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Die naaŋ wa die dɩ pɔgɩlɩ tɩ vuosi aŋaŋ bɩaŋ, ta die mugise be dɩ ba vʋarɩ ba ballɩnyaalɩsɩ ba tige me a taaŋ aŋ ba kpi.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Die ba keŋ mɩɩrɩ Mosisi, ta die ʋ vɩɩna pam, ta Ŋmɩŋ nine die dɩ suuli wo. Die ʋ bie wo ʋ chɔɔŋ tigiŋ me chɩɩtɩ ataa.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: die ba kenne nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ ba tigiri me saŋŋa dieke wo, die naaŋ wa hɔgʋlɩa die dɩ keŋ a nagɩ wa a wubi wo, ta yiwo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ bʋa.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Die ba dagɩ Mosisi yɩaŋ dieke mana Ijipiti vuosisi dɩ yalla, die ʋ keŋ bɩrɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna wʋbalɩka ta tʋma hagɩrɩŋ tʋʋma.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Die Mosisi die dɩ tʋgɩ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa Ijipiti vuosi jigiŋ, die ʋ yile daaŋ kaanɩ dɩ ʋ ga a waasɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ buuriŋ Izara vuosisi.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Die ʋ ganana wa die ʋ ye Ijipiti vuoŋ dɩ mugisinene Izara vuoŋ, die ʋ ga dɩ ʋ gbatɩ wa a taaŋ, ta die a kʋʋ Ijipiti vuoke.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Die ʋ yile sɩba ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi nan mɩŋŋɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ baa ʋ nagɩ wa wʋnɩŋ a tʋŋ a gbatɩ ba a taaŋ, ama die ba ka mɩŋŋɩ die.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa, Mosisi die dɩ bɩ ye Izara vuosi bale dɩ wagɩnana, die ʋ yaala ʋ wʋnsɩ ba sʋnsʋŋ, ta balɩ ba dɩ, N zʋamba, nɩ yiwo nɩmballɩ, bɩa nɩ waga taŋ die.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Ama vuodieke die dɩ yaala waagɩbʋ die dɩ kpaŋŋɩ Mosisi a taaŋ lʋgɩŋ, ta die a pɩasɩ wa dɩ Mɩnɩa yine fʋ tɩ jakʋʋŋ aŋaŋ tɩ sarɩyadiiru.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yaala fʋ kʋʋ mɩŋ mɩŋ sɩba diene wo fʋ kʋna Ijipiti vuoke.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Mosisi die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ʋ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma a ga bie Midiani tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta die dɩ keŋ faarɩ hɔgʋ a mɩɩrɩ ballɩ dembisi bale mi.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ ga haagɩŋ daaŋ kaanɩ a ga tʋgɩ Sanayi kunkogiŋ jigiŋ ta ye bolinyaaŋ dɩ dinene haŋgɔɔsɩ tɩɩŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ bolibu sʋŋ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Die ʋ yene die wo, die dɩ yiwo mamachi, die ʋ chʋŋ a ga gbigi dɩ ʋ mɩŋŋɩ a daansɩ ka, ta die a wʋŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩŋ, ta ka bala wa dɩ;.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Manɩŋ n yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dieke fʋ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ dɩ jɩanna, Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu Ŋmɩŋ. Mosisi die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ʋ cheeli aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ ta ka bɩ yaala ʋ daansɩ mi.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ Vʋarɩ fʋ nɩɩraha, dama jigidieke fʋ zene wo yiwo jigikasɩ.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Wusie, manɩŋ n yeye mɩŋ n vuosisi dɩ dinene wahala die Ijipiti me, ta wʋŋ ba kpaŋ. Die wɩa maŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ dɩ n gbatɩ ba a taaŋ. Die wɩa n nan yiŋŋi tʋŋ fʋ Ijipiti me.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Mosisi wo gie die yine vuodieke Izara vuosisi die dɩ zetine wo, ta die pɩasɩ wa dɩ Mɩnɩa yine fʋ tɩ jakʋʋŋ aŋaŋ tɩ sarɩyadiiru? Ama wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ yi ba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ ta gbatɩ ba taaŋ, ta malaka dieke dɩ nyɩna wʋnɩŋ Mosisi jigiri bolinyaabʋ ma haŋgɔɔsɩ tɩɩka jigiri die suŋŋi wo.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ Mosisi die yine vuodieke die dɩ vʋarɩna ba a nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma, die ʋ bɩ tʋŋ mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke die ba gana a garɩsɩ Mʋgɩkpɩɩŋmɩnbʋ wa, aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke die ba benne haagɩŋ ma wa bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa wa.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Mosisi wo gie die bala a yɩ Izara vuosisi dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan daansɩ tʋŋ nɩ jigiŋ ʋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ sɩba ʋ tʋnna mɩŋ, ʋ nan yi nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ buuriŋ.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Mosisi wo gie die dɩ benne aŋaŋ tɩ Izara vuosisi lagɩŋkʋ ma haagɩŋ ma. Die ʋ bie wo aŋaŋ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba aŋaŋ malaka dieke die dɩ bala wɩa a yɩ wa kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩna Sinayi wa. Mi die ʋ tuo Ŋmɩŋ miivoli wʋbalɩkaha dɩ ʋ nagɩ a yɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Ama die tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ zeti ta ka dɩ wa dama ba sʋŋanyile die yiŋŋi ga wa Ijipiti me.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: die wɩa die ba balɩ a yi Arɔni dɩ Mɩɩ ŋmɩŋ a yɩ tɩ aŋ ka dɩa tɩ nɩŋŋa. Tɩnɩŋ tɩ ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yine Mosisi, vuodieke dɩ nagɩna tɩ a nyɩŋ Ijipiti ma wa.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Saŋka mi die ba mɩɩ buuŋ sɩba naraabɩŋ, a kaaba a yɩa ka, ta die die dʋʋga ta bɩra jadieke ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba mɩna wa aŋaŋ nuusisi.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vaa ba a taaŋ dɩ ba jɩama chɩŋmarɩsɩ, sɩba die dɩ maagɩna die wo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba gbaŋkʋ ma wa, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ: Izara vuosi, die nɩ kʋnana dɔŋɩsɩ ta kaaba haagɩŋ ma bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa wa, daa manɩŋ die nɩ kaaba yɩa.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Die nɩ ŋaana naga buuŋ Moloki vɩɩŋ ta bɩ naga chɩŋmarɩŋ Refani nɩasɩŋ ta a yi bugile ta jɩama. Naa chɩaŋ ma n nan vaa nɩ dataasɩ vʋara nɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ tɩŋ ma a yaa nɩ a ga saasaa a poli Babiloni.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die kpi wo Ŋmɩŋ vɩɩŋ haagɩrɩ ma ta die daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ bie ba jigiŋ. Die ba wʋnsɩ ka mɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balla Mosisi die wo. Die ka sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vana Mosisi die dɩ yene ke die wo.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ keŋ nagɩ vɩɩkʋ a yɩ ba ballɩ, ta ba tuo ke a yaa ka gara, ta banɩŋ aŋaŋ Josua die dɩ keŋ keŋ gie ta gbatɩ tɩŋgbaŋ diekemba die buuri gaasɩ die dɩ sɩna. Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ benne ba nɩŋŋa a yagɩ vuosisi a taaŋ wa ta die viiku bie gie deŋ deŋ a keŋ tʋgɩ Davidi saŋŋa.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Davidi beriŋ die dɩ suuli Ŋmɩŋ nine, ta die keŋ jʋʋsɩ dɩ ʋ vaa ʋ mɩɩ tigiŋ a yɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke die dɩ ʋ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Jakobu Ŋmɩnnɩ.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Ama wʋnɩŋ Davidi bʋadembiŋ Solomoni die dɩ keŋ mɩɩ die tigiri.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ ka bie tige diekemba vuota dɩ mɩna. Naa sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa die dɩ bala dɩ.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ yine n naarɩ gbanɩŋ ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie dɩaŋ dɩ yi n nagɩsɩ jigizielikiŋ. Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a mɩɩ tɩgɩ dieke dɩ mʋna mɩŋ a yɩ mɩŋ? Yaa jigidieke n baaŋ nan voosi?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Daa manɩŋ die n naana nyinti gie mana.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yiwo tɩbɩhagɩrɩtieliŋ, ta nɩ sʋŋanyile dɩ yʋa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ pam ta nɩ ka saaga dɩ nɩ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Nɩnɩŋ nɩ zetine Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ sɩba nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ zetine die wo.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ die wori ta nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ dɩ ka mugisiye. Ta bɩ kʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntɩba diekemba die dɩ bʋgɩna balɩ Ŋmɩŋ Vuokasɩka Masia wa keniŋ wɩa. Ta lele nɩ yine vuodiekemba dɩ posine ʋ chɩaŋ ta kʋʋ wa.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine vuodiekemba die dɩ tuone Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha a nyɩŋ malakasisi jigiŋ, ama ta ka saaga a dɩa ha.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Die lagɩŋkʋ vuosi die dɩ wʋnna Sitiiveni balɩkʋ die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ pam ta ŋɔba ba nyɩna.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Ama die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ suuli Sitiiveni, ʋ kɔtɩ ʋ sikpeŋ a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a ye Ŋmɩŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta ye Yisa dɩ zene Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Ye, n ye arɩzanna sanʋaŋ dɩ yuorine ta vuota Bʋa wa dɩ zie Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Die ba wʋnna na ba keesi aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ, ta nagɩ ba nuusi a ligi ba tɩba. Womi ba mana die dɩ chɩgɩ a keŋ taaŋ a giliŋ wo bʋnyɩ.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: a datɩ wa nyɩŋ tɩka lʋgɩŋ a piili a taa wa tana. Die vuodiekemba die dɩ taana wa tana wa die dɩ vʋarɩ ba nyinyeeke dʋaŋ dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ dine Sɔɔli dɩ ʋ daansɩma ha.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Die ba taanana wa die ta die ʋ keŋ wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa, tuo n haalɩŋ.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Ta sʋʋŋ gbirigi ta keesi aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, da keŋ nagɩ ba taalɩ gie a chʋʋsɩ ba. Die ʋ balɩ wa naa ta kpi.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Ta Sɔɔli die dɩ saagɩna ʋ kʋʋla wɩa. Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die mugisibɩaŋ die dɩ nan Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma; ta ba mana die dɩ nyɩŋ a jaasɩ ga Judia aŋaŋ Samaria lʋga mana ama ta va tʋntʋntɩba nyɩɩna mi.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Vuodiekemba die dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ guu Sitiiveni ta faasɩ kʋŋ ʋ kumbu.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Ama Sɔɔli die dɩ yaala ʋ chʋʋsɩ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, ta die gara tige tige, ta yige Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ, dembisi aŋaŋ hɔgʋba mana a keŋ nyɩna ta a yaa ba a lige dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba die dɩ jaasɩ wa die dɩ ga jigiŋ mana a ga mʋʋla Yisa wɩaha.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Ta Filipi die dɩ sʋʋŋ ga Samaria tɩka ma a ga mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wɩa a yɩ mi vuosisi.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Die daadamba pam mi die dɩ keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a yuori ba tɩba a wʋŋ Filipi balɩkʋ, ta bɩ ye ʋ yinene mamachi wudieke wo.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Die jɩmbɩatɩ pam die dɩ chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ daadaŋ pam ma aŋaŋ keesiŋ pam; ta die nyɩŋgbaŋ kpiikiŋ tieliŋ aŋaŋ gbarɩsɩ pam die dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Die wɩa die sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ die dɩ bie Samaria tɩŋ mana ma.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Daa wʋnyɩ gbaŋ die benne tɩka ma ba wasa wa Simoni ta yaa tɩɩŋ, saŋŋa a taŋ ʋ ŋaaŋ yie mamachi wuyiike ta a yie vuosisi mamachi, ta die a bala dɩ ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne tɩka ma, zɔɔlɩntieliŋ aŋaŋ vuokpɩɩma mana die dɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma ta wʋmma a yɩa wa. Die ba bala dɩ Simoni yiwo vuodieke ŋmɩŋ dieke ba wasanana Yiko Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tarɩgɩna. .

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Die ba wʋmma a yɩa wa dama die ʋ yi wo mamachi wuyiikehe a yʋasɩ ba jigiŋ.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Ama die Filipi dɩ keŋ a mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋvɩɩnaha aŋaŋ Yisa wɩa, dembisi aŋaŋ hɔɔŋ die dɩ saagɩ ʋ mʋʋlɩkʋ, ta ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Simoni gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ yi Filipi balɩkʋ yada, die ʋ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ. Die ʋ dɩ Filipi kʋaŋ a yese mamachi wʋkpɩɩŋ diekemba ʋ yinene, dɩ yi wo mamachi.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Die Yisa tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ benne Jerusalemi dɩ wʋŋ dɩ Samaria vuosi tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha a dii, die ba tʋŋ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn ba jigiŋ.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Die ba gana a ga tʋgɩ mi, die ba jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ ba dɩ ba tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: dama die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die ye ka keŋ a sʋʋŋ ba wʋnyɩ mana ma; die ba ko sɩɩ ba wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa saaŋ ma nyɩɩna.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Womi Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ nagɩ ba nuusi a dɩɩsɩ ba ma ta ba tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simoni die dɩ ye dɩ tʋntʋntɩba dɩ nagɩna ba nuusi a dɩɩsɩ ba ma ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ ba Halɩkasɩka, die wɩa die ʋ nagɩ ligire a yaala ʋ yɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: ta baarɩ Nɩ yɩ mɩŋ gbaŋ yiko ka gie amʋ maŋ keŋ nagɩ n nuuŋ a dɩɩsɩ vuodieke mana ma, ʋ nan tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ Fʋnɩŋ nan kpi yɔrɩ aŋaŋ fʋ ligirehe dama fʋ yile dɩ fʋ nan bɩagɩ a daa Ŋmɩŋ piini aŋaŋ ligire.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Die wɩa fʋ nuuŋ wo tɩ tʋʋma ma, dama fʋ sʋŋanyile ka vɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Die wɩa chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ fʋ sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ gie me, ta jʋʋsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ amʋ ʋ nagɩ fʋ sʋŋanyilibɩakʋ gie a chaa fʋ.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: dama n yeye ta fʋ suuli wo aŋaŋ sʋgɩbɩaŋ ta bɩaŋ dɩ suuli fʋ.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Womi Simoni die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Piita aŋaŋ Jɔɔn dɩ, Nɩ jʋʋsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ a yɩ mɩŋ, amʋ nyinti gie wʋnyɩ mana nɩ bala wa da keŋ keŋ n ma.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Die ba bala wudieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yine aŋaŋ ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha a kpatɩ, die ba yiŋŋi a ga Jerusalemi. Die ba ganana die ba mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha Samaria tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ pam ma.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ malaka die dɩ keŋ a balɩ a yɩ Filipi dɩ Hagɩ a ga a daagɩ sie dieke dɩ nyɩna Jerusalemi ta sʋʋŋ gara Gaza wa. Ba ka bɩ daaga sieku gie lele.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ a piili chɔnɩŋ, die ʋ ganana ʋ ga tuoli Ituopia daayaabɩŋ sieku me ta ʋ kule. Die ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ ta daansɩ Ituopia hɔgʋ naaŋ wa ligire. Die ʋ ga wa Jerusalemi a ga jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: ta wa yiŋŋi kuli tigiŋ ta kalɩ ʋ toriku me ta duune dɩ data ka ta ʋ karɩma gbaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya dɩ maagɩna.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Filipi dɩ Vɩɩsɩ gbigi torikuke.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Womi Filipi die dɩ chɩgɩ a ga gbigike a wʋŋ daa wa dɩ karɩnnana gbaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya dɩ maagɩna wa. Die ʋ pɩasɩ wa dɩ Fʋ sɩba wudieke fʋ karɩnnana chɩaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ta ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, N baaŋ yi wo lalɩa a sɩmma ka chɩaŋ, ntaala vuoŋ dagɩ mɩŋ? Die wɩa ʋ jʋʋsɩ Filipi dɩ ʋ keŋ jʋalɩ kala torikuke me aŋaŋ wa.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Die ʋ karɩnnana wudieke wo yiwo naa dɩ: Die ʋ nɩasɩ wa yii dieke ba yalla a ga dɩ ba kɔrɩgɩ, ta die beri sigisigi sɩba yiipɔlɩ dieke ba ŋaana gobe ka kobiti ka beri sigisigi die, die ʋ ka yuori ʋ nʋaŋ a balɩ wɩɩŋ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Die ba yiwo viivi, ta zeti dɩ ba kaaŋ dii ʋ sarɩya vɩɩnɩŋ. Ta vuoŋ wori a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a balɩ ʋ haagɩsɩ wɩa, dama die ba woliŋ vʋarɩ wa ʋ miivoli a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Daa wa die dɩ pɩasɩ Filipi dɩ, Balɩ mɩŋ, mɩnɩa wɩa naazʋa wa die dɩ bala wa? Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wɩa yaa vuogaaŋ.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Die Filipi dɩ piili a nyɩŋ daa wa die dɩ karɩnna jigidieke wo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ a balɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha yaa gamma Yisa wɩa.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Die ba sʋʋna gara sieku me wo, die ba keŋ tʋgɩ jigiŋ kaanɩ nyaaŋ bʋtaŋ dɩ dʋa, daa wa die dɩ baarɩ, Ye, nyaaŋ wʋnna bɩa bala ka kagɩ mɩŋ amʋ n daa sɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ? .

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Die daayaabɩrɩ dɩ yi ta duunehe dɩ zie, ta wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Filipi die dɩ sʋʋŋ ga nyaabʋ ma, Filipi die dɩ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Die ba kenne nyɩŋ nyaabʋ ma wa, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Haalɩŋ die dɩ nagɩ Filipi a nyɩŋ mi bʋnyɩ, ta daa wa die ka bɩ ye wo bɩbra, ta die chʋŋ kule aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Filipi die dɩ keŋ ye ta ʋ bie Azotusi a die dɩ dɩa ka tɩgɩsɩ mana a mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha, yaa die a ga tʋgɩ Siisiria.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Tɔ, Sɔɔli die ko nɩga nyuŋ dɩ ʋ nan dɩ mugise ta kʋa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ, die ʋ ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ jigiŋ.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ yɩ wa gbaŋtɩ aŋ a dagɩ vuodieke dɩ yine wo aŋaŋ yiko dieke ʋ yalla aŋ ʋ ga Damasikusi a ga dagɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me nyɩŋkʋra a mʋ dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ ye vuodieke dɩ dɩna sie dieke Yisa dɩ dagɩna, hɔgʋ yaa dembiŋ ʋ nan bɩagɩ yigi be a yaa ba a yiŋŋi keŋ Jerusalemi.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Die ʋ benne sieku me a gara Damasikusi ta keŋ gbigi tɩka, die chaanɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ chaaŋ giliŋ wo bʋnyɩ.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Womi die ʋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ ta wʋŋ lɔlɩŋ dɩ balala a yɩa wa dɩ, Sɔɔli, Sɔɔli, bɩa yine fʋ mugise mɩŋ.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Die ʋ pɩasɩ dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine fʋ n Yɔmʋtieŋ? Lɔlɩkʋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ Yisa, vuodieke fʋ mugisinene wo.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Ama hagɩ a ga tɩka sʋŋ, mi ba nan dagɩ fʋ wudieke fʋ mʋna fʋ yi.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Saŋka mi dembiŋ diekemba die dɩ ganana aŋaŋ Sɔɔli wa die dɩ wɩarɩ zie surumm. Die ba wʋŋ wa lɔlɩkʋ ama die ba ka ye vuoŋ.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ta Sɔɔli die dɩ hagɩ tɩŋgbaŋka ma a yuori ʋ nine ama ta ka yese. Die wɩa die ba yigi ʋ nuuŋ me a yaa wa a ga Damasikusi.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Damʋʋma ataa die ʋ ka bɩagɩ yese. Die daraa gie nɩŋ mana die ʋ ka dii jaaŋ yaa a nyuu jaaŋ mana.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wʋnyɩ die benne Damasikusi ma ba wasa wa Ananiasi, ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ wa wa daansa sʋŋ dɩ, Ananiasi! Die ʋ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n wʋnna n Yɔmʋtieŋ.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Gɔmɩsɩ ga sie dieke ba wasɩnana Sieŋ-tʋgɩsɩkʋ ma a ga Judasi tigiŋ me a pɩasɩ daa dieke dɩ nyɩna Taasusi ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Sɔɔli wa wɩa. Ʋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ lele gie.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Die Sɔɔli daansa sʋŋ, ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Ananiasi dɩ kenne juu a nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma, a mʋ ʋ nan bɩ yese bɩbra.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Ananiasi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, vuosi pam wɔŋ balɩ mɩŋ daa wa gie wɩa, yaa gamma wʋbɩaŋ diekemba ʋ yinene fʋ vuosi dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma wa.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: Ta ʋ wa keŋ giena aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ yiko dɩ ʋ yigi vuodieke mana dɩ wasɩnana fʋ saaŋ.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Ama ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ balɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ ko gamma, dama wʋnɩŋ maŋ vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ tʋma n tʋʋma, ta vaa buurigaasɩ aŋaŋ ba naakpɩɩma aŋaŋ Izara vuosi mana sɩmma n saaŋ:.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: ta manɩŋ n nan dagɩ wa gbalɩgɩ diekemba mana ʋ bala ʋ gbalɩgɩ n saaŋ chɩaŋ ma.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Die wɩa Ananiasi die dɩ ga a ga juu tigiri me a nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi Sɔɔli ma, ta baarɩ dɩ, N nɩmbʋa Sɔɔli, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa vuodieke dɩ nyɩna fʋ jigiŋ sieku me wo saŋŋa dieke fʋ kennene giena wa tʋnna mɩŋ amʋ fʋ nan bɩ ye bɩbra ta suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Lele womi jaaŋ kaanɩ die dɩ sɩna sɩba zaasɩŋ hagɩtɩ die dɩ nyɩŋ Sɔɔli nine me a nan, die ʋ bɩ bɩagɩ yese bɩbra. Die ʋ hagɩ a zie, ta ba sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Die ʋ dii nyindiike ta ʋ hagɩrɩŋ dɩ yiŋŋi keŋ. Saŋka mi Sɔɔli die dɩ wɩarɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ benne Damasikusi ma wa jigiŋ daraa ale.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: ta die ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me lagɩ lagɩ a ga mʋʋla Yisa wɩa dɩ, Yisa seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wʋnna wa wa die dɩ yi be mamachi ta ba pɩasa dɩ, Da daa wa gie die benne Jerusalemi ma a kʋa vuodiekemba die dɩ wasɩnana saarɩ gie wo? Ʋ ka keŋ giena dɩ ʋ yigi wo Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta nagɩ ba a yiŋŋi ga yɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Ama ta Sɔɔli mʋʋlɩkʋ die dɩ a ko gʋta aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ, ʋ mʋʋlɩkʋ die dɩ dagɩ dɩ Yisa seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa ta chɩɩla die dɩ wori: die wɩa Juu vuosi diekemba die benne Damasikusi die ka bɩagɩ a balɩ wa wɩɩŋ.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Die daraa pam die dɩ tɩaŋ, ta Juu vuosisi die dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ a saŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ wa;.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: ama die vuoŋ dɩ balɩ wa wudieke ba sanna dɩ ba yi wo. Die ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ mana die ba ŋaaŋ gbarɩ wa tɩka sanʋa mana ma dɩ ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Ama yuŋ kaanɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ nagɩ wa a yi kparɩŋ ma a nagɩ wa a daagɩ tɩka paŋ sikpeŋ a nyɩŋ.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Womi Sɔɔli die dɩ ga Jerusalemi a mɩa dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a lagɩŋ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ma, ama die ba ka yiwo yada dɩ lele, ʋ wa yiwo Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ, ta die wa chɩgɩ wa ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Ta Banabasi die dɩ keŋ suŋŋi wo a yaa wa ga tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ, a balɩ ba Sɔɔli die dɩ yene tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa sieŋ me ta ʋ balɩ wɩa a yɩ wa dene. Die ʋ bɩ balɩ ba Sɔɔli die dɩ dine dembisi a mʋʋla Yisa wɩa Damasikusi ma dene.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Die daa wʋnyɩ die benne Siisiria ma ba wasa wa Koniliawosi, die ʋ yiwo sojasisi jakʋʋŋ ta pɔgɩlɩ sojasisi kɔbɩga ʋ chaakʋ ma. Die ba wasa ʋ chaakʋ Itali tɩŋgbaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Die wɩa Sɔɔli die dɩ beri aŋaŋ ba ta die dɩ dɩa Jerusalemi jige mana a mʋʋla tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ aŋaŋ dembisi.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ mana die jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ta chɩga Ŋmɩŋ. Die ʋ kpaŋŋɩ ʋ gbaŋ pam a suŋŋe Juu vuosi zɔɔlɩntielibe, ta die jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Die ʋ bɩ bala aŋaŋ Juu vuodiekemba dɩ bala Giriiki jabalɩkɩŋ yaa gamma Yisa wɩa, ama die ba nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ wa, ta mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ die agbaa die dɩ nɩgɩna a tɩaŋ hawasɩ ataa die ʋ daansɩ a ye Ŋmɩŋ malaka dɩ kenne juu ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Koniliawosi.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Ama saŋŋa dieke ta Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna naa die ba nagɩ wa a sʋʋŋ ga Siisiria ta vaa ʋ kuli Taasusi me.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Koniliawosi die dɩ yene malakaka die ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yigi wo, ta ʋ baarɩ dɩ N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩ bɩa? Die malakaka dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ tuo fʋ jʋʋsɩŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ yɩnana zɔɔlɩntieliŋ piini diekemba ta bɩ tɩɩnsɩ fʋ wɩa.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Womi die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dieke dɩ benne Judia aŋaŋ Galili aŋaŋ Samaria mana ma die ka bɩ ye mugisiŋ. Die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ yɩ ba hagɩrɩŋ ta kpaŋŋɩsɩ ba, die ba gʋta ta beri aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ chɩgɩla.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Lele, tʋŋ dembisi bataŋ aŋ ba ga Joopa ma a ga wa daa wʋnyɩ saaŋ dɩ dine Simoni Piita.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Piita die dɩ dɩa a ga jigiŋ mana, ta die keŋ sʋʋŋ ga dɩ ʋ kaagɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi die dɩ benne Liida.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: Ʋ yiwo chaanʋ a bie gbaŋwʋnsɩrʋ dieke ba wasɩnana Simoni tigiŋ, ta ʋ tigiri dɩ gbigi mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Mi die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Aniasi, die ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die kpine ta ʋ ka bɩagɩ haga ʋ jadʋakɩŋ ma bɩna anɩɩ.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Die malakaka die dɩ balala wɩaha a yɩa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi ga ta va wa, womi die Koniliawosi die dɩ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ bale aŋaŋ sojasisi wʋnyɩ, vuodieke die dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ ta bɩ yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ gbɩgbarɩtʋ wʋnyɩ.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Aniasi, Yisa Masia gbaannana fʋ, hagɩ ta wʋnsɩ fʋ jadʋakɩrɩ. Lele womi die ʋ hagɩ a zie.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Die ʋ balɩ ba wudieke dɩ yine, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tʋŋ ba Joopa ma ma.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Die vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Liida aŋaŋ Saroni ma die dɩ ye wo die dɩ tarɩgɩ ba beriŋ, ta dɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sieŋ.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die ba benne sieku me a gara wa a keŋ gbigi Joopa, saŋka mi Piita die dɩ ga jʋalɩ kɔsʋŋ sikpeŋ agbaa saŋŋa dɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ benne Joopa ma ba wasa wa Tabita, ama Giriiki jabalɩkɩŋ ba wasa wa Dɔkasi, ka chɩaŋ yine Haabiiŋ ta die a yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ. Die ʋ nagɩ ʋ saŋŋa mana a yie wʋvɩɩna yɩa vuosi ta suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieliŋ.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Kɔŋ die dɩ keŋ yigi wo, die ʋ yaala jaaŋ dɩ ʋ dii; die ba yene dige nyindiikehe die ʋ daansɩ.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Die saŋka mi yʋagɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo die ʋ kpi. Die ba sɩɩ wa nyaaŋ ta nagɩ wa a jʋalɩ dʋaŋ jarɩnbɩsa juoŋ me.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: Ta die a ye ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ yuorine, ta jaaŋ kaanɩ dɩ keŋ sʋʋna a sɩɩ sɩba garɩteelikiŋ ba yigine ka kʋanʋa anɩɩsa wa a yaa keŋ sʋʋna tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Joopa die ka faasɩ yʋa aŋaŋ Liida; die wɩa die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna dɩ Piita bie Liida ma wa die ba tʋŋ dembisi bale ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ba ga balɩ wa dɩ, Tɩ jʋʋsa fʋ yi lagɩ lagɩ a keŋ tɩ jigiŋ.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Die ka ma dɔŋɩsɩ yiri yiri aŋaŋ nyindiekemba dɩ vuunene tɩŋgbaŋ aŋaŋ nembisi.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ yi siri wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba die dɩ ga. Die ʋ gana a ga tʋgɩ wa ba nagɩ wa jʋalɩ ga juoku me. Mi kpihɔgʋba mana die dɩ taaŋ giliŋ Piita a kʋma ta daga wa nyinyeeki diekemba Dɔkasi die dɩ baalɩna yɩ ba wa saŋŋa dieke ʋ benne ʋ mɩsɩ ma wa.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ta die ʋ wʋŋ lɔlɩŋ dɩ balala a yɩa wa dɩ, Piita, hagɩ a kʋʋ a ŋɔbɩ.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Ama Piita die dɩ yagɩ ba mana dɩ nyɩŋ juoku me, ta die sʋʋŋ a gbirigi ʋ dune me a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta die yiŋŋi a daansɩ kumbu ta baarɩ dɩ, Tabita, hagɩ! Die ʋ yuori ʋ nine a ye Piita, ta die hagɩ a kalɩ.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Ama Piita dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, aayɩ, n ye ka ŋɔbɩ jadieke tɩ chʋarɩna.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
