local: Piita die dɩ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a suŋŋi wo ʋ hagɩ zie ta wa kpihɔgʋbaha aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ banɩŋ ba a nagɩ wa dagɩ ba dɩ ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ wo ʋ miivoli me mɩŋ.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Die lɔlɩkʋ dɩ bɩ balɩ yɩ wa bɩbra dɩ, Da keŋ wa jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine kasɩ jadɩngɩtɩŋ.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Wɩɩrɩ gie die dɩ dɩa ga Joopa mana ma ta vuosi pam die dɩ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa yada ta dɩ ʋ sieŋ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Antiyoku ma, die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ dɩdagɩrɩŋ bataŋ die bie wo Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ sʋŋ: die ba yine Banabasi aŋaŋ Simiani , aŋaŋ Lususi, vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Sarene aŋaŋ Manani aŋaŋ Sɔɔli.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Naa die yiwo bʋtaa, ta die jaabʋ dɩ yiŋŋi a ga jʋalɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Piita die dɩ wɩarɩ a bie Joopa ma a yʋasɩ; die ʋ bie wo daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ wʋnsɩnana gbaŋtɩ ba wasa wa Simoni tigiŋ me mɩŋ.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ die ba jɩanna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ta bɔbɩ nʋaha, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vʋarɩ Banabasi aŋaŋ Sɔɔli aŋ ba tʋŋ tʋŋ dieke wɩa n wana ba wa dɩ ba tʋŋ wa.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Piita die ye ko yile ʋ daansɩkʋ chɩaŋ, die Koniliawosi die dɩ tʋnna dembiŋ diekemba ba die dɩ ye Simoni tigiri dɩ benne jigidieke a keŋ zie ka sanʋarɩ tɩŋ.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba bɩ bɔbɩ nʋa ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta nagɩ ba nuusi a dɩɩsɩ ba ma ta die taaŋ ba ba ga.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: a wa a pɩasɩ dɩ, Chaanʋ bie giena ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Simoni Piita mɩŋ.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ tʋnna Banabasi aŋaŋ Sɔɔli wa dɩ ba ga tʋŋ ʋ tʋʋmaha, die ba sʋʋŋ ga Selusia ma a nagɩ haarɩŋ a ga Salamasi, tɩgɩdieke die benne Saapurusi tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Ama Piita die ye ko mɩa ʋ baaŋ yi die a sɩba daansɩkʋ chɩaŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ dɩ balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Wʋmma dembisi bataa bie giena a dɩ dɩa a yaala fʋ.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Die ba tʋgɩna Salamisi ma wa die ba ga juu Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a ga mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha mi. Die Jɔɔn Maki die bie mi a yi ba susuŋŋiru.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Die wɩa hagɩ a sʋʋŋ ga ta da chɩɩlɩ dɩ fʋ aŋaŋ ba ga, dama manɩŋ n tʋnna ba.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba nyɩŋ Salamisi ma ta die a dɩa a tɩaŋ ga tʋgɩ Pafosi. Mi die ba haarɩ daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ yine yuŋaŋvuoŋ ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Bajaasusi, ta yi Juu vuodieke die dɩ gaanana vuosi dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma, Piita die dɩ sʋʋŋ a ga balɩ dembisisi dɩ, Manɩŋ nɩ yaala wa. Bɩa wɩa nɩ keŋ.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: Die ʋ yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka mi jakʋʋŋ Sejusi Pɔɔlusi zʋa. Die jakʋʋrɩ die yiwo yɩantieŋ ta die tʋŋ wa Banabasi aŋaŋ Sɔɔli dɩ ba keŋ a balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa aŋ ʋ wʋŋ.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Ta ba yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ Koniliawosi tʋnna tɩ. Ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ta jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ta Juu vuosi mana dɩ faasɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma pam. Ŋmɩŋ malakasisi kaanɩ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ ʋ wa fʋ a keŋ ʋ tigiŋ me amʋ ʋ nan wʋŋ wudieke fʋ yalla dɩ fʋ balɩ.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Ama die yuŋaŋvuoke, vuodieke die dɩ dine Elimasi Giriiki jabalɩkɩŋ ma wa die ka saagɩ a yɩ ba: ta die mɩa dɩ ʋ kagɩ jakʋʋrɩ aŋ ʋ daa keŋ yi Yisa yada.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Die Piita dɩ yaa ba a juu gbaaŋ, die ba dʋa mi tʋŋ dɩ vʋŋ. Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋʋna ʋ yi siri wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba dɩ ga; die ʋ chanchaalɩba bataŋ die dɩ benne Joopa ma wa die dɩ beeli wo.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Womi die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ die dɩ suuli Sɔɔli vuodieke die dɩ bɩna a dɩ Pɔɔli, die ʋ daansɩ yuŋaŋvuoke die.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa die ba ga tʋgɩ Siisiria ma, jigidieke Koniliawosi aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ aŋaŋ ʋ zʋalɩŋ die dɩ kalɩna a chɩɩsa wa.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: ta balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yiwo Sitaani bʋa ta bɩ yi tʋnvɩɩna mana dataaŋ ta suuli aŋaŋ wʋbɩatɩ yiri yiri mana, ta mɩa saŋŋa mana dɩ fʋ tarɩgɩ Ŋmɩŋ wusie wʋbalɩkaha a yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Die Piita die dɩ dɩna baa ʋ juu gbaaŋkʋ sʋŋ, ta Koniliawosi die dɩ keŋ tuoli wo sanʋarɩ ma, ta sʋʋŋ gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa aŋaŋ jɩlɩma.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Lele Ŋmɩŋ baa ʋ datɩ fʋ tɩbɩŋ ta vaa fʋ yɩɩŋ ta kaaŋ dɩ yese daraa ale. Lele womi die mɩɩlɩgɩsɔbɩlɩŋ dɩ nan daa wa nine me ta ʋ dɩ dɩa a gaba a yaala vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan yigi ʋ nuuŋ me.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Ama ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Hagɩ, dama n gbaŋ gbaŋ n yiwo vuota.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Die jakʋʋrɩ dɩ yene wudieke dɩ yine wo, die ʋ yi Yisa yada, dama die dɩ faasɩ yiwo mamachi aŋaŋ wudieke ba dagɩnana yaa gamma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ma wa.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Piita die ko bala wɩa a yɩa Koniliawosi ta die a ga juo tigiri me, a die ye vuosi pam dɩ lagɩsɩna a kalɩ.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ nagɩ haarɩŋ a nyɩŋ Pafosi me a ga Pega dɩ benne Pamifilia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, mi die Jɔɔn Maki dɩ vaa ba ta yiŋŋi ga Jerusalemi.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi mɩra ka yɩ wa sieŋ dɩ tɩ lagɩŋ yaa a kaagɩ vuodieke dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuoŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ dagɩ mɩŋ dɩ n daa zetime vuoŋ ʋ kana ka yi Juu vuoke wɩa.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Die ba nyɩŋ Pega a tɩaŋ ga Antiyoku dɩ benne Pisidia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ die ba ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me a sʋʋŋ kalɩ.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Die wɩa nɩ tʋnna a wa mɩŋ wa maŋ keŋ ta ka zetiye. Lele nɩ vaa n pɩasɩ nɩ, Bɩa wɩa nɩ tʋŋ a wa mɩŋ? .

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Die ba karɩnna Mosisi mɩraha sʋŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba maagɩkʋ sʋŋ a kpatɩ wa, Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku sʋŋ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ balɩ ba dɩ, Tɩ nɩmballɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ yaa wʋvɩɩna a baaŋ nan balɩ yɩ vuosisi gie nɩ balɩ.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Koniliawosi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Deeri kanɩŋ wa saŋka gie gbaŋ gbaŋ agbaa dɩ nɩgɩna a tɩaŋ hawasɩ ataa die maŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ n tigiri me, womi maŋ ye daa wʋnyɩ dɩ yeegine nyɩŋnyɩgɩsa dɩ kenne zie n nɩŋŋa.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Womi Pɔɔli die dɩ hagɩ zie ta a kɔtɩ ʋ nuuŋ ta piili a bala dɩ: Nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ dieke Izara vuosi dɩ jɩanna, nɩ wʋmma mɩŋ.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: a baarɩ dɩ, Koniliawosi! Ŋmɩŋ wʋŋ fʋ jʋʋsɩŋ ta tɩɩnsɩ piini dieke fʋ yɩnana zɔɔlɩntielibe.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Tɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩna tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba a yi ba faasɩ dala saŋŋa dieke die ba benne Ijipiti me a yi chaamba wa. Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩna ba a nyɩŋ mi aŋaŋ ʋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Die wɩa, tʋŋ vuoŋ aŋ ʋ ga Joopa ma a ga wa daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩna Simoni Piita. Ʋ yiwo chaanʋ a bie Simoni gbaŋwʋnsɩrʋ wa tigiŋ me vuodieke tigiri dɩ gbigine mʋgɩrɩ.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: Ta die daansɩ ba bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa saŋŋa dieke die ba benne hagɩrɩ ma, aŋaŋ ba dene ka tuo ʋ nʋarɩ mana.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Die wɩa maŋ tʋŋ a wa fʋ lagɩ lagɩ wa, ta fʋ dɩaŋ fʋ kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ a keŋ. Tɔ, tɩ mana bie wo Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa dɩ tɩ wʋŋ wudieke mana tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yɩna fʋ nʋaŋ dɩ fʋ balɩ tɩ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Saŋka gie mi gbaŋ gbaŋ naakpɩɩŋ Herodi die dɩ mugise Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Die ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ chʋʋsɩ buuriŋ ayʋpɔyɩ die dɩ benne Keenani tɩŋgbaŋ ma ta die vaa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi Izara vuosi dɩ sɩɩ.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ piili a bala dɩ, Lele n wa sɩba dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ Ŋmɩŋ ka luge.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Die ʋ yi sojasi nʋaŋ ba gobi Jemisi, Jɔɔn nɩmbʋa sikpeŋ aŋaŋ jɩbɩwaŋ.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Wɩaha gie mana die yiye mɩŋ bɩna kɔbɩsɩ-nɩɩsa aŋaŋ baŋɩsɩ-nʋ sʋnsʋŋ. Saŋŋa dieke ba sɩna tɩŋgbaŋka, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ ba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ ba dii ba nɩŋŋa a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Samuli dɩ kenne.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: ama ʋ tuose buuriŋ nɩŋ mana vuodieke dɩ chɩgɩnana wa ta yie wudieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba bɩ keŋ pɩasɩ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Samuli dɩ ʋ yɩ ba naaŋ, die wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩ Sɔɔli, Kisi bʋa vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Benjamani buuriŋ ma dɩ ʋ yi ba naaŋ. Die ʋ yiwo ba naaŋ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Die ʋ yene ta Juu vuosi sʋgɩtɩ fɩalɩya wa aŋaŋ wudieke ʋ yine wo, die ʋ vaa ba yigi Piita saŋŋa dieke ba dinene paanʋ dieke ba kana ka naga dabɔtɩ a yie wo dʋʋgaka.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Nɩ sɩba dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ wa Izara vuosi wudieke dɩ dagɩna wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa, dɩ Yisa Masia ma tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ nansɩ aŋaŋ taŋ; wʋnɩŋ Yisa Masia yiwo tɩ mana Yɔmʋtieŋ.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ wuuri ʋ naarɩ ta vaa Davidi dɩ dii naarɩ. Naa die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ yaa gamma Davidi ma dɩ, Davidi, Jese bʋa wa yine daa dieke chanchaalɩŋ n yaalala, ʋ yie wudieke mana n yaalala dɩ ʋ yi.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Die ʋ yigine Piita wa kpatɩ wa ʋ yi ba ligi wo dansarɩka ma ta yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ sojasi lagɩmɩŋ lagɩmɩŋ bʋnɩɩsa gbara wa a tarɩgɩma taŋ. Die lagɩmɩŋ mana die yiwo sojasi banɩɩsa. Ta a yile sɩba ʋ vʋarɩ wa gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋgaka saŋŋa a dii ʋ sarɩya yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Nɩ wɔŋ sɩba wudieke mana die dɩ yine Judia mana ma ma mɩŋ. Die wɩaha gie mana die piili wo Galili me, Jɔɔn die dɩ bala dɩ vuoŋ mana tuo Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ sɩɩla kʋaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ Davidi haagɩsɩ ma vuoŋ wʋnyɩ bala ʋ keŋ a gbatɩ Izara vuosi a taaŋ, wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi yine Yisa.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Die wɩa, die Piita die dɩ bie dansarɩka ma ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ faasɩ a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩa wa.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die nagɩ wa ʋ Halɩkasɩka aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ a yɩ Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Nazeriti wa. Die ʋ bie wo Yisa jigiŋ saŋŋa dieke die ʋ dɩna dɩ dɩa a tʋma tʋnvɩɩna a yɩa vuosi ta gbaama vuodiekemba die dɩ benne Sitaani nuusi me.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Ama Jɔɔn die dɩ woliŋ keŋ a mʋʋlɩ a yi Izara vuosi mana dɩ ba vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ aŋ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ wa keŋ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ bala ka vʋŋ aŋ Herodi nagɩ Piita a yɩ vuosisi, dɩnɩŋ daarɩ yuku mi die Piita dɩ ye ko bɔbɩ aŋaŋ chɔrɩma a dʋa gʋʋra sojasi bale sʋnsʋŋ. Die ba bɔbɩ chɔrɩmaha kaanɩ aŋaŋ sojasi wʋnyɩ ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ ta kaanɩ dɩaŋ dɩ bɔbɩ ʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ sojasi ta sojasisi bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ gbarɩ dansarɩkaka sanʋadaaŋ.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tɩ mana die ye Yisa die dɩ yine wudieke Jerusalemi tɩŋ ma aŋaŋ Juu vuosi tɩgɩhanɩŋ ba mana, ta tɩ yiwo ʋ daansɩatieliŋ. Die ba kpaasɩ wa wa dagarɩkɩŋ ma die ʋ kpi.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Die Jɔɔn tʋʋmaha die dɩ gbine kpatɩŋ die ʋ pɩasɩ vuosisi dɩ, Mɩnɩa nɩ yile dɩ yi ye? N ka yi Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa. Nɩ wʋmma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ baa ʋ keŋ wo n kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta maŋ ka mʋ dɩ n vʋarɩ ʋ nɩɩra gbaŋ.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Womi ta Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ, ta chaanɩŋ dɩ chaaŋ dansarɩka ka ma. Die malakaka dɩ dɔŋ dɔŋ Piita bɩŋkpɩaŋ ma a sugiri wa ta baarɩ dɩ, Hagɩ lagɩ lagɩ! Ta ʋ hagɩ. Die ʋ hagɩna wa, chɔrɩma ha dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ nuusi me a nan.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Ama ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa kumbu me daraa ataa daraaŋ ta yi ʋ hagɩ ta tɩ ye wo ʋ seŋ yi vuota.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Lele, nɩnɩŋ Abarahami haagɩsɩ aŋaŋ nɩnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta bie giena a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ, nɩ wʋmma wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna tɩ dɩ ka daga tɩ baaŋ nan yi die a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ dama ka yiwo vuoŋ mana sɩɩtɩ.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Malakaka dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Mɩŋŋɩ bɔbɩ fʋ gbanɩŋ ta sʋʋ fʋ nɩɩra. Piita dɩ yi die. Die malakaka dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Yeegi fʋ jayeekiŋ a dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Daa vuoŋ mana die yene wo. Die dɩ yiwo tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ tɩ yi ʋ daansɩa tieliŋ die yene wo, ta die die ta nyuo aŋaŋ wa ʋ kumbu sʋgɩrɩŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma aŋaŋ ba nyɩŋkʋraha die ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Yisa yine Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ wa, ta ka bɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋbalɩkaha ba ŋaana karɩma davʋʋsɩkɩŋ daaŋ mana wa chɩaŋ. Die wɩa die ba balɩ a chʋʋsɩ wa sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ bala die wo.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Piita die dɩ dɩ malakaka a nyɩŋ dansarɩka ka ma, ama die ʋ ka sɩba dɩ ʋ daansa mɩŋ yaa ka seŋ yie mɩŋ.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ tɩ nʋaŋ dɩ tɩ mʋʋlɩ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna a yɩma vuosi dɩ Yisa yine vuodieke ʋ vʋarɩna dɩ ʋ yi vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba mɩsɩ ma aŋaŋ kunti sarɩya diiru.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Aŋaŋ die ba kana a ka ye wudieke ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ wa ta mʋ dɩ ba kʋʋ wa wa mana, ama die ba balɩ a yɩ Paliti dɩ ʋ yi ba kʋʋ wa.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Die ba ga garɩsɩ nɩŋŋaŋ sojasisi die dɩ gbarɩna wa aŋaŋ banɩŋ ba die dɩ diisine wo ta die keŋ tʋgɩ kʋʋtɩ gbɩabɩ dieke dɩ benne gaamɩŋ ma die dɩ yuorinene a juo tɩka sʋŋ, die ka gbaŋ gbaŋ ka yuori. Die ba chʋŋ a nyɩŋ a chʋŋ a sʋʋna siekpɩɩkʋ ma womi malakaka die dɩ bɩa ta va Piita.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naazʋamba mana die baarɩ dɩ vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine Yisa yada, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa Yisa wa saaŋ ma.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Die ba yine wo wudiekemba mana Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bala yaa gamma ʋ ma wa a kpatɩ wa, die ba nagɩ wa a keŋ sʋʋŋ dagarɩkʋ ma a nagɩ wa a ga sʋʋŋ dʋaŋ vɔrɩŋ ma.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Piita die dɩ yene ko bala wɩaha ta die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ die dɩ keŋ a sʋʋŋ vuodiekemba mana die dɩ wʋnnana ʋ wʋbalɩkaha ma.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Die Piita dɩ wa mɩŋŋɩ wudieke dɩ yinene wo ta baarɩ dɩ, Lele n sɩba a baarɩ dɩ seŋ yiwo wusie, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna ʋ malaka ʋ keŋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ Naaŋ Herodi nuusi me, ta bɩ vʋarɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ Juu vuosisi die dɩ yilinene dɩ ba yi mɩŋ die wo mana mana ma ma.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Ama ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa kumbu me.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Die dɩ yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba dɩ yine Juu vuosi ta beeli Piita a nyɩŋ Joopa a keŋ wo mamachi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ nagɩna ʋ piini dieke dɩ yine ʋ Halɩkasɩka a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi wo.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩna naa wa, die ʋ ga Jɔɔn Maki nuŋ Meri tigiŋ. Die vuosi pam die lagɩsɩna mi a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Die ʋ nyɩŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ dɩna ʋ kʋaŋ a nyɩŋ Galili a keŋ tʋgɩ Jerusalemi wa jigiŋ daraa pam. Lele banɩŋ ba wana yi ʋ daansɩatieliŋ vuosisi jigiŋ.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Dama die ba wʋŋ ba dɩaŋ ba balɩnana wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ ta bɩra Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ. Womi Piita dɩ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Piita die dɩ ga kpaasɩ sanʋarɩ ta havʋʋbiŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ yine tʋntʋntʋ mi ta Roda die dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ ye vuodieke dɩ kpaasɩnana wa.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ wa tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba nʋaŋ, ta tɩ bie gie dɩ tɩ bala nɩ wʋvɩɩnaha.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Vuosi gie tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka sɩba die tɩ tuone die wo, vuoŋ beri a baaŋ bɩagɩ kagɩ ba dɩ ba daa sɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩna Piita lɔlɩkʋ die dɩ yiwo nansɩŋ die ʋ chɩgɩ a yiŋŋi a ga ta ka yuori sanʋarɩ, a nata a baarɩ dɩ, Piita zie sanʋarɩ tɩŋ.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: dɩ ʋ seŋ yi he mɩŋ a yɩ tɩ tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine ba haagɩsɩ aŋaŋ ʋ sʋgɩrɩna Yisa a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo. Die Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ, yɩla die dɩ gʋtɩna bule wa ma dɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine n Bʋadembiŋ, ta jinne n nan dagɩ vuosi dɩ n seŋ yiwo fʋ Chʋa.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Die ba balɩ wa dɩ, Juo yɩɩŋyaatɩ. Ama die ʋ kpaŋŋɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bala dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie. Die ba baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ nan yiwo ʋ malaka.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba sɩɩ ba; die ba sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma. Die wɩa die ba balɩ Piita dɩ ʋ wɩɩrɩ ba jigiŋ daraa ale.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ nan sʋgɩrɩ Yisa kuŋ me ta ʋ kaaŋ bɩ kpi bɩbra. Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bala dɩ, N nan yɩ nɩ nʋaŋ kasɩ dieke gbaŋ gbaŋ die n yɩna Davidi wo.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Ta Piita die dɩ ye ko a kpaasa sanʋarɩ, die ba ga yuori a ye wo, die dɩ yi be mamachi.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Die ʋ kɔtɩ ʋ nuuŋ dɩ ba tarɩ watɩ, ta balɩ ba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yine die a vʋarɩ wa a nyɩŋ dansarɩka ka sʋŋ. Ta die balɩ dɩ, Nɩ balɩ Jemisi aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ banɩŋ ba wudieke dɩ yine. A kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ nyɩŋ mi a ga jigigaaŋ.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Ta yɩlaha gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ bɩbra Davidi die balɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, Fʋ kaaŋ vaa fʋ Vuokasɩ wa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ hʋʋgɩ vɔrɩŋ ma.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Die tuŋ dɩ vʋʋna sʋkʋʋŋ die dɩ faasɩ mugisi sojasisi die dɩ gbarɩna wa. Die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, Bɩa yine Piita wa.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Die Davidi dɩ benne ʋ mɩsɩ ma wa die ʋ dɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ. Die ʋ kpine wo ba guu wo ba buuriŋ vuota jigiguusikiŋ ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ hʋʋgɩ.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaalɩ wa, ama die ba ka ye wo. Die wɩa die ʋ vaa ba pɩasɩ gbɩgbarɩtɩba wʋpɩasɩka ta die yi nʋaŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba. Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ nyɩŋ Judia a sʋʋŋ ga Siisiria a die yi daraa ale mi.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die sʋgɩrɩ wa Yisa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ ka hʋʋgɩya.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Herodi die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni vuosi; die wɩa die ba lagɩŋ taŋ dɩ ba ga ye wo. Die ba woliŋ ye Bilasitusi dɩ ʋ suŋŋi be, die ʋ yiwo vuodieke die dɩ yine naaŋ wa tigiri nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ. Die ba keŋ ga Herodi jigiŋ a ga nɩgɩ nɩɩpɔɔŋ dɩ ʋ vaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bemme ba sʋnsʋŋ dama Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni die yese ba nyindiike a nyɩna naaŋ Herodi tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Die wɩa n nɩmballɩ, n yaala nɩ sɩmma dɩ dɩɩ yi nɩ yi Yisa yada Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ nɩ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ. Ama Mosisi mɩraha nɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ wʋbɩatɩ ma.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Die naaŋ Herodi die vʋarɩ daaŋ a zieŋ, die daarɩ dɩ tʋgɩna wa Herodi die dɩ yeegi ʋ naala nyiŋyeekikpɩɩma, ta nyɩŋ kalɩ ʋ naaŋ gbaŋtɩ mama ta balɩ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Ama vuodieke mana dɩ yine Yisa yada ye wo gbatɩtaanɩŋ a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Die vuosisi die dɩ natɩ a baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ lɔlɩkʋ sɩɩ sɩba ŋmɩŋ daa vuota.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Nɩ sɩmma dɩ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dɩ bala wa da keŋ yi nɩ. Die ba baarɩ dɩ.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Lele wo mi Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ vaa yʋagɩbɩaŋ dɩ yigi wo, die ʋ nyuuti die dɩ suuli aŋaŋ gbarɩtɩ a nyuo wo ʋ kpi dama die ʋ ka yi Ŋmɩŋ kanɩŋ jɩlɩma ka mi.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Wʋmma, nɩnɩŋ kalinbaantieliŋ, vaa a ka yi nɩ mamachi aŋ nɩ kpi! Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ vʋarɩnana Ŋmɩŋ fala ha aŋaŋ ʋ bala wudieke wo N nan yi wɩɩŋ nɩ yene bie nɩ mɩsɩ ma ta nɩ kaaŋ tuo dii dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ keŋ dagɩ nɩ a chɩasɩ gbaŋ. .

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Ama saŋka mi die vuosi pam die dɩ wʋŋ wɩaha ta yi yada.

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ nyɩnana lagɩŋkʋ jigiri, vuosisi die dɩ jʋʋsɩ ba dɩ ba viiri keŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daa dieke dɩ kienene wo a faasɩ dagɩ ba wɩaha gie bɩbra.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Banabasi aŋaŋ Sɔɔli die dɩ kpatɩna ba tʋʋmaha dieke die ba gana dɩ ba yiwo, die ba nagɩ Jɔɔn Maki a gʋtɩ ba ma a nyɩŋ Jerusalemi a ga Antiyoku.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Die lagɩŋkʋ die dɩ hagɩna wa, Juu vuosi pam aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi ama ta tarɩgɩ ba jɩamɩŋ ta dɩ Juu vuosi jɩaŋkʋ, die ba mana die dɩdɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi kʋaŋ. Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ kpaaŋ ba pam dɩ ba kpaŋŋɩ ba gbaŋ a dɩa Ŋmɩŋ die aŋ ʋ chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Kʋaŋ chaaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ die dɩ tʋgɩna die dɩ ko sɩɩ sɩba tɩka vuosi mana die gana dɩ ba wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Die Juu vuosi die dɩ yene kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yi be sʋgɩyɔɔŋ pam. Die ba zɩa Pɔɔli ta bala chʋʋsa wudieke mana ʋ balala.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Ama Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ wa a faasɩ bala aŋaŋ sikimiŋ dɩ, Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ tɩ woliŋ balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha a yɩ nɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi ta wa balɩ vuogaasɩ. Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ zeti wɩaha mɩŋ ta bɩ yile dɩ nɩ ka mʋ aŋaŋ miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ wɩa, die yine tɩ vasa nɩ, ta gara vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi jigiŋ.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Dama nʋarɩ gie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ tɩ dɩ: Manɩŋ n vʋara fʋ mɩŋ dɩ fʋ yi chaanɩŋ a yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi, amʋ dʋnɩa mana ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. .

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ wʋnna naa wa, ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ fɩalɩ, ba bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha. Ta vuodiekemba mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ ba ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ dɩ yi Yisa Masia yada.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩa die dɩ dɩa mi tɩgɩsɩ mana mana.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Juu vuosisi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ga tɩka dembisi dieke dɩ yine vuokpɩɩma aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpɩɩma die dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ ta ba yie ba jɩlɩma pam, a vaa ba sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ hagɩ yaa gamma Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi ma, ta piili yaala wɩa a yɩa ba a yagɩ ba a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka mi lʋgɩŋ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Die wɩa Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ kpaantɩ ba nataala taŋkɔlɩŋ a taaŋ tɩka mi dɩ ka yi dagɩtɩ bɩaŋ a yɩ ba ta die ga Yikonumi tɩŋ ma.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Ta Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne Antiyoku ma wa die dɩ suuli aŋaŋ Halɩkasɩka aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Saŋka mi die Yisa tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne Judia lʋga mana die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi gbaŋ tuo Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ yiŋŋine a keŋ Jerusalemi ma, Juu vuosi vuodiekemba die dɩ yine Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ dɩ piili a nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ wa. Die ba yaala vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ gobi ba kɔla.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: ta baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ ga bie wo vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta die aŋaŋ ba gbaŋ.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Womi Piita die dɩ piili a bala ba wudieke gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ yine dɩ:.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: Die n bie wo Joopa tɩŋ ma a jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ta keŋ a ye ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ yuorine ta jaaŋ kaanɩ dɩ sɩna sɩba garɩteelikiŋ dɩ kenne sʋʋŋ n jigiŋ.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Die n daansɩna ka sʋkʋ die maŋ ye tigiŋ dɔŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ haagɩŋ dɔŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ nyinvuuke aŋaŋ nembisi.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ta die maŋ wʋŋ lɔlɩŋ dɩ balala yɩa mɩŋ dɩ, Piita, hagɩ a kʋʋ nyinti gie a ŋɔbɩ.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Die maŋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ n kaaŋ bɩagɩ yi die, n ye ka ŋɔbɩ jadieke tɩ chʋarɩna.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ta lɔlɩkʋ dɩ bɩ nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma a balɩ yɩ mɩŋ bɩbra dɩ, Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ baa dɩ jaaŋ vɩɩna aŋaŋ diile da keŋ baa ka ka mʋ aŋaŋ diile.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Naa die yiwo die bʋtaa, ta jaabʋ mana die dɩ yiŋŋi ga arɩzanna ma.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Lele womi dembisi bataa die dɩ nyɩna Siisiria dɩ keŋ zie tigi dieke n benne wo sanʋarɩ tɩŋ.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ balɩ yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n gamma aŋaŋ ba ta da vaa ka mugisi mɩŋ. Womi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ bayʋaba diekemba die dɩ benne Joopa ma die dɩ beeli mɩŋ tɩ ga Siisiria a ga Koniliawosi tigiŋ.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: ʋ balɩ tɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ malaka nyɩnna ʋ jigiŋ. Dɩ ʋ tʋŋ aŋ ba ga Joopa a ga wa vuoŋ wʋnyɩ saaŋ dɩ dine Simoni Piita.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Dɩ Piita wa nan balɩ wa wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi mana dɩ baaŋ nan yi die a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Die n piiline a bala wa, die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ die dɩ a keŋ a sʋʋŋ ba ma sɩba die dɩ kenne sʋʋŋ tɩ ma piiliku me wo.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mi die maŋ tɩɩnsɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ balla dɩ, Jɔɔn die sɩwa aŋaŋ nyaaŋ ama Ŋmɩŋ nan sɩɩ nɩ aŋaŋ ʋ Halɩkasɩka.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ banɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi piini dieke die ʋ yɩna tɩ saŋŋa dieke die tɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia yada wa; mɩnɩa yine mɩŋ dɩ n kagɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ da yi wudieke ʋ yaalala ʋ yi.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Die ba wʋnna Piita balɩkʋ wa, die ba ka bɩ nɩgɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ ta piili a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ a bala dɩ, Lele Ŋmɩŋ vaa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ba vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ta yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Die Sitiiveni kʋʋla kʋaŋ chaaŋ mugisibɩaŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ma die ba chɩgɩ jaasɩ jigiŋ mana. Die bataŋ die chɩgɩ ga Fonisia aŋaŋ Saapurusi aŋaŋ Antiyoku ta die mʋʋla Yisa wɩa a yɩa Juu vuosi nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Ama Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dieke dɩ yine Saapurusi vuosi aŋaŋ Saarini vuosi die dɩ ga Antiyoku a ga mʋʋla tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ die dɩ bie ba jigiŋ, die vuosi pam die dɩ tarɩgɩ ba beriŋ ta yi Yisa yada.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma wa dɩ wʋŋ wudieke dɩ yinene wo, ta die tʋŋ Banabasi Antiyoku ma.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Die ʋ gana a ga tʋgɩ mi a ye Ŋmɩŋ dɩ suŋŋine be die wo, ʋ sʋŋ die dɩ fɩalɩ ta die ʋ kpaŋŋɩsɩ ba mana dɩ ba da vaa Yisa kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasi die yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ta yaa yada pam ta suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, die vuosi pam die dɩ keŋ yi Yisa yada ta bɩrɩŋ ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Die Banabasi dɩ ga Taasusi dɩ ʋ yaalɩ Sɔɔli.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Die ʋ yene wo wo, die ʋ yaa wa a yiŋŋi keŋ Antiyoku, mi ba bale wo mana die dɩ nagɩ biŋ mʋʋmɩŋ a lagɩma aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ta dagɩ vuosi pam mi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Die dɩ yiwo Antiyoku ma die ba woliŋ wa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ, Kirisito vuosi.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Die saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma dɩ keŋ Antiyoku.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ saaŋ die yine Agabusi, ta Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ die dɩ suŋŋi wo ʋ balɩ dɩ kɔŋ bɩaŋ nan nan jigiŋ mana dʋnɩa ma. Die dɩ keŋ yi mɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Kolodiasi dɩ yine naakpɩɩŋ.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ benne Antiyoku ma wa die dɩ saŋ dɩ ba nan suŋŋi ba nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ benne Judia ma, dɩ vuoŋ mana chɩasɩ ʋ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ die.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Die ba yi die a nagɩ ligirehe a tʋŋ Banabasi aŋaŋ Sɔɔli dɩ ba yaa ga yɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ benne Judia ma.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi dɩ kenne tʋgɩ Yikonumi ma, die ba ga Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me a ga mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩna mi sɩba die ba yine die Antiyoku ma wa, ta vuosi pam die yi Yisa yada, bataŋ die yiwo Juu vuosi bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Ama ta Juu vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Yisa yada wa dɩ balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ ba da keŋ tuo dii, ta die balɩ ba dɩ wudieke ba bala yaa gamma Yisa ma ka yi wusie. Die wɩa vuodiekemba die dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne mi wo.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ bie mi daraa pam ta die a faasɩ bala Yisa wɩa aŋaŋ dembisi. Die Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ ba hagɩrɩŋ die ba tʋŋ mamachi tʋʋma, ta die dagɩ dɩ ba wɩaha yaa gamma ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha seŋ yiwo wusie.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Die tɩka vuosi die dɩ puo bule, bataŋ die dɩ dɩɩ Juu vuosisi kʋaŋ ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩdɩ tʋntʋntɩba kʋaŋ.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Die wɩa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi bataŋ aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi aŋaŋ ba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ saŋ dɩ ba yi Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi bɩaŋ ta taa ba aŋaŋ tana a kʋʋ.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Ama tʋntʋntɩba bale wo dɩ mɩŋŋɩna wudieke dɩ yinene wo die ba maansɩ a nyɩŋ chɩgɩ a ga Likaonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die ba ga wa Lisitira aŋaŋ Debe tɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ diekemba dɩ gbigine mi.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Mi die ba mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Lisitira ma daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ beri a yi gbarɩgɩŋ a nyɩŋ ʋ ballɩbɩsɩ me ta ka bɩagɩ chʋŋ.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Ta die kalɩ mi a wʋmma Pɔɔli wʋbalɩkaha. Pɔɔli die dɩ daansɩ wa die a ye dɩ ʋ yaa yada Yisa ma ta nan bɩagɩ ye gbaamɩŋ.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Die wɩa Pɔɔli dɩ natɩ aŋaŋ lɔlɩkpɩɩŋ dɩ, Hagɩ a zie fʋ nagɩsɩ ma! Daa wa die dɩ hagɩ a yugi a zie ta piili a chʋŋ gilime.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yene wudieke Pɔɔli dɩ yine wo, ba piili a nata ba gbaŋ gbaŋ jabalɩkɩŋ ma dɩ, Tɩ ŋmɩnaha bɩrɩŋ wa vuota a keŋ sʋʋŋ tɩ jigiŋ.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Womi vuosisi die dɩ yi Banabasi saaŋ dɩ Zusi ta Pɔɔli dɩaŋ dɩ Hemisi dama die ʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balla wʋbalɩkaha.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Die Zusi jɩamɩŋ juoku die zie gbigi wo sanʋa dieke dɩ kenne juu tɩka sʋŋ, die ka bʋkaabɩtʋ aŋaŋ kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ yaa naarasɩ aŋaŋ tɩɩwute a keŋ mi dɩ ba yi kaaba a yɩ Banabasi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Ama Banabasi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ wʋnna ba bala ba yi wudieke wo, die ba chɩɩrɩ ba nyinyeeke aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ta chɩgɩ juu kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ a nata dɩ:.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: Bɩa nɩ yie die? Tɩ yiwo vuota sɩba nɩnɩŋ! Tɩ keŋ wo gie dɩ tɩ mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha aŋ nɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ wʋtɔgɩtɩ gie me a keŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ naanna arɩzanna aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ aŋaŋ nyaakpɩɩbʋ aŋaŋ nyindiekemba mana dɩ benne a sʋŋ.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa Ŋmɩŋ die vaa buuriŋ mana dɩ yie ba dʋŋŋʋ.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Ama saŋŋa gie ʋ daga dɩ ʋ seŋ beri mɩŋ aŋaŋ wʋvɩɩŋ diekemba ʋ yinene wo; ʋ yɩnana nɩ nɩɩŋ a nyɩna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta vasa nyinti dɩ bɩrɩma, a yie nɩ nyindiike ta vasa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ fɩala.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Banabasi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ bala wudieke wo mana yɔrɩ, die dɩ hagɩrɩ mɩŋ dɩ ba kagɩ ba dɩ ba da yi kaaba a yɩ ba.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ Antiyoku dieke dɩ benne Pisidia tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ Yikonumi a keŋ balɩ a yiŋŋi kpɩkpaakʋ sʋgɩtɩ ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Pɔɔli. Ta die taa wa tana a datɩ wa nyɩŋ tɩka lʋgɩŋ ta yile dɩ ʋ kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Ama Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ taaŋ a giliŋ wo die ʋ keŋ hagɩ zie a yiŋŋi ga tɩka sʋŋ. Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Banabasi dɩ nyɩŋ ga Debe ma.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi dɩ mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha Debe ma ta die ye vuosi pam ba yi Yisa yada. Womi die ba yiŋŋi ga Lisitira a nyɩŋ mi ga Yikonumi ta bɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Antiyoku dɩ benne Pisidia ma.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Die ba kpaaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ta jʋʋsɩ ba dɩ ba ko yi yada dene. Die ba balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩ mʋ tɩ tuoli wahala ta ye juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ vʋarɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba lagɩmɩŋ lagɩmɩŋ mana ma. Womi die ba bɔbɩ nʋa a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mi.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ daagɩ Pisidia tɩŋgbaŋ ma a ga Pamifilia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: mi die ba mʋʋlɩ Yisa wɩaha Pega tɩŋ ma. Womi die ba sʋʋŋ ga Atalia ma.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: ta die a wa nyɩŋ mi a nagɩ haarɩŋ a yiŋŋi ga Antiyoku dɩ benne Siria ma. Mi die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ die wɔŋ nagɩ ba yi Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me dɩ ʋ suŋŋi be ba tʋʋma ma. Ta lele ba wa tʋŋ tʋʋmaha kpatɩ mɩŋ.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Die ba kenne a tʋgɩ Antiyoku wa die ba lagɩsɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana. A die a balɩ ba wudieke mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ suŋŋine be ba yi, aŋaŋ ʋ yuorine sieŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ba yi Yisa yada a yi ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dene.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Ama die ba wɩarɩ mi aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba a yʋasɩ.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi die dɩ dɩa a daagɩ Amifipolisi aŋaŋ Apolonia ma a ga tʋgɩ Tesalonika. Mi die Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ die dɩ beri.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ ga juu juoku me ta jɩama Ŋmɩŋ sɩba ʋ ŋaana yie dene wo. Ta die nagɩ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraa ataa a balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi. Die ʋ nagɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: a daga ba dɩ Masia wa mʋ ba kʋʋ wa mɩŋ, ama ʋ nan hagɩ kuŋ me. Pɔɔli dɩ balɩ ba dɩ Yisa wa yine Masia ka ʋ balala wa.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Die bataŋ die dɩ tuo Pɔɔli dɩ bala wudieke wo a dii ta die bɩrɩŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ a gʋtɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi ma. Die vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ama ta jɩama Juu vuosi jɩaŋkʋ bataŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpɩɩma pam die dɩ tuo wɩɩrɩ dii.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Juu vuosi die dɩ yene dɩ vuosi pam gʋta Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi ma wa, die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba, die wɩa die ba lagɩsɩ vuotɔgɩtɩ nyʋkʋ jigiŋ a yi kpɩkpaaŋ. Die ba yie watɩ ta piili yaala waagɩŋ ta die ga a ligi daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Jasoni tigiŋ a yaala Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi dɩ ba vʋarɩ ba a yɩ vuosisi.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Ama die ba kenne ta ka ye be wo die ba yigi Jasoni aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ a yaa ba ga tɩka nyɩŋkʋra jigiŋ ta natɩ dɩ, Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi dɩ dɩa a chʋʋsa tɩgɩsɩ. Lele ta wa keŋ giena.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: ta Jasoni dɩ tuo be chaantɩ ʋ tigiŋ me. Ba mana chʋʋsa naakpɩɩka mɩraha ta baarɩ dɩ naakpɩɩŋ wʋnyɩ benne ba wasa wa Yisa.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Ba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ vuotamba dɩ wʋnna naa, die dɩ faasɩ mugisi be.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Die wɩa nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ vaa Jasoni aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba banɩŋ ba dɩ tuŋ ligire ta die ba vaa ba ba ga.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Yuŋ die dɩ kenne a sibi, die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaa Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi dɩ nyɩŋ ga Beria ma. Die ba gana a ga tʋgɩ mi wo ba ga juu Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Beria vuosi die dɩ faasɩ yaala ba wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha a tɩaŋ Tesalonika vuosisi. Die ba wʋmma wɩaha aŋaŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ mana ta die daaŋ mana ba karɩma Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ ba ye dɩ wɩaha yiwo wusie.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Ba vuosi pam die dɩ yi yada a bɩrɩŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ. Giriiki hɔgʋkpɩɩma aŋaŋ dembisi pam gbaŋ die dɩ yi Yisa yada.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Die Juu vuosi die dɩ benne Tesalonika ma wa die dɩ wʋnna dɩ Pɔɔli bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa Beria ma wa die ba bɩ ga mi a piili a bala chʋʋsa Pɔɔli a yɩa kpɩkpaakʋ ta vaa ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Lele womi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ taaŋ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ dembisi dɩ sʋʋŋ ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaaŋ ama Silasi aŋaŋ Timoti die dɩ wɩarɩ a bie Beria ma.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Dembisi bataŋ die dɩ ga aŋaŋ Pɔɔli a ga tʋgɩ Atensi ta die yiŋŋi aŋaŋ tʋntʋmɩŋ a keŋ balɩ Silasi aŋaŋ Timoti dɩ ba keŋ wʋnɩŋ Pɔɔli jigiŋ lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Die Pɔɔli dɩ chɩɩsɩnana Silasi aŋaŋ Timoti Atensi ma wa die ʋ sʋŋ dɩ faasɩ chʋʋsɩ ʋ yene dɩ tɩka suuli wo aŋaŋ bugile.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ ga Juu vuosi jɩamɩŋ jigiŋ a bala yɩa banɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ jɩanna aŋaŋ ba. Ta daaŋ mana ʋ bala a yɩa vuodiekemba ʋ haarɩna nyʋkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Bataŋ die yiwo Epikuriani aŋaŋ Sitoyiki dɩdagɩrɩŋ ta die nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ Pɔɔli. Bataŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Bɩa vuodieke dɩ bala dɩ ʋ yiwo sɩsɩbɩrʋ wa gie dɩ yaala ʋ dagɩ? Ta bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ bala tɩŋgbaŋ gaasɩ bugile wɩa. Die ba balɩ wa naa dama Pɔɔli die bala Yisa aŋaŋ kuŋ hagɩŋ wɩa.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Die wɩa die ba yaa wa a ga tɩka nyɩŋkʋra lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ ba wasɩnana Aropagusi a baarɩ dɩ, Balɩ tɩ fʋ dagɩhaalɩkʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Tɩ wʋŋya fʋ bala wʋhaala a taŋ ta wa yaala tɩ sɩmma a chɩasɩ.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Die Pɔɔli dɩ hagɩ zie lagɩŋkʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ dɩ: Atensi vuosi, n yeye dɩ nɩ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ jɩanna ŋmɩna pam aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Dama n chʋnnana a gilime tɩka sʋŋ a daansa jigidiekemba nɩ jɩanna wa, maŋ ye kaabɩŋ koriŋ kaanɩ ba maagɩna ka ma dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ Dieke Tɩ Zɩna . Nɩ jɩama Ŋmɩnnɩ gie ta ka mɩŋŋɩ sɩba wa. N yaala n balɩ nɩ mɩŋ ʋ wɩa.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Ŋmɩnnɩ gie naanna dʋnɩa aŋaŋ nyindiekemba mana dɩ benne ka sʋŋ. Ta yi ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie tieŋ ta ka bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me vuota dɩ nagɩna ba nuusi a mɩɩ.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Ʋ ka yaala suŋŋiŋ a nyɩna vuoŋ mana jigiŋ, ʋ yɩnana vuota miivoli aŋaŋ voosiŋ aŋaŋ jaaŋ mana.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die naaŋ wa vuota yiri yiri mana a nyɩŋ chɔɔŋkʋbalɩmɩŋ ma ta vaa ba bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie mana ma. Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die ʋ ziene saŋŋa dieke aŋaŋ jigidieke buuriŋ mana dɩ baaŋ nan dɩ beri.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yiwo naa amʋ tɩ nan yaalɩ ʋ jigiŋ a ye wo, dama ʋ ka yʋa aŋaŋ tɩ wʋnyɩ mana.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: ta yɩa tɩ hagɩrɩŋ tɩ baaŋ dɩ beri ta dɩ dɩa chʋŋ ta bɩ yi vuodiekemba tɩ yine. Tɩ yiwo ʋ ballɩ, sɩba vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bala die wo.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Tɩ wana yi ʋ ballɩ naa tɩ ka mʋ tɩ yilime dɩ ʋ sɩɩ sɩba buuŋ vuoŋ dɩ nagɩna salɩma yaa taŋ mɩɩ. Ʋ ka sɩɩ sɩba jadieke vuota dɩ yiline ta mɩɩ.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa ta vuosi die dɩ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wa, die ʋ ka naga ba taalɩ. Ama lele wo ʋ baarɩ vuodieke mana dɩ benne jigidieke mana vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ta yiŋŋi keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Dama ʋ vʋarɩ wa daaŋ a zieŋ ʋ baaŋ nan dii dʋnɩa vuosisi mana sarɩya aŋaŋ wusie. Ta vʋarɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ dii sarɩya ka a yɩ wa. Ŋmɩŋ dagɩ tɩ mɩŋ dɩ ka seŋ yiwo wusie aŋaŋ ʋ sʋgɩrɩna Yisa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me wo.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Die ba wʋnna kumbu hagɩkʋ wa wɩa bataŋ dɩ vʋara Pɔɔli fala ta bataŋ dɩ baarɩ, Tɩ yaala fʋ bɩ a balɩ die tɩ wʋŋ.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Die wɩa Pɔɔli die nyɩŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Dembisisi bataŋ die dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ta yi Yisa yada. Ba sʋŋ die wone Diyonisusi die ʋ yiwo Aropagusisi vuosi wʋnyɩ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ dieke saaŋ dɩ dine Damerisi aŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Saŋŋa mi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ Judia ma a keŋ Antiyoku ma a die a piili a daga Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ ye gbatɩtaantɩŋ sie ba gobi ba kɔla sɩba Mosisi die dɩ dagɩna dene wo.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi aŋaŋ ba die dɩ faasɩ a nɩgɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ pam yaa gamma wɩɩrɩ gie ba dagɩnana wa, die ba vʋarɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ a tʋŋ ba Jerusalemi ma dɩ ba ga ye Yisa tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nyɩŋkʋraha yaa gamma wɩɩrɩ gie wɩa.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Die vuodiekemba Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ tʋnna wa die dɩ daagɩ Fonisia aŋaŋ Samaria a bala yɩa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne mi dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi bataŋ tarɩgɩ wa ba beriŋ a bɩrɩŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ. Wɩɩrɩ gie die dɩ yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Die ba gana a ga tʋgɩ Jerusalemi wa die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ ba nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ tuo be chaantɩ. Die wɩa Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi die dɩ balɩ ba wudieke mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ suŋŋine be ba tʋŋ.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Ama Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ yine Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ hagɩ zie a baarɩ dɩ, Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ buuri gaasɩ dɩ yine Yisa yada wa gobi ba kɔla ta bɩ dɩa Mosisi mɩraka.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Die wɩa, die tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ dɩ ba daansɩ jabalɩkɩrɩ.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Die ba balɩ ka a yʋasɩ aŋaŋ taŋ, ta Piita die dɩ keŋ hagɩ a baarɩ dɩ, N zʋalɩŋ, nɩ sɩba dɩ saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ nɩ ma dɩ n mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi, amʋ ba nan wʋŋ ta yi Yisa yada ta bɩrɩŋ ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna vuota sʋŋ die dɩ dagɩ dɩ ʋ tuo be mɩŋ ta yɩ ba Halɩkasɩka sɩba die ʋ yɩna tɩ die wo.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ die ka luge tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba, dama die ba yine Yisa yada wa wɩa, die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Bɩa nɩ yaala nɩ magɩsɩ Ŋmɩŋ? Bɩa nɩ yaala nɩ yi buurigaasɩ diekemba dɩ bɩrɩnna Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ pɔgɩlɩ mɩɩrɩ dieke tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ dɩ kana ka bɩagɩ pɔgɩlɩ?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die chɩgɩ tɩ wa zɔɔlɩŋ tɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ tɩ yine wo yada wa wɩa sɩba buurigaasɩ die.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Die vuoŋ mana die dɩ tarɩ ta wʋmma Banabasi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ balala yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna ba hagɩrɩŋ ba tʋŋ mamachi tʋʋma a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dene.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Die ba bala naa a kpatɩ, Jemisi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ wʋmma mɩŋ!.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simoni Piita wɔŋ balɩ tɩ, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wolinne a ga vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi jigiŋ a vʋarɩ bataŋ ba bɩrɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi dene.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Naa, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋbalɩka die dɩ dagɩ sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋtɩ ma dɩ.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ manɩŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nan yiŋŋi a keŋ, a keŋ mɩɩ Davidi tɩgɩdieke dɩ nanna wa. N nan mɩɩ ka a zie a nyɩŋ ka chʋʋsɩŋ ma ta vaa ka hagɩrɩma bɩbra.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Ta vuota mana nan keŋ n jigiŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta maŋ wa dɩ ba yi n vuosi wo.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Naa Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ, wʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ vana ba sɩba wɩɩrɩ gie a nyɩŋ kʋrɩŋ ma wa. .

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Jemisi die dɩ bɩ balɩ a gʋtɩ dɩ, Dɩ yiwo manɩŋ n sʋŋanyile dɩ tɩ da mugisi buurigaasɩsɩ dɩ yiŋŋine a dɩ Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ tʋana.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Ama dɩ mʋ dɩ tɩ maagɩ gbanɩŋ a balɩ ba dɩ ba da keŋ dii jadiiki dieke ba kaabɩna a yi bugile, ta balɩ ba dɩ ba yigi ba gbaŋ aŋaŋ daakpana yaa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, ta bɩ daa ŋɔbɩma dɔŋ diekemba ba ŋmʋna kʋʋ ta daa bɩ ŋɔbɩma zɩŋ.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Mosisi mɩraha nɩŋ, ba karɩŋ ha mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana kʋrɩŋ ma wa, ta mʋʋlaha tɩŋ mana ma.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Die wɩa die tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ yigi zieŋ dɩ ba vʋarɩ dembisi bataŋ aŋ ba dɩa Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi a ga Antiyoku. Die ba vʋarɩ wa Silasi aŋaŋ Judasi vuodieke saaŋ dɩ bɩna yi Basabasi, vuosi bale wa gie die yiwo Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ ba yɩa jɩlɩma.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Die ba maagɩ wa gbanɩŋ dɩ: Tɩnɩŋ Yisa tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ maagɩna gbaŋkʋ gie a waasa nɩnɩŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta bie Antiyoku aŋaŋ Siria aŋaŋ Silisia ma mana.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Tɩ wʋŋya dɩ tɩ vuosi bataŋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ, a balɩ wɩa ataŋ dɩ mugisi nɩ pam ta chʋʋsɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ, ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ ka tʋŋ ba nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Die wɩa tɩ mana dɩ lagɩsɩ a saagɩ dɩ tɩ vʋarɩ dembisi bataŋ a tʋŋ ba aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ choti Banabasi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Vuosi gie, Judasi aŋaŋ Silasi, yiwo vuodiekemba die dɩ nagɩna ba mɩsɩ a dʋaŋ talɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩaha gie wɩa.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Judasi aŋaŋ Silasi nan bɩ balɩ nɩ ka chɩaŋ, wudiekemba tɩ maagɩnana wa.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dagɩ tɩ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ da keŋ nagɩ mɩrɩ gaasɩ a gʋtɩ nɩ bɩbra.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Wudieke dɩ kana ka mʋ dɩ nɩ yi wʋnna: Nɩ da keŋ a dii jadiiki dieke mana ba nagɩna a yi kaaba a yɩ bugile, nɩ da keŋ ŋɔbɩ zɩŋ, nɩ da keŋ ŋɔbɩma dɔŋ dieke ba ŋmʋna kʋʋ, ta bɩ yigi nɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma ma. Dɩɩ yi nɩ dɩ wɩaha gie, dɩ nan dɩ vɩɩna a yɩ nɩ. A mana wonde.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Die ba tʋŋ ba, ba sʋʋŋ ga Antiyoku, mi die ba lagɩsɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana ta nagɩ gbaŋkʋ a yɩ ba.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Die vuosisi dɩ karɩŋ gbaŋkʋ gie, ba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ aŋaŋ ka kpaŋŋɩsɩna ba dene wo.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Die Judasi aŋaŋ Silasi, vuodiekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ die bie ba jigiŋ a yʋasɩ ta die a balɩ wɩa pam a kpaŋŋɩsɩ ba ta yi ba yada dɩ bɩ hagɩrɩ a gʋtɩ.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Die ba bie mi a yʋasɩ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne mi wo dɩ taaŋ ba dɩ ba waasɩ tigiŋ, ba yiŋŋi ga vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnna ba jigiŋ. .

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi gbaŋ die dɩ yʋasɩ Antiyoku ma, mi banɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi pam die dɩ daga ta mʋʋla tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩa a yɩa vuosi.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Die dɩ yʋasɩna bɩta Pɔɔli die dɩ balɩ yi Banabasi dɩ, Vaa aŋ tɩ yiŋŋi ga a kaagɩ tɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ diekemba dɩ benne tɩgɩ diekemba mana die tɩ mʋʋlɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩaha a ye ba sɩna dene.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Banabasi die dɩ yaala ʋ nagɩ Jɔɔn Maki a gʋtɩ ba ma aŋ ba ga.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Ama Pɔɔli die ka yaala dene dama Maki die ka kpatɩ tʋʋmaha aŋaŋ ba ta die vaa ba Pamifilia ma ta yiŋŋi kuli.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Banabasi dɩ nɩgɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ ta die puo taŋ. Banabasi die dɩ nagɩ Maki ba nagɩ haarɩŋ a ga Saapurusi.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Ta Pɔɔli dɩaŋ dɩ nagɩ Silasi ta Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nagɩ ba yi Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me dɩ Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi be.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Die ba chʋŋ daagɩ Siria aŋaŋ Silisia chaaŋ ma a kpaŋŋɩsa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ nyɩŋ Atensi ma a ga Korinti ma.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: mi die ʋ haarɩ Juu daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Akuyila ama die ba mɩɩrɩ wa wa Pontusi ma, ta die nyɩŋ Itali aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ Pirisila, dama naakpɩɩŋ Kolodiawosi die yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ Juu vuosi mana nyɩŋ Roma tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Ta Pɔɔli die dɩ ga dɩ ʋ ye be.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: ta die ye dɩ ba yiwo gbaŋwʋnsɩrɩŋ sɩba ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ wɩarɩ ba jigiŋ ba lagɩsa tʋma.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Ta davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana Pɔɔli ŋaaŋ ga wa Juu vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a bala yɩa Juu vuosisi aŋaŋ buurigaasɩsɩ a yaala ʋ tarɩgɩ ba.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Ama Silasi aŋaŋ Timoti die dɩ nyɩna Masedonia a keŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nagɩ ʋ saŋŋa mana a mʋʋla a yɩa Juu vuosisi dɩ Yisa yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba yiŋŋi a waga wa ta zɩa wa. Die wɩa die ʋ kpaantɩ ʋ nyinyeeke taŋkɔlɩŋ a taaŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ waarɩ nɩ miivoli, ka wa chii wo nɩ sikpeŋ me. N nuuŋ bɩ wo mi. A nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma sie n vaa nɩ ta ga mʋʋlɩ Yisa wɩaha a yɩ buurigaasɩsɩ.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ nyɩŋ mi a ga daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Titusi Jusitusi tigiŋ. Die ʋ ka yi Juu vuoŋ ama ta jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ta ʋ tigiŋ die dɩ gbigi Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Kirisipusi vuodieke die yine Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ die dɩ yi Yisa yada, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ mana; ta vuodiekemba dɩ benne Korinti ma die dɩ wʋŋ wɩaha ta yi yada ta die ba sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Yuŋ kaanɩ Pɔɔli die dɩ daansɩ, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Da keŋ vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ, fʋnɩŋ ko a mʋʋlɩma n wɩaha ta da keŋ vaa.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: dama n bie wo fʋ jigiŋ, vuoŋ wori a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi fʋ daŋŋa, dama n yaa vuosi pam tɩka gie me.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Die wɩa Pɔɔli die dɩ bie mi biŋ aŋaŋ chɩɩtɩ ayʋaba a daga vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Die saŋŋa dieke Galiwo die dɩ yine Akaya tɩŋgbaŋ jakʋʋrɩ, die Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ lagɩsɩ a yigi Pɔɔli ta die a yaa wa ga sarɩya diile jigiŋ.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: a ga baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie mɩa dɩ ʋ vaa tɩ vuosisi jɩama Ŋmɩŋ sie dieke tɩ mɩrɩka dɩ kana ka yɩ sieŋ.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Pɔɔli die baa ʋ balɩ wɩɩŋ ta Galiwo dɩ balɩ a yɩ Juu vuosisi dɩ, Nɩ wʋmma, nɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi dɩ tɩŋ yi daa wa dɩ yiwo wʋbɩaŋ n tɩŋ nan wʋŋ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: ama ka yine nɩnhagɩrɩŋ yaa gamma wʋbalɩka aŋaŋ saara aŋaŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ mɩraha wɩa nɩŋ, n kaaŋ dii a die sarɩya nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ wʋnsɩ wɩɩrɩ.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Ta die yagɩ ba a nyɩŋ sarɩya diilehe juoku me.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Die wɩa kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yigi Sosetenesi vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩ wa a nɩgɩ wa sarɩya diile juoku nɩŋŋa, ama Galiwo baga die wo ba ma.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ bie Korinti tɩŋ ma aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba daraa pam, ta die vaa ba ta nagɩ Pirisila aŋaŋ Akuyila a gʋtɩ ʋ ma ba nagɩ haarɩŋ a dɔŋ gara Siria. Die ba yaalala dɩ ba ga wa Pɔɔli die dɩ wɔŋ ʋ sikpeŋ Senkiria ma dama die ʋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Die ba ga a tʋgɩ Efesusi, Pɔɔli die dɩ vaa Pirisila aŋaŋ Akuyila mi. Ta die ga Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku ma ta die dɩ balɩ Yisa wɩa aŋaŋ vuosisi.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Die ba balɩ dɩ ʋ bemme ba jigiŋ a yʋasɩ ama die ʋ zeti.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Ama die ʋ taaŋ ba ta baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ n nan yiŋŋi keŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Ta die yiŋŋi keŋ juu haarɩkʋ a ga.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Die ʋ kenne tʋgɩ Siisiria ma die ʋ tɩaŋ ga Jerusalemi a ga waasɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mi ta sʋʋŋ ga Antiyoku.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: die ʋ yi daraa ale mi, ta die nyɩŋ mi a ga Galasia aŋaŋ Firigia chaatɩ a suŋŋe Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana mi dɩ ba yada gʋtɩ.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Die Juu daa wʋnyɩ die ba wasɩnana Apolosi die dɩ keŋ Efesusi, die ba mɩɩrɩ wa wa Alekizandira tɩŋ ma. Die ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna balɩŋ ta bɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ wɩa pam.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ bʋgɩrɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩaha, ta balɩ ha aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam. Ta wudieke ʋ dagɩnana yaa gamma Yisa ma wa die dɩ yi wusie, ama wudieke die ʋ sɩbɩna die yine Jɔɔn wʋbalɩka yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sɩɩla wɩa nyɩɩna.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Apolosi die dɩ piili a bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me ta ka chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ. Ama Pirisila aŋaŋ Akuyila die dɩ wʋnna ʋ balɩkʋ die ba nagɩ wa ga ba tigiŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a dagɩ wa sie dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dagɩna dɩ tɩ dɩaha.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Apolosi die dɩ saŋ dɩ ʋ ga Akaya ma, die wɩa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne Efesusi ma dɩ maagɩ gbanɩŋ a yɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne mi wo dɩ ba tuo wo chaantɩ. Die ʋ gana a ga tʋgɩ mi wo die ʋ suŋŋi vuodiekemba mana dɩ yine yada Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩla wɩa.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi die dɩ faasɩ nɩgɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ pam yaalɩŋ ma ta die ʋ nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ wɩa a dagɩ ba dɩ Yisa yine Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa ta die nyaŋŋɩ ba.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Die Apolosi die dɩ yene bie Korinti ma wa, Pɔɔli nɩŋ die dɩ ga Galasia aŋaŋ Firigia chaatɩ a ga Efesusi. Mi die ʋ haarɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: Ta die pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Die nɩ yine Yisa yada wa die Halɩkasɩka keŋ nɩ ma mɩŋ? Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ, tɩ ye ka wɔŋ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka wɩa.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, Die nɩŋ ba sɩna nɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ wa chɩaŋ? Ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Ba sɩɩ tɩ sɩba Jɔɔn die dɩ ŋaana sɩa vuosi die wo.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi baarɩ dɩ, Jɔɔn die sɩa vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ ka dagɩ dɩ ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ ba wʋbɩatɩ ma ta yiŋŋi keŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Ama ta die a bɩ balɩ ba dɩ vuoŋ wʋnyɩ kieŋ ʋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ dɩ ba yiwo yada. Yisa die yine vuodieke Jɔɔn dɩ balala yaa gamma wa.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Die ba wʋnna naa wa, die ba sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa saaŋ ma.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Ta Pɔɔli die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ba ma ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ keŋ a sʋʋŋ ba ma, die ba bala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ haala, ta bala wudiekemba dɩ nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Die ba yiwo sɩba dembisi baŋ aŋaŋ ale.
