eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ chʋŋ gara Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me die chɩɩtɩ ataa ta daga vuosisi yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa ta ka chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ ta die mɩa dɩ ʋ tarɩgɩ ba yɩaŋ.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Ama vuosisi bataŋ die yiwo sikpeŋ hagiritieliŋ ta zeti dɩ ba kaaŋ tuo a dii ta die balɩ wʋbɩatɩ pam yaa gamma sie dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna wa kpɩkpaakʋ nine me. Naa chɩaŋ ma Pɔɔli die dɩ vaa ba ta nagɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ba ga Tiranusi dagɩŋ juoŋ me ta daga mi daaŋ mana.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Die ʋ dagɩ wa die bɩna ale ta vuodiekemba mana die dɩ benne Asia tɩŋgbaŋka ma wa ta yi Juu vuosi yaa buurigaasɩ die dɩ wʋŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩaha.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ Pɔɔli hagɩrɩŋ ʋ tʋma mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nagɩ garɩ diekemba dɩ gbɩna Pɔɔli nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a chiihe gara vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna jigiŋ ta garɩtɩ gie dɩ vasa be yese alaafɩa, ta jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ nyɩna ba ma.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Die Juu dembisi bataŋ die dɩ piili a dɩdɩa a mɩa dɩ ba vʋarɩ jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ ma. Die ba ŋaŋ balɩ yɩ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ dɩ N yɩa nɩ nʋaŋ, Yisa dieke Pɔɔli dɩ balala ʋ wɩaha saaŋ ma dɩ nɩ keŋ nyɩŋ.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ ba wasɩnana Sikeva ballɩ bayʋpɔyɩ dɩ yie naa.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Ama jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N sɩba vuodieke Yisa dɩ yine ta wʋŋ Pɔɔli wɩa, ta nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ mɩnɩa yine nɩ.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Womi daa dieke jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ yalla wa die dɩ yugi a jʋalɩ ba a nɩgɩ ba pam ta yi ba daŋŋa a chɩɩrɩ ba garɩtɩ, ba chɩgɩ nyɩŋ tigiri me yɔrɩ.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Die Juu vuosisi aŋaŋ buurigaasɩsɩ dɩ benne Efesusi ma wa dɩ wʋŋ naa wa, die dɩ yi be ŋmaamɩŋ pam ta bɩrɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa saaŋ pam.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba pam die dɩ piili a bala wʋbɩatɩ diekemba ba yine a yɩa vuosisi yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Ta vuodieke die dɩ yalla yuŋ die chii ba gbaŋtɩ a keŋ nyɩŋ a jʋʋ ha daadamba nine me. Die gbaŋtɩ gie ligire die yi sɩba ligire bie tuse baŋɩsɩ-nʋ.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩaha die dɩ mʋʋlɩ ga jigiŋ mana ta bɩ gʋta aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Genhe mana kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vaa Pɔɔli dɩ saŋ dɩ ʋ ga kaagɩ Masedonia aŋaŋ Akaya ta tɩaŋ ga Jerusalemi. Ta die baarɩ Maŋ keŋ nyɩŋ mi n nan ga Romi.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ susuŋŋiru bale, Timoti aŋaŋ Erasitusi dɩ ba ga Masedonia ma, ama die ʋ wɩarɩ Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma daraa ale.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Saŋka mi die wumugisike pam die dɩ nan yaa gamma sie dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna wɩa.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Die macheeri wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Demetirusi, die ʋ naga siliva a wʋnsa juobisi sɩba ba ŋmɩŋ Diana jɩamɩŋ juoku, ta die tʋʋmaha dɩ yɩa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba nyʋarɩ pam.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Die wɩa die ʋ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba mana aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ tʋnnana ʋ tʋʋmaha chanchaaŋ a lagɩsɩ a balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, N zʋamba, nɩ sɩba a baarɩ tɩ yese tɩ nyʋarɩ a nyɩna tɩ tʋʋmaha gie mi.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yeye ta wʋŋ wudieke Pɔɔli dɩ yinene. Ʋ baarɩ dɩ ŋmɩna diekemba vuota dɩ wʋnsɩna ka yi ŋmɩna, ta bɩagɩ a tarɩgɩ vuosi pam yɩaŋ Efesusi ma giena aŋaŋ Asia lʋga mana.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Vuosi nan piili a bala wʋbɩatɩ yaa gamma tɩ tʋʋmaha, ba kaaŋ bɩ yɩ Diana jɩamɩŋ juoku jɩlɩma, ŋmɩŋ dieke Asia aŋaŋ dʋnɩa mana dɩ jɩanna. Ba nan daaŋ tɩ ŋmɩŋ kpɩɩkʋ Diana wɩa.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ wʋnna naa wa ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta piili a nata dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ Efesusi vuosi ŋmɩŋ Diana yiwo ŋmɩŋ kpɩɩŋ.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Ta tɩka mana die dɩ dɔŋ. Ta ba yigi Gayusi aŋaŋ Arisitakusi vuodiekemba die dɩ dɩna Pɔɔli a nyɩŋ Masedonia a keŋ wo a nagɩ ba ga tɩka lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yaala ʋ nyɩŋ ga a balɩ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi ama Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die ka yɩ wa sieŋ.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Die tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha bataŋ die dɩ yine Pɔɔli zʋalɩŋ die dɩ tʋŋ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ keŋ a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ da keŋ keŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Saŋka mi die vuodiekemba die dɩ benne lagɩŋkʋ jigiri die dɩ nata tɔra tɔra ta vuoŋ mana ka sɩba wudieke ʋ yinene, ta pam dɩaŋ dɩ ka sɩba lagɩŋkʋ chɩaŋ.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Ta Juu vuosisi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba pam die kpaŋŋɩ daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Alekizanda a nyɩŋ zieŋ kpɩkpaakʋ nɩŋŋa a piili a bala wa wudieke ʋ baaŋ nan balɩ. Ta Alekizanda die dɩ kɔtɩ ʋ nuuŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta mɩa dɩ ʋ dagɩ wudieke dɩ yinene wo chɩaŋ.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Ama kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ʋ yiwo Juu vuoke ba mana die dɩ natɩ die hawasɩ ale dɩ Tɩnɩŋ Efesusi ŋmɩnnɩ Diana yiwo ŋmɩŋ kpɩɩŋ.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Ka kpatɩŋ ta tɩka jakʋʋŋ dɩ yi kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ tarɩ watɩ, ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩnɩŋ Efesusi vuosi, mɩnɩa benne dʋnɩa ka gie me ta ka sɩba dɩ Efesusi vuosi gbarɩna ta daansa Diana ŋmɩŋ kpɩɩŋ juoku. Mɩnɩa kana ka sɩba dɩ ʋ buubu die dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a keŋ sʋʋŋ wa bie wo giena?.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a chiisi wɩɩrɩ gie, die wɩa nɩ fɩalɩ nɩ gbaŋ ta nɩ da keŋ yi wudieke dɩ kana ka mʋ.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Nɩ chii wo vuosi gie keŋ juu gie, vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka gaarɩ tɩ jɩaŋkʋ juoku jaaŋ yaa a balɩ wʋbɩatɩ a yɩ tɩ ŋmɩŋ Diana.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Dɩɩ yi Demetirusi aŋaŋ ʋ tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yaa wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi gie, tɩ yaa sarɩya diile juone aŋaŋ sarɩya diiriŋ, nɩ vaa ba yaa ba wɩa ga mi.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi nɩ yaala nɩ yi wɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ die, die nɩŋ dɩ mʋ nɩ yaa wɩɩrɩ keŋ wo tɩka nyɩŋkʋra nɩŋŋa.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Romani vuosi nan bɩagɩ galɩŋ tɩ yaa gamma wudieke tɩ yine jinne wo. Dama tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ watɩtɩ gie chɩaŋ.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Die ʋ bala naa a kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ taaŋ vuosisi.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Die watɩtɩ dɩ tɩanna kʋaŋ chaaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ wa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba a kpaŋŋɩsɩ ba ta die taaŋ ba ta nyɩŋ ga Masedonia.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: Die ʋ dɩa ga mi chaakʋ tɩgɩsɩ mana a kpaŋŋɩsa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ wɩa pam. Ka kpatɩŋ die ʋ wa ga Giriisi.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: ta die bie mi a ga tʋgɩ chɩɩtɩ ataa. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ gɔmɩsɩ dɩ ʋ ga Siiria ta die mɩŋŋɩ dɩ Juu vuosisi saŋya mɩŋ a gbarɩ wa; naa chɩaŋ mana die ʋ saŋ dɩ ʋ yiŋŋi a daagɩ Masedonia a ga.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ gʋtɩna ʋ ma wa die yiwo Sopata, Pirusi bʋa ta nyɩŋ Beria aŋaŋ Arisitakusi aŋaŋ Sekondusi vuodiekemba die dɩ nyɩnna Tesalonika. A nagɩ Gayusi vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Debe, aŋaŋ Tichikusi aŋaŋ Tirofimusi vuodiekemba die dɩ nyɩnna Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ Timoti.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Die ba woliŋ dii tɩ nɩŋŋa ga a chɩɩsa tɩ Tiroasi ma.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Die dʋʋga dieke ba dinene paanʋ ta ka nagɩ dabɔtɩ a yi dɩ kenne a tɩaŋ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die tɩ nagɩ haarɩŋ a nyɩŋ Filipi, a gara Tiroasi. Daraa anʋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die tɩ ga haarɩ ba Tiroasi ma, ta die bie mi daraa ayʋpɔyɩ.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Die ka davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ jɩŋmɩŋ, die tɩ lagɩsɩ taŋ dɩ tɩ dii tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nyindiike. Pɔɔli die dɩ bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩa vuosisi die a ga tʋgɩ tanseese dama die ʋ baa ʋ nyɩŋ wa tɩka ma ka tʋnvʋʋsa.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Die popolisi pam die benne juodieke ma tɩ lagɩsɩna jarɩnbɩsaka sikpeku.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Die dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ die ba wasɩnana Yutikusi die dɩ kalɩ a takɔrɔ ma, ta Pɔɔli die dɩ balala wɩaha gbieŋ die dɩ faasɩ yigi Yutikusi. Ta die keŋ gʋʋrɩ ta yiŋŋi nan kʋaŋ a nyɩŋ jarɩnbɩsaka bʋtaa wa ma a keŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ. Die ba sʋʋŋ nagɩ wa ta ʋ kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Die Pɔɔli die dɩ sʋʋŋ ga a sʋʋŋ zʋʋrɩ Yutikusi ma a nagɩ wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuusi ta baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ da vaa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ chʋʋsɩ ta ʋ ka kpiye.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ wa jʋalɩ ga jarɩnbɩsaka sikpeŋ a gbieri paanʋ tɩ aŋaŋ wa dɩ ŋɔbɩ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha die tʋŋ die dɩ ga vʋʋŋ ta ʋ wa ga.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ die dɩ ga nagɩ dalʋagɩkɩrɩ ta ʋ bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ba yaa wa a kuli tigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ saŋ dɩ ʋ chʋŋ nagɩsɩ a ga Asosi ama tɩ vuotɩalɩkaha die dɩ nagɩ haarɩŋ a woliŋ ga mi, mi die tɩ baa tɩ nagɩ wa.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Die ʋ haarɩna tɩ Asosi ma die ʋ keŋ juu haarɩkʋ die tɩ dɔŋ ga Mitilene.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die tɩ keŋ jigiŋ kaanɩ dɩ gbine Kiyosi ta ka tʋnvʋʋsa dɩaŋ die tɩ ga tʋgɩ Samosi. Ka tʋŋ die dɩ bɩ vʋŋ tɩ ga tʋgɩ Militusi.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔli die wɔŋ saŋ mɩŋ dɩ ʋ dɔŋ tɩaŋ Efesusi dama die ʋ ka yaala ʋ yʋasɩ Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die ʋ yaala lagɩ lagɩ dɩ ʋ ga tʋgɩ Jerusalemi ʋ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a tʋgɩ dʋʋga dieke ba wasɩnana Pentikosi daraaŋ.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Die Pɔɔli die dɩ tʋŋ tʋntʋmɩŋ a nyɩŋ Militusi dɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nyɩŋkʋraha mana die dɩ benne Efesusi keŋ haarɩ wa mi.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Die ba kenne tʋgɩ mi wo ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ sɩba wudieke mana n yine saŋŋa dieke n wolinne a keŋ Asia ma ta bie nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Die Juu vuosi bataŋ die dɩ saŋ yaa gamma n ma ta die vaa maŋ dii wahala aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ pam. Ama die maŋ tʋma Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma ta sʋʋna n gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Die n mʋʋlɩnana Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha yaalɩŋ ma wa aŋaŋ nɩ tige me wo, die n ka lɔbɩrɩ wudieke dɩ baaŋ suŋŋi nɩ.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: Die n balɩ Juu vuosisi aŋaŋ buurigaasɩsɩ dɩ ba vaa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ yiile ta dɩa Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ ta yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa yada.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Ama lele n tuone Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ nʋarɩ wɩa maŋ gara Jerusalemi ama ta ka sɩba wudieke dɩ baaŋ nan yi mɩŋ mi.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Ama n dɩna dɩ dɩa tɩgɩsɩ mana Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ŋaŋ balɩ yɩ mɩŋ dɩ dansarɩka juule aŋaŋ wahala gbara mɩŋ Jerusalemi ma.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Ama n ka daansɩya ta n mɩsɩ yiwo jaaŋ, dama manɩŋ n yaala n tʋŋ tʋŋ dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa dɩ yɩna mɩŋ wa a kpatɩ, ta tʋʋmaha yiwo dɩ n mʋʋlɩma wʋvɩɩŋ dieke dɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wa.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Die n dɩ dɩa mɩŋ jige a mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa a yɩa nɩ. Ta lele, n sɩba a baarɩ nɩ wʋnyɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Die wɩa jinne n bala nɩ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ bɩa n nuuŋ bɩ wo ʋ ma.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Dama manɩŋ n bala nɩ wudieke mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala dɩ nɩ sɩmma.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Die wɩa nɩ mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ nɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ nagɩna yi nɩ nuusi me dɩ nɩ yi ba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta daansɩma ba wa. Nɩ yi sɩba yiyagɩrɩŋ a yɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba. Dɩ yiwo banɩŋ vuodiekemba ʋ gbatɩna a taaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ Yisa zɩŋ.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: N sɩba a baarɩ n garɩŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ vuobɩatɩ bataŋ nan keŋ a chʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ sɩba gbaanchʋra dɩ ŋaana yige yiise dene wo.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Ta saŋŋa bɩ kieŋ ta vuosi bataŋ nan daansɩ nyɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ jigiŋ a chɩɩba ŋmɩŋchɩbɩsɩ dɩ ba tarɩgɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba yɩaŋ aŋ ba dɩa ba.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Die wɩa nɩ gbara nɩ gbaŋ ta tɩɩnsɩma die n kpaanana nɩ aŋaŋ nɩntaantɩ yuŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩntʋasɩ die bɩna ataa wa.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Lele manɩŋ n nagɩ nɩ yie Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me aŋaŋ ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩaha, wɩɩrɩ gie nan bɩagɩ suŋŋi nɩ ta yɩ nɩ jadieke dɩ yine nɩ sɩɩtɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ vuosi.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ n ka wɔŋ yaala vuoŋ ligire yaa ʋ nyinyeeke.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ die n nagɩ wa n nuusi gie a tʋma tʋʋma a yaala jadieke manɩŋ aŋaŋ n chanchaalɩba dɩ yaalala.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Die n yine wudieke mana, dagɩya dɩ nɩ yallɩma nɩŋŋmɩna aŋaŋ tʋʋma ta bɩagɩ a suŋŋime wabɩtɩ, ta tɩɩnsɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa die dɩ bala dɩ, Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam nan yi vuodieke dɩ yɩnana piini a tɩaŋ vuodieke dɩ tuosinene. .

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ balɩna naa a kpatɩ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ sʋʋŋ gbirigi aŋaŋ ba ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Die ba yaala ba taaŋ wa saŋŋa dieke wo, zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi ba mana ba mana die dɩ kʋma ta nagɩ ba nuusi a goori wo.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Die ba sʋgɩtɩ die faasɩ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ bala ba dɩ Nɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra. Die ba beeli wo a ga haarɩkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi die dɩ yiŋŋi ga Debe aŋaŋ Lisitira ma, mi die ba haarɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Timoti. Ʋ nuŋ gbaŋ die yiwo Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ. Die ʋ nuŋ wo yiwo Juu vuoŋ ta ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ yi Giriiki vuoŋ.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne Lisitira aŋaŋ Ikonumi ma wa die dɩ balɩ wʋvɩɩna yaa gamma Timoti ma.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yaala ʋ nagɩ Timoti a gʋtɩ ʋ ma aŋ ba dɩa ʋ dɩasɩsɩ, die wɩa die ʋ gobi ʋ kɔlɩ. Die ʋ yi wo die dama Juu vuodiekemba die dɩ benne mi chaakʋ die sɩba a baarɩ Timoti chɔɔŋ yiwo Giriiki vuoŋ.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Die ba dɩna dɩdɩa tɩgɩsɩsɩ, die ba bala a yɩa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mɩrɩ diekemba Yisa tʋntʋntɩba aŋaŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma wa dɩ ziene wo, ta die balɩ ba dɩ ba dɩa ha.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Die wɩa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba yada die dɩ gʋtɩ ta die daaŋ mana vuosi pam dɩ yie yada a gʋta Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ma.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Die Pɔɔli aŋaŋ ʋ zʋalɩba die dɩ dɩa a daagɩ Firigia aŋaŋ Galasia chaakʋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die ka vaa ba balɩ Yisa wɩa Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Die ba gana ga tʋgɩ Misia, die ba mɩa dɩ ba juu Bitinia tɩŋ ma, ama Yisa Halɩkasɩka die ka yɩ ba sieŋ.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Die wɩa die ba daagɩ Misia ma a sʋʋŋ ga Tiroasi ma.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ yuku mi, Pɔɔli die dɩ ye ʋ daansa ma vuoŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ nyɩna Masedonia a keŋ zie a jʋʋsa wa dɩ, Keŋ Masedonia ma a keŋ suŋŋi tɩ.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔli daansaha kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die tɩ gɔmɩsɩ lagɩ lagɩ dɩ tɩ nyɩŋ ga Masedonia, dama die tɩ ye a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ balɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ ga Masedonia chaakʋ a ga mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩma vuosi mi.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Die tɩ nagɩ haarɩŋ a nyɩŋ Tirosi a daagɩ ga Samotirasi, ka tʋnvʋʋsa die tɩ ga Niipolisi. Mi die tɩ vaa haarɩkʋ.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: ta nyɩŋ mi a ga Filipi me, jigidieke die dɩ yine tɩgɩkpɩɩŋ Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die ka yiwo Romani vuosi sɩɩtɩ. Die tɩ yi daraa a ŋmɩna mi.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Ka davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ die tɩ nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ a ga jigidieke dɩ gbigine mʋgɩrɩ, die tɩ yile dɩ Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ nan dɩ bie mi. Ta die sʋʋŋ kalɩ a bala wɩa aŋaŋ hɔgʋ diekemba dɩ kenne mi wo.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Ba wʋnyɩ saaŋ die yine Lidia, die ʋ nyɩŋ wa Tiatira tɩŋ ma ta yaa garɩŋmɩnɩ diekemba dɩ yalla ligire pam a daa ta die bɩ yi vuodieke dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋ die yuori ʋ sʋŋ dɩ ʋ wʋŋ wudieke Pɔɔli dɩ balala wa.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Die tɩ sɩɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ jʋʋsɩ tɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi nɩ sɩba dɩ n seŋ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yada aŋaŋ wusie nɩ keŋ a bemme n tigiŋ. Die tɩ saagɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩkʋ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ die tɩ keŋ bɩ gara Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ, a tuoli havʋʋbiŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ yine yɔmʋ. Die ʋ yaa wa jɩmbɩaŋ ʋ ma ka yɩa wa hagɩrɩŋ ʋ bɩagɩ bala soriŋ daaŋ wɩa. Die ʋ yie naa a yese ligire pam a yɩa ʋ tieliŋ.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Havʋʋbike gie die dɩdɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ tɩ kʋaŋ a nata ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a bala dɩ, Dembisi gie yiwo Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ wo tʋntʋntɩŋ. Ba bala nɩ wudieke nɩ baaŋ yi a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: Die ʋ ko bala naa daraa pam. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ keŋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yiŋŋi a balɩ yi jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ, Yisa Masia saaŋ ma, n yɩ fʋ nʋaŋ, nyɩŋ ʋ ma! Lele womi jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ ma.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Die ʋ tielibe dɩ yene dɩ sie dieke ba nagɩna yese ligirehe chʋʋsɩya wa die ba yigi Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi a datɩ ba ga sarɩya diile jigiŋ.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Die ba balɩ a yɩ Romani nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, Dembisi gie yiwo Juu vuosi ta yaala ba chʋʋsɩ tɩ tɩka.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Ba daga tɩ wudiekemba tɩnɩŋ Romani vuosi dɩ kana ka mʋ dɩ tɩ yi.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ benne me gbaŋ die gʋtɩ a yaala ba nɩgɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi. Womi die Romani nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ wuuri Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi nyinyeeke ta yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ ba aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Die ba faasɩna a vɩɩrɩ ba a kpatɩ wa, ba nagɩ ba a yi dansarɩka ma, ta die balɩ dansarɩka gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa dɩ ʋ mɩŋŋɩ gbara ba vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Die gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa die dɩ yi sɩba ba bala wa dene. Die ʋ nagɩ ba a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ saasaa ta bɔbɩ ba nagɩsɩ aŋaŋ chɔrɩma a yaa ga bɔbɩ dakʋʋlɩkpɩɩma juoku sʋŋ.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Sɩba yuŋ tanseese, Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi die dɩ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ta chɩa yɩla a bɩra Ŋmɩŋ, ta ba dansarɩka jujuri taŋ dɩ wʋmma ba.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Lele wo mi tɩŋbaŋka die dɩ dɔŋ bʋnyɩ dansarɩka juoku chɩaŋ. Lagɩ lagɩ ka sanʋaha dɩ yuori ta chɔrɩmaha die dɩ kpaa kpaa dansarɩka jujuribe mana nuusi aŋaŋ ba nagɩsɩ ma ta nan.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Die dansarɩka gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa die dɩ hagɩna a ye ta sanʋaha yuori ye wo, die ʋ yile dansarɩkaka jujuribe mana chɩgɩ bɩa mɩŋ. Womi die ʋ datɩ a vʋarɩ takoobi dɩ ʋ kʋʋ ʋ gbaŋ.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Ama ta Pɔɔli die dɩ faasɩ a natɩ dɩ, Da keŋ a yi fʋ gbaŋ daŋŋa ta tɩ mana bie giena.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Die gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa die dɩ pɩasɩ a yaalɩ popoli a chɩgɩ juu dansarɩka sʋŋ ta sʋʋŋ gbirigi Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi nɩŋŋa ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ cheeli.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Womi die ʋ dii ba nɩŋŋa a yaa ba a keŋ nyɩŋ ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Nyɩŋkʋra, bɩa maŋ nan yi a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa yada, ta nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ deŋ mana.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Womi Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi dɩ balɩ wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne ʋ tigiŋ me Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Yuku mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa die dɩ nagɩ ba a ga sugiri ba kʋjagaha, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba sɩɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi mana Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Ta die ʋ wa nagɩ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi a ga ʋ tigiŋ me a die yɩ ba nyindiike ba dii. Die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ mana sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ fɩalɩ dama die ba wa yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yada.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa Romani nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ tʋŋ ba susuŋŋiriŋ dɩ ba ga balɩ gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa dɩ ʋ vaa Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi ga.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Die gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa dɩ balɩ a yi Pɔɔli dɩ, Nyɩŋkʋraha yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ n vaa fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Silasi ga yɔrɩ. Die wɩa nɩ gamma aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Ama ta Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ a yɩ susuŋŋiribe dɩ, Tɩ gbaŋ tɩ yiwo vuodiekemba Romani vuosi dɩ yɩna tɩ sieŋ, ta Romani nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ ka dii tɩ sarɩya ama ta vaa ba vɩɩrɩ tɩ aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma, ta nagɩ tɩ a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ. Lele ba yile dɩ ba nan bɩagɩ a lɔbɩrɩ vʋarɩ tɩ a taaŋ? Aayɩ, ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ, sie ba keŋ gie ba gbaŋ gbaŋ a vʋarɩ tɩ.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Die susuŋŋiribe dɩ gana a balɩ nyɩŋkʋraha wudieke Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi dɩ bala wa, die ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Die wɩa die ba ga Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi jigiŋ a ga nɩgɩ nuupɔɔŋ. Die ba dɩɩ ba nɩŋŋa a yaa ba a keŋ nyɩŋ dansarɩka ka sʋŋ ta die balɩ ba dɩ ba jʋʋsa ba ba nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Ta Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Silasi die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Lidia tigiŋ me. Mi die ba ye Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta kpaaŋ ba ta wa nyɩŋ tɩka ma.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Die tɩ balɩ a taaŋ ba ta nagɩ haarɩŋ a ga Kosi. Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die tɩ ga tʋgɩ Rodesi, ta bɩ nyɩŋ mi a nagɩ haarɩkʋ a ga Patara.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Mi die tɩ ye haarɩŋ dɩ ganana Fonisia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, die tɩ juuke a ga.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Saŋŋa dieke tɩ kenne yese Saapurusi tɩ daagɩ tɩ nuugalɩ chaaŋ a ga Taya, Siiria tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Mi die ba baa ba vʋarɩ chiititi a nyɩŋ haarɩkʋ ma.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Mi die tɩ ye Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ ta die bie ba jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ayʋpɔyɩ. Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vaa ba balɩ Pɔɔli dɩ ʋ da keŋ ga Jerusalemi.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Ama daraa ayʋpɔyɩ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die tɩ bɩ piili chɔnɩŋ bɩbra. Die dembisisi mana aŋaŋ ba hɔgʋba aŋaŋ ba ballɩ die dɩ beeli tɩ a nyɩŋ tɩka ma a ga tʋgɩ mʋgɩrɩ nʋaŋ, mi die tɩ mana die dɩ sʋʋŋ a gbirigi a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Die tɩ wana a balɩ taaŋ tamba wa die tɩ juu haarɩkʋ ta ba yiŋŋi kuli tigiŋ.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Die tɩ nagɩ haarɩkʋ a nyɩŋ Taya a ga Tolemayisi. Mi die tɩ ga waasɩ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ta wɩarɩ ba jigiŋ dabalɩmɩŋ.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die tɩ ga tʋgɩ Siisiria ta die ga juu Filipi vuodieke die dɩ mʋʋlɩnana Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha tigiŋ ta die bie mi. Die ʋ yiwo dembisi bayʋpɔyɩ diekemba die ba vʋarɩna Jerusalemi ma dɩ ba yi susuŋŋiribe wʋnyɩ.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Die ʋ yaa wa hɔgʋlɩaŋ banɩɩsa vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka yallɩya, ta ba gbaŋ die dɩ bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Die tɩ bie mi daraa pam ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Agabusi die dɩ nyɩŋ Judia ma a keŋ mi.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Die ʋ keŋ tɩ jigiŋ a nagɩ Pɔɔli gbanɩŋ a bɔbɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nuusi aŋaŋ ʋ nagɩsɩ ta baarɩ dɩ, Naa Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ balɩ dɩ Juu vuosi nan bɔbɩ gbaŋkʋ gie tieŋ sɩba naa Jerusalemi ma, ta nagɩ wa yi buuri gaasɩsɩ nuusi me.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Die tɩ wʋnna naa, tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ banɩŋ ba mana die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Pɔɔli dɩ ʋ da keŋ a ga Jerusalemi ma.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ama die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ tɩ dɩ, Bɩa nɩ kʋma ta chʋʋsa n sʋŋ? N ka yiwo siri dɩ ba bɔbɩ mɩŋ Jerusalemi ma nyɩɩna ma, ama n yiwo siri dɩ n kpi mi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩa.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: Die tɩ kana ka bɩagɩ a balɩ a tarɩgɩ ʋ sʋŋ wɩa, die tɩ baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ vaa fʋ dʋŋŋʋ yi.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Die tɩ yine daraa aŋmɩna mi kʋaŋ chaaŋ die tɩ gɔmɩsɩ tɩ nyinti a nyɩŋ ga Jerusalemi.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Ta Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ die nyɩna Siisari wa gbaŋ die dɩ tɩ a ga mi, ta yaa tɩ ga Minasoni tigiŋ me, mi die tɩ baa tɩ bemme. Manasoni die nyɩŋ wa Saapurusi ta die yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩkʋraha wʋnyɩ.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Die tɩ gana a ga tʋgɩ Jerusalemi wa, Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tuo tɩ chaantɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Ka tuŋ vʋʋsa Pɔɔli die dɩ nagɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ ga waasɩ Jemisi. Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋraha mana die bie mi.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ waasɩ ba mana ta balɩ ba wudiekemba mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi, wʋnɩŋ Pɔɔli tʋʋma wɩa.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Die ba wʋnna naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba mana dɩ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die balɩ Pɔɔli dɩ, Tɩ nɩmbʋa Pɔɔli, sɩba a baarɩ Juu vuosi tuse tuse bɩrɩna Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta ba mana dɩ bɩ dɩ Mosisi mɩraha keŋ keŋ keŋ.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ama ba balɩ ba mɩŋ fʋ wɩa dɩ daga Juu vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne buurigaasɩ tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa dɩ ba vaa Mosisi mɩraha taaŋ, ta bɩ balɩ dɩ ba da gobi ba dembibisi kɔla ta da bɩ dɩa Juu vuosi chʋaraha.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Bɩa tɩ baaŋ yi lele ba wʋnna dɩ keŋye naa?.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Die wɩa yi wudieke tɩ bala fʋ. Dembisi banɩɩsa benne giena ta kaa kaatɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Die wɩa gamma aŋaŋ ba a ga wʋnsɩ nɩ gbaŋ ta tuŋ ba wʋnsɩkʋ ligirehe ta nɩ wa wʋŋ nɩ sikpigile. Fʋ yi naa vuoŋ mana nan dɩ sɩba dɩ wudieke ba bala yaa gamma fʋ ma wa ka yi wusie, ba nan dɩ sɩba a baarɩ fʋ gbaŋ dɩ Mosisi mɩraha mɩŋ.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Ama buurigaasɩsɩ die dɩ bɩrɩna Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nɩŋ tɩ wɔŋ maagɩ wa gbanɩŋ a yɩ ba dɩ ba da keŋ a dii nyindiike dieke ba kaabɩna a yɩ bugile, yaa a ŋɔbɩma zɩŋ yaa dɔŋ dieke ba ŋmʋna a kʋʋ, ta bɩ vʋarɩ ba gbaŋ a nyɩŋ daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma ma.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Naa chɩaŋ ma ka tʋnvʋʋsa Pɔɔli die dɩ nagɩ dembisi wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ banɩŋ mana die dɩ wʋnsɩ ba gbaŋ ta juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a dagɩ daa dieke ba bala ba wʋnsɩ ba gbaŋ a kpatɩ wa aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke ba bala ba kaabɩ a yi ba wʋnyɩ mana.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Die daraa ayʋpɔyɩ daraaŋ dɩ gbinene die Juu vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩna Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma die dɩ ye Pɔɔli Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ ta die wa kpɩkpaaŋ a keŋ lagɩŋ ta piili a tuole wo.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Ta die nata dɩ, Juu vuosi! Nɩ suŋŋi tɩ. Daa wa gie dɩna dɩ dɩa a bala wʋbɩatɩ yaa gamma tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie aŋaŋ Mosisi mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie. Ta lele a bɩ nagɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi a keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ gie a yi ke dɩgɩntɩ.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Die ba balɩ wa naa dama, die ba ye wo Tirofimusi vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Efesusi aŋaŋ Pɔɔli tɩka sʋŋ ta yile dɩ Pɔɔli nagɩ wa mɩŋ a ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Die tɩka mana die dɩ dɔŋ ta vuosi mana dɩ chɩgɩ a keŋ lagɩsɩ taŋ a yigi Pɔɔli a datɩ wa a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ. Die lele wo mi die ba ligi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sanʋaha mana.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Die ba wa mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ Pɔɔli. Womi Romani sojasisi jakʋʋŋ die dɩ wʋŋ dɩ waagɩŋ bala ka nan Jerusalemi ma.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Die ʋ yi lagɩ lagɩ a nagɩ sojasi aŋaŋ nyɩŋkʋra bataŋ ba chɩgɩ ga jigidieke kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ lagɩsɩna wa. Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ dene a ye jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ sojasisi die ba vaa Pɔɔli nɩgɩŋ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ ga a ga yigi Pɔɔli ta vaa ba bɔbɩ wa chɔrɩsɩ ale ta pɩasɩ ba dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine daa wa gie? Ta bɩa ʋ yi.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ nata tɔra tɔra. Die ba watɩ wɩa die sojasi jakʋʋrɩ dɩ ka bɩagɩ sɩba wudieke dɩ yine, die wɩa die ʋ yɩ ʋ sojasi nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaa Pɔɔli a jʋalɩ ga ba sojasisi jigiberisikiri.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Die ba kenne tʋgɩ ka sanʋarɩ kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ wa faasɩ tʋagɩ die wɩa sojasisi die dɩ nagɩ Pɔɔli a chii.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ dɩɩ ba kʋaŋ a nata dɩ, Nɩ kʋʋ wa! Nɩ kʋʋ wa.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Die sojasisi die dɩ bala ba yaa wa juu wo, ʋ pɩasɩ jakʋʋrɩ dɩ, N nan bɩagɩ a balɩ wɩɩŋ a yɩ fʋ? Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Lalɩa fʋ yi a bɩagɩ bala Giriiki jabalɩkɩŋ?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Daa fʋ yine Ijipiti vuoke die dɩ vana waagɩŋ dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋka mi saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa ta die dɩ vuokʋʋla tuse anɩɩsa nɩŋŋa a yaa ba juu hagɩyeŋ.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yiwo Juu vuoŋ, ta ba mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ Taasusi ma, tɩgɩkpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ benne Silisia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. N jʋʋsa fʋ, yi mɩŋ sieŋ aŋ n balɩ wɩa a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ yi wa sieŋ, die Pɔɔli dɩ hagɩ zie a nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a diiŋ kpɩkpaakʋ ta balɩ ba dɩ ba tarɩ watɩ. Die ba tarɩna watɩtɩ Pɔɔli die dɩ nagɩ Juu vuosi jabalɩkɩŋ a bala wɩa a yɩa ba:.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: N chʋamba aŋaŋ n zʋalɩŋ nɩ wʋmma wudieke n bala n balɩ a yɩ nɩ wa.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Die ba wʋnna ʋ bala Juu vuosi jabalɩkɩŋ a yɩa ba wa, die ba tarɩ sɔmm. Womi Pɔɔli dɩ baarɩ dɩ.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: N yiwo Juu vuoŋ, ta ba mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ Tasusi tɩŋ dieke dɩ benne Silisia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ama ta ba wubi mɩŋ Jerusalemi ka gie mi. N dɩdagɩrʋ die yiwo Gamaliali ta die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ a dagɩ mɩŋ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba mɩraha. Ta die maŋ paalɩ dɩa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna sɩba nɩ mana dɩ dɩna die wo jinne.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Ta die mugise vuodieke mana die dɩ dɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sieku ta kʋʋ bataŋ gbaŋ. Ta die yigi ba dembisi bataŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋba bataŋ a ligi be dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha nan dii siara dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie. Die ba ŋaaŋ yɩ mɩŋ gbaŋtɩ gbaŋ dɩ n yaa ga Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne Damasikusi ma wa jigiŋ, amʋ n nan bɩagɩ yigi vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sieku mi a yaa ba a yiŋŋi keŋ Jerusalemi aŋ ba datɩ ba tɩbɩŋ.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Die daaŋ kaanɩ yaa gamma agbaa saŋŋa die maŋ ga gbige Damasikusi, womi die chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ nyɩgɩsɩ bʋnyɩ a chaaŋ giliŋ mɩŋ.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Die maŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ ta die wʋŋ lɔlɩŋ dɩ balala a yɩa mɩŋ dɩ, Sɔɔli, Sɔɔli bɩa wɩa fʋ mugise mɩŋ.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Die maŋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Mɩnɩa yine fʋ n Yɔmʋtieŋ? Ta die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yiwo Yisa, Nazeriti vuoke, manɩŋ n yine vuodieke fʋ mugisinene wo.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ beelinene mɩŋ die ye chaaŋkʋ ama die ba ka wʋŋ lɔlɩkʋ.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Die maŋ pɩasɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, bɩa fʋ yaala n yi? Womi die ʋ balɩ a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Hagɩ a ga Damasikusi, dɩɩ yi fʋ keŋ tʋgɩ mi ba nan dagɩ fʋ fʋ bala fʋ yi wudieke.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Die chaaŋkʋ die faasɩ nyɩgɩsɩ mɩŋ die maŋ yɩɩŋ. Die wɩa manɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chʋnnana wa die dɩ datɩ n nuuŋ a yaa mɩŋ a ga Damasikusi ma.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Daa wʋnyɩ die benne mi ba wasa wa Ananiasi. Die ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie ta dɩ Mosisi mɩraha ta Juu vuosisi mana die dɩ benne Damasikusi ma die faasɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Die ʋ keŋ a zie n jigiŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, N zʋa Sɔɔli, yesime bɩbra. Lele womi die maŋ bɩagɩ yese.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Womi Ananiasi die dɩ balɩ a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ dieke tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba dɩ jɩanna wa vʋarɩna fʋ dɩ fʋ sɩmma wudieke ʋ yaalala dɩ fʋ yi. Ʋ vʋarɩ fʋ mɩŋ dɩ fʋ ye Masia wa ta bɩ wʋŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ lɔlɩŋ.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Dɩ mʋ fʋ balɩ vuoŋ mana wudieke fʋ yene ta wʋŋ wa.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Ta lele bɩa fʋ bɩ chɩɩsa? Hagɩ aŋ n sɩɩ fʋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, aŋ Ŋmɩŋ vaa fʋ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa fʋ fʋ jʋʋsɩna wa wɩa.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Ta die maŋ yiŋŋi ga Jerusalemi a ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ dɩ n jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Mi die maŋ daansɩ.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: a ye tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala a yɩa mɩŋ dɩ, Yi lagɩ lagɩ a nyɩŋ Jerusalemi ma, dama dɩɩ yi fʋ bala ba n wɩaha ba kaaŋ tuo dii.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Die maŋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ ba wɔŋ sɩba dɩ die n ŋaaŋ gara Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a yige ta nɩga vuodiekemba dɩ tuone fʋ a dii wo.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Ta saŋŋa dieke die ba kʋna fʋ daansɩatieŋ Sitiiveni wa die n gbaŋ bie mi ta saagɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ wa, ta die zie a gbarɩ kʋkʋʋrɩba nyinyeeke.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Ama die tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ balɩ a yɩ mɩŋ dɩ n gamma, ta yɩ mɩŋ nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yaa mɩŋ a ga saasaa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi jigiŋ.

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Die kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ wʋmma Pɔɔli a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ bala naa a kpatɩ wa. Womi die ba piili a nata dɩ, Nɩ kʋʋ daa wa gie ʋ ka mʋ dɩ ʋ bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Die ba keese ta naga ba garɩtɩ viise ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta yɔɔra tantɩ a taana ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Die Romani sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba nagɩ Pɔɔli a juu juoku me ta tɩbɩ wa aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ. Amʋ ʋ nan balɩ wudieke ʋ yine wo ta Juu vuosi gie dɩ nata aŋaŋ wa.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Die ba bɔbɩ wa a dʋaŋ dɩ ba tɩbɩ wa wa die ʋ pɩasɩ sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ dɩ zene mi dɩ, Mɩra yɩ sieŋ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ tɩbɩ vuodieke Romani naaŋ wa dɩ yɩna wa sieŋ ta ye ka dii ʋ sarɩya.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ dɩ wʋnna naa wa die ʋ ga ba nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Bɩa fʋ yie? Daa wa gie yiwo vuodieke ta Romani naaŋ wa dɩ yɩna wa sieŋ.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Sojasisi nyɩŋkʋra nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa die dɩ ga Pɔɔli jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Balɩ mɩŋ, yiwo vuodieke ta Romani naaŋ wa dɩ yɩna sieŋ? Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Wa.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Sojasisi nyɩŋkʋra nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n tʋŋ wo ligire pam ta wa bɩrɩŋ vuodieke Romani naaŋ dɩ yɩna sieŋ. Ama Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ nɩnɩŋ ba wɔŋ mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ kanɩŋ sieku.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Die dembiŋ diekemba dɩ mɩnana dɩ ba tɩbɩ wa ta pɩasɩ wa wʋpɩasɩka ha die dɩ yiŋŋi kʋaŋ lagɩ lagɩ. Ta die ŋmaamɩŋ die yigi sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa gbaŋ die ʋ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ʋ vaa ba bɔbɩ wa vuodieke Romani naaŋ wa dɩ yɩna wa sieŋ aŋaŋ chɔrɩmaha wɩa.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa die sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa dɩ yaala ʋ mɩŋŋɩ wudieke wɩa Juu vuosisi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ bala dɩ Pɔɔli yi chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ vaa ba vʋarɩ chɔrɩmaha Pɔɔli ma ta die yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha lagɩŋ. Womi die ʋ yaa Pɔɔli a keŋ juu ta vaa ʋ zie ba nɩŋŋa.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Ka daraa anʋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ Aniasi aŋaŋ ba nyɩŋkʋraha bataŋ aŋaŋ lɔya wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Tetulusi die dɩ ga Siisiria ta ga vuokpɩɩŋ Felikisi jigiŋ dɩ ba balɩ wudieke Pɔɔli dɩ yine a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Die wɩa die ba wa Pɔɔli a keŋ juu ta Tetulusi die dɩ wa bala Pɔɔli dɩ yine wudieke wo dɩ: Jakʋʋŋ Felikisi, fʋ wɩa yine tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie dɩ fɩalɩ ta fʋ baga dɩ benne tɩ ma wɩa fʋ wʋnsa tɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Tɩ mana nine suuliye aŋaŋ wudieke fʋ yinene wo ta tɩ waasa fʋ.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: N ka yaala n chʋʋsɩ fʋ saŋŋa ama ko dii suguru aŋaŋ tɩ ta fʋ wʋŋ tɩ wɩaha gie bɩta.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Tɩ ye ta daa wa gie yiwo vuobɩaŋ ta yi jigiŋmabɩrʋ ta vasa Juu vuosisi dɩ waga tɩŋgbaŋka gie mana. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ bɩ yiwo Nazarenesi jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ gbaŋ.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Die ʋ mɩa dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, die wɩa tɩ yigi wo. Die tɩ tɩŋ yaala tɩ dii ʋ sarɩya aŋaŋ tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna die wo.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Lisiasi die dɩ yi nʋaŋ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ balala dɩ ʋ yiwo bɩabʋ keŋ fʋ nɩŋŋa. Dɩɩ yi fʋ pɩasɩ daa wa gie fʋ gbaŋ nan dɩ sɩba wudieke tɩ balala dɩ ʋ yiwo mana yiwo wusie.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Die Juu vuosisi die dɩ lagɩsɩna mi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ wɩaha mana yiwo wusie.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Mi die naaŋ wa die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a diiŋ Pɔɔli dɩ ʋ balɩ wɩa. Die ʋ piili dɩ: N sɩba a baarɩ fʋ yine tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi sarɩya diiru bɩna pam, die wɩa n yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ n balɩ vʋarɩ n gbaŋ fʋ naalɩakʋ gie me.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ yaala, nan bɩagɩ a pɩasɩ a sɩmma dɩ ka ka tɩaŋ daraa baŋ aŋaŋ ale die maŋ jʋalɩ ga Jerusalemi dɩ n ga jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Die Juu vuosisi die ka ye mɩŋ ta n nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ yaa a lagɩsa vuosi a vasa ba sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ haga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me yaa tɩka sʋŋ.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ a dagɩ dɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ dieke ba chɩbɩna a yɩ mɩŋ wa yiwo wusie.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Manɩŋ n bala nɩ dɩ sie dieke n dɩna ta Juu vuosisi dɩ bala dɩ die sieku ka vɩɩna wa ama manɩŋ n ye ko jɩama tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ Ŋmɩnnɩ. Wudieke mana dɩ maagɩna Mosisi mɩraha gbaŋtɩ sʋŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba gbaŋtɩ sʋŋ wa, n yi a mana yada mɩŋ.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: N yaa tama sɩba vuosi gie, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan daansɩ vaa vuovɩɩna yaa vuobɩatɩ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Die wɩa n mɩa mɩŋ dɩ n yi wudieke dɩ mʋna Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ vuota nɩŋŋa ta ka chɩɩlɩya n sʋŋ ma.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: Die n nyɩna Jerusalemi a yʋasɩ wa wɩa, maŋ yiŋŋi ga aŋaŋ piini dɩ n yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ mi ta bɩ yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Die n wʋnsɩna n gbaŋ a kpatɩ wa ta die bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋkʋ ta die yie kaabaha die ba juu ye mɩŋ. Saŋka mi ta vuosi ye ka dala mi ta watɩ die dɩ wori.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Ama Juu vuosi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩna Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma gbaŋ die bie mi saŋka mi: ta dɩɩ yi die ba ye n chʋʋsɩŋ ka tɩŋ mʋ ba keŋ gie a keŋ balɩ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Yaa vaa vuodiekemba dɩ benne gie dagɩ wudieke a yine a chʋʋsɩ saŋŋa dieke n zene ba nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋŋa wa.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Die ba ka ye wudieke n chʋʋsɩna ntaala wʋbalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma maŋ balɩ ba sʋŋ dɩ, Nɩ die mɩŋ sarɩya jinne dama n tuone a dii dɩ kunti nan daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me wo. .

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Felikisi die sɩba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sieti wɩa ta die vaa wɩɩrɩ ta baarɩ dɩ, N nan balɩ wudieke n yalla dɩ yi sojasisi jakʋʋŋ Lisiasi dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Ta die yi soja dieke dɩ gbarɩna Pɔɔli nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ gbarɩ wa, ama ta vaa Pɔɔli bemme ʋ dʋŋŋʋ ta vaa ʋ zʋalɩŋ yɩma wa jadieke ʋ yaalala.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Daraa a ŋmɩna kʋaŋ chaaŋ, jakʋʋŋ Felikisi dɩ keŋ aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ Dirusila, vuodieke dɩ yine Juu vuoŋ ta die vaa ba wa Pɔɔli ʋ keŋ, ta Pɔɔli die dɩ bala yaa gamma vuoŋ dɩ baaŋ nan yi Yisa Masia yada dene. Ta wʋnɩŋ Felikisi die dɩ wʋmma.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Ama Pɔɔli die dɩ bɩ bala yaa gamma vɩɩnɩŋ yiile aŋaŋ yigi n gbaŋ aŋaŋ dʋnɩa kpatɩ sarɩya diile wɩaha, ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi jakʋʋŋ Felikisi ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Lele nan bɩagɩ ga. N nan bɩ wa fʋ maŋ keŋ ye sieŋ.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Ama Felikisi saŋka mi die yile dɩ Pɔɔli nan kpalɩ wa aŋaŋ ligire, die wɩa ʋ ŋaŋ wa wa mɩŋ, ʋ aŋaŋ wa dɩ bala wɩa.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Ka bɩna ale kʋaŋ chaaŋ Posiwosi Fesitusi die dɩ tuo vuokpɩɩŋ Filikisi naŋ ma a zie. Ama vuokpɩɩŋ Filikisi die dɩ balla ʋ vaa tʋʋmaha, die ʋ yaala Juu vuosi yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ wa, die wɩa die ʋ vaa Pɔɔli dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ daansɩ nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ lagɩsɩna wa nine mi die ta die keŋ a baarɩ dɩ, N nɩmballɩ, n ka chɩɩlɩya n sʋŋ ma yaa gamma n beriŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa a yaa keŋ tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ Ananiasi die dɩ yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ zene Pɔɔli lʋgɩrɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba nɩgɩ ʋ nʋaŋ ma.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ gaamɩŋ dieke ba pentine jayɩalɩŋ a zieŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan nɩgɩ fʋnɩŋ. Kalɩ wa giena a die mɩŋ sarɩya aŋaŋ Mosisi mɩraha ta bɩ balɩ dɩ ba nɩgɩ mɩŋ ta chʋʋsɩ mɩraha dama tɩ mɩraha ka yi sieŋ dɩ ba nɩgɩ vuoŋ.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Dembiŋ diekemba dɩ zene gbigi Pɔɔli wa die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, Fʋ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ zɩa wa Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, N nɩmballɩ, n ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ. Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ balɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ da keŋ a balɩ wʋbɩatɩ a yɩ tɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Die Pɔɔli dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ lagɩŋkʋ nyɩŋkʋraha bataŋ die yiwo Sadusisi ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yi Farasisi die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, N nɩmballɩ n yiwo Farasi vuoŋ ta bɩ yi Farasisi vuoŋ bʋa. Ba die mɩŋ sarɩya giena dama n tuo dii mɩŋ dɩ kunti nan daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Die ʋ bala naa kpatɩ wa, nɩnhagɩrɩŋ die dɩ nan Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi sʋnsʋŋ ta die ba puo bule.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Die wɩa die ba wa faasɩ nata ta mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ yine Farasisi vuosisi die dɩ hagɩ zie ta baarɩ dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ tɩ ka ye wudieke daa wa gie dɩ yine chʋʋsɩ, dɩ nan bɩagɩ yi haalɩŋ yaa malaka bala wɩaha a yɩa wa.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Die nɩnhagɩrɩbʋ die dɩ wa faasɩ ga tʋa ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wa dɩ ba nan keŋ yi Pɔɔli daŋŋa. Die wɩa die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ sojasisi juu kpɩkpaakʋ sʋŋ a gbatɩ Pɔɔli a taaŋ a nagɩ wa a yiŋŋi ga ba jigiberisikiri.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Ka yuŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ keŋ zie Pɔɔli jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ, sɩba die fʋ bala n wɩa die Jerusalemi ma wa die gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nan balɩ n wɩa Romi ma.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa sʋkʋʋŋ Juu vuosi bataŋ die lagɩsɩ a saŋ. Die ba hʋʋ dɩ ba kaaŋ dii jadiikiŋ yaa a nyu nyaaŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ba baaŋ nan kʋʋ Pɔɔli.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Die vuodiekemba die dɩ sanna wa die tɩaŋ vuosi baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Die wɩa die ba ga Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ lagɩŋ hʋʋ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ kaaŋ dii jaaŋ mana a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke tɩ baaŋ nan kʋʋ Pɔɔli.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Die wɩa nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ lagɩŋkʋ nyɩŋkʋraha ga Romani sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ jigiŋ a paalɩ balɩ sɩba nɩ yaala nɩ paalɩ ye wudieke ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ. Ta balɩ ba dɩ ba yaa wa keŋ nɩ sarɩya diile jigiŋ. Ama tɩ nan yi siri dɩ tɩ kʋʋ wa ta ʋ wa keŋ juu giena.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Ama die Pɔɔli taa bʋa die dɩ wʋŋ ba saŋkʋ, die wɩa die ʋ ga sojasisi jigiberisikiri a balɩ Pɔɔli.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ wʋnna naa wa die ʋ wa sojasisi nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ a balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, Yaa dalʋakɩrɩ gie a ga jakʋʋrɩ jigiŋ, ta ʋ yaa wa wɩɩŋ a baa ʋ balɩ wa.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Ta nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ wa die dɩ ʋ nɩŋŋa a yaa wa a ga jakʋʋrɩ jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, Pɔɔli vuodieke dɩ benne dansarɩka ma wa wana mɩŋ, a balɩ mɩŋ dɩ n yaa dalʋakɩrɩ gie a keŋ fʋ jigiŋ dama ʋ yaa wɩɩŋ a baa ʋ balɩ fʋ.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ yigi ʋ nuuŋ me a yaa wa nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, Bɩa wɩɩŋ fʋ yaala fʋ balɩ mɩŋ.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Dembisi bataŋ sannana dɩ ba keŋ balɩ fʋ dɩ soriŋ fʋ yaa Pɔɔli a keŋ sʋʋŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋra lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ. Ba nan paalɩ yi sɩba ba yaala ba sɩmma wudieke ʋ paalɩ a yi chʋʋsɩ.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Ama da keŋ a tɔgɩŋ ba dama dembisi tɩaŋ baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa lɔbɩrɩna a gbarɩ wa. Dama ba hʋʋya mɩŋ dɩ ba kaaŋ dii jaaŋ yaa a nyuu nyaaŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ba baaŋ nan kʋʋ wa. Ta lele ba wa yiwo siri dɩ ba yi wudiekemba ba sanna wa ta chɩɩsa wudieke fʋ baaŋ nan balɩ.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ mana dɩ balɩ mɩŋ wɩɩrɩ gie. Ta die taaŋ wa ʋ ga.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Womi die jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ wa ʋ nyɩŋkʋra bale a balɩ ba dɩ, Nɩ vʋarɩ sojasi kɔbɩsɩ-le aŋaŋ duunjʋalɩtɩŋ baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ tɩŋtaarɩŋ kɔbɩsɩ-le aŋ ba ga Siisiria yuku gie yaa gamma bimbilimiŋ awayɩ saŋŋa.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Ta nɩ yaalɩ duune a yɩ Pɔɔli aŋ ʋ jʋalɩ ta nɩ mɩŋŋɩ a daansɩma wa a yaa ga tʋgɩ vuokpɩɩŋ Felikisi jigiŋ.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ maagɩ gbanɩŋ dɩ:.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Manɩŋ Kolodiasi Lisiasi waasa fʋnɩŋ vuokpɩɩŋ Felikisi.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Juu vuosi bataŋ die yigine daa wa gie a mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ wa. Ama die n mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ʋ yiwo vuodieke Romani naaŋ wa dɩ yɩna sieŋ, die wɩa maŋ aŋaŋ n sojasi bataŋ dɩ ga gbatɩ wa taaŋ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: Die n tɩŋ yaala n sɩmma wudieke ba bala dɩ ʋ yi chʋʋsɩ wa, die wɩa die maŋ yaa wa a ga ba nyɩŋkʋra lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Die maŋ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ʋ ka yi wudieke dɩ mʋna dɩ ba kʋʋ wa yaa ba nagɩ wa yi dansarɩka sʋŋ; die ba balala wudieke a galɩma wa die yiwo ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba mɩraha wɩa.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Die maŋ wʋŋ dɩ Juu vuosi bataŋ sama mɩŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ wa, maŋ tʋŋ dɩ ba yaa wa keŋ fʋ jigiŋ. Ta balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ galɩnnana wa dɩ ba yaa wɩɩrɩ a keŋ fʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Sojasisi die dɩ yi wudieke jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ bala wa, ta die nagɩ Pɔɔli yuku mi a yaa ga Antipatirisi tɩŋ ma.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die sojasisi die dɩ chʋnna nagɩsɩsɩ die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ ba jigiberisikiri ta vaa duunjʋalɩtɩba dɩ ba gamma aŋaŋ Pɔɔli.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Die ba nagɩ wa a ga Siisiria ta die nagɩ gbaŋkʋ a yɩ vuokpɩɩkʋ Felikisi ta nagɩ Pɔɔli yi ʋ nuusi me.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Vuokpɩɩkʋ die dɩ karɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ta die pɩasɩ Pɔɔli tɩgɩdieke ʋ nyɩna. Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ Pɔɔli nyɩŋ wa Silisia tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: ʋ baarɩ dɩ, N nan wʋŋ fʋ wɩaha dɩɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ galɩnnana fʋ wa dɩ keŋ. Die ʋ balɩna kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaa Pɔɔli a ga yi naaŋ Herodi tigiŋ me a gbarɩ wa sɩba dansarɩka vuoŋ.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Jakʋʋŋ Fesitusi die dɩ kenne Siisiria, ka daraa ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ nyɩŋ mi ta jʋalɩ ga Jerusalemi.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Mi die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha die dɩ keŋ Fesitusi jigiŋ a chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩ Pɔɔli ta die jʋʋsɩ Fesitusi dɩ.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: ʋ vaa Pɔɔli keŋ Jerusalemi ma. Die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ yi naa dama die ba wɔŋ saŋ mɩŋ dɩ ba lɔbɩrɩ a kʋʋ Pɔɔli sieŋ me mɩŋ.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Fesitusi die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Pɔɔli bemme dansarɩka sʋŋ Siisiria, ta n gbaŋ n nan yiŋŋi a ga mi daraa ale ka gie nʋaŋ.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa nɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ ga aŋaŋ mɩŋ Siisiria a ga balɩ wudieke ʋ yine, dɩɩ yi ʋ yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Fesitusi die dɩ bɩ dii daraa anɩɩ yaa daraa baŋ ba jigiŋ ta die nyɩŋ ga Siisiria. Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die ʋ juu kalɩ ʋ sarɩya diile gbaŋtɩ ma ta yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaa Pɔɔli keŋ juu.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Die Pɔɔli dɩ kenne juu wo die Juu vuodiekemba dɩ nyɩna Jerusalemi a keŋ wo dɩ zie a giliŋ wo, a piili bala wʋbɩatɩ a chʋʋsa wa, ama die ba ka bɩagɩ dagɩ dɩ ba wɩɩrɩ yiwo wusie.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Die wɩa Pɔɔli die dɩ bala dɩ ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ, Manɩŋ n ka yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ Juu vuosi mɩraha yaa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ jaaŋ yaa Romani vuosi naakpɩɩka.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Ama Fesitusi die dɩ yaala ʋ yi aŋ Juu vuosisi sʋŋ fɩalɩ, die wɩa die ʋ pɩasɩ Pɔɔli dɩ, Nan dɩ yaala fʋ ga Jerusalemi aŋ n ga dii fʋ sarɩya aŋaŋ wɩaha gie mi.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Aayɩ, n yaala Romani naakpɩɩŋ gbaŋ dii n sarɩya. N ka yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ Juu vuosisi sɩba fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ sɩbɩna dene.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ chʋʋsɩ wa ba mɩraha ta yi wudieke dɩ mʋna kuŋ n tɩŋ kaaŋ baarɩ nɩ da kʋʋ mɩŋ. Ama dɩɩ yi wudieke ba bala dɩ n yiye wo dɩ ka yi wusie, vuoŋ wori a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ mɩŋ a yi ba nuusi me. Ama lele n jʋʋsa dɩ nɩ vaa Romani naakpɩɩŋ Siiza gbaŋ gbaŋ dii n sarɩya.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Womi Fesitusi aŋaŋ lagɩŋkʋ nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ ye taŋ ta ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ jʋʋsɩya mɩŋ dɩ nan nagɩ fʋ wɩɩrɩ a poli ga naakpɩɩŋ Siiza jigiŋ, die wɩa ba yaa fʋ ga ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Die daraa ale kʋaŋ chaaŋ, naaŋ Agiripa aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ Beniisi dɩ keŋ Siisiria dɩ ba kaagɩ jakʋʋŋ Fesitusi.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Die ba yine daraa ŋmɩna mi, ta Fesitusi die dɩ keŋ balɩ Pɔɔli dɩ benne wudieke ma a yɩ naaŋ Agiripa dɩ, Felikisi vaa wa daa wʋnyɩ giena dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: ta die maŋ ga Jerusalemi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋra dɩ keŋ n jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ n dii ʋ sarɩya a baarɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Ama die maŋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ tɩnɩŋ Romanisi mɩra wo sieŋ dɩ ba nagɩ vuodieke vuosi dɩ balala dɩ ʋ yiwo wʋbɩaŋ a yi ba nuusi me, ta ba ye ka yaa wa ga sarɩya diile jigiŋ ta wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba tuoli taŋ dɩ ʋ balɩ a vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Die wɩa die ba kenne giena wa die n ka chʋʋsɩ saŋŋa, ka tʋnvʋʋsa die maŋ juu kalɩ n sarɩya gbaŋtɩ ma ta yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba yaa Pɔɔli a keŋ.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Ama vuodiekemba die dɩ balala a chʋʋsa wa wa dɩ hagɩna zie dɩ ba balɩ wudieke ʋ yine wo, die ba ka balɩ wʋbɩaŋ diekemba n yilinene dɩ ba nan balɩ wa.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Ama die ka yiwo nɩnhagɩrɩŋ ba nɩgɩna aŋaŋ wa yaa gamma ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba jɩamɩŋ aŋaŋ daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ kpine ta ʋ saaŋ dɩ dɩ Yisa wɩa, vuodieke Pɔɔli dɩ bala dɩ ʋ bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Die n zɩ n baaŋ nan dii sarɩyaka dene wɩa, die maŋ pɩasɩ Pɔɔli dɩ ʋ nan dɩ yaala ʋ ga Jerusalemi aŋ n dii wo sarɩya mi.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Ama die ʋ baarɩ dɩ ba vaa ʋ bemme dansarɩka ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke Romani naakpɩɩka dɩ baaŋ nan dii ʋ sarɩya ka. Die wɩa die maŋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba nagɩ wa yi dansarɩka sʋŋ giena a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan yaa wa a ga Romani naakpɩɩka jigiŋ.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Womi naaŋ Agiripa dɩ balɩ yɩ Fesitusi dɩ, N gbaŋ n yaala n wʋŋ daa wa wɩaha a nyɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nʋaŋ ma. Fesitusi die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, Nan wʋŋ wa soriŋ.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa naaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ Beniisi die dɩ bɔbɩ aŋaŋ nyinyeeki dʋnsa a keŋ juu lagɩŋkʋ juoku me. Die sojasisi nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ dɩɩ ba kʋaŋ a keŋ juu mi. Fesitusi die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ ba yaa Pɔɔli keŋ juu.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Fesitusi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Naaŋ Agiripa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne gie nɩ daansɩ daa wa gie. Juu vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Siisiria keŋ wo n jigiŋ ta nata dɩ ʋ mʋ kuŋ.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Ama manɩŋ n ka ye wudieke ʋ yine a chʋʋsɩ ta mʋ kuŋ. Ta ʋ gbaŋ dɩ bala dɩ tɩ yaa wa ga Romani naakpɩɩka jigiŋ aŋ ʋ dii wo ʋ sarɩya ka. Die wɩa n saŋya mɩŋ dɩ n yaa wa ga Romi.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Ama n paalɩ wo wudieke n baaŋ nan maagɩ yaa gamma daa wa gie wɩa a yaa ga yɩ Romanisi naakpɩɩka. Die wɩa maŋ yaa wa keŋ nɩ mana nɩŋŋa aŋaŋ fʋnɩŋ naaŋ Agiripa. Tɩ keŋ balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie kpatɩ n nan wa yaa wɩɩŋ aŋ n maagɩ.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Dama dɩ wo chɩaŋ aŋ n vaa dansarɩka vuoŋ ga naakpɩɩka jigiŋ ta kaaŋ bɩagɩ dagɩ wudieke ʋ yine chʋʋsɩ.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Womi Agiripa die dɩ balɩ yɩ Pɔɔli dɩ, N yɩ fʋ sieŋ dɩ fʋ balɩ a vʋarɩ fʋ gbaŋ. Pɔɔli die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a diiŋ ta baarɩ dɩ:.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Naaŋ Agiripa! N sʋŋ fɩalɩya mɩŋ fʋ yɩna mɩŋ sieku gie dɩ n balɩ a vʋarɩ n gbaŋ fʋ nɩŋŋa jinne, yaa gamma wudiekemba mana n gbaŋ gbaŋ n vuosi dɩ bala dɩ n yi chʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Fʋnɩŋ fʋ sɩbɩna tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi mɩraha aŋaŋ wudiekemba dɩ yine tɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ mana. Die wɩa n jʋʋsa fʋ, dii suguru ta fʋ wʋmma wudieke n bala n balɩ naa.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Die n yine bʋabiŋ a yaa ga mʋʋna Juu vuosi mana wɔŋ sɩba die n benne die n tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ Jerusalemi ma.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Ba wɔŋ sɩba a yʋasɩ. Dɩɩ yi ba yaala ba nan bɩagɩ dagɩ fʋ dɩ die n gʋtɩ wa Farasisi diekemba die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a dɩa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Ama lele ba die mɩŋ sarɩya dama n yaa tama aŋaŋ nʋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba kʋrɩŋ ma wa.
