eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Nʋarɩ gie wɩa ta tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi buuriŋ baŋ aŋaŋ ale die dɩ yaa tama ta yile dɩ tɩ nan daansɩ yeke. Die wɩa tɩ jɩama Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ mana. Jakʋʋŋ Agiripa, tamaka gie wɩa Juu vuosisi dɩ bala dɩ n chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Bɩa wɩa dɩ hagɩrɩ a yɩ nɩ dɩ nɩ tuo a dii dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vasa kunti dɩ haga kuŋ me?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Piiliku me die n gbaŋ n mɩa mɩŋ dɩ n yi wɩɩŋ mana a chʋʋsɩ Yisa vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Nazeriti wa saaŋ.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Naa die maŋ yi Jerusalemi ma ma. Die maŋ tuose yiko gbanɩŋ a nyɩna Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha jigiŋ ta die yige Ŋmɩŋ vuosi pam a yie dansarɩka sʋŋ. Ba keŋ dii ba sarɩya ta baarɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba n gbaŋ gbaŋ ŋaŋ gʋta wa vuodiekemba dɩ bala dɩ ba kʋʋ ba wa ma.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Saŋŋa mana n ŋaŋ mugisi be mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me dɩ ba chiisi dɩ ba ka dɩ Yisa. Die ba wɩa dɩ paala yaa mɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ wɩa maŋ gara tɩŋgbaŋ gaasɩ a yaala ba jigiŋ.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Naa wɩa, daaŋ kaanɩ die maŋ gara Damasikusi dɩ n mugisi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ yiko dieke Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha die yɩna mɩŋ ta bɩ yɩ mɩŋ nʋaŋ dɩ n yi wo.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Jakʋʋŋ Agiripa, die ka yiwo ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta maŋ bie sieŋ me a gara, ta die keŋ ye chaanɩŋ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a chaana a tɩaŋ ŋmɩŋ, ta nyɩgɩsa ta taaŋ giliŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ n siechɔntɩ chanchaalɩŋ.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Die tɩ mana die dɩ nan tɩŋgbaŋ, ta die maŋ wʋŋ lɔlɩŋ dɩ balɩ tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi jabalɩkɩŋ ma dɩ, Sɔɔli Sɔɔli, bɩa wɩa fʋ mugise mɩŋ? Dɩ yiwo gaantɩŋ fʋ wagɩnana aŋaŋ manɩŋ.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Die maŋ yiŋŋi pɩasɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, mɩnɩa yine fʋ? Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ Yisa fʋ mugise die.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Ama hagɩ a zie fʋ nagɩsɩ ma. N nyɩŋ fʋ jigiŋ dama fʋnɩŋ maŋ vʋarɩ dɩ fʋ yi n tʋntʋntʋ. Yime siara a balɩ vuosi wudieke fʋ yene yaa gamma n wɩa jinne aŋaŋ wudieke n baaŋ nan dagɩ fʋ soriŋ daaŋ.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: N nan daansɩ gbatɩ fʋ a nyɩŋ Juu vuosi nuusi me aŋaŋ buurigaasɩ dieke jigiŋ n bala n tʋŋ fʋ nuusi me.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: Fʋnɩŋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ yuori ba nine a vʋarɩ ba nyɩŋ lɩmɩŋ ma a ga chaanɩŋ ma, a bɩ vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ Sitaani nuusi me aŋ ba juu Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me, aŋ ʋ vaa ba wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba aŋ ba gʋtɩ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna wa ma, dama ba yine mɩŋ yada wa wɩa.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Die wɩa, naaŋ Agiripa, die n ka zetiye die n yene daansɩ dieke a nyɩŋ arɩzanna ma wa.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Die n woliŋ a mʋʋlɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ Damasikusi vuosi, ta die nyɩŋ ga Jerusalemi aŋaŋ Judia tɩgɩsɩ mana. Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ maŋ ga mʋʋlɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi. Wudieke n balla a yɩa vuosisi gie mana yiwo, ba vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ta dɩa Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ, ta tʋmma tʋnvɩɩna aŋ ka dagɩ sɩba ba seŋ vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Naa wɩa die Juu vuosisi die dɩ yigi mɩŋ, die n ŋaana bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ, ta die yaala ba kʋʋ mɩŋ wa.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋine mɩŋ dene a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne daarɩ gie maŋ zie dɩ n balɩ wusie a yɩ vuoŋ mana, wabɩtɩ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩtieliŋ. Wudieke n bala aŋaŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ Mosisi die dɩ balla a nan yiwo, yiwo jabalɩmɩŋ.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: N balɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa die dɩ bala ʋ gbalɩgɩ gbalɩgɩ dieke ta kpi, ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ yi bʋmbʋŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ woliŋ a hagɩ kuŋ me, die wɩa ʋ nan balɩ yɩ Juu vuosisi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ ba nan bɩagɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Naa nan yi sɩba chaanɩŋ a yɩ ba.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ balala dɩ ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taaŋ wa, Fesitusi die dɩ natɩ wa a baarɩ dɩ: Pɔɔli, yiwo yɩɩŋyaaŋ! Fʋ bʋgɩrɩna nyinti pam wa yine fʋ juu yɩɩŋyaatɩ die.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Ta Pɔɔli die yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Jakʋʋŋ Fesitusi, n ka juu yɩɩŋyaatɩ! Wudieke n balɩnana yiwo wusie ta bɩ yaa chɩaŋ.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Jakʋʋŋ Agiripa, n yaa sikimiŋ ta nan bɩagɩ balɩ a yɩ fʋ dama fʋ gbaŋ fʋ sɩbɩna ha wɩa, ta bɩ yi yada dɩ wɩaha gie yaa gamma Yisa wa ka lɔbɩrɩ fʋ jigiŋ, dama wudieke die yine wo die ka yi lɩmɩŋ ma.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Die Pɔɔli dɩ bɩ balɩ a yɩ naaŋ Agiripa dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ, yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha yada mɩŋ? N wɔŋ sɩba a baarɩ fʋ yi yada mɩŋ.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Ta naaŋ Agiripa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ Pɔɔli dɩ, Aŋaŋ fʋ wʋbalɩka bɩta ka gie fʋ yile dɩ nan bɩagɩ tarɩgɩ mɩŋ a yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Wʋbalɩkɩ waŋ yaa wʋbalɩkɩ gobiŋ, manɩŋ n Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ yiwo dɩ fʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi gie mana die dɩ wʋnna n wʋbalɩkaha jinne wo bɩrɩŋ Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ sɩba manɩŋ ntaala dansarɩka gie nyɩɩna ma n ka yaala nɩ juu.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Womi naaŋ Agiripa aŋaŋ jakʋʋŋ Fesitusi aŋaŋ Beniisi aŋaŋ vuodieke mana die dɩ benne ba jigiŋ die dɩ hagɩ nyɩŋ.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Ama die ba bala ba nyɩŋ wa die ba baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie ka yi wʋbɩaŋ kaanɩ gbaŋ. Ʋ ka mʋ ba kʋʋ wa yaa ba nagɩ wa a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Ta naaŋ Agiripa die dɩ balɩ yɩ jakʋʋŋ Fesitusi dɩ, Tɩ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ daa wa gie a taaŋ dɩɩ yi die ʋ tɩŋ ka poli ʋ wɩɩrɩ gie a ga naakpeŋkpɩɩŋ Siiza jigiŋ.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Die ba kenne saŋ dɩ ba nagɩ Pɔɔli a ga Romi, Itali tɩŋ ma, die ba nagɩ wa aŋaŋ dansarɩka vuosi bataŋ a yɩ Romani sojasisi jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ nuuŋ me ba wasɩnana wa Julusi, die ʋ benne sojasisi chaakʋ die ba wasɩnana Naalɩba jakʋʋŋ Chaakʋ.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Tɩ mana die dɩ ga juu haarɩ dieke die dɩ nyɩna Adiramitiwomi, dama die haarɩkʋ die wɔŋ yi siri dɩ ka nyɩŋ Siisiria a ga Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma tɩgɩ diekemba die dɩ gbɩgɩna mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ. Ta Masedonia vuoŋ, Arisitakusi, vuodieke dɩ nyɩna Tesalonika die gʋtɩ wa tɩ ma.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die tɩ tʋgɩ Sidoni. Sojasisi jakʋʋŋ, Julusi die mɩŋŋɩ a pɔgɩlɩ Pɔɔli ta die yɩ wo sieŋ dɩ ʋ ga ʋ zʋalɩŋ jigiŋ aŋ ba yi wa ʋ nyɩŋyaalɩka.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Die tɩ nyɩna Sidoni ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ nɩga a tuole tɩ, die wɩa die tɩ dɔŋ daagɩ Saapurusi tɩŋgbaŋ chaakʋ, ta tɩŋgbaŋka die dɩ kagɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ bʋlɔgɩsɩkʋ ma.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Die tɩ dɔŋ tɩaŋ ga Silisia aŋaŋ Pamifilia tɩŋgbaŋ chaakʋ ta ga tʋgɩ Mira tɩŋ, Lisia tɩŋgbaŋ mama, ta sʋʋŋ mi.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Mi die sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ ye haarɩ dieke dɩ nyɩna Alekizandira a tɩaŋ a gara Itali tɩŋgbaŋ, ʋ vaa tɩ juu kanɩŋ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Die tɩ dɔŋ sɔmm sɔmm daraa pam a keŋ paalɩ gbalɩgɩ ta ye keŋ tʋgɩ Kinidusi tɩŋ ma. Bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ faasɩ tuoli tɩ mi chaakʋ wɩa die tɩ ka bɩagɩ dɔŋ gara mi. Die wɩa die tɩ daagɩ ga Kireeti tɩŋgbaŋ lʋgɩŋ. Dama mi die bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ ka faasɩ hagɩrɩ, die tɩ keŋ gbigi Salimoni, Kireeti tɩŋgbaŋ ma, a ga.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Die tɩ dɔma sɔmm sɔmm mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ kʋanʋaŋ ta die wa keŋ tʋgɩ jigidieke ba wasɩnana Haarɩtɩ zielivɩɩnɩŋ jigiŋ mi die ka yʋa aŋaŋ Lisia tɩŋ.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Die tɩ yʋasɩna mi wɩa die saŋŋa dieke mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ haarɩŋ dɔmɩŋ dɩ faasɩna yaa daanpɔyɩ dɩ wone keŋ tʋgɩ. Saŋka dɩaŋ ta Juu vuosi dʋʋga daakpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke die ba kaabɩna ba wʋbɩatɩ naa chaala die tɩanya mɩŋ. Die wɩa Pɔɔli die dɩ kpaaŋ ba dɩ.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: N zʋalɩŋ, n sɩba a baarɩ tɩ sieku gie nan yi daanpɔyɩ, tɩ chiititi aŋaŋ tɩ haarɩkʋ nan chʋʋsɩ ta vuosi bataŋ nan waarɩ ba mɩsɩ gbaŋ.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Ama die sojasi jakʋʋrɩ baga die wo wudieke Pɔɔli die dɩ bala wa ma dama die ʋ wʋmma yie haarɩkʋ jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ ka tieŋ wo.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Die haarɩsɩ jigizielikiri die ka vɩɩna aŋaŋ ba zie mi tafaaŋ saŋŋa, die wɩa ba jusu die baarɩ dɩ ba hagɩ mi a gamma Fonisi. Mi die yiwo Kireeti tɩŋgbaŋ tɩŋ ta haarɩsɩ jigizielikiri die dɩ bie mi a tuoli ŋmɩŋ jʋʋkɩŋ chaaŋ, ta die ba yaala ba bemme mi tafaaŋ saŋŋa.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Saŋŋa mi die ba ye bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana nyɩna ba nuugalɩ chaaŋ a kieŋ sɔmm sɔmm, ta die ba yile dɩ ka yi wo bʋlɔgɩsɩvɩɩnɩŋ wudieke dɩ baaŋ nan suŋŋine be sɩba ba sanna die wo. Die wɩa die ba datɩ kʋʋdʋnsɩ dieke dɩ yigine haarɩkʋ dɩ zie wo a yi haarɩkʋ sʋŋ a dɔma gbige Kireeti mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Ama die dɩ ka yʋasɩya ta bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ nɩga nyɩna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ a keŋ sʋʋna mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Die bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ keŋ hʋbʋbʋ haarɩkʋ ma, ta die dɩ hagɩrɩ pam aŋaŋ vuoŋ dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ dɔŋ haarɩkʋ a tuolibu, die wɩa die tɩ vaa mɩsɩ ta vaa bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ dɩ yaa haarɩkʋ chʋŋ gara.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Die bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ yaa tɩ ga tɩaŋ tɩgɩbɩŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Kɔda wa jigiŋ. Die tɩ tɩannana die tɩgɩbɩka die dɩ kagɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ wa kpɩa. Die tɩ mɩɩ a bɩagɩ a yigi haarɩbike dieke dɩ taasɩna haarɩkpɩɩkʋ ma wa.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Die ba datɩ ka a juu haarɩkpɩɩkʋ ma a nagɩ guune a mɩŋŋɩ bɔbɩ giliŋ haarɩkpɩɩkʋ keŋ keŋ. Die ba chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ ba nan ga gbigi Libia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, jigidieke nyaabʋ dɩ kana ka goli wo. Die wɩa die ba datɩ haarɩkʋ garɩkʋ a sʋʋŋ ta vaa bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ dɩ bɩ yaa haarɩkʋ chʋŋ.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Die bʋlɔgɩsɩkpɩɩbʋ dɩ ye ko nɩga, die wɩa ka tʋnvʋʋsa die ba piili a vʋara haarɩkʋ chiititi a taana.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Die ka tʋnvʋʋsa bɩbra ba vʋarɩ haarɩkʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ nyintiti aŋaŋ ba nuusi a vigi taana nyaabʋ sʋŋ.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Die dɩ yi daraa pam ta tɩ ka yese ŋmɩnnɩ yaa chɩŋmarɩsɩsɩ, nɩɩŋmara die dɩ ligine ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ wɩa. Ta bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die ye ko faasɩ nɩga ta die tɩ bɩ wo tama dɩ tɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Die dɩ faasɩna a yʋasɩ ta vuosisi die ka dii jaaŋ, ta Pɔɔli dɩ hagɩ zie ba nɩŋŋa a baarɩ dɩ, N zʋalɩŋ, dɩ tɩŋ mʋ nɩ tuo n nʋarɩ ta da dɔŋ nyɩŋ Kireeti, tɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ juu daanpɔyɩ aŋaŋ wahala ka gie me.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Ama lele n wa jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ yigi sikimiŋ ta nɩ wʋnyɩ mana kaaŋ waarɩ ʋ mɩsɩ; ama haarɩkʋ nyɩɩna bala ka chʋʋsɩ.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Dama dienewo yuku Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ sɩna mɩŋ ta maŋ jɩama wa malaka die keŋ wo n jigiŋ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: a keŋ balɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Pɔɔli, da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ. Sie ba nagɩ fʋ a ga zie naalɩba jakʋʋŋ Siiza jigiŋ aŋ ʋ dii fʋ sarɩya; fʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ baa ʋ gbatɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ benne haarɩkʋ gie me a taaŋ.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Die wɩa n zʋalɩŋ, nɩ dii sikimiŋ, dama n yi Ŋmɩŋ yada mɩŋ dɩ wudieke ʋ bala mɩŋ wa nan seŋ yi.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Ama bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ nan kpaŋŋɩ tɩ a ga zieŋ tɩŋgbaŋ gaaŋ ta nyaaŋ dɩ giline kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Die ka yugiti baŋ-anɩɩsa die wonde ta bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die ye ko kpaŋŋa tɩ chʋŋ Miditerana mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ. Ama yaa gamma ka tanseese haarɩ dɔntɩba dɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ tɩ gbige gaaŋ.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Die wɩa die ba nagɩ jamagɩsɩŋ a magɩsɩ nyaabʋgoliŋ a die ye ta nyaabʋ goliŋ yiwo sɩba naŋ magɩsa kobɩga aŋaŋ baŋɩsɩ-le: die dɩ bɩ yi sʋaa die ba bɩ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ ta ye ta bʋ goli yiwo naŋ magɩsɩŋ baŋɩsɩ-wayɩ.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yigi be dama die ba yile dɩ ba haarɩkʋ nan keŋ jʋalɩ tana. Die wɩa die ba nagɩ kʋʋdʋnsa anɩɩsa dieke dɩ datɩnana haarɩkʋ dɩ ziele wo a yi nyaaŋ sʋŋ haarɩkʋ kʋaŋ ta die jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋnvʋŋ.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Die haarɩ dɔntɩba die dɩ yaala ba chɩgɩ ta vaa haarɩkʋ die wɩa die ba vʋarɩ haarɩbike a yi nyaabʋ sʋŋ ta paalɩ yie sɩba ba baa ba ga vʋarɩ wa kʋʋdʋnsɩha a taŋ taaŋ nyaabʋ sʋŋ haarɩkʋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Ama Pɔɔli die dɩ balɩ sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ aŋaŋ sojasisi dɩ, Dɩɩ yi haarɩ dɔntɩba gie dɩ ka wɩarɩ kalɩ haarɩkʋ sʋŋ, nɩ wʋnyɩ mana kaaŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Die wɩa die sojasisi die dɩ gobi gobi guuŋ dieke dɩ bɔbɩna haarɩbike a yallɩ wa ta vaa ka nan nyaabʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Die tuŋ bɩlla vʋʋnɩŋ ta die Pɔɔli die dɩ jʋʋsɩ ba mana dɩ ba dii nyindiike, die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Daraa baŋ anɩɩsa wʋnna ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yallɩ nɩ ta nɩ ka dii jaaŋ mana.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Die wɩa n wa jʋʋsa nɩ ko dii nyindiike, dama ka yiwo jadieke dɩ baaŋ nan yi nɩ hagɩrɩŋ. Nɩ wʋnyɩ kaaŋ wɔŋ ye daŋŋa.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Pɔɔli dɩ bala naa kpatɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nagɩ paanʋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ba mana nɩŋŋa a yi ke gbieri a piili ŋɔba.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Die ba dii sikimiŋ ta wa dii nyindiike.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Die tɩ mana die yiwo vuosi kɔbɩsɩ-le aŋaŋ baŋɩsɩ-yʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ ba yʋaba a bie haarɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Die vuoŋ mana dɩ dine a chagɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ba vʋarɩ paanʋ zaa tɩalɩkaha a taaŋ nyaabʋ sʋŋ amʋ haarɩkʋ da dʋnsɩma.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Die tʋŋ dɩ vʋʋŋ, haarɩ dɔntɩba die dɩ ye tɩŋgbaŋka ama ta ka mɩŋŋɩ mi, ama ta ye gbagɩŋ kaanɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgɩŋ ta ka yallɩ gaaŋ ba yile dɩ ba tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ ba yi haarɩkʋ ga mi a ga fɩrɩ tambʋsɩkʋ me.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Die wɩa die ba chɩa gobi kʋʋdʋnsaha guune a vaa ha a sʋʋŋ ga nyaabʋ chɩaŋ, ta bɩ forisi guuŋ diekemba dɩ bɔbɩna daa diekemba ba yalla a dɔma wa ta die kɔtɩ garɩ dieke dɩ benne haarɩkʋ nɩŋka amʋ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ nan dɩ nɩga a kpaŋŋa haarɩkʋ a gara nɩŋŋa. Die ba keŋ gbigi mʋgɩrɩ kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Ama haarɩkʋ nɩŋŋa die dɩ ga gbeŋ tambʋsɩbʋ ta faasɩ fɩrɩ ta nyapoŋiku dɩ keŋ a nɩgɩ ka kʋaka a ŋmʋʋrɩ ŋmʋʋrɩ.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Tɔ, sojasisi die dɩ saŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ dansarɩka jujuribe amʋ ba wʋnyɩ gbaŋ kaaŋ suo jʋalɩ gaaŋ a chɩgɩ bɩa.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Ama sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ die yaala ʋ gbatɩ Pɔɔli a taaŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ kagɩ ba dɩ ba daa yi die. Ama die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ sɩbɩna suolehe nan nyaabʋ ma a suo jʋalɩ gaaŋ;.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Ta vuo tɩalɩkaha dɩ kana ka sɩba suolehe yigi haarɩkʋ daatɩtɩ a jʋalɩ. Naa die tɩ yi tɩ mana dɩ jʋalɩ gaaŋ ta wɩɩŋ dɩ ka yi tɩ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Gbaŋkʋ gie nyɩŋ wa manɩŋ Pɔɔli vuodieke dɩ yine Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ, ta bɩ yi tʋntʋntʋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ n mʋʋlɩ ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha jigiŋ mana wa jigiŋ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die woliŋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ yaa gamma ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha gie ma, ta ʋ naazʋalɩŋ die dɩ mʋʋlɩha, sɩba a maagɩna dene ʋ kasɩ gbaŋkʋ ma wa.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Wʋvɩɩnaha gie daga yaa gamma ʋ Bʋa wa; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia; die ʋ bɩrɩŋ wa vuota, ta ba mɩɩrɩ wa ʋ yi naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: ta Halɩkasɩka die dɩ dagɩ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ dɩ Yisa seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa ʋ kumbu hagɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Ʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ mɩŋ sieŋ dɩ n yi ʋ tʋntʋntʋ, ta maŋ nagɩ ʋ saaŋ a gara buuriŋ mana jigiŋ, ta vasa ba yie wo yada ta tuose ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Romi ma wa gbaŋ gʋtɩ wa vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wana dɩ ba bɩrɩŋ Yisa Masia sɩɩtɩ wa ma mɩŋ.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Die wɩa n maaga gbaŋkʋ gie a yɩa nɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne Romi tɩŋ ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ cho nɩ ta wa nɩ dɩ nɩ yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi wa. Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ ta yɩ nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Wudieke n yaalala dɩ n woliŋ balɩ wʋnna, n waasa Ŋmɩŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma, nɩ mana wɩa, dama tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana wʋmma nɩ yada ka wɩa.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yine n siaratieŋ dɩ n ka va Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ nɩ wɩa. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke n jɩannana aŋaŋ n sʋŋ mana ta mʋʋla wʋvɩɩnaha yaa gamma ʋ Bʋa wa ma.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Maŋ keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana, n ŋaaŋ jʋʋsɩ wa mɩŋ dɩ dɩɩ yi ʋ dʋŋŋʋ, ʋ yɩ mɩŋ sieŋ aŋ n keŋ kaagɩ nɩ.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: N faasɩ yaala n suŋŋi nɩ aŋ nɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka piini dieke dɩ baaŋ vaa nɩ mɩŋŋɩ a zie ta yaa hagɩrɩŋ ʋ dɩɩsɩŋ ma.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Wudieke n dagɩnana wa yiwo naa, dɩ manɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine yada wa nan bɩagɩ a kpaŋŋɩsɩ taŋ tɩ yada ka ma.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n yaala nɩ sɩmma dɩ n mɩya mɩŋ saŋŋa pam dɩ n keŋ kaagɩ nɩ ama wɩa a taŋ dɩ kaga mɩŋ. N yaala n yi vuodiekemba dɩ benne nɩ jigiŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩɩtɩ sɩba n yine vuogaasɩ jigiŋ dene wo.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ n balɩ vuosi mana wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa, vuodiekemba nine dɩ yuorine aŋaŋ vuodiekemba nine dɩ kana ka yuoriye, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla yɩaŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ wone yɩaŋ.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Die wɩa maŋ cheele dɩ n mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Romi tɩŋ ma.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Viivi ka yallɩ mɩŋ n mʋʋlɩnana wʋvɩɩnaha, dama dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ gbatɩnana vuodiekemba mana dɩ yine Yisa yada a taana; ka woliŋ yiwo Juu vuosi sɩɩtɩ, ta bɩ yi buuriŋ mana sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Wʋvɩɩnaha daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ vuoŋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa ʋ yada yiile wɩa, genke nyɩŋ wa yaalɩŋ ma a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yine Yisa yada, sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dene wo dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ yine vuovɩɩnʋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa ʋ yadaka wɩa, dɩ tieŋ nan dɩ yaa miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dagɩ vuota dɩ ʋ jɩɩ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba, dama ba yie wʋbɩatɩ ta ka dɩ ʋ sieŋ, ta ba wʋbɩatɩ dɩ vaa vuosi dɩ ka sɩba wusieke.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Ŋmɩŋ data ba tɩba, dama wudieke dɩ mʋna dɩ vuota sɩmma yaa gamma ʋ ma wa, ʋ yuori dagɩ ba mɩŋ.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naanna tɩŋgbaŋka gie wo a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, ʋ naaŋkʋ ma ba sɩba ʋ beri dieke vuota nine dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a ye, ta bɩ sɩba dɩ ʋ yaa hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, ta seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ. Die wɩa, ba bɩ wo sieŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa a baaŋ baarɩ dɩ ba ka sɩba ba wʋbɩatɩ.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Ba sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩŋ, ama ba ka yɩa wa jɩlɩma dieke ba mʋna dɩ ba yɩ wa, ta bɩ ka waasa wa. Ama ba sʋŋanyile yiwo sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ, ta ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ wʋtɔgɩtɩ aŋaŋ lɩmɩŋ.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ba baarɩ dɩ ba yaa wa yɩaŋ ama ba yiwo gaantɩŋ;.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: ba tɩnna mʋ ba jɩama Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne saŋŋa mana wa, ba vaya mɩŋ ta jɩama ŋmɩna diekemba vuota dɩ wʋnsɩna a nɩasɩ vuota yaa nembisi yaa dɔŋɩsɩ yaa nyinvuuke.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ va ba, ta ba yie tʋntʋmbɩatɩ diekemba ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ yaalala, ta yie viivi wɩa aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ta nagɩ wudieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ wusieke a taaŋ, ta saagɩ yɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ, ta tarɩgɩ a jɩama wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naanna, ta vaa wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ naanna ha ta mʋ dɩ tɩ bɩrɩ wa wa yaa gamma haahuu wo. Wusie.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Die wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vaa ba, ta ba yie ba dʋŋŋʋ dɩ yalla viivi, ta ba hɔgʋba dɩ ka bɩ yaala ba dʋagɩma aŋaŋ dembisi ta yiŋŋi a dʋaga aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ta dembisi dɩ ka bɩ yaala ba dʋagɩma aŋaŋ hɔgʋba, ta yiŋŋi dʋaga aŋaŋ taŋ, ta ba faasɩ yaa lɔla pam aŋaŋ taŋ. Dembisi dɩ yie nyinti ataŋ die dɩ yalla viivi aŋaŋ taŋ, die wɩa ba yese tɩbɩdatɩ dieke ba mʋna ba wʋbɩatɩtɩ wɩa.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Vuota die dɩ kana ka yaala ʋ sɩmma Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ʋ vaa ba aŋaŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ, ta ba yie tʋŋ dieke dɩ kana ka vɩɩna.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Ba sʋgɩtɩ suuliye mɩŋ aŋaŋ tʋmbɩatɩ yiri yiri, sɩba bɩaŋ aŋaŋ halɩ, ta yie bɩaŋ a yɩa taŋ, ta yaa sʋgɩbɩaŋ, ta kʋa taŋ, ta waga aŋaŋ taŋ, ta chɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ, ta yile wʋbɩatɩ a yɩa taŋ, ta gaara taŋ kʋasɩ.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: ta bala chʋʋsa taŋ, ta yi Ŋmɩŋ dataasɩ, ta zɩa wa taŋ, ta yaa kalinbaani, ta kule, ta yaala sieti dɩ ba yi bɩaŋ, ta ka yɩa ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ jɩlɩma;.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: ta bɩ wo yɩaŋ ba ma, ta ka bɩ bala wusie, ta wo choti ta ka chɩga zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ba sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dagɩya dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene wɩaha gie nyʋarɩ yiwo kuŋ, ama hanɩŋ ba ko yie, ta bɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene he wo.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Die tɩ kenne a jʋalɩ gaarɩ ta wɩɩŋ dɩ ka yi tɩ wo, die tɩ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ tɩŋgbaŋka mi saaŋ yiwo Malita.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Die tɩka vuosi die mɩŋŋɩ tuo tɩ, dama saŋŋa mi nɩɩŋ die nɩɩnana ta watɩ die faasɩ beri die wɩa die ba chɔgɩsɩ boliŋ dɩ tɩ weeli ta yi tɩ aŋsɩa.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Die Pɔɔli die dɩ lagɩsɩ daatɩ a nagɩha taana bolibu me, womi die javʋʋkɩŋ die dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ daatɩ ma bolibu dɩ tʋʋlɩna wɩa a dʋŋ Pɔɔli ta nagɩ ka gbaŋ vili ʋ nuuŋ.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Die tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi die dɩ ye javʋʋkɩrɩ die dɩ leeŋinene Pɔɔli nuuŋ me ta die bala yɩa tamba dɩ, Daa wa gie nan yi vuokʋʋrʋ, aŋaŋ ʋ gbatɩna ʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ wa ma, ama bugile kaaŋ vaa ʋ bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Ama Pɔɔli die dɩ kpaantɩ javʋʋkɩrɩ a taaŋ bolibu me ta wɩɩŋ mana dɩ ka yi wo.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Die vuosisi die dɩ yile dɩ ʋ nan fʋʋsɩ yaa ʋ nan nan bʋnyɩ a kpi. Die ba yilinene die wo a keŋ gbalɩgɩ ta wɩɩŋ mana ka yi wo wo, die ba tarɩgɩ ba sʋŋanyile ta baarɩ dɩ, Daa wa gie yiwo buuŋ.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Mi die ka yʋa aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka naaŋ Pubiluwosi kʋatɩ. Die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ tuo tɩ chaantɩ ta die tɩ yi ʋ chaanɩŋ daraa ataa.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Pubiluwosi chɔɔŋ die dɩ dʋa a yʋagɩ, nyɩŋgbaŋ tʋʋla aŋaŋ chʋʋrɩŋ die yalla wa. Ta Pɔɔli die dɩ ga juu ʋ juoku me a nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a diisi ʋ ma a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa die ʋ ye alaafɩa.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ die dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋka ma die dɩ keŋ Pɔɔli jigiŋ die ʋ gbaaŋ ba mana.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Die ba yɩ tɩ piinisi pam ta die tɩ balla tɩ ga wa ba nagɩ jadieke mana tɩ yaalala a yi haarɩkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Die tɩ benne mi chɩɩtɩ ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die tɩ nagɩ haarɩ dieke ba wasɩnana Ŋmɩŋyɩɩba wa ta die dɩ nyɩna Alekizandira ta bie Malita tafaaŋ saŋka mi wo.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Die tɩ ga tʋgɩ tɩŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Sarakusi ta die yi daraa ataa mi.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: ta die bɩ nagɩ haarɩkʋ a ga tʋgɩ tɩŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Regumi wa. Ka tʋnvʋʋsa die bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ piili a nɩga nyɩna nuugalɩ chaaŋ, ta ka daraa ale daraaŋ die tɩ keŋ tʋgɩ Putiwoli tɩŋ ma.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Mi die tɩ ye Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ, die ba balɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ wɩarɩ ba jigiŋ daraa ayʋpɔyɩ. Die tɩ keŋ nyɩŋ mi a ga Romi.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Die Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ benne Romi wa die dɩ wʋŋ tɩ wɩa ta die keŋ tuoli tɩ sieŋ Apiyusi nyʋŋ ma, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ tuoli tɩ chaanɩŋ juone ataa dieke die dɩ gbine Romi wa. Pɔɔli die dɩ yene be wo die dɩ kpaŋŋɩsɩ wa ta ʋ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Die tɩ gana a ga tʋgɩ Romi die ba yɩ Pɔɔli sieŋ dɩ ʋ bemme ʋ dʋŋŋʋ ta vaa soja balɩmɩŋ gbara wa.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Daraa ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Pɔɔli die dɩ wa Juu vuosi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a lagɩsɩ. Die ba lagɩsɩna wa ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, N nɩmballɩ, aŋaŋ die n kana ka yi wɩɩŋ mana a chʋʋsɩ tɩ vuosisi yaa ta chʋʋsɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba chʋaraha, die ba nagɩ mɩŋ yi wo dansarɩka sʋŋ Jerusalemi ma, ta nagɩ mɩŋ yi Romani vuosi nuusi me.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Die ba pɩasɩ mɩŋ wudieke n yine a chʋʋsɩ ta die yaala ba vʋarɩ mɩŋ, dama die ba mɩŋŋɩya dɩ n ka yi wudieke dɩ mʋna dɩ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Ama Juu vuosisi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ka saagɩya, die wɩa die maŋ baarɩ dɩ n nan nagɩ n wɩɩrɩ a poli a keŋ naaŋ jakʋʋŋ Siiza jigiŋ, ama n wo wɩɩŋ a baaŋ balɩ chʋʋsɩ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n vuosi.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Die wɩa maŋ baaŋ n ye nɩ a balɩ nɩ wɩɩrɩ. N mʋʋlɩnana Ŋmɩŋ Vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ wɩaha, vuodieke tɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi dɩ yalla tama wa, die wɩa ba bɔbɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ chɔrɩmaha gie.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Ta ba balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, Tɩnɩŋ tɩ ka tuo gbanɩŋ a nyɩŋ Judia yaa gamma fʋ ma, ta vuoŋ ka nyɩŋ a keŋ gie aŋaŋ wɩɩrɩ yaa a balɩ wʋbɩaŋ yaa gamma fʋ ma.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Ama tɩ nan dɩ yaala tɩ sɩmma fʋ sʋŋanyile. Tɩ wɔŋ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ jigiŋ mana vuosi bala a chʋʋsa jɩamɩŋ dieke fʋ dɩna wa.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Die wɩa die ba nagɩ daaŋ zieŋ aŋaŋ Pɔɔli. Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi daadaŋ pam dɩ keŋ a lagɩsɩ Pɔɔli die dɩ benne jigiŋ dieke wo. Die ʋ dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa a nyɩŋ sʋkʋʋŋ a ga tʋgɩ yuŋ, ta die mɩa dɩ ʋ tarɩgɩ ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ ba tuo Yisa, ta die vʋara wɩa a nyɩna Mosisi mɩraha ma aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ maagɩna wudiekemba ba.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Die bataŋ sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ tarɩgɩ aŋaŋ ʋ wʋbalɩkaha ma ama bataŋ die dɩ ka tuo dii.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Die wɩa die ba hagɩ mi aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ yaa gamma wʋbalɩkaha gie. Pɔɔli die dɩ balɩ dɩ, Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ vana Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die balɩ yɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba wa, die ʋ balɩ wusie mɩŋ.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Ga a balɩ naa yɩ vuosi gie dɩ: Nɩ nan keŋ wʋŋ a bɩ wʋŋ ama dɩ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba a chɩaŋ; ta nɩ nan keŋ daansɩ a bɩ daansɩ ama nɩ kaaŋ ye.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: dama vuosi gie yiwo yɩaŋkpiititieliŋ, ta bɩ ligi ba tɩba dɩ ba kaaŋ wʋŋ, ta bɩ muo ba nine dɩ ba da yesime. Dɩɩ yi die ba ka muo ba nine ba tɩŋ nan ye, ba tɩŋ ka bɩ ligi ba tɩba, ba tɩŋ nan wʋŋ, ta ba tɩŋ ka bɩ yi yɩaŋkpititieliŋ ba tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩba wɩaha gie chɩasɩ. Ta ba tɩŋ nan yiŋŋi keŋ n jigiŋ aŋ n gbaaŋ ba. .

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha yaa gamma ʋ gbatɩtaaŋkʋ tʋgɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi jigiŋ mɩŋ, ta ba nan wʋŋ. .

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔli die dɩ tuŋ ligire a yɩ jigidieke ʋ benne wo a bie mi bɩna ale ta mɩŋŋɩ tuose chaaŋ diekemba mana dɩ kienene ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ba ye wo.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ta die mʋʋlɩ wɩa yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ta daga vuosi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩa, ta bala ʋ dʋŋŋʋ aŋaŋ sikimiŋ.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Bɩa nyʋarɩ Juu vuosi dɩ ye a tɩaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi? Ba kɔla gobiŋ yaa nyʋarɩ?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Ka yaa nyʋarɩ pam. Wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna Juu vuosisi ka yi nɩŋŋmɩna jaaŋ a tɩaŋ a mana die ʋ yiwo ʋ wɩaha, dɩ ba gbara.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ bataŋ die ka yi wɩaha yada. Die nɩŋ dɩ dagɩya dɩ Ŋmɩŋ ka yi wusietieŋ ba kana ka yi yada wa wɩa?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Aayɩ, ka ka dagɩ die. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ mana dɩ yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩtieliŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ yiwo wusiebɩbalɩtʋ. Sɩba ba maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die wo dɩ: Dɩɩ yi fʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ fʋ balɩ wɩa, ba nan ye dɩ yaa wusie ta ba dii fʋ sarɩya, nan dii wusie.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi tɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ dɩ dagɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yaa wusie, bɩa tɩ baaŋ tɩ balɩ? Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ datɩ tɩ tɩba tɩ nan bɩagɩ balɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo bɩaŋ?.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Aayɩ daa die! Ŋmɩŋ dɩ seŋ ka yi wusiebɩbalɩtʋ, lalɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi a dii tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sarɩya?.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Vuoŋ nan dɩ nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi n ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ dɩ daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ seŋ yiwo wusietieŋ, ta vuosi dɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma, bɩa wɩa ʋ nan datɩ n tɩbɩŋ dɩ n yiwo wʋbɩayiiru.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ bala dɩ, Nɩ vaa tɩ yi wʋbɩatɩ amʋ wʋvɩɩna nan keŋ. Vuosi bataŋ zɩa mɩŋ ta baarɩ dɩ manɩŋ n bala naa. Ŋmɩŋ nan datɩ ba tɩbɩŋ sɩba ba mʋna die.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Lalɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine Juu vuosi wo beriŋ tɩaŋ wa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi beriŋ mɩŋ? Aayɩ, n wɔŋ balɩ mɩŋ dɩ Juu vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi mana bie wo bɩaŋ ma.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Vuoŋ wori a yi vuovɩɩnʋ, Aayɩ, daa vuobalɩmɩŋ gbaŋ.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Vuoŋ wori ta yaa sɩbɩŋ, ta vuoŋ ka jɩama Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Vuoŋ mana yiŋŋiye mɩŋ ta vaa Ŋmɩŋ, vuoŋ mana yiwo bɩaŋ; vuoŋ wori a yie vɩɩnɩŋ, daa vuobalɩmɩŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ba nʋa sɩɩ sɩba kunti vɔrɩtɩ ta ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ dɩ nyɩnna a ma, ta wudieke dɩ nyɩnnana ba nʋa ma sɩɩ sɩba javʋʋkɩŋ tʋaŋ;.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: ba nʋa suuli wo aŋaŋ kaatɩtʋatɩ.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Ta ba yie lagɩ dɩ ba yi vuoŋ daŋŋa ta kʋʋ;.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: ta jigidieke mana ba gana, chʋʋsɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ŋaaŋ kieŋ mi.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Ta ba ka sɩba sie dieke ba baa nan dɩɩ a ye sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Ta ba ka bɩ chɩga Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Lele, tɩ sɩba dɩ wudieke mana Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ bala wa, bala yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ benne ka yiko me, amʋ vuoŋ mana kaaŋ bɩagɩ yuori ʋ nʋaŋ a balɩ wɩɩŋ, ta tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi mana bemme Ŋmɩŋ sarɩya diile me.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Dama vuoŋ wori a nan bɩagɩ a dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna die a yi vuovɩɩnʋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa; mɩraha wɩa vuota dɩ sɩba dɩ ʋ tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Ama lele, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ daagɩnana sie dieke a vasɩnana vuota dɩ bɩrɩma vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa wa wa bie yaalɩŋ ma, ta ʋ naazʋalɩŋ wɩa aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩ daga a wɩa, ama dɩ ka nyɩŋ mɩraha dɩɩsɩŋ ma.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vasa vuota dɩ bɩrɩma vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa ba yine Yisa Masia yada wa wɩa. Ŋmɩŋ yie naa, a yɩa vuodiekemba mana dɩ yine Yisa Masia yada ta ka luge.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: dama vuota mana yiwo bɩaŋ die wɩa tɩ ka bie vɩɩnɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala dene wo.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa ʋ baarɩ dɩ ba yi vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa, ka yiwo piini, dama Yisa Masia gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ mɩŋ yɔrɩ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die vaa wa Yisa Masia dɩ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma, ta bɩ yi ʋ zɩŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna ʋ ma wa dɩ yi kaabɩŋ tɩ bɩaŋ wɩa, dɩɩ yi tɩ yi naa yada Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ chaa tɩ tɩ talɩ; naa daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ. Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa, Ŋmɩŋ die ka datɩ vuota tɩbɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, dama ʋ suguru wɩa;.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: ama lele nɩŋ die ʋ yie, amʋ ka nan dagɩ tɩ dɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ, ta vasa vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine Yisa yada dɩ bɩrɩma vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Die wɩa, jaaŋ beri mɩŋ tɩ nan kuuli aŋaŋ ka? Aayɩ, jaaŋ wori. Daa vuota dɩ dɩna mɩraha wɩa tɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, ama tɩ yine Yisa Masia yada wa wɩa.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Dama tɩ bala a baarɩ dɩ vuota yada yiile wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yi vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa; daa ʋ dɩna mɩraha wɩa.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yiwo Juu vuosi nyɩɩna ma Ŋmɩŋ? Aayɩ, Ʋ yiwo Juu vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ balɩmɩŋ ta nan balɩ dɩ Juu vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi mana yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa, ba yada wɩa.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Tɩ balala dɩ vuota yese gbatɩtaanɩŋ ʋ yada wɩa, die nan yi mɩraha yi yɔrɩ? Aayɩ, daa die. Ama tɩ ye zie wo mɩraha kʋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Wʋbɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ yaa gamma tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Abarahami aŋaŋ ʋ beriŋ me?.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ tʋnvɩɩna wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ, die nɩŋ ʋ tɩŋ nan dɩ yaa jaaŋ dɩ ʋ kuule aŋaŋ ka, ama ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kuule Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Abarahami die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yada, ʋ yada ka wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ tʋma tʋʋma, ʋ nyʋarɩ ka yi piini, ama ka yiwo ʋ tune.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Ama vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a vaa Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ aŋaŋ wudieke ʋ tʋnna. Ŋmɩŋ tuose tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ dɩɩ yi ba yi wo yada.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Davidi die balɩ wa naa gbaŋ gbaŋ, dɩ vuodieke nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ wa ʋ yi vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa ta dɩ ka yi ʋ tʋntʋnvɩɩna wɩa, die vuoke yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Davidi die bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba ta bɩ ligihe, banɩŋ ba yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ;.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kana ka tɩɩnsa ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ, yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Sʋgɩfɩalɩkʋ gie yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ gobine ba kɔla nyɩɩna ma sɩɩtɩ? Aayɩ, dɩ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka gobi ba kɔlaha gbaŋ sɩɩtɩ. Tɩ wɔŋ a baarɩ dɩ dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Abarahami die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yada, ta ʋ yada ka wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Saŋŋa bɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi wɩɩrɩ gie? Ŋmɩŋ die wa wa wa vuovɩɩnʋ ta ʋ ye keŋ gobi ʋ kɔlɩ yaa die ʋ gobi wo ʋ kɔlɩ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ wa wa wa vuovɩɩnʋ? Ŋmɩŋ die wa wa wa vuovɩɩnʋ ta ʋ ye gobi ʋ kɔlɩ.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ gobi ʋ kɔlɩ, ama ʋ gobiku die yiwo siara dɩ wʋnɩŋ Abarahami yada wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wa wa wa vuovɩɩnʋ, ta ʋ ye gobi ʋ kɔlɩ. Die wɩa, vuodiekemba mana dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada ama a ka gobi ba kɔla, Abarahami yine ba chɔɔŋ; ba yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wa ba vuovɩɩna.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Abarahami bɩna yi vuodiekemba dɩ gobine ba kɔlaha chɔɔŋ, daa ba gobinene wɩa, ama ba yine Ŋmɩŋ yada sɩba tɩ chɔɔŋ Abarahami die dɩ yine die, ta ye gobi ʋ kɔlɩ.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ wa Abarahami aŋaŋ ʋ haagɩsɩ nʋaŋ dɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nan yi ba sɩɩtɩ. Die ʋ yɩ wa nʋarɩ gie, daa Abarahami die dɩ dɩna mɩraha wɩa, ama die ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wa ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ nʋa dɩ baa a yi vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna mɩraha sɩɩtɩ, die nɩŋ vuoŋ yada tɩŋ nan yi yɔrɩ, ta Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ kaaŋ dɩ yaa nyʋarɩ.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Mɩraha zetiŋ yalla Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a kieŋ, ama mɩraha dɩ wori, ka zetiŋ wori.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ yine yada sɩɩtɩ, ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa, die ʋ yɩ wa nʋarɩ ta die ka seŋ yi Abarahami haagɩsɩ mana sɩɩtɩ, daa vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna mɩraha nyɩɩna ma sɩɩtɩ, ama vuodiekemba mana dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada sɩba Abarahami die dɩ yine yada die wo, ka yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama wʋnɩŋ Abarahami yiwo tɩ mana chɔɔŋ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, N yi fʋ wo buuriŋ pam chɔɔŋ. Die wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ Abarahami yiwo tɩ chɔɔŋ, Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke Abarahami dɩ yine yada wa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yɩnana kunti miivoli, ta vasa jadieke dɩ wone dɩ keŋ beri.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Abarahami die yiwo yada ta yaa tama saŋŋa dieke wɩɩŋ dɩ kana ka dagɩ dɩ tama beri wo, die wɩa die ʋ keŋ bɩrɩŋ buuriŋ pam chɔɔŋ. Sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Fʋ haagɩsɩ nan dɩ dala sɩba chɩŋmarɩsɩ.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abarahami die yiwo sɩba bɩna kɔbɩga, ta sɩba dɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ gbarɩgɩya mɩŋ, ta ʋ hɔgʋ Sara dɩaŋ dɩ yi hɔgʋpɩrɩŋ, aŋaŋ die mana yɔrɩ, Abarahami yada die ka muuriye.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Die ʋ faasɩ a yi Ŋmɩŋ yada mɩŋ, ta ka chɩɩlɩya aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ, die ʋ yada ka dɩ yɩ wa hagɩrɩŋ pam, ta ʋ bɩra Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Ta die sɩba aŋaŋ wusie dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi wudieke ʋ yɩna wa nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yi wo.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ wa dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ, ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Ama daa Abarahami nyɩɩna ma wɩa dɩ maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa mɩŋ a bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩnʋ.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: Ama a maagɩya mɩŋ a yi tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ tuo tɩ dɩ tɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa wa gbaŋ, dama tɩ yine wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sʋgɩrɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo yada wa wɩa.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ nagɩna Yisa a yɩ ba kʋʋ wa tɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, ta bɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kumbu me, dɩ ʋ vaa tɩ bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ yalla yada Yisa Masia ma wa wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ tɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ, ta sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ dɩ bie tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ sʋnsʋŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩa.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Ʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ, tɩ yada ka wɩa, ta tɩ ye ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha ta yallɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, dama tɩ yaa tama dɩ tɩ nan daansɩ ye beri vɩɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ yɩ tɩ wa.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Ta tɩ bɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ tɩ wahala diile me, dama tɩ sɩba dɩ wahalaha diile yɩa tɩ wa sikimiŋ.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: ta bɩ yɩa tɩ beri vɩɩnɩŋ, ta beri vɩɩnɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yɩa tɩ tama.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Ta tamaka mi dɩ ka vasa tɩ die viivi, dama Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa choti dieke ʋ yalla a yi tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma, ʋ Halɩkasɩka ʋ nagɩna a yɩ tɩ wa wɩa.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Dama saŋŋa dieke tɩ wone suŋŋiŋ wo, ta Masia dɩ tuo tɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ kuŋ a kpi, die ka yiwo saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna a zieŋ.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Dɩ hagɩrɩ mɩŋ dɩ vuoŋ tuo vuodieke dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩɩsa mɩra kuŋ a kpi, ama vuoŋ zɩɩ, vuoŋ nan tuo vuovɩɩnʋ kuŋ a kpi.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Ama dɩ dagɩ tɩ wa ʋ chone tɩ dene, dama die tɩ yene yi tʋmbɩatɩtielibe Masia dɩ tuo tɩ kuŋ a kpi.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Ʋ zɩmbʋ wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ tɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna; die wɩa gbaŋ ʋ nan vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩ dieke dɩ kienene tɩ ma wa.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Die tɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dataasɩ, ama ʋ wa yi tɩ wo ʋ zʋalɩŋ, ʋ Bʋa wa kumbu wɩa. Lele tɩ wana a yi Ŋmɩŋ zʋalɩŋ naa, chɩɩlɩŋ bɩ wori dɩ ʋ nan gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ Masia miivoli wɩa.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Daa die nyɩɩna ma, tɩ bɩ yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ ma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩa, dama ʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi tɩ ʋ zʋalɩŋ.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma bɩaŋ dɩ daagɩ a juu tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta bɩabʋ wɩa kuŋ dɩ beri. Die wɩa kuŋ dɩ kʋa vuota mana, dama vuota mana yiwo bɩaŋ.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Die bɩaŋ wɔŋ bie wo tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ye keŋ yɩ tɩ Mosisi mɩraha; ama dɩɩ yi mɩra dɩ wori, vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ balɩ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩ mɩraha mɩŋ.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Ama a nyɩŋ Adami saŋka a keŋ tʋgɩ Mosisi saŋka, kuŋ yaa wa vuota mana yiko, ta kʋa vuodiekemba bɩaŋ dɩ kana ka tʋgɩ Adami bɩabʋ. Adami die yiwo vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ keŋ ʋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ nɩasɩŋ.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Ama ba bale wo ka yi bʋnyɩ, dama Ŋmɩŋ piini dieke ʋ nagɩna a yɩ tɩ yɔrɩ wa ka sɩɩ sɩba Adami bɩabʋ. Dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ vuosi pam kpiye mɩŋ vuobalɩŋka tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa. Ama Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla faasɩ dala mɩŋ, ta ʋ piini dieke ʋ yɩnana vuosi pam, aŋ ka yi vuobalɩŋka Yisa Masia zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa gbaŋ dala mɩŋ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Ŋmɩŋ piinike aŋaŋ Adami bɩabʋ ka yi bʋnyɩ. Kanɩŋ bɩabʋ mi die yaa wa tɩbɩdatɩŋ a keŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ piini dieke ʋ yɩna tɩ yɔrɩ wa yiwo tɩ wʋbɩatɩ naachaala tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ pam kʋaŋ chaaŋ.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Vuobalɩŋka tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa kuŋ dɩ kʋa vuoŋ mana, wʋnɩŋ vuobalɩŋka mi wɩa. Ama wudieke dɩ dɩna ka kʋaka, vuobalɩŋka Yisa Masia tʋʋmaha tɩaŋ die. Ŋmɩŋ chɩgɩ tɩ wa zɔɔlɩŋ pam ta tuo tɩ Yisa Masia wɩa, die wɩa tɩ nan ye nyaŋŋɩŋ aŋaŋ miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ Yisa Masia ma.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Die vuoŋ mana die baa ʋ ye wo tɩbɩdatɩŋ Adami bɩabʋ wɩa. Ama wʋvɩɩŋ dieke Masia dɩ yine wo wɩa Ŋmɩŋ tuo tɩ mɩŋ ta yɩ tɩ piini dieke dɩ yine miivoli.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Adami die zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ ta yi vuosi pam dɩ yi tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ, ama Yisa tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ, ta nan yi vuosi pam bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Mɩraha keŋye dɩ ka dagɩ bɩaŋ dɩ dala dene, ama bɩaŋ dɩ dala dene wo, Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla faasɩ dala mɩŋ pam a tɩaŋ die.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Die wɩa, bɩaŋ dieke dɩ yine vuota dɩ kpie die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa ʋ baarɩ dɩ ba yi vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ, ta yɩ ba miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia ma.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Die wɩa, bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩ nan dɩ bie tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ ma amʋ Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla gʋtɩ yaa?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Aayɩ, daa die. Yaa gamma bɩaŋ wɩa, tɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩ kpiye mɩŋ, ta bɩaŋ dɩ bɩ wo tɩ hagɩrɩŋ. Die wɩa tɩ kaaŋ wɩarɩ tʋmbɩatɩ ma.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba ba sɩna tɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma wa, tɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩ lagɩŋ a kpi mɩŋ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Die wɩa, die tɩ lagɩŋ a kpi mɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa Masia ta ba guu tɩ aŋaŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ sɩɩla ma; ta ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ ʋ hagɩrɩŋ a sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, die gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ gbaŋ dɩ hagɩ dɩ tɩ bemme miivoli haalɩkʋ ma.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Masia die dɩ lagɩŋ a kpi, die gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ baa tɩ lagɩŋ a hagɩ kuŋ me sɩba die ʋ hagɩna die wo.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ tɩ berikʋʋrɩ die lagɩŋ a kpi mɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa ʋ dagarɩkʋ ma, dɩ tɩ berikʋʋrɩ daa bɩ yallɩma tɩ hagɩrɩŋ, ta tɩ kaaŋ bɩ yi yɔŋɩsɩ bɩaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: dama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ kpi ʋ bɩ wo bɩaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Tɩ dene kpi aŋaŋ Masia wa, tɩ yaa yada dɩ tɩ nan dɩ yallɩ miivoli aŋaŋ wa.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ sʋgɩrɩ wa Masia a nyɩŋ kuŋ me ta ʋ kaaŋ bɩ kpi; kuŋ bɩ wo ʋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Die ʋ kpiye mɩŋ bʋnyɩ tɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, ama ʋ wana bie miivoli me wo, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ lagɩŋ na a beri mɩŋ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ gbaŋ nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ sɩba nɩ kpiye mɩŋ ta bɩaŋ dɩ bɩ wo nɩ hagɩrɩŋ, ama Yisa Masia wɩa nɩ bie wo nɩ miivoli me Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ daa vaa bɩaŋ pɔgɩlɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ta vasa nɩ yime bɩaŋ choti.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Nɩ daa yɩma nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ lʋgɩŋ kaanɩ hagɩrɩŋ aŋ ka tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ, ama nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta sɩɩ sɩba nɩ nyɩŋ wa kuŋ me ta juu miivoli me; nɩ nagɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ʋ nagɩ nɩ a tʋŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ tʋʋma.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Bɩaŋ da yallɩma dɩ hagɩrɩŋ, dama mɩraha ka sɩ nɩ, ama Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha sɩna nɩ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Mɩraha dɩ kana ka sɩ tɩ wa, ama ta Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha dɩ sɩ tɩ naa, tɩ nan wɩarɩ tʋmbɩatɩ yiile me yaa? Aayɩ, tɩ kaaŋ yi die.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ yiwo yɔmʋ a yɩ vuodieke fʋ tuosinene ʋ nʋarɩ? Dɩɩ yi fʋ saagɩ a yie tʋmbɩatɩ, yiwo bɩaŋ yɔmʋ, ta ka kpatɩŋ yiwo kuŋ, ama fʋ saagɩ Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ, die nan vaa fʋ yi vuovɩɩnʋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ, bɩaŋ die yalla nɩ hagɩrɩŋ, ama lele nɩŋ n waasa Ŋmɩŋ nɩ faasɩna a tuo wusie dagɩkʋ dieke n dagɩna nɩ wa.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ yɔŋɩsɩ ma, amʋ nɩ yi wudieke dɩ baaŋ nan fɩalɩ ʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Sɩba die nɩ ŋaana a naga nɩ gbaŋ a tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ a gʋta die wo, lele nɩŋ nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a tʋma Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma amʋ nɩ nan seŋ yi ʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Saŋŋa dieke die nɩ yine bɩaŋ yɔŋɩsɩ wa, die dɩ ka yi fɔgɩmɩ dɩ nɩ fɩalɩ Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Tʋŋ dieke die nɩ tʋnna ta lele ka wa yaa viivi naa, bɩa nyʋarɩ nɩ ye? Hanɩŋ tʋʋmaha mi chanchaalɩŋ yaa kuŋ a kieŋ mɩŋ.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Ama lele nɩŋ nɩ wa nyɩŋ wa bɩaŋ yɔŋɩsɩ ma, ta bɩrɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ yɔŋɩsɩ. Naa nan vaa nɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Dama tʋntʋmbɩatɩ nyʋarɩ yiwo kuŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ piini yiwo miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, tɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ mana sɩba mɩra, die wɩa nɩ nan bɩagɩ sɩba wudieke n bala n balɩ naa chɩaŋ. Mɩra yige vuodieke dɩ benne ʋ miivoli me mɩŋ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Die wɩa mɩraha sʋŋ, dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ hɔgʋ lagɩma aŋaŋ ʋ chʋrʋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ chʋrʋ dɩ yene bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ama saŋŋa dieke ʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ keŋ kpi, mɩraha ka bɩ yigi hɔgʋ wa.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi saŋŋa dieke ʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ yene bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ta ʋ beri aŋaŋ dembigaaŋ, ba nan dɩ wasa wa daakpannagɩrʋ; ama dɩɩ yi ʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ kpi, mɩraha kaaŋ bɩ yigi wo. Dɩɩ yi ʋ yallɩ dembigaaŋ ʋ chʋrʋ wa kuŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ, ʋ ka yi daakpannagɩrʋ.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: N nɩmballɩ, die gbaŋ die dɩ sɩɩ aŋaŋ nɩ, nɩ yiwo Masia nyɩŋgbanɩŋ lʋgɩŋ, ta die kpi ta mɩraha ka bɩ yige nɩ, ama lele nɩŋ nɩ taasɩya mɩŋ aŋaŋ Masia vuodieke die dɩ hagɩna kumbu me wo, nɩ yime wʋvɩɩna a yɩma Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa die tɩ yie tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ choti ta mɩraha dɩ vaa tɩ yaa lɔlɩbɩatɩ ta a tʋma tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma, ta yaa wudieke dɩ yine kuŋ a kieŋ tɩ ma.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Ama lele nɩŋ, mɩraha ka bɩ yigi tɩ, dama die tɩ kpiye mɩŋ ta mɩraha dɩ bɩ wo tɩ hagɩrɩŋ. Die wɩa, tɩ wa bɩagɩ a tʋma yɩa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ miivoli haalɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩna tɩ wa; tɩ ka bɩ tʋma aŋaŋ mɩrɩkʋraha die ba maagɩna gbaŋkʋ ma wa.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Die wɩa, bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩ nan balɩ dɩ mɩraha ka vɩɩna yaa? Aayɩ, tɩ kaaŋ balɩ die. Mɩraha dɩ tɩŋ wori, n tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba bɩaŋ dɩ yine jadieke. Mɩraha dɩ tɩŋ ka dagɩya dɩ n daa yallɩma lɔla aŋaŋ n chanchaaŋ jaaŋ, n tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba lɔla dɩ yine jadieke.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Ama bɩaŋ ye wo ka hagɩrɩŋ a nyɩŋ wa mɩraha sʋŋ, ta lɔla yiri yiri pam dɩ hagɩ n sʋŋ ma. Mɩraha dɩ tɩŋ wori, bɩaŋ tɩŋ nan yi jakpiikiŋ.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Saŋŋa dieke die n kana ka sɩba mɩraha, die n yaa miivoli mɩŋ; ama n kenne sɩba mɩraha, bɩaŋ dieke dɩ benne n sʋkʋ dɩ hagɩ.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: ta maŋ kpi. Ta mɩra dieke dɩ tɩnna mʋ ka yaa miivoli a keŋ wo, die dɩ yaa kuŋ a keŋ.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Bɩaŋ die dɩ ye sieŋ mɩraha sʋŋ ta gaaŋ mɩŋ, ta kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Mɩraha aŋaŋ ka dagɩkʋ nɩŋ yiwo kasɩ, ta yi wusie ta bɩ vɩɩna.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Die nɩŋ, dɩ baaŋ dagɩ dɩ wudieke dɩ vɩɩna wa yalla kumbu a keŋ n ma wa? Aayɩ, daa die. Bɩaŋ dɩ wa wudieke dɩ vɩɩna a yaa kumbu a keŋ n ma, amʋ ka nan yuori dagɩ tɩ bɩaŋ dɩ sɩna dene. Die wɩa tɩ wa sɩba dɩ bɩaŋ faasɩ yiwo jabɩaŋ pam.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ mɩraha nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ama manɩŋ nɩŋ n yiwo vuota, ta yi bɩaŋ yɔmʋ.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: N ka sɩba wudieke n yinene chɩaŋ; ta ka yie wʋvɩɩna dieke n yaalala dɩ n yi; ama ta yie tʋmbɩatɩ dieke n hana.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ yie tʋmbɩatɩ dieke n kana ka yaala dɩ n yi, dɩ dagɩya dɩ n saagɩya mɩŋ dɩ mɩraha vɩɩna mɩŋ.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Die wɩa, dɩ ka yi n gbaŋ n tʋnnanaha, ama dɩ yiwo bɩa dieke dɩ benne a ma wa vanana maŋ yie he.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: N sɩba a baarɩ dɩ wʋvɩɩnɩŋ wo n sʋŋ ma, n yaala n yiwo wudieke dɩ vɩɩna, ama n ka bɩagɩ yie ke.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Wʋvɩɩŋ dieke n yaalala dɩ n yi wo, n ka bɩagɩ yie, ama ta tʋmbɩatɩ dieke n kana ka yaala dɩ n yiwo, kanɩŋ ma yie.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi maŋ yie wudieke n kana ka yaala dɩ n yiwo, dɩ dagɩya dɩ daa manɩŋ n gbaŋ n yinene he ama dɩ yiwo bɩa dieke dɩ benne n ma yinene he.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Wʋbalɩmɩŋ maŋ ye ta ka bie n ma saŋŋa mana, maŋ keŋ yaala dɩ n yi wudieke dɩ vɩɩna, bɩaŋ ŋaaŋ beri aŋaŋ mɩŋ.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha yɩnana mɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: ta maŋ yese mɩrɩ dieke dɩ tʋnnana n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma a waga aŋaŋ mɩrɩ dieke n sʋŋ dɩ yaalala, ta bɔbɩ mɩŋ sɩba dansarɩka vuoŋ, dɩ n saagɩ a yi bɩa dieke dɩ benne n sʋŋ ma wa.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Aba, n yiwo sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ tieŋ. Mɩnɩa baaŋ gbatɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yalla mɩŋ a gara kuŋ me naa?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: N bɩra Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ gbatɩna mɩŋ a taaŋ naa, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia wɩa. Die wɩa, n dɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha n sʋŋ ma, ama ta n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ dɩ bɩaŋ sieku.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Die wɩa, lele Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ dii vuodiekemba dɩ benne Yisa Masia ma wa sarɩya a baarɩ dɩ ba chʋʋsɩya.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka nan yɩ ba miivoli dieke dɩ nyɩnna Yisa Masia jigiŋ ta gbatɩ ba a taaŋ yɔrɩ, a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ aŋaŋ kuŋ me.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Ŋmɩŋ tʋŋ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ Bʋa tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ta ʋ keŋ aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ sɩba tɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ, ʋ keŋ tuo tɩ kuŋ a kpi. Ta ʋ kumbu wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ bɩa dieke die dɩ benne tɩnɩŋ vuota sʋgɩtɩ ma wa hagɩrɩŋ. Mosisi mɩraha ka bɩagɩ yi naa, dama tɩnɩŋ vuota ka bɩagɩ dɩ mɩraha vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yiwo naa amʋ tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba beriŋ dɩ dɩna Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka daa vuota choti wo, nan saagɩ a dɩɩ wusie sieku sɩba mɩraha dɩ dagɩna dene wo.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Vuodiekemba beriŋ dɩ dɩna vuota choti wo, naga ba sʋŋanyile a yie vuota choti me mɩŋ, ama vuodiekemba beriŋ dɩ dɩna Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩaŋ dɩ naga ba sʋŋanyile a yie Halɩkasɩka choti me mɩŋ.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩ fʋ yɩaŋ a taasɩ aŋaŋ vuota choti, dɩ yiwo kuŋ, ama dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩ fʋ yɩaŋ a taasɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka choti, dɩ yiwo miivoli aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩ fʋ yɩaŋ a taasɩ aŋaŋ vuota choti, yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dataaŋ, ta ka dɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, ta kaaŋ seŋ a dɩ ha.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna vuota choti kaaŋ bɩagɩ yi wɩɩŋ a fɩalɩ Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Lele nɩ ka bɩ beri sɩba nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ yaalala nɩ bemme dene, ama nɩ beriŋ sɩɩ sɩba Halɩkasɩka dɩ dagɩna nɩ dene, dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ seŋ bie nɩ ma. Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ wone Masia Halɩkasɩka ka yi ʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Dɩɩ yi Masia dɩ bie nɩ ma, Halɩkasɩka nan yɩ nɩ miivoli dama Ŋmɩŋ tuo nɩ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa ama nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ nan kpi nɩ bɩaŋ wɩa.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sʋgɩrɩna Yisa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me wo, dɩɩ yi ta ʋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ bie nɩ ma, wʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sʋgɩrɩna Yisa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me wo, wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ yɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ miivoli haalɩŋ, ʋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ benne nɩ ma wɩa.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Die wɩa, n nɩmballɩ, nɩ sɩmma ta daa dɩa nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ choti.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ dɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ choti nɩ baa nɩ kpi mɩŋ, ama dɩɩ yi nɩ kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ choti Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ ma, nɩ nan ye miivoli.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ dagɩnana ba sieku, banɩŋ ba yine Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Dama Halɩkasɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nɩ wa, ka yi nɩ bɩrɩŋ wa yɔŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana ŋmaamɩŋ, ama ʋ yi nɩ bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ, ta yɩ nɩ hagɩrɩŋ nɩ wasa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ, Tɩ Chɔɔŋ.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka vana tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ haalɩtɩ dɩ sɩba dɩ tɩ yi wo Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Tɩ senne a yi Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ naa wɩa, tɩ nan ye nyɩnvɩɩna diekemba ʋ nagɩna a dʋaŋ a gbarɩ ʋ vuosisi; tɩ nan keŋ gʋtɩ ye ʋ jɩlɩma. Dama dɩɩ yi tɩ die wahala Masia wɩa, ʋ nan keŋ kɔtɩ tɩ.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: N sɩba dɩ wahala dieke tɩ dinene lele gie wo kaaŋ bɩagɩ nagɩ mɔgɩsɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ yɩ tɩ wa.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Dʋnɩa nyinti mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naana chɩɩsa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna a gbarɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ vʋarɩ ʋ ballɩ yeŋ me.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
