local: dama dʋnɩa nyinti mana die chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ ta die bɩ wo nyʋarɩ, die ka ka yi ka gbaŋ ka dʋŋŋʋ, die ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ ʋ yi die, ama ʋ bɩ vaa ka yaa tama.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: Daaŋ kaanɩ nan dɩ beri ta dʋnɩaka nyinti mana nan keŋ nyɩŋ kuŋ yɔŋɩsɩ ma, ta kuŋ kaaŋ bɩ pɔgɩlɩha, ta nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ dɩ bala ba keŋ ye wo.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dʋnɩa nyinti mana bie tʋaŋ ma ta ŋmuuse a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne sɩba hɔgʋ dɩ bala ʋ mɩɩrɩ ta ŋmuuse dene wo.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Ama daa dʋnɩa nyinti nyɩɩna ma ŋmuusinene, ama tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yine piini dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wolinne a yɩ gbaŋ ŋmuuse mɩŋ tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma ta chɩɩsa saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ wa tɩ ʋ ballɩ, a gbatɩ tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a nyɩŋ yɔŋɩsɩ ma.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Die tɩ yalla tama wɩa tɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi tɩ ye jadieke tɩ yalla tama dɩ tɩ nan ye, die nɩŋ ka ka bɩ yi tama. Mɩnɩa baa ʋ yaa tama jadieke ʋ wone ye wɩa?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi tɩ yaa tama dɩ tɩ nan ye jadieke tɩ yene ka yeye, die nɩŋ tɩ nan chɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ suguru.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ suŋŋe tɩ tɩ gbalɩgɩsɩ ma. Tɩ ŋaaŋ ka sɩba tɩ mʋna tɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dene ama Halɩkasɩka ŋaaŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩa tɩ aŋaŋ ŋmuusi dieke nʋawɩɩŋ dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna vuota sʋŋanyile sɩba Halɩkasɩka sʋŋanyile mɩŋ, dama Halɩkasɩka jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩa ʋ vuosi a dɩɩ sie dieke wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaala.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wana, ʋ wa ba wa wɩɩŋ wɩa, ta ba yaa choti aŋaŋ wa, ta tɩ sɩba dɩ wudieke mana dɩ yinene, ʋ vasa a yie wʋvɩɩna a yɩa ba.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Ta vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wone a vʋarɩ wa, die ʋ vʋarɩ ba mɩŋ dɩ ba sɩmma ʋ Bʋa wa, amʋ ʋ Bʋa wa nan yi Bʋajakʋʋŋ ta yallɩ nɩmballɩ pam.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Ŋmɩŋ wa wa vuodiekemba ʋ vʋarɩna wa, ta yi banɩŋ vuodiekemba ʋ wana wa dɩ ba yi vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa, ta vuodiekemba ʋ bala dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa wa, ʋ kɔtɩ ba ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ yaa gamma geŋ he mana ma? Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bie tɩ chaaŋ, mɩnɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ a tuoli tɩ?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die ka mʋŋ tɩ ʋ Bʋa wa, ama die ʋ nagɩ wa mɩŋ a bɩrɩŋ kaaba tɩ wɩa. Dɩɩ yi die ʋ yɩ tɩ ʋ Bʋa, bɩa wɩa ʋ kaaŋ yɩ tɩ nyintɩalɩkaha mana.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Mɩnɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ chʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩka? Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ baarɩ dɩ ba wo bɩaŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Mɩnɩa benne a baaŋ bɩagɩ a dii ba sarɩya ta baarɩ dɩ ba chʋʋsɩya? Vuoŋ wori. Masia die kpiye mɩŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, ta lele ʋ kalɩ Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ a jʋʋsa wa a yɩa tɩ.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Bɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ Masia choti me? Wumugisike yaa, wahala yaa mugisiŋ yaa kɔŋ yaa zɔɔlɩŋ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ yaa kuŋ?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Sɩba ba maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ: Fʋ wɩa vuosi yaala ba kʋʋ tɩ mɩŋ saŋŋa mana; ta tɩ sɩɩ sɩba yii diekemba ba yalla a gara dɩ ba kɔrɩgɩ wa.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Ama wɩaha gie mana ma, tɩ yaa wa nyaŋŋɩŋ, Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ chone tɩ wo ma.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Dama n seŋ a sɩba dɩ jaaŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ Masia choti me; kuŋ yaa, mɩsɩ yaa, malakasi yaa, jɩmbɩatɩ yaa, wudieke dɩ yinene lele yaa, wudieke dɩ bala ka yi saŋŋa dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ wo.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: yaa jadieke dɩ benne ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, yaa tɩŋgbaŋka chɩaŋ, jaaŋ wori naamɩŋ mana mana ma a baaŋ bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ choti me, choti dieke dɩ nyɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia ma wa.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: N yiwo Masia sɩɩtɩ wɩa, wudieke n balala wa yiwo wusie, n ka chɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ. Ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ dii n siara dɩ n ka chɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: dɩɩ yi maŋ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: n gbaŋ gbaŋ buuriŋ wɩa. Dɩ tɩŋ nan yi mɩŋ nansɩŋ maŋ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a zie vuosi gie naŋ ma dɩ Masia chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ, n tɩŋ nan saagɩ.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Banɩŋ ba yiwo Izara vuosi, ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩ ba, ba yi ʋ ballɩ, ta dagɩ ba ʋ chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ, ta saagɩ aŋaŋ ba, ta yɩ ba ʋ mɩraha, ta dagɩ ba wusie jɩamɩŋ, ta yɩ ba nʋa.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ die yiwo vuokpɩɩma, ta Masia die dɩ tarɩgɩna a yi vuota wa die yiwo ba wʋnyɩ. Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yalla yiko jaaŋ mana ma wa ye bɩrɩ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ. Wusie.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: N ka baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ ka tʋma; dama daa Izara vuosi mana yine Ŋmɩŋ vuosi wusie.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Daa Abarahami haagɩsɩ mana yine ʋ haagɩsɩ wusie, dama Ŋmɩŋ die balɩ Abarahami dɩ, Aziki maarɩŋ bala ba keŋ yi fʋ haagɩsɩ.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Die wɩa, daa vuodiekemba Abarahami dɩ mɩɩrɩna vuota mɩɩrɩŋ yine Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ. Vuodiekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ mɩɩrɩŋ ballɩ banɩŋ ba senne yi Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ wa nʋarɩ gie dɩ, Bɩŋ chaaŋ saŋka gie n nan yiŋŋi keŋ, ta Sara mɩɩrɩ wa Bʋadembiŋ.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Ama daa a mana wonde. Rebeka die mɩɩrɩ wa yɩɩba ta ba yi dembisi ta yaa chɔɔŋ balɩmɩŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Aziki.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Ama saŋŋa dieke die ba yene ka mɩɩrɩ ba ta ba ye ka yi wudieke dɩ yine vɩɩnɩŋ yaa bɩaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ Rebeka dɩ jakʋʋrɩ nan keŋ jɩaŋ bʋabike. Ŋmɩŋ die bala naa dɩ ʋ choti yi. Die ʋ choti yiwo dɩ ʋ vʋarɩ vuosi, daa ba tʋʋma wɩa; ama dɩ yiwo ʋ chone ta vʋarɩ dene.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala wudieke ʋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, N vʋarɩ wa Jakobu ama ta zeti Esɔɔ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩ nan balɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ ka yie wudieke dɩ vɩɩna yaa? Aayɩ, daa die.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die balɩ wa Mosisi dɩ, Vuodiekemba n yaalala n chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ banɩŋ maŋ nan chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Die wɩa, dɩ ka dɩ vuota dɩ yaalɩnana yaa a yie wudieke, ama dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma a balɩ yɩ Faaro dɩ, Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ n yi fʋ wo naaŋ naa wɩa, dɩ n nagɩ fʋ a dagɩ n hagɩrɩŋ, ta vaa n saaŋ mʋʋlɩ a ga tɩŋgbaŋka gie lʋga mana.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Die wɩa, ʋ chɩga vuodiekemba ʋ yaalala dɩ ʋ chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ, ta yie bataŋ tɩba dɩ ka wʋmma wɩa sɩba ʋ yaala dene.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Nɩ wʋnyɩ nan pɩasɩ mɩŋ dɩ dɩɩ yi die, bɩa wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yese vuota chʋʋsɩŋ? Mɩnɩa baaŋ bɩagɩ a zeti Ŋmɩŋ choti?.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Ama n zʋa, mɩnɩa yine fʋ dɩ fʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wɩɩŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ? Kpagɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a pɩasɩ vuodieke dɩ wʋnsɩna ka wa dɩ, Bɩa fʋ wʋnsɩ mɩŋ naa.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Dama vuodieke dɩ wʋnsɩna kpaga yalla sieŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ tɔgɩŋ a wʋnsɩ kpaga ale a nyɩŋ tɔgɩrɩ mi, ta kaanɩ dɩ vɩɩna ta kaanɩ dɩaŋ dɩ ka vɩɩna.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi; Die ʋ yaala ʋ dagɩ wa ʋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋ vuoŋ mana sɩmma ʋ hagɩrɩŋ. Ama die ʋ dii wo suguru aŋaŋ vuodiekemba ʋ jɩɩna sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba wa ta dɩ mʋ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩ ba wa.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Die ʋ yiwo naa, dɩ ʋ dagɩ ʋ yalla hagɩrɩŋ pam dene, ta die chɩgɩ vuodiekemba ʋ vʋarɩna zɔɔlɩŋ dɩ ba gʋtɩ ye ʋ hagɩrɩbʋ a taaŋ.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Dama tɩ yine vuodiekemba ʋ wana daa Juu vuosi nyɩɩna ma, ama aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi gbaŋ.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Naa die yi sɩba Hosia die dɩ maagɩna dene, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodiekemba die dɩ kana ka yi n vuosisi, banɩŋ maŋ nan nagɩ aŋ ba yi n vuosi; ta buuri dieke die n kana ka cho wo, banɩŋ maŋ nan wa ba n buuri choti.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Ta jigidieke die ba balɩ ba dɩ ba ka yi n vuosi wo, mi ba nan wa ba Miivolitieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die balɩ wa naa yaa gamma Izara vuosi ma dɩ, Dɩɩ yi Izara vuosi dɩ dala sɩba mʋgɩŋ tambʋsɩŋ ama ba bɩta nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ;.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ nan datɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi tɩba a kpatɩ lagɩ lagɩ sɩba ʋ bala die wo.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ wolinne a balɩ wa dɩ, Dɩɩ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ vuodieke dɩ yine hagɩrɩtieŋ wo die dɩ vaa tɩ buuriŋ bataŋ, dɩ tɩŋ nan haarɩ tɩ sɩba dɩ haarɩna Sodomi aŋaŋ Gomora tɩgɩsɩ vuosi dene wo.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ta lele, bɩa tɩ nan wa balɩ? Tɩ nan balɩ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi wo die ka mɩa dɩ ba yi vuovɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa, ba yada wɩa.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Ama Izara vuodiekemba die dɩ mɩnana dɩ ba yi mɩrɩ diekemba dɩ baaŋ yi aŋ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa wa dɩ waarɩ ka.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Bɩa wɩa die ba waarɩ ka? Die ba waarɩ ka mɩŋ dama die ba mɩa mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ balɩ dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa daa ba yada wɩa ama ba tʋʋma wɩa. Die wɩa, die ba gbiŋ taŋgbiŋkiri a nan.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: taŋ dieke ba maagɩna ka wɩa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Ye, manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa taŋ a dʋaŋ Zayɔni tɩŋ ma, taŋ dieke dɩ baaŋ vaa vuosi gbiŋ aŋaŋ ba nagɩsɩ, ta yi taŋ dieke dɩ baaŋ vaa ba nan. Ama vuodieke dɩ yi wo yada kaaŋ dii viivi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ n sʋŋ mana dɩ ʋ vaa n gbaŋ gbaŋ n buuriŋ Juu vuosi ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: N nan bɩagɩ a dii ba siara dɩ ba dɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna; ama ba ka sɩba ʋ sieŋ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Ba ka sɩba sie dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ daagɩnana a vasa vuosi dɩ yie vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ, ama ta yaala ba gbaŋ gbaŋ sieŋ, die wɩa ba ka sʋʋna ba gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ a yɩa sie dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ daagɩnana a vasa vuosi dɩ yie vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa wa.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Dama Mosisi mɩraha dɩɩsɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ bɩagɩ vaa Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ vuoŋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yi Masia yada, Ŋmɩŋ nan baarɩ dɩ wʋnɩŋ vuoke yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Mosisi die maagɩ wa naa yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ vaa vuoŋ yi vuovɩɩnʋ ʋ nɩŋŋa dɩɩ yi ʋ dɩɩ mɩraha; die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yinene wudieke mɩraha dɩ dagɩna nan ye miivoli ka wɩa.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Ama beri vɩɩŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna yada ma balɩ wa naa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Da keŋ yile fʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ mɩnɩa baaŋ jʋalɩ ga arɩzanna.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Da keŋ bɩ yilime dɩ mɩnɩa baaŋ sʋʋŋ ga kpiile tigiŋ.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Wudieke Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ dagɩna yiwo naa, dɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha keŋ gbigi nɩ mɩŋ; ka bie nɩ nʋa ma aŋaŋ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma. Wʋvɩɩnaha yiwo yada wudieke tɩ mʋʋlɩnana wa.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ yuori fʋ nʋaŋ a balɩ dɩ, Yisa yiwo Yɔmʋtieŋ, ta yi yada fʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die sʋgɩrɩ wa mɩŋ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, Ŋmɩŋ nan gbatɩ fʋ a taaŋ.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Dama tɩ yine Yisa yada tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ tɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ nɩŋŋa, ta bɩ yuori tɩ nʋa a bala yadaka wɩa tɩ yese gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuodieke dɩ yine wo yada, ʋ kaaŋ wɔŋ dii viivi.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Jaaŋ ka puo Juu vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi. Ŋmɩŋ balɩŋka yine tɩ mana Yɔmʋtieŋ ta suŋŋe vuodiekemba mana dɩ jʋʋsɩnana wa.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ jʋʋsɩnana tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ta yaala suŋŋiŋ, nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuosi dɩ ka yi wo yada, lalɩa ba baa ba jʋʋsɩ dɩ ʋ suŋŋi be? Ta dɩɩ yi ba ka wʋŋ wʋvɩɩnaha, lalɩa ba baa ba yi wo yada? Ta ba ka mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha, lalɩa ba baa ba yi a wʋŋ ka wɩa?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ta dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ka tʋŋ tʋntʋntʋ mi, lalɩa wʋvɩɩnaha dɩ baa a yi a mʋʋlɩ? Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ ganana a mʋʋla wʋvɩɩnaha keniŋ vɩɩna pam.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Ama daa vuoŋ mana die tuone wʋvɩɩnaha. Azaya die maagɩya mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ, mɩnɩa tuosinene wʋvɩɩnaha gie.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ wʋŋ wʋvɩɩnaha gie, die baaŋ vaa vuoke yi Yisa yada, ta dɩɩ yi vuosi dɩ mʋʋla Masia wʋvɩɩnaha die baaŋ vaa ba wʋŋ.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: N yaala n pɩasɩ a ye dɩ vuosi ka wʋŋ wʋvɩɩnaha yaa? Wusie, ba wʋŋya. Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuosi wʋŋ ba lɔlɩŋ aŋaŋ ba wʋbalɩka tɩŋgbaŋka gie lʋga mana mɩŋ.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: N yaala n bɩ pɩasɩ a ye dɩ Izara vuosi die ka sɩba ka chɩaŋ yaa? Mosisi gbaŋ gbaŋ die wolinne a yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, N nan yi Izara vuosi yallɩma nɩnŋmaarɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ wone buuriŋ. Ta vaa ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ buuri dieke dɩ yine gaatɩŋ.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Azaya die dii wo dembisi, ta baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, Vuodiekemba baga die dɩ wone n ma ye mɩŋ mɩŋ; Ta nagɩ n gbaŋ a dagɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka pɩasa yaala n jigiŋ.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Ama ʋ balɩ wa naa yaa gamma Izara vuosi ma dɩ, N tɩɩrɩ wa n nuuŋ ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ dɩ n tuo be ama ba yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka wʋmma wɩa ta zeti n nʋaŋ.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: N bɩ yaala n pɩasɩ mɩŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ zeti wo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi dɩ yine Izara vuosi yaa? Aayɩ, ʋ ka zeti be. Manɩŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n yiwo Izara vuoŋ, ta yi Abarahami haagɩŋ, ta nyɩŋ Benjamani buuriŋ ma.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ ka zeti ʋ vuosi ʋ vʋarɩna a nyɩŋ piiliku me wo. Nɩ sɩba wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma yaa gamma Elaja die dɩ fɔbɩlɩnana a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ yaa gamma Izara wɩa dene:.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ba kʋʋ fʋ naazʋalɩba mana ta chʋʋsɩ fʋ korititi, manɩŋ n nyɩɩna ma tɩala ta ba mɩa dɩ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, Manɩŋ n yaa wa dembisi tuse ayʋpɔyɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka jɩaŋ buuŋ Baali.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Die dɩ sɩɩ leleke gie gbaŋ: Izara vuosi bɩta ye beri ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ ba ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ vuosi dɩ yi wo ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa ʋ vʋarɩ ba daa ba tʋʋma wɩa. Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ vʋara vuosi ba tʋʋma wɩa, die nɩŋ ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha ka yi wusie zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Naa dagɩya dɩ Izara vuosi bɩta dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna wa die yene jadieke ba mana die dɩ dɩna dɩdɩa a yaala wa. Ama vuotɩalɩkaha sikpeŋ die hagɩrɩ mɩŋ.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: Naa die yi sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Bɩna dieke dɩ tɩanna a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yi ba ka sɩba wɩa chɩasɩ, ta ka bɩ yese yaa wʋmma.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Dɩ bɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ Davidi die baarɩ dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ, vaa vuosi gie dʋʋga yi chachabɩsɩ a yigi be aŋ ba dii wahala;.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: ta vaa ba nine dɩ yɩɩŋ, amʋ ba kaaŋ dɩ yese, ta vaa ba sʋʋŋ a zʋʋrɩ ta daa bɩagɩ a tɩɩntɩ.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: N yaala n pɩasɩ a ye dɩ Juu vuosi die dɩ gbinne naŋ a nan wa, die ba nanya mɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ mana? Aayɩ, daa die. Die ba yine bɩaŋ wɩa, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ gbatɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi a taaŋ. Ŋmɩŋ die yi naa amʋ Juu vuosisi nan dɩ yaa nɩnŋmaarɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Juu vuosi bɩaŋ wɩa dɩ yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ; ta banɩŋ Juu vuosi dɩ kana ka yi Ŋmɩŋ choti wɩa yine vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, die wɩa dɩɩ yi Juu vuosisi mana dɩaŋ dɩ vaa ba tʋmbɩatɩ ta ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi nan faasɩ ye alibarika a tɩaŋ naa.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: N bala yɩa nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi, n yene yi Masia tʋntʋntʋ nɩ jigiŋ naa, n nan dɩ yaa nyʋʋŋ nɩgɩŋ n tʋʋma ma.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi n gbaŋ gbaŋ n vuosi dɩ yine Juu vuosisi yallɩma nɩnŋmaarɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi, vuoŋ zɩɩ dɩ tɩŋ nan yi bataŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ zetine Juu vuosisi, die ʋ yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi ʋ zʋalɩŋ, ama dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ saagɩ a tuo be ba yi ʋ vuosi bɩbra, dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ lalɩa? Dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba ba sʋgɩrɩna vuoŋ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me die wo.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Dɩɩ yi bʋmbʋŋaŋ paanʋkʋ dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ, nyintɩalɩkaha gbaŋ yiwo ʋ sɩɩtɩ. Dɩɩ yi tɩɩŋ nagɩrɩsɩ dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ, ka nagɩsɩsɩ gbaŋ yiwo ʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Juu vuosi sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tɩɩ dieke dɩ vɩɩna, ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ gobi tɩɩka nagɩsɩ ataŋ, ta nagɩ haagɩŋ tɩɩŋ nagɩsɩ a taasɩ aŋaŋ jadieke dɩ benne tigiŋ me wo, a taasɩ a bɩrɩŋ jabalɩmɩŋ. Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi sɩɩ sɩba haagɩŋ tɩɩka naŋ, ta taasɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩna dieke dɩ nyɩnna Juu vuosi jigiŋ, ta sɩɩ sɩba haagɩŋ tɩɩka naŋ dieke dɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ javɩɩŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna tigiŋ tɩɩka ma.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Die wɩa nɩ daa yilime dɩ nɩ tɩaŋ naŋ diekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chɩana a taaŋ wa ta a sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tɩɩka nagɩsɩsɩ wa. Nɩ daa kuulime, dama nɩ yine tɩɩŋ naŋ, ta tɩɩka nagɩrɩsɩ dɩ yɩa nɩ suŋŋiŋ, daa nɩ suŋŋinene tɩɩka nagɩrɩsɩsɩ.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Ama nɩ nan baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, Ŋmɩŋ chɩa nagɩsɩ mi a taaŋ mɩŋ ta yi manɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine haagɩŋ naŋka sieŋ.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Ka seŋ yiwo wusie, ba kana ka yi yada wa wɩa ʋ chɩa ba, ta fʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine yada wa wɩa ʋ nagɩ fʋ a taasɩ ka ma. Die wɩa, da kuulime ka wɩa, ama chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Juu vuosi vuodiekemba die dɩ sɩna sɩba tigiŋ tɩɩka, Ŋmɩŋ die datɩ wa ba tɩba, die wɩa nɩ da yilime dɩ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a datɩ nɩ tɩba dɩaŋ.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Tɩ ye Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chɩgɩnana zɔɔlɩŋ ta bɩ tʋa dene. Ʋ tʋa mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi yada, ama ta nan dɩ chɩga nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ dɩɩ yi nɩ yie yada; ama nɩ ka yie yada ʋ nan chɩa nɩ gbaŋ a taaŋ.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Dɩɩ yi Juu vuosisi dɩ tarɩgɩ ba sʋŋanyile ta yiŋŋi a yi Yisa yada, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ba a taasɩ jigidieke die ba wone a beri wo, dama ʋ nan bɩagɩ yi die.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi sɩɩ sɩba haagɩŋ tɩɩŋ nagɩsɩ, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chɩa a taasɩ aŋaŋ tigiŋ tɩɩŋ. Dɩ kaaŋ dɩ hagɩrɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ nagɩsɩ dieke ʋ chɩana a nyɩŋ tigiŋ tɩɩka ma, ta bɩ nagɩ ka taasɩ aŋaŋ ka gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩɩka.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n yaala nɩ sɩmma wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩŋ jaaŋ, ka nan yi aŋ nɩ daa yilime dɩ nɩ yaa sɩbɩŋ: Ka yiwo dɩ Juu vuosi bataŋ yaa sikpeŋ hagɩrɩŋ, dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ die a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke vuodiekemba mana dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ ba dɩ ba keŋ yi Yisa sɩɩtɩ dɩ keŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Die Juu vuosi mana dɩ nan keŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Gbatɩtaantʋ wa nan nyɩŋ Zayɔni tɩŋ ma, a keŋ vʋarɩ Jakobu haagɩsɩ a nyɩŋ ba bɩaŋ mana ma.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: N nan zieŋ nʋarɩ gie aŋaŋ ba, saŋŋa dieke maŋ keŋ vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ ba bɩaŋ ma.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Juu vuosi die dɩ zetine Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha die yine ba yi Ŋmɩŋ dataasɩ, ta die wɩa nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩna Juu vuosi buuriŋ ta cho be wo, die dɩ yiwo ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba wɩa.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: dama dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ vuosi a yɩ ʋ piini, ʋ ka tarɩga ʋ sʋŋanyile.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi die zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa, ama lele ʋ chɩgɩ nɩ wa zɔɔlɩŋ, dama Juu vuosisi dɩ zetine ʋ nʋaŋ wɩa.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Lele Juu vuosisi zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ gbaŋ die nɩ zetine die wo, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ chɩgɩna nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ dene wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nan chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yaala vuosi mana sɩmma dɩ ba yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ zetine ʋ nʋaŋ, amʋ ʋ nan chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nyinti wo kpatɩŋ; ta ʋ yɩaŋ aŋaŋ ʋ sɩbɩŋ wo magɩsɩŋ. Mɩnɩa sɩbɩna Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋanyile ta nan bɩagɩ sɩba ʋ sieti?.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Mɩnɩa sɩbɩna Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋanyile, ta nan bɩagɩ a kpaaŋ wa?.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Mɩnɩa yene yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jaaŋ, ta ʋ mʋ dɩ ʋ yiŋŋi tuŋ wo.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die naana jaaŋ mana ta pɔgɩlɩ a mana ta ʋ wɩa a beri. Nɩ vaa ʋ ye bɩrɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu. Wusie.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Die wɩa, n nɩmballɩ, Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa n jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ nagɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a yɩ wa, aŋ ka yi kaabɩ dieke dɩ yalla miivoli, ta dɩɩ ʋ nyɩɩna ma, die ʋ yaala. Nɩ yi naa, nɩ nan dɩ jɩama wa ʋ yaala nɩ jɩama wa die wo.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Nɩ daa vaa nɩ beriŋ sɩmma sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi beriŋ dɩ sɩna die wo. Nɩ vaa aŋ Ŋmɩŋ tarɩgɩ nɩ sʋŋanyile aŋaŋ wusie, ta wʋnsɩ nɩ beriŋ vɩɩnɩŋ; die nɩ nan dɩ sɩba ta yie wudiekemba dɩ vɩɩna ta fɩala ʋ sʋŋ.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ chɩgɩna mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ wɩa maŋ bala nɩ mana dɩ vuoŋ da yilime dɩ ʋ yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ ʋ sɩna die; Nɩ vaa vuoŋ mana yilime ta sɩmma yada dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna wa magɩsɩŋ.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Vuota nyɩŋgbanɩŋ puoye mɩŋ, ta lʋgɩŋ mana yaa ka tʋʋma, ama ta lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Die gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, sɩba tɩ dala die wo, ama tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Masia dɩ lagɩŋ a yiwo nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ, ta tɩ mana nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa tɩ mana piini yiri yiri mɩŋ dɩ tɩ nagɩ a tʋŋ. Die wɩa, vuoŋ mana nagɩ ʋ piini dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩna wa wa a tʋŋ sɩba ʋ yɩna wa dene. Dɩɩ yi fʋ piini dɩ yi dɩ fʋ yiŋŋi bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, dɩ mʋ dɩ fʋ tʋma fʋ yada dɩ sɩna die.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: ta dɩɩ yi fʋ piinike dɩ yi dɩ fʋ suŋŋime fʋ chanchaalɩŋ, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ fʋ suŋŋi, dɩɩ yi dɩ fʋ dagɩma fʋ chanchaalɩŋ, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ fʋ dagɩ.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Ta dɩ bɩ yi dɩ fʋ kpaŋŋɩsɩma fʋ chanchaalɩŋ, mʋ dɩ fʋ yiwo dene. Dɩɩ yi vuosi suŋŋiŋ, dɩ vɩɩna fʋ suŋŋime be aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana, ta dɩɩ yi fʋ yaa yiko vuosi me, fʋ tʋmma yikoke tʋʋma vɩɩnɩŋ; ta dɩɩ yi fʋ piinike dɩ yi dɩ fʋ chɩgɩma vuosisi zɔɔlɩŋ, dɩ mʋ fʋ chɩgɩma zɔɔlɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Vaa fʋ choti yi wusie choti, ta hama jadieke dɩ yine bɩaŋ, ta mɩŋŋɩ a pɔgɩlɩ jadieke dɩ vɩɩna.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Nɩ chome vɩɩnɩŋ sɩba vuoŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ chone taŋ dene wo, ta yɩma taŋ jɩlɩma.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Nɩ tʋmma aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta daa yallɩma walɩnkpɩasɩ, ta tʋmma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋmaha aŋaŋ nɩ sʋŋ mana.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: ta vaa nɩ tamaka yɩ nɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ta dime suguru nɩ wahala ma, ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: ta vʋarɩ nɩ nyinti a yɩma nɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ wone wo, ta tuosime Ŋmɩŋ vuosi dɩ yine chaanɩŋ nɩ tige me ta yi ba chaantɩ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ yi alibarika a yi vuodiekemba mana dɩ mugisinene nɩ, nɩ yi naa, nɩ daa jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ kakaatɩ a yɩ ba.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ wa, ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Nɩ yallɩma sʋŋanyili balɩmɩŋ, ta daa yallɩma kalinbaani, ama nɩ lagɩma aŋaŋ wabɩtɩ, ta daa yilime dɩ nɩ yaa ninyuoriŋ.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Nɩ daa tʋmma bɩaŋ a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnna bɩaŋ a yɩ nɩ; nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a yi wudieke vuoŋ mana dɩ sɩbɩna dɩ ka vɩɩna.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ mana.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: N nɩmballɩ choti, nɩ daa keŋ a yi bɩaŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yine nɩ bɩaŋ, ama nɩ vaa Ŋmɩŋ tuŋ hanɩ mi. Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Manɩŋ n nyɩɩna ma yalla yiko dɩ n datɩ vuosi tɩba; n balla n tuŋ hanɩ mi, Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ n balla naa.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Ama nɩ yime sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Dɩɩ yi kɔŋ dɩ yallɩ fʋ dataaŋ, yɩ wa nyindiike, dɩɩ yi nyanyuule dɩ yallɩ wa yɩ wa nyaaŋ aŋ ʋ nyuu; dama fʋ yie naa, yi wo wo viivi me.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Nɩ daa vaa bɩaŋ nyaŋŋɩ nɩ, ama nɩ nagɩ tʋnvɩɩna a nyaŋŋɩ bɩaŋ.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Nɩ tuo vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana ka dala ta daa nɩgɩma nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba, ba yada wɩa.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Vuoŋ wʋnyɩ yada nan vaa ʋ dii jaaŋ mana, ama vuodieke yada dɩ kana ka dala wa chʋarɩ wa nɔŋ.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Ama nɩ daa galɩma taŋ ba dine yaa ba kana ka die jaaŋ wɩa, dama Ŋmɩŋ tuo nɩ mana mɩŋ.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Mɩnɩa yine fʋ, fʋ baaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ chʋʋsɩ vuoŋ tʋntʋntʋ? Dɩ yiwo ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo baaŋ balɩ dɩ ʋ tʋʋmaha vɩɩna yaa dɩ ka vɩɩna. Ama ʋ tʋʋma nan dɩ vɩɩna dama ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo nan bɩagɩ a yi aŋ ʋ tʋʋma dɩ vɩɩna.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Vuosi bataŋ yile dɩ daraa ataŋ vɩɩna a tɩaŋ daraa ataŋ, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yile dɩ daaŋ mana vɩɩna mɩŋ. Vuoŋ mana pɔgɩlɩ wudieke ʋ yine yada wa.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yilinene dɩ daaŋ kaanɩ vɩɩna a tɩaŋ daraa a taŋ wa, tʋmma die a yɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jɩlɩma; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ dine nyindiike mana, ʋ gbaŋ ʋ yi die ta yɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jɩlɩma; dama ʋ waasa Ŋmɩŋ ka ma; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ chʋarɩna nyindiikehe a taŋ wa yiye die a yɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jɩlɩma ta ʋ gbaŋ ʋ waasa Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ yaa miivoli yaa tɩ kpi, ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ wɩa daa tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ wɩa.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ yaa miivoli, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩa yine tɩ yaa miivolike; dɩɩ yi ta tɩ kpi, ʋ choti wɩa ta tɩ kpi. Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi tɩ kpi yaa tɩ yaa miivoli tɩ yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Masia die kpiye mɩŋ ta hagɩ kuŋ me dɩ ʋ yi vuodiekemba dɩ yalla miivoli aŋaŋ kunti Yɔmʋtieŋ.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Ta fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ bɩa yine fʋ bala chʋʋsa fʋ nɩmbʋa? Yaa bɩa yine fʋ ka yɩa fʋ nɩmballɩ jɩlɩma? Tɩ mana nan daansɩ keŋ a zie Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa aŋ ʋ dii tɩ sarɩya.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, N senne beri naa, buuriŋ mana nan gbirigi n nɩŋŋa, ta vuoŋ mana nan balɩ dɩ n seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ. Manɩŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ bala naa.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Die wɩa, vuoŋ mana nan keŋ balɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ wuyiike.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ daa chʋʋsɩma tɩ chanchaalɩŋ sara, ama ta sɩmma tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ tɩ kaaŋ yi wudieke dɩ baaŋ vaa tɩ nɩmbʋa nan.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa vaa maŋ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nyindiike wori ta yaa dɩgɩntɩ ta ka vɩɩna aŋaŋ diile. Ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yi yada dɩ nyindiike ataŋ chʋarɩ, die nɩŋ ʋ vaa aŋ ka chʋarɩma ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ nɩmbʋa sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ jadieke fʋ dine wɩa, die nɩŋ ka bɩ tʋma choti tʋʋma. Da vaa nyindiike dieke fʋ dine wo chʋʋsɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa dieke Masia dɩ kpine ʋ wɩa wa.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Jadieke fʋ yilinene dɩ ka vɩɩna wa, daa vaa vuosi balɩ wʋbɩaŋ yaa gamma ka ma.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋbalɩka ka yi diile yaa nyuule wʋbalɩka, ama ka yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩnana wa.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Vuodieke dɩ dɩna Masia die, Ŋmɩŋ nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ wa, ta vuosi nan dɩ yɩa wa jɩlɩma.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ kpaŋŋɩ tɩ gbaŋ a yime wudieke dɩ baaŋ yaa sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ a keŋ aŋaŋ wudieke tɩ baaŋ yi a suŋŋi taŋ.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Nɩ daa keŋ chʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma nyindiike wɩa. Nyindiike mana vɩɩna mɩŋ, ama dɩ ka vɩɩna dɩ fʋ dii nyindiike dieke dɩ baaŋ yi fʋ nɩmbʋa yi bɩaŋ.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Wudieke dɩ mʋna dɩ nɩ yi yiwo naa, dɩ kpɩa dɩ nɩ vaa nɔŋ ŋɔbɩŋ aŋaŋ daaŋ nyuule yaa nɩ baaŋ dɩ yie wudieke dɩ baaŋ yi nɩ nɩmbʋa yi bɩaŋ.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Nɩ yine yada die yaa gamma nyindiike diile die wo, nɩ vaa ka bemme nɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ sʋnsʋŋ. Wudieke vuoŋ dɩ yine ta yile dɩ dɩ vɩɩna ta ka chɩɩlɩya, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ʋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ʋ chɩɩlɩ yaa gamma jadieke ʋ dinene me, ama ta dii, die nɩŋ ʋ yiwo bɩaŋ, dama ʋ ka dii ke aŋaŋ yada. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ ka yi yada dɩ jaaŋ vɩɩna, ama ta keŋ yi die, dɩ yiwo bɩaŋ.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: N zʋamba, dɩɩ yi nɩ die vuosi sarɩya, fʋ sɩɩ die mana kaaŋ bɩagɩ baarɩ ka yi bɩaŋ. Dama dɩɩ yi fʋ die vuosi sarɩya ama ta yie wudieke ba yinene gbaŋ gbaŋ, die nɩŋ, die fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ sarɩya.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan keŋ dii vuodiekemba dɩ yine die sarɩya, ta ʋ sarɩya diilehe yiwo wusie.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Fʋ dagɩnana vuosi dɩ yinene tʋmbɩatɩ dene wo, ama ta yie die, yile dɩ Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ ye ta datɩ fʋ tɩbɩŋ?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Yaa fʋ nine ka suuliye chotikpɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ suguru pam dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla aŋaŋ tɩnɩŋ wa? Ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ wudieke wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chɩgɩna nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ yiwo ʋ yaala nɩ yiŋŋi keŋ ʋ jigiri?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Ama fʋ sikpeŋ hagɩrɩ mɩŋ, die wɩa fʋ ka saagɩ fʋ vaa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tʋʋma, die wɩa gʋta fʋ tɩbɩdatɩ dieke fʋ bala fʋ keŋ a ye daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ bala ka keŋ a nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma, aŋ ʋ keŋ dii vuosi sarɩya aŋaŋ wusie wo.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ nan tuŋ vuoŋ mana yaa gamma ʋ tʋnna dene.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yinene vɩɩnɩŋ ta yaala Ŋmɩŋ bɩrɩ ba ta yɩ ba nyʋarɩ aŋaŋ miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ, banɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan vaa ba bemme yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Ama vuodiekemba dɩ yaala waagɩŋ, ta zeti wusieke, ta yie bɩaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ nan dɩ bie ba ma.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yine vuobɩatɩtɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan datɩ ba tɩbɩŋ aŋ ba dii wahala aŋaŋ mugisiŋ. Juu vuosi bala ba woliŋ ye tɩbɩdatɩkʋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩaŋ wa diisi.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene wudieke dɩ vɩɩna bɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ jɩlɩma aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ, Juu vuosi bala ba woliŋ tuo, ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩaŋ wa diisi;.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ ka luge.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, Ŋmɩŋ nan datɩ ba tɩbɩŋ aŋaŋ wʋbɩa dieke ba yine. Ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ sɩbɩna Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ mɩraha a dii ba sarɩya.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Daa vuodiekemba dɩ wʋnna Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha yine vuovɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ama vuodiekemba dɩ yinene wudieke mɩraha dɩ dagɩna, banɩŋ ba yine vuovɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ka tuo mɩraha a nyɩŋ Mosisi jigiŋ, ama dɩɩ yi ba yie wudieke mɩraha dɩ dagɩna aŋaŋ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ sʋŋanyile, ba yaa ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba mɩraha ba sʋgɩtɩ ma ama die ba ka tuo mɩraha a nyɩŋ Mosisi jigiŋ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Ba yinene wudieke wo daga dɩ ba yaa mɩra ba sʋgɩtɩ ma. Ba sʋŋanyile gbaŋ daga dɩ dɩ yiwo wusie, dama saŋŋa kaanɩ ba sʋŋanyile ŋaaŋ dagɩ ba dɩ wudieke ba yine wo yiwo bɩaŋ, ta saŋŋa kaanɩ dɩaŋ ka daga ba dɩ dɩ vɩɩna mɩŋ.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Naa bala ka yi daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ dii vuota sʋŋ sarɩya Yisa Masia wɩa, sɩba n mʋʋlɩna wʋvɩɩnaha die wo.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Nɩ bataŋ wasa nɩ gbaŋ Juu vuosi, ta yi mɩraha yada, ta nɩga nyʋʋŋ dɩ nɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩka.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: nɩ sɩba wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala nɩ yi, ta nɩ bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ mɩraha ma a sɩba wudieke dɩ vɩɩna;.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: nɩ yi yada dɩ nɩ bɩagɩ dagɩ yɩɩsɩ sieŋ, ta bɩ yi chaanɩŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne lɩmɩŋ ma;.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: ta bɩagɩ kpaama gaantɩŋ, ta bɩ daga vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba, dama nɩ sɩba wusieke dɩ maagɩna die Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Nɩ daga vuosi wusieke, bɩa wɩa nɩ ka daga nɩ gbaŋ? Nɩ mʋʋla dɩ, vuosi da keŋ gaarɩ, ama ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ gaara.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Nɩ daga vuosi dɩ ba da keŋ a tʋŋ daakpana yaa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, ama ta nɩ tʋmaha. Nɩ ka yaala bugile wɩa ama ta gaara bugile jɩamɩŋ juone nyinti.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Nɩ kuule dɩ nɩ yaa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha mɩŋ, ama ta ka dɩɩ ha ta yie Ŋmɩŋ viivi me.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Nɩ Juu vuosi wɩa ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ bala wʋbɩatɩ a chʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, die nɩŋ nɩ kɔla gobiŋ yaa nyʋarɩ, ama dɩɩ yi nɩ ka dɩa mɩraha, ka ka sɩɩ sɩba gobiye.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta ka gobi ba kɔla, ama ta dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha, Ŋmɩŋ nan tuo be sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ gobine ba kɔla.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Nɩnɩŋ Juu vuosi, nɩ yaa mɩraha a maagɩ gbaŋkʋ ma a yɩ nɩ, ta nɩ gobe nɩ kɔla, ama nɩ gbaŋ nɩ chʋʋsɩnana mɩraha; vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka gobe ba kɔla ama ta dɩɩ mɩraha, banɩŋ ba bala ba dii nɩ sarɩya ta baarɩ nɩ chʋʋsɩya.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Daa fʋ benne sɩba Juu vuoke ta ba gobi fʋ kɔlɩ yine fʋ Juu vuoŋ wusie.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Juu vuoŋ wusie yiwo vuodieke dɩ dɩna mɩraha. Wusie gobiŋ bie sʋŋ ma, ta kanɩŋ gobiku mi dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka tʋʋma, daa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma. Wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi yese bɩrɩŋ a nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, daa vuota jigiŋ.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ hagɩrɩna wa, mʋ tɩ suŋŋi wo vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana ka dala wa, ta daa tʋmma tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ choti nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ mana baga bemme tɩ nɩmballɩ ma ta suŋŋi be aŋ ba dɩa Yisa Masia sieku vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Wʋnɩŋ Masia die ka yaala ʋ yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ choti. Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Vuodiekemba dɩ zanana fʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ, zɩa wa manɩŋ.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Wudieke mana dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma wa, maagɩya mɩŋ dɩ tɩ bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ ka ma. Ʋ gbaŋkʋ yɩa tɩ wa suguru ta bɩ yɩa tɩ kpaadensiŋ dɩ tɩ yallɩma tama.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana suguru aŋaŋ kpaadensiŋ yɩ nɩ sʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ sɩba Yisa Masia dɩ yaala die wo.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: amʋ nɩ mana nan lagɩsɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia Chɔɔŋ wa.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Nɩ tuosime taŋ sɩba Masia dɩ tuone nɩ dene wo amʋ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ nan ye bɩrɩŋ.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Dama Masia die bɩrɩŋ wa Juu vuosisi tʋntʋntʋ, dɩ ʋ dagɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo wusietieŋ, ta vaa nʋa diekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba keŋ yi wusie.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: ta bɩ vaa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha wɩa. Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ Davidi die baarɩ dɩ: N nan bɩrɩ fʋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi jigiŋ; ta chɩa yɩla a bɩrɩ fʋ saaŋ.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Dɩ bɩ maagɩ dɩ: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Ka bɩ maagɩ dɩ: Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi mana bɩrɩma Ŋmɩŋ, buuriŋ mana bɩrɩma wa.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Azaya gbaŋ die maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ: Jeesi haagɩŋ nan daansɩ keŋ, ʋ nan keŋ daansa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi, ta ba nan dɩ yaa tama ʋ ma.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩna nɩ tamaka, nɩ yine wo yada wɩa, suuli nɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ amʋ nɩ tamaka nan dɩ gʋta aŋaŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ wɩa.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n sɩba n sʋŋ ma dɩ nɩ beriŋ vɩɩna, ta nɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha vɩɩnɩŋ ta bɩagɩ kpaama taŋ.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: N maagɩnana gbaŋkʋ n dii wo dembisi dɩ n tɩɩnsɩ nɩ nɩ wɩa a taŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ die chɩgɩ wa n zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: ta vaa maŋ yi Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ, dɩ n mʋʋlɩma wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi, ta ba yi sɩba piini a yɩa wa, ta ʋ Halɩkasɩka nan vaa ba yi vuovɩɩna ta fɩala ʋ sʋŋ.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Yisa Masia wɩa n nan bɩagɩ kuule aŋaŋ n tʋnnana tʋŋ dieke a yɩa Ŋmɩnnɩ.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: N yaala n dagɩ nɩ tʋŋ dieke Masia dɩ vana maŋ yi, ama n kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ yaa gamma vuogaasɩ tʋʋma ma, ʋ vaa maŋ balɩ ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi, ta tʋŋ mamachi tʋnkpɩɩma diekemba dɩ vana ba yi yada.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ die vana maŋ tʋŋ mamachi tʋʋmaha gie, die wɩa n dene nyɩŋ Jerusalemi a dɩ dɩa tɩgɩsɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ Yilirikumi tɩŋ ma wa, ta die a mʋʋlɩ Masia wʋvɩɩnaha jigiŋ mana.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Die wɩa, die n dɩ dɩa mɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta mʋʋla Masia wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka wʋŋya; n ka yaala n ga jigidieke vuoŋ dɩ wone a mʋʋlɩ wɩaha.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ; Vuodiekemba ba yene ka mʋʋlɩ ʋ wɩaha a yɩ ba nan ye ke, ta vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka wʋŋ ʋ wɩaha nan sɩba a chɩasɩ.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Wɩa pam die kagɩna mɩŋ sieŋ maŋ ka bɩagɩ keŋ a kaagɩ nɩ.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Ama lele n kpatɩna n tʋʋmaha tɩŋgbaŋka gie chaakʋ naa, ta dɩ yi bɩna pam wʋnna n yaalala dɩ n keŋ a kaagɩ nɩ.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: ama lele n nan kaagɩ nɩ, maŋ kaagɩ nɩ kʋaŋ chaaŋ dɩ kaaŋ yʋasɩ pam, n nan dɩ yaala dɩ nɩ suŋŋi mɩŋ aŋ n ga Speeni tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Ama lele nɩŋ, n gara Jerusalemi dɩ n ga suŋŋi Ŋmɩŋ vuosi mi.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Dama Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba dɩ benne Masedonia aŋaŋ Akaya tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ ma wa saŋya mɩŋ dɩ ba chɩasɩ ligire a suŋŋi Ŋmɩŋ vuosi diekemba dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma ta yi zɔɔlɩntielibe.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ, dɩ yiwo ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba choti ba yɩa ba piinike mi. Dɩ vɩɩna dɩ ba yɩ ba, dama Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha die nyɩŋ wa Jerusalemi vuosi jigiŋ a keŋ ba jigiŋ. Die wɩa dɩ vɩɩna dɩ ba yɩ ba piinisi gie.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Maŋ keŋ Jerusalemi a nagɩ piinisi a yɩ ba n nan nyɩŋ mi a ga Speeni tɩŋgbaŋ ma ta kaagɩ nɩ maŋ keŋ gara mi.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: N sɩba a baarɩ maŋ keŋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ, Masia suŋŋiŋ nan dɩ bie tɩ ma pam.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: N nɩmballɩ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka choti wɩa, nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna n wɩa ta suŋŋi mɩŋ.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ kagɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Yisa Masia yada ta bie Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa nuusi me, ta bɩ jʋʋsɩma dɩ piini dieke n bala n nagɩ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuosisi, ba nan tuoke aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ mɩŋ naa, ʋ saagɩ, n nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ta keŋ voosi bɩta nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana tɩ sʋgɩdʋakʋ bemme aŋaŋ nɩ mana. Wusie.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: N yaala n balɩ nɩ yaa gamma tɩ taa Fiibi wɩa, ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ yine Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba susuŋŋiru Senkiria tɩŋ ma.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: Ʋ keŋ keŋ nɩ mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ ma sɩba Ŋmɩŋ vuosi dɩ mʋna ba tuo chaanʋ dene. Ta suŋŋi wo ʋ yaalɩnana suŋŋi dieke mana a nyɩna nɩ jigiŋ, dama ʋ suŋŋi wo vuosi pam, aŋaŋ n gbaŋ.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: N waasa Pirisila aŋaŋ ʋ chʋrʋ Akuyila, vuodiekemba dɩ yine n chanchaalɩŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ Yisa Masia tʋʋma ma.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Die ba nagɩ wa ba mɩsɩ a gbatɩ n miivoli, die wɩa maŋ waasa ba, daa n nyɩɩna ma waasɩnana ba, ama manɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ama ta yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba wa.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: N bɩ waasa Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba dɩ lagɩnanna ba tigiri me ta jɩama Ŋmɩnnɩ. Ta waasa n zʋavɩɩnʋ Epanetusi, vuodieke die dɩ wolinne a yi Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Ta waasa Meri, vuodieke die dɩ faasɩna a tʋŋ a yɩ nɩ;.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: ta waasa Andironikusi aŋaŋ Juniasi vuodiekemba dɩ yine n buuriŋ, ta die maŋ ŋaŋ ba die dɩ lagɩŋ a bie dansarɩka ma wa; ta Masia tʋntʋntɩba dɩ sɩba vɩɩnɩŋ, banɩŋ ba wolinne a yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ n ma.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Ta waasa n zʋavɩɩnʋ Ampiliatusi, vuodieke n chone tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sieŋ me.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Ta bɩ waasa Hubanusi, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ chanchaaŋ tʋntʋntʋ Masia tʋʋmaha ma, aŋaŋ n zʋavɩɩnʋ Sitakisi.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Ta waasa Apilisi, vuodieke dɩ yene magɩsɩŋ ama ta bɩ dɩ Masia aŋaŋ wusie wo, ta waasa Arisitobulusi tigiŋ vuosi mana.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Ta bɩ waasa Herodoni, vuodieke dɩ yine n buuriŋ, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Nasisusi tigiŋ me wo ta yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba wa.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Ta bɩ waasa Tirifaana aŋaŋ Tirifosa, vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnnana tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋmaha, aŋaŋ n zʋavɩɩnʋ Pesisi, vuodieke dɩ faasɩna a tʋŋ tʋʋma a yɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: N bɩ waasa Rufusi, vuodieke dɩ faasɩna a tʋma a yɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ, vuodieke dɩ nagɩna mɩŋ a bɩrɩŋ ʋ bʋa wa.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Ta waasa Asikiritusi aŋaŋ Feligoni aŋaŋ Hemisi aŋaŋ Patorobasi aŋaŋ Hemasi aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba mana dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mi wo.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: N bɩ waasa Filologusi aŋaŋ Julia aŋaŋ Nerusi aŋaŋ ʋ taa wa aŋaŋ Olimpasi aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ bene ba jigiri.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Nɩ waasɩ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ. Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne jigiŋ mana waasa nɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: N nɩmballɩ n kpaama nɩ mɩŋ, dɩ nɩ sɩmma vuodiekemba dɩ puonene Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta kaga ba ʋ sieŋ me, ta ka bɩ dɩ dagɩ dieke nɩ tuone wo, nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ gbaŋ ta va ba.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Dama vuosi mi ka jɩama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Masia ama ba dɩ wa ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba lɔla. Ba yaa nʋanansɩŋ a gaama vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba wa.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Vuoŋ mana wʋŋya mɩŋ nɩ dɩna Masia wʋvɩɩnaha dene, die wɩa n yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam aŋaŋ nɩ. Ta yaala nɩ yallɩma yɩaŋ yaa gamma jadieke dɩ vɩɩna ma, ama nɩ da tuosime jabɩaŋ.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Dɩ kaaŋ yʋasɩ aŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana tɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ wa nan vaa aŋ nɩ tɩbɩ tɩbɩ Sitaani sikpeŋ me. Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: N tʋntʋŋ chanchaalɩŋ Timoti waasa nɩ; ta n nɩmballɩ Lususi aŋaŋ Jasoni aŋaŋ Sosipata dɩaŋ dɩ waasa nɩ.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Manɩŋ Tesusi vuodieke dɩ maagɩna wudieke Pɔɔli dɩ bala naa waasa nɩ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa saaŋ ma.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gayusi, vuodieke dɩ yine manɩŋ Pɔɔli tigiŋ tieŋ wo, ta Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ jɩama ʋ tigiri me gbaŋ waasa nɩ. Erasitusi, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩka gie ligire gbɩgbarɩtʋ wa, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmbʋa Kositusi gbaŋ waasa nɩ. .

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ, dama ʋ bɩagɩna vaa nɩ ye yigi nɩ yadaka a yallɩ keŋ keŋ, aŋaŋ die n mʋʋlɩna wʋvɩɩna yaa gamma Yisa Masia ma wa, ka yuori a dagɩ nɩ wusie dieke die dɩ lɔbɩrɩna wa bɩna bɩna die dɩ tɩanna wa.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die tuohe mɩŋ a maagɩha a yi gbanɩŋ ma ta yuori wusieke; ta Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ benne saŋŋa mana wa dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ buuriŋ mana nan dɩ sɩba ka, amʋ ba nan yi Masia yada ta dɩɩ ʋ nʋaŋ.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ nyɩɩna ma ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ ta yi yɩantieŋ, vuoŋ mana bɩrɩma wa yaa gamma haahuu, Yisa Masia saaŋ ma. Wusie.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Vuoŋ mana mʋ ʋ tuosime tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋraha nʋa; dama vuoŋ wori ta yaa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yiko ta ka ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Ŋmɩŋ yine vuodiekemba dɩ yalla yikoke dɩ yallɩ yiko.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Die wɩa, vuodieke dɩ kana ka tuose nyɩŋkʋraha nʋa zeti wo wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine, ta banɩŋ vuosi mi nan ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Dɩ yi wo vuodiekemba dɩ yinene bɩabʋ chɩgɩnana tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩŋkʋraha ŋmaamɩŋ, ama vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yie bɩabʋ ka chɩga ba ŋmaamɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi fʋ ka yaala fʋ chɩgɩma jakʋʋŋ ŋmaamɩŋ die nɩŋ yime wudieke dɩ vɩɩna ta ʋ nan bɩrɩ fʋ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Dama ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ a tʋma wudieke dɩ vɩɩna a yɩa nɩ, ama dɩɩ yi fʋ yie bɩaŋ, die nɩŋ chɩgɩma wa, dama ʋ yaa sieŋ dɩ ʋ datɩ fʋ tɩbɩŋ. Ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ ta zie Ŋmɩŋ naŋ ma a data vuodiekemba dɩ yinene bɩaŋ tɩbɩŋ.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Die wɩa, dɩ vɩɩna nɩ tuosime nyɩŋkʋraha nʋa, daa nɩ faasɩna a chɩga tɩbɩdatɩŋ wɩa, ama dɩ yiwo wudieke dɩ vɩɩna.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Nɩ mʋ nɩ tumme lampo dama nyɩŋkʋraha yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ, ta dɩ yi ba tʋʋma dɩ ba nagɩ lampoke a mɩŋŋɩ daansɩma tɩŋgbaŋka.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ yɩ ba jadieke dɩ yine ba sɩɩtɩ, dɩɩ yi saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ dɩ nɩ tuŋ vuota lampo, yaa nyindiekemba dɩ mʋna dɩ fʋ tuŋ a lampo; ta yɩma ba mana jɩlɩma ta jɩama ba.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Nɩ daa yallɩma vuoŋ hamɩŋ. Hamɩŋ dieke nɩ mʋna nɩ yallɩma yine, nɩ chome taŋ. Vuodieke dɩ chone ʋ chanchaalɩŋ, ʋ yiwo wudiekemba mana mɩraha dɩ dagɩna wa.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Dama mɩraha dagɩya dɩ, Da keŋ tʋŋ hɔgʋkpana yaa daakpana tʋʋma, da keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ; da keŋ a gaarɩ; da keŋ yaalɩma lɔla aŋaŋ fʋ chanchaaŋ jaaŋ ta mɩraha ataŋ dɩ tɩalɩ a gʋtɩ naa ma ta a mana dɩ lagɩŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ chone fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die wo.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ cho fʋ chanchaaŋ, kaaŋ yiwo bɩaŋ; dama fʋ yaa choti dɩ dagɩya dɩ dɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha mana.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Dɩ mʋ wa nɩ yime naa dama nɩ sɩba saŋŋa dieke tɩ benne wo dɩ sɩna dene. Saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ dɩ nɩ hagɩ nɩ gbɩɩŋ ma. Dama tɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ, ka ka yʋa sɩba die tɩ piiline a yi Yisa Masia yada wa.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Yuku tɩanna mɩŋ, ta tʋŋ dɩ vʋʋna. Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ vaa lɩmɩŋ ma tʋʋmaha, ta nagɩ tɩ chaanɩŋ ma tɔŋ taala nyinti.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Nɩ vaa aŋ tɩ tʋʋma vɩɩŋ ma sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ benne chaanɩŋ ma tʋʋma, ta da nyume daaŋ a bugime, yaa a tʋmma hɔgʋkpana yaa daakpana tʋʋma, yaa ta tʋmma dɩgɩntɩ tʋʋma, yaa a tʋmma tʋntʋmbɩatɩ yiri yiri, yaa ta waga waagɩŋ yaa ta yaa nɩnŋmaarɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia kagɩ nɩ, ama ta vaa nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ lɔlɩbɩatɩ, ta da yilime a wɩa.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Manɩŋ Pɔɔli vuodieke Yisa Masia die dɩ wana dɩ n yi ʋ tʋntʋntʋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ choti ma, manɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmbʋa Sosenesi maagɩnana gbaŋkʋ gie.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: a yɩa nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna Ŋmɩŋ a bie Korinti tɩŋ ma, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ nɩ yi ʋ vuokasɩmba ba, ta yi ʋ sɩɩtɩ a lagɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne jige mana ta jɩama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine ba Yɔmʋtieŋ, ta bɩ yi tɩnɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ ta bɩ yɩ nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Manɩŋ n waasa Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa mana nɩ wɩa aŋaŋ ʋ chɩgɩna nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ ta suŋŋe nɩ Yisa Masia wɩa.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: dama nɩ yese jaaŋ mana Yisa Masia ma mɩŋ, ta mɩŋŋɩ bala wɩa ta bɩ mɩŋŋɩ sɩba wudieke mana ʋ bala nɩ chɩasɩ.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Masia wudieke die tɩ dagɩna nɩ, nɩ mɩŋŋɩ tuohe mɩŋ a pɔgɩlɩ.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Die wɩa nɩ ka waarɩ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka piini kaanɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ nɩ gbarɩna aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna daa dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia dɩ bala ʋ keŋ wo.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ʋ nan mɩŋŋɩ gbarɩ nɩ amʋ nɩ nan dɩ hagɩrɩ a ga tʋgɩ daa dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ wo ta nɩ kaaŋ dɩ yallɩ wʋbɩaŋ nɩ ma.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baarɩ ʋ nan yi, kanɩŋ ʋ yie. Ʋ vʋarɩna nɩ dɩ nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ Bʋa Yisa Masia yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ a bemme.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n jʋʋsa nɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma dɩ nɩ mana lagɩŋ aŋaŋ nʋabalɩmɩŋ, ta nɩnhagɩrɩŋ daa bemme nɩ sʋnsʋŋ, ta nɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋŋanyilibalɩmɩŋ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: N nɩmballɩ, Kilowo tigiŋ vuosi balɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yallɩ wa nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Ba balɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ bataŋ baarɩ ba dɩ wa manɩŋ, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ba dɩ wa Apolosi, bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ba dɩ wa Piita, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ba dɩ wa Masia.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Masia wa puo puo mɩŋ? Die ba kpaasɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dagarɩkʋ ma nɩ wɩa? Yaa die ba sɩwa nɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ manɩŋ Pɔɔli saaŋ ma?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: N waasa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ n ka sɩɩ nɩ wʋnyɩ mana Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ ntaala Kirisipusi aŋaŋ Gayusi.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Die wɩa nɩ wʋnyɩ mana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a baarɩ ba sɩɩ nɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ n saaŋ ma dɩ nɩ dɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
local: N bɩ sɩwa Sitifanasi aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ama n ka bɩ tɩɩnsɩ dɩ n bɩ sɩɩ vuoŋ bɩbra.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Dama Masia ka tʋŋ mɩŋ dɩ n keŋ sɩma vuosi nyaabʋ; Ʋ tʋŋ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ n keŋ mʋʋlɩma ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩma vuosi. Daa n nagɩma vuota sɩbɩŋ a balɩma yɩma ba; die maŋ tɩŋ yie die, dɩ tɩŋ nan yi Masia vuodieke dɩ kpine dagarɩkʋ ma wa kumbu waarɩ ka hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ bana Ŋmɩŋ sieku banɩŋ ba yile dɩ Masia dagarɩkʋ ma kumbu yiwo wʋtɔgʋ; ama tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yene gbatɩtaaŋkʋ sɩba a baarɩ ka yiwo Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, N nan chʋʋsɩ yɩantieliŋ sɩbɩŋ ta vaa sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ chɔrɩŋ.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Die wɩa, tɩŋgbaŋka gie yɩantieliŋ aŋaŋ sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩ nɩgɩrɩba bie wo sɩa? Ŋmɩŋ vaa wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie yɩantieliŋ dɩ yi gaantɩŋ.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yalla yɩabʋ yine tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi yɩaŋ dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ yi ta vuosi sɩmma wa. Die wɩa ʋ yi vuodiekemba dɩ yine Yisa yada wa dɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, dama wudiekemba tɩ mʋʋlɩnana a yɩa vuosi ta vuosi dɩ bala dɩ a yiwo gaantɩ wʋbalɩkaha wɩa.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Juu vuosi yaala ba ye mamachi wɩa ta ye yi yada; ta Giriiki vuosi dɩaŋ dɩ yaala yɩaŋ wɩa.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Ama tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ mʋʋla ta daga ba Yisa dɩ kpine die dagarɩkʋ ma, ta wɩɩrɩ gie dɩ yie Juu vuosisi sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ haga, ta bɩ yi wʋtɔgʋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Ama Juu vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ ba wa nɩŋ sɩba a baarɩ Masia kumbu wɩaha yiwo Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ yɩaŋ.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine ta vuota dɩ nagɩka dɩ ka yiwo gaaŋ wuyiikiŋ, kanɩŋ ka yaa yɩaŋ a tɩaŋ vuota yɩaŋ; ta wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine ta vuota dɩ yile dɩ ka wo hagɩrɩŋ, ama ka yaa hagɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ vuota hagɩrɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ yilime die nɩ sɩna die saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wa nɩ. Vuota jigiŋ die nɩ vuosi bɩta die yalla yɩaŋ ta die yi vuokpɩɩma yaa a yi naballɩ.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa gaantɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi jigiŋ, dɩ ʋ yi aŋ yɩantieliŋ dii viivi; ta bɩ vʋarɩ wabɩtɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi jigiŋ, dɩ ʋ yi aŋ hagɩrɩtieliŋ dii viivi.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie wabɩtɩ, aŋaŋ vuotɔgɩtɩ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi jaaŋ, dɩ ʋ yi wudieke vuosi dɩ yilinene dɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ wa yɔrɩ.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: Naa daga dɩ vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a kuuli Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ taasɩna tɩ aŋaŋ Yisa Masia ta ʋ yine tɩ yɩaŋ. Ʋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma, ta baarɩ tɩ yiwo vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ, ta bɩ yi ʋ vuosi.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Die wɩa nɩ yime wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yaalɩnana ʋ kuuli, ʋ kuulime yaa gamma wudieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yine.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: N nɩmballɩ, die n kenne nɩ jigiri die n ka mʋʋla Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha a yɩa nɩ aŋaŋ nʋanansɩŋ yaa vuota sɩbɩŋ.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Dama saŋŋa dieke die n benne aŋaŋ nɩ wa, die n wo sʋŋanyile a gʋtɩ Yisa Masia aŋaŋ ʋ dagarɩkʋ ma kumbu wɩa.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: Die wɩa die n kenne nɩ jigiri, die n bɩrɩŋ wa wabʋ ta cheele aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ.
