eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: ta die n ka dagɩ ta mʋʋlɩ wɩaha aŋaŋ vuota sɩbɩŋ die dɩ baaŋ nan yi vuosi nansɩŋ ta ba ye tuoke, ama die n mʋʋlɩya mɩŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ dagɩ aŋaŋ ka hagɩrɩŋ dɩ a yiwo wusie.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Die wɩa dɩ ka yi vuota sɩbɩŋ vana nɩ yi Yisa Masia yada ama dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Wusie, tɩ bala aŋaŋ yɩaŋ a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha. Ama daa tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi yɩaŋ yaa tɩŋgbaŋka gie naakpɩɩma, vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba waarɩ ba hagɩrɩbʋ.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Yɩaŋ dieke n balala naa yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yɩaŋ lɔbɩrɩkɩŋ ʋ nagɩna a lɔbɩrɩ vuota, yɩaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ wolinne a vʋarɩ ta ye ka naaŋ dʋnɩa dɩ ka nan vaa tɩ ye jɩlɩma ka.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Tɩŋgbaŋka gie naakpɩɩmaha wʋnyɩ wori a ye sɩba yɩabʋ gie. Ba tɩŋ sɩbaka ba tɩŋ kaaŋ kpaasɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dagarɩkʋ ma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ sɩna bɩrɩŋ mana.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ: Jadieke vuota dɩ yene ka yeye yaa ta wʋŋ, ta ka bɩ yile dɩ ka nan bɩagɩ a yi, kanɩŋ ka yine jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine siri a zieŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chone wo.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Kanɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yi tɩ sɩbaka, dama Halɩkasɩka sɩba jaaŋ mana aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋlɔbɩrɩka wɩa gbaŋ.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Vuota haalɩbʋ dieke dɩ benne ʋ sʋkʋ kasɩŋ sɩbɩna vuota sʋŋanyile. Die gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, vuoŋ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋanyile sie Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka kasɩŋ.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Haalɩbʋ dieke tɩ tuone wo, ʋ ka yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie haalɩbʋ, ama ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dieke dɩ baaŋ nan vaa tɩ sɩmma piini dieke ʋ yɩna tɩ wa.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ ka bala wudieke dɩ yine vuota yɩaŋ dagɩŋ; ama tɩ bala wudieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ dagɩna tɩ, ta bɩ daga Ŋmɩŋ wusieke a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yalla Halɩkasɩka.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ wone Halɩkasɩka nɩŋ, die vuoke ka tuose Halɩkasɩka wɩaha, dama ka yiwo wʋtɔgʋ a yɩ wa ta ʋ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ, dama Halɩkasɩka suŋŋinene vuosi ba sɩba a chɩasɩ.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ yalla Halɩkasɩka sɩba jaaŋ mana dɩ sɩna dene, ama vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ sɩba ʋ sɩna dene.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Sɩba a maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die wo, dɩ Mɩnɩa baaŋ nan bɩagɩ mɩŋŋɩ Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋanyile ta kpaaŋ wa? Ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ sɩba Yisa Masia sʋŋanyile mɩŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: N nɩmballɩ, ka yiwo wusie, die n ka bɩagɩ a balɩ a yɩ nɩ sɩba n tɩŋ nan balɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla Halɩkasɩka, ama die n balɩ a yɩ nɩ sɩba n ŋaana bala a yɩa tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi die wo, ta nɩ beriŋ sɩɩ sɩba ballɩ beriŋ Masia sieŋ me.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Biisiŋ die maŋ yɩ nɩ daa nyindiiki dʋnsa, dama die nɩ ka tʋgɩ die. Leleke gie gbaŋ nɩ ka tʋgɩ die.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: dama nɩ beriŋ ye sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi beriŋ; dama dɩɩ yi sʋgɩbɩaŋ aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ bie nɩ sʋnsʋŋ, daa tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi beriŋ nɩ dɩ?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ dɩ wa Pɔɔli, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ dɩ wa Apolosi, die nɩŋ nɩ ka beri wo sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Manɩa yine Apolosi, ta manɩa yine Pɔɔli? Tɩ mana ko yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ diekemba die dɩ suŋŋine nɩ nɩ yi Yisa yada. Tɩ wʋnyɩ mana tʋma tʋŋ dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yɩnana wa dɩ ʋ tʋŋ.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Manɩŋ die n bʋrɩna nyɩnbʋrɩkaha, ta Apolosi dɩ jaasɩha nyaaŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ die vana a nyuuŋ ta bɩrɩŋ.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Vuodieke dɩ bʋrɩnana aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ jasɩnana nyaaŋ ka yi wɩɩŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ yine wɩɩŋ, dama wʋnɩŋ ʋ vana a bɩrɩŋ.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Jaaŋ wori a puo vuodieke dɩ bʋrɩnana aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ jasɩnana nyaaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan tʋŋ ba wʋnyɩ mana yaa gamma ʋ tʋnna mʋ dene.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Dama tɩ lagɩsɩ tʋma mɩŋ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ. Nɩ sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ kʋaŋ, ta bɩ sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ juoŋ.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: N nagɩ wa sɩbɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna mɩŋ wa, manɩŋ sɩɩ sɩba mɩmɩɩrɩvɩɩnʋ wa, a piili a dʋaŋ juoku nyabɩlɩŋ, ta vuogaaŋ dɩ keŋ mɩa ka sikpeŋ. Ama vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ keŋ mɩa ka sikpeŋ ʋ sɩmma ʋ mɩnana die.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Ŋmɩŋ wɔŋ nagɩ wa Yisa Masia a yi juoku nyabɩlɩŋ, ta nyabɩlɩŋ bɩ wori ta vuoŋ dɩ baaŋ piili a dʋaŋ.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Vuosi bataŋ nan nagɩ salɩma yaa kʋʋtɩ yaa tannyɩgɩsa a mɩa juoku chɩaka ba dʋana wa sikpeŋ; bataŋ dɩaŋ nan nagɩ daatɩ yaa hʋʋtɩ yaa kʋnchɩaga a kpii ka ma.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Masia keniŋ daarɩ ta vuoŋ mana tʋʋma dɩ sɩna die nan daansɩ a nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma. Boliŋ bala ka magɩsɩ vuoŋ mana tʋʋma ta yuori dagɩ ka sɩna dene.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta bolibu ka dii jadieke vuoke dɩ mɩna a zieŋ juoku nyabɩlɩkʋ ma wa, die nɩŋ ʋ nan ye tune.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Vuodieke jaaŋ dɩ dine bolibu, ʋ kaaŋ ye ka tune, ama ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ ama ka nan sɩɩ sɩba ʋ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ wa boliŋ me.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Nɩ mana sɩba a baarɩ nɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ juoŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka bie nɩ ma, daa die?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke dɩ chʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ juoku, Ŋmɩŋ nan chʋʋsɩ die vuoke. Dama Ŋmɩŋ juoŋ yiwo kasɩ, ta nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine kanɩŋ juoku mi.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Vuoŋ daa gaama ʋ gbaŋ. Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ bie nɩ ma a yile dɩ ʋ yiwo yɩantieŋ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ yilinene die, dɩ vɩɩna ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ gaamʋ amʋ ʋ nan seŋ bɩrɩŋ yɩantieŋ.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Jadieke tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ yilinene dɩ ka yiwo yɩaŋ, ka yine wʋtɔgʋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, sɩba ka maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die wo, dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ yige yɩantieliŋ ba yɩaŋ wuyiike me.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: ta bɩ maagɩ jigiŋ kaanɩ bɩbra dɩ, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩba dɩ yɩantielibe sʋŋanyile yiwo yɔrɩ.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Die wɩa, vuoŋ daa keŋ kuulime yaa gamma wudieke vuota dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ yi. Jaaŋ mana yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: manɩŋ Pɔɔli aŋaŋ Apolosi aŋaŋ Piita aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie aŋaŋ miivoli aŋaŋ kuŋ, aŋaŋ saŋka gie aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ wo mana yiwo nɩ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Ta Masia dɩaŋ dɩ sɩɩ nɩ, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ sɩɩ Masia.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Die wɩa nɩ nagɩ tɩ a yi Masia tʋntʋntʋ diekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna tɩ ta nagɩ ʋ wusie wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩrɩ wʋbalɩkaha a yi tɩ nuusi me.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Die wɩa, wudieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna wʋnna, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ tʋntʋntʋ tʋma aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Ama n baga wori dɩɩ yi nɩ die mɩŋ sarɩya yaa vuoŋ dɩ dii n sarɩya sɩba vuota dɩ ŋaana die ba chanchaalɩŋ sarɩya die wo; dama n ka dii n gbaŋ gbaŋ n sarɩya.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: N sʋŋ yɩantɩya mɩŋ, ama dɩ ka dagɩ sɩba n ka tʋma chʋʋsa, ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yine vuodieke dɩ dinene n sarɩya.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ da dime vuoŋ sarɩya lele gie, ama nɩ chɩsɩma saŋŋa dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ wo, wʋnɩŋ ʋ bala ʋ nagɩ jadieke dɩ benne lɩmɩŋ ma a dagɩ yaalɩŋ ma, ta yuori sʋgɩtɩ sʋŋ wɩa yaalɩŋ ma; saŋka mi vuoŋ mana nan ye ʋ bɩrɩŋ dɩ sɩna die Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ wɩa maŋ nagɩ n gbaŋ aŋaŋ Apolosi a yi dagɩtɩ amʋ nɩ nan bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ tɩ jigiŋ amʋ nɩ nan dɩ sɩba ba keŋ baarɩ dɩ, Da keŋ yi poli wudieke dɩ maagɩna wa chɩaŋ. Ta nɩ wʋnyɩ mana da keŋ kɔtɩma ʋ gbaŋ vuoŋ wʋnyɩ wɩa a tɩaŋ vuogaaŋ.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Manɩa yɩna nɩ nyɩŋkʋrɩtɩ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sʋŋ? Da Ŋmɩŋ yɩna nɩ jadieke mana nɩ yalla wa? Ta dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yɩna nɩ, bɩa wɩa nɩ kuule sɩba ka ka yiwo piini?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Nɩ wɔŋ ye jadieke mana nɩ yaalala mɩŋ? Nɩ wɔŋ yiwo nyintitieliŋ? Nɩ yile dɩ nɩ wɔŋ yiwo naalɩŋ ta vaa tɩ? Dɩ tɩŋ nan yi mɩŋ nansɩŋ pam dɩɩ yi nɩ seŋ yi naalɩŋ amʋ tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ tɩŋ nan lagɩŋ a yi naara.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Manɩŋ n jigiŋ n yile dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ a vaa tɩ yi wabɩtɩ, ta vaa tɩ sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba ba bala ba kʋʋ yaalɩŋ ma, ta malakasi aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ vʋara tɩ fala.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Tɩ yiwo gaantɩŋ Masia wɩa, ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a yi yɩantieliŋ Masia sieŋ me. Tɩnɩŋ tɩ yiwo wabɩtɩ ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a yi hagɩrɩtieliŋ, ta vuosi dɩ yɩa nɩ jɩlɩma ama ta tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ ba nine ka suuli tɩ.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Saŋŋa kaanɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, tɩ dɩ dɩa mɩŋ aŋaŋ kɔŋ aŋaŋ nyanyuule ta bɔba gachɔɔtɩ, ta ba nɩga tɩ, ta tɩ dɩ dɩa ta wo jigiberisikiŋ.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Tɩ faasɩ tʋma mɩŋ a die a nyɩna tɩ walɩŋ ma, ba keŋ zɩa tɩ, tɩ ŋaaŋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa wʋvɩɩna a yɩ ba, ta ba keŋ mugise tɩ tɩ ŋaaŋ dii wo suguru;.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: ba balɩ a chʋʋsɩ tɩ saara, tɩ ŋaaŋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba aŋaŋ choti. Tɩ bɩrɩŋ wa wabɩtɩ, ta sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka taŋgɔɔŋ jagɩma, ta bɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie dɩgɩntɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: N ka maaga naa a yɩa nɩ dɩ n yi nɩ viivi, ama n maaga dɩ n kpaaŋ nɩ nɩ yine n ballɩchoti wɩa.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Nɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ Masia wa wɩa, dɩɩ yi ta nɩ yallɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ tuse baŋ, ama nɩ yallɩ wa chɔɔŋ balɩmɩŋ dama Yisa Masia ma maŋ bɩrɩŋ nɩ chɔɔŋ dama manɩŋ n nagɩna wʋvɩɩnaha a keŋ balɩ nɩ.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: N jʋʋsa nɩ, nɩ dɩa n beriŋ.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Wɩɩrɩ gie wɩa maŋ tʋma Timoti nɩ jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine n bʋachoti ta yi wusietieŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩa ma; ʋ nan tɩɩnsɩ nɩ yaa gamma n beriŋ Masia ma, aŋaŋ wudieke n dɩna dɩ dɩa a daga Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ mana.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Nɩ bataŋ wa yaa wa kalinbaani, ta yile dɩ n kaaŋ keŋ a kaagɩ nɩ.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yɩ mɩŋ sieŋ, n nan keŋ kaagɩ nɩ daraa ale wo gie nʋaŋ, ta ye kalinbaantielibe gie, daa ba wʋbalɩka wɩa, ama n yaala n ye dɩ ba seŋ yaa hagɩrɩŋ yaa ba ka yallɩ.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ka yiwo nʋaŋ wʋbalɩka, ama ka yiwo hagɩrɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Bɩa nɩ yaala? Nɩ yaala n keŋ nɩ jigiŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna yaa n keŋ sɔmm aŋaŋ choti.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Ka seŋ yiwo wusie ba bala dɩ dɩgɩntɩ bie nɩ ma, wudieke vuodiekemba dɩ kaabɩnana bugile gbaŋ kaaŋ yi. N wʋŋya dɩ wʋnyɩ benne nɩ sʋnsʋŋ ta dʋaga aŋaŋ ʋ nɩsa.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Ama nɩ yaa kalinbaani ta ka chɩga viivi ka wɩa. Nɩ tɩŋ mʋ nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ta vuodieke dɩ yine die wo nɩ tɩŋ vʋarɩ wa a nyɩŋ nɩ lagɩŋkʋ ma.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Aŋaŋ n yʋana aŋaŋ nɩ wa, ama n sʋŋanyile bie aŋaŋ nɩ, ta n wɔŋ yile a dii daa wa sarɩya, ta dɩ sɩɩ sɩba n lagɩŋ beri mɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Nɩ keŋ lagɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa saaŋ ma, ta n sʋŋanyile dɩ beri aŋaŋ nɩ, ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa hagɩrɩŋ dɩ beri aŋaŋ nɩ.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: nɩ vʋarɩ daa dieke dɩ yine wʋbɩakʋ gie wo a yi Sitaani nuusi me aŋ ʋ chʋʋsɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ, ta naa nan yi aŋ ʋ haalɩŋ keŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ daraaŋ.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Dɩ ka vɩɩna dɩ nɩ yallɩma kalinbaani. Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ dabɔtɩ bɩta ŋaana yie paanʋ zɔŋ dɩ yire?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vʋarɩ dembike gie a nyɩŋ nɩ ma, amʋ nɩ nan seŋ yi kasɩ, ta sɩɩ sɩba paanʋ zɔŋ haalɩŋ dɩ wone dabɔtɩ, wusie n sɩba mɩŋ dɩ die nɩ seŋ sɩɩ, dama Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga yiipɔlɩka wɔŋ tuo tɩ kuŋ a kpi mɩŋ.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ dɩna ʋ sieku die wo, nɩ vaa tɩ vaa berikʋʋrɩ, ta vaa sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ yiile, ta wa yi ʋ sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie, amʋ bɩaŋ kaaŋ dɩ bie tɩ ma.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: N wɔŋ maagɩ gbanɩŋ a yɩ nɩ dɩ nɩ da dɩma daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tieliŋ.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Ama n ka daga dɩ nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie hɔgʋkpana aŋaŋ daakpana ma yaa halɩtieliŋ yaa gaarɩŋ yaa vuodiekemba dɩ kaabɩnana bugile, dɩɩ yi die, die nɩŋ sie nɩ nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: N maaga bala nɩ dɩ nɩ daa chʋmma aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ bala dɩ wʋnɩŋ ʋ dɩ wa Masia ama ta yie hɔgʋkpana aŋaŋ daakpana tʋʋma, yaa a yi halɩtieŋ yaa ta kaaba bugile yaa a yi kʋagaarʋ yaa a yi vuodieke dɩ nyunene daaŋ a buge, yaa a yi gaarʋ; nɩ daa keŋ kala aŋaŋ wa gbaŋ a dii nyindiike.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Ka ka yi n tʋʋma dɩ n dime vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩɩ Masia sarɩya. Da nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna Masia wa mʋna nɩ dii nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩya?.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Ŋmɩŋ bala ʋ dii vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Masia wa sarɩya. Ka wɔŋ maagɩ mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Yagɩma wʋbɩatɩtieŋ a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ yaa wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaaŋ vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ, ta ka nagɩ wɩarɩ a ga Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ jigiŋ dɩ ba ga wʋnsɩ, ama ta nagɩ ka a ga vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Masia wa sarɩya diile jigiŋ, ka wo viivi?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba bala ba keŋ dii tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sarɩya? Ta dɩɩ yi nɩ nan daansɩ dii tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sarɩya die nɩnɩŋ nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a dii sarɩyabɩsɩ lele gie?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba dɩ tɩ bala tɩ keŋ dii malakasi sarɩya? Dɩɩ yi die, bɩa yine tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a dii lele gie beriŋ sarɩya?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ yaa wɩa, bɩa wɩa nɩ yaa gara vuodiekemba Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ dɩ yilinene dɩ ba ka yi jaaŋ jigiŋ?.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Naa yiwo viivi a yɩ nɩ. Dɩɩ yi nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ nan nɩ sʋnsʋŋ, vuoŋ wori nɩ sʋŋ a yaa yɩaŋ a baaŋ bɩagɩ wʋnsɩ ka?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Ama nɩ bataŋ naga taŋ a gara sarɩyadiiliŋ diekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia yada wa jigiŋ ba die nɩ sarɩya.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Nɩ wone yaa wɩa aŋaŋ nɩ nɩmballɩ gbaŋ, dagɩya dɩ nɩ wɔŋ chʋʋsɩya. Dɩɩ yi fʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ chʋʋsɩ fʋ yaa a nyɩrɩ fʋ ta fʋ saagɩ, dɩ ka kpɩa?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine vuodiekemba dɩ nyɩrɩnana ta gbata vuosi aŋaŋ nɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ chanchaalɩŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba baarɩ dɩ vuobɩatɩ kaaŋ ye Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ a juu? Nɩ gaama nɩ gbaŋ, vuodiekemba dɩ yine dɩgɩntɩ tʋʋma yaa ta kaaba bugile yaa hɔgʋkpana aŋaŋ daakpana tʋʋma yaa dembiŋ diekemba dɩ yaa lɔla a dʋaga aŋaŋ ba dembiŋ chanchaalɩŋ.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: yaa gbɩgbatɩrɩŋ yaa halɩtieliŋ yaa vuodiekemba dɩ nyunene daaŋ a buge yaa kʋagaarɩŋ yaa gaarɩŋ, genbe mana wʋnyɩ kaaŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Nɩ bataŋ die sɩɩwa dene; ama ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sugiri nɩ wʋbɩatɩ a taaŋ, ta nɩ wa yi ʋ vuosi ta bɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ ʋ jigiŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka wɩa.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Nɩ bataŋ bala dɩ, Wɩɩŋ ka chʋarɩ. Dɩ yiwo wusie, ama daa jaaŋ mana vɩɩna a yɩ tɩ. Nɩ bala dɩ jaaŋ ka chʋarɩ, ka yiwo wusie, ama manɩŋ n kaaŋ vaa jaaŋ yalla mɩŋ a tʋmma.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Nɩ bataŋ baarɩ dɩ, Nyindiike yiwo nyuuti sɩɩtɩ, ta nyuuti dɩaŋ yiwo nyindiike sɩɩtɩ. Ka yiwo wusie, ama Ŋmɩŋ nan chʋʋsɩ a mana. Ŋmɩŋ ka naaŋ tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ tɩ yaa tʋma dɩgɩntɩ tʋʋma, ama dɩ tɩ yaa jɩama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ. Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩna tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die sʋgɩrɩ wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, die gbaŋ ʋ nan daansɩ a sʋgɩrɩ tɩ aŋaŋ ʋ hagɩrɩbʋ.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ taasɩ wa aŋaŋ Masia nyɩŋgbanɩŋ? Die wɩa, nɩ nan nagɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ Masia nyɩŋgbaŋka a nagɩ taasɩ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ yinene hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma? Die kaaŋ wɔŋ yi, naada.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ vuodieke dɩ nagɩna ʋ gbaŋ a lagɩma aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ yinene hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoke mi bɩrɩŋ wa nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ? Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ nan lagɩŋ taŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ nagɩna ʋ gbaŋ a lagɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ aŋaŋ wa lagɩŋ yiwo haalɩbalɩmɩŋ.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Nɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma ma. Da tʋmbɩatɩ mana yinene vuota nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma; ama vuodiekemba dɩ yinene daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma nɩŋ tʋma bɩaŋ a yɩa ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ yine Halɩkasɩ dieke nɩ tuone nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ juoŋ, ta ʋ bie mi? Nɩ ka sɩ nɩ gbaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ sɩna nɩ;.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: dama ʋ nagɩ wa Masia zɩmbʋ a daa nɩ mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ nagɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a yime wudiekemba dɩ baaŋ bɩrɩ ʋ saaŋ.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Tɔ, lele n wa yaala n balɩ mɩŋ yaa gamma bugile nɔŋ ŋɔbɩŋ ma, tɩ sɩba a baarɩ tɩ mana yaa sɩbɩŋ, aŋ ka seŋ yi wusie. Sɩbɩŋ vasa vuota dɩ kɔta ʋ gbaŋ, ama choti vasa tɩ kpaŋŋɩsa tɩ chanchaalɩŋ.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yilinene dɩ ʋ sɩba jaaŋ, wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi ye ka sɩba ʋ tɩnna mʋ ʋ sɩmma dene.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ chone Ŋmɩŋ, wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩba ʋ wɩa.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Tɔ, yaa gamma nɔŋ dieke ba kaabɩna a yɩ bugile ŋɔbɩŋ wɩa; tɩ sɩba a baarɩ buuŋ yiwo jadieke dɩ wone mɩsɩ, ta tɩ bɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ balɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna benne.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta bugile dɩ beri gbaŋ, ta bie ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ yaa tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ jigiŋ nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ balɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma benne; Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ naanna jaaŋ mana, ʋ ma tɩ yaa miivoli; ta Yɔmʋtieŋ balɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma benne, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Yisa Masia. Ʋ ma die jaaŋ mana dɩ naaŋ; ta ʋ ma tɩ bɩ yaa miivoli.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Daa vuoŋ mana sɩbɩna wusieke gie. Vuosi bataŋ kaabɩ wa bugile a yʋasɩ, die wɩa ba keŋ dii nyindiike me, ba ŋaaŋ yile dɩ a yiwo buuŋ nyindiike. Ba yada ka hagɩrɩ, die wɩa ba ŋaaŋ yile dɩ ba yaa bɩaŋ.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Daa nyindiike diekemba tɩ dinene baaŋ vaa tɩ gbigi Ŋmɩŋ; tɩ kaaŋ waarɩ jaaŋ dɩɩ yi ta tɩ ka dii nyindiikehe, yaa tɩ kaaŋ ye jaaŋ dɩɩ yi tɩ diihe.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ka chʋarɩ bugile nyindiike diile ama nɩ sɩmma ta daa vaa a diile gaaŋ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba yada dɩ kana ka dala wa.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Ta vuodieke yada dɩ kana ka dala wa dɩ ye nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ bala dɩ nɩ yaa sɩbɩŋ ta nɩ bie buuŋ juoŋ me a die jadieke ba kaabɩna yɩ bugilehe, naa nan vaa ʋ gbaŋ ʋ ga dii nyindiike diekemba dɩ yine buuŋ nyindiike.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Fʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine sɩbɩŋtieŋ wo, fʋ sɩbɩkʋ nan chʋʋsɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa dieke yada dɩ kana ka dala wa, vuodieke Masia die dɩ kpine a gbatɩ wa a taaŋ wa.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Fʋ yie naa a yie bɩaŋ a yɩa fʋ nɩmballɩ, ta chʋʋsa ba yada dieke dɩ kana ka dala wa, ka daga dɩ yie bɩaŋ yɩa Masia mɩŋ.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi nyindiike dieke n dinene wa dɩ vasa n nɩmbʋa dɩ yie bɩaŋ, die nɩŋ n kaaŋ bɩ ŋɔbɩ nɔŋ bɩbra amʋ n kaaŋ vaa ʋ yi bɩaŋ.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Lele, n yaala n yiŋŋi wʋpɩasɩ diekemba nɩ maagɩna a pɩasɩ mɩŋ wa mɩŋ. Die nɩ pɩasɩya dɩ, Dɩ vɩɩna mɩŋ dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ ka faarɩ hɔgʋ yaa a yallɩ chʋrʋ.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma dɩ faasɩna dala wɩa, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ dembiŋ mana yallɩma ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ hɔgʋ, ta hɔgʋ mana dɩaŋ yallɩma ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ chʋrʋ.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Dɩ vɩɩna dɩ dembiŋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yɩ ʋ hɔgʋ ta hɔgʋ dɩaŋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yɩ ʋ chʋrʋ.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Hɔgʋ wa ka sɩɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ, ʋ chʋrʋ sɩna. Dembiŋ dɩaŋ ka sɩɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ʋ hɔgʋ sɩna.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Nɩ daa zetime taŋ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ choti, sie saŋŋa dieke nɩ keŋ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ dɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ; ta a kʋaŋ chaaŋ nɩ bɩ lagɩma taŋ, amʋ Sitaani kaaŋ bɩagɩ magɩsɩ nɩ, ta nyaŋŋɩ nɩ nɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ yigi nɩ gbaŋ wɩa.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Dɩ yiwo wʋbalɩka maŋ balɩ ama daa mɩra.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: N tɩŋ nan yaala dɩ vuoŋ mana bemme sɩba n benne die wo ta ka faarɩ hɔgʋ, ama vuoŋ mana yaa wa wudieke ʋ bɩagɩna yie ka yi piini yiri yiri a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: N kpaamɩŋ wʋnna a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka faarɩ hɔɔŋ yaa ye ka yallɩ chʋrʋ aŋaŋ kpihɔgʋba. Nɩ bemme nɩ nyɩɩna ma sɩba n benne dene wo.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ yigi nɩ gbaŋ, die nɩŋ dembisi faarɩma hɔɔŋ ta hɔɔŋ yallɩma chʋllɩŋ, dama nɩ faarɩ hɔɔŋ yaa yallɩ chʋllɩŋ dɩ kpana aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ dɩ yaa ka lɔla ama nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ kʋʋ ka.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ faarɩna hɔɔŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla chʋllɩŋ, n yɩa nɩ mɩra, daa manɩŋ n zienneke, ama dɩ yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ zienneke, dɩ hɔgʋ da keŋ nyɩŋ ta vaa ʋ chʋrʋ.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: ama dɩɩ yi ʋ yi die, dɩ mʋ ʋ bemme ʋ nyɩɩna ma yaa ʋ yiŋŋi ga ʋ chʋrʋ wa jigiŋ, ta dembiŋ dɩaŋ da keŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: N bala naa a yɩa vuotɩalɩkaha dɩaŋ, dɩ, dɩɩ yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ dɩ yaa hɔgʋ, ta hɔgʋ wa dɩ ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ ama ta saagɩ dɩ ʋ aŋaŋ wa nan dɩ beri, dembike daa keŋ zeti wo.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Ta hɔgʋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ ta yaa daa dieke dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ, ama ta daa wa dɩ saagɩ dɩ ʋ nan dɩ beri aŋaŋ wa, hɔgʋ wa da keŋ nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Dembiŋ dieke dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ ama ta ʋ hɔgʋ dɩ yi, hɔgʋ wa wɩa Ŋmɩŋ daansa chʋrʋ wa sɩba wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi; ta dɩɩ yi ta hɔgʋ wa dɩaŋ dɩ ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ ama ta ʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ yi, Ŋmɩŋ daansa hɔgʋ wa sɩba wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi. Dɩ daa die ba ballɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka jɩama wusie Ŋmɩnnɩ ballɩ, ama dɩ sɩna die wo, Ŋmɩŋ tuo be mɩŋ.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wa dɩ yaala ʋ zeti vuodieke dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wa ʋ vaa wa aŋ ʋ ga. Dama die nɩŋ mɩraha ka bɩ bɔbɩ ba wʋnyɩ mana. Ŋmɩŋ wa tɩ dɩ tɩ bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Fʋnɩŋ hɔgʋ dieke dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wa, lalɩa fʋ baa fʋ yi a sɩmma dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ fʋ chʋrʋ a taaŋ? Ta fʋnɩŋ daa dieke dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ wa, lalɩa fʋ baa fʋ yi a sɩmma dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ fʋ hɔgʋ a taaŋ?.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Nɩ wʋnyɩ mana bemme beri dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ ziene fʋ wo aŋaŋ fʋ sɩna die wo ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna fʋ wa. Naa maŋ daga Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ lagɩŋkʋ jigiŋ mana dɩ ba dɩa.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Vuodieke dɩ gobine ʋ kɔlɩ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ye a wa wa, ʋ bemme die; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kana ka gobi ʋ kɔlɩ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wa wa ʋ dɩaŋ ʋ bemme die.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Vuodieke dɩ gobi aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kana ka gobiye a mana yiwo nyʋarɩ. Wudieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna yine nɩ dɩma Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Vuoŋ mana bemme die ʋ sɩna die ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ wa wa.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta die yiwo yɔmʋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ vʋarɩ fʋ, daa vaa ka mugisi fʋ, ama dɩɩ yi fʋ tieŋ dɩ yɩ fʋ sieŋ dɩ fʋ ga yɔrɩ nan bɩagɩ yi die.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Vuodieke die yine yɔmʋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ keŋ vʋarɩ wa ʋ wa bɩrɩŋ wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩɩtɩ ta bie ʋ dʋŋŋʋ; die gbaŋ vuodieke dɩ sɩna ʋ gbaŋ ta Masia dɩ keŋ vʋarɩ wa, ʋ wa bɩrɩŋ wʋnɩŋ Masia yɔmʋ.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Ŋmɩŋ daa nɩ mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ daa bɩrɩŋ vuosi yɔŋɩsɩ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ bemme aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ benne die saŋŋa dieke die nɩ bɩrɩna Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Lele nɩŋ, n bala yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yene ka faarɩŋ hɔɔŋ yaa dɩ yene ka yallɩ chʋllɩŋ, ama daa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yɩna mɩŋ sieŋ dɩ n balɩ naa; ama n bala n sʋŋanyile dɩ dagɩna mɩŋ dene; dama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ chɩgɩna mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ wɩa ʋ suŋŋi mɩŋ maŋ yi wusie bɩbalɩtʋ.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Saŋka gie dɩ tʋana wɩa, n yile dɩ dɩ vɩɩna dɩ vuoŋ bemme ʋ wone beri die wo.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ yaa hɔgʋ, die nɩŋ daa keŋ zeti wo. Ta dɩɩ yi fʋ ye ka faarɩŋ hɔgʋ, daa keŋ faarɩ hɔgʋ bɩbra.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ faarɩ wa hɔgʋ yaa a yallɩ chʋrʋ, ka ka yi bɩaŋ, ta dɩɩ yi hɔgʋ dieke dɩ yene ka yallɩ chʋrʋ dɩ yallɩ ka ka yi bɩaŋ; ama n yaala n kagɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ wa mugisi dieke vuodiekemba dɩ yalla hɔɔŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla chʋllɩŋ dɩ yesinene tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo ma mɩŋ.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: N nɩmballɩ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ keniŋ gbigiye mɩŋ, die wɩa a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma dɩ vɩɩna dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla hɔɔŋ bemme sɩba ba ka faarɩ hɔɔŋ.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: ta vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnana wa bemme sɩba ba wo sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ; ta vuodiekemba dɩ lalana bemme sɩba ba wo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ; ta vuodiekemba dinene nyʋŋ bemme sɩba ba wo jaaŋ mana;.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: ta wudiekemba vuoŋ mana dɩ yinene tɩŋgbaŋka gie me daa vaa ba baga faasɩ bemme a ma. Dama tɩŋgbaŋka gie dɩ sɩna die lele gie wo, ka kaaŋ yʋasɩ aŋ ka tɩaŋ ga.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: N ka yaala dɩ jaaŋ mugisi nɩ. Daakpaŋ nɩŋ daansa ʋ baaŋ yi die a tʋŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋma aŋ ka fɩalɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sʋŋ.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ yalla hɔgʋ nɩŋ baga bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyinti ma, dama ʋ yaala ʋ yi mɩŋ aŋ ʋ hɔgʋ sʋŋ fɩalɩ.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: Die wɩa ʋ yallɩ wa sʋŋanyile bule. Hɔgʋ dieke dɩ bɩna wo chʋrʋ yaa hɔgʋlɩa baga gbaŋ bie tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋma ma aŋaŋ ʋ baaŋ yi die a nagɩ ʋ wɩɩŋ mana a bɩrɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ, wudieke ʋ yinene aŋaŋ ʋ yilinene wudieke wo; ama hɔgʋ dieke dɩ yalla chʋrʋ nɩŋ baga bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyinti ma, dama ʋ yaala ʋ yi mɩŋ aŋ ʋ chʋrʋ sʋŋ fɩalɩ.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: N bala naa dama n yaala n suŋŋi nɩ mɩŋ, n ka yaala n kagɩ nɩ, ama n yaala nɩ bemme vɩɩnɩŋ, ta nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋma ma ta daa vasɩma.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Dɩɩ yi dembiŋ dɩ yaa bandaa ta ba keŋ saŋ dɩ ba kaaŋ faarɩ taŋ, ta dɩɩ yi dembike dɩ yile yi dɩ ka mɩŋŋɩ yi bandaa wa, ta ʋ yile dɩ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ yigi ʋ gbaŋ, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ ʋ yime wudieke ʋ yaalala aŋ ba faarɩ taŋ dama bɩaŋ wo a ma.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Ama vuodieke sʋŋ dɩ hagɩrɩŋ, ta ʋ sɩba a baarɩ wɩɩŋ kaaŋ mugisi wo, ta ʋ nan bɩagɩ yigi ʋ gbaŋ, ta yile ʋ sʋŋ ma daa vuoŋ mugisine wo dɩ ʋ kaaŋ faarɩ hɔgʋlɩa wa, die vɩɩŋ na.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Vuodieke faarɩna ʋ bandaa wa, dɩ vɩɩna; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kana ka faarɩ ʋ bandaa wa, yiwo wudieke dɩ faasɩna a vɩɩna tɩaŋ vuodieke dɩ faarɩna ʋ bandaa wa.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Dɩɩ yi hɔgʋ dɩ yaa chʋrʋ ta ʋ ye bie ʋ mɩsɩ ma, ʋ mʋ ʋ bemme aŋaŋ wa mɩŋ, ama dɩɩ yi ʋ chʋrʋ wa dɩ keŋ kpi, die nɩŋ ʋ yaa sieŋ dɩ ʋ yallɩ daa dieke ʋ yaalala ama dɩ mʋ ʋ daa wa yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Ama manɩŋ n jigiŋ ʋ nan yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam dɩɩ yi ʋ beri ʋ nyɩɩna ta ka yallɩya; manɩŋ n sʋŋanyile wonde, ama n sɩba a baarɩ n gbaŋ n yaa wa Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka mɩŋ.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: N ka sɩɩ n gbaŋ? N ka yi Masia tʋntʋntʋ? N ka ye tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa? Daa n tʋʋma dieke n tʋnnana a yɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa wɩa nɩ keŋ yi yada?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta vuosi bataŋ dɩ ka saagɩ dɩ n yiwo Masia tʋntʋntʋ, ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ dɩ yiwo talasɩ nɩ saagɩ! Dama nɩ nagɩna nɩ gbaŋ a taasɩ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo dagɩna dɩ n seŋ yiwo Masia tʋntʋntʋ.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuosi dɩ bala a chʋʋsa n saaŋ, n nan yiŋŋi balɩ naa a vʋarɩ n gbaŋ:.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Tɩ wo sieŋ dɩ tɩ dime ta nyume tɩ tʋʋma wɩa?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Tɩ bɩ wo sie dieke tɩ baaŋ nan nagɩ tɩ hɔgʋba diekemba dɩ yine Masia yada wa a dɩ dɩa aŋaŋ ba sɩba Masia tʋntʋntɩŋ bataŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ Piita die dɩ yi die wo?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Yaa nɩ yile dɩ manɩŋ aŋaŋ Banabasi nyɩɩna ma mʋna tɩ tʋma tɩ nyuuti wɩa?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Soja bɩa tʋnnana tʋʋma ta bɩ tune ʋ gbaŋ? Ta mɩnɩa bala ʋ suone tɩɩsɩ ama ta ka dii a biehe? Yaa mɩnɩa bala ʋ yagɩma dɔŋɩsɩ ama ta kaaŋ nyu a biisiŋ?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: N nagɩna vuota beriŋ a balɩ naa; daa die gbaŋ Mosisi mɩraha dɩ balɩ?.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Dɩ maagɩya Mosisi mɩraha ma dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩ naakʋakɩŋ a tʋma fʋ kʋaŋ tʋʋma, daa bɔbɩ ka nʋaŋ ma dɩ ka daa ŋɔbɩma. Ŋmɩŋ baga bie wo niige nyɩɩna ma?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Daa tɩ wɩa die ʋ balɩ naa? Wusie, die a maagɩ wa tɩ wɩa. Vuodieke dɩ kʋanana aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ gobinene tʋma ba tʋʋmaha ta yaa tama dɩ ba nan keŋ puo zaaha mɩŋ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Die tɩ suo wo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ nyinti nɩ ma, dɩ ka mʋ dɩ tɩ tuo nyɩŋgbanɩŋ nyinti a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuosi bataŋ dɩ yaa sieŋ dɩ ba tuo suŋŋiŋ gie a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, dɩ ka mʋ dɩ tɩnɩŋ tɩ faasɩ tuoke? Ama die tɩ ka nagɩ tɩ yalla sieku gie a tuo nyinti a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ama die tɩ die wahala jaaŋ mana ma; dama die tɩ ka yaala tɩ vaa Masia wʋvɩɩnaha gie wɩarɩ kʋaŋ.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnnana Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ yese ba nyindiike me, ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ tʋnnana kaabɩŋ koriŋ jigiŋ yese ba nyindiike a nyɩna kaabɩkʋ ma?.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi nʋaŋ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ mʋʋlɩnana ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha yesime ba nyindiike a nyɩnna ka ma.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Ama manɩŋ n ka nagɩ sieti gie kaanɩ mana a tʋŋ tʋʋma, ta n ka bɩ maaga naa dɩ nɩ yɩ mɩŋ jaaŋ. Maŋ kpi dɩ kpɩa aŋaŋ wudieke wɩa n yalla nyʋʋŋ nɩgɩŋ baaŋ nyɩŋ n nuusi me.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: N ka yaa nyʋʋŋ nɩgɩŋ yaa gamma wʋvɩɩna dieke n mʋʋlɩnana wɩa, dama dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ n mʋʋlɩma ha. Wʋbɩaŋ nan dɩ bie n ma dɩɩ yi maŋ ka mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ tɩŋ vʋarɩ wa tʋʋmaha n gbaŋ gbaŋ, n tɩŋ nan ye tune, ama n ka vʋarɩ ka n gbaŋ gbaŋ, Ŋmɩŋ die nagɩna a yi n nuusi me dɩ n tʋma.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Bɩa yine n tune? N tune yine n mʋʋlɩnana wʋvɩɩnaha yɔrɩ wa, ta ka naga sie dieke n yalla wʋvɩɩnaha tʋʋma ma wa a tuose jaaŋ wa.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: N bie wo n dʋŋŋʋ, n ka yi vuoŋ yɔmʋ, ama n nagɩ wa n gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ vuoŋ mana yɔmʋ dɩ n vaa vuosi pam keŋ Yisa Masia jigiŋ.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Juu vuosi jigiŋ, n nagɩ n gbaŋ a yi sɩba banɩŋ dɩ n yi aŋ ba ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Vuodiekemba dɩaŋ dɩ dɩna Mosisi mɩraha, n nagɩ wa n gbaŋ a yi sɩba ba wʋnyɩ mɩŋ, ama n gbaŋ mɩraha ka yigi mɩŋ, ta yaala dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna mɩraha ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ mɩraha, n nagɩ wa n gbaŋ a yi sɩba ba wʋnyɩ dɩ ba ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Naa ka dagɩ sɩba n ka dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha; n dɩa Masia mɩraha mɩŋ.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Vuodiekemba ba yada dɩ kana ka dala wa n tarɩgɩya mɩŋ sɩba ba wʋnyɩ dɩ ba ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. N tarɩgɩ wa n gbaŋ sɩba vuoŋ mana mɩŋ a yi vuoŋ mana dɩ dɩ sɩɩ die mana bataŋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: N yie wɩaha gie mana wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa, dɩ n keŋ gʋtɩ a ye ka nyʋarɩ.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ chɩgɩla ma vuosi ŋaana chɩga ama vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma ŋaana dii, ta tuo ka tune. Die wɩa, nɩ chɩgɩma a dii ta tuo ka tune.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Chɩchɩgɩla mana mɩa mɩŋ ta yige ba gbaŋ dɩ ba keŋ tuo tune dieke dɩ kana ka yʋasa aŋaŋ kpatɩŋ, ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ tʋma dɩ tɩ keŋ tuo tune dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ haahuu.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Die wɩa maŋ chɩga ta n nine dɩ bie ka kpatɩŋ ma ta ka chɩga yɔrɩ yɔrɩ; die wɩa maŋ sɩɩ sɩba nuuguŋnɩgɩrʋ dieke dɩ kana ka viise n nuugume yɔrɩ yɔrɩ.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: N ka yaala manɩŋ vuodieke dɩ mʋʋlɩnana wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩa vuosi keŋ waarɩ ka tune. Die wɩa maŋ mugise n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋ ka saaga a yɩa mɩŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n yaala nɩ sɩmma dɩ nɩɩŋmaaŋ die lige tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba mana mɩŋ, ta ba mana die dɩ garɩsɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ mɩŋ.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Die ba mana die lagɩsɩna a sɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ nɩɩŋmaakʋ aŋaŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ, ta die bɩrɩŋ Mosisi kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Ba mana die lagɩsɩya mɩŋ a dii nyindiike dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩna ba.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: ta die mana dɩ bɩ lagɩŋ a nyuu nyaaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩna ba wa; dama ba mana die lagɩsɩna a nyuu nyaaŋ dieke die dɩ nyɩna Ŋmɩŋ haalɩbʋ taŋ dieke die dɩ beline be wo. Tannɩ die yiwo Masia.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Aŋaŋ die mana, Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ die ka fɩalɩya aŋaŋ ba jʋsʋ, die wɩa die ʋ vaa ba kpi kpi, ta ba vɔrɩtɩ die dɩ jaasɩ a dʋa dʋa hagɩrɩ ma.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Die wɩa, wɩaha gie yiwo dagɩtɩ a kpaana tɩ dɩ tɩ daa yaalɩma bɩaŋ sɩba die ba yaalɩnana die wo.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: yaa tɩ jɩama bugile sɩba ba bataŋ die dɩ yine die wo, ka maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ta vuosisi die dɩ sʋʋŋ kalɩ a dii ta nyuu ta die hagɩ mi a sie ta jɩama bugile.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Tɩ tʋma dɩgɩntɩ tʋʋma sɩba bataŋ die dɩ tʋma die wo, ta dabalɩmɩŋ ba vuosi tuse baŋɩsɩ-le aŋaŋ ba taa dɩ kpi wo.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Tɩ daa magɩsɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ sɩba bataŋ die dɩ yine die, ta die nyinvuuke die dɩ dʋŋ ba a kʋʋ wa.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Tɩ da vʋʋma sɩba bataŋ die dɩ yine die wo ta kuŋ malaka die dɩ keŋ kʋʋ ba wa.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Naa mana die yiye mɩŋ a yi ba dɩ ka yi dagɩtɩ, ta ba maagɩ ha a dʋaŋ dɩ a yi kpaamɩŋ a yi tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne saŋŋa dieke dʋnɩa dɩ bala ka kpatɩ wa naa.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Die wɩa, vuodieke dɩ yilinene dɩ ʋ mɩŋŋɩ zie, sɩmma ta daa keŋ nan.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Magɩsɩ dieke mana dɩ kenne nɩ ma yiwo magɩsɩ dieke dɩ ŋaana a kieŋ vuosi ma. Ama Ŋmɩŋ yiwo wusietieŋ ta kaaŋ vaa nɩ juu magɩsɩ dieke dɩ hagɩrɩna a tɩaŋ nɩ; ama saŋŋa dieke magɩsɩŋ dɩ keŋ juu nɩ ʋ nan yɩ nɩ hagɩrɩ dieke nɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ ka a nyɩŋ ka ma.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Die wɩa, n nɩmballɩ, nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ bugile yiri yiri kaabɩŋ ma.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: N bala yɩa nɩ dama nɩ yiwo yɩantieliŋ, nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yilime yaa gamma wudieke n bala naa wa.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Daa dieke tɩ ŋaana bɩra Ŋmɩŋ ta nyuo wo wo dagɩya dɩ tɩ mana lagɩŋ a tɩɩnsa Masia zɩmbʋ mɩŋ; ta paanʋ dieke tɩ ŋaana gbieri gbieri a ŋɔba wa, dagɩya dɩ tɩ mana lagɩŋ a tɩɩnsa Masia nyɩŋgbaŋka mɩŋ.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Ka yine paanʋ gɔlɩŋ balɩmɩŋ, ta tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ dala mɩŋ ta lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ, dama tɩ mana lagɩŋya a ŋɔba paanʋ gɔlɩŋ balɩŋka.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Daansɩma, Juu vuosisi. Ba vuodiekemba dɩ dinene jadieke ba kaabɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ lagɩŋya mɩŋ aŋaŋ taŋ a yie kaabaha koriku me.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Manɩŋ n balala wudieke wo, dɩ dagɩya mɩŋ dɩ buubu aŋaŋ nɔŋ dieke ba kaabɩna a yi ka wa yaa nyʋarɩ? Aayɩ, ka wo nyʋarɩ.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: N bala mɩŋ dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kaabɩna kaaba a yɩa bugile, ba kaaba yɩa jɩmbɩatɩ mɩŋ, daa Ŋmɩŋ. Die wɩa n ka yaala nɩ lagɩma aŋaŋ jɩmbɩatɩ.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: N ka saagɩya dɩ nɩ lagɩŋ a nyume tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Masia nyinnyuuke, ta bɩ nyume jɩmbɩatɩ nyinnyuuke. N ka saagɩya dɩ nɩ dime a nyɩŋ ma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ kpalɩ ma ta bɩ dime a nyɩŋ ma jɩmbɩatɩ kpalɩ ma.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Tɩ mɩa mɩŋ dɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yallɩma nyantaatɩ? Tɩ hagɩrɩ a tɩaŋ wa mɩŋ?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Vuosi bataŋ baarɩ dɩ, Tɩ yaa sieŋ dɩ tɩ yi wɩɩŋ mana. Wusie; ama daa jaaŋ mana vɩɩna. Tɩ yaa sieŋ dɩ tɩ yi wɩɩŋ mana. Ama daa wɩɩŋ mana yalla nyʋarɩ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Da yilime fʋ nyɩɩna ma fʋ wɩa, ama yilime fʋ chanchaalɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Nɩ ŋɔbɩma nɔŋ dieke ba yalla daa nyʋŋ ma, ta daa pɩasɩma wʋpɩasɩka diekemba nan mugisi nɩ sʋŋanyile.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Tɩŋgbaŋka gie aŋaŋ ka nyinti mana yiwo Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ ama ta keŋ bɔmɩsɩ fʋ nyindiike ta fʋ yaala fʋ ga, die nɩŋ dime nyindiike dieke mana ʋ nagɩna zieŋ fʋ ta daa pɩasɩma wʋpɩasɩka diekemba dɩ baaŋ mugisi fʋ sʋŋanyile.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ balɩ fʋ dɩ Ba kaabɩ wa buuŋ, die nɩŋ da keŋ dii nyindiikehe vuoke mi sʋŋanyile wɩa.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: Daa fʋ sʋŋanyile ama vuodieke dɩ balɩ fʋ wa sʋŋanyile. Lalɩa vuoŋ sʋŋanyile dɩ baa ka chʋʋsɩ n dʋŋŋʋ beriŋ?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ n nyindiike ma ta ye dii, lalɩa vuoŋ dɩ baa ʋ galɩŋ mɩŋ yaa gamma nyindiike dieke n bɩrɩna Ŋmɩŋ a wɩa.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Tɔ, dɩɩ yi diiŋ yaa nyɩŋnyuule, wudieke mana nɩ yinene, nɩ yime wudieke dɩ balla a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Daa yime wudieke dɩ baaŋ vaa aŋ vuoŋ nan, dɩɩ yi ʋ yi Juu vuoŋ, yaa Giriiki, yaa Ŋmɩŋ vuosi.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Nɩ yime sɩba n yinene die wo; n mɩa mɩŋ dɩ yi aŋ vuoŋ mana sʋŋ fɩalɩ n yinene wudieke wo, ta ka yile yaa gamma n gbaŋ beri vɩɩnɩŋ wɩa, ama vuoŋ mana vɩɩnɩŋ, amʋ ba nan keŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Die wɩa nɩ dɩa n beriŋ sɩba n dɩna Masia beriŋ dene wo.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: N bɩra nɩ saŋŋa mana nɩ tɩɩnsɩnana n wɩa ta dɩ n dagɩkʋ n nagɩna a yɩ nɩ wa.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Ama wɩɩŋ kaanɩ maŋ yaala nɩ sɩmma, dɩ Masia yiwo dembiŋ mana sikpeŋ tieŋ, ta dembiŋ mana dɩ yi ʋ hɔgʋ sikpeŋ tieŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yi Masia sikpeŋ tieŋ.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Die wɩa dembiŋ dieke dɩ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩŋ yaa a bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yaalɩŋ ma ta ligi ʋ sikpeŋ aŋaŋ jaaŋ ka yɩa Masia jɩlɩma.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Hɔgʋ dieke dɩaŋ dɩ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩŋ yaa a bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ama ta ka ligi ʋ sikpeŋ aŋaŋ jaaŋ, ʋ chʋrʋ wa ʋ yie viivi me, ka nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba ʋ wʋŋ wa ʋ sikpeŋ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi hɔgʋ wa dɩ ka ligi ʋ sikpeŋ, die nɩŋ dɩ vɩɩna dɩ ʋ chɩa ʋ sikpeŋ zoosi. Ama ka ŋaana yi viivi dɩ hɔgʋ wʋŋ ʋ sikpeŋ yaa chɩa ʋ sikpeŋ zoosi wo, ʋ mʋ ʋ ligi wo ʋ sikpeŋ.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Dɩ ka mʋ dɩ dembiŋ ligi ʋ sikpeŋ dama Ŋmɩŋ die naanna wa ʋ nɩasɩ wa ta bɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ bɩrɩŋ, ama hɔgʋ nɩnɩŋ yiwo dembiŋ bɩrɩŋ.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Dama dembiŋ ka naaŋ nyɩŋ hɔgʋ ma, ama hɔgʋ naanna nyɩŋ dembiŋ me.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die ka naaŋ wa dembiŋ a yɩ hɔgʋ ama die ʋ naaŋ wa hɔgʋ a yɩ dembiŋ.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Ta malakasi wɩa, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ hɔgʋ ligi ʋ sikpeŋ aŋ ka yi dagɩtɩ dɩ ʋ chʋrʋ wa yiwo ʋ sikpeŋ tieŋ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jigiŋ nɩŋ, dɩɩ yi dembiŋ dɩ wori, hɔgʋ kaaŋ dɩ beri, ta dɩɩ yi hɔgʋ dɩaŋ dɩ wori, dembiŋ dɩaŋ kaaŋ dɩ beri.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ die nagɩ wa dembiŋ gambiŋ a naaŋ hɔgʋ, ta hɔgʋ dɩaŋ dɩ mɩra dembiŋ, ama jaaŋ mana nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Nɩ pɩasɩ nɩ gbaŋ sɩba dɩ vɩɩna dɩ hɔgʋ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ ta ka ligi ʋ sikpeŋ?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Ama tɩ beriŋ ma dagɩya dɩ dɩɩ yi ta dembiŋ dɩ yaa sikpeŋ zoowagɩtɩ ka yiwo viivi a yɩ wa;.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: ama dɩɩ yi hɔgʋ dɩ yaa zoowagɩtɩ ka yɩa wa vɩɩnɩŋ, hɔgʋ zoosi yiwo ʋ sibubiŋ.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaala ʋ nɩgɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ yaa gamma ka ma, n nan balɩ dɩ tɩnɩŋ yaa Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ chanchaalɩŋ die dɩ benne mana wo chʋara a gʋtɩ naa ma.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Wudieke n yaala n balɩ naa nɩŋ, n ka bɩra nɩ ka wɩa, dama nɩ ŋaana lagɩma aŋaŋ taŋ wa a yie wudieke wo, wo nyʋarɩ a yɩa nɩ ama ka chʋʋsa nɩ mɩŋ.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Bʋmbʋŋaŋ wɩɩŋ yiwo, n wʋŋya mɩŋ ba balala dɩ nɩ ŋaana lagɩma tamba wa, lugiŋ ŋaana bie nɩ sʋnsʋŋ, ta maŋ tuo a taŋ dii dɩ a yiwo wusie.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Dɩ yiwo wusie dɩ lugiŋ nan dɩ beri, dama kanɩŋ ka bala ka dagɩ vuodiekemba dɩ senne dɩ wusie sieku yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Nɩ ŋaana keŋ lagɩma wa daa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nyindiike nɩ ŋaaŋ die.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Dama nɩ ŋaana lagɩŋ wa, vuoŋ mana ŋaaŋ die ʋ diile ta ka chɩɩsa ʋ chanchaalɩŋ, ta bataŋ kʋma kɔŋ ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ die a chaga, ta bataŋ dɩ nyuo a buge, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ ka yese a nyuo.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Nɩ wo tige a baaŋ nyuu ta dii mi? Yaa nɩ ka yɩa Ŋmɩŋ vuosi jɩlɩma ta yie zɔɔlɩntielibe dɩ die viivi? Bɩa nɩ yaala n bala nɩ yaa gamma wɩɩrɩ gie mi? N nan bɩrɩ nɩ wɩaha gie ma? Aayɩ, n kaaŋ bɩrɩ nɩ.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: N tuo wo dagɩkʋ gie a nyɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jigiŋ ta nagɩ yɩa nɩ: dɩ yuŋ dieke ba posine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa chɩaŋ, die ʋ nagɩ paanʋkʋ.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: a waasɩ Ŋmɩŋ, a yi ke gbieri gbieri a nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba ŋɔbɩ, ta baarɩ dɩ, N nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wʋnna maŋ baa n nagɩ aŋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ nɩ wɩa. Nɩ yime naa a tɩɩnsɩma n wɩa.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Nyindiikehe kʋaŋ, ta ʋ bɩ nagɩ daaŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋchibiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, Daabʋ gie yiwo n zɩŋ dagɩtɩ a daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa nʋahaalɩŋ a yɩ vuota, saŋŋa dieke nɩ yaala nɩ nyu, nɩ nyume a tɩɩnsɩma n wɩa.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Saŋŋa dieke mana nɩ keŋ ŋɔba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ paanʋ gulimiŋ ta nyuo daabʋ gie, nɩ bala vuosi dɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die kpiye mɩŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ bala ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Die wɩa, vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ ŋɔbɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ paanʋkʋ ta nyu daabʋ, sie dieke ʋ kana ka mʋ dɩ ʋ yi die, die vuoke mi yiwo bɩaŋ a yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ zɩmbʋ mɩŋ.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Vuoŋ mana mɩŋŋɩ wʋnsɩ ʋ gbaŋ, ta ye ŋɔbɩ paanʋkʋ ta nyu a daabʋ.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ dɩ kenne ŋɔbɩ paanʋkʋ ta nyu daabʋ ta ka sɩba dɩ ka zie tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nyɩŋgbaŋka naŋ ma, ʋ nan ye tɩbɩdatɩ dieke dɩ yine bɩaŋ ʋ ŋɔbɩna ta nyuo wɩa.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Die yine nɩ pam dɩ yʋagɩ ta yi wabɩtɩ, ta bataŋ dɩ kpi kpi.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi tɩ nan woliŋ a wʋnsɩ tɩ gbaŋ ta ye ŋɔbɩ ta nyu, tɩ kaaŋ ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die tɩ sarɩya ta data tɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ, amʋ ʋ kaaŋ chʋʋsɩ tɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ bala ʋ chʋʋsɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi wo.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Die wɩa, n nɩmballɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ lagɩsɩ taŋ dɩ nɩ dii tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nyindiikehe, nɩ chɩɩsɩma taŋ.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Ama vuodieke nɩŋ kɔŋ dɩ yalla, ta ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a chɩɩsɩ, die nɩŋ ʋ woliŋ dii ʋ tigiŋ me, ta ye keŋ, die kaaŋ dɩ yaa tɩbɩdatɩŋ a keŋ ʋ ma saŋŋa dieke nɩ kenne lagɩŋ tamba wa. Wʋtɩalɩkaha nɩŋ maŋ keŋ keŋ, n nan balɩ nɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: N nɩmballɩ, yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, n yaala nɩ sɩmma ka piinisi dɩ sɩna die aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ yene ka yi Masia yada wa, die nɩ jɩama bugile, jadieke dɩ kana ka bala wɩa, ta die yaa nɩ gaama sieti pam ma.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: N yaala nɩ sɩmma dɩ dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ bie vuodieke ma, die vuoke kaaŋ bɩagɩ yuori ʋ nʋaŋ a chʋʋsɩ Yisa saaŋ; ta vuoŋ dɩaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a balɩ dɩ Yisa yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, sie Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka bie ʋ ma.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka piinisi yiri yiri benne, ama Halɩkasɩ balɩŋka yɩnana a mana.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ yiri yiri benne, ama Yɔmʋtieŋ balɩŋka tɩ jɩama.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Tʋʋma yiri yiri dɩaŋ benne, ama Ŋmɩŋ balɩŋka vasɩnana vuoŋ mana dɩ tʋma ʋ tʋʋma.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana hagɩrɩŋ yiri yiri mɩŋ dɩ a dagɩ dɩ Halɩkasɩka bie ba ma, dɩ tɩ suŋŋi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Halɩkasɩka nan yɩ vuoŋ yɩaŋ wʋbalɩka; ta bɩ yɩ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ sɩbɩŋ ta ʋ bala sɩbɩŋ wʋbalɩka, ama a mana nyɩnna Halɩkasɩ balɩŋka jigiŋ.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Halɩkasɩka yɩa vuoŋ yada, ta bɩ yɩa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ vasɩnana yʋagɩtieliŋ dɩ yese gbaamɩŋ;.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: ta bɩ yɩa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ʋ bɩagɩ tʋma mamachi tʋʋma; ta bɩ yɩa ʋ wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ yɩaŋ ʋ bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa; ta bɩ yɩa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ yɩaŋ dieke ʋ bɩagɩ a sɩba piini dieke dɩ nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka jigiŋ aŋaŋ jadieke dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ Halɩkasɩka jigiŋ; ta bɩ yɩa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ yɩaŋ ʋ bɩagɩ bala jabalɩkɩ yiri yiri diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma; ta bɩ yɩa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ yɩaŋ ʋ bɩagɩ yiŋŋi bala jabalɩkɩ dieke ʋ kana ka wʋmma ha chɩaŋ.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Naa yiwo Halɩkasɩka tʋʋma, ta ʋ yɩa vuoŋ mana die piini sɩba wʋnɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yaalala die.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Masia sɩɩ sɩba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ; nyɩŋgbanɩŋ yiwo nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ ama ta puo puo lʋga lʋga, ama ka puone puo die mana ka ye yiwo nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Tɩ mana vuodiekemba dɩ yine Juu vuosi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi, aŋaŋ yɔŋɩsɩ, aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ sɩna ba gbaŋ, ba sɩɩ tɩ mana Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ aŋaŋ Halɩkasɩ balɩŋka mɩŋ dɩ tɩ lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ; ta Halɩkasɩ balɩŋka dɩ suuli tɩ mana.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Nyɩŋgbaŋka ka yi chɩbɩbalɩmɩŋ, ama a dala mɩŋ.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Dɩɩ yi naŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, N kana ka yi nuuŋ wɩa n ka gʋtɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka ma daa die naŋ dɩ baa ka yi aŋ ka kaaŋ gʋtɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka ma.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi ta tɩbɩŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n ka yi nimbiŋ die wɩa n ka gʋtɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka ma, daa die tɩbɩŋ dɩ baa ka yi aŋ ka kaaŋ gʋtɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka ma.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta nyɩŋgbaŋka mana dɩ lagɩŋ a yi nimbiŋ, lalɩa ka baa ka yi a wʋmma wɩa? Ta dɩɩ yi ka lagɩŋ yi tɩbɩŋ, lalɩa ka baa ka yi a wʋŋ nyuugiŋ?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ lagɩŋ wa nyɩŋgbaŋka gie lʋga lʋga mana a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ ʋ yaalala dene.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Dɩɩ yi a mana dɩ lagɩŋ yi jabalɩmɩŋ, ka tɩŋ nan yi nyɩŋgbanɩŋ?.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Dɩ sɩna die wo, nyindiekemba dɩ taasɩna nyɩŋgbaŋka dala mɩŋ, ama a mana yiwo nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Die wɩa, nimbiŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ balɩ yɩ nuuŋ dɩ, N ka yaala fʋ, yaa sikpeŋ balɩ yɩ naŋ dɩ, N ka yaala fʋ.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Ta tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ lʋga lʋga diekemba dɩ taasɩna nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma ta sɩɩ sɩba a wo hagɩrɩbʋ, dɩɩ yi a wori nyɩŋgbaŋka kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi wɩɩŋ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Nyɩŋgbaŋ lʋga diekemba ta tɩ daansɩna ta ka faasɩ wo jɩlɩma wa, hanɩŋ tɩ mɩŋŋɩ daansa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna. Die wɩa tɩ mɩŋŋɩ lige vuosi nine me tɩ lʋga diekemba dɩ kana ka mʋ dɩ vuosi ye.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: ta lʋga diekemba dɩ mʋna vuosi yesime tɩ ka ligihe. Ŋmɩŋ lagɩŋ wa tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka sie dieke me lʋga diekemba dɩ wone jɩlɩma wa tɩ faasɩ daansa.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Ʋ yiwo naa dɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka lʋga mana lagɩŋ a tʋma vɩɩnɩŋ, ta lʋga mana baga bemme hanɩŋ ba ma.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Dɩɩ yi nyɩŋgbaŋka lʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ dɩ die wahala nyɩŋgbaŋka mana dinene wahala dene; ta dɩɩ yi nyɩŋgbaŋka lʋgɩbalɩmɩŋ dɩ ye jɩlɩma nyɩŋgbaŋka mana yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ lagɩŋ yiwo Masia nyɩŋgbaŋka, ta nɩ wʋnyɩ mana yiwo ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ lʋgɩŋ.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ Masia tʋntʋntɩba Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ma, ta ba yi nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ; a bɩ gʋtɩ vʋarɩ naazʋalɩŋ; ta vʋarɩ dɩdagɩrɩŋ; a bɩ gʋtɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnnana mamachi wɩa; a bɩ gʋtɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ ʋ yɩna ba hagɩrɩŋ ba bɩagɩ gbaama yʋagɩtieliŋ; ta vʋarɩ susuŋŋiriŋ; a bɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba ʋ yɩna ba yɩaŋ ba bɩagɩ daansa tʋʋmaha; ta bɩ gʋtɩ vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ bɩagɩ balala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ yiri yiri diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Daa vuoŋ mana yine Masia tʋntʋntʋ, yaa naazʋa, yaa dɩdagɩrʋ yaa vuodiekemba dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ a bɩagɩ tʋma mamachi tʋʋma.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Daa vuoŋ mana yalla hagɩrɩŋ a bɩagɩ gbaama yʋagɩtieliŋ, yaa a bɩagɩ bala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma, yaa daa vuoŋ mana nan bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma a chɩasɩ.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Nɩ yaalɩma piini diekemba dɩ yine piinikpɩɩma aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna. N nan dagɩ nɩ sie dieke dɩ tɩanna a mana.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Tɔ, Dɩɩ yi maŋ bɩagɩ bala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ mana, yaa a bɩagɩ bala malakasi jabalɩkɩŋ, ama ta wo choti aŋaŋ vuosi, n sɩɩ sɩba gɩŋgaŋ yaa bimbilimiŋ watɩ mɩŋ.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ yaa piini dieke a baaŋ bɩagɩ yiŋŋi bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, yaa ta yaa sɩbɩŋ mana yaa ta bɩagɩ sɩba wʋlɔbɩrɩka wɩa mana, yaa ta yaa yada dieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a yi ta tankpeŋkpɩɩŋ vʋʋ jigiŋ kaanɩ a ga jigigaaŋ, ama ta wo choti aŋaŋ vuosi, n yiwo yɔrɩ.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ nagɩ n nyinti diekemba n yalla mana a yi zɔɔlɩntieliŋ, ta nagɩ n gbaŋ a yɩ dɩ ba juo boliŋ Masia wɩa, ama ta wo choti aŋaŋ vuosi, ka wo nyʋarɩ a yɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yaa choti, yaa wa suguru ta bɩ chɩga vuosi zɔɔlɩŋ, ʋ wo sʋgɩbɩaŋ, yaa kuuliŋ yaa kɔtɩŋ-n gbaŋ.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: ʋ wo kalinbaani, choti ka yie ʋ chanchaalɩŋ yɔrɩ yɔrɩ, yaa ta faasɩna yaala ʋ kasɩŋ ma vɩɩnɩŋ, yaa ta jɩa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ yɔrɩ yɔrɩ, ʋ ka naga bɩaŋ dieke ba yinene wo a yie ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yalla choti ka yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yaa gamma bɩaŋ ma, ama ta yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ wusieke.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yalla choti ka gbalɩga; ʋ yada aŋaŋ ʋ tama aŋaŋ ʋ suguru wo kpatɩŋ.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Choti wo kpatɩŋ. Ama dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ ka nan keŋ kpatɩ, ta dɩɩ yi wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ balɩŋ ka nan keŋ kpatɩ, ta dɩɩ yi sɩbɩŋ ka nan keŋ kpatɩ.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Lele nɩŋ, tɩ sɩba wa bɩta ta Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ piinike gbaŋ dɩ yuori a dagɩtɩ bɩta mɩŋ.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Ama saŋŋa dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dagɩtɩ a mana, wudieke dɩ benne bɩta wa nan kpatɩ.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Saŋŋa dieke die n yine bʋabiŋ die n bala sɩba bʋabiŋ, ta die yie nyinti sɩba bʋabiŋ, ta die yile sɩba bʋabiŋ, ama wana lʋa naa, n vaa wa balɩbɩsɩŋaŋ wɩa.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Wudieke tɩ sɩbɩna lele wo yayaa mɩŋ, ta sɩɩ sɩba tɩ ŋaana daansa dige me ta ka yese vɩɩnɩŋ; ama tɩ nan keŋ ye he yaalɩŋ ma. Wudieke n sɩbɩna lele gie wo yayaa mɩŋ, ama n nan keŋ sɩbaha vɩɩnɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩbɩna mɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ die wo.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Tɔ, yada aŋaŋ tama aŋaŋ choti, nyinti ataa wa gie benne; ama choti tɩaŋ a mana.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n yaala n tɩɩnsɩ nɩ mɩŋ yaa gamma wʋvɩɩna dieke die n mʋʋlɩna a yɩ nɩ wa, ta nɩ tuo a mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩha wa.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlaha nɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, daa die nɩ yadaka nan yi yɔrɩ.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Wudieke n tuone wo kanɩŋ maŋ nagɩ a yɩ nɩ wa, ta ka yine wʋkpɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ wɩɩŋ mana; dɩ Masia die tuo wo tɩnɩŋ wʋbɩayiiriŋ kuŋ a kpi, sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ ma dene wo;.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: dɩ die ba guu wo mɩŋ, ta ʋ hagɩ kumbu me ka daraa ataa daraaŋ sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dene wo.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: ta die nyɩŋ Piita jigiŋ, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ nyɩŋ ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo jigiŋ.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nyɩŋ bʋnyɩ ʋ dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ tɩanna vuosi kɔbɩsɩ-nʋ wa jigiŋ, bataŋ pam ye bie ba mɩsɩ ma ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ kpi.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ nyɩŋ Jemisi jigiŋ, ta die keŋ nyɩŋ ʋ tʋntʋntɩba mana jigiŋ.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ die ʋ keŋ nyɩŋ n gbaŋ n jigiŋ, manɩŋ vuodieke die dɩ sɩna sɩba n daaŋ ka tʋgɩya ta ba mɩɩrɩ mɩŋ wa.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Masia tʋntʋntɩba mana ma manɩŋ n yine vuodieke dɩ wɩarɩna ba kʋaŋ, ta die ka mʋ dɩ ba wasɩma mɩŋ Masia tʋntʋntʋ, dama die n vaa Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ dii wahala pam.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die chɩgɩma mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ ta suŋŋi mɩŋ maŋ yi vuodieke n yine jinne naa, ta die ʋ yɩna mɩŋ suŋŋi dieke wo dɩ ka yi yɔrɩ. Dama n ŋmɩntɩ wa niŋ a tʋŋ a tɩaŋ tʋntʋntɩ banɩŋ ba mana, ama dɩ ka yi manɩŋ n tʋna naa, ama Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi dieke die dɩ benne n ma wa tʋna die.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Dɩɩ yi manɩŋ yaa banɩŋ ba mʋʋlɩnana wɩaha, wʋbalɩŋka tɩ mana dɩ mʋʋla, ta kanɩŋ nɩ yi yada wa.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ mʋʋla dɩ Masia die hagɩ kuŋ me, lalɩa nɩ bataŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ kunti ka daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me?.
