eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Dɩɩ yi kunti dɩ kaaŋ daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me, die nɩŋ Masia gbaŋ die ka hagɩya kuŋ me.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Ta dɩɩ yi Masia die dɩ ka hagɩ kuŋ me, die nɩŋ tɩ wo wɩɩŋ a baaŋ mʋʋlɩ a yɩ nɩ, ta nɩ dɩaŋ dɩ wo wɩɩŋ a baaŋ tuo dii.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: A bɩ gʋtɩ, tɩ bɩ chɩba wɩa a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ dama tɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ sʋgɩrɩna Masia a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, ama dɩɩ yi kuŋ hagɩŋ dɩ wori die nɩŋ die ʋ ka sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Dɩɩ yi wusie dɩ kunti dɩ ka haga kʋŋ me, die nɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die ka sʋgɩrɩ Masia a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Dɩɩ yi Masia dɩ ka hagɩ kuŋ me, die nɩŋ nɩ yadaka yiwo yɔrɩ, ta nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ye bie nɩ ma.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Ta ka nan bɩ dagɩ dɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ yine Masia yada wa ta kpi wo baya mɩŋ.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Tɩ yine Masia yada wa, dɩɩ yi ka yaa nyʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie beriŋ nyɩɩna ma, die nɩŋ tɩ wɩa tɩŋ nan dɩ yaa zɔɔlɩŋ a tɩaŋ vuoŋ mana.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Ama ka yiwo wusie dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die seŋ sʋgɩrɩ wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me mɩŋ. Wʋnɩŋ Masia yiwo vuodieke die dɩ woliŋ hagɩ kuŋ me, ta ka dagɩ dɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ kpine gbaŋ nan daansɩ hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Vuota die yaa kuŋ a keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me; die gbaŋ gbaŋ vuota dɩ bɩ yaa kuŋ hagɩŋ a keŋ.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Dama Adami wɩa ta vuota mana dɩ kpie, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Masia wɩa vuota nan hagɩ kuŋ me.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Vuoŋ mana yaa ʋ kuŋ hagɩŋ saŋŋa mɩŋ; Masia die woliŋ hagɩ, ta vuodiekemba dɩ yine ʋ vuosisi nan daansɩ hagɩ ʋ keniŋ daraaŋ.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ kpatɩŋ nan keŋ, Masia nan daansɩ a nyaŋŋɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ naara aŋaŋ yikotieliŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩtieliŋ, ta nagɩ naarɩ a yɩ ʋ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Masia nan daansɩ yi naaŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ nyaŋŋɩ ʋ dataasɩ mana a nagɩ ba yi ʋ nuusi me.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Dataaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ wɩarɩ kʋaŋ a chʋʋsɩ yine kuŋ.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ʋ nan nagɩ jaaŋ mana a yi ʋ nuusi me, ama ka ka dagɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gʋtɩ wa jaaŋ mana wa ma, dama wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ bala ʋ nagɩ jaaŋ mana a yi Masia nuusi me.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Ta jaaŋ mana dɩ keŋ a bie Masia nuusi me saŋŋa dieke wo, ʋ gbaŋ ʋ nan daansɩ bie Ŋmɩŋ nuusi me, vuodieke dɩ yine ta nyinti mana dɩ bie ʋ nuusi me wo; ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sɩɩ jaaŋ mana.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Dɩɩ yi kunti dɩ ka haga kuŋ me, bɩa wɩa vuosi bataŋ dɩ ziele vuodiekemba dɩ kpine naŋ ma a sɩa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ? Dɩɩ yi kunti dɩ ka haga, bɩa wɩa ba yie die?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Dɩɩ yi kunti dɩ ka haga kuŋ me, bɩa yine ta tɩnɩŋ tɩ juo wʋkpɩɩma ma saŋŋa mana?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n tuole kuŋ daaŋ mana mɩŋ! N yaa nyʋʋŋ nɩgɩŋ nɩ ma aŋaŋ nɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ Yisa Masia tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, naa wɩa maŋ bala die.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Dɩɩ yi wudiekemba Efesusi tɩŋ vuosisi die yine mɩŋ aŋ ka sɩɩ sɩba n waga aŋaŋ dɔmbɩatɩ naa, bɩa nyʋarɩ maŋ nan ye aŋaŋ n waga aŋaŋ ba wa dɩɩ yi kunti dɩ ka haga kuŋ me? Dɩɩ yi dɩ kunti dɩ ka haga kuŋ me die nɩŋ, Nɩ vaa tɩ dime ta nyume, ta sɩmma a baarɩ soriŋ tɩ nan kpi.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Nɩ da vaa vuoŋ gaaŋ nɩ. Dɩɩ yi vuovɩɩna aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ dɩ lagɩsɩŋ chʋŋ, ka chʋʋsa vuovɩɩnaha beriŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Nɩ yiŋŋi ye yɩaŋ ta vaa nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ. Nɩ bataŋ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ, die wɩa maŋ maaga naa dɩ n yi nɩ viivi.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Vuoŋ nan pɩasɩ dɩ, Lalɩa kunti dɩ baa ka hagɩ kuŋ me? Ba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ baa ka sɩmma lalɩa.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Gaamʋ wa gie, dɩɩ yi fʋ keŋ bʋrɩ jaaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma ka ka nyuune ntaala ka hʋʋgɩya.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Fʋ keŋ bʋra, daa tɩɩka gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ a bʋrɩ, ama ka bie fʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ a bʋrɩ sɩba zaa yaa jadieke mana.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa bini mi nyuuŋ ta sɩɩ sɩba ʋ yaala ka sɩmma die, ta biŋ mana yaa ka tɩɩŋ dɩ sɩna die.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Nyɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ mana ka yi bʋnyɩ, vuota yaa nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ, ta dɔŋɩsɩ yaa nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ, ta nembisi dɩaŋ dɩ yaa nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ, ta zaasɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yaa nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Ŋmɩŋ sikpeŋ nyinti yaa a nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ta tɩŋgbaŋ ŋaŋ nyinti dɩaŋ dɩ yaa a nyɩŋgbanɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ nyinti yaa a tʋaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ ta tɩŋgbaŋ ŋaŋ nyinti dɩaŋ dɩ yaa a tʋaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Ŋmɩnnɩ yaa ka tʋaŋ chaanɩŋ, ta chɩɩkʋ dɩaŋ dɩ yaa ka tʋaŋ chaanɩŋ, ta chɩŋmarɩsɩsɩ dɩaŋ dɩ yaa a tʋaŋ chaanɩŋ. Ta chɩŋmarɩsɩ sʋŋ a mana dɩ yaa chaanɩŋ tɔra tɔra.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Die dɩ vaa dɩ sɩmma kunti dɩ keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me. Vuota nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ŋaaŋ kpie mɩŋ, ta ba guohe, ama a hagɩ kuŋ me a nan tarɩgɩ, dama ka kaaŋ daansɩ kpi.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Ba ka guo tɩ aŋaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, ama tɩ nan keŋ hagɩ aŋaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, ba bɩ ka guo tɩ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ, ama tɩ nan keŋ hagɩ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Ba guo tɩ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mɩŋ, ama tɩ keŋ hagɩ tɩ nan ye nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yine Haalɩbʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ. Nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yine tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩŋgbanɩŋ beri mɩŋ ta nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yine Haalɩbʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ beri.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Bʋmbʋŋaŋ vuoŋ Adami die yiwo vuota; ama vuodieke dɩ kenne kʋaka yiwo Haalɩ dieke dɩ yɩna tɩ miivoli wo.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Daa nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yine Haalɩbʋ die wolinne keŋ, tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die wolinne keŋ, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta Haalɩbʋ tʋaŋ dɩ keŋ.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Bʋmbʋŋaŋ vuoke die nyɩŋ wa tantɩ ma, ta vuodieke dɩ diisine bule wo dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Lele, n yaala n dagɩ nɩ yaa gamma ligire dieke nɩ chɩasɩnana dɩ nɩ suŋŋi Ŋmɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia ma wa wɩa, n yaala nɩ yime sɩba n dagɩna Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ diekemba dɩ benne Galasia tɩŋ ma mɩŋ.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yine tɩŋgbaŋka gie sɩɩtɩ sɩɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ nyɩna tantɩ ma mɩŋ, ta vuodiekemba dɩ yine arɩzanna sɩɩtɩ dɩ sɩɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ nyɩna arɩzanna ma wa.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Alasiri daaŋ mana, vuoŋ mana vʋarɩ ligire ʋ baaŋ bɩagɩ dene a nyɩŋ ʋ tʋnna a ye ligire dieke wo a dʋaŋ, amʋ maŋ keŋ keŋ nɩ kaaŋ bɩ chɩasɩ bɩbra.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Ta tɩ mana nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ nɩasɩna Adami vuodieke die nyɩna tantɩ ma wa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ daaŋ kaanɩ tɩ arɩzanna nyɩŋgbanɩŋ nan sɩɩ sɩba Masia nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Maŋ keŋ keŋ n nan maagɩ gbaŋtɩ a tʋŋ vuodiekemba nɩ vʋarɩna aŋ ba yaa nɩ piinisisi a ga Jerusalemi.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n bala nɩ dɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ yine nɔŋ aŋaŋ zɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma; ta nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ hʋʋgɩna kaaŋ bɩagɩ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Dɩɩ yi dɩ mʋ dɩ n ga n gbaŋ, die nɩnɩŋ tɩ nan lagɩsɩ ga.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: N yaala n balɩ nɩ wʋlɔbɩrɩkɩrɩ gie mɩŋ, tɩ bataŋ nan kpi, ta tɩ bataŋ dɩaŋ nan dɩ bie ba mɩsɩ ma; ama tɩ mana nan tarɩgɩ.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: N saŋya dɩ n daagɩ Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma; ta a kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan keŋ nɩ jigiŋ a kaagɩ nɩ, dama dɩ mʋ dɩ n ga Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi kantaŋ dɩ kʋŋ, tɩ mana nan tarɩgɩ bʋnyɩ lagɩ lagɩ sɩba ninkamɩsɩŋ. Kantana dɩ kʋŋ, kunti nan hagɩ kuŋ me aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dieke dɩ kana kaaŋ kpi, ta tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne tɩ miivoli mi dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi wo, nyɩŋgbanɩŋ nan tarɩgɩ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Fʋ zɩ n nan dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ daraa ale yaa n nan wɩarɩ nɩ jigiŋ wʋyaalɩka gie mana; ta nɩ nan keŋ suŋŋi mɩŋ aŋ n tɩaŋ ga jigidieke n yaala n ga.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ hʋʋgɩnana keŋ tarɩgɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kana ka hʋʋga, ta nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kpinene keŋ tarɩgɩ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kana ka kpiye.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: N ka yaala dɩ n keŋ kaagɩ nɩ daraa ŋmɩna ta tɩaŋ ga, ama dɩɩ yi tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yi sieŋ, n nan yʋasɩ nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Die wɩa naa dɩ keŋ yi, ta nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ hʋʋgɩnana dɩ keŋ tarɩgɩ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kana ka hʋʋga, ta nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kpinene keŋ tarɩgɩ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ kana ka kpiye, saŋka mi wudieke dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ nan suuli, dɩ, Ʋ nyaŋŋɩ wa kuŋ a kpatɩ. Dɩ bɩ maagɩ dɩ.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: N nan wɩarɩ Efesusi gie a ga tʋgɩ Pentikosi dʋʋga saŋŋa.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Kuŋ sɩa fʋ nyaŋŋɩkʋ dɩ beri bɩbra? Kuŋ, lalɩa fʋ baa fʋ yi a nagɩ tɩ yi wahala ma bɩbra.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: dama sieŋ yuori yɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ dɩ n mɩŋŋɩ tʋŋ Masia tʋʋmaha, ama dataasɩ pam dɩaŋ benne.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Bɩaŋ vasa kuŋ dɩ ŋaaŋ yɩa tɩ wahala, ta bɩaŋ dɩ yese ka hagɩrɩŋ a nyɩna mɩraha ma.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Dɩɩ yi Timoti dɩ keŋ keŋ nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo chaantɩ, dama ʋ gbaŋ ʋ tʋma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋmaha sɩba n tʋnnana die.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Ama tɩ waasa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ yɩna tɩ nyaŋŋɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia ma.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Nɩ sɩmma dɩ vuoŋ keŋ daansɩ wa yɔrɩ, ama nɩ suŋŋi wo ta vaa aŋ ʋ nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ amʋ ʋ nan viiri keŋ n jigiŋ, dama n daansa wa aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmballɩlɩ sieŋ me.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Die wɩa n nɩmballɩ, nɩ mɩŋŋɩ zie keŋ keŋ ta daa vaa jaaŋ vʋarɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ Masia kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ ma. Nɩ nagɩ nɩ gbaŋ a tʋma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ tʋʋma saŋŋa mana, dama nɩ sɩba a baarɩ tʋʋma diekemba nɩ tʋnnana a yɩa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, kaaŋ daansɩ nan yɔrɩ.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Yaa gamma tɩ nɩmbʋa Apolosi, n jʋʋsa wa dɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ keŋ kaagɩ nɩ, ama ʋ ka yaala ʋ keŋ lele gie, ama ʋ keŋ ye sieŋ ʋ nan keŋ.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Nɩ gbara ta mɩŋŋɩ zie keŋ keŋ nɩ yadaka ma, ta yallɩma dembisi aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Yime jaaŋ mana aŋaŋ choti.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Nɩ wɔŋ sɩba Sitifanasi aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ, die ba yine vuodiekemba dɩ wolinne yi Masia yada Akaya tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta die nagɩ ba gbaŋ a tʋma yɩa Ŋmɩŋ vuosi. N jʋʋsa nɩ n nɩmballɩ.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: dɩ nɩ saagɩ a yɩma vuosi gie chanchaalɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ gʋtɩna ba ma a tʋma pam a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ vuosi.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: N sʋŋ fɩalɩya mɩŋ aŋaŋ Sitefanatusi aŋaŋ Fotunatusi aŋaŋ Akayisi keniŋ wɩa, dama die nɩ wone n jigiri, die n yaa wa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, ama ba kenne wo n bɩ wo sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Die ba kpaŋŋɩsɩ mɩŋ sɩba die ba kpaŋŋɩsɩna nɩ die wo, nɩ yɩma vuosi gie chanchaalɩŋ jɩlɩma.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ diekemba dɩ benne Asia tɩŋgbaŋ mana waasa nɩ. Akuyila aŋaŋ Pirisila Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ lagɩsɩnana jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ba tigiri me wo waasa nɩ vɩɩnɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ ma.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Tɩ nɩmballɩ mana dɩ benne giena waasa nɩ. Nɩ waasɩ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Manɩŋ Pɔɔli maagɩna waasɩkʋ gie giena aŋaŋ n nuuŋ.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka cho tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, kaatɩ bie ʋ ma! Maranata ; ka chɩaŋ yiwo, Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, keŋ.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa suŋŋi nɩ.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: N cho nɩ mana aŋaŋ wusie Yisa Masia saaŋ ma.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Nɩ yaalɩma choti a tɩaŋ jaaŋ mana; ama ta bɩ yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka piini aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, a tɩaŋ a mana nɩ faasɩ yaalɩma piini dieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Vuodieke dɩ balala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ʋ kana ka wʋmma ta Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yɩ wa wa, ʋ bala yɩa Ŋmɩŋ daa vuota, dama vuoŋ ka sɩba a chɩasɩ, ʋ ŋaana bala lɔbɩrɩŋ wɩa Halɩkasɩka hagɩrɩŋ ma wa.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ yiŋŋine bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, bala yɩa vuota ta suŋŋe be ta kpaŋŋɩsa ba ta yɩa ba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Vuodieke dɩ balala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ dieke ʋ kana ka wʋmma suŋŋe ʋ gbaŋ nyɩɩna; ama vuodieke dɩ yiŋŋine bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa suŋŋe Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: N tɩŋ yaala nɩ mana balɩma nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba nɩ kana ka wʋmma, ama a tɩaŋ ka n tɩŋ yaala nɩ mana yiŋŋi balɩma Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mɩŋ. Vuodieke dɩ yiŋŋi bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ka yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ vuodieke dɩ balala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ dieke ʋ kana ka wʋmma, sie vuoŋ bie mi ta nan bɩagɩ yiŋŋi balɩ wɩaha a dagɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ aŋ ka suŋŋi be.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: N nɩmballɩ, dɩɩ yi maŋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ a bala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba n kana ka wʋmma, bɩa suŋŋiŋ maŋ yɩa nɩ? Sie maŋ keŋ a yuori nyindieke dɩ nyɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ a dagɩ nɩ, yaa yɩaŋ, yaa a yiŋŋi balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩ nɩ, yaa dagɩkʋ a taŋ.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Dɩɩ yi nyindiekemba dɩ wone mɩsɩ ba yaa laala dɩ ka tarɩga a kpaŋ, lalɩa vuosi dɩ baa ba yi a sɩmma a kpaŋ chɩaŋ?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuodieke wɩɩnana kantana dɩ ka mɩŋŋɩ wɩɩ nɩŋŋmɩna wɩɩla, mɩnɩa baaŋ yi siri aŋaŋ tɔŋ?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ, dɩɩ yi fʋ bala yɩa vuosi aŋaŋ jabalɩkɩ dieke ba kana ka wʋmma, lalɩa ba baa ba yi a sɩmma wudieke fʋ balala chɩaŋ? Dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba bala yɩa wa bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Nyɩŋbalɩka yiri yiri pam benne dʋnɩa ma, ta a mana dɩ yaa a chɩasɩ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi maŋ ka sɩba jabalɩkɩ dieke vuoŋ dɩ bala yɩa mɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ balala a yɩa mɩŋ nan dɩ yi chaanʋ n jigiŋ ta n dɩaŋ yi chaanʋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Nɩ senne yaalala Halɩkasɩka piini wo, nɩ yaalɩma piini dieke dɩ baaŋ suŋŋi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Vuodieke dɩ balala nyɩŋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ʋ kana ka wʋmma, mʋ ʋ jʋʋsɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ a ye piini dieke ʋ baaŋ bɩagɩ a daga a chɩasɩ.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ dieke n kana ka wʋmma, n haalɩŋ jʋʋsɩnana ama n ka sɩba wudieke n balɩnana chɩasɩ.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Lalɩa maŋ baa yi? N nan jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ n haalɩŋ, ta bɩ jʋʋsɩ wa aŋaŋ n sʋŋanyile; ta chɩa yɩla a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ n haalɩŋ; ta bɩ chɩa yɩla aŋaŋ n sʋŋanyile.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Dama fʋ bɩra Ŋmɩŋ ta waasa wa aŋaŋ fʋ haalɩbʋ nyɩɩna ma, lalɩa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba a chɩasɩsɩ dɩ baa ba gʋtɩ fʋ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ? Dɩɩ yi ba ka sɩba a chɩasɩsɩ, lalɩa ba baa ba sɩmma dɩ fʋ bɩrɩŋ yiwo wusie?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Dɩɩ yi fʋ Ŋmɩŋ bɩrɩkʋ dɩ vɩɩna gbaŋ, ama ka kaaŋ suŋŋi vuoŋ.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: N waasa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ n bɩagɩ bala wʋbalɩkɩ dieke n kana ka wʋmma a tɩaŋ nɩ wʋnyɩ mana.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Ama maŋ bie Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ lagɩmɩŋ sʋŋ, n nan dɩ yaala dɩ n balɩ nʋawɩɩŋ anʋ diekemba dɩ yalla chɩasɩ aŋ ka suŋŋi n chanchaalɩŋ, a tɩaŋ n baa bala nʋawɩɩŋ tuse tuse jabalɩkɩ dieke n kana ka wʋmma ma.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ daa yilime sɩba ballɩbɩsɩsɩ, ama bɩaŋ yiile nɩŋ nɩ sɩmma sɩba ballɩbɩsɩ, ama nɩ yilime sɩba nyɩŋkʋra.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ, N nan daansɩ vaa chaanɩŋ diekemba dɩ balala jabalɩkɩ gaasɩ aŋ ba balɩ wɩa a yɩ n vuosi. Ba nan balɩ a yɩ ba tɩŋgbaŋ gaaŋ nyɩŋbalɩka ma die mana yɔrɩ n vuosi kaaŋ wʋŋ a yɩ mɩŋ.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Die wɩa, wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ dagɩtɩ a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia yada wa, daa vuodiekemba dɩ yine wo yada wa. Ama Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ yiwo dagɩtɩ a yi yada tieliŋ, daa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia yada wa.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Dɩɩ yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ mana dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ, ta vuoŋ mana dɩ bala jabalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma, ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ yaa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba a wɩa dɩ keŋ juu, ba kaaŋ balɩ dɩ nɩ juo yɩɩŋyaatɩ?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ dɩ lagɩŋ taŋ, ta tuose Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yiŋŋi bala yɩa taŋ ta vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ yaa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba a wɩa dɩ keŋ juu, ba nan mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ba yiwo tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ yaa gamma wudieke ba wʋnna wa ta dii ba gbaŋ gbaŋ sarɩya.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Ta yuori balɩ wudieke dɩ benne ba sʋgɩtɩ ma, ta sʋʋŋ gbigiri a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta baarɩ dɩ, Wusie, Ŋmɩŋ seŋ bie nɩ jigiŋ.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n balɩkʋ gie chɩaŋ? Dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ lagɩsɩ taŋ dɩ nɩ jɩama Ŋmɩŋ, ta wʋnyɩ ŋaaŋ yaa wa yɩlɩ, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dagɩŋ, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yuorine a dagɩ wa, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ʋ kana ka wʋmma, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ bɩagɩ bala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ʋ kana ka wʋmma a chɩasɩ, nɩ yime genhe mana sie dieke dɩ mʋna a suŋŋime Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba yaala ba balɩ wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma ba daa tɩaŋ vuosi bale yaa bataa, ba balɩma wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ, ta vuoŋ dɩaŋ bemme mi a daga a chɩasɩ kaanɩ kaanɩ.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta vuodieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a dagɩ a chɩasɩ dɩ wori, die nɩŋ vuodieke dɩ balala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ʋ kana ka wʋmma tarɩ ta balɩma yɩma ʋ nyɩɩna ma aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Nɩ vaa naazʋalɩŋ bale yaa bataa balɩma Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, ta vuotɩalɩkaha jɩɩma wudiekemba ba balala wa.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ kalɩ nɩ sʋŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yuori ʋ wɩaha a dagɩ wa, ta ʋ yaala ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, vuodieke dɩ balala wa tarɩ.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Nɩ mana nan bɩagɩ yiŋŋi balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa wʋnyɩ wʋnyɩ aŋ ka yi vuoŋ mana bʋgɩrɩ aŋ ka kpaŋŋɩsɩ vuoŋ mana.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Vuodieke dɩ yalla piini dieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ nan bɩagɩ yigi ʋ gbaŋ a chɩɩsɩ ʋ saŋŋa.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ ka wa tɩ dɩ tɩ yime wɩa tɔra tɔra, ama ʋ wa tɩ dɩ tɩ bemme sɔmm. Die dɩ mʋ dɩ sɩmma saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ vuosi dɩ keŋ lagɩŋ.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: Hɔgʋba mʋ ba mʋʋ ba nʋa sɔmm Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ, ama ba mʋ ba sʋʋŋ ba gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta wʋmma sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ dagɩna die wo.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba yaala ba sɩmma wɩɩŋ, ba pɩasɩ ba chʋllɩŋ tige me, dama ka yiwo viivi dɩ hɔgʋ balɩ wɩa Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie die ka woliŋ piili wo nɩ jigiŋ, yaa daa nɩ nyɩɩna ma jigiŋ ka keŋ tʋgɩ.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Vuodieke dɩ sɩba dɩ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa yaa ʋ yaa Halɩkasɩka piini, ʋ sɩmma a baarɩ wudieke n maagɩnana yɩa nɩ naa yiwo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ mɩra mɩŋ.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ zeti wɩaha gie, nɩ gbaŋ daa tuosime ʋ wɩa.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Die wɩa n nɩmballɩ, nɩ yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yiŋŋi balɩkʋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, ama nɩ da kagɩma vuodiekemba dɩ yaala wʋbalɩkɩ gaasɩ diekemba ba kana ka wʋmma balɩŋ.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Nɩ yime wɩɩŋ mana vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ aŋ a dɩa taŋ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Manɩŋ Pɔɔli vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ n yi Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmbʋa Timoti maagɩnana gbaŋkʋ gie, a yɩa nɩnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Korinti tɩŋ ma aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Akaya tɩŋgbaŋ mana.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ ta bɩ yɩ nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ waasɩ Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia Chɔɔŋ wa, ta bɩ yi vuodieke dɩ chɩgɩnana tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ pam wa ta tɩ suŋŋiŋ mana dɩ nyɩna ʋ jigiri.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ʋ suŋŋe tɩ mɩŋ tɩ wumugisike me, amʋ tɩ nan bɩagɩ a suŋŋi vuodiekemba dɩ benne wumugisike yiri yiri me wo aŋaŋ suŋŋi dieke tɩ tuone a nyɩŋ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiri.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Tɩ wahala diile Masia wɩa dɩ dala die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ suŋŋiŋ nan dɩ dala wʋnɩŋ Masia wɩa.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ die wahala, dɩ yiwo nɩ suŋŋiŋ aŋaŋ nɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ wɩa. Dɩɩ yi tɩ ye suŋŋiŋ, dɩ yiwo nɩ wɩa, dɩ tɩ suŋŋi nɩ, ta nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ye suguru ta bɩagɩ a dii wahala sɩba tɩ dine dene wo.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ ka chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ nɩ wɩa, dama tɩ sɩba a baarɩ nɩ dine wahala sɩba tɩ dine die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ nan dɩ yese suŋŋiŋ sɩba tɩ yesinene die wo.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Tɩ nɩmballɩ, tɩ yaala tɩ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ yaa gamma wahala dieke tɩ dine Asia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Die wahalɩka die faasɩ dala mɩŋ a tɩaŋ tɩ hagɩrɩŋ ta die tɩ bɩ wo tama dɩ tɩ nan dɩ bie tɩ miivoli me.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: ta die yile dɩ tɩ baa tɩ kpi mɩŋ. Naa die yiye mɩŋ dɩ ka dagɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ daa yallɩma tama tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ ma, ama tɩ nagɩma tɩ yada a yi Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma ma, vuodieke dɩ sʋgɩrɩnana kunti a nyɩna kuŋ me wo.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Wʋnɩŋ die ʋ gbatɩna tɩ a nyɩŋ kuŋ daampɔyɩ ma, ta ʋ nan bɩ a gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ; tɩ yaa wa tama ʋ ma ta sɩba dɩ ʋ nan dɩ gbata tɩ saŋŋa mana mɩŋ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: Nɩ suŋŋi tɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ nan tuo vuosi pam dɩ jʋʋsɩnana a yɩa tɩ wa jʋʋsɩŋ, ta nan suŋŋi tɩ, aŋ vuosi pam nan waasɩ wa tɩ wɩa.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Wudieke tɩ yalla kuule yiwo naa: dɩ tɩ bɩagɩ bala dɩ wudieke mana tɩ yine tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die tɩ yiye mɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋchɩgɩla aŋaŋ sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, die die dɩ faasɩ yi saŋŋa dieke tɩ bene nɩ jigiri. Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ wɩa die vana tɩ beri die wo daa vuota yɩaŋ.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Tɩ maaga wudieke nɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a karɩŋ ta sɩba a chɩasɩ a yɩa nɩ, ama ta yaa tama dɩ nɩ nan keŋ sɩba ha vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: sɩba nɩ wone sɩba tɩ wɩaha bɩta die wo, dɩ nɩ nan keŋ a sɩba a mana ta kuule tɩ wɩa sɩba tɩ gbaŋ tɩ bala tɩ kuule nɩ wɩa wa daa dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia dɩ bala ʋ keŋ wo.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Die n wɔŋ sɩba naa wɩa ta die saŋ dɩ n yiŋŋi keŋ kaagɩ nɩ, amʋ nɩ nan ye suŋŋiŋ bule.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Dama die n saŋya mɩŋ dɩ n keŋ kaagɩ nɩ maŋ keŋ tɩaŋ gara Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta bɩ keŋ a kaagɩ nɩ maŋ keŋ yiŋŋi kieŋ, aŋ nɩ suŋŋi mɩŋ aŋ n ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Die n sɩŋsaŋkaha die yiwo laalɩŋ? Yaa wudieke die n sana dɩ n yiwo die yiwo sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ ŋaana bala dɩ, Wa, wa ta kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba bɩ bala dɩ, Aayɩ, aayɩ die wo?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine wusietieŋ wo yiwo n daansɩatieŋ, dɩ nʋa dieke die n yɩ nɩ wa ka yi Wa aŋaŋ Aayɩ.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Dama Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, vuodieke manɩŋ aŋaŋ Salasi aŋaŋ Timoti die dɩ mʋʋlɩ ʋ wɩa a yɩ nɩ wa, ʋ ka bala dɩ, Wa ta yiŋŋi bala dɩ, Aayɩ, ama ʋ ma jaaŋ mana yiwo Wa.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Dama ʋ yine Wa Ŋmɩŋ nʋa mana ma. Naa wɩa tɩ bala dɩ, Wusie Yisa Masia ma, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ ye bɩrɩŋ.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩna tɩ aŋaŋ nɩ dɩ tɩ tʋmma tʋʋma, ta ʋ mɩŋŋɩ a zieŋ tɩnɩŋ Masia ma.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: wʋnɩŋ ʋ magɩlɩna tɩnɩŋ dɩ tɩ yiwo ʋ sɩɩtɩ, ta ʋ woliŋ yi ʋ Halɩkasɩka tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma aŋ ka dagɩ dɩ ʋ nan keŋ yɩ tɩ wudieke mana ʋ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ tɩ wa.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yine n daansɩatieŋ dɩ wudieke die dɩ yine ta maŋ ka keŋ Korinti nɩ jigiŋ yiwo die n ka yaala n datɩ nɩ tɩbɩŋ wɩa.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Tɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a mugisi nɩ ta dagɩ nɩ nɩ mʋna nɩ yi yada die, dama tɩ sɩba dɩ nɩ yaa yada ta mɩŋŋɩ zie vɩɩnɩŋ, ama tɩ tʋma aŋaŋ nɩ dɩ tɩ dagɩ nɩ, nɩ baaŋ yi die ta yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: N saŋya mɩŋ dɩ n kaaŋ bɩ yiŋŋi keŋ nɩ jigiŋ a yi nɩ sʋŋ chʋʋsɩ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Dama dɩɩ yi maŋ chʋʋsɩ nɩ sʋŋ, mɩnɩa bala ʋ tɩalɩ a yɩ mɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ? Daa nɩnɩŋ?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Naa wɩa die maŋ maagɩ gbanɩŋ dama die n ka yaala n keŋ aŋ vuodiekemba dɩ tɩnna mʋ ba yɩma mɩŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩkʋ, chʋʋsɩ n sʋŋ. Dama n yaa tama dɩ n sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Die n maagɩ gbaŋkʋ a yɩ nɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ pam aŋaŋ nɩntaantɩ; dɩ ka sɩɩ sɩba dɩ n yaala nɩ sʋŋ chʋʋsɩ mɩŋ ama die n yaala nɩ sɩmma n faasɩna cho nɩ dene.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ yi wɩɩŋ ta n sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ, ʋ ka yi a chʋʋsɩ wa n nyɩɩna ma, ama ʋ yi chʋʋsɩ wa nɩ mana mɩŋ, dɩɩ yi ʋ ka chʋʋsɩ nɩ mana ama nɩ jʋsʋ, n bala die dama n ka yaala n faasɩ nagɩ nɩŋŋmɩna aŋaŋ wa wa wɩa.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Nɩ mana die lagɩŋya mɩŋ a datɩ ʋ tɩbɩŋ die wɩa dɩ mʋ.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Ama lele dɩ mʋ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ nagɩ chaa wa ta kpaŋŋɩsɩ wa, amʋ ʋ kaaŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ pam ta nan.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: N jʋʋsa nɩ, nɩ vaa ʋ sɩmma dɩ nɩ seŋ cho wo mɩŋ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Wudieke wɩa die n maagɩna nɩ gbaŋkʋ gie yiwo naa, die n yaala n magɩsɩ nɩ mɩŋ a ye dɩ nɩ tuose n nʋaŋ jaaŋ mana ma mɩŋ?.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ nagɩ vuoŋ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa wa, n gbaŋ n nagɩ a chaa wa mɩŋ, dɩɩ yi vuoke dɩ yi wudieke dɩ mʋ dɩ n nagɩ chaa, n nagɩ chaa wa mɩŋ nɩ wɩa Masia nɩŋŋa.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: amʋ Sitaani da yesime sieŋ a gaaŋ tɩ, dama tɩ sɩba ʋ sieti dɩ sɩna die mɩŋ.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Die n gana a tʋgɩ Tirosi tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa dɩ n mʋʋlɩ Yisa wʋvɩɩnaha die maŋ mɩŋŋɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yuori wa ʋ tʋʋma sieŋ mi mɩŋ a yɩ mɩŋ.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Ama die dɩ faasɩ mugisi mɩŋ mɩŋ die n kana ka ye n nɩmbʋa Tatusi mi wo wɩa. Die wɩa, maŋ taaŋ vuosi mi, ta tɩaŋ ga Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Tɩ mʋ tɩ bɩrɩma Ŋmɩŋ, dama tɩ taasɩna aŋaŋ Masia wa wɩa die vana tɩ nyaŋŋa jaaŋ mana ma. Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩna tɩ dɩ tɩ yuori Masia dagɩ jigiŋ mana; ta ʋ wʋvɩɩnaha sɩbɩŋ sɩɩ sɩba tulaari dieke nyʋʋgɩŋ dɩ faasɩna vɩɩna ta nyʋʋgɩ jigiŋ die wo.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Dama tɩ sɩɩ sɩba tulaari dieke nyʋʋgɩŋ dɩ vɩɩna Masia dɩ nagɩna tɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta nyʋʋgɩbʋ dɩ juo vuodiekemba dɩ yesinene gbatɩtaanɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yese gbatɩtaanɩŋ ma.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yese gbatɩtaaŋkʋ nɩŋ jigiŋ, tɩ sɩɩ sɩba nyʋʋgɩ dieke dɩ yalla kuŋ a kieŋ, ama vuodiekemba dɩ yesinene gbatɩtaaŋkʋ nɩŋ jigiŋ, tɩ sɩɩ sɩba nyʋʋgɩ dieke dɩ yalla miivoli a kieŋ. Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ tʋŋ tʋʋmaha gie.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Tɩ ka sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ nagɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha a bɩrɩŋ nyʋŋ diilehe, ama tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ bala wusie dama wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ tʋnna tɩ; die wɩa tɩ bie ʋ nɩŋŋa, ta mʋʋla ʋ wɩaha Yisa yiko me.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Naa bɩ dagɩ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ daga tɩ gbaŋ mɩŋ? Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ vuoŋ maagɩ tɩ siara gbaŋtɩ a yɩ nɩ? Yaa tɩ tuo tɩ siara gbaŋtɩ nɩ jigiŋ sɩba vuosi bataŋ dɩ yinene wo mɩŋ? Aayɩ.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine tɩ siara gbanɩŋ ta marɩ tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ vuoŋ mana karɩŋ a sɩmma.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Dɩ bie wo yaalɩŋ ma dɩ nɩnɩŋ nɩ yine Masia gbaŋkʋ mi ta ʋ maagɩ a tʋŋ tɩ aŋaŋ ka, dɩ ka yi gbaŋ dieke vuota dɩ maagɩna aŋaŋ jamaakɩŋ nyaaŋ a dɩɩsɩ tanteelikiŋ me, ama Masia die maagɩna ha aŋaŋ miivolitieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka vuota sʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Wudieke wɩa n bala naa, yiwo tɩ yaa wa kpaadensiŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa Masia suŋŋiŋ wɩa.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Tɩ ka daga tɩ gbaŋ dɩ tɩ nan bɩagɩ yi wɩɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ hagɩrɩŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ wɩa tɩ bɩagɩ tʋma tʋʋmaha.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ vana tɩ mʋ aŋaŋ ʋ nʋahaalɩkʋ wɩaha tʋʋma; ta nʋahaalɩkʋ ka yi Mosisi mɩrɩ diekemba die ʋ maagɩna wa, dɩ yiwo Halɩkasɩka sɩɩtɩ. Mɩraha die yaa wa kuŋ a kieŋ ama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yɩa tɩ miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ maagɩna ʋ mɩraha a diisi tanteelikiri me wo, saŋka mi die ʋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ die sʋʋŋ a vaa Mosisi nine dɩ chaaŋ; chaaŋkʋ wɩa Izara vuosisi die ka bɩagɩ a daansa wʋnɩŋ Mosisi nine chaaŋ aŋaŋ ka chaaŋkʋ dɩ banana mana. Dɩɩ yi mɩrɩ dieke dɩ yalla kuŋ a kieŋ wo dɩ keŋ aŋaŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: die nɩŋ Halɩkasɩka tʋʋma sieŋ nɩŋ nan faasɩ dala pam a tɩaŋ die.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Dɩɩ yi mɩrɩ dieke dɩ dagɩna vuosi dɩ ba yaa bɩabʋ die dɩ keŋ aŋaŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka tʋʋma sie dieke dɩ vana vuosi dɩ bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩnaha Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa nan dɩ beri aŋaŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ a faasɩ tɩaŋ die.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Wusie, mɩraha die dɩ keŋ waarɩ ka chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ dama chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ tɩanna ka kenne.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta mɩrɩ dieke dɩ tɩaŋ gara die dɩ keŋ aŋaŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ, die nɩŋ Halɩkasɩka tʋʋma sie dieke dɩ bala ka bemme yaa gamma haahuu wo nan dɩ yaa chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ a faasɩ tɩaŋ die.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Tama ka gie wɩa tɩ faasɩ yaa sikimiŋ dene.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Ama tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ ka sɩɩ sɩba Mosisi, die dɩ ŋaana naga garɩŋ a ligi ʋ nine ta ka yaala Izara vuosi ye chuuli dieke dɩ benne ʋ nine chaaŋ ta tɩaŋ gara wa.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Ama ba sʋŋanyile die ka yuoriye; saŋŋa dieke ba keŋ karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ nʋakʋʋrɩ gbaŋkʋ, dɩ ŋaaŋ sɩɩ sɩba garɩkʋ ye ligi wo ba sʋŋanyile a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne. Garɩkʋ ŋaaŋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a taasɩ aŋaŋ Masia nyɩɩna ma.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: A keŋ tʋgɩ jinne ba keŋ karɩŋ Mosisi mɩraha, garɩkʋ ŋaaŋ ligi ba sʋgɩtɩ mɩŋ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yiŋŋi a dɩɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, garɩkʋ ŋaaŋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Ta lele tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo yine Halɩkasɩka, ta jigidieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ bene, dʋŋŋʋ beriŋ beri mɩŋ.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Die wɩa tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ garɩkʋ ka bɩ ligi tɩ nine, ta tɩ mana dɩ yese tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ yalla chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ pam dene, ta a tarɩga tɩ ta tɩ nɩasa wa, ta tɩ chuuliŋ bɩ gʋta saŋŋa mana, ta chuuliku dɩ nyɩna wʋnɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ vuodieke dɩ yine Halɩkasɩka jigiŋ.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa ʋ nagɩ tʋʋmaha gie a yɩ tɩ nuusi me, die wɩa tɩ ka chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Die wɩa tɩ zeti lɩmɩŋ ma tʋʋma aŋaŋ tʋmbɩatɩ diekemba dɩ yalla viivi a kieŋ wo, ta tʋma ta ka lɔbɩra vuosisi, ta tɩ ka tarɩga Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha dɩ tɩ gaama vuosi, ama tɩ gbaŋ tɩ bie wo Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa ta mʋʋla ʋ wɩaha yaalɩŋ ma aŋaŋ wusie, dɩ vuoŋ da yallɩma sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ yaa gamma tɩ tʋʋma ma.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha dɩ lɔbɩrɩ, dɩ lɔbɩrɩ wa vuodiekemba dɩ faasɩna bɩa ta kpie jigiŋ nyɩɩna ma mɩŋ.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Ba ka yi Yisa yada dama Sitaani vuodieke tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi dɩ jɩanna ligi wo ba sʋŋanyile mɩŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ ligine ba nine dɩ ba daa yesime chaaŋkʋ dɩ kenne ba jigiri. Chaaŋkʋ nyɩŋ wa wʋvɩɩŋ dieke dɩ dagɩna Masia chuuliŋ wa jigiŋ; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ nɩasɩŋ dee dee.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Tɩ ka mʋʋla wɩaha yaa gamma tɩ ma, ama tɩ mʋʋla yaa gamma Yisa Masia ma mɩŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, ama ta daga dɩ tɩnɩŋ tɩ yiwo ʋ tʋntʋntɩŋ a yi nɩ, ʋ saaŋ wɩa.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ kʋrɩŋ ma wa dɩ, Chaanɩŋ chaaŋ lɩmɩŋ ma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ vana chaaŋkʋ dɩ chaaŋ tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma, dɩ tɩ sɩmma ʋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ sɩna die; kanɩŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ chaannana Yisa Masia nine me.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Ama tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla nyindiekemba dɩ yalla nyʋarɩ gie sɩɩ sɩba tɔgɩŋ kpaga, dɩ a dagɩ dɩ hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ tɩanna hagɩrɩŋ mana yiwo Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ ama daa tɩnɩŋ.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Tɩ die wahala pam saŋŋa mana, ama a ka nyaŋŋa tɩ; saŋŋa a taŋ tɩ ŋaaŋ ka sɩba tɩ baaŋ yi die, ama tɩ ŋaaŋ yaa wa tama.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: ba mugise tɩ mɩŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ ka vasa tɩ, ba ŋaaŋ nagɩ tɩ taana ama ba ka bɩagɩ kʋa tɩ.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Wɩaha gie tɩ yesinene dagɩya dɩ tɩ saaga saŋŋa mana mɩŋ dɩ tɩ kpi kuŋ dieke chanchaaŋ Yisa dɩ kpine amʋ vuosi nan ye ta sɩmma dɩ tɩ yaa wa ʋ miivoli mi.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Tɩ yene bie tɩ mɩsɩ ma naa tɩ bie wo kuŋ nuusi me Yisa wɩa, amʋ ʋ miivoli dieke dɩ benne tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dieke dɩ balla ka keŋ kpi wo keŋ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Naa dagɩya dɩ kuŋ yigi wo tɩ nuusi a yallɩ, dɩ nɩnɩŋ nɩ ye miivoli haalɩŋ.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, N yalla yada wɩa maŋ balɩ wɩa, tɩ gbaŋ tɩ yaa wa Halɩkasɩ dieke dɩ vana vuoke mi dɩ yi yada wa, die wɩa tɩ gbaŋ dɩ yi yada ta bala naa.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Dama tɩ sɩba dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die sʋgɩrɩ wa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan daansɩ sʋgɩrɩ tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dɩna Masia wa kuŋ me, a yaa tɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Wɩaha mana beri mɩŋ dɩ a suŋŋi nɩ, dɩɩ yi vuosi dɩ yie Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha yada dɩ keŋ dala ta gʋta, aŋwaasɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ tuo a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ nan faasɩ dala, amʋ ʋ nan ye jɩlɩma.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Naa wɩa tɩ yaa wa tama ta gbalɩga; aŋaŋ tɩ kʋrɩnnana ta tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ gbalɩga naa, ama Ŋmɩŋ yɩa tɩ haalɩtɩ hagɩrɩŋ daaŋ mana mɩŋ.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Die wɩa, wahala bɩta dieke tɩ dinene ta kaaŋ yʋasɩ wa, nan dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke dɩ dala ta wo kpatɩŋ a yɩ tɩ, ta sʋgɩfɩalɩkʋ nan tɩaŋ wahalaka pam.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Dama tɩ baga ka bie nyindieke tɩ yesinene me, ama tɩ baga bie wo nyindieke tɩ kana ka yese me mɩŋ. Jadieke tɩ yesinene kaaŋ yʋasɩ ta tɩaŋ ga, ama jadieke tɩ kana ka yese wo nɩŋ nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke tɩ yalla lele naa sɩɩ sɩba tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie tigiŋ mɩŋ, dɩɩ yi a keŋ kpi, tɩ yaa wa tigi dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan yɩ tɩ arɩzanna ma, daa vuota mɩna tigiri mi, ka nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Tɩ fɔbɩla mɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka gie, ta chɩɩsa daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balla ʋ wuri tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩŋgbaŋka ta yɩ tɩ arɩzanna ma nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Dama tɩ keŋ ye tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ haalɩkʋ tɩ haalɩtɩ kaaŋ wɔŋ a waarɩ jigiberisikiŋ.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Ama tɩ haalɩtɩ dɩ yene suu nyɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo, tɩ ŋmuuse mɩŋ. Daa tɩ ka bɩ yaala nyɩŋgbaŋka gie beriŋ, ama tɩ yaala arɩzanna nyɩŋgbaŋka mɩŋ, dɩ dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi miivolike nan keŋ nyii kuŋ aŋ a kpatɩ.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Ŋmɩŋ yine vuodieke dɩ wʋnsɩna tɩ ta vaa tɩ yi siri yaa gamma tarɩgɩkʋ wɩa, ta yɩ tɩ ʋ Halɩkasɩka aŋ ka daga dɩ ʋ nan keŋ yi wudieke ʋ balla dɩ ʋ nan keŋ yiwo mana.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ yallɩ wa kpaadensiŋ saŋŋa mana mɩŋ, ta tɩ wɔŋ sɩba dɩ dɩɩ yi tɩ haalɩtɩ dɩ bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma, tɩ wo tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jigiŋ.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: dama tɩ dɩa yada sieŋ mɩŋ daa jadieke tɩ nine dɩ yesinene.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Tɩ yallɩ wa kpaadensiŋ sɩba a wone balɩ wa, dama dɩ mʋ dɩ tɩ vaa nyɩŋgbaŋka gie ta ga tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ jigiŋ.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi tɩ bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me yaa tɩ keŋ bie ʋ jigiŋ, wudieke tɩ faasɩna yaala yiwo dɩ tɩ fɩalɩma ʋ sʋŋ.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Dama tɩ mana nan keŋ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma Masia nɩŋŋa sarɩya diile daraaŋ, aŋ vuoŋ mana nan keŋ tuo ʋ tune sɩba ʋ tʋʋma dɩ sɩna dene tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ka vɩɩna yaa dɩ ka vɩɩna dɩ mʋ wa dɩ tieŋ.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Tɩ chɩgɩnana Ŋmɩŋ dene wo, die wɩa tɩ kpaŋŋɩsa vuosi dɩ ba yiŋŋi keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Tɩ beriŋ dʋa wa yaalɩŋ ma mɩŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta n gbaŋ dɩ yaa tama dɩ ka bɩ dʋa wa yaalɩŋ ma a yɩ nɩ.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Tɩ ka bɩ kuule nɩ jigiŋ, ama tɩ yaala tɩ yɩ nɩ sieŋ mɩŋ aŋ nɩ bɩagɩ a nɩgɩ nɩ nyʋa tɩ wɩa, amʋ nɩ nan bɩagɩ a yaa wɩɩŋ dɩ nɩ yiŋŋi balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kuulinene yaa gamma wudieke ba yene vuota dɩ yinene, ama ta da wudieke dɩ benne ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Dɩɩ yi tɩ juo yɩɩŋyaatɩ, dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, ta dɩɩ yi ta tɩ bie tɩ sʋŋanyile me, dɩ yiwo nɩ wɩa.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Masia choti pɔgɩlɩna tɩ; tɩ wana sɩba dɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ die tuo vuoŋ mana kuŋ a kpi wo, dɩ dagɩya dɩ vuoŋ mana kpiye mɩŋ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Wʋnɩŋ die ʋ tuone vuoŋ mana kuŋ a kpi dɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yene bie ba miivoli me daa bemme a yɩma ba gbaŋ ama ba bemme a yɩ wʋnɩŋ vuodieke die dɩ tuone ba kuŋ a kpi wo, ta bɩ hagɩ kumbu me wo.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ ka bɩ daansa vuota sʋŋanyile dɩ sɩna die ta die vuosi sarɩya; die tɩ daansɩ wa Masia aŋaŋ vuota sʋŋanyile dɩ sɩna dene ta dii ʋ sarɩya, ama lele tɩ ka bɩ daansa dene.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ taasɩ aŋaŋ Masia die vuoke yiwo vuohaalʋ, ʋ berikʋʋrɩ gaya mɩŋ ta berihaalɩŋ dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Naa mana nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ tarɩgɩna tɩ Masia wɩa, dɩ tɩ daa bɩ yi ʋ dataasɩ, ama ta yi ʋ zʋalɩŋ, ta bɩ yɩ tɩ ʋ tʋʋma dɩ tɩ vaa vuosi gbaŋ yi ʋ zʋalɩŋ.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Naa dagɩya dɩ Masia die dɩ kpine wo die yɩna Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ dɩ ʋ yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi ʋ zʋalɩŋ, ta nagɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba, ama ta nagɩ ʋ wʋvɩɩna dieke dɩ dagɩna ba ʋ yine be ʋ zʋalɩŋ dene, a yi tɩ nuusi me.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Die wɩa tɩ yiwo Masia tʋntʋntɩŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vana tɩ wasa vuosi. Masia naŋ ma tɩ zie ta jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ saagɩ ta yi Ŋmɩŋ zʋalɩŋ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Dama tɩ wɩa ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi Masia, vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi bɩaŋ, dɩ zie tɩnɩŋ tʋmbɩatɩtieliŋ jigizielikiŋ ta chii tɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ amʋ tɩ nan yi vuovɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ wʋnɩŋ Masia wɩa.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Die wɩa, tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ tʋnnana Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋmaha ʋ suŋŋiŋ wɩa naa, n jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ daa vaa ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha yi yɔrɩ.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ: Die saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩna dɩ n chɩgɩ nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ, die n wʋŋ nɩ jʋʋsɩŋ mɩŋ; ta saŋŋa dɩ tʋgɩna dɩ n gbatɩ nɩ a taaŋ wa, die n gbatɩ nɩ mɩŋ. Nɩ wʋmma! Saŋka keŋye mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ chɩgɩ nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ. Daarɩ gie nɩ baa nɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ ka yie wudieke dɩ baaŋ kagɩ vuoŋ Yisa Masia sieku me, amʋ vuoŋ keŋ baarɩ dɩ tɩ tʋʋmaha ka vɩɩna.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Ama tɩ beriŋ dagɩya dɩ tɩ seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntɩŋ, dama tɩ yese wahala, aŋaŋ mugisiŋ, aŋaŋ wɩa, ama tɩ dii suguru a mana ma mɩŋ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Vuosi die tɩba tɩ aŋaŋ kpaasa, ta naga tɩ a yie dansarɩka juoŋ me, ta tɩ wɩa kpɩkpaaŋ dɩ ŋaaŋ lagɩsa a yaa watɩ, ta tɩ bɩ tʋma tʋʋmaha a gbalɩgɩ, ta ka gʋʋra yuŋ a taŋ, ta ka die saŋŋa a taŋ gbaŋ.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Tɩ yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ, ta sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, ta yaa suguru aŋaŋ burichini ta suuli aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka ta cho vuosi aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: ta bala ba Ŋmɩŋ wudieke dɩ yine wusie wo, ta yallɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩbʋ, ta bɩ yaa chaanɩŋ beri dieke dɩ yine tɩ dembisiŋaŋ jaaŋ tɩ yaa waga ta bɩ yaa gbarɩ tɩ gbaŋ.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Ta vuosi bataŋ dɩ yɩa tɩ jɩlɩma ta bataŋ dɩaŋ zɩa tɩ, ta bataŋ dɩ chʋʋsa tɩ saara, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ bɩra tɩ. Bataŋ dɩa gaama vuosi ama ta tɩ bala wusie.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Tɩ wo saaŋ vuota jigiŋ, ama ta yaa saaŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta vuosi dɩ yile dɩ tɩ baa tɩ kpi mɩŋ ama ta tɩ ko bie tɩ mɩsɩ ma, ta nɩga tɩ pam ama tɩ ka kpiye;.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: ta tɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ ŋaaŋ yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ saŋŋa mana. Tɩ yiwo zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ama ta yi vuosi dɩ bɩrɩŋ nyintitieliŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ; ta wo nyinti ama ta sɩɩ nyinti mana.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Tɩ zʋalɩŋ Korinti vuosi, tɩ wɔŋ balɩ nɩ tɩ sʋŋ wɩa mana a yɩ nɩ, tɩ cho nɩ pam.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Daa tɩnɩŋ tɩ zetine dɩ tɩ kaaŋ cho nɩ, ama dɩ yiwo nɩnɩŋ nɩ zetine dɩ nɩ kaaŋ dɩ cho tɩ.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Lele n wa bala a yɩa nɩ sɩba nɩ yiwo n ballɩ: nɩ cho tɩ aŋaŋ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ mana sɩba tɩ chone nɩ dene wo.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Nɩ daa yi jabalɩmɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Masia yada! Vuovɩɩnʋ aŋaŋ vuobɩaŋ nan bɩagɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ? Ta chaanɩŋ aŋaŋ lɩmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ bie aŋaŋ taŋ?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Masia aŋaŋ Sitaani nan bɩagɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ? Vuodieke dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kana ka yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrʋ nan bɩagɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Bugile nan bɩagɩ bie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me? Tɩnɩŋ tɩ yine Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku, dama tɩnɩŋ die Ŋmɩŋ die baarɩ dɩ: Manɩŋ aŋaŋ n vuosi nan lagɩŋ a beri ta n nan dɩ bie aŋaŋ ba; n nan dɩ yi ba Ŋmɩŋ, ta ba dɩaŋ dɩ yi n vuosi.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Die wɩa ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ gbaŋ, a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi sʋnsʋŋ, ta vaa bɩaŋ mana yiile, ta n nan tuo nɩ.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: N nan yi nɩ Chɔɔŋ, ta nɩ yi n nahaalɩŋ aŋaŋ n hɔgʋlɩasɩ. Manɩŋ Hagɩrɩtieŋ Ŋmɩŋ bala naa.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: N nɩmballɩ, tɩ yene nʋaha gie a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ naa wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ vʋarɩ tɩ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ jadieke nɩŋ mana dɩ nan bɩagɩ a yi tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩtɩ dɩgɩntɩ, ama ta chɩgɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Nɩ yuori nɩ sʋgɩtɩ a yɩ tɩ. Tɩ ye ka yi nɩ wʋnyɩ mana bɩaŋ, yaa a yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ nɩ wʋnyɩ, yaa a nyɩrɩ vuoŋ.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: N ka bala naa dɩ n galɩŋ nɩ. Dama die n wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ faasɩ cho nɩ pam. Dɩɩ yi tɩ kpi yaa tɩ bie tɩ mɩsɩ ma, tɩ nan dɩ beri aŋaŋ nɩ.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: N yaa wa tama pam nɩ ma, ta nɩga nyʋʋŋ nɩ wɩa. Nɩ kpaŋŋɩsɩ mɩŋ pam, ta tɩ wahala diile me mana ama n yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam mɩŋ.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Die tɩ kenne a tʋgɩ Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa, die tɩ ka bɩagɩ voosi. Die tɩ ye mugisiŋ pam a nyɩŋ lʋga mana; vuosi die waga aŋaŋ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie wɩa; ta tɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ kpaŋŋɩsɩnana vuodiekemba sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩna wa die vaa Tatusi dɩ keŋ tɩ jigiŋ a keŋ a kpaŋŋɩsɩ tɩ.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Daa ʋ keŋku nyɩɩna ma die kpaŋŋɩsɩna tɩ, ama die ʋ bɩna a balɩ tɩ nɩ kpaŋŋɩsɩna wa dene wo. Ta balɩ tɩ nɩ bɩna faasɩ yaala nɩ ye mɩŋ dene, dɩ nɩ faasɩ yaa wa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, ta bɩ balɩ nɩ nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩ dene dɩ nɩ tuo n nʋarɩ, naa die vaa n sʋŋ dɩ faasɩ fɩalɩ.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Dɩɩ yi gbaŋ dieke die n maagɩna a yɩ nɩ wa dɩ yi nɩ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, ama n sʋŋ ka chʋʋsɩya n maagɩna ka. Die n yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ bɩta die n mɩŋŋɩna dɩ ka vaa nɩ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa bɩta wa.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Ama lele n yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, daa n yine nɩ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ wɩa, ama nɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩkʋ dɩ vana nɩ vaa nɩ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ wɩa. Ŋmɩŋ die vaa nɩ ye sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, die dɩ yine kanɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ yaa nyʋarɩ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Dama dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ wɩa, kanɩŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩkʋ mi nan yi ʋ vaa ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ ta ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, ta kaaŋ chʋʋsɩ ʋ sʋŋ bɩbra; ama tɩŋgbaŋka gie sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ nɩŋ yaa kuŋ kieŋ.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vana nɩ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ wa, nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ye ka yɩna nɩ nyʋarɩ; nɩ baga bie Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ma, ta nɩ bɩ faasɩ yaala nɩ dagɩ dɩ nɩ wo bɩaŋ; ta nɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ hagɩ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ, ta bɩ chɩga Ŋmɩŋ; nɩ bɩ yaala nɩ ye mɩŋ; nɩ yaala nɩ yi choti; nɩ wɔŋ yi lagɩ a datɩ vuodieke dɩ yine bɩabʋ tɩbɩŋ mɩŋ. Nɩ yine naa wa wɩa, ka seŋ dagɩ dɩ nɩ wo bɩaŋ wɩɩrɩ ma.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Die wɩa, daa vuodieke dɩ yine bɩabʋ wɩa die maŋ maagɩ gbaŋkʋ mi yaa vuodieke ʋ yine bɩabʋ. Ama die n maagɩ ka mɩŋ dɩ nɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ nɩ baga die dɩ bemme tɩ ma dene.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Lele nɩŋ tɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ kpatɩya mɩŋ. Ta die tɩ bɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam dama die nɩ vaa Tatusi sʋŋ dɩ dʋa ta die yiwo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam, die wɩa tɩ gbaŋ tɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Die n wɔŋ bɩrɩ nɩ mɩŋ a yɩ wa ta nɩ ka vaa maŋ dii viivi. Die tɩ ŋaana bala nɩ wudieke wo yiwo wusie. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ wudieke tɩ bala a yɩ Tatusi yaa gamma nɩ ma wa dɩ keŋ yi wusie.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Die wɩa ʋ keŋ tɩɩnsɩ nɩ tuone wo chaantɩ dene aŋaŋ jɩlɩma, ta tuo wudieke ʋ bala nɩ wa aŋaŋ jɩlɩma dene ʋ ŋaaŋ faasɩ cho nɩ mɩŋ.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: N yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam dɩ n nan bɩagɩ nagɩ n tama a yi nɩ ma.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Tɩ nɩmballɩ, tɩ yaala nɩ sɩmma Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla dɩ vana Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ benne Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ ma dɩ yine wudieke.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Die ba ye wahala aŋaŋ mugisiŋ aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ pam, ama die mana yɔrɩ, die ba yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam ta dagɩ burichini ta yi Ŋmɩŋ piini pam.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: N nan bɩagɩ dii ba siara dɩ ba yɩya mɩŋ a tʋgɩ ba hagɩrɩŋ nʋaŋ, die ba yɩ a tɩaŋ ba tɩnna mʋ ba yɩ dene gbaŋ. Die dɩ yiwo ba dʋŋŋʋ ba yɩa.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: ta die faasɩ jʋʋsɩ tɩ mɩŋ, dɩ tɩ yɩ ba sieŋ aŋ ba suŋŋime Ŋmɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Die ba yi a tɩaŋ tɩ tɩŋ nan yile dɩ ba nan yi dene wo. Die ba woliŋ nagɩ wa ba gbaŋ a yɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba nagɩ ba gbaŋ a yɩ tɩ, sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalɩnana dene wo.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Die Tatusi die yine vuodieke dɩ yine nɩ piili tʋʋmaha, die wɩa tɩ jʋʋsɩ wa mɩŋ dɩ ʋ suŋŋi nɩ aŋ nɩ kpatɩ nɩ choti tʋŋ vɩɩŋ dieke nɩ piiline wo.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Sɩba nɩ yine Yisa yada, ta mɩŋŋɩ yie jaaŋ mana vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ wa, ta sɩba ʋ wɩa vɩɩnɩŋ, ta bɩagɩ bala taŋ ʋ wɩaha, ta dɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, ta bɩ cho tɩ dene wo, tɩ yaala nɩ yime tʋnvɩɩnaha mi gbaŋ dene aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Wudieke n bala nɩ naa ka yi mugisiŋ dɩ nɩ yime, n wɔŋ balɩ nɩ mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi bataŋ nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩ dene dɩ ba yɩ piini, dɩ nɩ yi aŋ n ye nɩ senne cho be dene.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Nɩ sɩba tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla dɩ sɩna dene. Die ʋ yiwo nyintitieŋ, ama nɩ wɩa die ʋ bɩrɩŋ wa zɔɔlɩntieŋ, ʋ bɩrɩna zɔɔlɩntieŋ amʋ nɩ nan bɩrɩŋ nyintitieliŋ.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Kpaaŋ dieke n yaala dɩ n yɩ nɩ wʋnna: Die bɩnɩ ma, die nɩ yine vuodiekemba die dɩ wolinne yaala nɩ yɩ piinike, ta yi vuodiekemba dɩ wolinne a yɩ. Nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a tʋŋ ka a kpatɩ.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Nɩ nine die ŋmɩntɩya mɩŋ aŋaŋ piinike yɩla. Die wɩa nɩ vaa nɩ nine bɩ ŋmɩntɩ aŋ nɩ yɩ kpatɩ, nɩ yɩma sɩba nɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a yɩ dene.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaala ʋ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ piini, ta yɩ ʋ yalla dene, Ŋmɩŋ nan tuoke. Ŋmɩŋ ka yaala vuoŋ yɩ wa piini a tɩaŋ ʋ yalla dene.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: N ka yaala nɩ yɩma nɩ chanchaalɩŋ ta dime wahala ka ma. N yaala nɩ mʋʋma taŋ.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Wudieke n balala wʋnna: Sɩba nɩ yalla pam lele gie wo, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ nɩ suŋŋi wo vuodiekemba dɩ wone wo, ta dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ wo saŋŋa dieke, ama ta ba yallɩ pam, ba nan suŋŋi yɩ nɩ gbaŋ. Naa nan vaa nɩ mʋ taŋ.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dene wo dɩ, Vuodiekemba die dɩ lagɩsɩna maana pam wa die dɩ ka dii ta tɩala, ta vuodiekemba dɩaŋ die dɩ kana ka lagɩsɩna pam wa, die ka waarɩ jaaŋ.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Tɩ bɩra Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ ŋaana yi Tatusi baga dɩ bie nɩ ma sɩba tɩ baga dɩ benne nɩ ma dene wo.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Die tɩ jʋʋsɩna Tatusi dɩ ʋ kaagɩ nɩ wa, die ʋ saagɩya mɩŋ, ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yine nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ʋ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ; ʋ kieŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ choti.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Tɩ nan vaa tɩ nɩmballɩ wʋnyɩ aŋaŋ wa keŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Tɩ nɩmbʋa wa gie dɩ mʋʋlɩnana Masia wʋvɩɩnaha dene wɩa die wɩa vana Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana dɩ bɩra wa.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: A bɩ gʋtɩ, Yisa dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana vʋarɩna wa dɩ ʋ beeli tɩ aŋ tɩ nagɩ piinisisi a ga suŋŋi Ŋmɩŋ vuosi dɩ yine zɔɔlɩntielibe ta bie Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma. Naa nan kɔtɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ, ta bɩ dagɩ dɩ tɩ faasɩ yaala tɩ suŋŋime tɩ chanchaalɩŋ dene.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Tɩ nan nagɩ ligirehe pam a ga suŋŋi be, tɩ yaala wɩɩŋ mana tʋgɩsɩ mɩŋ amʋ vuoŋ kaaŋ ye sieŋ a balɩ a chʋʋsɩ tɩ saaŋ yaa gamma tɩ pɔgɩlɩna ligirikpɩɩmaha gie dene wo.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Tɩ yaala tɩ yiwo wudieke dɩ mʋna Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ vuota nɩŋŋa mɩŋ.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Tɩ nan bɩ vaa tɩ nɩmbʋa wʋnyɩ gʋtɩ ʋ ma a keŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Saŋŋa mana tɩ yese tɩ nɩmbʋa wa beriŋ dɩ sɩna dene, ta sɩba a baarɩ ʋ nine ŋmɩntɩya mɩŋ dɩ ʋ tʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋmaha. Ta lele ʋ yalla tama pam nɩ ma wa wɩa, ʋ nine dɩ faasɩ ŋmɩntɩ dɩ ʋ suŋŋi nɩ.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Tatusi nɩŋ, ʋ yiwo n tʋntʋŋ chanchaaŋ ta gʋta suŋŋe mɩŋ maŋ suŋŋe nɩ. Ta tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ kenne aŋaŋ Tatusi wa nɩŋ ba zene Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba naŋ ma, ta ba beriŋ dɩ vasa vuosi dɩ bɩra Masia saaŋ.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Nɩ dagɩma tɩ nɩmballɩ mi nɩ choti amʋ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba mana nan dɩ sɩba dɩ tɩ yaa wusie tɩ nɩgɩna nyʋʋŋ yaa gamma nɩ ma wa.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Dɩ ka bɩ yi talasɩ dɩ n maagɩ gbanɩŋ a balɩ nɩ yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ vuosisi dɩ bene Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa suŋŋiŋ wɩa.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: N wɔŋ sɩba a baarɩ nɩ yiwo nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ nɩ suŋŋi, ta nɩ wɩa maŋ kuule a yɩa Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ vuosisi, ta balɩ ba dɩ bɩŋ ma mana nɩnɩŋ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ diekemba dɩ benne Akaya tɩŋgbaŋ ma dɩ yi siri dɩ nɩ suŋŋi be. Nɩ yalla chotike mi dɩ nɩ suŋŋi wo wɩa, Masedonia vuosi jusu gbaŋ yiwo siri dɩ ba nan dɩ suŋŋe.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Die wɩa n tʋma tɩ nɩmballɩ gie nɩ jigiŋ amʋ nɩ nan yi siri aŋaŋ nɩ suŋŋiŋ aŋ n kuuline dene yaa gamma nɩ ma wa kaaŋ yi yɔrɩ.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Dɩ daa die, dɩɩ yi Masedonia tɩŋgbaŋ vuosi dɩ dɩ n kʋaŋ a keŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ta nɩ ka yi siri, tɩ nan dii viivi, dama die tɩ bɩrɩ nɩ ba jigiŋ mɩŋ.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Die wɩa, maŋ yile dɩ dɩ vɩɩna n tʋŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ gie aŋ ba woliŋ keŋ n ma ta suŋŋi nɩ aŋ nɩ lagɩsɩ piini dieke nɩ yine nʋaŋ dɩ nɩ nan yɩ wa. Amʋ maŋ keŋ keŋ a nan yi siri aŋ ka dagɩ dɩ nɩ yɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ choti, daa vuoŋ mugisine nɩ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Nɩ tɩɩnsɩ balɩkʋ gie: vuodieke dɩ bʋrɩna bɩta nan gobi bɩta, ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ bʋrɩna pam nan gobi pam.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Vuoŋ mana yɩ ʋ sanna ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ nan yɩ dene, ta daa yɩ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ yaa mugisiŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ yaala vuodieke dɩ yɩnana piini aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ yɩ nɩ jaaŋ mana ta nɩ kaaŋ waarɩ jaaŋ. Nɩ nan dɩ yallɩ nyindiekemba mana nɩ yaalala saŋŋa mana, ta nan ye a tɩaŋ nɩ yaalala dene, ta nɩ nan bɩagɩ a suŋŋe vuodiekemba dɩ wone.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: sɩba dɩ maagɩna dene Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dene wo dɩ, Vuodieke nuuŋ dɩ tɩɩrɩnana a yɩa zɔɔlɩntieliŋ, ʋ burichini wo kpatɩŋ.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana kpaarʋ nyɩmbʋrɩkaha, ta yɩa wa vuoŋ mana nyindiikehe, wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan yɩ nɩ jaaŋ mana nɩ yaalala aŋ a dalɩma, ta nɩ nan faasɩ a suŋŋe vuosi.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Ʋ nan vaa nɩ yallɩma nyinti pam saŋŋa mana, amʋ nɩ nan bɩagɩ suŋŋe vuosi saŋŋa mana, amʋ vuosi pam nan bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ yaa gamma nɩ piini dieke ba tuone a nyɩŋ tɩ jigiri.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ suŋŋe Ŋmɩŋ vuosi a yɩa ba jadieke ba wone, naa nan vaa vuosi pam bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ suŋŋe be, die nan dagɩ ba dɩ nɩ seŋ a tuo Masia wʋvɩɩnaha mɩŋ ta dɩ ha, ta vuosi pam bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ die wɩa ta bɩ bɩrɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩ suŋŋine be aŋaŋ vuoŋ mana dene wo.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Die wɩa ba keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩa nɩ, ba nan jʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ choti, dama ba ye dɩ Ŋmɩŋ bie nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma pam ta suŋŋe nɩ pam.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
