local: dama vuodiekemba dɩ yaalala ba bɩrɩŋ ligiretielibe, banɩŋ ba junene magɩsɩŋ ma, aŋaŋ chachabɩ ma, ta yaa gaanlɔla aŋaŋ lɔlɩbɩatɩ diekemba dɩ chʋʋsɩnana vuosi ta vasa ba nanna yɔrɩ wa.

eng: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. Some have wandered away from the faith and have impaled themselves with a lot of pain because they made money their goal.
local: Ligire yaalɩŋ pam yiwo wʋbɩatɩ yiri yiri piiliŋ. Vuosi bataŋ nagɩ wa ba gbaŋ a yi ligire yaala ma die wɩa ba vaa yadaka sieku ta ka bɩ yi Yisa yada, die wɩa ba yese sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ yiri yiri ba faasɩna a yaala ligirehe wɩa.

eng: But as for you, man of God, run away from all these things. Instead, pursue righteousness, holy living, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
local: Ama fʋnɩŋ Timoti, yiwo vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ vuoŋ, dɩ vɩɩna dɩ fʋ vʋarɩ fʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ wɩaha gie mana ma, ta fʋ kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ a yime wudieke dɩ nannana Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ ta bemme sɩba wʋnɩŋ. Yallɩma yada, aŋaŋ choti, aŋaŋ hanɩa ta bemme sɔmm.

eng: Compete in the good fight of faith. Grab hold of eternal life—you were called to it, and you made a good confession of it in the presence of many witnesses.
local: Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ ta wagɩma waagɩ vɩɩnɩŋ yaa gamma yadaka ma, ta pɔgɩlɩma miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ wa; Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ wana fʋ mɩŋ ta fʋ tuo miivolike saŋŋa dieke die fʋ yɩna fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ siara vɩɩnɩŋ, vuosi pam nɩŋŋa wa.

eng: I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when testifying before Pontius Pilate.
local: Ta lele n wa yaala fʋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa vuodieke dɩ yɩnana jaaŋ mana miivoli, aŋaŋ Yisa Masia vuodieke die dɩ bala yɩ Pontiwosi Paliti aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna yaa gamma ʋ yada ma yaalɩŋ ma wa.

eng: Obey this order without fault or failure until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ fʋ nan dɩdɩ wudiekemba mana n yɩna fʋ wa amʋ vuoŋ kaaŋ galɩŋ fʋ, a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia dɩ bala ʋ yiŋŋi keŋ wo.

eng: The timing of this appearance is revealed by God alone, who is the blessed and only master, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dieke tɩ bɩrɩnana wa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ nyɩɩna yine jakʋʋŋ, ta yi naakpɩɩma mana dɩaŋ Naaŋ, ta bɩ yi nyɩŋkʋra mana jakʋʋŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ bala ʋ vaa Yisa Masia yiŋŋi keŋ saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: He alone has immortality and lives in light that no one can come near. No human being has ever seen or is able to see him. Honor and eternal power belong to him. Amen.
local: Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma kana ka kpie; ʋ bie wo chaankpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke vuoŋ dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a gbigi mi; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo vuodieke vuota dɩ yene ka yeye yaa a nan bɩagɩ ye wo; wʋnɩŋ ʋ mʋna bɩrɩŋ ta ʋ hagɩrɩŋ nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu. Wusie.

eng: Tell people who are rich at this time not to become egotistical and not to place their hope on their finances, which are uncertain. Instead, they need to hope in God, who richly provides everything for our enjoyment.
local: Kpaaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine nyintitieliŋ tɩŋgbaŋka ma wa dɩ ba daa nɩgɩma ba nyʋra yaa nagɩ ba tama a yi a ma, dama die nyinti yiwo bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ, ama balɩ ba dɩ ba yallɩma ba tama Ŋmɩŋ ma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine nyintitieŋ ta yɩa tɩ jadieke mana tɩ yaalala dɩ tɩ tuome ta bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ.

eng: Tell them to do good, to be rich in the good things they do, to be generous, and to share with others.
local: Kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba tʋma tʋnvɩɩna, ta ba tʋnvɩɩnaha faasɩ dalama ta yi vuodiekemba nuusi dɩ tɩɩrɩnana aŋaŋ piini yɩɩla.

eng: When they do these things, they will save a treasure for themselves that is a good foundation for the future. That way they can take hold of what is truly life.
local: Die nan yi aŋ ba yallɩma nyinti arɩzanna ma, nyindiekemba dɩ kana ka chʋʋsa aŋ ka gbarɩ ba a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ba bala ba keŋ mi wo; ta ba nan dɩ yallɩ miivoli dieke dɩ yine wusie miivolike.

eng: Timothy, protect what has been given to you in trust. Avoid godless and pointless discussions and the contradictory claims of so-called knowledge.
local: Timoti, mɩŋŋɩ gbara wʋvɩɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna yi fʋ nuusi me wo. Vʋarɩ fʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ gaantɩ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ azama dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ ta ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta vuosi bataŋ dɩ zɩ ta wasa ka sɩbɩŋ wa.

eng: When some people adopted this false knowledge, they missed the goal of faith. May grace be with you all.
local: dama vuosi bataŋ baarɩ dɩ ba yallɩ wa sɩbɩkʋ gie, die wɩa ba vaa yadaka sieku. Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ bemme nɩ ma.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ keŋ dii naarɩ ta dii vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba miivoli me aŋaŋ kpiiliŋ sarɩya wa, wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa maŋ bala fʋ naa.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: dɩ fʋ mʋʋlɩma ʋ wɩa saŋŋa mana, dɩɩ yi saŋŋa dɩ mʋ yaa ka ka mʋ, ta balɩma vuosi ba wʋbɩatɩ, ta kpaama ba, ta kpaŋŋɩsɩma ba ta dagɩma ba wɩaha aŋaŋ suguru saŋŋa mana.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Dama saŋŋa kieŋ ta vuosi kaaŋ dɩ wʋmma dagɩvɩɩnʋ, ama ba nan keŋ dɩ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba lɔlɩbɩatɩ, ta nan dɩ wʋmma yɩa dɩdagɩrɩŋ pam diekemba dɩ dagɩnana wa wudieke ba yaalala ba wʋŋ.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Ba kaaŋ dɩ wʋmma wusieke, ama ta wʋmma yɩa ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ dagɩŋ.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: Ama fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ, mʋ fʋ yigi wo fʋ gbaŋ wɩɩŋ mana ma, ta yallɩma hanɩa wahala ma ta mʋʋlɩma Yisa Masia wʋvɩɩnaha, ta tʋma tʋŋ dieke mana Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a yɩ fʋ wa.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Dama manɩŋ nɩŋ, saŋŋa gbigiye mɩŋ dɩ n yi sɩba kaaba Ŋmɩŋ koriŋ me. Saŋŋa keŋye mɩŋ dɩ n nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: N mɩwa mɩvɩɩnɩŋ, ta tʋŋ n tʋʋma kpatɩ, ta ye pɔgɩlɩ yada wɩaha keŋ keŋ.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Die wɩa, nyʋarɩ dieke dɩ yine sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ nyʋarɩ dʋa gbarɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ arɩzanna ma; ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, vuodieke dɩ dine vuota sarɩya aŋaŋ wusie wo nan keŋ yɩ mɩŋ kanɩŋ nyʋarɩ sarɩya diile daraaŋ. Daa n nyɩɩna ma ʋ baa ʋ yɩ, ama ʋ nan yɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩaŋ dɩ yaalala dɩ ʋ keŋ wo.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ a keŋ n jigiŋ daraa ale wa gie.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: Dama Demasi dɩ faasɩna cho tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyinti pam wɩa, ʋ va mɩŋ mɩŋ ta ga Tesalonika tɩŋ, ta Kiresikensi dɩaŋ dɩ ga Galasia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta Tatusi dɩaŋ dɩ ga Dalimasia tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Luki nyɩɩna ma benne n jigiŋ. Nagɩ Maki a keŋ aŋaŋ wa, dama ʋ tʋʋma yaa nyʋarɩ pam a yɩ mɩŋ.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: N tʋŋ wa Tikikusi Efesusi tɩŋ ma.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: Fʋ keŋ kieŋ saŋŋa dieke, fʋ nagɩ n jayeekiwaŋ dieke n vana a taaŋ Tiroasi tɩŋ ma Kapusi jigiri aŋaŋ n gbaŋ pilikehe aŋaŋ gbaŋ tɩalɩkaha.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Alekizanda, vuodieke dɩ yine macheeri wo yi mɩŋ wʋbɩaŋ pam; Ŋmɩŋ nan datɩ ʋ tɩbɩŋ ʋ yine naa wɩa.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: Sɩmma fʋ gbaŋ aŋaŋ wa, dama ʋ waga aŋaŋ wʋvɩɩŋ dieke tɩ mʋʋlɩnana pam.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Die n wolinne bala dɩ n vʋarɩ n gbaŋ sarɩya diile saŋŋa wa, die vuoŋ ka zie n kʋaŋ, ba mana die chɩgɩ ta va mɩŋ mɩŋ; Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ chʋʋsɩ ba wɩɩrɩ gie wɩa.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ bie n jigiŋ ta yɩ mɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ maŋ bɩagɩ a mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha mana a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ ba wʋŋ. Wʋnɩŋ die gbatɩna mɩŋ a nyɩŋ naachigiŋ nʋaŋ ma.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ bɩna baa ʋ vʋarɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ mana ma, ta nagɩ mɩŋ a ga juu ʋ naarɩ ma arɩzanna ma aŋaŋ alaafɩa. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ sɩna bɩrɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu. Wusie.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: N waasa Pirisila aŋaŋ Akuyila, aŋaŋ Onesiforusi tigiŋ vuosi mana.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Erasitusi die wɩarɩ bie wo Korinti tɩŋ ma, ta maŋ vaa Tirofimusi Militusi tɩŋ ma dama die ʋ wo alaafɩa.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ ta fʋ keŋ ta tafaaŋ wa tʋgɩ. Yubulusi aŋaŋ Pudensi aŋaŋ Linusi aŋaŋ Kolodia aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmballɩ mana waasa nɩ.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ bemme aŋaŋ nɩ. Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ bemme nɩ mana ma.

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Manɩŋ Pɔɔli, vuodieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yɔmʋ ta bɩ yi Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ wa, manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩ a tʋŋ mɩŋ ʋ vuovʋarɩkaha jigiŋ dɩ n vaa ba yada hagɩrɩma, ta vaa ba sɩmma wusie dieke tɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ dɩ dagɩna.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: ta bɩ yɩa ba tama dɩ ba nan ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ kana ka chɩba wɩaha die wɔŋ yɩ tɩ nʋaŋ mɩŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ tɩ miivoli gie, ta wa keŋ naaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: ta saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna wa, die ʋ yuorihe a dagɩ tɩ ʋ wɩaha ma; ta die nagɩ wɩaha a yi n nuusi me maŋ mʋʋlaha sɩba tɩ Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ dagɩna mɩŋ dene wo.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: Manɩŋ n maaga yɩa fʋnɩŋ Tatusi, fʋnɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine n bʋavɩɩnʋ yada balɩŋka ma. Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi fʋ ta bɩ yɩ fʋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: Die n vaa fʋ mɩŋ Kireeti tɩŋ ma dɩ fʋ wʋnsɩ wudiekemba mana dɩ yine talasɩ, ta bɩ vʋarɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋra tɩŋ mana ma, sɩba n dagɩna fʋ dene wo.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wome bɩaŋ; ta yallɩma hɔgʋbalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna; ta ʋ ballɩ yi Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta da yi ballɩbɩatɩ aŋaŋ tɩbɩhagɩrɩtieliŋ.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Dama Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrʋ sɩna Ŋmɩŋ tʋʋma, die wɩa dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ ʋ da yallɩma bɩaŋ, ʋ ka mʋ ʋ yallɩma kalinbaani, yaa a jɩma sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ yɔrɩ yɔrɩ, yaa a yi vuodieke dɩ nyunene daaŋ a buge, yaa vuodieke dɩ yaalala waagɩŋ, yaa vuodieke dɩ faasɩna a yaala ligire pam.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: Ama ʋ mʋ ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ mɩŋŋɩna tuose chaanɩŋ ʋ tigiŋ me, ta cho dɩ ʋ yime wudieke dɩ vɩɩna, ta bɩ yi vuodieke dɩ yalla yɩaŋ, ta bemme vɩɩnɩŋ, ta bɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ, ta bɩagɩ a yige ʋ gbaŋ.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: Ta yi vuodieke dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a pɔgɩlɩ wusie wɩaha sɩba a dagɩna dene. Die nan vaa ʋ mɩŋŋɩ dagɩ vuosi dagɩ dieke dɩ vɩɩŋna, ta bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋa vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka saaga yɩa wusie dagɩkʋ.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: N bala fʋ naa dama nʋazetitielibe dala pam, ta Juu vuosi dɩ sɩɩ dene, ba naga ba wudiekemba dɩ wone nyʋarɩka a gaama vuosi mɩŋ.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ fʋ kagɩ ba wʋbalɩkaha gie, dama ba yalla ligire chotibɩatɩ wɩa ba daga vuosi wudieke dɩ kana ka vɩɩna dɩ ba dagɩ, die dɩ chɔrɩŋ tige ataŋ vuosi sʋŋanyile.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: Kireeti daa wʋnyɩ vuodieke dɩ yine dɩdagɩrɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die baarɩ dɩ, Kireeti vuosi mana yiwo ŋmɩnchɩbɩtieliŋ, ta bɩ yi vuobɩatɩ sɩba haagɩŋ dɔmbɩatɩ ta bɩ yi walɩnkpɩatieliŋ ta yaa wutuliŋ pam.

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Wudieke die ʋ bala wa yiwo wusie, die wɩa galɩŋ ba ba nine me amʋ ba nan yi wudieke dɩ yine wusie yada.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: ta daa bɩ wʋmma Juu vuosisi sunsobilitɔgɩtɩ, ta daa bɩ wʋmma a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ zetine wusie wɩaha dagɩkʋ.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ jigiŋ nɩŋ, jaaŋ mana yɩantɩya mɩŋ a yɩ ba, ama wʋbɩayiiribe dɩ kana ka yi Masia yada wa jigiŋ nɩŋ, jaaŋ ka yɩantɩya a yɩ ba, dama ba sʋŋanyile aŋaŋ ba yɩaŋ ka yɩantɩya.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Ba wasa ba gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi ama ba wuyiike dɩ ka dagɩ dɩ ba yiwo Ŋmɩŋ vuosi. Ba yiwo nʋazetitieliŋ, ta haa taŋ, ta ka mʋ ba yi wudieke dɩ vɩɩŋna.

eng: But you should talk in a way that is consistent with sound teaching.
local: Tatusi, mʋ fʋ dagɩma wudieke dɩ yine wusie.

eng: Tell the older men to be sober, dignified, sensible, and healthy in respect to their faith, love, and patience.
local: Kpaama nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ ba bemme sɔmm, ta yallɩma yɩaŋ, ta bɩ yigi ba gbaŋ, ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ Masia wɩaha, ta chome vuosi, ta bɩ yallɩma sikimiŋ.

eng: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in their behavior, teaching what is good, rather than being gossips or addicted to heavy drinking.
local: Bɩ kpaaŋ hɔgʋ nyɩŋkʋraha die gbaŋ gbaŋ, dɩ ba bemme sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ benne kasɩ dɩ mʋna dɩ ba bemme dene, ta daa yi kʋagaarɩŋ, ta daa bɩ nyume daaŋ a bugime. Ba mʋ ba dagɩma wudiekemba dɩ vɩɩŋna.

eng: That way they can mentor young women to love their husbands and children.
local: ta dagɩma hɔgʋlɩabaha dɩ ba chome ba chʋllɩŋ aŋaŋ ba ballɩ.

eng: and to be sensible, morally pure, working at home, kind and submissive to their own husbands, so that Gods word wont be ridiculed.
local: dɩ ba bɩ yigime ba gbaŋ, ta bemme kasɩ, ta yi hɔgʋvɩɩna ba chʋllɩŋ tige me, ta tuosime ba chʋllɩŋ nʋa. Die nan yi aŋ vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ balɩ wʋbɩatɩ yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha.

eng: Likewise, encourage the younger men to be sensible.
local: Bɩ kpaaŋ dalʋakaha die gbaŋ gbaŋ, dɩ ba yigime ba gbaŋ.

eng: in every way. Offer yourself as a role model of good actions. Show integrity, seriousness.
local: Fʋ gbaŋ bemme vɩɩnɩŋ jaaŋ mana ma, aŋ ka yi dagɩtɩ a yɩ ba. Dagɩma ba Masia wɩaha aŋaŋ wusie, ta vaa fʋ nine ŋmɩntɩ.

eng: and a sound message that is above criticism when you teach, so that any opponent will be ashamed because they wont find anything bad to say about us.
local: ta balɩma wudieke dɩ tʋgɩsɩna amʋ fʋ dataasɩsɩ kaaŋ ye wɩɩŋ a balɩ a chʋʋsɩ tɩ, die nan vaa ba dii viivi.

eng: Tell slaves to submit to their own masters and please them in everything they do. They shouldnt talk back.
local: Dɩ vɩɩna dɩ yɔŋɩsɩ tuosime ba tieliŋ nʋa, ta yime wudieke dɩ fɩalɩnana ba sʋŋ jaaŋ mana ma. Ba tielibe dɩ keŋ bala wɩa, ba da nɩgɩma nɩnhagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ ba.

eng: or steal. Instead, they should show that they are completely reliable in everything so that they might make the teaching about God our savior attractive in every way.
local: yaa ba gaarɩma ba. Dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ ba dagɩ dɩ ba yiwo vuovɩɩna ta yi wusietieliŋ saŋŋa mana amʋ ba nan vaa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ wa dagɩkʋ ye bɩrɩŋ wudieke mana ba yinene me.

eng: The grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa vuota mana sieŋ dɩ ʋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ; naa dagɩya ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla dɩ dala dene.

eng: It educates us so that we can live sensible, ethical, and godly lives right now by rejecting ungodly lives and the desires of this world.
local: Kanɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaka mi dagɩ tɩ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ vaa wudiekemba mana dɩ kana ka dɩ Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ, aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie lɔlɩbɩatɩ, ama ta yigi tɩ gbaŋ, ta bemme vɩɩnɩŋ, ta dɩa Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: At the same time we wait for the blessed hope and the glorious appearance of our great God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: dama tɩ chɩɩsa mɩŋ ta yaa tama dieke dɩ yɩnana tɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ kpeŋkpɩɩka aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia nan keŋ nyɩŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ ma.

eng: He gave himself for us in order to rescue us from every kind of lawless behavior, and cleanse a special people for himself who are eager to do good actions.
local: Die ʋ tuo wo tɩ kuŋ a kpi dɩ ʋ gbatɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ wʋbɩatɩ mana ma, ta yi tɩ sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ dɩ yine ʋ nyɩɩna ma sɩɩtɩ, dɩ tɩ yi vuodiekemba nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩna aŋaŋ tʋnvɩɩna yiile.

eng: Talk about these things. Encourage and correct with complete authority. Dont let anyone disrespect you.
local: Dagɩma ba wɩaha gie mana, ta kpaŋŋɩsɩma ba, ta kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna fʋ yiko dene, ta da vaa vuoŋ daansɩ fʋ yɔrɩ.

eng: Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities. They should be obedient and ready to do every good thing.
local: Kpaaŋ fʋ vuosisi dɩ ba yɩma naalɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla yiko jɩlɩma, ta tuosime ba nʋa ta yime siri dɩ ba yi wudiekemba mana dɩ vɩɩŋna.

eng: They shouldnt speak disrespectfully about anyone, but they should be peaceful, kind, and show complete courtesy toward everyone.
local: Balɩ ba dɩ ba da balɩma wʋbɩatɩ yaa gamma vuosi mi, ta da wagɩma taŋ, ta bemme sɔmm, ta yɩma vuoŋ mana jɩlɩma.

eng: We were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, and slaves to our desires and various pleasures too. We were spending our lives in evil behavior and jealousy. We were disgusting, and we hated other people.
local: Dama saŋŋa dieke die dɩ tɩanna wa die tɩ gbaŋ die tɩ wo yɩaŋ, ta yi nʋazetitieliŋ, ta dɩɩ sie dieke dɩ kana ka tʋgɩsɩya, ta dɩɩ lɔlɩbɩatɩ sieti yiri yiri. Die tɩ wo sʋŋanyilivɩɩna, ta die yaa sʋgɩbɩaŋ, die tɩ haa tɩ chanchaalɩŋ, ta ba dɩaŋ ba haa tɩ.

eng: But when God our saviors kindness and love appeared.
local: Ama saŋŋa die dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ tɩ gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ dagɩ tɩ dɩ ʋ yaa zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla ta cho vuoŋ mana.

eng: he saved us because of his mercy, not because of righteous things we had done. He did it through the washing of new birth and the renewing by the Holy Spirit.
local: ta gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ. Die dɩ ka yiwo tɩ tʋnvɩɩna wɩa ama die dɩ yiwo ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa die ʋ gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ, ta die dɩ vaa ʋ Halɩkasɩka die dɩ sɩɩ tɩ ta vʋarɩ tɩ tʋmbɩatɩ, ta yɩ tɩ mɩrɩhaalɩŋ aŋaŋ miivoli haalɩŋ.

eng: which God poured out upon us generously through Jesus Christ our savior.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ tɩ wa ʋ Halɩkasɩka, tɩ Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Yisa Masia wɩa.

eng: So, since we have been made righteous by his grace, we can inherit the hope for eternal life.
local: amʋ ʋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa tɩ nan bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩna ʋ jigiŋ, ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ, ta tɩ yaa tama dɩ tɩ nan ye wo.

eng: This saying is reliable. And I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God might give careful attention to doing good. These things are good and useful for everyone.
local: Wɩaha gie yiwo wusie. N yaala fʋ nagɩ fʋ sʋŋanyile mana a dagɩma ba naa, ta vuodiekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wa nan nagɩ ba saŋŋa mana a yie wʋvɩɩna. Wɩaha gie vɩɩna mɩŋ ta yaa nyʋarɩ a yɩa vuoŋ mana.

eng: Avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, and fights about the Law, because they are useless and worthless.
local: Ama nɩ vaa nɩnhagɩrɩtɔgɩtɩ, ta bɩ vaa nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ buuriŋ saara bɩɩsɩŋ, ta vaa waagɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ yaa gamma mɩraha ma, dama a yiwo wʋtɔgɩtɩ ta wo nyʋarɩ.

eng: After a first and second warning, have nothing more to do with a person who causes conflict.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yi jigiŋmabɩrʋ, fʋ kpaaŋ wa bʋnyɩ yaa bule me; dɩɩ yi ʋ baga dɩ wo kpaaŋkʋ ma, fʋ va wa ta da bɩ tɔgɩŋ wa.

eng: because you know that someone like this is twisted and sinful—so they condemn themselves.
local: Nan dɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi ka bɩ dɩɩ Masia, ta ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ dɩ dagɩ dɩ ʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ.

eng: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, try to come to me in Nicopolis, because Ive decided to spend the winter there.
local: Dɩɩ yi maŋ keŋ tʋŋ Atemasi yaa Tichikusi fʋ jigiŋ, fʋ kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ a keŋ n jigiŋ Nikopolisi tɩŋ ma, dama n nan dɩ bie mi wʋyaalɩka gie.

eng: Help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with enthusiasm so that they wont need anything.
local: Kpaŋŋɩ fʋ gbaŋ a suŋŋi Senasi, vuodieke dɩ yine lɔya wa, aŋaŋ Apolosi, aŋ ba ye wudieke mana ba yaalala a piili ba sieku.

eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: Dɩ mʋ dɩ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba yime tʋnvɩɩna saŋŋa mana, ta suŋŋime vuosi ba wumugisike me, ta da bemme beri dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: Vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne n jigiri waasa fʋ. Waasɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba ta cho tɩ wo. Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi nɩ mana.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Manɩŋ Pɔɔli vuodieke dɩ benne dansarɩka sʋŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ wɩa wa, aŋaŋ tɩ nɩmbʋa Timoti maagɩnana gbaŋkʋ gie a yɩa fʋnɩŋ tɩ tʋntʋŋ chanchaaŋ, vuodieke tɩ faasɩna cho wo.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: aŋaŋ Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ diekemba dɩ lagɩŋna a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ fʋ tigiri me wo, ta bɩ yɩa tɩ taa Afɩa aŋaŋ tɩ tʋntʋŋ chanchaaŋ Akapusi.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ ta bɩ yɩ nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: N nɩmbʋa Filimoni, saŋŋa dieke mana maŋ keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ, ta tɩɩnsɩ fʋ wɩa n ŋaaŋ waasɩ wa mɩŋ.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: Dama n wʋŋ fʋ yalla yada tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa ma dene ta bɩ cho Ŋmɩŋ vuosi mana dene.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: N jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ mɩŋ tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a yi Yisa Masia yada naa, die vaa tɩ mɩŋŋɩ sɩba berivɩɩnɩŋ dieke dɩ yine tɩ sɩɩtɩ ʋ ma.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: N nɩmbʋa, fʋ chotiti gie yɩ mɩŋ wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta bɩ kpaŋŋɩsɩ mɩŋ, dama kpaŋŋɩsa Ŋmɩŋ vuosi mana mɩŋ.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Masia wɩa n tɩŋ kaaŋ chɩgɩ ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ n balɩ fʋ aŋ fʋ yi wudieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: Ama choti wɩa, n baa jʋʋsɩ fʋ mɩŋ, manɩŋ Pɔɔli vuodieke dɩ yine Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ ta lele a bie dansarɩka sʋŋ ʋ wɩa wa.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: Die wɩa, n zie wo Onisimusi naŋ ma, vuodieke dɩ yine n gbaŋ gbaŋ n bʋa Masia ma wa a jʋʋsa fʋ, dama n benne dansarɩka sʋŋ giena wa n balɩ wa mɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha ʋ yi yada.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: Saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna wa die ʋ wo nyʋarɩ a yɩ fʋ, ama lele nɩŋ, ʋ yaa nyʋarɩ a yɩ manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ mana mɩŋ.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: Lele n yiŋŋi tʋŋ wa mɩŋ fʋ jigiŋ, ta n sʋŋ mana bie ʋ ma.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: N tɩŋ nan dɩ yaala ʋ zie fʋ naŋ ma n jigiŋ ta suŋŋime mɩŋ n benne dansarɩka ma Masia wʋvɩɩnaha wɩa wa.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: Ama n ka yaala n mugisi fʋ aŋ fʋ suŋŋi mɩŋ, ama n yaala fʋ yi mɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ dʋŋŋʋ. Die wɩa, n kaaŋ yi wɩɩŋ ntaala saagɩya.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: Vuoŋ zɩ, Onisimusi die gaya mɩŋ ta va fʋ saŋŋa bɩta amʋ nan dɩ yallɩ wa yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: Ta lele nɩŋ ʋ ka die yiwo yɔmʋ, dama ʋ tɩaŋ wa yɔmʋ, ʋ yiwo tɩ nɩmbʋa choti, Masia ma. N faasɩna a cho ʋ wɩa dene wo, mʋ fʋ cho wo a tɩaŋ dene, dama ʋ yiwo fʋ yɔmʋ aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmbʋa tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ ma.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi fʋ nagɩ mɩŋ sɩba fʋ tʋntʋŋ chanchaaŋ, die nɩŋ mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo sɩba fʋ baaŋ nan tuo mɩŋ dene wo.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ yi fʋ wɩɩŋ yaa ʋ dii fʋ hamɩŋ, fʋ vaa n tuŋ.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: N maaga wɩɩrɩ gie aŋaŋ n gbaŋ gbaŋ n nuuŋ mɩŋ. Manɩŋ Pɔɔli n nan tuŋ fʋ; ama daa manɩŋ n bala n balɩ fʋ, dama fʋ gbaŋ sɩba a baarɩ yaa wa n hamɩŋ fʋ miivoli wɩa.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Die wɩa n nɩmbʋa, ko suŋŋi mɩŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩa ta kpaŋŋɩsɩ mɩŋ tɩ yine dɩdɩɩsɩrɩtaŋ Masia ma wɩa.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: N maagɩna wudieke naa, n yaa tama dɩ nan yi tɩaŋ n bala fʋ dɩ fʋ yi wudieke wo.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Bɩ yaalɩ juoŋ a gbara mɩŋ dama nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ wɩa n yaa tama dɩ n nan keŋ kaagɩ nɩ.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Epafirasi, vuodieke dɩ gʋtɩna a bie dansarɩka sʋŋ aŋaŋ mɩŋ Yisa Masia wɩa wa waasa nɩ.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: Ta n tʋntʋŋ chanchaalɩŋ, Maki aŋaŋ Arisitakusi, aŋaŋ Demasi, aŋaŋ Luki, gbaŋ waasa nɩ.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia suŋŋi nɩ mana.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Kʋrɩŋ ma wa Ŋmɩŋ die daaga ʋ naazʋalɩba ma a bala wɩa a yɩa tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba saŋŋa pam aŋaŋ sieti pam ma.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: ama lele nɩŋ ʋ vaa wa ʋ Bʋadembike dɩ balɩ a yɩ tɩ. Ʋ ma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ daagɩ a naaŋ dʋnɩa ta bɩ vʋarɩ wa dɩ ʋ sɩmma jaaŋ mana.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩasɩŋ, ta chaana Ŋmɩŋ chaanɩŋ, ta pɔgɩlɩ dʋnɩaka gie lʋga mana aŋaŋ ʋ nʋawɩɩŋ dieke dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ wa. Die ʋ yine wudieke dɩ baaŋ vaa vuosi ye ba bɩaŋ naachaala a kpatɩ wa, ʋ wa kalɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine hagɩrɩtieŋ wo nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ arɩzanna ma.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine ʋ Bʋadembike saaŋ dɩ tɩaŋ malakasi saaraha, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Bʋadembike dɩ tɩaŋ ba.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ ye ka balɩ yɩ ʋ malakasi wʋnyɩ dɩ: Fʋnɩŋ yiwo n Bʋadembiŋ, ta jinne maŋ yi fʋ Chɔɔŋ. Yaa Ŋmɩŋ ye balɩ yɩ ʋ malakasi wʋnyɩ dɩ: N nan keŋ yi ʋ Chɔɔŋ ta ʋ yi n Bʋadembiŋ.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bala ʋ tʋŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ dieke dɩ yine nyɩŋnaaŋka mana sikpeŋtieŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo die ʋ bɩ balɩ dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ malakasi mana mʋ ba jɩama wa mɩŋ.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Ama naa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ yaa gamma malakasi ma, dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ yiwo ʋ malakasi dɩ yi haalɩtɩ ta bɩ yi ba yi bolinyaaŋ.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Ama ta balɩ yɩ ʋ Bʋadembike dɩ: Yiwo Ŋmɩŋ, ta fʋ naarɩ nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu. Ta daansa fʋ vuosi aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: Fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ cho wo vɩɩnɩŋ ama ta haa bɩaŋ die wɩa manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine fʋ Ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ vʋarɩ fʋ ta yɩ fʋ jɩlɩma ta fʋ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke dɩ faasɩna tɩaŋ fʋ zʋalɩŋ.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Ta die bɩ balɩ naa dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ, piiliku me wo naaŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie, ta naaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ fʋ nuusi.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: A mana nan keŋ kpi, ama fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nan wɩarɩ a beri; a mana nan keŋ kʋrɩŋ a chɩɩrɩ sɩba gatɩ.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: Nan keŋ kpeŋhe sɩba jayeekikpɩɩŋ, ta nan tarɩgɩha sɩba fʋ tarɩgɩnana gatɩ. Ama fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ wo tarɩgɩŋ, ta nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Ŋmɩŋ ye ka balɩ a yɩ ʋ malakasi wʋnyɩ dɩ Kala n nuudiigiri gie chaaŋ, a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n balla n nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuusi me.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Malakasi mana yiwo haalɩtɩ ta jɩama Ŋmɩŋ ta ʋ tʋma ba dɩ ba keŋ a suŋŋi vuodiekemba dɩ balla ba ye gbatɩtaaŋkʋ.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Tɩ mʋ tɩ yigi wo wusie diekemba tɩ wʋnna a yalla keŋ keŋ amʋ tɩ kaaŋ taarɩ ta va ha.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Wudiekemba malakasi dɩ mʋʋlɩna a yɩ tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die yiwo wusie ta vuodieke die dɩ kana ka dɩ ha die ye wo tɩbɩdatɩ dieke dɩ mʋna.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: Die wɩa, dɩɩ yi tɩ ka saagɩ a wʋmma gbatɩtaaŋ kpeŋkpɩɩkʋ wɩaha vɩɩnɩŋ, tɩ baa tɩ yiwo lalɩa a nyɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ ma? Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die wolinne a mʋʋlɩ yaa gamma gbatɩtaaŋkʋ gie mi, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ wʋnna wa dɩ dagɩ tɩ dɩ ka seŋ yiwo wusie.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋŋ mamachi wʋkpɩɩma aŋaŋ dagɩtɩ pam ta die bɩ yɩ vuosisi ʋ Halɩkasɩka piinisi ʋ yaalala dene, die dɩ dagɩ dɩ ʋ wɩaha yiwo wusie.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die ka vʋarɩ malakasisi dɩ ba daansɩma dʋnɩa dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ ta tɩ bala naa wa.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Dɩ maagɩya mɩŋ jigiŋ kaanɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ, vuota yiwo bɩa fʋ yile ʋ wɩa? Ta bɩ yi bɩa fʋ baga dɩ bie ʋ ma?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: Naaŋ wa mɩŋ ta ʋ ka tʋgɩ malakasi ta bɩ yi ʋ ye bɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ pɩmpagɩŋ.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: ta vaa ʋ sɩɩ jaaŋ mana. Dɩ balɩya mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vaa wa vuota dɩ yi Jaaŋ mana sikpeŋtieŋ. Dɩ dagɩya dɩ ʋ sɩɩ jaaŋ mana mɩŋ, ama lele vuota ye ka sɩɩ jaaŋ mana.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Ama tɩ sɩba yaa gamma Yisa wɩa. Wʋnɩŋ saŋŋa bɩta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ va ʋ ka tʋgɩ malakasi, dɩ ʋ tuo vuota mana kuŋ a kpi, wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla wɩa. Ʋ kumbu wahala diile wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vaa ʋ ye bɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ pɩmpagɩŋ.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ naanna jaaŋ mana ta bɩ pɔgɩlɩ jaaŋ mana wʋnɩŋ ʋ vana ʋ ballɩ pam dɩ ʋ juo ʋ naarɩ ma, naa die tʋgɩsɩna a yɩ wa dɩ ʋ vaa ba gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Yisa seŋ yi ba Gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ aŋaŋ wusie, ʋ wahala diilehe wɩa.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yinene vuota dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ vuosi, ta wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba mana dɩ lagɩŋ a yaa Chɔɔŋ balɩmɩŋ. Die wɩa Yisa dɩ ka chɩga viivi dɩ ʋ wasɩma ba ʋ nɩmballɩ.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: Die ʋ balɩ yɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ: N nan balɩ n vuosi wudieke fʋ yine; a bɩrɩ fʋ saaŋ ba lagɩmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Die ʋ bɩ balɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ nɩnɩŋ n nan yi Ŋmɩŋ yada, ta bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ aŋaŋ ballɩ diekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna mɩŋ wa lagɩŋna beri naa.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ dɩ yine vuota ta yaa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋaŋ zɩŋ wɩa, Yisa dɩaŋ die dɩ keŋ yi vuota sɩba banɩŋ, amʋ ʋ kumbu me ʋ keŋ chʋʋsɩ Sitaani vuodieke dɩ yalla kuŋ hagɩrɩŋ wa.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: ta vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana kuŋ ba ŋmaamɩŋ ma, dama die ba yiwo ŋmaamɩŋ yɔŋɩsɩ ba miivoli mana ma.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: Wusie, daa malakasisi ʋ suŋŋe, ama Abarahami haagɩsɩsɩ ʋ suŋŋe.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Naa wɩa dɩ mʋ ʋ sɩmma sɩba ʋ nɩmballɩlɩ jaaŋ mana ma amʋ ʋ nan bɩagɩ yi kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ dieke dɩ yine wusietieŋ ta bɩ chɩga zɔɔlɩŋ, ta nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaaba vuota wɩa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ba wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Ta lele, ʋ nan bɩagɩ suŋŋi vuodiekemba Sitaani dɩ magɩsɩna dama Sitaani die magɩsɩ ʋ gbaŋ mɩŋ ta ʋ dii wahala.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: N nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ vuosi ta ʋ wa nɩ naa, nɩ yilime yaa gamma Yisa ma, vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋnna dɩ ʋ keŋ yi kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ tɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ sieŋ me wo.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: Die ʋ yiwo wusietieŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ vʋarɩna wa dɩ ʋ keŋ tʋŋ tʋʋmaha gie wo sɩba Mosisi die dɩ yine wusietieŋ ʋ tʋʋma ma Ŋmɩŋ vuosi sʋkʋ.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Mɩmɩɩrʋ dɩ yese bɩrɩŋ pam a tɩaŋ tigiŋ dieke ʋ mɩna wa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Yisa dɩ mʋ ʋ ye bɩrɩŋ pam atɩaŋ Mosisi.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Tigiŋ nɩŋ mana vuoŋ mɩnana ka, ama Ŋmɩŋ mɩna jaaŋ mana.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Mosisi die yiwo wusie tʋntʋntʋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi mana jigiŋ, a bala wudiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ daansɩ a balɩ.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Ama Masia nɩŋ ʋ yiwo wusietieŋ ta sɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ tigiri mi ta yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ. Dɩɩ yi tɩ yaa kenyiti aŋaŋ kpaadensiŋ yaa gamma tɩ tamaka ma, die nɩŋ tɩnɩŋ tɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ tigiri vuosi mi.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Die wɩa, nɩ saagɩ a yi wudieke Halɩkasɩka dɩ bala wa, dɩ: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩŋ jinne.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: nɩ da vaa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ hagɩrɩma sɩba nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ zetine Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ, sɩba die ba sɩna die wo hagɩyeŋ me wo ta magɩsɩ wa wa.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die balɩya mɩŋ dɩ: Die ba wɔŋ ye wudieke n yinene a ga tʋgɩ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa wa ama mi die ba magɩsɩ mɩŋ.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Die wɩa maŋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi mi ta baarɩ dɩ, Ba sʋgɩtɩ ma, ba zeti mɩŋ mɩŋ, ta ka dɩɩ n sieti.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Die maŋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta yɩ nʋarɩ gie dɩ, ba kaaŋ wɔŋ juu tɩŋgbaŋ dieke n tɩnna baa n yɩ ba voosiku wo me. .

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: N zʋalɩŋ, nɩ sɩmma ta da vaa sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ aŋaŋ chɩɩlɩŋ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ yiŋŋi kʋaŋ ta va miivolitieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Ama nɩ suŋŋime taŋ daaŋ mana tɩ yene bie tɩ miivoli me naa, ta nɩ daa vaa bɩaŋ gaaŋ nɩ aŋ nɩ yi tɩbɩhagɩrɩtieliŋ.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Dama dɩɩ yi tɩ yigi yada dieke tɩ yalla a nyɩŋ piiliku me wo a yallɩ keŋ keŋ a ga tʋgɩ kpatɩŋ, tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Masia nan yi tʋntʋntɩ chanchaalɩŋ.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Naa die ba maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩŋ jinne, nɩ daa ligi nɩ tɩba, sɩba nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ die dɩ ligine ba tɩba dene wo ta die wagɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ ta zeti wo wo.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Mɩnɩa mba die wʋnna Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩkʋ ta zeti ke wo? Dɩ yiwo vuodiekemba mana die Mosisi dɩ dɩna ba nɩŋŋa a yaa ba a nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Mɩnɩa mba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ bɩna baŋɩsɩ-nɩɩsa wa? Dɩ yiwo vuodiekemba die dɩ yine bɩabʋ a kpi hagɩyeŋ me wo.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Mɩnɩa mba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ die balɩ a yɩ dɩ, Ba kaaŋ wɔŋ a juu tɩŋgbaŋ dieke n tɩnna baa n yɩ ba voosiku wo me? Die dɩ yiwo vuodiekemba die dɩ zetine ʋ nʋarɩ.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Die wɩa tɩ wa sɩba dɩ die ba ka bɩagɩ juu kanɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka me, dama die ba wone yada wɩa.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Lele nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ wa yɩ tɩ nʋarɩ mi dɩ tɩ keŋ a ye voosi dieke ʋ bala ka wɩa wa. Die wɩa nɩ mana sɩmma amʋ nɩ wʋnyɩ kaaŋ waarɩ ka.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Tɩ gbaŋ tɩ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha mɩŋ sɩba die ba wʋnna dene wo. Die ba wʋŋ wʋvɩɩnaha mɩŋ ama die ka wo nyʋarɩ a yɩ ba, dama die ba wʋnna wa ba ka tuo ke aŋaŋ yada.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Die wɩa, tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada naa, nan keŋ juu voosi dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ wa. Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bala die wo, dɩ Manɩŋ die n jɩɩ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta yɩ nʋarɩ gie: Ba kaaŋ wɔŋ juu tɩŋgbaŋ dieke n tɩnna baa n yɩ ba voosiku. Die ʋ keŋ bala naa, ta ʋ wɔŋ kpatɩ ʋ naamɩŋ tʋʋma piiliku me mɩŋ.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ jigiŋ kaanɩ ʋ wɔŋ balɩ mɩŋ yaa gamma daraa ayʋpɔyɩ wa gie daarɩ, dɩ: Ŋmɩŋ die vaa wa ʋ tʋʋma mana ta voosi daraa ayʋpɔyɩ wa daraaŋ.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Ta die bɩ balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ: Ba kaaŋ wɔŋ a juu tɩŋgbaŋ dieke n tɩnna yaala n yɩ ba voosiku.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ voosiŋ jigiŋ beri dɩ vuosi juu. Vuodiekemba die dɩ wolinne wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha die ka ye kanɩŋ voosiku mi, dama die ba ka yi Ŋmɩŋ yada.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ bɩ zieŋ wo daaŋ kaanɩ, ta wasa daarɩ mi Jinne. Ta bɩna pam kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bɩ daagɩ Davidi ma a balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie sɩba dɩ wone a maagɩ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dene wo dɩ: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩŋ jinne, nɩ daa keŋ vaa nɩ sʋgɩtɩ hagɩrɩma.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Dɩ tɩŋ yi Josua dɩ bɩagɩ a yɩ ba voosiŋ, Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ balɩ yaa gamma daaŋ kaanɩ.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Naa dagɩya dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi voosiku ye kieŋ mɩŋ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ voosine daraa ayʋpɔyɩ daraaŋ dene wo.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Dɩnda daarɩ mi, Ŋmɩŋ vuosi nan vaa ba tʋʋma ta voosi sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vana ʋ tʋʋma ta voosi dene wo.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ kpaŋŋɩ tɩ gbaŋ ta ye voosiku mi, amʋ tɩ wʋnyɩ kaaŋ waarɩ ka sɩba die ba zetine Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ ta waarɩ ka dene wo.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha yiwo wudieke dɩ yalla miivoli, ta yaa hagɩrɩŋ, ta bɩ die a tɩaŋ takoobi dieke dɩ yalla nʋa ale. Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha bɩagɩ gobe a tʋga vuota haalɩŋ aŋaŋ miivoli jigiŋ, ta tʋga ʋ gbime aŋaŋ ʋ kɔba kpaaŋ, ta tʋga ta puo ʋ sʋŋanyile aŋaŋ wudieke ʋ yilinene ʋ sʋŋ ma.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Ta nyɩŋnaaŋka mana dɩ bie yaalɩŋ ma, ta Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ bala ʋ keŋ dii tɩ sarɩya wa nine dɩ yese he; ta jaaŋ wori a lɔbɩrɩ.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Tɩ yaa wa kaabɩŋ jakʋʋŋ, vuodieke dɩ wolinne ga Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ jigiŋ arɩzanna ma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Yisa, Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike. Die wɩa, tɩ mʋ tɩ yigi keŋ keŋ yada dieke tɩ bala yaalɩŋ ma wa.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Tɩ kaabɩŋ jakʋʋrɩ ka yi vuodieke dɩ kana ka chɩga tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ tɩ wabɩŋ ma, ama wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ die ʋ ye wo magɩsɩŋ yiri yiri sɩba tɩ gbaŋ, ama wʋnɩŋ die ʋ ka yi bɩaŋ.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ yaa kpaadensiŋ ta ga Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine burichini wo jigiŋ, ta ye zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla aŋaŋ suŋŋiŋ aŋ ka suŋŋi tɩ saŋŋa dieke tɩ keŋ yaala.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Kaabɩŋ jakʋʋŋ mana Ŋmɩŋ ŋaana vʋara wa a nyɩna ʋ chanchaalɩŋ sʋŋ dɩ ʋ zie ba naŋ ma a kaabɩma a yɩma wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ ba bɩaŋ wɩa.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wabɩna sieti pam ma wɩa, ʋ bɩagɩ pɔgɩla vuodiekemba dɩ waarɩna yɩaŋ ta taarɩ nyɩŋ sieku me sɔmm.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Ta ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wabɩna wɩa, ʋ ka yie kaabaha vuosi nyɩɩna ma wʋbɩatɩ wɩa, ama ʋ yie mɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wʋbɩatɩ wɩa.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Vuoŋ ka vʋara ʋ gbaŋ a yie kaabɩŋ jakʋʋŋ, sie Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa sɩba die ʋ vʋarɩna Arɔni dene wo.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ, Masia die dɩ ka kɔtɩ ʋ gbaŋ ta vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ ʋ yi kaabɩŋ jakʋʋŋ ama Ŋmɩŋ die bala a yɩ wa dɩ: Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine n Bʋadembiŋ; ta jinne maŋ bɩrɩŋ fʋ chɔɔŋ.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Ta ʋ balɩ jigiŋ kaanɩ dɩaŋ Fʋnɩŋ nan keŋ yi kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma haahuu, sɩba Melikizedeki.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Die Yisa dɩ yene bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo, die ʋ ŋaaŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ta bala ʋ wumugisike aŋaŋ nɩntaantɩ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ gbatɩ wa a nyɩŋ kumbu me wo. Die ʋ yine vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ wʋŋ ʋ jʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Die ʋ seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ wusie, ama die ʋ dii wo wahala ʋ tuone Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ wɩa.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: ta die keŋ dii wahalaka kpatɩ, ta yi vuodiekemba mana dɩ tuone ʋ nʋaŋ dɩ ye gbatɩtaaŋ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ die vʋarɩ wa mɩŋ dɩ ʋ yi kaabɩŋ jakʋʋŋ sɩba Melikizedeki.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Tɩ tɩŋ nan balɩ nɩ wɩa pam yaa gamma Masia aŋaŋ Melikizedeki ma, ama dɩ hagɩrɩ mɩŋ dɩ tɩ dagɩ nɩ a chɩasɩ, dama nɩ ka sɩba wɩa chɩasɩ lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Dɩ tɩ mʋ nɩ yiwo dɩdagɩrɩŋ saŋka gie, ama nɩ ye yaala dɩdagɩrɩŋ aŋ ba dagɩ nɩ yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha wupiilikehe. Nɩ sɩɩ sɩba ballɩnyaalɩsɩ ta mʋ nɩ nyume biisiŋ daa nyindiiki dʋnsa.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Vuodieke dɩ nyunene biisiŋ ye yiwo bʋanyaalɩŋ, ta ka sɩba wɩɩŋ mana yaa gamma vɩɩnɩŋ yaa bɩaŋ ma.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Nyindiiki dʋnsa nɩŋ yiwo nyɩŋkʋra sɩɩtɩ, banɩŋ ba dagɩ ba mɩŋ ba sɩba wudieke dɩ yine vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ yine bɩaŋ.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Melikizedeki wa gie die yiwo Salemi tɩŋ naaŋ, ta bɩ yi Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ. Die Abarahami die dɩ nyɩnna tɔŋ dieke die ʋ bɩagɩna a nyaŋŋɩ naalɩba banɩɩsa a yiŋŋi a kieŋ wo, die Melikizedeki die dɩ tuoli wo sieŋ me a yi alibarika a yi wo.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Abarahami die dɩ yɩ wa nyindiekemba mana ʋ nagɩna a nyɩŋ mi wo baŋ mana sʋŋ kaanɩ. Ʋ saarɩ Melikizedeki wa chɩaŋ yine Sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ Naaŋ ta ʋ die bɩ yi Salemi tɩŋ naaŋ, ta Salemi chɩaŋ dɩ yi Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ die wɩa ʋ bɩ yi Sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ Naaŋ.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Wɩɩŋ die wori a daga yaa gamma Melikizedeki chɔɔŋ wɩa yaa ʋ nuŋ wɩa yaa ʋ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba wʋnyɩ wɩa yaa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ maarɩŋ yaa ʋ kuŋ wɩa. Ʋ sɩɩ sɩba Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike, ta yi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Ye Melikizedeki die dɩ yine vuokpɩɩŋ dene, ta tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋkpɩɩŋ Abarahami die yɩ wa jadieke mana die ʋ wagɩna gbatɩ a nyɩŋ tɔŋ ma baŋ sʋŋ mana kaanɩ.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Mɩraha die dagɩya dɩ Izara vuosisi gbaŋ mʋ ba vʋarɩ wa ba nyinti baŋ sʋŋ mana kaanɩ a yɩ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba buuriŋ die dɩ yine Liiva haagɩsɩsɩ ta yi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba aŋaŋ ba mana die dɩ yine Abarahami haagɩsɩ mana yɔrɩ.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Melikizedeki die ka yi Liiva haagɩŋ ama Abarahami die yɩ wa wa ʋ nyinti baŋ sʋŋ mana kaanɩ. Die Melikizedeki die dɩ yi alibarika a yi Abarahami ma, wʋnɩŋ vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋarɩ.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Vuoŋ mana sɩba mɩŋ dɩ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana alibarika yiwo vuokpɩɩŋ a tɩaŋ vuodieke dɩ tuosinene alibarika ka.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Kɩkaabɩtɩba diekemba die dɩ tuosinene nyinti baŋ mana sʋŋ kaanɩ a nyɩna vuosi jigiŋ die yiwo vuota ta die dɩ keŋ kpie, ama Melikizedeki vuodieke die dɩ tuosinene nyinti baŋ mana sʋŋ kaanɩ a nyɩna Abarahami jigiŋ ye bie wo ʋ miivoli me sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ dagɩna dene wo.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: A bɩ gʋtɩ tɩ nan bɩagɩ a baarɩ dɩ Liiva haagɩsɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ tuosinene nyinti baŋ mana sʋŋ kaanɩ wa, gbaŋ die yɩ wa Melikizedeki ba nyinti baŋ mana kaanɩ saŋŋa dieke Abarahami die dɩ yɩna wa.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Dama saŋŋa dieke Melikizedeki die dɩ haarɩna Abarahami wa, die ba ye ka mɩɩrɩ Liiva ama wʋnɩŋ Liiva die bie wo ʋ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Abarahami wa ma.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die yɩ Izara vuosi mɩra ta die dagɩ Liiva maarɩŋ ba kaabɩŋ tʋʋma dɩ sɩna die. Dɩɩ yi ta kɩkaabɩtɩba diekemba die dɩ nyɩna Liiva maarɩŋ ma tʋʋma dɩ bɩagɩ a yie vuosi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ, dɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ yi talasɩ dɩ kɩkaabɩtʋ gaaŋ vuodieke dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ Arɔni maarɩŋ ma ama ta yi kɩkaabɩtʋ sɩba Melikizedeki keŋ.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Dama ba keŋ tarɩgɩ kɩkaabɩtɩba vʋarɩŋ, dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ ba tarɩgɩ mɩraha gbaŋ.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ vuodieke wɩa tɩ balala naa wa, nyɩŋ wa buurigaaŋ ma. Ta vuoŋ dɩ ye ka nyɩŋ ʋ buuriŋ ma a yi kɩkaabɩtʋ.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: Vuoŋ mana wɔŋ sɩba a baarɩ wʋnɩŋ Yisa die nyɩŋ wa Juda buuriŋ ma, ta Mosisi die dɩ ka balɩ dɩ kɩkaabɩtɩba nan nyɩŋ ba buuriŋ ma.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Saŋŋa dieke kɩkaabɩtʋ gaaŋ sɩba Melikizedeki die dɩ kenne keŋ wo die dɩ yi aŋ tɩ faasɩ sɩba wɩaha chɩaŋ.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Wʋnɩŋ die ʋ ka yi kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma vuota mɩraha ma, ama die ʋ bɩrɩŋ wa kɩkaabɩtʋ miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Nan yi kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma haahuu, sɩba Melikizedeki die dɩ sɩna die wo.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Die wɩa mɩrɩkʋʋrɩ ka bɩ tʋma dama ka wo hagɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ nyʋarɩ.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Dama Mosisi mɩraha die ka bɩagɩ yi jaaŋ dɩ beri vɩɩnɩŋ. Ama lele tɩ yaa tama dieke dɩ kpana ka, ka ma tɩ chʋŋ gbigi Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ yɩ nʋaŋ a gʋtɩ ka ma. Ŋmɩŋ die ka yɩa nʋaŋ die ba kalɩnnana kɩkaabɩtɩba mi wo.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Ama Yisa die keŋ yiwo kɩkaabɩtʋ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna wa nʋaŋ ta balɩ wa dɩ: Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ ta kaaŋ bɩ tarɩgɩ n sʋŋanyile, ta baarɩ Fʋnɩŋ fʋ bala fʋ yi kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma haahuu. .

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Die wɩa, Yisa yiwo vuodieke dɩ dagɩna dɩ nʋa dieke dɩ kpana beri mɩŋ ta wo tarɩgɩŋ.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Kʋrɩŋ ma wa kɩkaabɩtɩba mi wo die kpiye mɩŋ ta ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ tuose ba tʋʋma, die wɩa die ba dala.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Ama Yisa nɩŋ ʋ nan dɩ beri ta yi kɩkaabɩtʋ yaa gamma haahuu ta ʋ tʋʋmaha kaaŋ nyɩŋ ʋ nuusi me aŋ vuogaaŋ tuoke.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Die wɩa, vuodiekemba dɩ chʋnna gbigi Ŋmɩŋ, wʋnɩŋ Yisa saaŋ ma naa, ʋ nan bɩagɩ a gbatɩ ba a taaŋ leleke gie aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke dɩ kienene wo, dama wʋnɩŋ ʋ bie wo ʋ miivoli me saŋŋa mana mɩŋ, ta jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ a yɩa ba.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Die wɩa, Yisa yine tɩ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ dieke dɩ suŋŋinene tɩ tɩ yaalala dene. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ ta wo bɩaŋ: ʋ bie ʋ tʋaŋ ta ka sɩɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ yalla bɩaŋ wa, ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kɔtɩ wa a tɩaŋ arɩzanna ma tieliŋ mana.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra banɩŋ ba die woliŋ kaaba daaŋ nɩŋ mana ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba wʋbɩatɩ wɩa, ta bɩ kaaba yɩa vuosisi wʋbɩatɩ wɩa, wʋnɩŋ Yisa nɩŋ ka sɩɩ die, die ʋ nagɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaabɩ balɩmɩŋ, kaabɩŋ bɩ wori.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Mosisi mɩraha ŋaaŋ vʋara vuotaŋ a yie be kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra ama ta ba ka mɩŋŋɩ beri vɩɩnɩŋ a kpatɩ. Mɩraha kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ ta nagɩ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ a yi wo kɩkaabɩtʋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan dɩ beri vɩɩnɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Bʋmbʋŋaŋ nʋarɩ die yaa wa mɩra yaa gamma ba baaŋ nan jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dene aŋaŋ jigidieke ba baaŋ dɩ jɩama wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Die ba kpii wo dɔŋɩsɩ gbanɩŋ vɩɩŋ ta yaa juone ale. Die ba wasa juodieke die dɩ benne nɩŋŋa wa, Kasɩ Jigiri. Mi die ba zieŋ jadieke ba nagɩna popolisi a sʋga ka ma ta teebuli dɩ zie ka ma ta paanʋ dieke die ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ diisi teebulike me.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Die garɩ dieke dɩ puone juone ale wo kʋaŋ die ba wasa kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ jigiŋ.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: die ka sʋŋ die kori dieke ba nagɩna salɩma a wʋnsɩ wa ta ba jʋa nyɩnnyʋʋgɩvɩɩna ka ma a yɩa Ŋmɩnnɩ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nʋaha a daka dieke ba nagɩna salɩma a marɩ ka mana wa die dɩ beri. Ka ma salɩma gbieŋ die dɩ beri ta nyindiike dieke ba wasɩnana maana wa die dɩ bie ka ma aŋaŋ Arɔni daaŋgbanɩŋ dieke dɩ tɔsɩna vaatɩtɩ aŋaŋ taŋ teelike ale dieke Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ maagɩna a diisi a ma wa dɩ beri.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Die adaka ka sikpeŋ die nyinti ataŋ die yalla chobiti ta daga dɩ Ŋmɩŋ bie me, ta a chobititi die dɩ jatɩ a ligi jigidieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩnana wʋbɩatɩ a chaa wa. Ama lele n kaaŋ bɩagɩ a dagɩ wɩaha chɩasɩ mana.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Naa die dɩ sɩɩ, die kɩkaabɩtɩba die ŋaaŋ juo juodieke dɩ bene nɩŋka ma daaŋ mana a tʋma ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ tʋʋma.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: ama kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ nyɩɩna ma ŋaana juo juodieke dɩ bene sʋkʋ ma wa, bɩŋ mana bʋnyɩ ʋ juo mi. Ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa zɩŋ a juu yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ wʋbɩatɩ wɩa aŋaŋ vuosisi dɩ yine wudiekemba ta ka sɩba baarɩ a yiwo wʋbɩatɩtɩ.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die mɩŋŋɩ daga yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vɩɩkʋ gie, dɩ jigidieke dɩ yine kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ jigiri die ye ka yuori saŋŋa dieke juodieke dɩ bene nɩŋka dɩ yene zie wo.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Naa yiwo dagɩtɩ a yɩ tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ bene leleke gie wo, ta daga dɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ jɩanna Ŋmɩnnɩ ta die ŋaaŋ yɩa Ŋmɩŋ ba piinisi aŋaŋ ba dɔŋɩsɩ zɩmbʋ die ka bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ ba wʋbɩatɩ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: dama hanɩŋ nɩŋ die a yiwo nyindiike aŋaŋ nyiŋnyuuki diekemba die dɩ chʋarɩna aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ kana ka chʋarɩ, aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ wʋnsɩŋ wɩa. Genhe mana die yiwo nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma mɩraha dɩ ba dɩ ha yaa gamma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ yɩ ba mɩrɩhaalɩŋ.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Ama Masia vuodieke dɩ yine nyɩŋvɩɩŋ dieke dɩ benne leleke gie kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ. Vɩɩŋ dieke ʋ kenne juu ta tʋma ka ma naa yiwo vɩɩŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ benne vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie; daa jadieke vuota dɩ kpine, ta a ka bɩ yi tɩŋgbaŋka gie jaaŋ.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Masia die dɩ kenne a juu bʋnyɩ jɩamɩŋ vɩɩkʋ ma a ga tʋgɩ kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ jigiri saŋŋa dieke wo, die ʋ ka nagɩ bie aŋaŋ naarasɩ zɩŋ a yi kaaba, ama die ʋ nagɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ zɩŋ a tuŋ ta vʋarɩ tɩ a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Die ba ŋaaŋ naga bie aŋaŋ naarasɩ zɩŋ, ta die bɩ nagɩ naasaaŋ a yi kaaba ta jʋʋ ka nyɩŋgbanɩŋ boliŋ ta nagɩ ka tanyeeliŋ aŋaŋ zɩmbʋ a mɩsɩ mɩsɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla bɩabʋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa, dɩ ka wʋnsɩ ba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ aŋ ba yɩantɩ.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Ama Masia die wo bɩaŋ ta Halɩkasɩka dieke dɩ benne saŋŋa mana hagɩrɩŋ ma wʋnɩŋ Masia die dɩ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaabɩ dieke dɩ bene vɩɩnɩŋ vɩɩnɩŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Die wɩa ʋ zɩmbʋ dɩ faasɩna yaa hagɩrɩŋ ta vʋarɩ bɩaŋ tɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma. Ta lele tɩ nan bɩagɩ jɩaŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ ta kaaŋ bɩ yie wudiekemba dɩ baaŋ yaa tɩ a ga kuŋ me.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Wɩɩrɩ gie wɩa, Masia yine vuodieke dɩ vana nʋahaalɩkʋ dɩ beri amʋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wana wa nan ye alibarika dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ wa. Naa nan bɩagɩ yi dama Masia die kpiye mɩŋ, die dɩ vaa vuota dɩ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ a nyɩŋ ba wʋbɩatɩ ma saŋŋa dieke nʋakʋʋrɩ dɩ yene beri wo.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ maagɩ ʋ faarɩ diile gbanɩŋ, vuoŋ ka yese jaaŋ ntaala ba keŋ bɩagɩ a dagɩ dɩ vuodieke dɩ maagɩna gbaŋkʋ kpiye mɩŋ.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Dama saŋŋa dieke vuodieke dɩ maagɩna faarɩ gbaŋkʋ dɩ yene bie ʋ miivoli me wo, gbaŋkʋ mi wo yiko.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Die wɩa bʋmbʋŋaŋ nʋarɩ die dɩ wo yiko dɩɩ yi ba ka yi zɩmbʋ kaaba.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Mosisi die woliŋ mʋʋlɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha mana sɩba dɩ maagɩna dene wo. Ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ nagɩ vaatɩ a bɔbɩ aŋaŋ yiise kɔbɩŋmɩnɩŋ a nagɩ suge naarasɩ aŋaŋ bie zɩŋ dɩ lagɩŋnana nyaaŋ a nagɩ mɩsa mɩraha gbaŋkʋ ma aŋaŋ vuosisi mana ma.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: ta die bala dɩ, Zɩmbʋ gie yine nʋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nɩ dɩ nɩ dɩa dagɩtɩ.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Mosisi die dɩ bɩ nagɩ zɩmbʋ a mɩsɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ jadiekemba mana ba nagɩna a jɩama Ŋmɩnnɩ ma.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Wusie, Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ma zɩŋ die vana jaaŋ mana dɩ bie vɩɩnɩŋ ta wo dɩgɩntɩ, ta dɩɩ yi zɩŋ dɩ ka nyɩŋya, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ nagɩ vuosi wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Die wɩa, die dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ ba yi kaaba a wʋnsɩ nyindiekemba dɩ yine arɩzanna ma nyinti nɩasɩŋ. Ama kaabɩ dieke dɩ tɩanna naa balla ka wʋnsɩ arɩzanna ma nyinti gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Dama Masia die ka ga juu juokasi dieke vuota dɩ nagɩna ba nuusi a mɩɩ ta ka nɩasɩ jigikasɩ ka wa, ama die ʋ ga juu wo arɩzanna gbaŋ gbaŋ ma a zie Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa tɩ suŋŋiŋ wɩa.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Juu vuosi kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ ŋaaŋ juo kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ jigiri bɩŋ mana bʋnyɩ aŋaŋ dɔŋɩsɩ zɩŋ mɩŋ. Ama Masia die ka ga juu dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yime kaaba saŋŋa mana.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: dama die ʋ tɩŋ yi die, die nɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ nan yi talasɩ dɩ ʋ dime wahala saŋŋa saŋŋa a nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naanna tɩŋgbaŋka gie wo. Ama leleke gie, saŋŋa dɩ gbigine kpatɩŋ naa, die ʋ keŋ nyɩŋ bʋnyɩ dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaaba ta kpisi bɩaŋ.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Vuota mana baa ʋ kpi wo bʋnyɩ ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dii wo ʋ sarɩya.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ Masia gbaŋ die dɩ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaaba bʋnyɩ dɩ ʋ vʋarɩ vuosi pam a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma, ʋ nan daansɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ ama daa ʋ bɩ vʋarɩ vuosi a nyɩŋ ba bɩaŋ ma ama ta gbatɩ vuodiekemba dɩ chɩɩsɩnana wa wa a taaŋ.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Wudieke tɩ bala naa chɩaŋ yiwo naa, tɩ yaa wa kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ ta ʋ kalɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ mana nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ naarɩ ma arɩzanna ma.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: ta ʋ tʋma kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋra tʋʋma Ŋmɩŋ kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ vɩɩkʋ ma; kanɩŋ ka yine wusie jɩamɩŋ vɩɩŋ ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ die dɩ wʋnsɩ ka da vuota.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ dieke nɩŋ mana ba vʋarɩna, die dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ ʋ nagɩ piinisi ta bɩ yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ, die gbaŋ die dɩ yi talasɩ dɩ tɩ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ gbaŋ yɩ jaaŋ.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Dɩɩ yi ʋ tɩŋ bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, ʋ tɩŋ kaaŋ yi kɩkaabɩtʋ naada, dama kɩkaabɩtɩba diekemba dɩ yɩnana Ŋmɩŋ piinisisi sɩba Juu vuosi mɩraha dɩ dagɩna die wo wɔŋ beri mɩŋ.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Wudieke ba tʋnnana naa wa yiwo arɩzanna ma vɩɩŋ nɩasɩŋ. Naa wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ kpaaŋ Mosisi saŋŋa dieke die ʋ balla ʋ wʋnsɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vɩɩkʋ, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ kpaaŋ wa ta baarɩ dɩ, Yime jaaŋ mana mana sɩba n dagɩna fʋ tannɩ sikpeŋ wo.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ yɩ wa Yisa kaabɩŋ tʋŋ dieke dɩ tɩanna ba kaabɩŋ tʋʋmaha sɩba ʋ nʋaŋ dɩ tɩanna ta bie vuotaŋ aŋaŋ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosisi sʋnsʋkʋ dene wo, dama ʋ nʋarɩ taasɩ wa aŋaŋ nyɩnvɩɩna atɩaŋ nʋakʋʋrɩ.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Dama dɩɩ yi ta bʋmbʋŋaŋ nʋarɩ die vana ta vuosi dɩ bie vɩɩnɩŋ, die nɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ yi talasɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ yɩ nʋarɩ a gʋtɩ ka ma.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Ama Ŋmɩŋ die ye vuotaŋ bɩabʋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ balla dɩ: Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ bala naa: saŋŋa kieŋ ta n nan keŋ yi nʋahaalɩŋ a yɩ Izara vuosi aŋaŋ Juda vuosi.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Kanɩŋ ka kaaŋ dɩ sɩɩ sɩba nʋa dieke die n ziene aŋaŋ ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ daa dieke die n yigine ba nuuŋ ta vʋarɩ ba a nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma wa. Die ba ka dɩ nʋa dieke die n yɩna ba wa die wɩa n baga die dɩ wo ba ma.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ n nan zieŋ nʋarɩ gie aŋaŋ Izara vuosi daa diekemba dɩ kienene wo: N nan wa marɩ n mɩraha ba sʋŋanyile me ta maagɩ ha ba sʋgɩtɩ ma. Manɩŋ n nan yi ba Ŋmɩŋ, aŋ ba dɩaŋ ba yi n vuosi.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Dɩ kaaŋ bɩ yi talasɩ dɩ vuoŋ dagɩ ʋ chanchaaŋ yaa a balɩ ʋ chanchaaŋ dɩ Fʋnɩŋ, sɩmma Ŋmɩŋ. Dama ba mana nan keŋ sɩba mɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine vuokpɩɩma aŋaŋ wabɩtɩ mana.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: N nan keŋ vaa ba wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba ta kaaŋ dɩ tɩɩnsa ba wʋbɩatɩtɩ wɩa.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ dene balɩ nʋahaalɩŋ wɩa die yine bʋmbʋŋaŋ nʋarɩ dɩ yi nʋakʋʋrɩ, ta jadieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kʋʋrɩnna ta nyɩŋ, ka kaaŋ yʋasɩ ta ka bɩa.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Yada yiwo, fʋ tuone a dii dɩ fʋ yalla tama dɩ nan ye nyindiekemba wa nan seŋ ye he. Ta bɩ tuo dii dɩ fʋ kana ka yese nyindiekemba ba seŋ beri mɩŋ.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die bɩrɩ wa kʋrɩŋ ma vuosi ba yada wɩa.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Tɩ yada wɩa tɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die yuori wo ʋ nʋaŋ a balɩ ta ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie dɩ naaŋ. Die ʋ nagɩ wa jadieke tɩ kana ka bɩagɩ yese a naaŋ jadieke tɩ yesinene naa.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ da vaa tɩ wɩarɩ Masia wɩaha wupiilikehe dagɩŋ ma, ama nɩ vaa tɩ dagɩma wudiekemba dɩ mʋna a yɩma vuodiekemba dɩ kʋrɩnna Masia dɩɩsɩŋ ma wa. Die wɩa tɩ ka bɩ yaala tɩ balɩma yaa gamma wudieke wɩa tɩ mʋna tɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ tɩ wʋbɩatɩ ma ta yi Ŋmɩŋ yada.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Abeli die dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa, die ʋ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ kaabɩ dieke dɩ faasɩna a vɩɩna a tɩaŋ Keeni kaabɩkʋ. Ʋ yadaka wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnʋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ sʋŋ die fɩalɩya mɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ piinisisi. Abeli die kpiye mɩŋ, ama die ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa ʋ ye bala mɩŋ jinne, dama wudieke ʋ yine wo ye daga tɩ mɩŋ.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: ta bɩ balɩma yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sɩɩla wɩa aŋaŋ nuuŋ dɩɩsɩŋ vuosi ma aŋaŋ kumbu hagɩkʋ wɩa aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balla ʋ dii vuosi sarɩya dene ta ka wo kpatɩŋ dene.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Enɔki die dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa die ʋ ka kpiye, ama Ŋmɩŋ die nagɩ wa mɩŋ a jʋalɩ arɩzanna ma ta vuoŋ dɩ ka bɩ ye wo. Dɩ maagɩya mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Enɔki die yiwo vuodieke dɩ fɩalɩnana Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ ta ʋ keŋ nagɩ wa a jʋalɩ arɩzanna ma.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ saagɩ tɩ nan ga nɩŋŋa.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a fɩalɩ Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ dɩɩ yi dɩ tieŋ ka yi wo yada, dama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kienene Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ mʋ ʋ yi wo yada dɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ seŋ beri mɩŋ ta yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ yaalala wa nyʋarɩ.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩ diekemba dɩ zetine ʋ dɩɩsɩŋ, dɩ nan dɩ hagɩrɩŋ aŋ ba bɩagɩ a tarɩgɩ ba sʋŋanyile. Saŋŋa kaanɩ die ba bie wo Ŋmɩŋ chaanɩŋ ma, ta banɩŋ aŋaŋ ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka, ta laŋ arɩzanna ma piini a ye.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nowa yada wɩa die dɩ yi ta ʋ wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ kpaaŋkʋ yaa gamma nyindiekemba die ʋ kana ka bɩagɩ ye aŋaŋ ʋ nine wo. Die ʋ tuo Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ ta kpaasɩ haarɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ deŋ die dɩ juu ka ma ta ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ. Dene die dɩ dagɩ dɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi yaa wa bɩaŋ, ama ʋ yadaka wɩa die dɩ yi ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wa wa vuovɩɩnʋ.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: ta die sɩba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa nansɩŋ dɩ sɩna die, ta sɩba dʋnɩa dieke dɩ kienene wo hagɩrɩŋ wɩa.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abarahami die yiwo Ŋmɩŋ yada die wɩa saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋ dieke ʋ benne wo a ga tɩŋgbaŋ dieke wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ nagɩ yɩ wa wa, die ʋ saagɩ ta ga. Die ʋ vaa wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋgbaŋka ta ka sɩba jigidieke ʋ ganana.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: ama ta keŋ zeti Masia dɩɩsɩŋ. Dɩ nan dɩ hagɩrɩŋ dɩ ba yiŋŋi tarɩgɩ ba sʋŋanyile, dama ba bɩ kʋʋ wa Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembike dagarɩkʋ ma ta yi wo viivi yeŋ me mɩŋ.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Ʋ yadaka wɩa die dɩ yi ʋ ga yi chaanʋ tɩŋgbaŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ wa wa ma. Die ʋ bie wo dɔŋɩsɩ gbaŋtɩ vɩɩtɩ ma; ta ʋ ballɩ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu vuodiekemba die dɩ kenne tuo nʋarɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ bie mi.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Tɩŋgbaŋ dieke nɩɩŋ dɩ ŋaana nɩɩ ka dɩ sʋnsɩ ta yie nyindiike a yɩa vuodiekemba dɩ kʋna wa, Ŋmɩŋ yi alibarika yɩ kanɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka mi.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Dama Abarahami die chɩɩsa dɩ ʋ keŋ juu tɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tuurine ta mɩɩ wa ma, tɩka yiwo jadieke dɩ balla ka bemme haahuu.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi ka nyuuŋ haŋgɔɔsɩ aŋaŋ huubɩatɩ, die nɩŋ ka wo nyʋarɩ, dɩ kaaŋ yʋasɩ ta Ŋmɩŋ kaa kaatɩ yɩ ka aŋ ka dii boliŋ a chʋʋsɩ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Ʋ hɔgʋ Sara dɩaŋ die kʋrɩŋya mɩŋ ta yi hɔgʋpɩrɩŋ, ama wʋnɩŋ Sara yada wɩa die ʋ keŋ yigi nyuuti ta mɩɩrɩ, dama ʋ die sɩba a baarɩ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩna wa nʋarɩ yiwo wusietieŋ.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Ama tɩ nɩmballɩ, aŋaŋ tɩ bala naa mana, ama tɩ ka chele yaa gamma nɩ ma. Tɩ sɩba dɩ nɩ bie sievɩɩŋkʋ ma, gbatɩtaanɩŋ sievɩɩŋkʋ ma.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Abarahami vuodieke dɩ kana ka yʋa aŋaŋ kuŋ naa, ama ʋ kasɩŋ haagɩsɩ die dɩ keŋ dala sɩba chɩŋmarɩsɩ dɩ dala dene wo, ta bɩ dala sɩba mʋgɩŋ tambʋsɩŋ dɩ dala dene ta wo biisiŋ.
