eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ yiwo wusietieŋ. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ kaaŋ daaŋ tʋŋ dieke nɩ tʋnna wa, yaa choti dieke nɩ yalla aŋaŋ wa wa ta die suŋŋi ta ye suŋŋe Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩba wa.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Vuosi gie mana die keŋ kpi mɩŋ aŋaŋ ba yada, ama ta ye ka tuo wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ ba wa, ama ba yadaka wɩa die ba woliŋ ye ke saasaa, dama die ba sɩba ta saagɩ dɩ ba yiwo chaanɩŋ ta kaaŋ yʋasɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Tɩ yaala nɩ wʋnyɩ mana yallɩma kanɩŋ sʋkʋ mi a ga tʋgɩ kpatɩkʋ, amʋ nɩ yalla tama dɩ nɩ nan ye jadieke wo nɩ nan seŋ a ye ke.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ balala wɩaha gie wo yuori daga wa dɩ ba yaala ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Tɩ ka yaala nɩ yi walɩnkpɩatieliŋ, ama tɩ yaala nɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ yine Yisa yada, ta yallɩma suguru, ta keŋ ye jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ ba wa.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Die ba ka yile yaa gamma tɩŋgbaŋ dieke ba vana wa, dɩɩ yi die ba tɩŋ yile ka wɩa, ba tɩŋ nan ye sieŋ a yiŋŋi ga ka ma.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yɩna Abarahami nʋarɩ, die ʋ hʋʋ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ saaŋ ma, dama vuoŋ wori a tɩaŋ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ ʋ baaŋ nan hʋʋ vuoke mi saaŋ ma.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Ama die ba yile mɩŋ yaa gamma tɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ tɩanna ka ta bie arɩzanna ma. Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ka chɩga viivi dɩ ba wasɩma wa ba Ŋmɩŋ, dama die ʋ wʋnsɩ wa tigikpɩɩŋ a gbarɩ ba.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: Die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n yɩa fʋ nʋaŋ dɩ n nan yi alibarika a yi fʋ ma, ta yɩ fʋ haagɩsɩ pam.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Abarahami dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa, die ʋ nagɩ ʋ bʋa Aziki dɩ ʋ yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ magɩsɩna wa wa. Ye, wʋnɩŋ Abarahami wo mi die yine vuodieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋarɩ mi, wʋnɩŋ ʋ bɩna yi vuodieke dɩ nagɩna ʋ bʋadembi balɩmɩŋ dɩ ʋ yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Abarahami die dɩ dii suguru ta die keŋ ye jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋarɩ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ wa wa.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Ŋmɩŋ die wɔŋ balɩ wa mɩŋ dɩ, Aziki ma nan ye haagɩsɩ diekemba n yɩna nʋarɩ dɩ n nan yɩ fʋ wa.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Vuota dɩ keŋ baa ʋ hʋʋ ʋ ŋaaŋ hʋʋ wa vuodieke dɩ tɩanna wa saaŋ ma, ta ʋ hʋʋsɩsɩ dɩ kʋa nɩnhagɩrɩŋ mana.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abarahami die yiwo yada dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan bɩagɩ sʋgɩrɩ kunti a nyɩŋ kuŋ me, ta ka seŋ sɩɩ sɩba Abarahami die tuo wo Aziki a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ bala ba tuo nʋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ziene wo, die ʋ yaala ʋ dagɩ ba dɩ ʋ kaaŋ tarɩgɩ ʋ sʋŋanyile; die wɩa die ʋ hʋʋ a gʋtɩ ʋ nʋarɩ ma.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Aziki yada wɩa die ʋ balɩ wʋvɩɩŋ dieke die dɩ balla ka keŋ Jakobu aŋaŋ Esɔɔ ma wa.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ chɩbɩ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ. Die wɩa nyinti ale wa gie, ʋ nʋarɩ aŋaŋ ʋ hʋna wa wo tarɩgɩŋ. Die wɩa tɩ chɩgɩna a ga ʋ jigiŋ a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ naa wa, dɩ mʋ tɩ vaa ʋ nʋarɩ gie kpaŋŋɩsɩ tɩ aŋ tɩ yigi tama dieke tɩ yalla wa a yalla keŋ keŋ.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Jakobu die dɩ yalla yada wɩa, die ʋ balla ʋ kpi wo die ʋ balɩ wʋvɩɩna a yɩ Josefu ballɩdembisi wʋnyɩ mana ta die keŋ jalɩŋ ʋ daaŋgbanɩŋ ta jɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tama dieke tɩ yalla naa die vana tɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ hagɩrɩ, ta ga juu arɩzanna ma Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ a garɩsa garɩ dieke dɩ puone juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ a ga tʋgɩ juodieke dɩ yine kasɩ Ŋmɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ wa.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Josefu die dɩ yalla yada wa wɩa die ʋ balla ʋ kpi wo, die ʋ balɩ yaa gamma Izara vuosi dɩ balla ba nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma dene, ta die balɩ yaa gamma ba balla ba yi ʋ kɔba dene.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Mi die Yisa die dɩ wɔŋ zie tɩ naŋ ma a yi tɩ sieyiiritu, ta yi kaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ yaa gamma haahuu sɩba Melikizedeki.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Mosisi chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ die dɩ yalla yada wɩa, die ba mɩɩrɩna wa ta ye ʋ vɩɩna dene wo, die ba nagɩ wa a lɔbɩrɩ chɩɩta ataa, ta die ka chɩga naaŋ wa dɩ ziene nʋa dieke wo.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Mosisi die dɩ yalla yada wɩa, die ʋ bɩrɩnna wa die ʋ ka saagɩya dɩ ba wasɩma wa Naaŋ Faro lɩa bʋa.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: Die ʋ saagɩya mɩŋ dɩ ʋ dii wahala aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi ama ta ka saagɩya tʋntʋmbɩatɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩ dieke dɩ yine bɩta wa.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Die ʋ yiliye mɩŋ dɩ, dɩɩ yi ba zɩa wa Masia wɩa dɩ yaa nyʋarɩ pam a tɩaŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ nyinti mana, dama ʋ nine die bie wo nyʋarɩ dieke dɩ kienene wo me mɩŋ.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Mosisi die dɩ yalla yada wɩa, die ʋ nyɩŋ Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma ta die ka chɩga naaŋ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩ dieke dɩ baaŋ keŋ ʋ ma, ama ta die gara nɩŋŋa sɩba ʋ seŋ ye wo Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke vuoŋ dɩ kana ka yese wo aŋaŋ ʋ nine wo.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Die ʋ yalla yada wɩa die ʋ piili gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga, ta die yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ Izara vuosi nagɩ zɩŋ mɩsɩ ba gbɩabɩsɩ amʋ kuŋ malakaka kaaŋ keŋ a kʋʋ ba bʋmbʋŋaŋ ballɩdembisi.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Die Izara vuosi yada wɩa die dɩ yi ta mʋgɩŋmɩmbʋ die dɩ puo ta yi tɩŋgbaŋ kpɩaŋ a yɩ ba ba jʋalɩ, ta Ijipiti vuosisi dɩaŋ die dɩ mɩa dɩ ba jʋalɩ ama mʋgɩrɩ nyaabʋ die dɩ keŋ lagɩŋ ta dii be.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Izara vuosi yada wɩa die ba chʋnna a giliŋ Jeriko tɩka daraa ayʋpɔyɩ wa die ka gaama mana die dɩ nan dene.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Rahabi, hɔgʋ dieke die dɩ tʋnnana daakpana tʋʋmaha yada wɩa die Izara vuosisi die ka gʋtɩ kʋʋ wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba die dɩ zetine Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ, dama die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo Izara vuodiekemba die dɩ kenne dɩ ba maansɩ daansɩ tɩka chaantɩ.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: N bɩ balɩ nɩ vuosi bataŋ wɩa a gʋtɩ? Dɩɩ yi saŋŋa dɩ beri n tɩŋ nan balɩ nɩ yaa gamma Gidioni wɩa, aŋaŋ Baraki wɩa, aŋaŋ Samasini wɩa, aŋaŋ Jafita wɩa, aŋaŋ Davidi wɩa, aŋaŋ Samuli wɩa, aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩŋ wɩa.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Ba yada wɩa die ba bataŋ die dɩ wagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ, ta ba bataŋ wudieke dɩ mʋna, ta tuo Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ ba jadieke, ta ba bataŋ die dɩ vaa naachige dɩ ka bɩagɩ yuori a nʋa.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: ta ba bataŋ dɩ kpisi bolikpɩɩma, ta ba bataŋ die dɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ takoobi kuŋ me. Ta ba bataŋ die yiwo wabɩtɩ ama a keŋ bɩrɩŋ hagɩrɩtieliŋ, ta bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ taa tɔŋ aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ ta vaa tɩŋgbaŋ gaasɩ sojasisi dɩ chɩgɩ.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Ta hɔgʋba bataŋ maarɩtɩ vuodiekemba die dɩ kpine dɩ hagɩ kunti me. Ta ba bataŋ die dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ yada wɩa die ba nɩgɩ kʋʋ ba, dama die ba ka yaala ba zeti Ŋmɩŋ dɩɩsɩŋ ta chɩgɩ nyɩŋ kumbu me, ama die ba yaala ba hagɩ kuŋ me a ye miivoli dieke dɩ tɩanna die.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Die ba vʋarɩ wa ba bataŋ fala ta tɩbɩ ba, ta bɔbɩ ba bataŋ aŋaŋ chɔrɩma a nagɩ ba yi dansarɩka ma.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Die ba taa ba bataŋ tana, ta die gobi ba bataŋ bule, ta die kʋa ba bataŋ aŋaŋ takoobi. Ba bataŋ die bɔbɩ wa yiise aŋaŋ bie gbaŋtɩ ta dɩ dɩa aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, ta vuosi die dɩ mugise be ta yie be bɩaŋ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi die ka mʋ dɩ ba bemme aŋaŋ ba. Die ba dɩ dɩa mɩŋ hagɩyeŋ me aŋaŋ tana ma, ta juo taŋpaŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ vɔrɩtɩ diekemba dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Vuosi gie mana ba yada wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ bɩrɩ ba ama ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka tuo wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ ba wa.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: dama Ŋmɩŋ die wɔŋ saŋ wa wudieke dɩ faasɩna vɩɩna a zieŋ tɩ ama daa tɩ nyɩɩna ma, ba gbaŋ ba gʋtɩya mɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋ saŋkʋ die yiwo naa, dɩ banɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩ mana nan daansɩ a lagɩŋ a bemme vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Vuosi gie mana yiwo siaratieliŋ a giliŋ tɩ. Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ kpaŋŋɩ tɩ gbaŋ a vigi wudieke mana dɩ baaŋ kagɩ tɩ Masia sieŋ me a taaŋ, aŋaŋ bɩaŋ dieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a yigi tɩ; nɩ vaa tɩ dii suguru a mɩɩ mɩɩ dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ tɩ wo naa.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Nɩ ko vaa aŋ tɩ nine bemme Yisa ma; wʋnɩŋ tɩ yada dɩ taasɩ a nyɩŋ ka piiliŋ me aŋaŋ ka kpatɩŋ. Die ʋ ka chɩga viivi dɩ ʋ kpi dagarɩkʋ ma dama sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ die zene a gbarɩ wa ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die yine ʋ wa kalɩ Ŋmɩŋ naalagbaŋtɩ ma ʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ lele gie wo.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Nɩ yilime yaa gamma wudiekemba die dɩ haarɩna wa aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ die dɩ hana wa dene, amʋ tɩ kaaŋ gbalɩgɩ ta kʋʋ tamaha.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Dama nɩ kpaŋŋɩna nɩ gbaŋ ta waga aŋaŋ bɩaŋ naa, nɩ wʋnyɩ ye ka kpaŋŋɩ ʋ gbaŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ba kʋna wa.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Nɩ ka tɩɩnsa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kpaana nɩ sɩba chɔɔŋ dɩ kpaanana ʋ ballɩlɩ dene wo? Dɩ maagɩya dɩ: N bʋa, saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩa fʋ kpaamɩŋ, fʋ wʋmma dɩɩ yi ʋ datɩ fʋ tɩbɩŋ, daa vaa fʋ kpaŋŋɩnana fʋ gbaŋ dene wo.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Dama Ŋmɩŋ kpaama vuodieke ʋ chone mɩŋ, ta vuodieke nɩŋ mana ʋ tuone a yi ʋ bʋa wʋnɩŋ vuoke ʋ data ʋ tɩbɩŋ.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Nɩ yallɩma hanɩa nɩ wahala diile me, ta nagɩ ka sɩba chɔɔŋ dɩ ŋaana a data ʋ bʋa tɩbɩŋ dene wo. Nɩ wahala diilehe dagɩya mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ pɔgɩlɩ nɩ mɩŋ sɩba ʋ ballɩ. Bʋa wori ta ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ ka data ʋ tɩbɩŋ.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ ka ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ sɩba ʋ ballɩlɩ mana wa, die nɩŋ dɩ dagɩya mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yiwo daakpana ballɩ ta ka seŋ yi ʋ ballɩ aŋaŋ wusie.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Nɩ chʋalɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne tɩŋgbaŋka gie me data tɩ tɩba, ta tɩ yɩa ba jɩlɩma. Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ sʋʋŋ tɩ gbaŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ halɩtɩ Chɔɔŋ wa ta ye miivoli.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Tɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie chʋalɩŋ naga saŋŋa bɩta mɩŋ a data tɩ tɩba sie dieke me ba yene ta dɩ vɩɩna, ama Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ data tɩ tɩba dɩ ʋ suŋŋi tɩ, amʋ tɩ nan dɩ beri kasɩ sɩba ʋ bene kasɩ dene wo.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Tɩbɩdatɩŋ saŋŋa tʋa mɩŋ ta wo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dinene ka wahala ka ŋaŋ ye wo ka nyʋarɩ dieke dɩ yine sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ aŋaŋ berivɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Nɩ gbalɩgɩya mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a bɩ hagɩrɩma.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Nɩ bemme berivɩɩnɩŋ amʋ nɩ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ, ta nɩ gbalɩgɩsɩ kaaŋ vaa nɩ bɩa.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Nɩ kpaŋŋɩ nɩ gbaŋ a bemme vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ mana, ta bemme kasɩ dama vuoŋ wori a baaŋ nan ye tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo dɩɩ yi ʋ ka yi kasɩ.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Nɩ gbarɩ nɩ gbaŋ ta da keŋ waarɩ Ŋmɩŋ zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩlaha. Nɩ wʋnyɩ da yallɩma tʋaŋ ʋ sʋŋ ma aŋ kanɩŋ tʋabʋ mi keŋ yalla sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ a keŋ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ ma, ta chʋʋsɩ vuosi pam.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Nɩ gbarɩ nɩ gbaŋ ta da keŋ yime dɩgɩntɩ tʋʋma yaa ta daansa Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yɔrɩ sɩba Esɔɔ dɩ yine dene wo, ta die nagɩ ʋ nyɩŋkʋrɩtɩ a tarɩgɩ nyindiike a dii bʋnyɩ wa.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ yaala ʋ tuo alibarika a nyɩŋ ʋ chɔɔŋ wa jigiŋ ta die kʋŋ aŋaŋ nɩntaantɩ ama ta die ka yeye, dama die ʋ ka ye sie dieke ʋ baaŋ daagɩ a tarɩgɩ wudieke ʋ wolinne a chʋʋsɩ wa.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ nɩ ka keŋ jigidieke nɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ gbi sɩba Izara vuosi die dɩ kenne Sinayi tankpeŋkpɩɩŋ ma wa, jigidieke bolikpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ dinene ta lɩmɩŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ beri, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ nɩga wa.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: ta kantaŋ die dɩ kʋŋ ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ balɩ wa. Die ba wʋnna lɔlɩkʋ mi wo, ba piili jʋʋsa ta die ka yaala kanɩŋ lɔlɩkʋ bɩ balɩ bɩbra.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: dama mɩrɩ dieke die ba wʋnna wa die hagɩrɩŋ a yɩ ba mɩŋ. Die ka baarɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi dɔŋ gbaŋ dɩ gbi tankpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ba mʋ ba taa ka aŋaŋ tana a kʋʋ mɩŋ.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Mi die yaa ŋmaamɩŋ pam, die wɩa Mosisi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmaamɩŋ yalla mɩŋ, ta n nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ chele.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, nɩ keŋ wo kunkogi dieke ba wasɩnana Zayɔni, wudieke dɩ yine miivolitieŋ Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ ta yi arɩzanna ma Jerusalemi wa, jigidieke malakasi tuse tuse dɩ lagɩŋna aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩkʋ wa.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ nɩ keŋ wo vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine ʋ ballɩ ta ba saara dɩ maagɩ arɩzanna ma wa jigiŋ. Nɩ keŋ wo Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine vuota mana sarɩyadiiru wo jigiŋ, aŋaŋ sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yi ta ba halɩtɩ dɩ mɩŋŋɩ beri wo jigiŋ.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Nɩ keŋ wo Yisa, vuodieke dɩ yine nuupɔɔnɩgɩrʋ wa jigiŋ, ta bɩ keŋ nʋahaalɩkʋ jigiŋ. Ta zɩŋ dieke die dɩ nyɩnna daga wʋvɩɩŋ dieke dɩ tɩanna Abeli zɩmbʋ wɩa.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ sɩmma ta da zeti dɩ nɩ wʋŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ balala yɩa nɩ wa, dama Izara vuosi die dɩ zetine vuodieke die dɩ balala yɩa ba tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo nʋarɩ, die ʋ ka vaa ba tɩbɩdatɩŋ; ta dɩɩ yi tɩ zeti ʋ kpaaŋkʋ die dɩ nyɩnna arɩzanna ma, lalɩa tɩ baa tɩ yi a chɩgɩ nyɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ ma?.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Saŋka mi die Ŋmɩŋ lɔlɩŋ die dɩ dɔŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie, ama lele ʋ yɩ wa nʋaŋ dɩ, N ka baa n dɔŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩɩna ma bɩbra, ama n baa dɔŋ wa tɩŋgbaŋka aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ gbaŋ.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Bɩbra wa dagɩya dɩ nyɩnnaaŋka mana nan dɔŋ ta vʋʋ, amʋ nyindiekemba dɩ kana ka bɩagɩ dɔŋ wa nan tɩalɩ.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ tuone naarɩ dieke dɩ kana kaaŋ dɔŋ naa wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ wasɩma Ŋmɩŋ ta jɩama wa aŋaŋ jɩlɩma aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ aŋ ʋ nine suuli.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: dama Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩ sɩba boli dieke dɩ dinene.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Juu vuosi mɩraha die yiwo arɩzanna ma nyɩŋvɩɩna diekemba dɩ kienene wo nɩasɩŋ, ama daa ka gbaŋ gbaŋ. Die wɩa, kaabɩ dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ ta ba yie bɩŋ mana wa kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi vuodiekemba dɩ kienene aŋaŋ kaabɩkʋ kasɩ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Dɩɩ yi die kaabɩkʋ tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ wʋnsɩ ba sʋgɩtɩ, ba tɩŋ nan kaabɩ bʋnyɩ ta vaa, ta bɩaŋ kaaŋ bɩ dɩ ba.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Ama ba yie kaabaha mi mɩŋ bɩŋ mana ta a tɩɩnsa vuosi ba wʋbɩatɩ.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: dama naarasɩ aŋaŋ bie zɩmbʋ gie kaaŋ bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ bɩaŋ.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Die wɩa, die Masia die dɩ bala ʋ keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ: Fʋnɩŋ ka yaala kaaba aŋaŋ piinisi, ama wʋnsɩ wa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a yɩ mɩŋ.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Fʋ sʋŋ ka fɩala aŋaŋ dɔŋɩsɩ ba nagɩna a yie kaaba ta jʋha wʋbɩatɩ wɩa wa.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Die wɩa maŋ baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ, manɩŋ n wʋnna dɩ n yi wudieke fʋnɩŋ fʋ yaalala, sɩba dɩ maagɩna die wo fʋ gbaŋkʋ ma yaa gamma n wɩa wa. .

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Die ʋ woliŋ baarɩ dɩ, Fʋnɩŋ fʋ wo sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ kaaba aŋaŋ piinisi aŋaŋ dɔŋɩsɩ ba nagɩna yi kaaba ta jʋa ha wa aŋaŋ kaabɩ dieke ba ŋaana kaaba ba wʋbɩatɩ wɩa wa. Die ba kaaba kaabaha gie sɩba mɩraha dɩ dagɩna dɩ ba yime die wo.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Womi die ʋ bɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ, n wʋnna n nan yi fʋ choti. Die wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa kaaba kʋraha mana a taaŋ ta nagɩ Masia kaabɩkʋ a zieŋ a naŋ ma.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Yisa Masia die yiwo wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala ta die nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaaba bʋnyɩ ta vʋarɩ tɩ wʋbɩatɩ a taaŋ.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Kɩkaabɩbalɩŋ dieke Juu vuosi kɩkaabɩtɩba dɩ ŋaana yie daaŋ mana wa kaaŋ bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ wʋbɩatɩ a taaŋ.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Ama Masia nɩŋ die nagɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ a yi kaabɩŋ bʋnyɩ bɩaŋ wɩa, kaabɩ dieke dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu ta vʋara bɩaŋ saŋŋa mana wa. Ta lele ʋ wa kalɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Mi ʋ wa chɩɩsa saŋŋa dieke dɩ baaŋ keŋ tʋgɩ aŋ Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ ʋ dataasɩ a yi ʋ nuusi me.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Die wɩa, wʋnɩŋ die ʋ yiwo kɩkaabɩ balɩmɩŋ ta yi vuodiekemba ʋ vʋarɩna a nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma wa dɩ ba bemme vɩɩnɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ gbaŋ di wo siara yaa gamma wɩɩrɩ gie mi, dɩ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ, nʋarɩ gie maŋ baa n zieŋ aŋaŋ ba daa diekemba dɩ kienene wo: Manɩŋ n nan nagɩ n mɩraha a yi ba sʋgɩtɩ ma, ta maagɩha a diisi ba sɩkpara ma.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Ta die bɩ balɩ a gʋtɩ dɩ, Ta ba wʋbɩatɩtɩ aŋaŋ ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ, n kaaŋ bɩ tɩɩnsɩha bɩbra.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna wʋbɩatɩ a chaa naa, ʋ ka bɩ yaala kaabɩŋ mana bɩbra.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: N nɩmballɩ, Yisa kumbu wɩa tɩ yaa wa sikimiŋ dɩ tɩ ga juu kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ wa jigiŋ.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: Yisa nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dieke dɩ yine sie haalɩŋ aŋaŋ sie dieke dɩ yalla miivoli wo yuorine sanʋarɩ a yɩ tɩ.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Tɩ yalla kɩkaabɩtʋ kpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ sɩna Ŋmɩŋ tigiŋ me wɩa.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: nɩ vaa tɩ chʋŋ gbigi Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩ diekemba dɩ benne vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ wusie yada, ta da yallɩma chɩɩlɩŋ, aŋaŋ tɩ sʋgɩ diekemba Yisa zɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩna sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ me wo, aŋaŋ tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke die tɩ sɩna aŋaŋ nyaaŋ dieke die dɩ wone dɩgɩntɩ wa.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ yigi tama dieke tɩ bala dɩ tɩ yallɩ wa a yalla keŋ keŋ ta da chɩɩlɩma, dama vuodieke dɩ yɩna tɩ nʋarɩ yiwo wusietieŋ.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ baga bemme tamba ma ta suŋŋime taŋ yaa gamma choti aŋaŋ tʋnvɩɩna yiile.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Nɩ da vaa tɩ vaa lagɩmɩŋ sɩba vuosi bataŋ dɩ yinene dene wo, ama nɩ vaa tɩ faasɩ kpaŋŋɩma taŋ, sɩba tɩ yene ta tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ daarɩ faasɩ a chʋŋ gbige dene wo.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Tɩ sɩbɩna wusie dieke ba yuori dagɩ tɩ naa, dɩɩ yi tɩ ko yie bɩaŋ, kaabɩŋ bɩ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a vʋarɩ bɩabʋ mi.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Wudieke dɩ tɩalɩna yiwo sarɩya diile dieke dɩ yalla ŋmaamɩŋ aŋaŋ bolikpeŋkpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ kienene ta nan daansɩ a chʋʋsɩ vuodiekemba dɩ zetine Ŋmɩnnɩ.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Vuodieke nɩŋ mana die dɩ zetine Mosisi mɩraha dɩɩsɩŋ, ta vuosi dɩ sɩba ta yi siara vuosi bale yaa bataa, die ba ŋaaŋ kʋa dɩ tieŋ ta ka chɩga wa zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Die nɩŋ, yilime wudieke dɩ bala ka yi a yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yɩa Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa jɩlɩma ta ka bɩ yɩa nʋarɩ zɩŋ dieke dɩ yine tɩ kasɩ wa jɩlɩma ta zɩa Halɩkasɩ dieke dɩ suŋŋine tɩ wo, banɩŋ vuosi mi nan faasɩ ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ pam.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Dama tɩ sɩba dɩ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, Manɩŋ n balla n yiŋŋi tuŋ bɩaŋ. Ka bɩ maagɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan keŋ dii ʋ vuosi sarɩya.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Dɩ yaa ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ vuoŋ juu miivolitieŋ Ŋmɩnnɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ma.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Nɩ da daaŋ ma saŋŋa dieke nɩ wolinne a bʋgɩrɩ yaa gamma Masia ma wa, die nɩ dii wo wahala pam ama die a ka nyaŋŋɩ nɩ.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Saŋŋa a taŋ ba ŋaaŋ zɩa nɩ wa yaalɩŋ ma ta mugise nɩ, ta saŋŋa a taŋ dɩaŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ yi siri dɩ nɩ gʋtɩ wa vuodiekemba ba mugisinene wo.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Die nɩ ŋaaŋ chɩga zɔɔlɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ benne dansarɩka ma wa, ta saŋŋa a taŋ ba keŋ gbatɩ nɩ nyinti, nɩ ŋaaŋ yaa wa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dama nɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nɩ yaa nyindiekemba dɩ tɩanna die ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ nɩ wa aŋ ka bemme yaa gamma haahuu wo.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Die wɩa nɩ da vasɩma nɩ kpaŋŋɩnana nɩ gbaŋ dene wo, dama ka yaa wa nyʋarɩ pam a kieŋ.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Nɩ mʋ nɩ yallɩma suguru, ta bɩagɩ yi wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala, ta ye jadieke die ʋ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ tɩ wa.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Dɩ tɩalɩ wa bɩta pilii ta vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo nan keŋ ʋ kaaŋ yʋasɩ.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: N vuovɩɩnaha nɩŋ ba nan yi mɩŋ yada ta bemme; ama dɩɩ yi ba wʋnyɩ dɩ yiŋŋi kʋaŋ n sʋŋ kaaŋ fɩalɩ aŋaŋ wa.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Tɩnɩŋ tɩ ka yi vuodiekemba dɩ yiŋŋinene kʋaŋ ta nan yɔrɩ wa, ama tɩ yiwo Masia yada ta ʋ gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Nɩ yine Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ wɩa nɩ chome taŋ saŋŋa mana.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Nɩ da keŋ daaŋ dɩ nɩ tuo chaanɩŋ nɩ tige me, dama vuosi bataŋ yiwo die a tuo malakasi chaantɩ ta die ka sɩba dɩ ba yiwo malakasi.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Nɩ tɩɩnsɩma vuodiekemba dɩ benne dansarɩka ma wa sɩba nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ banɩŋ lagɩŋna bie dansarɩka ma. Ta tɩɩnsɩma vuodiekemba dɩ dinene wahalaka sɩba nɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ banɩŋ dinene wahalaka.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Nɩ yallɩma jɩlɩma aŋaŋ taŋ, nɩ hɔgʋba faarɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ dembisi yallɩŋ ma, ta da yime daakpana yaa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, dama Ŋmɩŋ nan keŋ datɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene die tɩba.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Nɩ vʋarɩ nɩ lɔla a nyɩŋ ligire yaalɩŋ ma, ta vaa nɩ nine suuli jadieke nɩ yalla, dama Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, N kaaŋ wɔŋ vaa nɩ, ta n kaaŋ wɔŋ daaŋ nɩ wɩa.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Die wɩa nɩ vaa tɩ yallɩma sikimiŋ ta balɩ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ yine n susuŋŋiru, n kaaŋ dɩ chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ. Bɩa vuota dɩ nan bɩagɩ a yi mɩŋ.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Nɩ da keŋ daaŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩkʋraha die dɩ bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩa nɩ wa, ta tɩɩnsɩ die ba benne dene ta keŋ kpi dene wo, ta chansɩma ba yada.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yisa Masia dɩ sɩna die diene wo, ʋ sɩɩ wa die jinne, ta nan dɩ sɩɩ die yaa gamma haahuu.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Nɩ da vaa dagɩhaalɩŋ yiri yiri vaa nɩ taarɩ a nyɩŋ sievɩɩŋkʋ ma. Dɩ vɩɩna dɩ Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋiŋ yɩ vuoŋ hagɩrɩŋ ʋ sʋŋ ma; nyindiike diile chʋaraha dɩɩsɩŋ kaaŋ yɩ wa hagɩrɩŋ. Vuodiekemba dɩ pɔgɩlɩna nyindiikehe chʋaraha kaaŋ wɔŋ ye suŋŋiŋ a nyɩnna ka ma.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Kɩkaabɩtɩba diekemba dɩ yinene kaaba Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ jigiŋ wo, wo sieŋ dɩ ba dii kaabaha dɩ diisine tɩ koriku me wo.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Ta Juu vuosi kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋrɩ ŋaaŋ yaa wa dɔŋɩsɩ zɩŋ a ga juu kasɩ dɩaŋ kasɩ jigiri dɩ ʋ yi kaaba bɩaŋ wɩa, ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba ŋaaŋ jʋʋ wa dɔŋɩsɩ nyɩŋgbaŋɩsɩsɩ tɩka kʋanʋaŋ.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Die wɩa Yisa gbaŋ die kpi wo tɩka kʋanʋaŋ, dɩ ʋ vʋarɩ vuosisi a nyɩŋ ba bɩaŋ ma aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ zɩŋ.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ nyɩŋ ga ʋ jigiŋ a ga dii viivi aŋaŋ wa.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Dama tɩŋ dieke dɩ bala ka bemme yaa gamma tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wori, ama tɩgɩ dieke dɩ kienene wo tɩ chɩɩsa.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ vaa tɩ bɩrɩma Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ saŋŋa mana Yisa Masia saaŋ ma; bɩrɩ dieke dɩ nyɩnana tɩ nʋa ma wa yine kaaba a yɩ wa.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Nɩ da vaa dɩ nɩ yime vɩɩnɩŋ a suŋŋime taŋ, dama a yine kaabɩ diekemba dɩ fɩalɩnana Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Nɩ saagɩma yɩma nɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ ta dɩa ba nʋa, banɩŋ ba daansɩnana nɩ haalɩtɩ ta ka voose, dama banɩŋ ba bala ba keŋ dagɩ ba tʋnna dene a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi nɩ saaga a yɩa ba ba nan dɩ tʋma ba tʋʋma aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ daa die ba nan dɩ tʋma aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, die kaaŋ suŋŋi nɩ.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ a yɩma tɩ. Tɩ sɩba dɩ tɩ ka yi wɩɩŋ a chʋʋsɩ ta yaala tɩ yime wudieke dɩ mʋna saŋŋa mana.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: Ama n faasɩ jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ vaa n yiŋŋi keŋ nɩ jigiŋ daraa gie nʋaŋ.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana tɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩkʋ die sʋgɩrɩna tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me. Wʋnɩŋ Yisa wa yine gbɩgbarɩtʋ kpeŋkpɩɩŋ, dama die ʋ kpiye mɩŋ, ta ʋ zɩŋ dɩ wʋnsɩ Ŋmɩŋ nʋarɩ dieke dɩ bala ka bemme yaa gamma haahuu wo.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: wʋnɩŋ ʋ mɩŋŋɩ a wʋnsɩ beri vɩɩnɩŋ amʋ nɩ nan dɩ yie wudieke ʋ yaalala, ta tʋma tʋnvɩɩna mana; Yisa Masia wɩa, ʋ vaa nɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ fɩalɩnana ʋ sʋŋ. Wʋnɩŋ Masia sɩna bɩrɩŋ yaa gamma haahuu. Wusie.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n jʋʋsa nɩ dɩ nɩ dii suguru ta wʋŋ n kpaaŋkʋ gie, dama gbaŋ dieke n maagɩna a yɩa nɩ naa yiwo gbaŋ gobiŋ.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: N yaala nɩ sɩmma dɩ ba vʋarɩ tɩ nɩmbʋa Timoti a nyɩŋ dansarɩkaka ma mɩŋ. Dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ n jigiŋ lele, maŋ keŋ kieŋ n nan yaa wa keŋ.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Nɩ waasɩ nɩ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuosi mana a yɩ tɩ. Tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ nyɩna Itali tɩŋgbaŋ ma waasa nɩ.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi nɩ mana.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Manɩŋ Jemisi, vuodieke dɩ yine Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntʋ aŋaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ, n waasa Izara buuriŋ baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo dɩ jaasɩna dʋnɩa mana ma wa mana.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ saŋŋa dieke nɩ dinene wahala yiri yiri.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: dama nɩ sɩba a baarɩ wahalaha mi keniŋ vasa nɩ sɩba dɩ nɩ seŋ yiwo Yisa Masia yada wusie, aŋ ka yi nɩ yaabɩɩla.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Nɩ yallɩma sikimiŋ a ga tʋgɩ kpatɩŋ ta nɩ da keŋ a nan, amʋ nɩ da keŋ waarɩ berivɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ jaaŋ mana.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ waarɩ yɩaŋ, ʋ jʋʋsɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yɩnana; dama ʋ wo halɩ aŋaŋ yɩɩla, ta yɩa vuoŋ mana ta ka galɩma ba, aŋ ʋ yɩ wa.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Fʋ keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ, fʋ yi yada ta da keŋ a chɩɩlɩ; ama vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsa aŋaŋ chɩɩlɩŋ, ʋ sɩɩ sɩba mʋgɩkpɩɩŋ nyapoŋikiŋ dieke bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ yalla dɔma.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi da keŋ yilime dɩ ʋ nan ye jaaŋ a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: dama ʋ yaa sʋŋanyile bule ta ka zie jigibalɩmɩŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Dɩ vɩɩna tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ yine zɔɔlɩntieliŋ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ kɔtɩna ba wa wɩa.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: ta tɩ nɩmballɩ diekemba dɩ yalla nyinti gbaŋ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ sʋʋna ba wɩa, dama ka kaaŋ yʋasɩ aŋ ba tɩaŋ ga sɩba tɩɩwute.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Dama ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ ka tuolibu ŋaaŋ nɩgɩ wa tɩɩka aŋ ka tɩɩwutehe jigi a nan, aŋ ka vɩɩnɩŋ mana chʋʋsɩ. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ sɩɩ a yɩ ligiretieliŋ; saŋŋa dieke ba yilinene dɩ ba tʋʋma ga nɩŋŋa wa saŋka mi ba kpie.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodieke dɩ nyaŋŋɩna magɩsɩŋ, dama Ŋmɩŋ nan yɩ ba nyʋarɩ dieke dɩ yine miivoli dieke ʋ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ chone wo wo.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ ye magɩsɩkʋ mi, ʋ da keŋ baarɩ dɩ, Magɩsɩkʋ gie nyɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ. Dama jaaŋ wori a nan bɩagɩ magɩsɩ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ yi bɩaŋ ta Ŋmɩŋ ka magɩsa vuoŋ dɩ ʋ yi bɩaŋ.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ yaala ʋ yi ʋ sʋgɩbɩaŋ tʋʋma die yine magɩsɩŋ.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Sʋgɩbɩaŋ dɩ keŋ yigi vuoŋ, ka ŋaaŋ yaa wa tʋmbɩatɩ a keŋ, ta tʋmbɩatɩ kpatɩŋ yiwo kuŋ.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: N nɩmballɩ choti, nɩ daa vaa jaaŋ gaaŋ nɩ.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Javɩɩnɩŋ mana nyɩna wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ, tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, vuodieke dɩ naanna arɩzanna chaanɩŋ mana. Wʋnɩŋ ʋ ka tarɩga sɩba ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ chaanɩŋ dɩ ŋaana a tarɩga die wo; wʋnɩŋ ʋ ye yiwo Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ yaalala ʋ yi tɩ ʋ ballɩ, ʋ wusie wʋbalɩka ma ta tɩ ye miivoli haalɩŋ, dɩ tɩ tɩaŋ ʋ nyɩnnaaŋka mana.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: N nɩmballɩ, n yaala nɩ sɩmma wɩɩrɩ gie; vuoŋ mana mʋ ʋ yɩ lagɩ a wʋmma, ama ta sɩmma ʋ balala die, ta da jɩɩma sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: dama vuota sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ka tʋma Ŋmɩŋ tʋnvɩɩna diekemba ʋ yaalala.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Die wɩa, n nɩmballɩ, nɩ vʋarɩ sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ mana aŋaŋ wʋbɩatɩ mana nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma ma, ta sʋʋŋ nɩ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa, ta tuo wudieke ʋ nagɩna yɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma wa, dama wɩɩrɩ mi yaa hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ baaŋ nan vʋarɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Ama nɩ yime wudieke wɩɩrɩ dɩ dagɩna nɩ; dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋŋ wɩɩrɩ, ama ta ka yie ka dagɩnana nɩ die wo, nɩ gaama nɩ gbaŋ.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana wɩɩrɩ, ama ta ka yie ka dagɩnana die wo sɩɩ sɩba vuoŋ dɩ daansɩna ʋ gbaŋ dige me a ye ʋ gbaŋ ʋ sɩna die.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: dama ʋ daansɩ ye ʋ gbaŋ digeke me, ta ga, ʋ ŋaaŋ daaŋ wa ʋ sɩna die.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Ama vuodieke dɩ yilinene mɩrɩvɩɩnaha diekemba dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ vuota a taaŋ, ta mɩŋŋɩ dɩ ha, ta ka yi vuodieke dɩ wʋnnana ta daama ha ama ta yie a dagɩnana wudieke wo, Ŋmɩŋ nan yi alibarika a yi wudieke mana ʋ yinene me.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Vuoŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ ama ta ka yige ʋ nʋaŋ, dɩ tieŋ gaama ʋ gbaŋ, ta ʋ Yisa kʋaŋdɩɩsɩkɩrɩ dɩ wo nyʋarɩ.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Vuodieke dɩ suŋŋinene bikpiisi aŋaŋ kpihɔgʋba ba wahala ma, ta bɩ vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ a nyɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie wʋbɩatɩ ma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ senne yi Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wusie, ta wo taalɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia vuodieke dɩ yine hagɩrɩtieŋ yada die wo, nɩ da keŋ lugime.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Dɩɩ yi nyintitieŋ dɩ yeegi nyiŋyeeki vɩɩnɩŋ ta yi salɩma nuupiriŋ a keŋ juu nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ me, ta zɔɔlɩntieŋ dɩaŋ dɩ yeegi garɩchɔɔtɩ a keŋ juu.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: ta nɩ yɩ nyintitieŋ wo jɩlɩma pam, ta balɩ yɩ wa dɩ ʋ keŋ a kala nɩŋŋa, ama ta balɩ a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieŋ wo dɩ zie yaa ʋ sʋʋŋ kala tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: die nɩŋ nɩ yiwo vuodiekemba dɩ luginene aŋaŋ taŋ aŋaŋ sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ wʋmma; Ŋmɩŋ vʋarɩ wa zɔɔlɩntieliŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi nɩŋŋa, dɩ ba yi nyintitieliŋ ʋ jigiŋ, dama ba yi wo mɩŋ yada, ta ʋ yi be ʋ ballɩ dieke dɩ bala ba dii naaŋ dieke die ʋ yɩna nʋaŋ dɩ ʋ nan yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ chone wo wo.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ zɩa wa zɔɔlɩntieliŋ. Daa ligiretielibe mugisinene nɩ wa, ta yaa nɩ a gara sarɩya diile jigiŋ wo?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Daa banɩŋ, ba balala a chʋʋsa Yisa Masia saaŋ vɩɩŋ dieke dɩ sɩna nɩ wa?.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, Chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ chone fʋ gbaŋ die wo. Dɩɩ yi fʋ dɩɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ mɩrɩka gie, dɩ vɩɩna.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi nɩ luge aŋaŋ taŋ nɩ yiwo bɩaŋ ta mɩraha dɩ yuori a dagɩ nɩ dɩ nɩ yiwo mɩraha chʋchʋʋsɩrɩŋ.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Vuodieke dɩ saagɩ a dɩɩ mɩraha mana, ta keŋ chʋʋsɩ a kaanɩ gbaŋ, chʋʋsɩ wa a mana.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Mɩraha sʋŋ, Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ bala dɩ, Da keŋ yi daakpana yaa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, wʋnɩŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ bɩna baarɩ Daa keŋ kʋʋ vuoŋ. Die wɩa dɩɩ yi fʋ ka yi daakpana yaa hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma, ama ta kʋʋ vuoŋ, chʋʋsɩ wa mɩraha mana mana.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Nɩ balɩma ta tʋmma ta sɩmma dɩ Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ mɩrɩ dieke dɩ gbatɩnana vuosi a nyɩnna ba wʋbɩatɩ ma wa a dii nɩ sarɩya.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka chɩgɩ ba chanchaalɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ nan dii ba sarɩya ta kaaŋ chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ; ama zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla vuoŋ ka chɩga sarɩya diile.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: N nɩmballɩ, vuodieke dɩ bala dɩ ʋ yi Yisa Masia yada, ama ta ka tʋma tʋŋ dieke dɩ mʋna ʋ yi, dɩ yaa nyʋarɩ? Kanɩŋ yada ka mi nan bɩagɩ gbatɩ wa a taaŋ?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Dɩɩ yi ta fʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ beri ta wo jayeekiŋ aŋaŋ nyindiike dɩ ʋ dii.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: ta fʋ balɩ wa dɩ, Waasɩ tigiŋ, ta bɔba ta die ama ta ka yɩ wa jaaŋ, dɩ yaa nyʋarɩ?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Dɩɩ yi yada nyɩɩna ma dɩ beri, ama ta tʋʋma dɩ wori, kanɩŋ yadaka mi yiwo yada kpiikiŋ.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Vuoŋ wʋnyɩ yaa yada, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ yaa tʋnvɩɩna, n gbaŋ n nan yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, Dɩɩ yi fʋ ka tʋma tʋnvɩɩna, bɩa bala ka dagɩ dɩ fʋ seŋ yaa yada? Ama n tʋnvɩɩna bala ka dagɩ dɩ n seŋ yaa yada.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Fʋ yi yada dɩ Ŋmɩŋ balɩmɩŋ benne? Dɩ vɩɩna. Jɩmbɩatɩ gbaŋ yi yada dɩ Ŋmɩŋ balɩmɩŋ benne, ta cheele aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Fʋnɩŋ gaamʋ wa gie, fʋ yaala ba dagɩ fʋ dɩ dɩɩ yi fʋ yaa yada ta wo tʋnvɩɩna, yiwo yɔrɩ?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: Da tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋŋ Abarahami die dɩ nagɩna ʋ bʋa Aziki a dɩɩsɩ kʋrɩŋ ma dɩ ʋ kʋʋ wa a yi kaaba a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ?.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Nɩ ka ye ye? Die ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ wɩa yada wa wɩa, die wɩa die ʋ tʋma tʋnvɩɩnaha; tʋʋmaha gbaŋ die dagɩna dɩ ʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada wusie.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Die Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩka die dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die yiwo wusie dɩ, Abarahami die yiwo wo yada, die wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die wɩa die ba wasa wa Ŋmɩŋ zʋa.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ye ye? Vuoŋ tʋnvɩɩna wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan baarɩ dɩ vuoke yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ; ama daa ʋ yada nyɩɩna ma wɩa.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ yi a yɩ Rahabi hɔgʋ dieke die dɩ tʋnnana daakpana tʋʋmaha, die ʋ dine a tuo tʋntʋntɩba dieke Izara vuosi die dɩ tʋnna wa, ta suŋŋi be ba daagɩ sie gaaŋ a yiŋŋi ga wa, ʋ tʋʋmaha gie wɩa Ŋmɩŋ die baarɩ dɩ ʋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Die wɩa, sɩba nyɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ wone haalɩŋ dɩ yine nyɩŋgbaŋ kpiikiri, die gbaŋ gbaŋ yada dɩ beri ta tʋnvɩɩna dɩ wori, ka gbaŋ ka yiwo yada kpiikiŋ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ vuosi pam da keŋ yaalɩma dɩ nɩ yi dɩdagɩrɩŋ, dama nɩ wɔŋ sɩba a baarɩ tɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine dɩdagɩrɩba sarɩya nan faasɩ tʋa a tɩaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi dɩdagɩrɩba.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Tɩ mana tɩ ka yie wudieke dɩ mʋna saŋŋa mana, ama vuodieke dɩ kana ka bala wʋbɩatɩ, die vuoke seŋ yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ, ta nan bɩagɩ pɔgɩlɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Tɩ keŋ nagɩ kabarɩka a bɔbɩ duuŋ nʋaŋ dɩ ka yi tɩ dʋŋŋʋ, ka nyɩŋgbanɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ mana ŋaaŋ bie wo tɩ nuusi me.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Bɩ yilime haarɩkpɩɩma wɩa, aŋaŋ a yine nyɩŋkpɩɩma mana, bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ dɩ keŋ nɩgɩ, a chʋŋ mɩŋ, ama dɩ yiwo daabɩŋ ta vuodieke dɩ dɔnnana ka dɩ yaa dɔma ka gara jigidieke ʋ yaalala.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Die dɩ sɩ aŋaŋ jiliku; ka yiwo jabɩŋ vuota nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma, ama vuota naga ka a bala wʋkpɩɩma. Daansɩ ye, bolikaalɩbɩŋ dɩ ŋaana bɩagɩ a dii boŋŋʋkpɩɩŋ die wo!.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Jiliku dɩaŋ sɩɩ sɩba boliŋ; ka bie tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma ta yallɩ wʋbɩatɩ yiri yiri mana ta nan bɩagɩ a chʋʋsɩ tɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana, ta bɩ dii vuota beriŋ sɩba boliŋ; kanɩŋ bolibu mi nyɩŋ wa Sitaani jigiŋ.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Vuota yaa dɔŋɩsɩ aŋaŋ nembisi aŋaŋ nyinvuuke aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ hagɩrɩŋ.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: ama vuota kaaŋ bɩagɩ pɔgɩlɩ ʋ jiliŋ, ka yiwo jabɩaŋ, ta suuli aŋaŋ tʋabɩaŋ dieke dɩ yalla kuŋ kieŋ, ta ka voose.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Jiliŋ dieke tɩ yalla a bɩra tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo, kanɩŋ jiliku gbaŋ gbaŋ tɩ yaa a bala wʋbɩatɩ a yɩa vuota, vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naanna sɩba ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Nʋa dieke wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩna dɩ nyɩnnana ka ma wa, kanɩŋ ka gbaŋ gbaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ bɩatɩ dɩ bɩ nyɩnna. N nɩmballɩ dɩ yi die, dɩ ka vɩɩna.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Buluŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnnana nyanansɩŋ nan bɩagɩ bɩ nyɩŋ nyatʋakɩŋ?.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: N nɩmballɩ, kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a nyɩŋ taama? Yaa jɩɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a nyɩŋ kɩnkama? Die gbaŋ nyatʋakɩŋ buluŋ dɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ nyɩŋ nyanansɩŋ.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Mɩnɩa yalla yɩaŋ nɩ sʋnsʋŋ ta yaa sɩbɩŋ? Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ beri ʋ bemme vɩɩnɩŋ ta sʋʋŋ ʋ gbaŋ ta tʋma tʋnvɩɩna, die baaŋ dagɩ dɩ ʋ seŋ yaa yɩaŋ.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Ama dɩɩ yi nɩ yallɩ sʋgɩbɩaŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩtʋaŋ aŋaŋ halɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma, nɩ da keŋ kuulime aŋaŋ ka, ta chɩbɩma ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ a yɩma wusieke.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Kanɩŋ yɩabʋ gie ka nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ka yiwo tɩŋgbaŋka gie aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ chotiŋ aŋaŋ Sitaani tʋaŋ.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Dama jigidieke ta sʋgɩbɩaŋ aŋaŋ halɩ dɩ benne, mi mugisiŋ aŋaŋ wʋbɩatɩ yiri yiri dɩ beri.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Ama yɩaŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, wo dɩgɩntɩ; vuodieke dɩ yalla kanɩŋ yɩaŋ, ʋ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta bie sɔmm, ta wʋmma yɩa ʋ chanchaalɩŋ, ta chɩga zɔɔlɩŋ pam, ta bɩ tʋma tʋnvɩɩna, ta ka luge, ta bala wusie.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ ta vasa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ dɩ beri vuosi sʋnsʋŋ, banɩŋ ba vanana Ŋmɩŋ chɩgɩla dɩ beri.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Bɩa taasɩnana waagɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ nɩ sʋnsʋŋ? A nyɩŋ wa sʋgɩbɩaŋ dɩ benne nɩ sʋgɩtɩ ma.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Nɩ yaala nyinti a taŋ, ama nɩ ka yese he, die wɩa nɩ ŋaaŋ yiwo siri aŋaŋ vuokʋʋla. Nɩ yaa sʋgɩbɩaŋ aŋaŋ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ nyinti, ama ta ka yese he, die wɩa waagɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩnhagɩrɩŋ bie wo nɩ sʋnsʋŋ. Nɩ wo jadieke nɩ yaalala dama nɩ ka jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ʋ yɩ nɩ kanɩŋ jaabʋ mi.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Nɩ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ ama nɩ ka yese dama nɩ yaa sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ, nɩ jʋʋsa yaala nɩ sʋgɩbɩatɩ nyinti.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Nɩ va wa Ŋmɩŋ ta dɩɩ nyɩŋgaasɩ; dɩɩ yi nɩ yaala tɩŋgbaŋka gie nyɩnbɩatɩ, nɩ ka sɩba dɩ nɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dataasɩ? Vuodieke dɩ yaala tɩŋgbaŋka nyɩnbɩatɩ, wʋnɩŋ vuoke mi bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ dataaŋ.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ka yiwo yɔrɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa dɩ baarɩ dɩ, Ʋ yaala Haalɩ dieke ʋ yɩna tɩ wa pam.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Ama ʋ bɩ yɩa tɩ wa suŋŋiŋ pam. Die wɩa dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Ŋmɩŋ waga kalinbaantieliŋ, ama ta suŋŋe vuodiekemba dɩ sʋʋna ba gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Die wɩa nɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ nʋaŋ, ama ta zetime Sitaani, ta ʋ nan chɩgɩ ta va nɩ.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Nɩ chʋŋ gbigi Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, ta ʋ gbaŋ ʋ nan keŋ gbigi nɩ. Nɩnɩŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tieliŋ nɩ vaa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ yiile; nɩnɩŋ sʋŋanyile bule tieliŋ nɩ wʋnsɩ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ ta kʋmma aŋaŋ nintaantɩ nɩ wʋbɩatɩ wɩa, ta daa bɩ laama, ta vaa nɩ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bɩrɩŋ nɩ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Nɩ sʋʋŋ nɩ gbaŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nɩŋŋa, ta ʋ nan kɔtɩ nɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ da keŋ balɩma wʋbɩatɩ a yɩma taŋ. Vuodieke dɩ bala wʋbɩatɩ yaa gamma ʋ nɩmbʋa ma a chʋʋsa wa, dɩ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ʋ bala wʋbɩatɩ a yɩa ta chʋʋsa die. Dɩɩ yi fʋ bala a chʋʋsa mɩraha, die nɩŋ ka bɩ yi vuodieke dɩ dɩna ha, ama vuodieke dɩ chʋʋsɩnana ha.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma yalla sieŋ a baaŋ bɩagɩ a zieŋ mɩra, ta bɩ bɩagɩ a dii tɩ sarɩya, ta bɩ yaa hagɩrɩ dieke dɩ baaŋ bɩagɩ a gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ, ta bɩ bɩagɩ chʋʋsɩ tɩ, ta mɩnɩa dɩaŋ benne a yaa sieŋ dɩ ʋ dii ʋ chanchaaŋ sarɩya?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Nɩ ye, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ balala dɩ, Jinne yaa soriŋ, tɩ nan dɩa a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ, tɩ nan dɩ bie mi a ga tʋgɩ bɩŋ ta dii nyʋŋ a ye ligire pam.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: ta ka wɔŋ sɩba wudieke dɩ bala ka yi soriŋ. Nɩ miivoli yine bɩa? Ka sɩɩ sɩba mɩɩlɩgɩŋ dɩ ŋaana nanna sʋkʋʋŋ die ama dɩ yi sʋaa dɩ bɩa wa.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Die wɩa, tɩ mʋna tɩ balɩ wudieke wʋnna, Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ, tɩ nan yaa miivoli, ta yi naa aŋaŋ naa.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Ama nɩ yallɩ wa kalinbaani ta kuule; nɩ kuulinene die wo ka vɩɩna.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Die wɩa vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna tʋnvɩɩna ha yiile, ama ta ka yiye, ka yiwo wʋbɩaŋ.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nyintitieliŋ, nɩ wʋmma! Nɩ kʋma aŋaŋ nintaantɩ yaa gamma wʋbɩa dieke dɩ kienene nɩ ma wa.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Nɩ nyintiti mana hʋʋgɩya mɩŋ ta tɔgɩŋ dɩ mʋgɩ nɩ nyiŋyeeke mana.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Nɩ ligire gbaŋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, ka nan dagɩ dɩ nɩ chʋʋsɩya, ta ka nan chʋʋsɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ sɩba boliŋ dɩ ŋaana die die wo. Nɩ zieŋ wo nɩ nyinti a gbarɩ nɩ sarɩya diile daaŋ.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Nɩ nyɩra tʋntʋntɩba dɩ gobinene nɩ zaa nɩ kʋatɩ ma, ta ka tune be, ta ba kʋma, ta ba kpambʋ dɩ tʋgɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ tɩba ma.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Nɩ bie wo tɩŋgbaŋka gie me nɩ dʋŋŋʋ ta dɩɩ nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ lɔla nyinti. Nɩ wubi wo nɩ gbaŋ ta beri sɩba naapɔlɩ diekemba dɩ gbarɩna kʋʋla.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ bala chʋʋsa vuovɩɩna ta kʋa ba ama die ba ka wagɩ nɩ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ dii suguru ta chɩɩsɩma tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ kenne daraaŋ. Nɩ ye kpaarʋ dɩ ŋaana dii sikimiŋ a chɩɩsa bʋmbʋŋaŋ nɩɩŋ a ga tʋgɩ kʋaŋannɩɩŋ ta ʋ nyindiikehe mɩŋŋɩ a yi dene.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Die wɩa nɩ gbaŋ nɩ dii suguru dene, ta vaa nɩ sɩkpara dʋa dama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ kenne daraaŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: N nɩmballɩ, nɩ da keŋ vʋʋma taŋ, ama dɩɩ yi nɩ yi die, Ŋmɩŋ nan dii nɩ sarɩya. Ye, sarɩyadiiru wo zie wo nɩ sanʋa nʋaŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: N nɩmballɩ, Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba vuodiekemba dɩ balla ʋ saaŋ ma, nɩ ye die ba dinene wahala die ta yaa suguru die wo, nɩ vaa aŋ ka yi dagɩŋ a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Banɩŋ vuosi die dɩ dine suguru wo, tɩ wasa ba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ tieliŋ. Nɩ wɔŋ wʋŋ Jobu die dɩ yalla suguru die wo, ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ keŋ vʋarɩ wa a nyɩŋ ʋ wahala ma. Dɩ dagɩ tɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩla dene ta chɩga tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: N nɩmballɩ, wudieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna wʋnna, nɩ daa keŋ hʋma. Nɩ da wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ yaa tɩŋgbaŋka gie yaa jaaŋ mana saaŋ ta hʋʋ. Nɩ vaa nɩ wa yi nɩ wa, ta nɩ aayɩ yi nɩ aayɩ amʋ Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ a datɩ nɩ tɩbɩŋ.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ bie nɩ sʋnsʋŋ ta yaa wumugisikiŋ, ʋ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ. Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ʋ chɩama yɩla a bɩrɩma Ŋmɩŋ.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Dɩ bɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ bie nɩ jigiŋ a yʋagɩ, ʋ wa Masia kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋra aŋ ba duuri wo aŋaŋ kpaaŋ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia saaŋ ma ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ wa.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba jʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ yada, ʋ nan ye gbaamɩŋ, tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ nan gbaaŋ wa, ta dɩɩ yi ʋ tʋŋ wa tʋntʋmbɩatɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan nagɩ ʋ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa wa.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Die wɩa nɩ balɩma nɩ wʋbɩatɩ a yɩma taŋ ta jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ a yɩma taŋ, ta Ŋmɩŋ nan gbaaŋ nɩ. Vuovɩɩnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ yaa hagɩrɩŋ pam.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Elaja die yiwo vuota sɩba tɩnɩŋ, ama die ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ nɩɩŋ da nɩɩ, ta die nɩɩkʋ dɩ seŋ ka nɩa a ga tʋgɩ bɩna ataa aŋaŋ chɩɩtɩ ayʋaba.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ a jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, ta nɩɩŋ die dɩ nɩɩ ta tɩŋgbaŋka die dɩ fɩalɩ ta nyindiike die dɩ yi vɩɩnɩŋ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: N nɩmballɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ nyɩŋ wusie sieku me, ta nɩ wʋnyɩ dɩ bɩ yaa wa yiŋŋi keŋ sieku me bɩbra.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: nɩ tɩɩnsɩ genke, dɩ vuodieke dɩ vʋarɩna wʋbɩayiiru a nyɩŋ ʋ wʋbɩatɩ sieŋ me, vʋarɩ wa ʋ mɩsɩ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me ta vaa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ tʋmbɩatɩ pam a chaa.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Manɩŋ Piita vuodieke dɩ yine Yisa Masia tʋntʋntʋ maagɩnana gbaŋkʋ gie a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkaha dɩ jaasɩna a bie Pontusi tɩŋgbaŋ ma aŋaŋ Galasia aŋaŋ Kapadosia aŋaŋ Asia aŋaŋ Bitinia tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ ma wa ta yi chaamba.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Tɩ Chɔɔŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die wolinne a vʋarɩ nɩ ta die sɩba dɩ nɩ nan keŋ yi ʋ vuosi, ta ʋ Halɩkasɩka dɩ yi nɩ sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ dɩ nɩ tuosime Yisa Masia nʋaŋ aŋ ʋ sugiri nɩ aŋaŋ ʋ zɩŋ. Ŋmɩŋ suŋŋi nɩ, ta yɩ nɩ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ pam.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Nɩ vaa tɩ waasɩ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ yine tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ Yisa Masia Chɔɔŋ wa, dama ʋ chɩgɩna tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ pam ta yi tɩ miivoli haalɩŋ Yisa Masia kumbu hagɩŋ wɩa. Naa yine aŋ tɩ yaa tama dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: dɩ tɩ nan keŋ ye nyindiekemba dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ sɩɩtɩ, nyindiekemba dɩ baaŋ dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu, ta kaaŋ chʋʋsɩ yaa hʋʋgɩ, aŋ ka vɩɩnɩŋ kaaŋ nyɩŋ ta bie arɩzanna ma.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: a gbarɩ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ dɩ gbarɩna nɩ, nɩ yada wɩa wa, dɩ nɩ nan keŋ ye nɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ nyʋarɩ dieke dɩ yine siri ta nan keŋ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma dakpatɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ wa.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Die wɩa nɩ yallɩma sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ ŋaana yaa sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa bɩta lele nɩ dinene wahala yiri yiri wɩa naa dama dɩ yiwo talasɩ dɩ nɩ dii wahala.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Wahalaha kieŋ mɩŋ dɩ Ŋmɩŋ ye dɩ nɩ seŋ a yi Masia yada mɩŋ. Dɩ sɩɩ sɩba ba ŋaana naga salɩma a yie boliŋ me wo dɩ ba ye ka vɩɩna dene wo. Aŋaŋ ba yinene naa mana, ama salɩma nan keŋ chʋʋsɩ ta nɩ yada yiile vɩɩna mɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ a tɩaŋ salɩma gbaŋ. Dɩɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ dɩ ye dɩ nɩ seŋ yi Masia yada, die baaŋ vaa nɩ ye bɩrɩŋ aŋaŋ pɩmpagɩŋ aŋaŋ jɩlɩma saŋŋa dieke Yisa Masia dɩ bala ʋ keŋ a nyɩŋ wa.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Nɩ ye ka ye Masia wa gie ama ta cho wo; ta nɩ ka yese wo lele ama ta yi wo yada, ta yaa sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ pam vuota dɩ kana kaaŋ bɩagɩ a dagɩ ka sɩna die.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Nɩ yine wo yada wa wɩa, nɩ yese gbatɩtaanɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Gbatɩtaaŋkʋ gie wɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ mɩa dɩ ba mɩŋŋɩ sɩmma ka chɩaŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, ta die yiŋŋi bala yaa gamma piinike gie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ daansɩ nagɩ yɩ nɩnɩŋ wa.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Masia Halɩkasɩka dieke dɩ benne ba ma wa die wolinne a dagɩ ba dɩ ʋ nan daansɩ dii wahala; ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ nan keŋ ye jɩlɩma; ta die ba mɩa dɩ ba sɩmma saŋŋa dieke naa dɩ bala ka yi, ta bɩ mɩa dɩ ba sɩmma ka bala ka yi dene.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ yuori dagɩ ba dɩ ba ka yiŋŋi bala ba gbaŋ gbaŋ suŋŋiŋ wɩa ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ suŋŋiŋ wɩa aŋaŋ ba ŋaana bala yaa gamma wudiekemba nɩ wʋnna lele a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋntɩba jigiri. Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩ dieke ʋ dene tʋŋ wa die vana ba mʋʋlɩ wʋvɩɩnaha gie a yɩ nɩ. Naa die malakasi gbaŋ die dɩ mɩa dɩ ba sɩmma ka chɩaŋ.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Die wɩa, nɩ yi siri aŋaŋ nɩ sʋŋanyile ta gbara, ta yigi nɩ gbaŋ, ta yallɩma tama aŋaŋ wusie yaa gamma wʋvɩɩŋ dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala ʋ keŋ yɩ nɩ saŋŋa dieke Yisa Masia dɩ bala ʋ keŋ a nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma wa.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Lele nɩ wana yi Ŋmɩŋ ballɩ naa, nɩ saagɩma a dɩa ʋ nʋaŋ, ta daa dɩma lɔlɩbɩatɩ diekemba die nɩ dɩna wa saŋŋa dieke nɩ yene ka sɩba Yisa Masia wa.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ vʋarɩ nɩ wa yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ, die wɩa nɩ gbaŋ nɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ nɩ wuyiike mana ma.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, Nɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ dama manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ yiwo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ Ŋmɩŋ dinene vuota mana sarɩya yaa gamma ʋ yine wudieke ta ka luge, ta wʋnɩŋ nɩ wasa wa Tɩ Chɔɔŋ, die wɩa nɩ yene bie tɩŋgbaŋka gie me wo sɩba chaamba naa, nɩ yɩma wa jɩlɩma.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ die nɩ yiwo yɔŋɩsɩ a yi wʋtɔgɩ diekemba die nɩ tuone a nyɩŋ nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba jigiri, ta nɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩna nɩ a nyɩŋ ka ma dene, die ʋ ka nagɩ jadieke dɩ chʋʋsɩnana sɩba salɩma yaa kʋʋyɩala a tuŋ, Aayɩ!.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: Ama die ʋ nagɩ wa Yisa Masia zɩŋ dieke dɩ vɩɩŋna a tɩanna ligire mana; aŋ ka sɩɩ sɩba kaabɩŋ yiivɩɩŋ dieke dɩ wone bɩaŋ yaa magɩlɩŋ ʋ ma wa.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yene ka naaŋ dʋnɩaka gie wo, ama die ʋ woliŋ a vʋarɩ wa Masia dɩ ʋ yine kaabɩkʋ gie, ta Masia dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ yaalɩŋ ma dakpatɩkɩrɩ gie nɩ wɩa.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Ʋ wɩa nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada; wʋnɩŋ die ʋ sʋgɩrɩna wa a nyɩŋ kuŋ me ta yɩ wa jɩlɩma; die wɩa nɩ yallɩ yada aŋaŋ tama Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Nɩ tuone Masia wusie wɩaha wɩa yine nɩ nyɩŋ bɩaŋ ma, ta lele nɩ wa cho nɩ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ wusie; die wɩa, nɩ chome taŋ aŋaŋ nɩ sʋgɩtɩ mana.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Nɩ ye wo miivoli haalɩŋ mɩŋ; miivoli mi ka nyɩŋ jabʋrɩ dieke dɩ kpinene jigiŋ ama ka nyɩŋ wa jabʋrɩ dieke dɩ kana ka kpie me; Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkɩ dieke dɩ yalla miivoli ta nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu nagɩna ka a yɩ nɩ.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ: Vuota mana sɩɩ sɩba hʋʋtɩ, ta ba vɩɩnɩŋ dɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩɩwute Hʋʋtɩ ŋaaŋ jigi mɩŋ ta a wutehe nan.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: ama tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩaha gie nɩŋ nan dɩ beri yaa gamma haahuu. Wɩaha gie yiwo wʋvɩɩŋ dieke die ba mʋʋlɩna a yɩ nɩ wa.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Die wɩa nɩ vaa wʋbɩatɩ mana, aŋaŋ gaamɩŋ mana, aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ, aŋaŋ sʋgɩbɩaŋ, aŋaŋ saaŋ chʋʋsɩŋ mana.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Ballɩnyaalɩsɩ ŋaaŋ yaala ba nyusi biisiŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna, nɩnɩŋ nɩ sɩmma sɩba ballɩnyaalɩsɩ: nɩ mʋ nɩ yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha sɩba bʋanyaalɩŋ dɩ yaalala biisiŋ dene wo; Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha sɩɩ sɩba wusie biisiŋ, dama wɩaha nan vaa nɩ bɩrɩŋ nɩ gbatɩtaanɩŋ sieŋ me.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: lele nɩ wana laŋ ta sɩba dɩ tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ vɩɩna naa.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Nɩnɩŋ nɩ mʋ nɩ kiere ʋ jigiŋ, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine miivoli taŋ dieke vuota dɩ zetine ama ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vʋarɩ wa ta ʋ yi nɩŋŋmɩna jaaŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Ta nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩɩ sɩba miivoli tana aŋ ba naga nɩ a mɩa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ. Juoku gie yiwo juodieke nɩnɩŋ sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ bala nɩ yime kaaba a yɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta ʋ nan dɩ tuose kaabaha Yisa Masia wɩa.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Dama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ Ŋmɩŋ die baarɩ dɩ: Ye, n dʋana taŋ Zayɔni tɩŋ ma, Manɩŋ n vʋarɩna tanɩ mi kanɩŋ ka yine juoku gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ tanɩ ta ka faasɩ yi nɩŋŋmɩna taŋ, ta vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ yine wo yada kaaŋ dii viivi.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Tɔ, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yine wo yada nɩŋ, tanɩ faasɩ yiwo nɩŋŋmɩna jaaŋ a yɩ nɩ; ama vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi wo yada wa nɩŋ, ka sɩɩ sɩba dɩ maagɩna Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dene wo dɩ: Taŋ dieke mɩmɩɩrɩba dɩ zetine wo kanɩŋ ka senne yi taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: Ta ka bɩ maagɩ dɩ: Tanɩ mi vuosi dɩ baa ba gbiŋ, ta ka bɩ vaa ba nan. Vuosi mi gbime nagɩsɩ ta nanna dama ba kana ka tuo Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha wɩa, naa die Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ dɩ ba nan dɩ sɩɩ.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, nɩ yiwo buuri dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vʋarɩna, ta bɩ yi naaŋ vuosi dɩ dɩnana vuosi nɩŋŋa ba jɩama Ŋmɩŋ, ta bɩ yi sʋgɩyɩalɩŋ vuosi, ta bɩ yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ vuosi, nɩnɩŋ nɩ dagɩnana Ŋmɩŋ chuulikpeŋkpɩɩŋ tʋʋmaha, wʋnɩŋ ʋ vʋarɩna nɩ a nyɩŋ lɩmɩŋkʋ ma, ta bɩ wa nɩ a keŋ ʋ mamachi chaankpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Saŋŋa dieke die dɩ tɩanna wa die nɩ ka yi Ŋmɩŋ vuosi ama lele nɩŋ nɩ wa yiwo ʋ vuosi; saŋŋa dieke dɩ tɩanna die ʋ ka chɩgɩ nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ ama lele nɩŋ, ʋ wa chɩgɩ nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ mɩŋ.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: N nɩmballɩ choti, nɩ wʋmma, n kpaama nɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ nɩ yine chaamba dʋnɩa ka gie me wɩa, nɩ vaa nyɩŋgbaŋka gie lɔlɩbɩatɩ diekemba nɩ wagɩna aŋaŋ nɩ naa.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: ama ta bemme vɩɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ sʋŋ, ta dɩɩ yi ba zɩa nɩ dɩ nɩ yiwo vuobɩatɩ, ba nan ye nɩ tʋnvɩɩna ta bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ, daa dieke ʋ bala ʋ keŋ a ye vuosi dɩ benne dene wo.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ wɩa nɩ sʋʋŋ nɩ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yalla yiko mana, nɩ saagɩma a yɩma naakpɩɩŋ dieke dɩ sɩna jigiŋ mana.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: aŋaŋ ʋ nyɩŋkʋra, wʋnɩŋ ʋ tʋnna ba dɩ ba keŋ a datɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene bɩaŋ tɩbaha ama ta bɩrɩ vuodiekemba dɩ yinene vɩɩŋkʋ.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Dama wudieke Ŋmɩŋ yaalala yiwo naa, ʋ yaala nɩ yime wʋvɩɩna amʋ nɩ nan kagɩ gɩgaantɩba dɩ balala wʋbalɩkɩ tɔgɩtɩ yaa gamma nɩ ma wa.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Nɩ bemme sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ sɩna ba gbaŋ, ama nɩ da vaa nɩ dʋŋŋʋ beriku gie vaa nɩ lɔbɩrɩ yi bɩaŋ, ama nɩ bemme sɩba Ŋmɩŋ yɔŋɩsɩ.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Nɩ yɩma vuota mana jɩlɩma ta chome Masia dɩdɩɩsɩrɩŋ chanchaalɩŋ mana, ta chɩgɩma Ŋmɩŋ ta yɩma naakpɩɩka jɩlɩma.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Nɩnɩŋ tʋntʋntɩba, nɩ mʋ nɩ sʋʋŋ wa nɩ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ a yɩma nɩ nyɩŋkʋra, ta yɩma ba jɩlɩma, daa vuodiekemba dɩ yine vuovɩɩna ta chɩga nɩ zɔɔlɩŋ nyɩɩna ma, ama vuodiekemba dɩ tʋana gbaŋ.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Dɩɩ yi ba nɩgɩ fʋ fʋ tʋnvɩɩna wɩa, ta fʋ yile Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ta dii suguru, die vɩɩŋna Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋŋa.
